# Thread: Help with my new Trifield meter? :)

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-07T01:31:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-my-new-trifield-meter.12546/post-151903>

Hi everyone! I just received my blue blockers, Jack's book, and my Trifield meter from Amazon today and I'm trying to figure out what exactly I ought to be checking with this meter. I'm already walking around figuring out how it works, but what should I be paying attention to? Is there a particular scale of reference for what I would want my router to be at, or my microwave, or the cordless phone? How high lamps ought to be, etc? Because I get readings of some kind or another off just about everything but don't know what it means.

I saw some people referring to testing their home, in a general way. Does that mean just standing in the middle of the room with the meter on the most sensitive setting and seeing if anything registers?

Thanks!

### Lahelada — 2015-01-07T01:40:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-my-new-trifield-meter.12546/post-151904>

I dont have one but if I did I would.
1\. check out my sleeping place, the mattress especially if it is a spring mattress.
2\. if the readjngs are not ideal, find out if there is an optimal spot in the house.
3\. Check out the walls if you have adjoining neigbours. Do they have a router near you,a smart meter?
4\. Your electrical appliances and sockets.
5\. even your floor readings may be interesting.
6\. your Pc/lap top.

Note down all the values ,post here and someone will tell you what is high.

No adjoining neighbors - we own a home on just over 5 acres of mostly conifer woods. Only one cell tower within 4 miles (it's actually under 2 miles away).

But.. one of the walls in my daughter's bedroom... the electrical outlet makes the magnetic (0-100 range) meter jump all the way up to 100 and stay pegged there when I point it at the outlet. I'm really really upset and sick about this right now. Does this mean the electrical back there in the addition was done bad?? What do I do? I'm so worried now!

Anyone? Thanks!

The remote control for the TV when not being pushed - 0. When pushing a button - 5. (I can now legitimately tell my husband that his channel changing is making me sick and to give me the remote or leave it alone! Ha!  )

I found a lot of the same things in my house. Its great to have a meter cuz now you know! My husband had fluorescent lights in the garage and I had him swap them back to regular bulb outlets. Dimmer switches are also bad. Replaced cordless phones with wired. The jacuzzi in master bath was really high even when not running. Since I don't use it, I turned off the breaker. My daughters room is above the garage so I went crazy trying to get that lowered...it still bugs me. We found a plug incorrectly wired. Played with circuit breakers to try to figure it out. The grounding plug on laptop makes a big diff too so well worth it. I have virtually everything unplugged. I used a body voltage meter too. I also found the basement was the best. I just got the TES-593 meter not too long ago and have done some readings but not finding too much with it...prob need to do a consult with Michael.

### Inger — 2015-01-07T12:11:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-my-new-trifield-meter.12546/post-151943>

Hope.... that is so cool you have measured your home... look now it is really easy to make improvements     yay!!!!

I suggest, do not sit in front of the heater ever anymore.... but just put on wool socks and more clothing instead for the beginning  you will slowly get warmer when you increase your CT!
And always keep the microwave unplugged when not is use... (better never use it anymore!)
I have close to zero pretty much everywhere in my home...... maybe 0,5 milligauss at max... i use an extra keyboard for my laptop.. and wired mouse for sure... it helps a lot! I keep my stove unplugged too (it had pretty bad readings....) and only plug it in when i use it, which is pretty seldom as i mostly eat my food raw

If you cook all your food you might consider to add MSM (sulfur) as it gets destroyed by cooking...... I have started to take MSM (even if I eat mostly raw...) and i swear it does something good to my mood! It is not expensive either..... might be worth a try

I stopped using my hairdrier and straightener the very day i measured them... the reading were horrible! And i used both every single day my whole adult life  My hair likes it way better without them too

love how you are moving along    you rock!

### av8r — 2015-01-07T18:18:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-my-new-trifield-meter.12546/post-151977>

I love the Back to the Future trilogy!

I unplug my microwave too; I would get rid of the damn thing if my wife didn’t use it on occasion. I also have a pellet stove that puts out very high EMF… no standing next to it when it is on.

Dirty electricity caused by switching power supplies can back feed through house wiring too and give you readings that are hard to trace down. Small plug -in power supplies for laptop computers (or other electronic devices) can create EMF nightmares. I have an outlet in my living room that feeds from wiring under the living room floor, if a laptop is plugged into that outlet, which happens on occasion, I can read very high EMF right down the middle of my living room floor… I also found a florescence drop light plugged in, but turned off, and hung from the ceiling of my basement right under my bedroom floor, I could read medium EMF levels at the head of my bed one floor above… the drop light was not even turned on!!! needless to say, that f@@@er is gone!

The saddest for me was measuring the seat of my Harley Sportster… in the 0-100 scale it pegged the EMF meter :-( ... even though that bike pegged my fun meter, I am not taking that risk of putting my prostate in that high of an EMF field. The bikes computer is mounted right under the seat, that is were the EMF is coming from. I sold the bike last fall. I will look for another bike but this one will be a much older model before computerized fuel injection.

### Martin — 2015-01-07T20:07:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-my-new-trifield-meter.12546/post-151994>

Welcome to testing emf's! You are finding out good things with your meter, but I've read and heard reviews by professional EMF researchers that Tri-Field's tend to read a little high. That might ease your worry a bit (remember, it causes stress! But you knew that).

Using a "Body Voltage" meter that runs a wire out the door and plugs into the ground is preferred over the "Electrical" setting on your meter. There are many meter options in getting those more accurate readings. Goggle it to get some more info.

The Tri is not good a reading microwaves very well. The microwave range starts out really high so you will miss the weaker readings that are very important. Still, the fact that it is telling you "something" is a plus and you got a great deal on this meter at the price you paid.

Magnetic fields: You've got some issues going on in your home. First, pay close attention to your sleeping area and turn off breakers as you read the field around your bed. Ditch the clock, horrible. Can openers, dehumidifiers, filters, anything with a motor (motors have a spinning metal thingy inside it that acts like a miniature iron core in the Earth) creates high mag fields. Move them away from sleeping areas and wherever you spend a lot of time. Get those fields down as close to zero as you can, especially at night during sleep (you do sleep at night I hope - shift work will kill ya quicker!).

Flourescent lights have a transformer in them that create large mag fields. Ditch them whenever/wherever. Watch out if you're over a crawlspace or second story as fixtures, h20 heaters, furnaces, can all create fields that can come from below.

Your 100 mil/gauss on an outlet is troubling. That's REALLY high. Is something plugged into it? Miss-wiring? Get an outlet checker at hardware ($8-10?) and plug it in to see any problems. Call electrician, and turn off the breaker when your kids are around it. I have a good meter and I get "0" from most of my outlets, and it takes placing the meter's probe very, very close to a motor or fluorescent fixture to get readings that high.

Totally, unmercifully, unquestionably, ditch the microwave oven. Forever.

Congrats on testing! I know it's a bit freaky now that you've checked, but you're ahead of the game now. Environment is where it's at!

NSH

Ditch the clock, horrible. Move them away from sleeping areas and wherever you spend a lot of time. I go to bed around 9:30 each night and get up at 5:30 in the morning with rare weekend or holiday exceptions. My quality of sleep is fairly poor but nowhere nearing insomnia thank goodness.

> Flourescent lights have a transformer in them that create large mag fields. Ditch them whenever/wherever. Watch out if you're over a crawlspace or second story as fixtures, h20 heaters, furnaces, can all create fields that can come from below.

Actually, my daughter's electrical outlet had her tv running from it when I measured it last night. I measured it with the tv off today and it has fairly benign readings like all of the outlets and light switches (around 4-6). I then turned the tv back on and it shot right up. The head of her bed is literally as far from that outlet as it can possibly be, at hte far other side of the room, so I told he to just minimize her TV time and sit back from it. Solid advice for anyone at any time I think!.

I told her about her kindle and cellphone readings today (she's almost 12 and in middle school). Then we went and read the desktop computer, the monitor, the mouse, everything back there including her sitting in the computer chair holding the meter - it's only a 2 except directly in front of the monitor so without being told, she has situated herself back there tonight instead of in her room on one of her other devices with her tv running for no reason. This is a win/win so far for several reasons.

> Totally, unmercifully, unquestionably, ditch the microwave oven. Forever.

I agree. I was going to get a new one, then I was going to leave this one unplugged except when we use it... then today I heard myself telling my husband "I think the microwave kills the water" and he gave me the funniest look until I said WTF radiation water? (not in so many words) and he agreed, hehe.

> Congrats on testing! I know it's a bit freaky now that you've checked, but you're ahead of the game now. Environment is where it's at!
>
> NSH

Thank you! I agree, and am so glad to know what I'm up against now! Out baseline mid-house reading in each room is around 1.5 - 2 but I suspect that's due to wifi. I will test with the router turned off later.

Oh I noticed our breaker box is really high like 3 feet out from it still (15ish) and maxed out over 100 when right in front of it.

The breaker box is in the kitchen right next to the cabinets with food in them.

What does this do to food, if anything?

Thanks again for all the help!

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-01-08T00:05:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-my-new-trifield-meter.12546/post-152025>

the body voltage meter measures positive charge build up on the skin surface
this drops when one is earthed

[http://www.paleoosteo.com/effect-of-earthing-via-earthing-mat-demonstrated/](http://www.paleoosteo.com/effect-of-earthing-via-earthing-mat-demonstrated/)

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-08T00:44:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-my-new-trifield-meter.12546/post-152028>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=152025)
>
> the body voltage meter measures positive charge build up on the skin surface
> this drops when one is earthed
>
> [http://www.paleoosteo.com/effect-of-earthing-via-earthing-mat-demonstrated/](http://www.paleoosteo.com/effect-of-earthing-via-earthing-mat-demonstrated/)

That is really very cool. Quite effective demonstration as well. Thank you for sharing that! My husband often complains I'm difficult to buy for on birthdays and anniversaries.. I believe I now have a small list of suggestions built up between labwork I want run and an earthing mat and who knows what else will come along (funny to request labs for your birthday but whatever!)!

### enyaw — 2016-04-01T02:23:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-my-new-trifield-meter.12546/post-191804>

> [Hope2Learn said:](/goto/post?id=151912)
>
> Downstairs bathroom (mentioned above) - 0 (which is weird because we do get wireless internet in there).
>
> **Wireless combination modem/router/includes digital phone (wall-mounted next to the kitchen table, cannot be moved) - 30 to 100+ fluctuating constantly**

You won't get an accurate reading from a WiFi router with the Trifield. The Trifield only reads freqs up to 100KHz while a WiFi router operates at either 2.4 or 5 GHz.

[http://ajpendo.physiology.org/content/296/4/E820](http://ajpendo.physiology.org/content/296/4/E820)

So it seems natural and NECESSARY for your vit D levels to drop in the winter, to enable uncoupling proteins to work their magic in the cold. BOOM!

As a side note, forgive me if this has been discussed. My search didn't bring up anything on this topic.

### AndrÃ©s — 2015-01-30T20:29:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-vitamin-d-inhibits-uncoupling-proteins-this-makes-perfect-sense.12555/post-154284>

_Non sequitur_. It doesn't matter that VDR-null mice have their furniture always on. The question is if they are better adapted to cold exposure than normal mice. Sincerely I doubt it since I suppose that I am not the only one keeping high levels of 25-OH-vitD year round (greater than 50ng/ml) at the same time that keeping CTing (well, I still do mostly [DIY-ICE-vest-2.0](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/diy-ice-vest-2-0.5263/), drive with AC full blast toward me and keep heat-off at my office) year round too. Brown fat turns on without problems as far as I can see.

I think prudent to follow [Vieth's hypothesis](http://ar.iiarjournals.org/content/29/9/3675.full) while not falsified.

# Thread: NTIA readies 100MHz in 3.5GHz spectrum for release
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ntia-readies-100mhz-in-3-5ghz-spectrum-for-release.12567/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2015-01-10T05:18:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ntia-readies-100mhz-in-3-5ghz-spectrum-for-release.12567/post-152293>

The US government has cleared the way for Google to become your next cellphone network provider – possibly without costing the company a pretty penny.

As we [revealed yesterday](http://www.theregister.co.uk/2015/01/08/googles_plan_to_become_your_phone_company/), the advertising giant wants to use the largely open spectrum at 3.5GHz to provide high-speed mobile data connections at low cost to phones and gadgets in cities.

The main stumbling block, for Google, is getting Uncle Sam to open up the radio space in a way that would give the company exclusive access to specific frequencies without having to buy an expensive license; cellphone networks typically spend hundreds of millions of dollars on buying the rights to use the ether.

Another hurdle is that the electronics in the vast majority of today's mobes and fondleslabs can't use that 3.5GHz slot, but it is in one of the [approved bands for LTE](http://niviuk.free.fr/lte_band.php). Another problem is that 3.5GHz signals will be rather short-range, so it's only good for cities and towns where lots of small cells can be deployed.

In a [release](http://www.ntia.doc.gov/blog/2015/promoting-spectrum-sharing-wireless-broadband-era) titled "Promoting Spectrum Sharing" put out on Friday, the US Department of Commerce's National Telecommunications and Information Administration ([NTIA](http://www.ntia.doc.gov/about)) announced that it is "to collaborate with the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to free up critical radio spectrum to fuel the breakneck growth of the wireless broadband market," noting that the issue is "more pressing than ever, with the wild popularity of smartphones and tablets driving unprecedented commercial demand for mobile bandwidth."

The NTIA then talks about the two bands – at 1695-1710MHz and 1755-1780MHz – that are being auctioned off for $44.7bn as of today.

But the bulk of the announcement is about the issue of "spectrum sharing" that is needed for more space to be allocated to non-government organizations. The 3.5GHz slot that Google is interested in, for example, is used by the Department of Defense.

"The future lies in sharing spectrum," says the NTIA, "across government agencies and commercial services, and across time, geography and other dimensions in the future."

On the key 3.5GHz band, the release notes: "We are collaborating with the FCC to explore the possibility of making 100MHz of spectrum available for shared small cell use in the 3.5GHz band currently used primarily for military radar systems. This could be an important pivot point toward a new sharing paradigm."

Which in plain English means: by the end of next year Google may offer you an unlimited data plan on your cellphone for much less than what you are currently paying – assuming Google can lay its hands on the frequencies it wants for free or a price it's willing to pay. It will have to reach into its deep pockets if someone else shows an interest; so far there's no mention of payment, but the FCC has said an auction will be held if more than one outfit wants exclusive rights to a frequency slot.

The announcement also notes that the government is looking at "the feasibility of increased sharing by unlicensed devices in the 5GHz band. And we are working with federal agencies to assess their spectrum usage in five bands accounting for 960MHz to prioritize bands for detailed sharing feasibility studies." ®


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Top Ten Tensegrity Tips
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/top-ten-tensegrity-tips.12579/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2015-01-11T20:09:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/top-ten-tensegrity-tips.12579/post-152419>

We all know that the best ways to improve tensegrity are sunlight, CT, or exercise like yoga. But what about those times when you are unable to hang from a mountain by your fingertips? How can you get that extra burst of energy to help you sleep or stay awake, to stop coughing or clear a stuffy nose, or maybe even to figure out what Jack is saying?

My current favorite technique is to gently rake the skin of the arms and legs with the back of the fingernails. The skin is stimulated and it tingles, without scratching the skin. Works like a charm. Feels like goosebumps. The backs of the toenails work well for the arch of the other foot while you are in bed. I've noticed my flabby parts don't respond ideally, but if you are persistent, they will come around.

### Josh — 2015-01-11T21:02:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/top-ten-tensegrity-tips.12579/post-152422>

For me the technique depends upon the time of day, but sitting meditation is the favorite with the most consistent results. One can actually sit and visualize almost any other technique imaginable, including complex yoga and qi gong movements and get the same effects. Just letting go and watching works the best for me at this point.

### Marie — 2015-01-11T23:17:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/top-ten-tensegrity-tips.12579/post-152437>

He noticed me watching him, and he told me it helped him stay awake. A decade or so later I tried it, and discovered it works quite well. But I used a brush. Trying to comb my mop would probably get me in a wreck...

I wonder if that is part of the tradition of 100 brush strokes before bed? Yeah, I tried that... once.

### nonchalant — 2015-01-15T00:17:11+0200

When we neared the city, I noticed I couldn't get much reaction from my skin. Not many tingly feelings. And I started getting thirsty. When we finally started back home that evening, I was having problems with keeping comfortable in the cool temps. That night, back home, I started a fire, but even after the rest of my body warmed up, my feet were very uncomfortable. I figured I was dehydrated and would have to wait days to recover.

The next morning I had to help DH warm his icy feet, which hasn't been a problem for a year or so. I followed my usual morning routine, and around noon tried my tensegrity brush again. Wow was it zingy!! I rubbed my arms and legs, and my skin felt like I had just stepped into a CT bath. I was so relieved! Later that day I started hiccuping, and found that yes, stimulating the skin will stop hiccuping. Neat!

# Thread: Bad news: caught a cold.  Good news: biohack!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-news-caught-a-cold-good-news-biohack.12580/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2015-01-11T21:04:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-news-caught-a-cold-good-news-biohack.12580/post-152423>

Wow, first cold in years! I blame the move. Once we were finally moved, the sun stayed behind clouds for 2 days. Throat, nose, ears, and lungs all involved. Once the sun re-appeared, I felt pretty good during the day. Then an hour or two before sunset, I would start feeling the cold temps. I would huddle around the fire, and when I went to the cold bed, I Did Not Shiver for my usual 5-10 minutes, though I still warmed up almost as well as usual. I never realized how much energy shivering takes.

A few hours before sunrise, all hell broke loose, thanks very much, Cortisol! But once the sun rose, the constant throat-clearing and coughing would stop and I'd start feeling good. After some sun bathing, I could garden, chop wood, even CT. But then in the afternoon I'd feel chilled, and the cycle repeated.

So how do I get more energy at night? Sun is gone, carbs and blue light are unacceptable. I'd been drinking water constantly already. Magnetico as always. IR light is great for chasing chills, but that's about it. I was using my tensegrity technique of firmly stroking my skin with the fingers to help me sleep, but my sleep was still disturbed often. I noticed that tensegrity seemed to help with the throat-clearing though. I was encouraged, and tried something a little stronger, using the backs of the fingernails. Woah! Very effective! See this post: [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/top-ten-tensegrity-tips.12579/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/top-ten-tensegrity-tips.12579/)

Twice I was able to stop coughing fits just with my fingernails. Amazing. So I am grateful for whatever bit me. Oh, I must confess, I ate a few slices of red bell pepper DH offered me. That gave me a scratchy throat right away, since they are out of season. Whatever bug was around saw the opportunity and grabbed it pronto. The scratchy throat never went away, and progressed into the illness.

I'm good now, but for the last couple nights I still only shiver a tiny bit when getting into that cold bed. I really miss it, because shivering puts me to sleep very quickly.

### Inger — 2015-01-11T22:31:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bad-news-caught-a-cold-good-news-biohack.12580/post-152432>

You might have caught the bugs from Nicole..... lol
Maybe the bell peppers were not organic and your body wanted to get rid of the poison right away?

I will try your finger trick

### Jude — 2015-01-11T22:57:20+0200

# Thread: Supplements
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements.12581/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Haley — 2015-01-11T21:26:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements.12581/post-152425>

I'm trying to determine what Jack's recommendations are as far as supplements go. After reading some blogs I notice there is a lot of significance in trying to not need supplements and getting it from food, environment, etc. Makes sense of course. And I realize it depends person to person. I am currently trying to do leptin rx and heal leaky gut. I've just finished a candida "cleanse"..not sure if or how that worked for me but this is the current supplement regimen i have been on:

high dose reservretrol, omega 3s, vitaminD3, high dose cucurmin (turmeric), kelp, magnesium complex, sacromycees bollard (until bottle runs out), rhodiola and then alternating herbal antibiotics (pau d' arco, oregano, olive leaf, grapefruit seed..now only taking a little each day of these).

Which of these are unnecessary & what can i do to increase them naturally (besides more fish and sunlight)?
What should I add in, if anything?

I would love to save some $ on supplements...and put it towards more seafood and such!

thanks!

### Inger — 2015-01-11T22:21:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements.12581/post-152429>

As I have been reading about MSM (sulfur. i am taking it right now for about 2 months and i love it...), i have learned it could help candida too... it could be worth a try? At least it is not expensive. A few people cannot tolerate it but I would try.. just start slowly and increase the amount.

IDK what more to say... except i have learned from Jack, if you live in high nnEMF you need to really be careful to supplement with vit D as it can backfire...... he talks about it in one of his more recent webinars (not free) from last year. You can get some vit D from fatty fish/seafood too, avocados have some... and UVB tanning beds (but this also needs the right context... ) The best is the sun, you really should tan all you can.... and better nude. That is very healing.
It might be okay to be a little low in vit D if it is winter time......

Have you tested your omega 3/6 ratio? Can you eat lots of fatty seafood minstead of taking the pills? I guess that would be way safer.....

### Haley — 2015-01-12T02:07:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements.12581/post-152460>

thanks inter! I've read great things about MSM as well but actually thought I may be in good shape because where I live in a mountain valley our tap water has a strong sulfur smell and that is what i bath in and often drink...?

I get outside at least 30 min-several hours a day even in the winter so I'm not too worried about vitamin D. The omegas I feel i will be getting from the seafood.

But kelp..maybe seaweed? magnesium?? and reversretrol? I believe that is only in wine in chocolate which i do no t have often. may actually have 2 glasses tonight....rare occasional...feel my mind may need a splurge. I've heard you can stay in ketosis and have a couple glasses. Sound right?

### Inger — 2015-01-12T09:14:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements.12581/post-152482>

Well.... if you have health issues I would not drink wine in the night....... especially if you have no great sleep. It sure hurts my sleep if i do (mostly) If you want to have wine, drink only 1 (!) glass, and before 5 PM, and it should be organic.... and a high resveratrol one like Argentinian Malbec. Alcohol also dehydrates you so if you have dehydration issues i would not drink any.......

I think wine is fine if in ketosis, but it will disturb the fat burning i have read...... at least for that evening. so if you want to lose weight I would not recommend drinking any alcohol - sure people might react different so you can try your own N=1  I sure do not do too well with alcohol... weirdly enough i have zero desire for it lately too....... I bought be a bottle of wine and i just do not want it... tried to drink a glass but my whole body and mind says no... so weird! I think some alcohol with good friends is also a different thing that when you drink it alone... seems i can tolerate it in a different way then...... that is very weird and i have no clue how it is but it must have something to do with quantum magic...lol
I think it really depend on the context.......

If you want chocolate for health issues, I recommend to use raw chocolate..... I can feel a difference between raw and heated cacao. I think the health benefits are way better from raw. And do 90% + cause you do not want the sugar in it!  especially not if you are in winter time..... You can buy raw organic cacao and mix it with coconut oil etc. = make your own chocolate

Seaweed is best eaten fresh but if you can get raw, dried seaweed you can soak it and drink the water and puree the rest into a smoothie and drink it too  I do that often and I love it! I use different kind of seaweeds because they have different health benefits. In my food i use Dulce mostly, it is so good added to raw salmon, cut into thin pieces. I do not soak that, it get nicely soft from the juice in my salmon chevice
I really recommend eating seaweed. You need to read the Epi paleo rx, seaweed has a big place in it.... tells about the benefits of different kind of them.. it is huge!

Yeah... omegas from food is way better . If you think you have too much omega 6 just quit eating nuts and olive oil etc. for a while and go heavy on the seafood  then it will naturally balance out  I eat so much fatty seafood so i eat omega 6 on purpose...lol I use raw nuts... and sometimes EVOO too. But not every day at all. Just once in a while. In winter i eat less from the omega 6. In fall i ate quite a bit of nuts...lol But that is when they naturally appear here too, so i guess that is just fine

Interesting about your water... are you sure it is sulfur? When, then it is really great! I guess if your skin is great and hair and nails it could mean you get enough of it! If not you could still try the MSM as the excess will just be peed out.....

### Haley — 2015-01-12T23:35:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements.12581/post-152597>

Inger, thank you for your wonderful replies I thoroughly enjoy and appreciate them.

I went ahead and drank almost an entire of bottle of wine last night...and even though I had a million reasons not to...such a beautifully rare occasion presented itself with the right people and it was worth it! Im in a season of learning optimal health through ALL aspects, but, with a focus on the mind

I am a bloated today but it seemed to especially strike after i ordered a "seaweed salad" from a local Japanese restaurant.. pretty sure it had some sugar in it. The seaweed itself was sooo good though! I went to our version of whole foods and looked at the seaweed dept in complete confusion. I would love to find the moist stringy kind like was in that salad! Any ideas what its called or where to buy it? is it possibly the dried stuff-rehydrated?

I don't eat nuts or really any other form of omega 6's...so I may even need to watch the omega 3 intake. My diet now solely consists of: seafood, coconut oil, coconut butter, veggies, grass fed beef bone broth, and occasional other meats like lamb,beef,chicken, turkey.
How does this sound? Anything I may be missing?

Im not positive about the sulfur in the water..it was more of an assumption after smelling it. Because the natural hot springs around here always have sulfur so i figured it may be in my water since i smelled the similar smell. But its possible its not..my nails and hair (especially) are not in the shape i would prefer them to be in...but i figured that was my thyroid.

# Thread: Need to raise FT3 and FT4, also lower TSH
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-raise-ft3-and-ft4-also-lower-tsh.12582/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### david weiss — 2015-01-11T22:06:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-raise-ft3-and-ft4-also-lower-tsh.12582/post-152427>

My last thyroid panel was as follows:
FT4: 0.792, ref range 0.90 to 1.90
FT3: 1.920, ref range 2.0 to 4.40
TSH: 4.00, ref range 0.270 to 4.20
Reverse T3: 14, ref range 8 t0 25
Negative for antibodies

My question is can I improve these numbers without prescription medicine. My doc started me on Synthyroid, 25mg, and it made me really nauseous, so I stopped. I only took it for 2 days.

Other info: My ASI test is normal, but I have microcytic anemia and my gut is terrible. I just completed Cyrex Assays 3 and 3, as well as the Genova complete stool analysis. With this information I can start working on the gut.

I started the LR 3 months ago, CT 2 months ago, and I am eating a very strict Epi-Paleo diet.

My body temperature averages around 97.3, and I am cold all the time. (Makes CT very challenging.)

Thanks for all replies.

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-01-12T07:06:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-raise-ft3-and-ft4-also-lower-tsh.12582/post-152472>

T4 is converted to T3 in the gut (20%) and in the liver (80%) showing that thyroid works to help protect our portal circulation from gut inflammation.
The increased secretion of TSH from the pituitary will affect the liver. It causes the liver to make hsCRP...here is the tie to leptin resistance.
If your gut is no good, the conversion of T4 to T3 will be impacted. hsCRP will be going up and creating leptin resistance at the hypothalamus, and probably locally at the liver...
a VAP lipid panel would no doubt show elevated sdLDL and decreased HDL, hsCRP would be valuable too...

### david weiss — 2015-01-12T16:35:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-raise-ft3-and-ft4-also-lower-tsh.12582/post-152529>

Thanks for the reply and clear explanation. I have not checked my hsCRP, but my lipid panel shows an elevated HDL and a low sdLDL.

Still, given your explanation, what should be my course of action? Heal the gut and the thyroid hormones will come up?

One other thing I don't understand, having a bad gut would explain lowered T3. But why is T4 also low?

### kovita — 2015-01-12T17:02:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-raise-ft3-and-ft4-also-lower-tsh.12582/post-152534>

David, just stick to epipaleo and other lkfestyle changes you learn on this side. Avoid nnEMF as a poison and load yourself with patience. Spend as much time in the nature as you can and accept and follow it's rhytm and cseasonal changes. Work on your "inner peace" and use your thoughts to help your body to get optimal. This is the best recipe I have discovered so far and it seems to be working for just anyone irrespectively of the individual health situation.

### david weiss — 2015-01-12T20:34:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-raise-ft3-and-ft4-also-lower-tsh.12582/post-152572>

Kovita,

I appreciate your thoughts. Patience is hard, especially since I have been dealing with these issues for many years without seeing any change. I only discovered JK a few months ago, but it has given me renewed hope. For now though, I need to make a decision on taking Synthroid. My doc, who is reluctant to use pharmaceuticals, believes it would be best. Obviously I would rather hear through natural methods. I still would like to hear if anyone on the list has been able to get over non Hashimotos hypothyroid without taking prescription medication. Anyone out there?

### Marie — 2015-01-12T20:49:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-raise-ft3-and-ft4-also-lower-tsh.12582/post-152573>

Try desicated thyroid. **WP Thyroid** by RLC Labs comes in 1/4, 1/2, 3/4, 1, 1 1/2, and 2 grain doses and is CHEAP.

[http://www.pdr.net/full-prescribing-information/wp-thyroid?druglabelid=3202](http://www.pdr.net/full-prescribing-information/wp-thyroid?druglabelid=3202)

### kovita — 2015-01-12T21:42:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-raise-ft3-and-ft4-also-lower-tsh.12582/post-152582>

> [david weiss said:](/goto/post?id=152572)
>
> Kovita,
>
> I appreciate your thoughts. Patience is hard, especially since I have been dealing with these issues for many years without seeing any change. I only discovered JK a few months ago, but it has given me renewed hope. For now though, I need to make a decision on taking Synthroid. My doc, who is reluctant to use pharmaceuticals, believes it would be best. Obviously I would rather hear through natural methods. I still would like to hear if anyone on the list has been able to get over non Hashimotos hypothyroid without taking prescription medication. Anyone out there?

David, the victories tend to come invisibly slowly irrespectively of our best wishes for instant victories. I know myslef how frustrating a long chronic disease/health issues can be. One gets tired and wants to die or disappear. Patience was never between my virtues, I had to learn to be patient and it surprised me by all I got back! I have no experience with hypothyroidism myself, but sometimes you have to start with drugs and wean ypurself off as you recover your natural ability to maintain your body in it's optimal status.

# Thread: Bone broth makes me feel like rat shit

### Ben1983 — 2015-01-12T09:50:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-me-feel-like-rat-shit.12587/post-152490>

Does anyone have any ideas as to why bone broth makes me feel irritable and slightly agitated after I drink it? I use bones from grass fed and finished cattle, reverse osmosis water, lemon or apple cider vinegar and I cook on low in a slow cooker for 24 hours. I've also cooked it for 30 hours and 48 hours on other occasions, but it doesn't seem to make any difference to how I feel after having it.

### Inger — 2015-01-12T10:40:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-me-feel-like-rat-shit.12587/post-152494>

I have read somewhere about it... it was a reason for it.. but i cannot remind me of what it was! I just know i have had that too.... sometimes... long ago.

You could try a fish bone smoothie to get the benefits from a bone broth? I have used herring bones leftovers from filleting herring. I just run them in my mixer with water and it turns out very smooth. I think that is a even healthier way to get the minerals in

### Ben1983 — 2015-01-12T12:35:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-me-feel-like-rat-shit.12587/post-152503>

Thanks, Inger. I might try that....come to think of it, I do eat sardine bones regularly....but they are tinned sardines so not sure how much goodness left in the bones...

### kovita — 2015-01-12T12:42:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-me-feel-like-rat-shit.12587/post-152504>

Ben, what could come to my mind is glutamin and glycin, because bone broth is very rich source specially if you use joints to prepare it. Some people do get too excited through potential glutamin excitotoxicity. You can easily test whether this is the culprit by trying pure collagen instead of the bone broth and experiencing the same issues.

### kovita — 2015-01-12T12:43:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-me-feel-like-rat-shit.12587/post-152505>

other way to test might be to get GABA after you consume it and see whether the symptoms go away...

### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-12T12:48:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-me-feel-like-rat-shit.12587/post-152506>

Anything else in the broth? There could be excess carbs in there, but I doubt the does is enough to be an issue.

Personal experience was that bone broth could lead to lots of bloating for myself. I initially though it may be cysteine related, but then realised that collagen contains very little cysteine. The viscosity of the broth could also affect gastric emptying rate, and could affect the absorption of nutrients from other foods.

Then some people may be sensitive to the histamines, to which a natural anti-histamine may help (like Quercetin from onions).

Also agreed with @[kovita](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9805/) that glutamine could be an issue, but that also likely signals an issue with a leaky blood-brain-barrier (which is the only way GABA supplementation would work).

In any case, it probably has to do with gut flora modifications and resultant downstream neurotransmitter effects. (still waiting for @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) to give the gut flora blog talking about light emittence of these critters and how we use that light)

Why specifically do you want to drink bone broth though? Given your current context, you may try to go without it for awhile.

If the goal is to heal a leaky gut, maybe try some "animal fibre" -- See [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...ntial-through-hydride-ions.10343/#post-118768](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/do-raw-foods-improve-redox-potential-through-hydride-ions.10343/#post-118768) and [http://caloriesproper.com/animal-fibre/](http://caloriesproper.com/animal-fibre/)

I haven't drunk bone broth in ages though, and definitely not since I've made my move to Cairns (where all my gut issues disappeared).

### kovita — 2015-01-12T13:04:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-me-feel-like-rat-shit.12587/post-152507>

thanks for the links, Yew! The animal fiber is interesting. I found myself chewing whole shrimps and even biting crab shells and any tendons/cartilages I found in my plate lately. It is just like I cannot leave it there, my kids are passing me all the skin from the fish and than have a great fun since this is the first think I eat. I lobe bone broth and it does me really good. I have just seen the latest intestine health tests results and must say my gut microflora is in better shape than ever before now, but cannot discount the pregnancy has something to do with....

### nicld — 2015-01-12T16:23:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-makes-me-feel-like-rat-shit.12587/post-152523>

I just listened to Ben Greenfield’s pod cast on Kombucha and the guest speaker he had on said that some people react to Komucha the same way you are reacting to the bone broth because the bad bacteria are putting up a fight once the good ones are trying to move in.

Just a thought…….

# Thread: salivary cortisol test - been awake 4 hours forgot to do it - too late today?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-cortisol-test-been-awake-4-hours-forgot-to-do-it-too-late-today.12594/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-12T16:18:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-cortisol-test-been-awake-4-hours-forgot-to-do-it-too-late-today.12594/post-152521>

\*sigh\*

I have a salivary cortisol test here from my doctor. One swab only. They said do it whenever and put the date and time on it and drop it off and they'll test it for me.

I wanted to do it first thing in the morning since I thought that would be most accurate. But I forgot and have been up for like 4 hours and drinking coffee the whole time.

Should I just wait until tomorrow to do it even though the results won't be back before my appointment on Friday if I do, or should I do it now? Does it even matter?

Thanks!

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-12T16:37:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-cortisol-test-been-awake-4-hours-forgot-to-do-it-too-late-today.12594/post-152530>

Oh dear, this is disheartening. I was not given instructions as to specific time frame, though when I asked the lab tech said she'd try to do it in the morning rather than evening, personally.

But now searching online for the past fifteen minutes found this:

"[http://www.labrix.com/SalivaCollection](http://www.labrix.com/SalivaCollection) - **The timing of the first morning collection is critical; it must be collected thrity \[sic\] minutes after awakening. Begin timing upon awakening even if you remain in bed.** "

Alright well I'll leave this thread here in case anyone else ever has this issue, at least this will turn up in search results.

They also didn't tell me to do anything else but rinse my mouth with water before I do the test and don't swallow the water (she stressed this very strongly).

So, here are the full set of specifications from the link above which I should've been told but wasn't -

-   Wash hands and rinse mouth with cool water before collecting saliva.

-   Wash face before bedtime the night before collecting saliva, and do not apply lotions or creams that night or next morning (some may contain interfering substances). Use of routine cosmetics is permitted.

-   Do not brush, floss teeth or have dental work done 30 minutes prior to collecting saliva. Slight bleeding from these may alter test results.

-   The timing of the first morning collection is critical; it must be collected thrity minutes after awakening. Begin timing upon awakening even if you remain in bed.

-   Avoid food one hour prior to saliva collection times. Food particles will contaminate the sample.

# Thread: urinalysis results: trace amounts of blood& ketones.. why?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/urinalysis-results-trace-amounts-of-blood-ketones-why.12608/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-14T14:10:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/urinalysis-results-trace-amounts-of-blood-ketones-why.12608/post-152729>

Hi everyone!
I just got a bunch of blood work back from my doctor this morning when I woke up - they were there in my online health portal but I haven't had a chance to go over them with him yet, and won't for some days because he's on vacation.

That being the case I thought I'd post at least some of it here to get some input.. and the thing that I am most curious about is the urinalysis results which stated "Abnormal Final Result" due to the presence of "Trace" amounts of ketones and blood in my urine.

When I hear "ketones in the urine" it makes me think of diabetes -- I am not diabetic but my mother was and my oldest brother is. I was tested for diabetes this summer and my glucose was at the top of the normal range. But normal.

When I hear "blood in the urine" it makes me think of a urinary tract infection -- I do not have one. I had one years ago and can't imagine that I have one now considering the pain I was in then, and the lack of it now. Plus, nitrites was negative.. not that you can always tell a UTI by that but still...

I also do not have and have never had issues with kidney stones. My liver and kidney bloodwork (Complete Metabolic Panel) was last done November 26 and it was all good. My urine Ph was normal at 6.5, with a standard range given as 5.0 to 8.0 (I understand Ph plays into formation of kidney stones but again I don't think that's the case here based on what I'm seeing, but I'm no doctor).

(In case it matters I am almost 42, not on any hormones at all yet, supplement with a woman's centrum daily multivitamin that I did not take prior to the test, and vitamin d3 that I did not take prior to the test. I was only on the d3 for two days before doing the bloodwork so can't imagine it would affect anything anyhow. I also take two fish oil pills per day but didn't take them that day either, and had only been on them for about a week prior to testing.)

I was wondering if anyone has any thoughts about this? Thanks!

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-14T14:23:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/urinalysis-results-trace-amounts-of-blood-ketones-why.12608/post-152730>

Here are the urinalysis results in their entirety:

Sure, NOW I'm starting to get less carbs because of switching out toast for beef in the morning, but that was not the case when this test was done. 2\. Urine - Beta-HCG: **negative**

3\. Vitamin D25 Hydroxy: (STANDARD RANGE: 32.0 - 100.00 ng/mL) - MINE: **38.9**
INTERPRETATION: "Sufficiency" (under 32 is "Insufficiency" and over 100 is "Potential toxicity")

4\. Cardio CRP: (STANDARD RANGE: 0.00 - 1.00 mg/L) - MINE: **0.85**

6\. T3, Total: (STANDARD RANGE: 80 - 200 ng/dL) - MINE: **124**

7\. Free T4: (STANDARD RANGE: 0.8 - 2.2 ng/dL) - MINE: **1.0**

8\. Lipid Profile - fasting (note that it's gotten worse and worse lately.. ugh):

9\. Cortisol \[ blood was taken after fasting for 12 hours, about 5 hours after waking. I get up at 5:30am, they took this blood at 10:25am.\] : (STANDARD RANGE: 4.5 - 22.7 ug/dL) - MINE: **8.1
**
_**NOTE -** FOR THE NEXT FEW TESTS YOU PROBABLY NEED TO KNOW MY CYCLE DAY ON TESTING ... THAT"S TRICKY. I have about a 28 day cycle pretty darn regularly, so if I'm due about the 18th (give or take a couple of days) then t**he 9th was probably around day 19** or something, which I \*think\* is the "Luteal phase" but I don't know much about this._

10\. FSH: (STANDARD RANGE: 1.4 - 18.1 mIU/mL) - MINE: **6.7**

11\. LH: (STANDARD RANGE: 1.30 - 8.00 mIU/mL) - MINE: **10.00

LH INTERPRETATION -
PHASE RANGE (mIU/mL):
**
LH FOLLICULAR PHASE: 0.6 - 12.1 (Begins on cycle day 1 with first full day of menstruation)
LH MID-CYCLE PHASE: 27.3 - 96.1
LH LUTEAL PHASE: 0.8 - 15.5
LH POST-MENOPAUSAL: 13.1 - 86.5

12\. Estradiol: (see interpretation below) - MINE: **140**

ESTRADIOL - PHASE RANGE (pg/mL):
FOLLICULAR: 19 - 83
MID-CYCLE: 150 - 528
LUTEAL: 58 - 157
POST-MENOPAUSAL: 0 - 31

\--------------
UPDATED:

13\. Thyroid Peroxidase Antibodies: (STANDARD RANGE: < 9 IU/mL) - **MINE: 74**
**(NOTE: Jack said on my thread about this "Its Hashi's"... so there's one answer!)**

14\. Thyroglobulin Antibodies: (STANDARD RANGE: <or = 1 IU/mL) - MINE: **<1**

15\. 17-Hydroxyprogesterone: (NO RANGE GIVEN BUT NOT FLAGGED) - MINE: **202**

16\. Serum Allergy RAST testing: not allergic to pollens, grasses, mites, or danders but **serum IgE was flagged as High at 142** (STANDARD RANGE: <115 KU/L)
_\*\* The point of this test was to be tested for food allergies, specifically seafood, but my GP neglected to note that fact on the script. oops. At least now I know there is a true food allergy there.. just, still don't know what it is!\*\*_

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-21T13:11:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-current-test-results-for-anyone-wanting-to-comment.12609/post-153526>

Serum DHEA testing came back this morning -
325 ug/mL

this puts me out of range on the HIGH end which is opposite of what I expected.

Problem is, I don't know if that's only because I took two 12.5 mg doses of DHEA in pill form about 3-5 days prior to the blood test. OR if it's truly high. (The standard range given by Quest Diagnostics, who my hospital/clinic uses and insurance will pay for, is "102 - 1185 NG/DL" without any specifics as to gender or age.)

How can I know? Anyone have an idea? Thanks as always!

\-----------------
[http://www.nlm.nih.gov/medlineplus/ency/article/003717.htm](http://www.nlm.nih.gov/medlineplus/ency/article/003717.htm) :

Typical normal ranges for females are:
Ages 18 - 19: 145 - 395 micrograms per deciliter (ug/dL)
Ages 20 - 29: 65 - 380 ug/dL <<<<<<< Mine was 325 when tested. I took two 12.5 doses of over the counter DHEA within 3-6 days prior to the blood test and got cystic acne immediately and stopped again. I just turned 42, BTW.
Ages 30 - 39: 45 - 270 ug/dL
Ages 40 - 49: 32 - 240 ug/dL
Ages 50 - 59: 26 - 200 ug/dL
Ages 60 - 69: 13 - 130 ug/dL
Ages 69 and older: 17 - 90 ug/dL

Not bad in overall. You ahve to change your diet, too much carbs...wrong lipid panel. Epipaleo is the answer. The thyroid you know already. The progesterone would be of better value with range and I would target lower estradiol (even your value is in range). Cortisol (in my opinion) only makes sense of you do time tracking - ASI. Higher DHEAs is better then low DHEAs, it is always good to see morning and evening values (most hormones have time specific release and profiles). Not sure how it was affected by the supps because I never supplemented DHEAs myself. Remember what i told you about IgE.? ;-) Look up boswelia. As said here before the most important thing is to start to change your life and stick to it. The things will slowly get better.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-22T14:14:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-current-test-results-for-anyone-wanting-to-comment.12609/post-153588>

Thanks ladies!

I finally had my consult with the compounding pharmacist who I was referred to for BHRT. I put a detailed account of the encounter in my blog. Bottom line is, the guy told me to switch to his diet he invented seven years ago, it's called Paleo, and it's all the rage. (I call BS.)

He also said forget DHA, it's not important and stop focusing on it and instead focus on Adrenal Fatigue. He said if I get that under control then my Hashi's will be CURED. Yes, CURED. Especially if he gives me iodine drops for both ingesting and rubbing on my neck directly over my thyroid.

He suggested I go on his special diet for six months and also buy his supplement regime that will cost me $600 for a six month supply, but which he declined to explain what was in them. I suppose if I decide to buy them I will be able to read the labels but this still is quite irritating to me, all things considered.

I asked him within three minutes of meeting him, if he knew who Jack Kruse was, because it sounds like he's regurgitating whatever he's memorized from Jack's books and website, right down to his feelings about the USDA and grains, choleserol intake, and my HPA axis being messed up.

On the one hand that makes him sound like he knows what he's talking about. On the other hand, I'm a skeptic by nature and my alarm bells are ringing about this guy. First good, then bad, and now I can't decide what to think. (Let me just point out that he also said that 90% of people who call him for help, he 'diagnoses' with Adrenal Fatigue, and says that he suggests his special diet and supplement plan to all of them as the solution to this diagnosis. To me this sounds like the biggest alarm bunch of cr@p ever, like he's nothing but a salesman of his own "protocol" which is actually not even his own but mostly Jack's!)

Bottom line is, for the purposes of BHRT he is suggesting lozenges that he will formulate from "real, not synthetic" progesterone and then cut in half. He suggests I take one per day on day 14 and 15 of my cycle.

That's it, or maybe it was two per day. He said anyone who comes to him who has ever been on birth control/synthetic progesterone, he goes straight for the lozenge because the cream isn't appropriate for those people.

I feel like I just wasted a month waiting for this appointment. He also told me all my labs are worthless, even the Hashi's antibodies because he said that any doctor who treats my particular Hashi's with any typical thyroid med would be in error, because he says those just have to do with T4 and he said my issue is T3. He said do reverse T3 and that's worthwhile but everything else I had done was a waste.

\*sigh\*

I guess all I want to know now is, what do you all think about this progesterone lozenge 2 x monthly, is that really going to solve my sex drive issue? Really?

All I want is BHRT. That is all I ever wanted. I realize ther's a lot more to it and assume by default I'm going to do all that stuff. And then focus on this dose of this medicine please, and tell me if he's being overly simplistic..?

I don't want to order progesterone online and self-dose but at this point I'm going to do that before I take his advice I think, unless people here think he's got some legit views. I think the reason he said the cream isn't appropriate for ANYONE who has EVER been on the pill or depo provera, etc, is just so that he can get people to buy the lozenges from him and he still gets a buck out of it. But again.. maybe that's paranoia.

He did give me a reference - he said some Dr. Walsh guy who is big in adrenal fatigue had pulled him aside to ask him for advice several years ago because Dr. Walsh wanted to know what this guy was doing to help his 6000 patients so far, because Dr. Walsh was so impressed with his work.

I was hoping maybe Jack heard of this guy since he said he goes to all kinds of conferences about 'this stuff" and gives talks on BHRT and AF. But I don't want to put his name out here in the forum. Well, that's it. Input is welcome as always. Thanks.

Edited to add: Apparently lozenge is "troche" in French. I saw the word "troche" on this forum awhile back and had no idea what it was so, for anyone who is confused, that's what we're talking about here.

### nonchalant — 2015-01-22T17:12:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-current-test-results-for-anyone-wanting-to-comment.12609/post-153605>

I think your lipid panel is gradually getting better. Your HDL is getting higher. Total cholesterol is getting better, closer to 200.
My husband's DHEA was low, so he took a supplement for a while. Then it went sky high. So he gradually cut back to once/week, and finally stopped. I think your DHEA result is because of your supplement, not your own production. Remember DHEA is a hormone the body creates from LDL cholesterol, with the help of vitamin A and thyroid hormone. With your hashi's, you probably aren't converting enough LDL to the various steroid hormones.

Do you get morning sunshine?

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-22T20:12:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-current-test-results-for-anyone-wanting-to-comment.12609/post-153615>

Thank you for you reply! That makes a lot of sense.. I figured the DHEA had to be from the pill because it makes no sense for it to be high otherwise. I agree about the LDL issue.. it's higher this time than ever before which seems consistant with Hashi's.

I get morning sunshine whenever there is any to be gotten, but there is a window between me and the sun for now due to temperatures where I am.

### PaulG — 2015-01-15T02:28:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-tuszynski-speaks-on-quantum-biology.12615/post-152783>

Long videos I know...but I for one need to better understand Quantum Mechanics...

I'm also reading the excellent biography of Paul Dirac called [The Strangest Man](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Strangest_Man)

> **Published on Mar 1, 2013**
> Table of Contents:
> [00:01:09](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AphjKRPk_Bg#) Basics of Quantum Mechanics
> [00:05:04](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AphjKRPk_Bg#) Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle
> [00:07:05](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AphjKRPk_Bg#) Max Plank was 42 at the time of fathering Quantum Mechanics
> [00:10:06](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AphjKRPk_Bg#) Photoelectric Effect

# Thread: New Wifi Device & how dangerous wifi can be
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-wifi-device-how-dangerous-wifi-can-be.12617/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BTA — 2015-01-15T03:24:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-wifi-device-how-dangerous-wifi-can-be.12617/post-152788>

**‘Smart’ Pacifier Tracks Tots and Takes Temperature, While Bathing them in Radiation**
**Is it a good idea to shove radiation-emitting devices in our children's mouths?**
[](http://www.infowars.com/smart-pacifier-tracks-tots-and-takes-temperature-while-bathing-them-in-radiation/)
Image Credits: [donnieray, Flickr](http://www.flickr.com/photos/donnieray/14535761241)
**Like babies, but hate the hassle of having to track their every move? Even worse is misplacing that pesky pacifier. And why’s it always like pulling teeth taking your kid’s temperature?**
For years, parents have frustratingly searched for answers to these questions. Luckily, the wait is over.
Blue Maestro, a British health technology company, has dumbed-down parents covered.
Introducing the _Pacif-i_, a high-tech pacifier that syncs with your smart phone to make caring where your baby is a thing of the past.
“ay your kid is the type to make a break for it,” poses [Yahoo! Tech](https://www.yahoo.com/tech/this-smart-pacifier-connects-to-your-smartphone-107295902644.html) as an example. “Anytime the Pacif-i moves more than 20 feet from your connected device, an alarm will go off.”
The _Pacif-i_ comes fully loaded with technology that sends your phone updates on your baby’s temperature, along with a built-in GPS locater to ensure satellites orbiting the Earth know exactly where your kid is at all times.
~[](http://hw.infowars.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/01/paci-1.jpg)~
Image via BlueMaestro.com
The portable sucking device also “emits a low-energy Bluetooth signal to your phone every two minutes,” reports Yahoo, meaning it’ll automatically douse your baby in microwave radiation every few seconds.
“Officially,” radiation emitted from wireless technologies, such as Bluetooth devices and cell phones, have been dismissed as having little to no adverse effects on human physiology.
However, a whole slew of studies also conversely show radio wave frequencies, the same ones put out by Bluetooth devices, to have a detrimental effect on humans down to the cellular level, even at low doses.
A 2014 [study](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0160412014001354) looking at the effects of radio-frequency electromagnetic radiation on sperm quality found that, even in low amounts, “mobile phone exposure was associated with reduced sperm motility and viability.”
Indeed, the health effects of wireless radiation have been known since cell phones began coming into fashion.
The Sunday Mirror in 2001 published an article entitled “[The Child Scrambler: What a Mobile Can Do To a Youngster’s Brain in 2 Min](https://web.archive.org/web/20011215122648/http://www.sundaymirror.co.uk/shtml/NEWS/P15S3.shtml),” which detailed the results of a Spanish study researching mobile phone effects on the minds of children.
“Scientists have discovered that a call lasting just two minutes can alter the natural electrical activity of a child’s brain for up to an hour afterwards,” reported the Mirror.
One government doctor called the study’s results “extremely disturbing.”
“It makes one wonder whether children, whose brains are still developing, should be using mobile phones,” British physicist Dr. Gerald Hyland told The Mirror.
“The results show that children’s brains are affected for long periods even after very short-term use.”
“Their brain wave patterns are abnormal and stay like that for a long period.”
“This could affect their mood and ability to learn in the classroom if they have been using a phone during break time, for instance.”
“We don’t know all the answers yet, but the alteration in brain waves could lead to things like a lack of concentration, memory loss, inability to learn and aggressive behaviour.”
In 2013, researchers with [BioInitiative 2012 brought the issue before the Federal Communications Commission](http://apps.fcc.gov/ecfs/document/view?id=7520940054) (FCC), along with a preponderance of evidence, proposing “Changes to the Commission’s Rules Regarding Human Exposure to Radiofrequency Electromagnetic Fields.”
[Their petition](http://apps.fcc.gov/ecfs/document/view?id=7520940054) was accompanied by a [detailed chart showing a long list of studies](http://www.bioinitiative.org/report/wp-content/uploads/pdfs/BioInitiativeReport-RF-Color-Charts.pdf) demonstrating the “Reported Biological Effects from Radiofrequency Radiation at Low-Intensity Exposure.”
The following video also illustrates how Bluetooth wi-fi headsets emit dangerous radio frequency levels even when they’re not in use.

### caroline — 2015-01-15T23:52:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/down-under-muscles.12621/post-152917>

I just noticed that part of my original post is missing  here is the rest of it......

These "Down under muscles" are un recognised, multi tasking achievers, thanklessly coordinating various tasks every day of our life.
We all know they help maintain continence and support pelvic organs etc. ...but I never thought about them protecting our spine and contributing to sexual sensation \[hence my new found interest!\]
I am shocked by how many women have horrible issues with pain etc. and Docs know very little...why does that surprise me??? Women are often told it is all in their head ...that shouldn't be a surprise either.

I almost always hold my waist in \[ to appear smaller\] not unusual apparently! but that is working the wrong muscle group ...and not letting the pelvic muscles do their job.

How are your muscles "down under"???

Are you able to recognise the correct muscles???

[http://www.katysays.com/kegel-queen/](http://www.katysays.com/kegel-queen/)

"_**PFD is a sign that your entire body is collapsing, from the inside-down.**_ While the sneeze-pee and organ prolapse may seem like a huge deal it is nothing compared to the neurological damage to the spine, nerves, and tissues that those nerves supply (especially the nerves running to the lower legs.) The kegel _is a drop in the bucket_ to what someone with PFD needs to do to heal all their structures. PFD is _a whole-body situation_ and for optimal, whole-body outcome, the body has to be treated holistically. Otherwise it’s like putting a new roof on a termite-ridden frame."

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-01-19T10:52:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/down-under-muscles.12621/post-153313>

**1\. 3 squat types with the alignments desired.**
i'll have jaime take some of me tonight and i will endeavor to upload ASAP**

2\. Examples of insufficiency in each region.**
insufficiencies in any region show up as grooving of the tissues. this may be in the form of a "six-pack," a puckering of the upper abdominal wall and oblique region, etc etc
any groove is a tightness, any tightness is a lack of adequate stability from the deep stabilizing system. i have used the picture of the cheetah on here before.
**
3\. Examples of how to develop intra-abdominal pressure (do you agree with something like this:**
the model you posted is close from my quick skimming, however the stabilizing function of diaphragm is different to the breathing function, even though they are coupled. the diaphragm must be horizontal to the upright, and a mirror image of the pelvic floor, they are both in a horizontal orientation.
the stabilizing function of IAP can be likened to the strain one undertakes when constipated. it is a concentric flattening of the diaphragm toward pelvis, like a coffee plunger. the ability to hold this push out against the abdominal wall whilst exhaling is paramount.

[http://www.markmcgrath.com.au/2014/...-and-training-that-is-intrinsic-to-all-of-us/](http://www.markmcgrath.com.au/2014/10/dns-a-perspective-on-a-new-paradigm-for-movement-exercise-and-training-that-is-intrinsic-to-all-of-us/)

**Also some questions:
\- Could you clarify the use of the stretch reflex at bottom of squat?**
This is to do with using the squat movement in motion in a continuous jump. the decelleration forces are turned into acceleration forces via stretch reflexes and sophisticated neural and fascial tensegrity system firing

**\- Does loading the front vs back squat alter the alignments?**
yes, as mark describes, loading at the back can result in a "looking up" type posture which accentuates the cervical and lumbar lordoses, and peels the diaphragm away from the pelvic floor as in the diagram at the bottom of the blog. this shortens the spine and results in tonic recruitment of phasic muscles

**\- Does stance width influence the breaking at knees vs. hips?**
the stance width can be variable but the constant must be the prerequisite stabilization of the spine from deep system

### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-21T00:59:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/down-under-muscles.12621/post-153472>

Completely Random thoughts ahead. Probably interesting to @[Josh (Paleo Osteo)](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9815/) and @[ssj3](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9728/) , and hopefully others

I haven't been following this thread, but the mention of intra-abdominal pressure (IAP) led me to think of one of the "contrarian opinions" which I delved into awhile ago.

The original motivation was to allow for safe rounded-back deadlifts. NOTE: I DO NOT endorse lifting heavy nonsense like the lifters above do, I believe in the ability to perform movements similar to a rounded-back deadlift, but not loaded to the extent as these lifters do, and not with a barbell. More on this below.

This is not just the thoracic flexion that we see lifters like Konstantin Konstantinovs employ:

We're talking completely flexed everything (lumbar including), like how a lifter like Jouko Ahola does:

The original inspiration came from Bob Peoples, who deadlifted something like 700+lbs at a 180lbs bodyweight. Matt Perryman (great dude!) gave the training protocol of Peoples a good overview here -- [http://www.myosynthesis.com/training-methods-bob-peoples](http://www.myosynthesis.com/training-methods-bob-peoples)

A quote from Peoples:

However I than started lifting on empty lungs and with at round back -- that is I would breath out to normal then do my dead lift. I feel this is much safer than following the customary advice of the experts to take a deep breath and than dead lift.

Breathing out you lessen the internal pressure and by lifting with a round back you lessen the leverage -- all of which helps add many lbs. to your lift. I realize this style may not work well with everyone but in my case it seems ideal.
​

\----

To me, when it comes to injury prevention and stable, efficient movement, what we are really looking for is **stability between vertebral discs**.

I have almost all of Stuart McGill's books, but I disagree that a neutral spine is of extreme and utmost importance. I thought to myself, "surely nature isn't so stupid as to make us unable to perform strenuous movement in a flexed lumbar position".

I have personally experimented with round-back deadlifting, and find that eventually you can learn to use your lats to stabilise against any frontal plane shear force, and actually keep a stable back without much IAP. This is similar to how strongmen lift Atlas stones.

However, lifting the Atlas stones bring up a different set of factors .... your hands get to feel the roughness of the stone, and it is pressed against your body, which can also feel the roughness of the stone. Both the added proprioception and physical support of the stone make round-backed lifting a lot safer, by allowing the body to activate a whole bunch of stabilising activity thanks to the added stimulus. This obviously scales to the texture of a tree branch, the texture of the gravel that you're stepping on, etc ....

With that said, Katy Bowman brought up an interesting point in her recent podcast with Joe Rogan -- [http://podcasts.joerogan.net/podcasts/katy-bowman](http://podcasts.joerogan.net/podcasts/katy-bowman) , in that a lot of your musculature and bone structure is influenced by how you moved from ages 0 to 5, and then to a lesser extent from 5 to 15. A lot of us screwed up that portion of our lives, creating muscular, fascial, and bone structures that are suboptimal for efficient movement.

That's a long way of saying that although a "normal" human should be able to perform tasks in so-called risky positions, many people today can't do that safely, and need a transition plan.

\----

For developing core stability, I like some of the exercises that Dean Somerset prescribes.

Most people will be able to start with something like a Bear Crawl, which will be difficult when starting out, and will also make apparent stability deficiencies pretty quick:

I like doing those at varying levels of back flexion. I personally also find 'Rolling Thunder' to be a fun thing to do on the beach:

I like the Butterfly Hip flow -- [http://deansomerset.com/best-exercise-ever-butterfly-hip-flow-series/](http://deansomerset.com/best-exercise-ever-butterfly-hip-flow-series/)

I actually like zercher squats

I can't wait for June when I go and visit Jeremy Thomley and get some of his movement wisdom (yoga + rock climbing )

\----

TL;DR -- For good core stability, hang from tree branches, walk on rocks, and crawl around a lot

\----

ok
this is the one area on this forum i can categorically and confidently say certain things are plain bullshit, as it is my area of expertise

you are correct in saying that stability of intervertebral discs is paramount in any movement, be it walking, running, lifting etc.
when we go to development, we get the only true source of stability, as it is inherent in the CNS and not taught.
anything is is somebodys opinion.
phylogenically and developmentally speaking we must be stabilised in the saggital plane before the cortex divides the body in half lengthways to organise ipsilateral stability, and eventually divide the body into a cross to organise contralateral sophistication needed for bipedal motion.
this stability in the saggital plane is organised and imprinted at 3-4 months of age in supine and prone positions in a healthy maturing CNS.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

When we sre stabilised in this frame of reference, the limbs know themselves via their reference poingt of punctuim fixum
this frees the limbs for movement freedom and liberates them from performing any kind of stability

if saggital stability of the lumbar spine is lost from any decrease in IAP or inability to meet the pelvic floor with pressure directed from diaphragmatic excursion, the hips lose their orientation ad we start to access our discs (size of a slice of kiwi fruit) and zygapophyseal joints (size of your little fingernail)
obviously not a good idea when moving loads.
the hip is about the size of a small fist and is the most congruent ball and socket joint in the system, further encapsulated by the labrum and extremely thick ligamentous complex (ever hear of someone spraining a hip?)
you are right in saying that it is indeed possible to move loads with a flexed spine, however this is simply not the inherent imprinted movement which is lost at age 5 due to sitting in chairs rather than on the floor as displayed cross culturally and during development.
tasks can and continue to be performed in all manner of risky positions that are biopmechanically un-sound (much to the delight of my bank account) the question remains is it optimal, and is it requiring inefficient expensive skeletal muscles to perform a stabilizing task?

the answers are no and yes respectively.

i have spoken to jeremy and we hope to hold a movement workshop on the cruise if he is able to attend.

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-01-21T02:03:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/down-under-muscles.12621/post-153479>

in the bear crawl video he has a complete loss of lumbar stability and length when transitioning
this IS NOT a good position to develop "core" strength, as it shows a complete lack of it to begin with
this may train one to employ a stabilizing strategy that is less that optimal (read: concentric activation of abdominal tissue AKA lockdown strategy)

### yewwei.tan — 2015-01-21T02:35:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/down-under-muscles.12621/post-153480>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=153478)
>
> ok
> this is the one area on this forum i can categorically and confidently say certain things are plain bullshit, as it is my area of expertise

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=153479)
>
> in the bear crawl video he has a complete loss of lumbar stability and length when transitioning
> this IS NOT a good position to develop "core" strength, as it shows a complete lack of it to begin with
> this may train one to employ a stabilizing strategy that is less that optimal (read: concentric activation of abdominal tissue AKA lockdown strategy)

META: I love stirring the pot . Every time I say something provocative but wrong, I get back a answer that teaches me a lot that I didn't know.

\----

More random questions (and I'm sure I'll have more as we go along):

When we are stabilised in this frame of reference, the limbs know themselves via their reference point of punctuim fixum. This frees the limbs for movement freedom and liberates them from performing any kind of stability

If saggital stability of the lumbar spine is lost from any decrease in IAP or inability to meet the pelvic floor with pressure directed from diaphragmatic excursion, the hips lose their orientation ad we start to access our discs (size of a slice of kiwi fruit) and zygapophyseal joints (size of your little fingernail)
obviously not a good idea when moving loads.​

Hip mobility has and continues to be an issue for me thanks to an L3-L4 herniation 5 years ago. The issue obviously has musco-skeletal components, but I feel is fundamentally a neuro-muscular connection issue.

The question is when it comes to re-wiring movement patterns which first require increases in the usable range of motion of a joint (in all 3 dimensions), what is your general philosophy on doing so?

The 2 extremes here I would think is the hardcore "just stretch the damn thing and your muscles and joints will adapt!" or "perform movements that force you into end ranges of motions and continue to grease them", and the cautious "let's fix stability in the usable range first, and then fix the neurological connections needed to maintain that stability, and then seek to expand that range through movements in a stable position"

Tasks can and continue to be performed in all manner of risky positions that are biomechanically un-sound (much to the delight of my bank account) the question remains is it optimal, and is it requiring inefficient expensive skeletal muscles to perform a stabilizing task?​

I've heard the philosophy from Kelly Starrett, that by formalising the methods needed to get into and maintain stable positions, you bolster your ability to buffer the harm of getting into bad positions (possibly with a load).

As I understood your statement about the bear crawl, it is bad because it actually re-wires the brain into bad stabilisation patterns. Is there any value in performing such unstable crawling movements as a way of "challenging the system"?

Heh, and to think that my motivation to knowing this is so that I can perform risky-as-hell activities like backflips and long distance running

\----

I need to attend that movement workshop!

.....
