# Thread: Copper toxicity from an IUD
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/copper-toxicity-from-an-iud.12872/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mr.Bliss — 2015-02-10T11:29:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/copper-toxicity-from-an-iud.12872/post-155201>

Hey friends!

Do you know anything about the health effects of a copper IUD? It is a non-hormonal IUD which is far better than the hormonal one but still it might lead to a copper toxicity and other health problems.
Me and my girlfriend are thinking about this. She already has one for 6 years and is wondering about the toxicity.
I have read on the Magnetico Sleep Pad website that it can detox many heavy metals in a short amount of time when it's combined with DMSA/Chlorella/Cilantro. And what about the Biomat?
So can you still keep the IUD since the Magnetico/Biomat protects you from the toxicity thanks to its strong detoxification properties?
We already have a Biomat Mini and plan to buy a 20 Gauss Magnetico and maybe later also a bigger Biomat.
Thank you for your answers.

Cheers

### Inger — 2015-02-10T12:19:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/copper-toxicity-from-an-iud.12872/post-155204>

I would never ever put a copper thing into my body these days...........

Imagine, it is acting like an antenna.... and most of us bath in huge amounts of nnEMF 7/24

get it out as soon as possible

### Hope — 2015-02-10T16:11:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/copper-toxicity-from-an-iud.12872/post-155228>

I'm w/Inger on this....copper is not your GF's friend...to say the least.

### CindyB — 2015-02-10T16:25:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/copper-toxicity-from-an-iud.12872/post-155230>

Most effective form of birth control while still allowing unsheathed P-V sex. Compared to getting pregnant, not dangerous. Compared to the antenna of her collagen fibers, it's a nothing antenna. She's eating seafood? She's getting plenty of zinc which is the main downside of too much copper (and I'm not saying she is getting \_any\_ copper from the IUD). If there is a concern about copper, eat more seafood.

### Penny — 2015-02-10T19:19:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/copper-toxicity-from-an-iud.12872/post-155250>

Especially oysters - tons of zinc...

### sjoshua — 2015-02-11T02:35:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/copper-toxicity-from-an-iud.12872/post-155306>

> [Mr.Bliss said:](/goto/post?id=155201)
>
> Hey friends!
>
> Do you know anything about the health effects of a copper IUD? It is a non-hormonal IUD which is far better than the hormonal one but still it might lead to a copper toxicity and other health problems.

Quick search on scholar.google.com brings up this:
[http://www.contraceptionjournal.org/article/S0010-7824(05)00075-2/abstract](http://www.contraceptionjournal.org/article/S0010-7824\(05\)00075-2/abstract)

Looks like blood copper levels are 2x that of non-users. Also worth considering, this is users in Mexico and in 2005. nnEMF has increased in general since then, due to the implementation of many new techs. I am unaware if this would cause a change in these levels, but would imagine overall increased dehydration would result in additional chaos.

> [Mr.Bliss said:](/goto/post?id=155201)
>
> Me and my girlfriend are thinking about this. She already has one for 6 years and is wondering about the toxicity.

If she's already had it for 6 years, then I'd imagine she's doing enough else right to be OK as others have mentioned.

> [Mr.Bliss said:](/goto/post?id=155201)
>
> I have read on the Magnetico Sleep Pad website that it can detox many heavy metals in a short amount of time when it's combined with DMSA/Chlorella/Cilantro.

My understanding is, the magnetico is providing a more direct field equivalent to the Earth's resonance, so it would assist in approaching optimal in many ways beyond heavy metal detox. If you can't sleep in a tent in the woods, and demand indoor accouterments, this would be a fair alternative.

> [Mr.Bliss said:](/goto/post?id=155201)
>
> And what about the Biomat?

I haven't researched this much, but the fact it plugs in tells me there are going to be pros/cons depending on the health of the user, the circuitry it is attached to, and the length of exposure. If you ar having positive results with it I'd see no reason to change what you are doing though.

> [Mr.Bliss said:](/goto/post?id=155201)
>
> So can you still keep the IUD since the Magnetico/Biomat protects you from the toxicity thanks to its strong detoxification properties?
> We already have a Biomat Mini and plan to buy a 20 Gauss Magnetico and maybe later also a bigger Biomat.
> Thank you for your answers.
>
> Cheers

It is all about N=1. If she has had the IUD for 6 years and your environment has been stable, her health is not adverse or deteriorating, and she is progressing/maintaining properly towards whatever she's set out to do, then she is likely doing enough good to offset the use. As others have mentioned, moar seafood doesn't hurt.

EDIT: Likely the largest factor, if you guys aren't ready for kiddos, and are opposed to other forms of BC, then not removing it would seem more of a no brainer...

Cheers, to you!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

### Hope2Learn — 2015-02-11T19:37:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-can-you-tell-me-about-cytochrome-p450.12891/post-155379>

I've been reading some things like [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cytochrome\_P450#CYP\_families\_in\_humans](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cytochrome_P450#CYP_families_in_humans)
and referring back to my test results, now sorted for entries containing "CYP", I can narrow down the most important issues, at least in part, to:

**CYP1 A2** - GS159 faster caffeine metabolism (flagged as "good)
& also:
rs762551 (A;A) faster caffeine metabolism in liver

(Don't know if this is actually good though.. wikipedia says CYP1's function is related to drug and steroid (especially estrogen) metabolizing.. if I metabolize caffeine faster do I also metabolize estrogen quicker? Is that a silly question of apples and oranges?)

**CYP2 C19** - multiple SNPs and notations - **this is placed at the top of my list no matter how it's sorted, due to having the highest magnitude on the "bad" scale**:
GS151 - reduced breakdown of certain drugs
rs4244285 (A;G) - "This variant is the most common reason for poor metabolism of compounds like mephenytoin (an anti-convulsant), some antidepressants, the anti-platelet drug Plavix, and some drugs used for ulcer conditions of various types. The risk allele is rs4244285(A). As a nonfunctioning CYP2C19, this variant would be expected to be a poor metabolizer of several commonly prescribed drugs, including anti-ulcer drugs like omeprazole (trade names Losec and Prilosec), esomeprazole (trade name Nexium), and lansoprazole (trade name Prevacid). In Caucasians, SNPs in CYP2C19 are relatively rare (in contrast to SNPs in CYP2D6), but SNPs in this gene are common in Asians."
rs12777823 (A;G) - "Avoid Plavix, higher risk for adverse cardiovascular events.**"** (related neighbors, these last 2)
rs 1045642 (C;T) - concentrations of certain drugs are significantly increased

**to name a few - these all pertain to poor breakdown of certain drugs** _\-( just about every class of anti-depressants is 7x less effective in me (I could've told them that years ago, and in fact tried to  ), Plavix has a high risk of cardiovascular adverse events in me, bupoprion or whatever will have no effect on me, etc etc.. too many kinds of drugs to mention. More rs's than just the CYP ones cause part of those issues though)_

**CYP2 D6\*4** - rs 3892097 (A;G) - "The (A) form disrupts proper mRNA formation, resulting in a nonfunctional CYP2D6 protein. The associated allele is also known as CYP2D6\*4. The CYP2D6\*4 allele is the most common nonfunctioning variant of CYP2D6. If two copies of this (or similar) changes are inherited, poor metabolism ('PM') of debrisoquine is observed. Many other drugs are typically first metabolized by CYP2D6 including dextromorphan, sparteine, metoprolol, nortriptyline and many other antidepressants and codeine"

**CYP2 A6\*5** - rs50310170 (G;T) - non-functioning variant, affects nicotine metabolism

**CYP2 D6\*10** - GS173 "reduced metabolism" (which is flagged as "Good")
& also:
rs1065852(C;T) - decreased or non-functioning variant (affects drug breakdown/metabolism)
Wikipedia says CYP2's function is drug and steroid metabolism also but doesn't say which kinds of steroids, but my CYP2 drug metabolism functions seem to be hurting so maybe it's steroid metabolism functions are also in trouble?

I don't know if this helps anyone else besides myself, but I'm a huge fan of learning and this way I can at least reference the ones I want to focus on. Thanks everyone!

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-02-13T01:54:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-can-you-tell-me-about-cytochrome-p450.12891/post-155501>

CYP1B1 is also decreased estrogen metabolism

### Hope — 2015-02-12T01:37:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-on-here-without-a-smart-phone.12893/post-155402>

And if so, how do you get email while traveling if you need it? Laptop? Tablet?

I would love to hear....

### JMO — 2015-02-12T02:02:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-on-here-without-a-smart-phone.12893/post-155408>

I don't have a smartphone just a Samsung. My understanding is that its not constantly sending a signal just when I get a txt or phone call. I take my iPad and keep it on airplane mode then just turn it on to check mail.

### Hope — 2015-02-12T04:09:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-on-here-without-a-smart-phone.12893/post-155425>

Thanks so much, JMO! I have thought about that as well.....we shall see.

### cinnamon — 2015-02-12T04:38:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-on-here-without-a-smart-phone.12893/post-155428>

I don't have any kind of cell phone - smart or not!

I have a small tablet that I use very rarely. When I travelled a couple of months ago, I turned it on in the airports to get on wifi and check email. It worked just fine for that.

Also, did you know you can send texts to people who have cell phones from your email account? Yes, most mobile providers have an "email to text" gateway. I was able to send a text to my DH (he \*does\* have a smartphone) while on the airport wifi to let him know when my luggage came off the carousel so he could bring the car around to pick me up.

So, I think tablets are a useful tool when travelling. They do require wifi. But that (unfortunately) seems to be ubiquitous these days.

cheers,
cinnamon

### rlee314 — 2015-02-12T05:59:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-on-here-without-a-smart-phone.12893/post-155433>

i don't have a smart phone.

### Lahelada — 2015-02-12T13:11:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-on-here-without-a-smart-phone.12893/post-155443>

Careful with the thought that a normal old phone is more benign. Any cellphone needs to position itself constantly. So even the most ancient cellphone will handshake to ask what do I need to connect with. Also if it can send mail it is plenty smart. A better way to evaluate your phone is from the Sar Value available from the FCC website,even if you are not Us based. If that value is not already on the phone(,behind the battery) you can look it up via the Model or Fcc ID. (also behind the battery)

I have a smartish phone but all things being equal would say that for occasional mail access a tablet would be a good choice . Put it in a case though and use it with a stylus.

\* note apart : my smart phone has a Sar value of 0.17W/kg and the FCC category says that it should be carried at least 1cm from the body and that if worn on a belt clip said clip must not contain metal...... Everyone should look up theirs.

### kovita — 2015-02-12T13:40:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-on-here-without-a-smart-phone.12893/post-155446>

I have a smartphone. Currently have it switched on shortly only few times a day, use it much more like a pager. At the end people got used to the fact, I am not available 24/7. For me this is a system that works just well. As for internet I use laptop or tablet for no more than 30min. a day, the same system, check few times a day.

### Inger — 2015-02-12T13:56:34+0200

### nicld — 2015-02-12T16:32:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/4am-really.12900/post-155464>

This is what time my bladder has been waking me up and it is getting OLD!!!!. I really try to ignore it and go back to sleep but sometimes I just cannot and have to go. Once I do get up, I try to go back to sleep, sometimes it works and sometimes it does not.

I reread CT7 in hopes of finding something that will let me know why this is happening and I could not really come up with anything.

The one thing that I do in the evenings (usually around 7 pm) is have a nice cup of tea but that is about the only liquid I have after supper but I do drink alot during the day. I will try to stop the evening tea and see if that helps.

If anyone has any ideas, I would greatly appreciate it.

### Hope — 2015-02-13T16:11:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/4am-really.12900/post-155529>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=155474)
>
> Not up on my acronyms, what is this???

ADH = Anti-diuretic hormone
CSM = Cholestyramine, old cholesterol drug
VIP = Vasoactive intestinal Peptide

I am not sure I agree. This would mostly apply in cases of bio toxin illness. There are many other potential causes of this.

ADH is an easy, often covered blood test.......but as we know if you're Bun/C is Ok.....you might be oK on the dehydration front??????

### Lahelada — 2015-02-13T16:38:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/4am-really.12900/post-155533>

Hope , i agree. ### Sue-UK — 2015-02-13T17:21:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/4am-really.12900/post-155536>

Nicole, I drank my litre of water over the evening last night, went to pee just before I went to bed, but then didn't need to go again until after 8 this morning, and that was after a large glass of water and then a cup of tea. I'm wondering if the cold water over the evening actually helped me get cooler ready for sleep, and reduced inflammation, rather than it being a hydration thing.

### nicld — 2015-02-13T17:30:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/4am-really.12900/post-155537>

Thanks Sue,

I did drink more water last night plus my cup of tea but still woke early to pee. I will stop the tea and see if that helps. Wondering how the HCG I am on is having an effect on the output. I will be done with it in about 2 weeks.

### nicld — 2015-02-15T01:00:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/4am-really.12900/post-155634>

No tea last night but still work up but I think it was a good thing. The dog needed to go out (grrrrrr) and I had locked the cat in the DD bedroom. Plus the DD had got herself stuck in between her bed and the mesh so good thing I was up. She had a bad night as well and is just way off today.

Hoping for a better night tonight. Doing CT in the early evening, drank lots of water and will get some oxy tonight.

### Sue-UK — 2015-02-15T11:30:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/4am-really.12900/post-155676>

My being able to go til 8 am had me looking at diurnal rhythm of ADH ... from what I saw I think there are "normal" levels from 8 a.m to 10 p.m and then it should be increased between 10 pm and 8 am. So maybe its less about my tweaking my water intake during the evening and more about an improvement in the ADH diurnal rhythm.....Whether that is increasing ADH levels at the right time, or my kidneys becoming more sensitive to the signals I'm not sure ..... I do think improving my water consumption has helped though. The other thought I've had is that if the ADH diurnal rhythm isn't entrained to the light cycle (maybe its the earth rotation?) perhaps my outside CT is helping ...... I always try and look up and observe the sun's position in the sky, or mentally acknowledge where it is if I can't see it, in winter it's like trying to set my internal clock to the right time by the earth's position in relation to the sun once a day, and hoping it will keeping ticking at the right tempo, bio hacking what might help or hinder

### nicld — 2015-02-15T17:06:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/4am-really.12900/post-155684>

Oh, sleep was much better last night for everyone. I did wake a bit when the DH was up for work but did not get up to pee. Maybe the deep CT and oxy helped. I did break down and give the DD 1mg of melatonin last night to make sure that she slept. I think she did have a seizure Friday night since she was way out of it yesterday.

I also used my sun lamp for quite a bit yesterday. It was nice and sunny outside yesterday but with -25F windchills there was no way I could stay out too long. I can handle sitting outside in the cold with the sun but that is a little too cold to be safe.

### nicld — 2015-02-16T16:15:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/4am-really.12900/post-155786>

Woke up early again but I know I slept really good until then. I did have a big glass of water before bed though and I need to see my chiropractor, the hips hurt. I think that about 4 am is about the end of a sleep cycle and if the bladder is full it triggers me to come completely out. Then if the cat knows that I am stirring he comes and sleeps on me.

### Lahelada — 2015-02-16T23:59:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/4am-really.12900/post-155815>

Glad you slept better. I joined you in the broken sleep cycle circuit. My time is 2 am though. So far I am feeling ok though,even remember dreams. Do you dream and remember them?

### nicld — 2015-02-17T15:58:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/4am-really.12900/post-155891>

Yep, I am dreaming when I wake and sometimes remember them. I sleep like a rock from about 9:30 pm to 4 am.

Woke up about 4:15 am this morning but did not get up but my cat knew I was stirring and came and laid by me. I was about to fall back asleep till I checked the clock and realized I would be needing to get up soon for work. Nothing worse then getting woke up in the middle of a sleep cycle.

I also saw my chiropractor yesterday and he got me all straightened out again and I am sure that help my sleep.

I am also wondering if my use of the sun lamp right when I get up may be causing me to start waking after 4. I may try not using it a couple of work mornings and see how it goes.

### nicld — 2015-02-23T19:22:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/4am-really.12900/post-156404>

Hmmm, sleep has improved. Estimate wake up time today, just before 5am which is not bad since I have to get up by about 5:15 am. Tried watching the end of the Oscars but no way!!! Those blueblocker glasses just knock me out.

Cycle is done as well as the HCG. I drank plenty of water yesterday plus a cup of tea before bed. I will be curious to see if this happens again in the second half of my cycle to see if it is the cycle of the HCG I was on.

### nicld — 2015-03-03T16:38:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/4am-really.12900/post-157297>

Hmmm, woke up again about 4 am. I just started my Pg cream again yesterday. I will have to monitor this and see what happens the next couple of days. For the longest time I was applying it only at night but last cycle I started morning and night and that may be when I started having issues.

Time to do a little hack and see if it continues for the next couple of days and if changing the times of application makes a difference. I NEED my sleep!!!

# Thread: The Right Way To Exercise
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-right-way-to-exercise.12902/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BTA — 2015-02-12T18:48:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-right-way-to-exercise.12902/post-155477>

When I see somebody doing crossfit or swinging kettlebells - I know they have no clue how to workout. People are brain washed to think running improves cardio, jumping on boxes improves performance and so on... all of which is garbage. The body has only one way to function - lift very heavy & very slow. Which is way the method called Super Slow is the only effective method based on science. Call it BS - that's fine but do your homework.

Great article:

**P90X, Insanity and Similar Nonsense**

By [Drew Baye](http://baye.com/author/baye/)

Hard workout today:
MedX Neck Extension
MedX Neck Flexion
Weighted Chin Up on the Nautilus Omni Multi Exercise (OME)
MedX Shoulder Press
MedX Leg Press
OME Wrist Curl with thick bar attachment
OME Wrist Extension with thick bar attachment
Total workout time was around 15 minutes. I’ll do it again in 3 or 4 days. Normally I’d take a _little_ longer, around 20 minutes or so – not because I do more exercises but because I rest longer between sets – but I had to get it done before my next client arrived for his workout.
Why so little? Because if done properly _that’s all it takes_. The most important factor in stimulating increases in muscular strength and size, as well as all the other possible benefits of exercise, is the degree of _effort_. The effort put into a workout is what determines its effectiveness, rather than the volume of work performed. If the level of effort is high enough, very little exercise is required, but no amount of exercise will produce much in the way of results if the effort is not high enough and if too much of any type of exercise is performed the results will be worse, not better.
I’m often asked what I think about programs like P90X, Insanity, and similar popular training programs, and one of the first things I usually point out is the volume of exercise is way too high while the quality – both in terms of type of exercise and the manner of performance instructed – is very low.
To save myself the time of having to answer the same questions about these programs over and over, the following is a brief summary of what is wrong with them, and what you should be doing instead.
**Form**
I’ve watched some of the videos and the form on the exercises is atrocious. Too fast, and little attention to proper form despite paying lip service to it. Very fast, sloppy reps.
_What you should be doing instead:_
Move in a slow, controlled manner, especially when reversing direction between lifting and lowering the weight. Don’t just make the weight go up and down, take your time with it and focus on intensely contracting the target muscles throughout the entire exercise.
**Volume**
These programs include almost an hour of exercise plus an additional and completely unnecessary fifteen minutes or so of [abdominal work](http://baye.com/ripped-abs/) a few days a week. These programs have you doing way too many exercises and sets. Unless you’re taking steroids this is overtraining, and even if you are this volume of exercise is completely unnecessary and counterproductive. With proper training and diet you could achieve the same or better results with _less than one hour of training per week, and in some cases less than thirty minutes._
Speaking of which, the results you see in the testimonials have everything to do with the diet and almost nothing to do with the idiotic training program. I’ve had men lose over 30 pounds of fat and women around 20 with less total training time over a period of _two months_ than the P90X or Insanity programs require in _a single week_.
_What you should be doing instead:_
One hard set of only one or two exercises per muscle group, working out no more than three non-consecutive days per week. Advanced trainees should do _less_, rather than more exercise.
**“Muscle Confusion”**
The concept of “muscle confusion” is nonsense based on a misunderstanding of motor learning principles. Muscles do not become resistant to stimulation from a particular exercise, they only appear to because improvements are faster initially due to neural adaptations and slow down after around six to eight weeks when adaptation starts to be more due to hypertrophy. Changing exercises too frequently is a huge mistake. I’ve already addressed this in detail in [The Ultimate Routine](http://baye.com/the-ultimate-routine/).
_What you should be doing instead:_
Don’t constantly vary your workouts. Consistently follow a [well designed workout or routine](http://baye.com/store/high-intensity-workouts/) that effectively works all the major muscle groups and focus on getting as strong as possible on that. If you plateau it’s more likely you need a reduction in workout volume or more recovery time between workouts and _not_ to “confuse” your muscles by changing exercises.
**Abdominal or “Core” Workouts**
Fifteen minutes of abdominal work is neither necessary nor beneficial. One or two exercises for the abdominal muscles, a flexion and a rotation or lateral flexion movement are all that is required. Maybe four, if you’re doing an occasional [abdominal specialization workout](http://baye.com/store/high-intensity-workouts/), but even this should only take a few minutes to complete, and the reason for doing so is to strengthen the muscles, not to improve abdominal definition. Getting [ripped abs](http://baye.com/ripped-abs/) is almost entirely a matter of diet and has very little to do with abdominal exercises or workouts.
_What you should be doing instead:_
Only one hard set of only one or two abdominal exercises at the end of your workout, which should take no more than one to three minutes. If you want ripped abs you need to focus on your diet.

**Plyometrics**
Plyometrics are incredibly stupid. They are relatively ineffective for building strength, unnecessary for developing speed or explosiveness or improving rate of force development, and carry a _very_ high risk of injury. They have no place in any training program.
_What you should be doing instead:_
If you want to develop speed or explosiveness _simply focus on getting stronger_. All these different things are expressions of strength, rather than different types as some people believe. You don’t need to train one way for “maximum strength”, another for “speed strength” another for “explosive strength” or any of the other types these people come up with to make their programs more complex and scientific sounding than they need to be.
**Cardio**
Cardio is included in these programs with the claim it is beneficial for both fat burning and cardiovascular conditioning, however it is very inefficient for fat burning and unnecessary for cardiovascular and metabolic conditioning if you’re already performing a proper high intensity strength training program. Additionally, the high impact and repetitive nature of the movements is more likely to cause injuries and contribute to joint problems in the long run, unlike high intensity strength training which is much safer for the joints when performed properly.
_What you should be doing instead:_
If you want to become leaner you don’t need extra activity to burn calories – which is generally a very inefficient waste of time – you need to eat fewer calories and consume a diet which creates a hormonal situation conducive to fat loss. If you want to improve cardiovascular and metabolic conditioning, high intensity strength training will do that, and you can emphasize it further by limiting rest between exercises.
**Supplements**
The P90X and Insanity programs also encourage the use of supplements, including a meal replacement shake and other products sold by Beachbody. While some supplements are beneficial, they are not at all necessary to achieve incredible results from training. The same seems to be the case with other companies and makes me wonder if the workout programs were developed secondary to the supplements as a means of marketing them, kind of like every new training program introduced on certain bodybuilding web sites.
_What you should be doing instead:_
Whatever your training goals, whether you want more muscle, less fat, better performance or health, your biggest nutritional concern should be your diet. Get that in order first, then _if you can identify a legitimate need or benefit_, consider supplementation. Be very skeptical of supplement advertising, though. Most claims are complete BS.

**But what about those testimonials?**
Beachbody, the company that sells P90X, Insanity, Hip Hop Abs and similar nonsense have done one thing right – marketing. Their infomercials are well done and they have some impressive testimonials. However, as I already mentioned, the results those people achieved were mostly due to the diet. They could have done the same or better faster and with only a small fraction of the total training time. **As Nautilus inventor Arthur Jones** was fond of saying, the fact that a particular method produced some result is not proof the same or better results could not have been achieved by some other means, more quickly and more efficiently.

### JoeBranca — 2015-03-01T18:27:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-right-way-to-exercise.12902/post-156976>

fwiw Ido weighs in on super slow w machines:

> "revolutionary approach" and a forgotten method that has been forgotten for a good reason. This has died many years ago, in athletic circles, with the failed attempts of movement transfer. Movement is a game in which 1+1+1=17. It might work in very specific and isolated cases in which a weak link is addressed but even then integration is needed. As for injury prevention - dont delude yourself. Machines only appear to be safer, just like sitting on the sofa, the type of injuries and their nature merely change. You increase your sensitivity for 'out of alignment' scenarios, you increase risk of overuse injuries, weaken stabilizers and end goal motor pattern and more and more. I've never met someone who trains with machines as a center piece and can demonstrate that strength in movement efficiently. It should be reserved for very very isolated cases.

### Harvey Cheyne Jr. — 2016-10-31T16:50:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-right-way-to-exercise.12902/post-203467>

My understanding/experience: going to failure easily leads to overtraining. I had a bout of overtraining with Superslow after reading that (SS founder) Ken Hutchins' best student would go so hard, he would lay down in the shower and puke afterward. I thought "I will do that; I like that kind of intensity. Sounds good."

"CNS burnout: just overtraining with a cool and scary name attached".

Maybe I did it too frequently. That was my mistake with cold showers; I didn't build up slowly enough and went too cold, too long, too often, and wound up with pneumonia three times until I learned to respect my limits. Now I am ramping the cold exposure much more slowly.

"The human body is very resilient. It ain't made out of glass. However, it has been the experience of many trainees that long term training to failure leaves them very burnt out. This can't be counteracted by perpetually decreasing volume and frequency. " So I no longer go to failure (with weights or cold), and I am continuing to increase my strength and cold tolerance (based on weights pushed/time and temperature in the cold).

[http://www.mikelipowski.com/2015/01/02/slow-training-bodybuilding/](http://www.mikelipowski.com/2015/01/02/slow-training-bodybuilding/)

My $.02

YMMV

### timmothysmith01 — 2023-01-05T16:36:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-right-way-to-exercise.12902/post-318704>

-   Follow Workout Hydration Rules
-   Use Proper Warm-Up Exercises
-   Cool Down Appropriately
-   Keep Moving and Heart Rate Elevated
-   Strength Train Properly
-   Consider High-Intensity Interval Training (HIIT)
-   Rest and Slowly Progress without Injury
-   Enjoy Your Exercise

# Thread: Cramping last night
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cramping-last-night.12905/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nittygrittydanny — 2015-02-12T19:18:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cramping-last-night.12905/post-155480>

I work in a Lab testing asphalt and granite. Yesterday was BUSY and I was in and out of the lab (bright sunny day in Marianna Fl.) I drank about 96ozs of good water throughout the day. As I was driving home (about 1hr 15min commute) my hands, mostly thumb and index fingers, pinky were cramping pretty bad, got worse in pain index after I ate dinner (flank steak, green beans and water). I had umpired a softball game the evening prior and drank bottled water while doing so. I noticed my toes cramping some early in the game but it subsided and didn't return? I drank about the same amount of water the day of the game, but it was cool and overcast most of the day so I didn't get as much sun exposure that day.

I had 2 bottles of Coconut water in the cabinet that I drank after dinner and ran my hands under cold water (semi CT), that helped alot. Just read this about potassium on here. Is there a better source of K+ than coconut water? I normally keep it on hand and drink prior to and after Reff'ing a football game due to the amount of sweating in addition to the hydration i do prior to working a game.

**You must pay attention to your Potassium (K+) levels**. The reason potassium is critical inside a cell, and supersedes magnesium and sodium ion concentration is because it is naturally tied to beta and gamma carboxyl side chain groups found in proteins, where potassium specifically binds because of “quantum advantages.” These beta and gamma carboxyl bonds allows K+ to ADSORB to this protein site and donate electrons (this is why K+ has the second highest redox potential to Lithium in life; see below) to the polarized water gel crystal.

Danny

# Thread: Thinking about Copper
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-about-copper.12906/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2015-02-12T23:18:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-about-copper.12906/post-155493>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685176/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685176/)

> **Copper methanobactin: a molecule whose time has come**
> [Ramakrishnan Balasubramanian](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Balasubramanian%20R%5Bauth%5D) and [Amy C. Rosenzweig](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Rosenzweig%20AC%5Bauth%5D)
> [Author information ►](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685176/#) [Copyright and License information ►](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685176/#)
>
> The publisher's final edited version of this article is available at [Curr Opin Chem Biol](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/elink.fcgi?dbfrom=pubmed&retmode=ref&cmd=prlinks&id=18313412)
> See other articles in PMC that [cite](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685176/citedby/) the published article.
>
> [Go to:](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685176/#)
> **Summary of recent advances**
> Copper plays a key role in the physiology of methanotrophs. One way that these bacteria meet their high copper requirement is by the biosynthesis and release of high affinity copper-binding compounds called methanobactins. Recent advances in methanobactin characterization include the first crystal structure, detailed spectroscopic analyses, and studies of metal ion specificity. Methanobactin may function in copper uptake, regulation of methane monooxygenase expression, protection against copper toxicity, and particulate methane monooxygenase activity. Methanobactin can extract copper from insoluble minerals and could be important for mineral weathering. Many methanobactin properties are reminiscent of iron siderophores, suggesting a similar mechanism of handling. Methanobactin-like compounds have also been identified in yeast mitochondria, suggesting that these molecules are a more universal phenomenon.
>
> [Go to:](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685176/#)
> **Introduction**
> Copper is central to the physiology of methanotrophs, gram-negative bacteria that use methane as their sole carbon source \[[1](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685176/#R1)\]. In the first step of carbon assimilation by methanotrophs, methane is oxidized to methanol by methane monooxygenase enzymes (MMOs). All methanotrophs except for one species produce particulate MMO (pMMO), a three-subunit, integral membrane protein complex that is widely believed to contain a copper active site \[[2](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685176/#R2)\]. Under conditions of copper starvation, a few methanotrophs can also express a soluble MMO (sMMO) that contains a diiron active site. In strains that express both pMMO and sMMO, copper in the growth medium represses transcription of the sMMO genes while leading to the expression of pMMO and formation of extensive intracytoplasmic membranes that house pMMO. A number of additional proteins are also differentially expressed as a function of copper concentrations in the growth medium \[[3](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685176/#R3)\]. Given the important role of copper in both metabolic switching and pMMO chemistry, an effective copper acquisition system is critical.
>
> Evidence for a specific copper uptake system was obtained initially in the early 1990s from studies of_Methylosinus trichosporium_ OB3b mutants that constitutively express sMMO and do not express active pMMO \[[4](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685176/#R4),[5](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685176/#R5)\]. The extracellular media from these strains also contains unusually high concentrations of copper. On the basis of these data, Georgiou and coworkers postulated the existence of small copper binding complexes that are excreted by the cells under copper starvation conditions \[[5](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685176/#R5)\]. Over the next decade, several laboratories isolated and partially characterized several such compounds from both _M. trichosporium_ OB3b \[[6](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685176/#R6),[7](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685176/#R7)\] and _Methylococcus capsulatus_ (Bath) \[[8](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685176/#R8)\]. Designated copper binding compounds (CBCs) or copper binding ligands (CBLs), these molecules had molecular masses ranging from 382 to 1218 Da and bound copper ions with high affinity. Issues with homogeneity and degradation precluded determining the chemical composition of these compounds until recently, however. After the development of a successful purification protocol by Graham, DiSpirito and coworkers, the crystal structure of a copper binding compound from _M. trichosporium_ OB3b was reported in 2004, and the molecule was renamed methanobactin (mb) \[[9](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685176/#R9)–[11](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685176/#R11)\]. The crystal structure and subsequent biochemical, spectroscopic, and functional studies over the past three years have provided new insight into the properties and function of mb. This review summarizes these recent advances and considers the possibility that mb-like molecules may be a more general phenomenon.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/minimising-blue-light.12907/post-155495>

So I have switched some of the lights in the house to red LEDS, which we use after sundown, but I just saw some comparison of light bulbs where they showed that LED light bulbs actually have peaks of different coloured light.

# Thread: Nenets

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2015-02-13T00:40:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nenets.12908/post-155497>

> The Nenets are reindeer herders, migrating across the Yamal peninsula, thriving for more then a millennium with temperatures from minus 50°C in winter to 35°C in summer. Their annual migration of over a 1000 km includes a 48 km crossing of the frozen waters of the Ob River.
> **_“If you don’t drink warm blood and eat fresh meat, you are doomed to die on the tundra”_**
> The discovery of oil and gas reserves in the 1970s and the expanding infrastructure on the peninsula, has challenged their indigenous lifestyle. From the late Stalin period, all children have been enrolled in Soviet boarding schools, this has become a part of the typical Nenets life cycle.

[http://www.beforethey.com/journey/siberia---yamal](http://www.beforethey.com/journey/siberia---yamal)

**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet

I've read a ton of good things about Apple Cider Vinegar...would the Coconut Vinegar be better in every way...better in some worse in others...?

[http://www.amazon.com/Raw-Coconut-Vinegar-Secret-12-7/dp/B003XB5LO8/ref=sr\_1\_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1423794248&sr=8-2&keywords=coconut vinegar](http://www.amazon.com/Raw-Coconut-Vinegar-Secret-12-7/dp/B003XB5LO8/ref=sr_1_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1423794248&sr=8-2&keywords=coconut+vinegar)

### LMJ21224 — 2015-03-12T21:14:20+0200

# Thread: Cold baths and migraine onset!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-baths-and-migraine-onset.12930/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SarahF — 2015-02-14T17:26:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-baths-and-migraine-onset.12930/post-155605>

After a week or so of immersing myself in the coldest water from the tap in the UK for about 30 minutes I started to get daily migraines. I have been a sufferer before but previously they were associated with my menstrual cycle. The only thing I could come up with after hours searching online, was a possible link with histamine release from any change in temperature, women in their late forties seem to be more vulnerable to this. So I have embarked upon a lower histamine diet to see if I can reintroduce the cold baths, but don't really have any idea if I am barking up the wrong tree or not. Does anyone have any other thoughts/experience with this sort of symptom?

### Inger — 2015-02-14T18:17:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-baths-and-migraine-onset.12930/post-155609>

The only thing I do know is, at the beginning of CT you might experience detox... I did too. Migraines could be because of that......? Just thinking out loud...
I would maybe take some liver support like milk thistle, and drink plenty of good water, back off a little with the CT and increase slower. Maybe some B vitamins too or loads of raw egg yolks. I craved those all the time when I started CT
You can always try a lower histamine diet too, it want hurt I think!

Have you bin doing the CT protocols like Jack has said it to be done, or did you just jumped straight into the baths?
If that is - it might be wise to follow the CT protocol........ and start with face dunks

### nonchalant — 2015-02-14T18:24:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-baths-and-migraine-onset.12930/post-155611>

At the beginning I only soaked for about 7 minutes or so. But in a week or two, I was able to be comfortable for much longer times.

> The only thing I do know is, at the beginning of CT you might experience detox... I did too. Migraines could be because of that......? Just thinking out loud...
> I would maybe take some liver support like milk thistle, and drink plenty of good water, back off a little with the CT and increase slower. Maybe some B vitamins too or loads of raw egg yolks. I craved those all the time when I started CT

>

> If that is - it might be wise to follow the CT protocol........ and start with face dunks

For instance, I had one headache that came on fast and furious at work even though I really tried all I could to avoid one ...like drink TONS of water. That doesn't happen usually.

### Janet Shold — 2018-02-20T08:19:28+0200

# Thread: RewiringTheBrain - Moving away from AF & Anxiety
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rewiringthebrain-moving-away-from-af-anxiety.12932/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Knghe — 2015-02-15T00:23:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rewiringthebrain-moving-away-from-af-anxiety.12932/post-155629>

So, as my last post described: My being (brain&body) is in a state of 'cellular' anxiousness. It is very distractable, anxious, unpeaceful, unable to focus, nervous, etc etc etc.

I am improving, but still in a state of imbalance (mind&body).
My brain feels very chaotic.

As Jack and the rest have suggested camping, yet I cannot do that at this time.

So in an attempt to rewire and calm my brain, nervous system, etc, I will embark on the follow routine/protocol:

1\. Jump in my cold pool (routinely).
2\. Ground myself at the park grass (routinely).
3\. Up my intake of raw oysters (weekly/biweekly).
4\. Do my best to reduce exposure to EMF (purchased a Q-Link Pendant => any thoughts?)
5\. Constantly use my breathing to ground myself (breathing with my whole body, breathing into my feet) \[I think this is key because my being feels very ungrounded right now, easily flustered\]

These steps I will take to heart and apply them into my life inorder to get me out of this sub optimal state of inner anxiousness. (I know what it feels like to be completely balanced because this is the first time I ever experienced any time of 'anxiety disorder')

Does any have any advice or anything else to say?
Has any1 ever beat this very strange sensation of bodily/cellular/neuro anxiety?
Are these good steps which will help me heal and get out of this chronic anxious state-of-being?

My brain just feels totally imbalanced at the moment (although improving).

### Inger — 2015-02-15T08:14:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rewiringthebrain-moving-away-from-af-anxiety.12932/post-155666>

Well... I have felt that anxiety often. I used to be a very anxious person.

My advice would be, the things you list above are great, + eat ketogenic with lots of seafood, raw always worked best for anxiety for me. Don´t ask me why!
Then, look up into the sky, often.... be as much outside in daylight as you can be in the dark in the evenings after sunset! This is so important. You need the opposites, the dark AND the light.
Try to spend at least 1 hour in the woods / nature every day when light outside. And watch up into the sky. Take off your glasses  (important!)

Jumps into the cold pool are great for anxiety

### Knghe — 2015-02-15T22:13:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rewiringthebrain-moving-away-from-af-anxiety.12932/post-155708>

Thank you so much,

My brain has been in a drifting/daydreaming/extremely, chronically introspective state for so long it sucks soo bad.

Its a very unlively state of existance.

### Antonis — 2018-07-23T10:08:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rewiringthebrain-moving-away-from-af-anxiety.12932/post-246498>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=155666)
>
> Well... I have felt that anxiety often. I used to be a very anxious person.
>
> My advice would be, the things you list above are great, + eat ketogenic with lots of seafood, raw always worked best for anxiety for me. Don´t ask me why!
> Then, look up into the sky, often.... be as much outside in daylight as you can be in the dark in the evenings after sunset! This is so important. You need the opposites, the dark AND the light.
> Try to spend at least 1 hour in the woods / nature every day when light outside. And watch up into the sky. Take off your glasses  (important!)
>
> Jumps into the cold pool are great for anxiety

This is so helpful. I am very anxious person and am trying to do the best to beat the anxiety. I think for some people including me are like this because of our environment. Thanks for the tips

### Kara Nova — 2019-05-11T21:57:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rewiringthebrain-moving-away-from-af-anxiety.12932/post-270224>

As a creative person/athlete who struggles with anxiety and finding balance, exercise that requires a strong mind-body connection like yoga or dance really helps to ground me. I’d also add that any creative activity, drawing, sculpting, photography, anything— helps to channel and ground that energy that needs somewhere to go. Give the anxiety an outlet and, at least while you’re “doing the thing,” you should feel less anxious.

### 8Phoenix — 2019-05-16T04:50:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rewiringthebrain-moving-away-from-af-anxiety.12932/post-270589>

i agree with Jack when he recomend to go camping

for either one , you just need to identify the danger

# Thread: awesome fish tacos (huhu)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/awesome-fish-tacos-huhu.12944/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-02-16T09:55:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/awesome-fish-tacos-huhu.12944/post-155760>

ok i just made up these tacos (lettuce wraps) and they were excellent. even @[MIKE WILKINSON](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/12660/) would love this

recipe (serves 2 hungry or 3-4 regular joes)

**for the fish:**
800g firm white fish (aussies: snapper, flathead, hapuka etc) (yanks: probably cod, talapia, halibut??)
(OR similar squid/prawns/shrimp etc)
1 cup arrowroot flour (for dusting)
1 tsp good quality smoked paprika
1tsp cumin
salt and pepper

combine spices and tapioca in plastic ziplock bag
cut fish to small strips 10cm x 3cm x 3cm (if using squid maybe rings, obvs prawns whole tails etc)
toss in dusting mix in bag, put aside
**for the salsa roja:**
1 punnet cherry tomatoes (i used multi-colour)
handful coriander (cilantro)
1 small white or red salad onion
2 small jalapenos
2tsp salt
pepper

dice onion, chop tomatoes small, roughly but finely chop coriander, finely dice jalapeno
toss in a metal bowl with the salt - set aside in fridge. the salt will begin to take water from the tomatos intensifying their flavour. pour off any liquid after removing from fridge after 10 mins

**for the guac:**
1.5 ripe avocados
juice 1 lime
2tsp salt
pepper
sour cream if tolerated

smash together to a fine paste

**other:**
3 long horn peppers (yellow pepper/yellow chilli) grilled
finely shredded purple cabbage
finely sliced red radishes

heat 2-3cm deep coconut oil til shimmering
cook fish on all sides about 1 min each side

serve in lettuce cups, fish on bottom, salsa, guac, peppers and cabbage
extra squirt of lime and hot sauce of your choice (i like tapatio or something vinegary like this)

### MIKE WILKINSON — 2015-02-16T12:42:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/awesome-fish-tacos-huhu.12944/post-155771>

Mmmm yep that will pass
Might have to get help from the better half just to make sure I don't stuff it up
See I can burn toast (even gluten free toast)

### nicld — 2015-02-16T16:02:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/awesome-fish-tacos-huhu.12944/post-155782>

I LOVE fish tacos with lettuce wraps. Mahi Mahi is a great fish to use and talapia is ok (not my favorite fish).

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-02-17T00:31:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/awesome-fish-tacos-huhu.12944/post-155820>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=155782)
>
> I LOVE fish tacos with lettuce wraps. Mahi Mahi is a great fish to use and talapia is ok (not my favorite fish).

### nonchalant — 2015-02-17T00:39:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/awesome-fish-tacos-huhu.12944/post-155823>

# Thread: Laxatives!

**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Erik — 2015-02-16T14:20:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laxatives.12950/post-155778>

If my body is laxative dependant, what should i do??? I´ve been taking Yerba Mate for almost 7 years, every other day, it always accelerated my transit time. Now i wanted lo stop drinking it and my stools just dont come out, or they do bot little pieces, like there is a lot inside. What should i do?

### nicld — 2015-02-16T16:00:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laxatives.12950/post-155781>

You have to start healing your gut.

I was the same way. Taking up to 2 capfulls of Mirilax a day to just go plus drinking smooth move tea. Since starting following Jack, being treated for SIBO and other gut issues, getting my circadian rhythm better, the gut health has improved immensely. I also make sure to eat fermented foods and drink kombucha.

It may be rough for a bit but the gut needs to heal.

That is good for “emergency” issues only since it is no different than a laxative. My GI doc said to take that if it had been a couple of days between BM’s. Works like a charm but does not get to the root of what is going on.

### nicld — 2015-02-16T19:22:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laxatives.12950/post-155802>

Have you signed up the the SCD Lifestyles email. I just got this one in my inbox.

How to Relieve Constipation with Natural Movement

By: Steven Wright
When it comes to improving digestion and fixing constipation the conversation typically revolves around food, supplements and functional medicine. Unfortunately, this is only part of the conversation. There are so many other factors that can help us achieve perfect poops.

Things like play, laughing and movement...

These 3 are underrated and underappreciated...

Which is why I invited my friends Chad and Brenda to share some of their insights. They focus on movement and how it impacts our overall health - including digestion.

The boring truth is sometimes if you can't poop then going for a walk or trying the exercises below might be the only thing you need to stimulate a bowel movement.

I'm really excited to begin trying some of their ideas in the video below. If you try them, please [post in the comments](http://scdlifestyle.us8.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=e3d445f507761811031b63636&id=37356eb73f&e=4017bf49ad) what you are noticing and we'll all learn together.

\[Enter Chad and Brenda\]

**Movement is Absolutely Essential in Life**
It’s what allows us to move from A to B, express the fullness of our physiology and keep our bodies healthy.

In the modern world, humans are largely immobile and we’ve lost our relationship with our natural ways of moving.

We’ve become caged in, much like that of a captive animal. No longer are we out in fields hunting, gathering or farming… these days most of us are stuck behind a desk, hunched over our computers and glued to our computer screens.

This entrapment comes at a cost. Research continues to pile up and links [CHRONIC SITTING to many modern diseases](http://scdlifestyle.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e3d445f507761811031b63636&id=67ea1b4db5&e=4017bf49ad) such as obesity, heart disease, cancer, and type II diabetes.

It’s also wreaking havoc on our postures! Look around and you’ll quickly notice forward heads, rounded shoulders and hunched spines. The more we look down towards our computers and phones, the worse this problem seems to get.

Chronic sitting in these poor postures can also have a drastic effect on other bodily functions such as our digestion. With this forward head posture, our upper traps get tight, shoulders round, our rib cage gets compressed and our diaphragm becomes restricted. We no longer breathe efficiently with the full expansion of the belly and diaphragm. Lack of movement lends itself to stagnation and constipation.

Also, we are frequently over-stimulated and chronically stressed, which puts our bodies in a sympathetic state (fight or flight mode) and keeps us from being in a parasympathetic state (rest and digest mode). The combination of poor postures, impaired breathing, lack of movement/blood flow and stress can negatively affect digestion.

**We've Lost Our Natural Ways of Moving**
As a culture, we are becoming more and more aware that many of the problems we’re facing are direct result of the foods we’ve been eating. People are moving away from processed and refined foods, toxic fats and high levels of sugar and noticing significant improvements in their energy, mood and digestion.

But tackling the food issue will only get us so far. We have to [consider movement](http://scdlifestyle.us8.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=e3d445f507761811031b63636&id=d8f2308e9b&e=4017bf49ad) and other lifestyle factors as part of the protocol for achieving optimal wellness.

This is specifically the case when it comes to digestion. There are some incredibly powerful things you can do in order to optimize the breakdown of the food you eat, relieve constipation and improve your immune system.

We show you some examples in this video:

[](http://scdlifestyle.us8.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=e3d445f507761811031b63636&id=d6a250b257&e=4017bf49ad)

These movements are just a few of the solutions we incorporate in [The Sitting Solution](http://scdlifestyle.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e3d445f507761811031b63636&id=16985477bc&e=4017bf49ad). As Doctors of Physical Therapy, we’ve worked with thousands of patients in chronic pain and we firmly believe that bringing wellness and activity around the workspace is essential to our health.

**Our Recommendation is Simple But Highly Effective!**
Get up and move in specific and intentional directions every 30-45 minutes for a period of 2-4 minutes.

This is your get out of jail free pass, with regards to the sitting problem. Our goal is to help you get out of pain, restore your posture and return to your natural ways of moving.

You can learn more about how you can incorporate more effective and intentional movement around your workspace [here](http://scdlifestyle.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e3d445f507761811031b63636&id=f2180695c4&e=4017bf49ad).

Move well. Move often.

Chad Walding DPT, OPEX L-1, RKC

Brenda Walding DPT, FDN

# Thread: Lifespan and Month of Birth
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifespan-and-month-of-birth.12978/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2015-02-19T02:18:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifespan-and-month-of-birth.12978/post-156013>

[http://www.pnas.org/content/98/5/2934.full](http://www.pnas.org/content/98/5/2934.full)

> Month of birth influences adult life expectancy at ages 50+. Why? In two countries of the Northern Hemisphere–Austria and Denmark–people born in autumn (October–December) live longer than those born in spring (April–June). Data for Australia show that, in the Southern Hemisphere, the pattern is shifted by half a year. The lifespan pattern of British immigrants to Australia is similar to that of Austrians and Danes and significantly different from that of Australians. These findings are based on population data with more than a million observations and little or no selectivity. The differences in lifespan are independent of the seasonal distribution of deaths and the social differences in the seasonal distribution of births. In the Northern Hemisphere, the excess mortality in the first year of life of infants born in spring does not support the explanation of selective infant survival. Instead, remaining life expectancy at age 50 appears to depend on factors that arise _in utero_ or early in infancy and that increase susceptibility to diseases later in life. This result is consistent with the finding that, at the turn of the last century, infants born in autumn had higher birth weights than those born in other seasons. Furthermore, differences in adult lifespan by month of birth decrease over time and are significantly smaller in more recent cohorts, which benefited from substantial improvements in maternal and infant health.
>
> Remarkable reductions in old-age mortality over the past half century have fueled rapid growth of the elderly population and have led to a substantial increase in life expectancy ([1](http://www.pnas.org/content/98/5/2934.full#ref-1)). Yet we still have only limited knowledge about the factors that affect mortality and survival in old age ([2](http://www.pnas.org/content/98/5/2934.full#ref-2)). Recent research highlights the role of early-life factors that affect late-life mortality ([3](http://www.pnas.org/content/98/5/2934.full#ref-3)). In particular, environmental conditions during the prenatal and early postnatal period have been found to influence adult health and mortality significantly ([4](http://www.pnas.org/content/98/5/2934.full#ref-4),[5](http://www.pnas.org/content/98/5/2934.full#ref-5)) although these results are still controversial ([6](http://www.pnas.org/content/98/5/2934.full#ref-6), [7](http://www.pnas.org/content/98/5/2934.full#ref-7)).


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Update: In the morning everything was ok. Yesterday i was brain fogged all day becasue of the S.Boulardii megadose.

Yesterday i read something that opened my mind: Large doses of Lactobacilus could produce a lot of lactid acid, wich could result in some brain acidic and toxicity. And since 3 weeks before the brain fog started, i have been megadosing lactobacilus, to help my body odor, i decided to drop them.

Could it be the lactobacillus?? today i stopped taking them, and had some "Quassia Amara" tea. Its an anti-parasites tea, and strongly anti-candida infusion. Hours later my head hurts like crazy. I will keep updating.

I read that this acidity in the body could be becouse of a malfunction of the liver. Something about a T3-T4 enzyme. Could it be?? If that is the case, what should i do??

I also read that postassium/magnessium helped brain fog. But i cant get good mineral supplements here. Would eating fish improve my Potassium/magnessium??? i never eat fish, i should :/

BTW Im sure if i go to a zero EMF place, in a matter of days my brain fog would be gone........

**Dear Stephen,**
I tried samento and banderol and I noticed that with this treatment my lymph nodes become swollen. I also tried cat’s claw alone and under this treatment I feel some heat in the lungs and in the spinal cord. Are these reactions good or bad? Thanks.

**Stephen’s response:**
Not sure what to say on this one. However, if your lymph nodes become swollen that means that there is a buildup of contents in that system. The use of red root will help this a lot. Usually this happens as the body is killing off bacteria and viruses and it routes them through the lymph system for disposal, sometimes it is just an infection in the lymph system itself. I always use red root for this. Not sure what to say about the heat you mention.

Jumping on a trampoline is also purported to help clear lymphatic sludge - due to gravity I imagine...

**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BTA — 2015-02-22T01:37:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-robert-becker-md-electricity.13013/post-156253>

I wonder what we could learn from Electric Eels? Amazing what they can do......

**ELECTRIC EELS AND THEIR SUPERCHARGED MUSCLES**

All muscle and nerve cells have electrical potential and a simple contraction of a muscle will release a small amount of voltage. Between 100 and 200 million years ago, some fish began to amplify that potential.

They evolved electrocytes from muscle cells, which were organized in sequence and capable of generating much higher voltages. Today, the electric eel is capable of producing electric shocks of up to 600 volts. Despite its ...name, it is not closely related to true eels. It has a long, scale-free cylindrical body and a square mouth at the end of its snout.

Two organs, known as the Hunter’s organ and the Sach’s organ, give the fish its ability to generate electric discharges. When the eel spots prey, it opens the ion channels in these organs, reversing the polarity and creating an electric potential. This generates an electric current, which it uses to immobilise small prey. The shock, however, is not likely to be fatal to humans.

**Electric eels control their prey WITHOUT touching it**

_Read more:_
[http://www.dailymail.co.uk/sciencetech/article-2861016/Electric-eels-control-prey-WITHOUT-touching-Creatures-send-shock-waves-manipulate-target-s-muscles.html#ixzz3SQST7rH9](http://www.dailymail.co.uk/sciencetech/article-2861016/Electric-eels-control-prey-WITHOUT-touching-Creatures-send-shock-waves-manipulate-target-s-muscles.html#ixzz3SQST7rH9)
.
,

# Thread: Great article on importance of touch
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-article-on-importance-of-touch.13045/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2015-02-24T15:49:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-article-on-importance-of-touch.13045/post-156475>

[http://www.huffingtonpost.com/kindn...on\_b\_6530658.html?ncid=tweetlnkushpmg00000067](http://www.huffingtonpost.com/kindness-blog/touch-as-nutrition_b_6530658.html?ncid=tweetlnkushpmg00000067)

We mistakenly think that touch occurs on the periphery of our self, a skin thing. But truthfully each surface stimulus travels far into the most hidden interior landscapes of our self, traversing long nerve cells right through the buried spinal core to enter and gather in the deep folds of our brain. It's not by accident that our skin and brain each are generated from a single **ectodermic substance**, cascading outwards and inwards as we grow in the womb, because right at the very root and origin of us, we are built to connect the inner and outer worlds.

### nonchalant — 2015-02-24T17:53:47+0200

>
> We mistakenly think that touch occurs on the periphery of our self, a skin thing. **But truthfully each surface stimulus travels far into the most hidden interior landscapes of our self,** traversing long nerve cells right through the buried spinal core to enter and gather in the deep folds of our brain.

Hope, that is a very interesting article, I was kind of speculating too how food could become a substitute for touch. Like the way Dr K talks about food as something that 'un-condenses' the body, things 'swell' as proteins are un-condensed and then this swelling causes pizo-electric currents to flow (Hope I am right about this!) result is we are getting a kind of 'internal massage' but if this was supplied by the environment by actual touch the need for 'internal touching' might be less. Could it be that the 'touching starvation' we are trying to make up with 'internal touching' through food. This might be an interesting idea in terms of food addiction or whatever. I count myself as someone who was/is 'addicted' to food and is in part occasioned I now think by 'touching starvation'. I think also that is why when Dr K says food is not primordial it is putting us into the right track if we believe it is we keep doing the same thing looking for a different result and not finding.................

Anyway that aside I really liked that article. Thanks Hope.

**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BTA — 2015-02-27T05:20:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flashing-lights-found-on-distant-planet.13080/post-156776>

**is there anybody out there? What an interesting story -**

A dwarf planet is shining two bright lights at a NASA spacecraft right now, and our smartest scientists are unsure what they are.
As bizarre as that sentence sounds, that's the situation with Ceres — the largest object in the asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter, officially designated as a dwarf planet (the same category as Pluto).
NASA's Dawn spacecraft is approaching Ceres ahead of a March 6 rendezvous. The picture above was taken February 19, from a distance of just unde...r 29,000 miles, and shows two very shiny areas on the same basin on Ceres' surface.

[http://mashable.com/2015/02/25/strange-lights-dwarf-planet-ceres/?utm\_cid=mash-com-fb-main-link](http://mashable.com/2015/02/25/strange-lights-dwarf-planet-ceres/?utm_cid=mash-com-fb-main-link)

[http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/](http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/)

> **9\. Please talk about how to maximize CT. Is there something to a compression garment or not? would we have to be moving, like in Vasper, to get this lactic acid/HGH benefit?**
> At NASA Ames Research Park, scientist Peter Wasowski has developed an alternative exercise machine called Vasper. The user wears compression cuffs on the arms and legs and core-cooling panels on the chest and head. During exercise, the machine traps large amounts of lactic acid in the body, stimulating the pituitary gland and producing more HGH or human growth hormone.
> Vasper is a great tool but it cost some serious cash. I love it and it has been tested in deep space and the data behind it show the result is not just pure hormesis. My [CT protocol](http://www.jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/) is designed for the common man to do in their home to get results. A [compression vest](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0000722HK?ie=UTF8&creativeASIN=B0000722HK&linkCode=xm2&tag=jackru-20) and ice water work too. It will just take more time than Vasper. If you can get access to Vasper, you’d be a fool not to do it.

# Thread: Killing mechanism that can underpin Alzheimer's and Parkinson's diseases discovered
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/killing-mechanism-that-can-underpin-alzheimers-and-parkinsons-diseases-discovered.13109/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2015-02-28T19:54:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/killing-mechanism-that-can-underpin-alzheimers-and-parkinsons-diseases-discovered.13109/post-156902>

**Killing mechanism that can underpin Alzheimer's and Parkinson's diseases discovered**
_Asian News International_

02-16-15

Washington, Feb 13 ([ANI](http://www.aninews.in/)): A team of scientists has for the first time discovered a killing mechanism that could underpin a range of the most intractable neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's, Parkinson's and ALS.

The new study revealed the mechanism of toxicity of a misfolded form of the protein that underlies prion diseases, such as bovine spongiform encephalopathy ("mad cow disease") and its human equivalent, Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease.

Researcher Corinne Lasmezas from the Florida campus of The Scripps Research Institute (TSRI) said that the study reveals a novel mechanism of neuronal death involved in a neurodegenerative protein-misfolding disease.

Lasmezas added that importantly, the death of these cells is preventable and in their study, ailing neurons in culture and in an animal model were completely rescued by treatment, despite the continued presence of the toxic misfolded protein. This work suggests treatment strategies for prion diseases and possibly other protein misfolding diseases such as Alzheimer's.

In the new study, the scientists used a misfolded form of the prion disease protein, called TPrP, a model they had previously developed, to study misfolded protein-induced neurodegeneration in the laboratory. Misfolded proteins are the common cause of the group of diseases comprising prion, Alzheimer's, Parkinson's diseases, ALS and other conditions.

Using biochemical techniques, the researchers demonstrated that TPrP induces neuronal death by profoundly depleting NAD+ (nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide), a metabolite well known as a coenzyme that is common to all cells and necessary for energy production and cellular homeostasis.

Restoring NAD+ proved to be the critical factor for the rescue of neurons subjected to TPrP injury. Even when added three days after TPrP exposure, an infusion of NAD+ reversed within only a few hours the fate of neurons that had been doomed to destruction.

The study shows for the first time that a failure of NAD+ metabolism is the cause of neuronal loss following exposure to a misfolded protein, said Lasmezas.

The study is published recently in the journal Brain. ([ANI](http://www.aninews.in/))


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Possible damaged abdominal muscles from ice packs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/possible-damaged-abdominal-muscles-from-ice-packs.13112/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kathy1967 — 2015-03-01T02:21:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/possible-damaged-abdominal-muscles-from-ice-packs.13112/post-156940>

Hello all! I've just joined Dr. Kruse's site and this is my 1st time posting in Dr. Kruse's forum - I'm not a technology person so I'm not sure if I'm even doing this correctly! Feb 2nd 2015 I attempted to do some extra cold therapy in addition to the 10 minute cold showers I started in mid January. I put an ice pack directly on my lower abs instead of having a cloth barrier between my skin and the pack. I left the pack on for about 40 minutes (thought it was only about 15!) and when I removed it 2 areas under the pack were hard. Almost 1 month later those areas are still hard and feel like bruises but they aren't discolored at all. They feel like cords of rope actually. I'm very nervous about what it could be - I thought it would resolve but it hasn't yet? I'm going to call a doctor on Monday. Do any of you Dr. Kruse followers have any idea what this could be? I'm still doing cold therapy (ice baths) and I have no problems what so ever (besides an hour of shivering!) Thanks in advance for any help!

# Thread: My word definitions helper extension for this site
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-word-definitions-helper-extension-for-this-site.13114/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2015-03-01T17:30:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-word-definitions-helper-extension-for-this-site.13114/post-156974>

Hey guys,

I just finished a basic extension that I would like you to try out and give me feedback. Basically you type a word and it's definition (for example EMF = **Electromotive force**) and then when you are reading through one of Jacks Blogs it will be highlighted green. If you click it, it's definition will show. It's a handy way to remember all the terms and acronyms flying about and you can word it in a way that helps you remember/learn.

Anyway it only works on google chrome for now. If you wana try it out and leave feedback I would be grateful. Here is the link : [https://chrome.google.com/webstore/...mbpbpcdhepkihfglmhohgl/related?hl=en-GB&gl=GB](https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/jack-kruse-helper/dimgagppjhmbpbpcdhepkihfglmhohgl/related?hl=en-GB&gl=GB)

To use it just click the JH icon in your toolbar next to the search bit then to add a word to your dictionary type in the word (make sure you spell it correctly) and definition then click update. To delete a word, type in the word and then leave definition blank.

Oh and you have to refresh the page to see any words you recently added. Bit of a pain but I couldn't find an easy way around it.

Let me know what you think!

### PaulG — 2015-03-02T13:16:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-word-definitions-helper-extension-for-this-site.13114/post-157151>

Hi Sean

I think what you are trying to achieve is fantastic (A Kruse-Community cloud based custom glossary...which I have wanted for a long time) so thanks so much for working on this.

but perhaps implementation is not quite where it should be yet! .

-   How do you for example stop people entering in misleading or inaccurate definitions?
-   I cannot find a way of editing definitions once entered.
-   People are at different levels of understanding, so you may end up with a page full of green text
-   The popup window is basic and cannot support other links/videos etc.
-   Does not readily support collaboration over defining terms that are mutually understandable in plain english etc.

As an alternative, I took a look at similar extension and installed one called [Inline Search & Lookup](http://max99x.com/chrome-extensions/inline-search-and-lookup) that allows searching of terms from dictionaries and encyclopedia's. Ideally I think Glossary terms should be defined in the [wiki](http://jackkruse.wikidot.com/) that @[yewwei.tan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6824/) set up (using a glossary filter). I would prefer the extension to be able to select multiple search engines as opposed to one and return results for each source. Eg. from JK wiki, then wikipedia or allow user to choose search engine using right click mouse context menu. Have emailed the developer about this.

..my email and reply from developer
Hi, thanks for your excellent extension

I think it would be a good feature to select instead of one active search source, top n. For example if you select Dictionary.com, wikidictionary and wikipedia then in the search results box you get to see the results of all three perhaps in collapsable/expandable groups. Alternatively or in addition put the top n search engines into right click menu and you can select the one you want to use.

Hope that makes sense

Hi Paul,

I'm glad the extension has been useful for you, but I have stopped maintaining it 4 years ago now, so I'm afraid no new features or even fixes are planned. That said, the source code is public
