Some cool idea's in there, thanks Paul.

Anyway I love the idea of a community based dictionary but would be unsure how to implement it, in part, due to reasons you stated. However this extension is currently just for personal use. So you have to type in each definition you want and it's only visible on your browser. Kind of a ball ache but side steps some issues with the community based one.

To edit the definition simply enter the word in again and the new definition. More advice is in the help button.

As for the popup, yea it's basic as fuck but I have just finished updating it a bit, should go live in an hour, check it out.

You can add links but you would have to put in your own html inside the description. (for example: blah blaaah blaa emfs! blah blah <a href="[http://www.emfsarebad.com](http://www.emfsarebad.com)"> link </a>

### PaulG — 2015-03-03T03:49:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-word-definitions-helper-extension-for-this-site.13114/post-157236>

Have you tried the Google Chrome extension [ezLinkPreview](https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/ezlinkpreview/nnkcfbiefgdaceeplickkkmifpicbpcc?hl=en)? or the Firefox [CoolPreviews](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-us/firefox/addon/coolpreviews/)?

I set up ezLinkPreview with some custom searches (Wikipedia, Google, Blog, Forum, DuckDuckGo etc) and I find its quite useful when researching the blogs and forum posts.

Below is some sample URL search links

Wikipedia
[Wiki entry on Melatonin](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Melatonin)
Jack Kruse
[Jack Kruse Blogs containing the word Melatonin](http://jackkruse.com/?s=Melatonin)
[Threads by Jack with Melatonin in the title](http://forum.jackkruse.com/search/search?users=jack%20kruse&user_content=thread&title_only=1&keywords=Melatonin)
[Threads by Jack containing the word Melatonin](http://forum.jackkruse.com/search/search?users=jack%20kruse&user_content=thread&keywords=Melatonin)
[Threads with Melatonin in the title](http://forum.jackkruse.com/search/search?user_content=thread&title_only=1&keywords=Melatonin)
[Threads containing the term Melatonin](http://forum.jackkruse.com/search/search?user_content=thread&keywords=Melatonin)
Google
[Google Search](http://www.google.com/search?q=Melatonin)
[Google Video Search](http://www.google.com/search?q=Melatonin&tbm=vid)
[Google Image Search](http://www.google.com/search?q=Melatonin&tbm=lsch)
[Google News Search](http://www.google.com/search?q=Melatonin&tbm=nws)

# Thread: Optimizing-Labs-PDF.pdf - Unit of measurement
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimizing-labs-pdf-pdf-unit-of-measurement.13115/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### dantothep — 2015-03-01T18:35:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimizing-labs-pdf-pdf-unit-of-measurement.13115/post-156979>

Hi All,

Especially US forum members, when Jack wrote the PDF that is attached he forgot to include the units of measurement.

Can anyone shine a light on what they might be as it can mean quite a difference whether my labs are optimal or completely ruined:

Sex-Hormone Binding Globuline 25 or below
Estrodial (E2) <25
IGF-1 225 or above
Free T3 3.3+
Free T4 1.5+
Free Testosterone Top quartile to top 10%
TSH .5 to 1.0
Pregnenalone <130

Thanks in advance,

Dan

### dantothep — 2015-03-05T12:53:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimizing-labs-pdf-pdf-unit-of-measurement.13115/post-157500>

Anyone help? Its taken me 4 months to save the money for these tests and now I cannot even compare them, so help would be most appreciated.

### fitness@home — 2015-03-05T15:02:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimizing-labs-pdf-pdf-unit-of-measurement.13115/post-157509>

I just had thyroid labs and the ranges are:

**TSH** - 0.450-4.500 ulU/mL
**T4** - 4.5-12.0 ug/dL
**T3 Free, Serum** - 2.0-4.4 pg/mL
**RT3, Serum** - 9.2-24.1 ng/dL
**T4, Free (Direct)** - 0.82-1.77 ng/dL
**Thyoid Peroxidase (TPO) Ab** - 0-34 IU/mL
**Thyroglobulin, Antibody** - 0.0-0.9 IU/mL

### dantothep — 2015-03-05T15:36:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimizing-labs-pdf-pdf-unit-of-measurement.13115/post-157512>

Thanks thats very helpful. These seem to match up with JK's sheet.

# Thread: calling nordic european countries members
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calling-nordic-european-countries-members.13143/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kovita — 2015-03-03T12:45:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calling-nordic-european-countries-members.13143/post-157262>

Guys, it seems like that our plans to move to France are complicating a way too much and we may end up in another transit destination being somewhere around Stockholm. Obviously for me the only option is the countryside. I wonder how the nordic pace of light changes during the year affects you, how do you deal with it and whether you find in this piece of world any special benefits, that you would consider worth it to mention. I have never really been there apart of some city breaks, but my ipression is full of stunning nature and relatively unspoilt environment with good access to high quality wild fish and seafood. Would you be so kind and spend a few minutes giving me your point of view? We have to decide in a matter of days... Thanks!

### prAna303 — 2015-03-03T19:28:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calling-nordic-european-countries-members.13143/post-157320>

> I wonder how the nordic pace of light changes during the year affects you

I must have it this way... I mean, four different seasons where summer and winter is long, it makes me function because i accept nature and spend much time there. If one goes the other way like almost everyone else you open a door to hell around these places.

> how do you deal with it

Easy, suck it up and expose myself everyday to raw and naked nature. Winter is needed after summers crazy lifestyle...

> whether you find in this piece of world any special benefits

Okey, i speak for the northern parts now... N A T U R E !

I have the sea, mountains, woods just around the corner. Big parts is covered with firs and deep woods, water is great from streams, the air in the woods, wow.

> We have to decide in a matter of days.

This is tricky... Stockholm is wonderful in summer but ugly as hell in winters, why? No real nature to get a connection with. If possible you must go to the coastline and then the prices goes, wicked...

Can you expand your area from there? What will you do in Sweden? Search for the north is possible...

Swedes love technology, therefor cities are hell. Not just because EMF but also because they walk around like zombies with their gadgets, one gets depressed from that sight.

Just something from the north...

[http://jonnajinton.se/](http://jonnajinton.se/)

### kovita — 2015-03-05T20:34:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calling-nordic-european-countries-members.13143/post-157546>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=157320)

>
>
>
> Easy, suck it up and expose myself everyday to raw and naked nature. Winter is needed after summers crazy lifestyle...
>
>
>
> Okey, i speak for the northern parts now... N A T U R E !
>
> I have the sea, mountains, woods just around the corner. Big parts is covered with firs and deep woods, water is great from streams, the air in the woods, wow.
>
>
>
> This is tricky... Stockholm is wonderful in summer but ugly as hell in winters, why? No real nature to get a connection with. If possible you must go to the coastline and then the prices goes, wicked...
>
> Can you expand your area from there? What will you do in Sweden? Search for the north is possible...
>
> Swedes love technology, therefor cities are hell. Not just because EMF but also because they walk around like zombies with their gadgets, one gets depressed from that sight.
>
> Just something from the north...
>
> [http://jonnajinton.se/](http://jonnajinton.se/)

thanks a lot for your input. My husband is offerd a job in Stockholm so that is why. I was shocked by the mobile coverage map, because the south is basically lost. I feel like i would love the north, becUse I do not mind the cold and I love nature, the kids are made from the same paste like me. That would mean again that my husband and myself we have to separate. This was part of the original plan, to live for a while this way till we are able to find out how to manage job on site. Sweden is plan b because of the job offer, so if we have to think about living in different parts of Sweden, than any other reasonable place in Europe will do. And this is problem again, it is tough to look for pristine places.

Questions:
if we decide to go north, are there options for education of the kids (preschool/primary) avoiding swedish? Our kids are fluent in 4 languages now and as this suppose to be stopover this may represent a big point for decision

what about the ticks? Plenty...infected? I am sorry for this question, but walking around with lyme has been pretty tough lesson for me despite of all the good that it indirectly brought to my life.

any place you could suggest to get an idea about the possible house renting? For some reason (most likely my complete lack of swedish ;-) I am unable to find anything reasonable

despite of not being really very social myself, I like to know the people living arpund me, specially in rural areas. I feel like the people in northern Sweden must have a lot to offer due to the very particular natural conditions and relative isolation. My expwrience with Swedes is so far quite monotonous...I repeatedly emcounter people who talk different things than they think. This is sort of annoying for me since I struggle to handle this approach. I must say I only met Swedes abroad. Any call here?

### prAna303 — 2015-03-06T15:48:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calling-nordic-european-countries-members.13143/post-157632>

> [kovita said:](/goto/post?id=157546)
>
> Questions:
> if we decide to go north, are there options for education of the kids (preschool/primary) avoiding swedish? Our kids are fluent in 4 languages now and as this suppose to be stopover this may represent a big point for decision

\- Most decently big cities have schools for english but only from a certain age i think.

> what about the ticks? Plenty...infected? I am sorry for this question, but walking around with lyme has been pretty tough lesson for me despite of all the good that it indirectly brought to my life.

\- Area around Stockholm is loaded with ticks... I would say you have to go at least 150km north to avoid them. Us climbers fear them down there... For sure they carry their stuff with them, many friends suffer from them.

> any place you could suggest to get an idea about the possible house renting? For some reason (most likely my complete lack of swedish ;-) I am unable to find anything reasonable

\- I would consider a place like Östersund, a great mix of good and bad. Even Söderhamn will do great, coastline deluxe. Google these places for help with renting a place.

> despite of not being really very social myself, I like to know the people living arpund me, specially in rural areas. I feel like the people in northern Sweden must have a lot to offer due to the very particular natural conditions and relative isolation. My expwrience with Swedes is so far quite monotonous...I repeatedly emcounter people who talk different things than they think. This is sort of annoying for me since I struggle to handle this approach. I must say I only met Swedes abroad. Any call here?

\- Tricky part... If you go to big towns like Stockholm you must get a place central, otherwise you end up in social projects. When you move up, we tend to be quite laidback but also not very social. I think you must choose what you main goal is, i would go with nature and build people from that.
\- We have issues with walk the talk here... One thing for sure, city humans do this but nature connected ones, not that much. This is why a town with a connection to nature is better... The ones i love is those above, climbers, surfers, skiers and wildlife persons.

> I was shocked by the mobile coverage map, because the south is basically lost.

\- We have quite good reception all over but i would say that it is better to have good than almost... This is why the most isolated places can be bad also.

Just some eyecandy.

Östersund:
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Söderhamn:

[http://soderhamn.se/](http://soderhamn.se/)
[http://www.ostersund.se/](http://www.ostersund.se/)

>
> \- Tricky part... If you go to big towns like Stockholm you must get a place central, otherwise you end up in social projects. When you move up, we tend to be quite laidback but also not very social. I think you must choose what you main goal is, i would go with nature and build people from that.
> \- We have issues with walk the talk here... One thing for sure, city humans do this but nature connected ones, not that much. This is why a town with a connection to nature is better... The ones i love is those above, climbers, surfers, skiers and wildlife persons.
>
>
> \- We have quite good reception all over but i would say that it is better to have good than almost... This is why the most isolated places can be bad also.
>
> Just some eyecandy.
>
> Östersund:
>
>
>
> Söderhamn:
>
>
>
> Visit their homepages:
> [http://soderhamn.se/](http://soderhamn.se/)
> [http://www.ostersund.se/](http://www.ostersund.se/)

you are a treasure! Sounds like a place one could fall in love. I am more and more sure Stockholm will not be possibly for me. I would rather stay on the coast. If I understood well, there are surfing sports, than would be fantastic.

### Hope — 2015-03-11T02:01:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calling-nordic-european-countries-members.13143/post-158147>

> [kovita said:](/goto/post?id=157639)
>
> you are a treasure! Sounds like a place one could fall in love. I am more and more sure Stockholm will not be possibly for me. I would rather stay on the coast. If I understood well, there are surfing sports, than would be fantastic.

I have no answers for you Kovita, but I have several European friends who adore going to Sweden. I don't think you will have a problem finding a place to live to your liking. I think as people Swedes are more prone to like nature...and I remember traditional Swedish foods - like rotten fish - is very popular too. I have a friend's sister who moved to Stockholm as well....30 years ago. Became an MD there and never left. Loves it there. When she comes here, to the US, she always takes shrimp for some reason......

### Hope — 2015-03-11T02:07:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calling-nordic-european-countries-members.13143/post-158148>

[https://sweden.se/culture-traditions/10-things-to-know-about-swedish-food/](https://sweden.se/culture-traditions/10-things-to-know-about-swedish-food/)
Ignore the sweets, check out the fermented fish. And Lingonberries.  think Inger

Chances are you can live here for years without learning a lick of Swedish. Swedes are widely rated as world number two at English as a second language. Therefore it might take you longer to learn Swedish, and the Catch-22 is that fluency in the language is crucial to full integration. Signing up for SFI (Swedish for immigrants) could be a step in the right direction.[Learning Swedish](http://sweden.se/culture-traditions/the-challenge-of-learning-swedish/) might be a challenge, but worth the effort.

[https://sweden.se/society/20-things-to-know-before-moving-to-sweden/](https://sweden.se/society/20-things-to-know-before-moving-to-sweden/)
Check out NUMBER 7....right up your alley

I know wherever you will go, you will make an amazing adventure!

# Thread: WOOO WHOOOOO
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wooo-whooooo.13151/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nittygrittydanny — 2015-03-03T17:15:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wooo-whooooo.13151/post-157300>

Sunshine again in North Florida.

Benn cloudy/raining for about a week and cold.

Outside working today as much as possible!!!

Funny how I look at sun/water/sleep now. Full moon has been an ass kicker the past few nights.

### fitness@home — 2015-03-03T18:23:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wooo-whooooo.13151/post-157311>

Missing sunshine here in Houston... I'm jealous

### nonchalant — 2015-03-03T19:11:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wooo-whooooo.13151/post-157317>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=157311)
>
> Missing sunshine here in Houston... I'm jealous

Doesn't it seem like it's been forever, Dawn?

### fitness@home — 2015-03-03T19:17:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wooo-whooooo.13151/post-157318>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=157317)
>
> Doesn't it seem like it's been forever, Dawn?

Yes. How much snow did you see Sally? Did you take advantage of the free CT?

### nonchalant — 2015-03-03T19:42:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wooo-whooooo.13151/post-157325>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=157318)
>
> Yes. How much snow did you see Sally? Did you take advantage of the free CT?

Oh, it was great! We were getting almost an inch per hour for the 7 hours it snowed. No wind, so the snow just piled up and bent the pine trees over. Lots of broken branches, unfortunately. And we were without electricity for a day and a half! Unfortunately DH finally got the generator going, lol. But getting more well water was worth it.

I still don't have the knack of walking barefoot in the snow. Too cold! But I could easily stand barefoot in patches of cleared dirt. Fun to see how I could warm my feet up from walking in the snow with flip flops by removing the shoes and standing on the ground. And I found a zip-up vest I could use as a coat, while still having my arms and legs bare. I'm sure the neighbor thinks I'm loony, wearing the same clothes year-round.

We were outside a lot, cutting firewood. The move last year put us behind on stacking up wood. Lucky we have lots of dead trees in the woods. Just have to get them before the termites do.

### Linz — 2015-03-04T10:41:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wooo-whooooo.13151/post-157398>

Beautiful sunny morning here in UK.

I start work outside in a sleeveless jacket too. It usually comes off after a short while. Just wondering if my old cashmere jumper is ancient enough to cut off the sleeves yet! Seems a crime but it is so much more comfortable than anything else.

### nicld — 2015-03-04T16:15:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wooo-whooooo.13151/post-157419>

It is finally going to warm up here (36F) so I can get outside and sit in the sun too.

# Thread: Your Work Environment
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-work-environment.13158/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fitness@home — 2015-03-04T00:04:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-work-environment.13158/post-157350>

Call it fate, call it coincidence or call it quantum entanglement.

This morning I grabbed my Trifield meter and brought it into work again. I had already adjusted my workspace so it would be "safer" last year. We had a renovation recently and today they came to installed cable management systems under our desks. They mounted it underneath the desktop (along with the surge protector).

As soon as he finished, I took readings with the trifield meter of my desk. It was off the scale in one area that I use daily (bet you can't guess where...). I asked the installer to take it down ASAP, took pictures and sent them to Facilities to back up my decision.

I can't do much about the wifi here, but my immediate workspace is where I spend about ten hours a day, five days a week.

Check your environment people!

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

# Thread: Selecting a new computer

### Lahelada — 2015-03-04T14:16:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/selecting-a-new-computer.13172/post-157410>

Help with selection please?
Specifically relating to EMF emission,are certain designs worse? My assumptiobs are that a PC with CPU as far away as possible is best. Next best a laptop not on lap though. Do we have any jdea jf netbooks based on android are better or worse?
In other words how does one select now after all that one knows now re EMF?
I don't care about any features except for reasonable speed,i.e pages change without delay.
Any suggestions for types,makes or models ?

### Lahelada — 2015-03-04T16:38:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/selecting-a-new-computer.13172/post-157420>

Found an interesting web site. Apparently to minimise EMF some measures are quite counterintuitive. He recommends laptops with aluminium case coupled with ext monitor and keyboard. A laptop used like this on battery power is better than a desktop Pc. Check monitor for dimming circuits. If a monitor has it then dimming emits more EMF than 100% brightness. In other words f lux may not be so brilliant in such circumstances but external djmming through filters and or glasses may be more appropriate.
Full explanation here.

[http://www.conradbiologic.com/articles/ LowEMFComputing.html](http://www.conradbiologic.com/articles/%20LowEMFComputing.html)

### fitness@home — 2015-03-04T21:07:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/selecting-a-new-computer.13172/post-157443>

> In other words f lux may not be so brilliant in such circumstances but **external djmming through filters** and or glasses may be more appropriate.

I have always used a privacy filter on my monitor screens at work. It does a good job of cutting the brightness.

### Lahelada — 2015-03-04T21:23:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/selecting-a-new-computer.13172/post-157444>

Thank you. I am glad they work as it is probably the only thing I can find.

For now my set up looks like it will be netbook,sep keyboard and a monitor at a later stage. Money is still tight.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/selecting-a-new-computer.13172/post-157595>

# Thread: Blue blockers during the day (when exposed to blue light)?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-during-the-day-when-exposed-to-blue-light.13177/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 34
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2015-03-04T16:10:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-during-the-day-when-exposed-to-blue-light.13177/post-157416>

Is it a good idea to wear blue blockers during the daytime anyway except for maybe noon since that is the colour spectrum that blue light matches the closest?

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-03-04T16:12:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-during-the-day-when-exposed-to-blue-light.13177/post-157418>

..at work we are required to wear safety glasses. I could easily get yellow tinted ones.

### Lahelada — 2015-03-04T16:49:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-during-the-day-when-exposed-to-blue-light.13177/post-157421>

Mike,
Yes it is and no it isn't.....
Lol, i mean that. Let me explain
Blue light is not intrinsically bad. We use it to mark the passage of time during the day. The idea of looking at the sun in the morning is to get yoir count down clock running aacurately for when it is time to do things (thinking of body functions)and when it is time to
sleep. If you wear blue blockers all day you miss the natural dose of blue light that you need.
Having said that if your office has that bright noon time frequency fluorescent lighting it might be an idea as it is also unnatural for your body to have the sun in its zenith for that long. If gour office has a natural unassjsted daylight rythm,i.e you put on artificial light at dusk I would mimic a more natural cycle putting them on when light is starting to fade. Intuitively I would say not to use it in the mornings. Just my musings .

### Shijin13 — 2015-03-04T19:15:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-during-the-day-when-exposed-to-blue-light.13177/post-157433>

mike

I wear blue blockers during the day, but I'm also stuck in an office building lit by fluorescent lighting, and getting bombarded by wifi. My new office environment may actually be worse than the Pentagon. I've been wearing blue bockers during the day at work for the past 3&1/2 years. during that time frame I've healed a retinal tear.

### Penny — 2015-03-04T19:39:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-during-the-day-when-exposed-to-blue-light.13177/post-157436>

Mike - I would wear the blue blockers anytime you look at a screen and when it's break time - go outside - especially in the morning between 9:00-noon - even though you might feel sleepy wearing them, at least you don't wipe out your eyes - you're young but as a canary in this particular coal mine - my eyes feel instant relief when I wear the blue blockers and when I don't, they start to ache and feel dry - so eat lots of fish and do the best you can with the light - at least look at the sun in the morning - DHA and zinc are great for your eyes - if mine ache, I can get instant relief with a zinc lozenger - it sucks because you need some blue light for your clock to work, but looking at this particular frequency wrecks your eyes... eat lots of seafood Mike

When I first started programming computers, it was a black background and red or green letters - nothing as unholy as it is today - and I can remember whenever I didn't take my vitamins - Centrum at the time - my eyes would hurt - but I've been in glasses since I was 2 so I might not be the norm... even funnier, I wouldn't watch t.v. at night because "I got too rested and couldn't sleep"... never did I correlate it with the brightness of the light coming from the t.v. -

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-03-04T22:26:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-during-the-day-when-exposed-to-blue-light.13177/post-157447>

So glad someone brought this up. When I go to take exams at college, I have to go to this ridiculously cramped room littered with computers and fluorescent lighting. My eyes are extremely sensitive as it is - I mean, if I go to shovel snow, if I don't wear glasses, it almost "burns" my retinas - I get the same sensation as if I just saw the flash of a camera, where there is an after-image in your vision for awhile after; only, this lasts for minutes to hours, and my entire visual field is stained another color, like red or blue or something. Anyhow, the bright lighting, particularly rich in blue and probably green frequencies, causes massive neurological symptoms. Everything on the white paper for the exam has trails behind it, I have after images, brain fog, etc.

I was thinking of using some really dark blue light blockers specifically when going to take these exams.

Also, I kind of disagree with us needing this "natural" burst of blue light during the day. Yes, we are exposed to blue light via the sun during the day. But compare a graph of the frequencies of light, and their intensity, contained in sunlight at different hours, incandescent bulbs, CFLs/fluroescent lights, etc. and you'll see that the amount we are exposed to from artificial light (though not incandescent bulbs, candles, etc) is in no way 'natural', and we should probably shield ourselves accordingly.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-03-05T02:35:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-during-the-day-when-exposed-to-blue-light.13177/post-157472>

Thank you for all the answers!
When those CFL'S originally came out with the blue light that they call "daylight", I never liked them. I think the colour temperature is 9800K or something like that.
The "soft white" is similar looking to incandescent and I think those are 3400K. Sometimes you really have to hunt for the numbers.
I'm not advocating using CFL's, I'm just saying that when you have no choice in your lighting, maybe our bosses will be willing to change the colour temperature of the bulbs, at least where you are working. If that even makes much of a difference but it all adds up.

> mike

> I wear blue blockers during the day, but I'm also stuck in an office building lit by fluorescent lighting, and getting bombarded by wifi. Congratulations on your retinal tear success!
Thanks for the advice. I'm definitely going to get myself some blue blockers for work!

### shads — 2015-04-06T02:31:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-during-the-day-when-exposed-to-blue-light.13177/post-160370>

Ahh, Ive only just noticed that you can actually change the daytime settings on [F.lux](https://justgetflux.com/) too. It can go down to 2700K (the standard daytime setting is 6500K), so that's pretty helpful.

### shads — 2015-04-06T02:38:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-during-the-day-when-exposed-to-blue-light.13177/post-160372>

> [Jenny S said:](/goto/post?id=160366)
>
> Would you pls tell me where you do this - fairly computer illiterate!

F.lux is probably going to be the easiest way to get a decent change. Just download it from their website ([https://justgetflux.com/](https://justgetflux.com/)) and install. You'll then have a little half orange half grey circle on the right side of your windows taskbar which you can right click for the 'settings' for day and night-time lighting.

If you want to go back to default lighting you can easily just uninstall it.

### Jenny S — 2015-04-06T02:39:38+0300

> F.lux is probably going to be the easiest way to get a decent change. Just download it from their website ([https://justgetflux.com/](https://justgetflux.com/)) and install. You'll then have a little half orange half grey circle on the right side of your windows taskbar which you can right click for the options for day and night-time lighting.
>
> If you want to go back to default lighting you can easily just uninstall it.

Thanks!

### cinnamon — 2019-05-07T20:01:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-during-the-day-when-exposed-to-blue-light.13177/post-269883>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=268815)
>
> This is really hardcore, have you regularly been using darkroom during the day since this post?

No, not darkroom mode. I have f.lux set at 800K both day and night (except when I occasionally need to see colours). For those who might not know, you can use the keyboard shortcut Alt-Shift-Page Down to choose a setting below the standard ones - you can go as low as 800K.

I also keep the screen dimmed as much as it will let me.

I don't use my blue blocking glasses during the day but I always add them at night, in addition to the f.lux.

Hardcore? Nah, hardcore would be throwing out the laptop! hehehe

### mrc — 2019-05-13T18:18:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blockers-during-the-day-when-exposed-to-blue-light.13177/post-270340>

Dr. Kruse's always recommended iris & not flux due to not just blue light, but flicker rate as mentioned in many free podcasts.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?search/22066685/&q=iris&o=date](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?search/22066685/&q=iris&o=date)

# Thread: What in the perplexity is my body doing?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-in-the-perplexity-is-my-body-doing.13178/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### takenoadvice — 2015-03-04T16:11:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-in-the-perplexity-is-my-body-doing.13178/post-157417>

What in the perplexity is my body doing? Looking for any feedback on data below. This is why I ask, below are my weights per day:

Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, 213.4, 213.4, 212.6, 212.6,
210.8, Monday, 213.6, 211.6, 211.0, 208.8, 208.0,
207.6, 208.4, 208.2, 207.2, 208.2, 206.4, 204.6,
206.6, 206.1, 205.8, 204.8, Thursday, Friday, Saturday
EOY2014: 215.6

First slot listed is Sunday, February 8, 2015. I put in day of week for when I did not weigh myself. I weigh normally in the morning.

My scale is consistent, usually max .2 pounds difference in repeated weighings. I started trying to reset about 3 weeks ago, after coming across Dr. Kruse on a YouTube interview, and having decided over a month ago to get into a paleo diet. So I'm ASTONISHED at my results so far. I haven't seen myself below 206 pounds IN ABOUT 12 YEARS lowest floor I'd hit before was around 208/209 some years back when I was lifting weights very regularly. Above I'm figuring about 9 lbs loss in 3 weeks.

My diet is roughly:
breakfast: 2 eggs, 2-3 pieces bacon, sauteed kale or spinach (coconut oil), perhaps chicken/turkey
lunch: salad of lettuce &| spinach, sometimes some carrot, chicken/turkey/hot dog, coconut flakes, milled flaxseed, balsamic vinaigrette
dinner: salad of lettuce &| spinach, sometimes some carrot, chicken/turkey/hot dog, coconut flakes, milled flaxseed, balsamic vinaigrette
Throw in occasional burger or pork chop above. Not had in 3 weeks: no dairy or milk cheese, no legumes, no non-dairy milk substitute, no fast food, no bread or other grains, no corn, no potatoes, no honey or sugar added to coffee, no cookies/donuts, no bananas.
I feel like I'm eating a lot of food at each meal. Not sure how many calories a day but I'm thinking 1700-2200, weekends I tend to eat a great deal more of turkey / chicken, also been making turkey soup stock and having that. I have coffee most days, usually keeping to less than 4-5 cups a day, which is way less than my previous norms. Sometimes chai tea. Sometimes sip a very small amount of light almond milk. Trying to drink more water. Sometimes put salmon / sardines / tilapia in salad. Indulging in a diet soft drink every so often (but interest in that is diminishing). On an online calculator, don't remember which one, it estimated me at 1750 calories consumption a day for my age and sedentary occupation, and it projected about 3-4 lbs fat loss first month on a regimen of 25/100/139 grams of carbohydrate/protein/fat respectively.

My habit is roughly:
up usually at 6, eating by 6:30
lunch at 12 or 1, also walk for half hour
dinner at 5, sometimes 6,
bed at 10-11 -- turning off lights

My hunger cravings have diminished, esp in past 7 days. I went crazy on 2 bags of cranberry/raisin/sunflower seed about 2 weeks ago and finished one bag in just a day, about 13x140 cal portions. No interest in finishing the remnants in the 2nd bag. I am beginning to eat less than what I've written here, as I feel full all the time. Only maybe twice have I eaten after 7 pm, and only once less than 4 hours before bed. Prior to 3-4 weeks ago, my diet was okay most days, but weekends brought fast food, also movie popcorn/candy.

I am beginning to sweat more. Not much. Overall for me, very little.

My next plans: 1) try to get in more vegetative variety. 2) add more variety of seafood. 3) study up more on this site. 4) add in some exercise in the evening, mainly kettlebells and calisthenics.

So ... WHAT IS MY BODY DOING? Not written above, but I weighed just before bed last night at 206.2, slept about 6 hours, and this morning I was 204.8. Numbers re-weighed both times. Wait -- WHAT? One lbs drop over night? WHAT IS THIS .... ?

What's the most important factor in what I'm doing right now?


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: started progesterone lozenges/troches...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-progesterone-lozenges-troches.13192/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-05T15:58:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-progesterone-lozenges-troches.13192/post-157515>

Hi everyone. I recently finally started the progesterone lozenges / troches that my gyn and compounding pharmacist decided would benefit me. I am to let one troche dissolve between my cheek and gum twice daily on days 12 - 26. I picked them up yesterday, on day 13, and began immediately.

(In case anyone wonders, it was covered by my insurance, I have no idea how or why but I'm thrilled! But if it wasn't they said it would cost me $45 per month supply.)

The dosage is 25 mg per dose, so 50mg per day.

This is brand new to me and I do not know if there is an optimal time of day to use them (other than approx. 12 hours apart), whether there are other things I ought to be aware/cautious of while using them (aside from avoid soy at all times), whether this is an average starting dose, whether there is a test I can/should do at some point to see how it's working, etc.

I would be very grateful for any input anyone can give me regarding these lozenges and/or progesterone in general.

Also, this month my period has gone insane for the first time ever. I am 42. I wonder if it is a symptom of peri-menopouse? The issue is, my period came 8 days early last month on February 5th and lasted the normal 5 days, thru Feb 10th. Then it came back on Feb 20th and has lasted with varying heaviness all the way through at least yesterday (which was 13 days of bleeding at that point). I have consulted my gyn a few times and we're keeping an eye on it for now since there's no pain and it's not heavy or anything. But I just wonder if this is typical of peri-menopouse, or what?

And, whatever it is, might progesterone help it to stop already? \*fingers crossed\* Thanks!

### fitness@home — 2015-03-05T19:29:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-progesterone-lozenges-troches.13192/post-157541>

Hope,

I take my progesterone in the evening.

Some say it helps you sleep better. Not sure if that's true or not...

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-06T13:53:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-progesterone-lozenges-troches.13192/post-157629>

Thanks fitness@home.. mine are to be taken 12 hours apart so I can't do that, personally.

But now I have another question for everyone:

Has anyone had any luck getting their lozenges switched out for some other mode of delivery? I don't have an hour per day to dedicate to progesterone and it's taking at least that long doing it this way. Combine the time and discomfort necessary (shoving a little rectangle between my gum and cheek is killing me, probably causing a sore on my gum by this time) with the absolutely horrible taste and this is gonna be short-lived if I have to keep doing it this way.

I have experimented with putting it in different locations in my mouth and some are less painful than others, but those are the ones where it keeps sliding upwards and onto my tongue and won't stay put.

But these take over half an hour to dissolve each time so that's an hour per day of avoiding people because it looks like I have chewing tobacco in my cheek, and that whole time I am guessing I'm not supposed to eat or drink anything which I can't do because first of all it tastes HORRIBLE so I have to drink every few seconds to wash the liquid down as it dissolves, and second of all because I don't GO that long without drinking something ever, it would be just about impossible to do.

It took from December 31 until March 3 to even finally get these things though, I don't want to have to wait until June to finally get them to give me something else that isn't disgusting and inconvenient. So is there progesterone I can just order myself online and use instead?

These are only 50mg per day anyway which I can't imagine being enough, and they're only for 2 weeks out of my cycle which I think probably also isn't going to work for me.

Thanks for any input any of you can provide. I feel like I'm barely treading water these days with this stuff. I've got a hairy upper lip starting on top of everything else and it's just got to stop.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-06T14:03:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-progesterone-lozenges-troches.13192/post-157630>

hey I finally realized what these actually taste like (which sure as heck isn't "vanilla" which they said the flavoring would be) - it's black licorice with a vanilla overtone. So so foul. I'm considering just chewing them like a hard candy and swallowing them from now on, or swallowing them whole. Will either option do ANYTHING? This is kinda like being forced into morning sickness twice a day. Which surely doesn't make me want to have sex, which is the whole point of going on this stuff.

### nicld — 2015-03-06T23:19:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-progesterone-lozenges-troches.13192/post-157663>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=157541)
>
> Hope,
>
> I take my progesterone in the evening.
>
> Some say it helps you sleep better. Not sure if that's true or not...

I will be going back to taking all of my Pg (on cream) in the evening and let you know if it helps. Last month I went back to what my doc has prescribed 1X in the morning and 1X in the evening and sleep when to crap 2nd half of cycle. This time I have been trying to do all in morning and that has not helped so tomorrow night, all at night.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-07T14:29:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-progesterone-lozenges-troches.13192/post-157734>

Something I noticed is that if I drink coffee with them they're the most tolerable. I just have to take them at a time of day that I can go grab a caramel latte at dunkin donuts to sip while this dissolves and I should be okay with the flavor and stuff.

Something even more interesting - last night I had hot flashes while sleeping. Not the full body ones I've had a couple of times a year or so ago, but all under the back of my hair and around my neck. That makes me wonder could it be the progesterone (well sure, it COULD be, but has anyone else gotten hot flashes while adjusting /going on pg?) and also makes me wonder if progesterone will affect my thyroid in some way, since I've got hashi's but no doctor/meds/plan for controlling it yet.

I'd love to get that thing turned back on so that I'm not always cold and lethargic and inflamed and depressed.

### nicld — 2015-03-07T19:38:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-progesterone-lozenges-troches.13192/post-157756>

Sometimes things get worse before they get better. Hang in there.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-11T16:02:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-progesterone-lozenges-troches.13192/post-158206>

Thanks! I'm still having hot flashes at night from the progesterone. Now am having them a couple of hours after taking the daytime dose as well. Not a big deal, just an observation. My goal with my hormones now is to control my Hashi's without needing thyroid meds or an endocrinologist. The tech who did my pelvic ultrasound yesterday doesnt see anything funky to explain the bleeding and says it should stop soon based on what she saw (thin lining). Hope so. But she also said that she thinks that hormones MIGHT all actually relate to people's HEALTH and that doctors need to realize that.

I wasn't sure what to say. hehe

# Thread: Semiconductors conduct better when heated says literature
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/semiconductors-conduct-better-when-heated-says-literature.13209/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2015-03-07T10:07:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/semiconductors-conduct-better-when-heated-says-literature.13209/post-157716>

What do I miss?

[http://www.pveducation.org/pvcdrom/pn-junction/conduction-in-semiconductors](http://www.pveducation.org/pvcdrom/pn-junction/conduction-in-semiconductors)

Is says the opposite. Not cold increases conduction but heat.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-03-07T23:28:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/semiconductors-conduct-better-when-heated-says-literature.13209/post-157796>

If we're talking about Intrinsic Semiconductors, then yes, in general you will see more conductivity with increasing heat. Simple explanation already stated on the site you linked to -- higher heat means more kinetic for electrons to jump the Band Gap.

Extrinsic semiconductors can behave very differently, especially those in lattice structures. Something that the CPU in my computer will generally perform better at lower temps, because phonon scattering ([http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Phonon](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Phonon)) is the limiting factor when it comes to electron mobility in the lattice. Phonon Scattering is reduced at lower temps, and allows for more electron mobility (especially in N-type semiconductors if I'm not wrong).

As for how exactly the body's semiconductors behave, I'll need to read more . Thankfully Robert Becker already wrote a whole book called 'The Body Electric' detailing these effects. I've ordered it and will get back to this thread once I've read it through.

...

### Butters — 2015-03-08T11:42:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/semiconductors-conduct-better-when-heated-says-literature.13209/post-157828>

Yeah I read the book. I am at page 160 or so but he just mentioned it that we are made out of semiconductors but nothing about the effects of cold and heat so far. Hoping he is getting there.

Sorry its in spanish, but basically it says that there will be internet all over the planet whit these new "flying machines", that provide internet EVERYWHERE on the planet. That means more nnEMF even in the lonely valleys of Mongolia. That means NO ESCAPE

### NeilBB — 2015-03-07T18:51:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally.13216/post-157749>

> [Erik said:](/goto/post?id=157740)

>
> Sorry its in spanish, but basically it says that there will be internet all over the planet whit these new "flying machines", that provide internet EVERYWHERE on the planet. That means more nnEMF even in the lonely valleys of Mongolia. That means NO ESCAPE

As someone who has been alive and pessimistic a lot longer than you, this is what I think:

Overall future looks bleak but many plot twists are still possible.

Despairing today over what may or may not happen tomorrow won't help you or anyone else.

If the subjects that you are focusing on now are too overwhelming to you, then narrow your focus down to a smaller realm in which you can effect some small positive change. Your range of focus and influence can increase over time. You are young.

Enjoy your life. We are all going to die one day and so will this planet. No matter what. Que sera, sera. To everything, there is a season...

# Thread: Decreased Brain Resilience Syndrome (DBR)...Nice Name
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decreased-brain-resilience-syndrome-dbr-nice-name.13219/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2015-03-07T21:09:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decreased-brain-resilience-syndrome-dbr-nice-name.13219/post-157781>

> **Abstract**
> The number of sports-related concussions has been steadily rising in recent years. Diminished brain resilience syndrome is a term coined by the lead author to describe a particular physiological state of nutrient functional deficiency and disrupted homeostatic mechanisms leading to increased susceptibility to previously considered innocuous concussion. **_We discuss how modern day environmental toxicant exposure, along with major changes in our food supply and lifestyle practices, profoundly reduce the bioavailability of neuro-critical nutrients such that the normal processes of homeostatic balance and resilience are no longer functional._** Their diminished capacity triggers physiological and biochemical ‘work around’ processes that result in undesirable downstream consequences. Exposure to certain environmental chemicals, particularly glyphosate, the active ingredient in the herbicide, Roundup®, may disrupt the body's innate switching mechanism, which normally turns off the immune response to brain injury once danger has been removed. Deficiencies in serotonin, due to disruption of the shikimate pathway, may lead to impaired melatonin supply, which reduces the resiliency of the brain through reduced antioxidant capacity and alterations in the cerebrospinal fluid, reducing critical protective buffering mechanisms in impact trauma. Depletion of certain rare minerals, overuse of sunscreen and/or overprotection from sun exposure, as well as overindulgence in heavily processed, nutrient deficient foods, further compromise the brain's resilience. Modifications to lifestyle practices, if widely implemented, could significantly reduce this trend of neurological damage.
>
> **Keywords:** Chronic traumatic encephalopathy, glyphosate, neurotoxins, postconcussion syndrome, sports-related concussion

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4093745/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4093745/)

### Josh — 2015-03-07T21:40:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decreased-brain-resilience-syndrome-dbr-nice-name.13219/post-157782>

I wonder if they are reading Dr. Kruse's stuff..

> However, we need to introduce early the key argument that is foundational to our hypothesis, which centers on the critical molecules, DHA and cholesterol sulfate (CS). DHA is the most abundant omega-3 fatty acid in the brain and retina,\[[46](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4093745/#ref46)\] and it has an ancient lineage related to photoreceptors.\[[47](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4093745/#ref47)\] There is an association between a high presence of CS in plasma membranes and a high concentration of DHA.\[[177](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4093745/#ref177)\] Because CS is more polar than cholesterol due to its sulfate moiety, it extends further into the aqueous environment surrounding the membrane bilayer, which allows its tetracyclic ring system to fit snugly into the pocket of DHA, in direct contrast to unsulfated cholesterol. The sulfate anion attached in this way directly to the membrane complex can produce a protective gelled region of “structured water” surrounding the DHA, where electrons activated by light can be trapped and then directed towards the zinc-containing cavity of an endothelial nitric oxide synthase (eNOS) or a neuronal NOS (nNOS) molecule, attached to the membrane at the lipid raft. Seneff _et al_.\[[181](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4093745/#ref181)\] have argued that sunlight induces the synthesis of sulfate by eNOS through such mechanisms, based on the similarity of the eNOS molecular structure to sulfate-synthesizing enzymes in microbes. This allows cholesterol to be sulfoconjugated to renew CS supplies. If these arguments are valid, then the ability to synthesize additional sulfate depends upon an adequate supply of CS as well as DHA. Thus, disruption of the supply of sulfate, as well as cholesterol and the essential omega-3 fatty acid, DHA, has a cascade effect, including changes in the density of the aqueous environment and subsequently increased vulnerability to sudden impact and/or rotational forces.....
>
> ....Here, we introduce a novel concept that the pineal gland, a tiny gland in the center of the brain, appears central to the pathology associated with PCS and related conditions, most critically with impact trauma. Specifically, deficiencies in heparan sulfate (HS) dramatically impair the buffering ability of the brain against impact injury due to insufficiently gelled water in the cerebrospinal fluid (CSF). We argue that the pineal gland responds to light stimuli by producing sulfate, and that the sulfate, conjugated to melatonin, is distributed throughout the brain via the CSF. Sulfate plays a critical role in buffering the brain against physical impact, and in degrading and recycling damaged proteins and mitochondria. The pineal gland is outside of the blood–brain barrier (BBB), and it is profusely supplied with blood, second only to the kidney. This makes it vulnerable to exposure to environmental toxicants. Thus, impaired sulfate supply, due to impaired function of the pineal gland, might explain the increased sensitivity to concussion and inability to recover from concussion observed in modern times.

### Josh — 2015-03-07T21:51:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decreased-brain-resilience-syndrome-dbr-nice-name.13219/post-157784>

Has me thinking about how buoyancy and Archimedes principle apply here at the macro and micro architecture levels. Loss of mass equivalence=DBR at all levels.....

> **A ROLE FOR SULFATES IN LOAD RESISTANCE**
> The glycocalyx is a negatively charged complex network of proteoglycans, glycoproteins, and glycolipids that decorates the plasma membrane of most cells.\[[207](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4093745/#ref207)\] The GAGs in the matrix are sulfated in an irregular pattern, and the health of the glycocalyx lining the vasculature depends on adequate sulfate content.\[[41](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4093745/#ref41)\] Responses of cells to signaling molecules become impaired when sulfate is depleted in the extracellular matrix.\[[144](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4093745/#ref144)\] The sulfated lipids are known as sulfatides, and they are prominent in the myelin sheath.\[[85](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4093745/#ref85)\] Sulfatides have been found to be severely depleted in Alzheimer's brains postmortem, even in the early stages.\[[69](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4093745/#ref69)\] This clearly suggests that impaired sulfate supply is a factor in AD.
>
> Sulfation, the last step in the synthesis of GAGs, consumes two ATP molecules for every sulfate ion attached to the GAG chain. There must be something highly significant about the need for such sulfation, given its costly energy requirements. Haskin _et al_.\[[71](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4093745/#ref71)\] proposed that an important purpose for sulfation can be found by considering the unusual property of sulfate anions to preferentially attach to potassium counterions rather than sodium, the preferred ion for both phosphates and carbonates. The result is a significant dewetting of the GAG molecule, resulting in significantly less water adhering to the extracellular matrix; that is, a smaller fluid/solid ratio. These authors explained as follows: “Areas with a larger fluid/solid ratio would undergo greater deformations in response to compressive loading and larger volumes of fluid would be forced in and out of the tissue during loading cycles. This would likely result in greater matrix destruction in areas with higher water content.”\[[71](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4093745/#ref71)\]
>
> We propose that the same concept applies in the brain; specifically, in the ventricular system that houses the CSF. The ependyma is the thin epithelial membrane that lines the ventricular system of the brain and spinal cord, part of whose role is to produce CSF. It has a thick glycocalyx, extending into the ventricular fluid area, which is likely to provide mechanical buffering to the delicate brain tissues.\[[165](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4093745/#ref165)\] The ventricles normally contain significant amounts of HS. A mouse model of autism revealed ventricle wall reduction and loss of HSs,\[[133](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4093745/#ref133)\] and postmortem analyses of brains of individuals with autism have also revealed a reduced amount of HS in the ventricles compared with normal age-matched controls.\[[157](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4093745/#ref157)\] It was proposed that aberrant extracellular matrix GAG function in the subventricular zone of the lateral ventricles is a potential biomarker for autism.

# Thread: Germans win coveted brain prize
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/germans-win-coveted-brain-prize.13252/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2015-03-10T18:43:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/germans-win-coveted-brain-prize.13252/post-158106>

[http://www.germany.info/Vertretung/usa/en/\_\_pr/P\_\_Wash/2015/03/09-Brainprize.html](http://www.germany.info/Vertretung/usa/en/__pr/P__Wash/2015/03/09-Brainprize.html)

Well, Americans won it too.....

So cool. They're using - wait for it - LIGHT - to look at individual brain cells.

Two-photon microscopy is a method of brain scanning that uses light to highlight regions of the brain. As a tool, it is similar to an MRI or CAT-scan. However, using the two-photon method, scientists are now able to look at much more concentrated areas. In fact, Konnerth, Tank, Svoboda and Denk have even used two-photon microscopy to examine individual brain cells, something previously considered impossible.

Wish they knew the principles we learn here.

# Thread: Melatonin Production... Light/Dark Cycle or Brain Wave State?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melatonin-production-light-dark-cycle-or-brain-wave-state.13254/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JC1980 — 2015-03-11T01:57:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melatonin-production-light-dark-cycle-or-brain-wave-state.13254/post-158146>

I'm curious about this topic because while it's rather common to assume that internal Melatonin production increases as darkness increases, there also seems to be a correlation with sleep cycles and brain wave states.

As most of you are quite aware there are five brain wave states commonly acknowledged by the public which are as follows.

**Gamma** - 27Hz and higher (REM Sleep)

**Beta** - 12Hz to 27Hz (90% Majority of Waking Hours)

**Alpha** - 8Hz to 12Hz (Relaxed, transition towards sleep or meditative/hypnotic state)

**Theta** - 4Hz to 8Hz (Light Sleep)

**Delta** - 0Hz to 4Hz (Deep Sleep)

Being that this lifestyle in 2015 is largely comprised of running around like a rat in a maze, we are predominantly in Beta brain waves from morning until the evening. However, when it comes to Delta waves and Gamma waves associated with deep sleep, there appears to be a correlation with peak Melatonin production as well.

My question is... is Melatonin production strictly a light/dark cycle phenom or can it be manipulated throughout the light cycle if a slower brain wave state is induced? Knowing the importance of Melatonin production on cellular & systemic function it would appear that being able to induce elevated production in the middle of the day might be a good thing?

@[ssj3](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9728/) has also found it effective

# Thread: Why might I think I fear CT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-might-i-think-i-fear-ct.13268/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty_M — 2015-03-11T17:30:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-might-i-think-i-fear-ct.13268/post-158218>

I have been as strict as possible epi-paleo for the last few months with some small deviation. I intend to remain ketogenic, epi-paleo, w/the Leptin Rx and Leaky Gut Rx until things improve a bit more (I have a journal thread if you want to know where I'm coming from).

I began CT with the face dunks for 3 weeks, about 5-7 dunks per session, averaging around 1:00-1:30 per dunk, with one session a day. I loved it, and added 14 lbs. of ice to my chest each night. After I was up to doing 10-15 dunks, averaging 1:30 each, sometimes nearing 3:00, and could do an hour with the two 7lb. ice bags on my bare chest (about 1.5 weeks), I decided to take to the bath.

It has been very cold where I am, outside of Philadelphia, so the tap water comes out around 42 F. I really just have to take the leap and put my foot in, get my torso under, and shiver. It is not too bad after I get in. The worst part is before getting in, the anticipation. I am not sure why.

It has been only three days, and each bath I went about 30 minutes torso under, and 10 minutes legs under, because once I warm up (after about 10 minutes), I feel like I could stay in for a bit. Then, when I come out, I shiver for about 30 minutes to an hour. I haven't noticed a significant difference in how I feel (yet), though I didn't expect to for a little bit.

The problem is that I am sort of "afraid" of getting into the tub, so it takes a lot of self-pressure to do something that I know I want to do anyway. Even now, hours before filling the tub, it makes me pretty nervous to think about.

How long might it take until I am adapted so the baths don't have to feel so cold? (or does the immense cold feeling not go away?)

Also, would going up a few degrees in temperature make a difference in feeling? I feel like it will be cold anyway so after getting in I would have preferred the colder temps.

Any advice? It just makes me nervous thinking about.

P.S. I do wear socks, gloves, and a hat while in the tub.

Thanks,

\- Matt

### shah78 — 2015-03-11T17:53:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-might-i-think-i-fear-ct.13268/post-158222>

Three years and over two thousand "dunks" later, I STILL NEVER enjoy the first three seconds. So be it. The next 5,000 seconds are not so bad . ..... More often than not there is a slight nervousness before each and every initial dunk. It has never gone away. I don't think it ever will. It gets better in summer. Duh? What a surprise. ... now go jump in!

### fitness@home — 2015-03-11T19:32:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-might-i-think-i-fear-ct.13268/post-158232>

Here's what I did when using a bathtub. I started by running just an inch or so of _lukewarm_ water and sit in it. Then I started turning the water to cold. It helped take the edge off so to speak. Much easier to turn on colder water when you are already sitting in some water.

Of course, I can only do that down here in the winter. In the summer our "cold" tap water is 70+ degrees.

### shah78 — 2015-03-11T19:45:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-might-i-think-i-fear-ct.13268/post-158234>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=158232)
>
> Here's what I did when using a bathtub. I started by running just an inch or so of _lukewarm_ water and sit in it. Then I started turning the water to cold. It helped take the edge off so to speak. Much easier to turn on colder water when you are already sitting in some water.
>
> Of course, I can only do that down here in the winter. In the summer our "cold" tap water is 70+ degrees.

You jarred my memory. I did that "death by a thousand cuts" for the first few weeks. I thought it was very useful.

### nicld — 2015-03-11T20:05:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-might-i-think-i-fear-ct.13268/post-158242>

It does get better in the summer, especially when you can do CT then go outside and rewarm in the sun naked

### Matty_M — 2015-03-11T21:00:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-might-i-think-i-fear-ct.13268/post-158252>

> [shah78 said:](/goto/post?id=158222)
>
> Three years and over two thousand "dunks" later, I STILL NEVER enjoy the first three seconds. So be it. The next 5,000 seconds are not so bad . ..... More often than not there is a slight nervousness before each and every initial dunk. It has never gone away. I don't think it ever will. It gets better in summer. Duh? What a surprise. ... now go jump in!

Thanks! This is really motivational.

Once cold adapted, do the baths feel better?

\- Matt

### shah78 — 2015-03-11T22:42:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-might-i-think-i-fear-ct.13268/post-158259>

The baths felt good from the get go . They got colder and longer and more frequent, but they don't really "feel any better".

### Matty_M — 2015-03-12T02:57:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-might-i-think-i-fear-ct.13268/post-158296>

> [shah78 said:](/goto/post?id=158259)
>
> The baths felt good from the get go . They got colder and longer and more frequent, but they don't really "feel any better".

I just want to get to the point where I can be like that Sherpa that Dave Asprey references often, who walked into a freezing stream in a light jacket, and took Dave's offered jacket and put it into his bag, as he "wasn't very cold".

How long might it take to become this adapted? I'm down for it.

### nicld — 2015-03-12T15:04:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-might-i-think-i-fear-ct.13268/post-158355>

It all depends how your redox is, your environment, your hormones and diet. Some adapt quickly some it takes much longer. For me, I started CT in the summer and by that winter I was better able to handle the cold of winter and started doing deep CT by adding snow to my tub.

Just keep doing it and go at a pace that works for you but still challenges you once in a while.

### Matty_M — 2015-03-13T13:55:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-might-i-think-i-fear-ct.13268/post-158431>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=158355)
>
> It all depends how your redox is, your environment, your hormones and diet. Some adapt quickly some it takes much longer. For me, I started CT in the summer and by that winter I was better able to handle the cold of winter and started doing deep CT by adding snow to my tub.
>
> Just keep doing it and go at a pace that works for you but still challenges you once in a while.

Thanks!

\- Matt

# Thread: Lab Result Intepretation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-result-intepretation.13280/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LMJ21224 — 2015-03-12T22:28:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-result-intepretation.13280/post-158390>

Hello Everyone,

I had a thyroid "panel" ran back in October. My doctor noted the following:

\-Low Cortisol
\-Low Pregnenolone
\-Low DHEA
\-Low Free T3
\-Low Iodine
\-TSH was in the lower range at 1.04

Additionally, A1C was 5.6 and fasting glucose was 101. I am at least 30 lbs overweight. I was under some mild stress that morning to get to the place since it was a new location for me. I may or may not have been to the gym that morning. I can't remember honestly.

I do drink a lot of Diet Pepsi and I was following Carb Nite at the time, but I know I was overdoing carbs and calories on the Carb Nite. Lastly, I do tend to run out of energy right after work and then seem to get somewhat of a second wind right about when I want to go to bed.

I was taking 50 mg dose of Zinc to help with suspected pyrolurria, which was later confirmed. I know I was taking other supplements at the time but I honestly don't remember which ones.

Thanks

all roads lead to circadian mismatch and leptin resistance

# Thread: Vancouver Talks: The Aftermath
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vancouver-talks-the-aftermath.13296/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Clayton — 2015-03-13T18:42:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vancouver-talks-the-aftermath.13296/post-158456>

So I just wanted to post a blurb here detailing the talk Jack gave last weekend at my school's symposium. To say the least I think people were shocked...not only by the topics he addressed but by the delivery. I have had the pleasure of seeing Jack speak live twice now and both times have been electric. Jack was the third and final speaker but had sufficiently introduced himself to the audience members well before his time to speak. In the open question period for each speaker he grabbed the attention of the audience with some boundary pushing questions for each speaker (those being Pollack and Harry Massey). He definitely stimulated some new ideas for Pollack and had him very engaged. All while wearing a flashy blue and white affliction shirt I might add..which added to the affect. Between speakers several people came up to me asking who the "intense guy in the affliction shirt was". I don't think anyone in that crowd had been in the presence of someone like Jack before. His personality is larger than life and he is magnetic. Even if people didn't like the message they were hearing they could not look away

I think from the evening before leaving NOLA to returning on Sunday he didn't go a day without some obstacle. He had a late evening on the thursday before leaving due to work committments, his plane hit a flock of birds taking off from Dallas and was delayed four hours...the hotel gave him a room up on the 30th floor..the bastards!! To top it off his slide show got erased and he didn't know until minutes before presenting but he just went with it and still blew everyone's mind, both students and faculty members.

I have had lots of people approach me this week about the talk and several people from lower years told me that Jack's presentation was all their classes talked about on Monday. People are now texting me pictures of the oysters they just picked up and asking me about SN-2 DHA so he definitely stimulated some discussion and got people thinking. I have not been on campus much this week because I do most of my clinical training at clinics that are off-site so I haven't had a chance to talk to everyone but I did run into my school dean and I could tell Jack had shook up his paradigm.

It's an experience in itself to hang around Jack in person. He gives everyone some of his time and is just a regular guy even though he is extraordinary. I would encourage anyone to meet with him live if ever given the opportunity. The conversation is never strictly about science and medicine..I have been part of fascinating talks with Jack that go well beyond that. Anyone who is going on the cruise this June is in for a treat.

### endless — 2015-03-13T18:57:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vancouver-talks-the-aftermath.13296/post-158459>

I'm glad to hear that people at the school are talking about it...I was wondering how many of them 'got it', or even some of it. I said to Sarah beside me that I was tempted to sit with my back to the stage while Jack was speaking so I could watch the crowd!

### Clayton — 2015-03-13T19:07:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vancouver-talks-the-aftermath.13296/post-158461>

According to Jack a lot of jaws were dropping

### caroline — 2015-03-14T04:41:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vancouver-talks-the-aftermath.13296/post-158492>

Definitely know the feeling ......

### caroline — 2015-03-14T04:41:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vancouver-talks-the-aftermath.13296/post-158493>

How was Pollack's talk?

### kovita — 2015-03-14T08:16:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vancouver-talks-the-aftermath.13296/post-158500>

it is great to hear that people paid attention and got in thoughtfull mode. i am always very happy to see people leaving the comfort of everydays dogmas because I still vividly remember how great move it was for me. There will still be about 90% of "fall off" with a bit of silly reasonds and sometimes even strong words ;-) But 10% is good enough to move on. my husband just got a job in strategy of IOT future, I have high hopes he is part of the 10% now ;-)

### endless — 2015-03-14T18:22:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vancouver-talks-the-aftermath.13296/post-158526>

I liked Pollack's talk...he's a good speaker and it made sense of some of the things I'd read in his book that I was having trouble grasping. The way the speakers were ordered was perfect...each one built upon the last, sort of. I think if Jack had spoken alone without the other two leading up to him fewer students would have understood.

# Thread: 2 small success stories; 1) chronic elbow pain, 2) neck pain
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-small-success-stories-1-chronic-elbow-pain-2-neck-pain.13313/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### billy — 2015-03-15T21:30:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-small-success-stories-1-chronic-elbow-pain-2-neck-pain.13313/post-158579>

1) Elbow pain for years after hyper-extending it and later beginning weight lifting using heavy compound movements.

Solution; Tried many cures. But eliminating nightshades and wheat seemed key. Also, I went to high fat/low carb diet for 7 months ...until my LDL shot way up.
Also- I switched to Serge Nubret's pump training;
[https://www.t-nation.com/training/serge-nubret-pump-training](https://www.t-nation.com/training/serge-nubret-pump-training)

This site was helpful and got me onto Collagen 1 & 3 as well;
[http://www.tendinosis.org/index.shtml](http://www.tendinosis.org/index.shtml)

Gone!

2) Chronic neck pain on my back left side. My ND didn't know what it could be, suggested a scan.

Solution; After looking at this...
[http://what-when-how.com/neuroscien...ization-of-the-central-nervous-system-part-3/](http://what-when-how.com/neuroscience/the-spinal-cord-organization-of-the-central-nervous-system-part-3/)
...I see that I must have been overtraining and taxed my CNS (the ventral \[anterior\] spinocerebellar tract must have been pissed off).

I took more rest days and shortened my workouts from the 1.5 hour pump training routine to 45-60 minutes. Studies indicate cortisol rises after 60 minutes anyway and I'm natty... so need to watch that.

Gone!

Hope this helps.

# Thread: Bioelectric Fields, Not the Genome, Spark Morphological Changes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bioelectric-fields-not-the-genome-spark-morphological-changes.13314/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-03-15T21:49:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bioelectric-fields-not-the-genome-spark-morphological-changes.13314/post-158581>

This is old news (studied in the 1920's in Europe and Russia), but new news for many in the "mainstream". Still nice to see it being explored, though: [http://www.nature.com/news/bioelectric-signals-spark-brain-growth-1.17087#/b1](http://www.nature.com/news/bioelectric-signals-spark-brain-growth-1.17087#/b1)

Just some snippits:

**"Over the years, Michael Levin’s lab at Tufts University in Medford, Massachusetts, has created a carnival’s worth of zoological oddities: a flatworm with four heads, a frog with six legs, a tadpole with a functioning third eye on its tail. But what is perhaps most striking about his curious menagerie is that the biologist has engineered many of these animals not by mutating their genomes but by altering the electrical gradients that cells use to communicate with one another.**"

"By modulating those voltage gradients, the researchers were able to stimulate the growth of extra brain tissue, even compensating for brain defects caused by a genetic mutation."

"The London pharmaceutical giant GlaxoSmithKline recently announced that it was launching a program to explore _‘electroceuticals’_ — an approach that would use electrical signals to restore health. But those programs are focused on using electricity to alter the activity of nerves, not other types of cells or organ development, notes Levin."

Now think about all the changes going on now - birth defects, smaller brain size, etc. I have hope that studying the fields of physics, electrobiology, etc. could allow us to reverse the changes, or perhaps cause more positive changes such as a denser, more efficient brain.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Compression socks for air travel

### nicld — 2015-03-16T00:27:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/compression-socks-for-air-travel.13316/post-158599>

I have a trip coming in two weeks and am wondering if I (and hubby) should wear compression socks.

First flight is 2 hours and 45 minutes then a 3 1/2 hour layover so I can do a bunch of walking and stretching.

Second flight is 5 1/2 hours (longest I have ever done). No time change but alot of time in the air.

Should I wear compression socks?

Planning on drinking LOTS of good water and eating oysters on the layover. 2nd night in the country is in the jungle with hopefully very little EMF's and lots of grounding.
