1 ------- Pregnenolone, Mass Spectrometry--Test Number: 140707 CPT Code: 84140
4 ------- Progesterone--Test Number: 004317 CPT Code: 84144

goals:

**Subforum:** Beginners Area

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### notsoperfick — 2015-04-14T15:12:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme.13855/post-161172>

If you were thinking of using this service, you might be advised to read this item first: [http://www.scientificamerican.com/article/23andme-is-terrifying-but-not-for-reasons-fda/](http://www.scientificamerican.com/article/23andme-is-terrifying-but-not-for-reasons-fda/)
Sounds kinda scary to me!

### fitness@home — 2015-04-14T15:21:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme.13855/post-161174>

Old news.

They no longer provide the Medical Report, but it's the raw data file I want anyway. I still have access to my Medical Report because it was done before they made that change.

I don't regret the purchase one iota.

### notsoperfick — 2015-04-17T23:08:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme.13855/post-161565>

How far down did you read? Did you get to the bit about why they are offering the service and why the price suddenly dropped? How the real purpose is to garner personal health information and then use it to target people for advertising and other purposes in the same way that Google did? I find that quite frightening.

### Nittygrittydanny — 2015-04-18T00:20:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme.13855/post-161571>

People with something to sell, will seldom tell the whole truth. _**Caveat emptor
**_

### Marie — 2015-04-18T00:41:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme.13855/post-161573>

_Money is the road to legitimacy in our culture._
In order for genomic products to be mainstream, there has to be a money trail. That is all they are trying to do.

I found the raw data to be greatly helpful in explaining "illnesses" that could not be classified as "disease" in my family. It is very helpful to sit down with metabolic pathway diagrams and a list of SNPs to see where the gaps are... then look at "illness" during 20s (IBS and GI types, for example) and 30s (adrenal fatigue, CFS, etc.). These these become "disease" (Gallbladder, appendix, colon cancer, Thyroid cancer, etc) in their 40s, and more significant ones in their 50s and beyond (MS, PD, or ALS, etc.)...

Combining this info with lifestyle, you can see where the disconnects occur and it reinforces what JK describes...

### Robert_S — 2015-04-25T19:06:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme.13855/post-162414>

Has anyone run the raw data through Prometheus? To get the medical stuff. A friend of mine (teaches genetics at a med achool), did and said it very cool. Privacy/google/skynet aside.

I haven't done either. Yet.

### fitness@home — 2015-04-25T23:39:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme.13855/post-162422>

Yes, I did Prometheus too...

### nonchalant — 2015-04-26T00:49:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme.13855/post-162425>

Once I learned that our DNA only code for the first two forms (folds) of proteins, and our redox/environment determine the other two forms (folds), I wasn't concerned about anyone accessing my genetic info. Our DNA codes for the primary and secondary forms, but in most cases those "juvenile" proteins aren't useful for anything. Then the proteins go elsewhere in the cell and undergo tertiary and quaternary folding, which result in useful proteins. Which means an identical DNA (in twins) can result in quite different finished proteins, because of environmental differences.

# Thread: EMF exposure level
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-exposure-level.13859/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### angie-s — 2015-04-14T23:48:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-exposure-level.13859/post-161204>

Hi, I need some help understanding the EMF readings I am getting from the new meter I have bought. It is cornet ED 78S meter and measure RF in mw/m2, LF600 in ut and LF30 in ut. Most of my readings around electronics(computer, laptop, cell phone) appear to be very low and always show green. They are in the typically in the following range

RF= 0.02-0.06
LF 600 = o.06
LF 30 = 0.02

My question is : What measurement is critical (RF or LF) and are these levels safe. What am I to shoot for from a health perspective.

Any help in getting some understanding in this area will be greatly appreciated.

I do keep a glass full of water by the laptop (because I think it reduces EMF exposure) and wear a sheildite pendant.

### nonchalant — 2015-04-15T00:47:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-exposure-level.13859/post-161206>

Hi Angie-s, I have a Cornet also. Your electronics will be low in RF unless something is using a wireless network or bluetooth. Since I'm out in the country, I barely get any reading on the graph display now. No smart meter, no wireless networks except for the cheapo cell phone, and I'm a couple miles from a tower antenna.

Now I mostly use my Trifield meter, to hunt out electrical and magnetic fields, instead of the RF fields the Cornet excels at.

Funny story: I was visiting someone recently in the ICU of a hospital, and I brought the Cornet. I turned it on and put it in my purse while still downstairs, but it started squealing in the hallway before I even got to the ICU. Lol! Something in my purse must have accidentally turned on the audible alarm. Plenty of RF there, at least in the waiting room.

### angie-s — 2015-04-15T02:04:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-exposure-level.13859/post-161215>

All the devices I measured work on wireless networks.

Are you saying that the Cornet does not measure Electrical and Magnetic fields? I bought it for that purpose only. I just cant tell which one is magnetic and which one is electrical and why they are so low.

The only time it measured high was by a decked phone or a working microwave. Never had any audible signals either home or at work.

### nonchalant — 2015-04-15T04:05:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-exposure-level.13859/post-161228>

Oh, I just looked up the model. It does look like it measures magnetism and electricity as well as RF. Sorry, my Cornet ED85EXS only measures RF. Can you test it by measuring magnetism or electricity near a motor? Like a washer, blender, etc?

### angie-s — 2015-04-15T05:26:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-exposure-level.13859/post-161234>

It goes to maximum (over limit) near a running motor.

What readings are we shooting for both electricity and magnetism?

# Thread: When to expose yourself to the sun..

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thomas — 2015-04-15T17:00:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-to-expose-yourself-to-the-sun.13887/post-161291>

The way I understand it, It's best to get the sun early morning and late afternoon. So you can expose yourself as much as you want(or as much as possible) when the solar angle is below about 40-50 degrees.
But what about closer to solar noon when the sun gets higher than 50 degrees. I think then you can expose yourself for briefs periods of time to get your vitaminD with uvb. Then when you feel you've got enough, stop.
Do I get that right?
To be honest skin cancer still scares me a bit..

### nonchalant — 2015-04-15T18:08:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-to-expose-yourself-to-the-sun.13887/post-161295>

Thomas, you can also use sunburn as a guide. I don't think you should be getting sunburn. That said, my resistance to sunburn is amazing now. I used to burn after an hour of sun even in the winter.

Currently, when laying out in the sun, after about 20-30 minutes I no longer feel desperate for sunshine, so I will get up and do something else, whether still outside, or back in the house. In summer, when the sun is high, I often seek the shade of a tree. I haven't had sunburn for years, nor have I used suntan lotion in years.

How long has it been since you have eaten some fried food from a restaurant? Do you eat lots of seafood? The health of your skin is key.

### Lahelada — 2015-04-15T18:23:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-to-expose-yourself-to-the-sun.13887/post-161297>

The best time is between 8-11 am, afternoon is second best. Exposure times go by how you feel. Some parts of me where I carry excess fat burn easily,my lean parts now tolerate the sun well,in my case arms,face and chest.

### Penny — 2015-04-15T19:54:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-to-expose-yourself-to-the-sun.13887/post-161313>

As I understand it, the more inflamed you are, the sooner you will burn - IMHO this is why astaxanthin is so effective against sunburns - it provides squashes inflammation - I think it's synthesized from krill oil - I think you have it right though - morning sun for mitochondria and high noon for vitamin d (UVB)

### kovita — 2015-04-15T20:24:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-to-expose-yourself-to-the-sun.13887/post-161316>

This weekend there is [a show called "Total Health 2015"](http://www.totalhealthshow.com/showInfo/) happening in Toronto. The speakers include Dr. Dean Bonlie (Magnetico), Dr. William Davis (author of "Wheat Belly"), Dr. Magda Havas (an nnEMF expert) and Roland Jentschura (yes, the same Jentschura family that is behind the AlkaBath product Inger introduced to us).

I had my spine checked by a chiropractor (and yes, he thinks I need an adjustment ).

I found an interesting group that does EMF assessments and sells meters and shielding.

And I spent 30 minutes with a Tesla coil around me! This is a pulsed EMF device. They had some really interesting exhibits of health related Tesla devices there. # Thread: Targeted treatment of cancer with artemisinin and artemisinin-tagged iron-carrying compounds.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targeted-treatment-of-cancer-with-artemisinin-and-artemisinin-tagged-iron-carrying-compounds.13896/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2015-04-16T08:43:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/targeted-treatment-of-cancer-with-artemisinin-and-artemisinin-tagged-iron-carrying-compounds.13896/post-161376>

Henry Lai couldn't get any more funding for non-ionizing EMF effects on DNA, so he decided to cure cancer with wormwood instead...

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16185154](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16185154)

**Abstract**
Artemisinin is a chemical compound that reacts with iron to form free radicals which can kill cells. Cancer cells require and uptake a large amount of iron to proliferate. They are more susceptible to the cytotoxic effect of artemisinin than normal cells. Cancer cells express a large concentration of cell surface transferrin receptors that facilitate uptake of the plasma iron-carrying protein transferrin via endocytosis. By covalently tagging artemisinin to transferrin, artemisinin could be selectively picked up and concentrated by cancer cells. Futhermore, both artemisinin and iron would be transported into the cell in one package. Once an artemisinin-tagged transferrin molecule is endocytosed, iron is released and reacts with artemisinin moieties tagged to transferrin. Formation of free radicals kills the cancer cell. The authors have found that artemisinin-tagged transferrin is highly selective and potent in killing cancer cells. Thus, artemisinin and artemisinin-tagged iron-carrying compounds could be developed into powerful anticancer drugs.

Robert Rowan hopped right onto that bandwagon and added a few lipids:
[http://www.cancure.org/12-links-page/43-artemesia](http://www.cancure.org/12-links-page/43-artemesia)

# Thread: Using JK ideas in agriculture (i.e. H20 & Magnetism) - Results are astounding
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-jk-ideas-in-agriculture-i-e-h20-magnetism-results-are-astounding.13964/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jeff Power — 2015-04-18T22:24:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-jk-ideas-in-agriculture-i-e-h20-magnetism-results-are-astounding.13964/post-161677>

I've been following the work on this forum for a year now and I've just received incredible results using the Kruse principals applied to growing crops.

My company manages a private equity fund focused on agriculture. Among other things, we redevelop farmland. We set up an initiative within the company this past year to experiment with new technology and techniques, and innovate new ways to achieve record setting yield and profitability within our agribusinesses - think Google Labs but for agriculture.

We have various experiments and tests running right now. Many are focused on water chemistry. In particular, we recently began running our water through a magnetic field within the pipeline. The results are amazing.

Results:

1.  Tissue samples show sodium content (we have salty water) dropped 50% in thirty days (this is unprecedented). It appears the magnetic field is breaking down the dissolved solids in the water to their simple element form.
2.  Plant tissue samples are showing an average of 30% increase in trace elements and micronutrients.
3.  The water is penetrating the soil multiple feet deeper than before (very important).

Other tests running:

1.  Running the water in the pipeline through a magnetic that also turbulizes the water.
2.  Oxygenating the water.

Ideas for upcoming tests:

1.  Minicking "implosion water" and adding oxygen to make the water.
2.  Cooling the water to ~50 degrees during hot summer months (we are in the Southwest where it gets very hot, hence the plants grow very fast and stressed in the high heat). Right now the water comes out of the ground in the summer at ~100 degrees.
3.  Installing infrared lights to the inside of the water pipelines (the water runs very fast so it may be too short of time-frame to do anything).

We have agronomists and engineers on staff (I am on the finance/portfolio management side of the business). I love thinking through the concepts we are exploring here and finding a way to apply them to growing bigger better healthier crops, & improving soil quality, etc. Any other ideas/comments/suggestions are welcomed.

Jeff, Im currently looking in to this. I wonder if you are still having promising results, or have any more insight into your trials. There is another grower here on the site that has reached out to me, he also has some personal success with some of these technologies. Please contact me.

### Starfish Prime — 2016-02-15T21:25:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-jk-ideas-in-agriculture-i-e-h20-magnetism-results-are-astounding.13964/post-189118>

You are paying attention to soil structure, micro flora like mycorrhyzal fungi, etc. yes?

### Sean Shick — 2016-02-15T22:10:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-jk-ideas-in-agriculture-i-e-h20-magnetism-results-are-astounding.13964/post-189120>

Yes, I have bio-assays pulled every year at least once. At last check we had on average 40% colonization of mycorrhzai. I re-innoculate once a season regardless of our current progress, and Im told that 40-50% colonization is the highest that they have seen in our crop. We use a active compost tea that is locally produced 3-4 times a year through our irrigation also, along with compost in the winter. I have been trying to steer our farm towards a more probiotic approach for the last 4ish years, away from typical industry standards. We have maintained and increased yields while lowering costs, so I am certainly a believer.

ATM Im really interested in applying a magnetic field to our irrigation systems(water). However, there are many contradicting views online, and I can't seem to get a clear answer to whether or not it will actually do any good. The folks that sell the magnetic products(extremely expensive btw) claim that the magnets "structure" the water, breaking the water into smaller clusters of water molecules. In Ag, there are many products out there that claim to do things, which they may very well do, problem is, many of those things already get done for free in the soil, etc. Im trying to figure out whether or not there is any truth to this "structuring" thing they claim will be helpful.

I heard Jack's partner on the Quantlet say in an interview, that there is no way to structure water. So I don't know if this might be a misunderstanding of semantics or what...

I am pondering an outside grounding bed for morning/evening sun exposure.

I have access to granite stones (small, 1/2" and smaller down to almost sand like size) from the mining we do for building roads. It comes from our Talbotton Ga. (USA) mine. I also have Limestone we use in our concrete plant. Which would you use and why?

I was thinking of digging down in my yard and making a couple beds. If it would work, how deep should I go and would a product that has to be exploded have bad energy left over from it's release from the earth and the crushing it goes through to be processed?

I have on average 3-10 5gal buckets of material every week, after I test it prior to using it for production in asphalt.

Your thoughts please.

Thanks

# Thread: Blueberry dreams ....Eat Drink Paleo / Irena Macri
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blueberry-dreams-eat-drink-paleo-irena-macri.14026/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2015-04-21T13:59:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blueberry-dreams-eat-drink-paleo-irena-macri.14026/post-161909>

This is very delicious ...much more so than you would think - in my opinion!

1/2 cup fresh or frozen blueberries
1/2 cup diced mango
1 tsp vanilla extract
1 TBSP lemon juice
1/2 cup coconut water
1/2 cup ice - I don't use this if using frozen blueberries and everything is very cold.
Combine all ingredients in a blender and puree until beautiful and thick

this turns out very thick and a gorgeous colour - enjoy!

I am going to make some coconut yogurt and I think it would be delicious in this too - maybe a couple of eggs too?

maybe add some spirits? tequila?????

### nicld — 2015-04-21T16:54:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blueberry-dreams-eat-drink-paleo-irena-macri.14026/post-161920>

Uhmmmmm, RUM!!!!!!! It goes very very well with fresh mango and coconut, just say'n

### caroline — 2015-04-22T03:09:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blueberry-dreams-eat-drink-paleo-irena-macri.14026/post-161993>

Honestly - please try this drink ....it is super delicious! for such simple ingredients.
Our mango season has ended - so I am going to use some peaches in juice - from a jar ....not ideal.

maybe half a banana and a couple of peach slices.....

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blueberry-dreams-eat-drink-paleo-irena-macri.14026/post-162198>

### caroline — 2015-04-24T10:23:10+0300

# Thread: brownie
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brownie.14082/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kovita — 2015-04-23T15:14:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brownie.14082/post-162199>

this recipe became quickly my kids favourite and our occasional guests usually do not believe the recipe works ;-) very fast and very simple and very good.

1\. take about half bar of butter and melt it (bano maria) together with about half bar of pure chocolate (for example 99% lindt available anywhere)

2\. separate 3 egg yolks and beat them in blender quickly with a spoon of hardwood xylit (you can avoid it and make the brownie competely without any sweetener and sprinkle some honey on top before serving) and vanilla to taste

3\. beat the egg whites to the foam and if you have any leftover egg white from breakfast, add it too. the brownie will have lighter structure with just one more egg white.

4\. now add to the mixture in blender the melted butter with chocolate, one more spoon of powedered cocoa (if you want the taste very intensive) and a spoon of fine coconut flour and blend it all together till well combined.

5\. now add the egg white foam amd mix well

6\. pour it to the cristal pan covered with baking paper and bake for about 14 min at 175 degrees celsius (it should be firm on touch)

7\. best served with honey, raw cream (if tolerated), dried or fresh berries etc.

### fitness@home — 2015-04-23T17:07:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brownie.14082/post-162206>

Sounds good!

Will try this when I have a dark chocolate craving (haven't craved it in the past 4-5 months at all).

### caroline — 2015-04-24T04:54:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brownie.14082/post-162257>

Oh man ..... Is this calling my name!

### lilly antic — 2015-10-03T19:24:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brownie.14082/post-178542>

wow, thanks for the recipe. i hate the pure chocolate from lindt, so this might be a good alternative to pimp it up...

# Thread: Considerations for relocating - cell towers, seafood, sunshine, etc.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/considerations-for-relocating-cell-towers-seafood-sunshine-etc.14111/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lookingforanswers — 2015-04-24T19:32:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/considerations-for-relocating-cell-towers-seafood-sunshine-etc.14111/post-162314>

Can y'all help me brainstorm some of the most important things to consider when relocating?

I had my previous posts where I mentioned we considered the south gulf coast of Florida and now we're considering Mississippi. Jack seemed to think both were good.

Here's some things I'm wondering:

\- As I'm doing searches I see cell towers EVERYWHERE! I guess there's just no way to get around them, but based on testing we've done previously it seems that topography is going to determine whether or not it hits us. Trees, hills, etc. I guess can block it, so we could theoretically be a mile from a tower and not have an issue, but also maybe be 4 miles from a tower and have a problem . . . has anyone found this to be true?

\- Seafood: Is there much difference between the south gulf coast and the panhandle area as far as getting fresh seafood?

\- I'm concerned that maybe we won't get enough sunshine over the winter in northern FL or MS. Thoughts?

\- What else should we be considering? This is a HUGE move and I want to make sure we're looking at everything.

Thanks for any insight!

### fitness@home — 2015-04-24T20:34:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/considerations-for-relocating-cell-towers-seafood-sunshine-etc.14111/post-162322>

Considerations for me would also be employment opportunities and access to good medical care (in case of emergency/hospital). Honestly, if I had to give up my current doctors and couldn't find adequate replacements I'd travel back to see them!

Good water (as in well water or springs) would be something to look for too.

I cannot comment on Mississippi though, because I've never lived in that state. I did enjoy having access to fresh seafood and beaches when we lived in Florida many moons ago.

### lookingforanswers — 2015-05-01T16:12:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/considerations-for-relocating-cell-towers-seafood-sunshine-etc.14111/post-162974>

Yes, definitely need to consider jobs, good water and medical care! Thanks for the input

Can anyone chime in about the location of cell towers? Am I on the right track thinking distance really isn't necessarily the most important consideration?!

# Thread: Is DNA the next internet... great article on biophoton repair et al -
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-dna-the-next-internet-great-article-on-biophoton-repair-et-al.14137/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2015-04-26T03:57:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-dna-the-next-internet-great-article-on-biophoton-repair-et-al.14137/post-162429>

[http://www.viewzone.com/dnax.html](http://www.viewzone.com/dnax.html)

Here is the start of the article:
"A Russian scientist, Pjotr Garjajev, had managed to intercept communication from a DNA molecule in the form of ultraviolet photons -- light! What's more, he claimed to have captured this communication from one organism (a frog embryo) with a laser beam and then transmitted it to another organisms DNA (a salamander embryo), causing the latter embryo to develop into a frog!

But this was just the beginning.

Dr. Garjajev claims that this communication is not something that happens only inside the individual cells or between one cell and another. He claims organisms use this "light" to "talk" to other organisms and suggested that this could explain telepathy and ESP. It was like human beings already had their own wireless internet based on our DNA. Wow!

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I tried to find a scientific journal that had this experiment. All I could find were blogs and other websites that carried the same story, word for word, without any references. That is until I stumbled on the work of Fritz-Albert Popp \[**right**\]. Then everything I had just read seemed very plausible.

Fritz-Albert Popp thought he had discovered a cure for cancer. I'm not convinced that he didn't.

It was 1970, and Popp, a theoretical biophysicist at the University of Marburg in Germany, had been teaching radiology -- the interaction of electromagnetic (EM) radiation on biological systems. Popp was too early to worry about things like cellphones and microwave towers which are now commonly linked with cancers and leukemia. His world was much smaller.

He'd been examining two almost identical molecules: benzo\[a\]pyrene, a polycyclic hydrocarbon known to be one of the most lethal carcinogens to humans, and its twin (save for a tiny alteration in its molecular makeup), benzo\[e\]pyrene. He had illuminated both molecules with ultraviolet (UV) light in an attempt to find exactly what made these two almost identical molecules so different."

Anyway - it really made the light thing easier for me to fathom - it might help someone else here too

I am trying to figure out how artimisin and mistletoe help with the photorepair in the body - I googled:
"mistletoe biophoton repair" -

### JMO — 2015-04-26T18:22:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-dna-the-next-internet-great-article-on-biophoton-repair-et-al.14137/post-162477>

I will always remember when I read Biology of belief by Bruce Lipton and he described a cell from verbiage taken from a computer manual. Opened my eyes that we are a freaking wireless device.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-04-27T07:32:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-dna-the-next-internet-great-article-on-biophoton-repair-et-al.14137/post-162518>

I was blown away to hear that our DNA acts like a high powered ANTENNA for EMF!

That would explain why the only thing that will eliminate my computer screen radiation is ME!
When I touch the screen, my Electrosmog meter goes to zero.

# Thread: Biophotons: the human body emits, communicates with, and is made from light
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biophotons-the-human-body-emits-communicates-with-and-is-made-from-light.14138/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2015-04-26T04:13:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biophotons-the-human-body-emits-communicates-with-and-is-made-from-light.14138/post-162430>

Interesting article - ties in to the last one I posted - loved this little snippet:

**"Meditation and Herbs Affect Biophoton Output**
Research has found an oxidative stress-mediated difference in biophoton emission among mediators versus non-meditators. Those who meditate regularly tend to have lower ultra-weak photon emission (UPE, biophoton emission), which is believed to result from the lower level of free radical reactions occurring in their bodies. In one clinical study involving practitioners of transcendental meditation (TM) researchers found:

The lowest UPE intensities were observed in two subjects who regularly meditate. Spectral analysis of human UPE has suggested that ultra-weak emission is probably, at least in part, a reflection of free radical reactions in a living system. It has been documented that various physiologic and biochemical shifts follow the long-term practice of meditation and it is inferred that meditation may impact free radical activity.\[13\]

Interestingly, an herb well-known for its use in stress reduction (including inducing measurable declines in cortisol), and associated heightened oxidative stress, has been tested clinically in reducing the level of biophotons emitted in human subjects. Known as [rhodiola](http://www.greenmedinfo.com/substance/rhodiola-tibetan-ginseng), a study published in 2009 in the journal _Phytotherapeutic Research_ found that those who took the herb for 1 week has a significant decrease in photon emission in comparison with the placebo group.\[14\]"

[http://drleonardcoldwell.com/2013/0...its-communicates-with-and-is-made-from-light/](http://drleonardcoldwell.com/2013/08/28/biophotons-the-human-body-emits-communicates-with-and-is-made-from-light/)

So, it seems to me if you are blue light toxic (MS), rhodiola might help you?

### Penny — 2015-04-26T06:21:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biophotons-the-human-body-emits-communicates-with-and-is-made-from-light.14138/post-162439>

For what it's worth, rhodiola is grown in the Arctic... maybe it stores red light and that counteracts blue light? Weirdly, artimisn is grown in Africa -

>

> Research has found an oxidative stress-mediated difference in biophoton emission among mediators versus non-meditators. Those who meditate regularly tend to have lower ultra-weak photon emission (UPE, biophoton emission), which is believed to result from the lower level of free radical reactions occurring in their bodies. In one clinical study involving practitioners of transcendental meditation (TM) researchers found:
>
> The lowest UPE intensities were observed in two subjects who regularly meditate. Spectral analysis of human UPE has suggested that ultra-weak emission is probably, at least in part, a reflection of free radical reactions in a living system. It has been documented that various physiologic and biochemical shifts follow the long-term practice of meditation and it is inferred that meditation may impact free radical activity.\[13\]
>
> Interestingly, an herb well-known for its use in stress reduction (including inducing measurable declines in cortisol), and associated heightened oxidative stress, has been tested clinically in reducing the level of biophotons emitted in human subjects. Known as [rhodiola](http://www.greenmedinfo.com/substance/rhodiola-tibetan-ginseng), a study published in 2009 in the journal _Phytotherapeutic Research_ found that those who took the herb for 1 week has a significant decrease in photon emission in comparison with the placebo group.\[14\]"
>
> [http://drleonardcoldwell.com/2013/0...its-communicates-with-and-is-made-from-light/](http://drleonardcoldwell.com/2013/08/28/biophotons-the-human-body-emits-communicates-with-and-is-made-from-light/)

Going to have to invest in some sort of light box for the shorter winter days though.

# Thread: D.H.A. - Raw seafood vs. cooked
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-h-a-raw-seafood-vs-cooked.14142/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2015-04-26T22:30:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-h-a-raw-seafood-vs-cooked.14142/post-162487>

In one of Jack's latest podcasts he talks about how DHA is very susceptible to oxidation from light and heat. This made me realize the importance of eating seafood raw since it makes sense that the DHA would be retained.
I'm just wondering; If we cook our seafood, is any of the DHA retained at all?

If not, that means EVERYBODY is deficient in DHA! (unless you eat sushi)

Very good podcast, has some funny moments:

I am constantly in awe with just how much Jack knows! The "proof is in the pudding", so to speak.
You can tell that his OWN brain is working extremely sharp!

### Clayton — 2015-04-27T05:47:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-h-a-raw-seafood-vs-cooked.14142/post-162510>

There is also loss of DHA from prolonged freezing. Yew posted an article on facebook showing decrease in DHA content after two weeks of freezing. Take advantage of the fresh seafood here in Vancouver. There are several places downtown that have "buck a shuck" specials daily. Chewies and the Fish Shack downtown as well as Merchant oyster bar on commercial

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-04-27T07:07:58+0300

Take advantage of the fresh seafood here in Vancouver. There are several places downtown that have "buck a shuck" specials daily. Chewies and the Fish Shack downtown as well as Merchant oyster bar on commercial

Thanks a bunch Clay. I have actually been buying the frozen ones just for convenience but if there is a significant loss in freezing I'll switch to fresh.
When I first started eating the oysters I would gag because I would chew them so that would make me opt for the canned ones and cooked shrimp. But now that I realized that there probably isn't any DHA in the cooked stuff I've started 'shucking' them down with water and its much easier! Can barely taste it!
Did you get to see Jack when he was in town? I can't believe I missed it! Carolyn told me it was cancelled. But he does mention on a couple webinars that he was here. Shit!

### Da-mo — 2015-04-27T08:07:05+0300

### Fractality — 2015-04-27T08:31:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-h-a-raw-seafood-vs-cooked.14142/post-162521>

### Inger — 2015-04-27T08:58:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-h-a-raw-seafood-vs-cooked.14142/post-162522>

I eat raw fatty seafood all the time... I LOVE it! I just did a round of homeopatic HCG to test... just for biohack as I am not overweight... and my main food was raw fatty mackerel (not in protocol..lol.. you cant have fatty fish) - mostly 2 meals a day fatty mackerel and one meal I had raw meat or cooked shrimp and I ate more that 500 calories / day! maybe 6-700. And I still lost a quite some kg of fat....... maybe 5 kg. how fun is that! And I felt awesome....

I do not understand why to cook fatty seafood at all. It taste better raw, way better. The only seafood I like better cooked is probably mussels. I always cook those. Oysters.. never. way better raw. Some non fatty fishes are great when cooked too.. and plaice I always cook gently because it is kinda chewy raw...lol I love it with butter...yum.

### Danny — 2015-04-27T19:35:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheet-at-night.14157/post-162559>

If you've been sleeping w/ an earthing sheet at night and have just accepted it as beneficial you might want to experiment going w/o it for a few days to a few weeks to see how your sleep quality changes.

It's only been 2 days so it's too early to tell. I went w/o an earthing sheet while working out of town for 7 days a few weeks back and slept really well. Obviously this doesn't control for other variables in the change of location, so I'm trying this at home now.

### Jeanne — 2015-04-27T23:12:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/would-love-to-hear-menopause-success-story.14158/post-162577>

Hey ladies! I really need some encouragement! I've been eating epi-paleo for 6 months; balanced hormones with bio-identical hormones and added in CT daily 4 weeks ago. I haven't lost a single pound or inch and I'm frustrated. I think I actually gained a few lbs.  I would love to hear from some ladies who have experienced successful fat loss....please?

### fitness@home — 2015-04-27T23:54:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/would-love-to-hear-menopause-success-story.14158/post-162579>

I am a work in progress. P/E ratio still needs correction.

I was 167 lbs at my heaviest and am currently 146. Going to fight my way back into a size 8 (pre-menopause size). Size 10 right now. Don't give up!

Feel great. When I look around me at other women my age, when out in public somewhere, I also feel blessed .

### fitness@home — 2015-04-27T23:55:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/would-love-to-hear-menopause-success-story.14158/post-162580>

### bionaut — 2015-04-27T23:58:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/would-love-to-hear-menopause-success-story.14158/post-162581>

Sounds like you have got some good things in place. Stay confidient that as you continue doing the right things you will improve. This process takes time to reverse and often you will take a step back and then take a step forward. The path is not a straight line, it meanders a lot.

Have you had a chance to perform the leptin reset?

### Jeanne — 2015-04-28T02:58:20+0300

I've been very low carb/keto for 2 1/2 years. It worked well until menopause came along. I refuse to accept there is nothing to be done and you just have to accept it. I will just keep looking for the answers.

### Valerie — 2015-04-28T03:20:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/would-love-to-hear-menopause-success-story.14158/post-162597>

Found cold thermogenesis reduced inches in stomach (likely inflammation). However after CTing my appetite increased and I gained weight the first year... 15 lbs (likely some muscle gain as well). Prior to this I was eating only 1000-1200 calories (low carb)/day (very leptin resistant), so increasing my food intake I felt was needed. I feel much healthier now with an overall better figure, but still trying to adjust from size 4 to size 6 in my pants. The only way I stopped weight gain was to added T3 (I have hashimoto's). Just my N-1.

### WereBear — 2015-06-05T16:02:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/would-love-to-hear-menopause-success-story.14158/post-166685>

Excellent point about pregnenolone steal! For those who don't know, this is the diversion of the body's production of pregnenolone, a hormone precursor, to stress hormones like cortisol... which deprives the other hormones of this vital building block. And I understand our bodies also don't produce as much pregnenolone as we age. Another thing to be aware of.

# Thread: How to get the CT session cold enough?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-get-the-ct-session-cold-enough.14171/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mark444200 — 2015-04-29T06:00:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-get-the-ct-session-cold-enough.14171/post-162722>

Admittedly I don't know currently how cold my CT sessions are (I've got a skin thermometer on the way from ebay though). I've built up to 24 minutes in a pure-cold water bath with 6 or so ice packs chucked in it.

I imagine if the bath was filled with ice cubes it'd be much colder, but how do people get these ice cubes, do you have to buy them from an ice machine every day?

Interested in other peoples techniques / experiences. I guess another option would be an outdoor tub.. or to do it cheaply, like a drum of some description filled with water?

### Da-mo — 2015-04-29T06:12:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-get-the-ct-session-cold-enough.14171/post-162723>

We bought a bunch of ice cube trays from a 2nd hand shop and just fill em and put them in the freezer.

I've found that the bought ice (the kind you buy in bags from gas stations etc.) tends not to cool the water as much - I'm sure they use water that is easier to freeze to use less power and as such it melts easier too.

We monitor the water temp using one of those floating swimming pool thermometers - cost $20 or so. Often take it to the waterfall as well so we can check the water temp there too.

Another thing to keep an eye on is skin color - that is a good indication too - see CT Protocol.

### Clayton — 2015-04-29T06:33:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-get-the-ct-session-cold-enough.14171/post-162725>

Freeze 4 litre milk jugs and leave them in for a 1/2 hour before beggining your session

### fitness@home — 2015-04-29T12:36:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-get-the-ct-session-cold-enough.14171/post-162762>

Since CT-ing in Texas can be a challenge for 8 months out of the year (hot here), I bought a chiller. In the past I have used frozen gallon jugs of water like Clayton. Fortunately we have a chest freezer and that made storage of them easier.

In our winter, I can use the pool outside. My husband just switched it over to a salt water system.

### nicld — 2015-04-29T16:19:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-get-the-ct-session-cold-enough.14171/post-162767>

I do the milk jugs too in the summer. In winter I just have to set buckets outside for a bit and make sure they don't freeze solid.

### mark444200 — 2015-04-30T03:57:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-get-the-ct-session-cold-enough.14171/post-162808>

thanks for the info guys.

I also did some digging around myself and heres a video for those interested;

he adds salt to the water, then fills up water balloons to freeze and re-use. bigger surface area than the milk jugs? but a lot more work. he says putting salt in the water will make it colder.

# Thread: The ORP and pH of water - drinking oxidizing vs. anti-oxidant water
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-orp-and-ph-of-water-drinking-oxidizing-vs-anti-oxidant-water.14195/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sjoshua — 2015-04-29T21:23:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-orp-and-ph-of-water-drinking-oxidizing-vs-anti-oxidant-water.14195/post-162783>

This site has a great description of both pH and ORP (Oxidation Reduction Potential), for gathering a basic understanding.

[http://www.aquahealthproducts.com/understanding-ph-and-orp](http://www.aquahealthproducts.com/understanding-ph-and-orp)

In summary,

'oxidizing' = electron steal
'anti-oxidizing' = electron giving

As with anything, balance is the key: which is why consuming too many 'anti-oxidant' foods (i.e. electron giving foods) can be as problematic as not consuming enough. Overloading a battery is no good...

But, what if we could be drinking an anti-oxidant solution?

I plan to explore this further.

Here is one using an ionizer to create it, but that could be quite pricey...
[http://www.health-science-spirit.com/highorp.html](http://www.health-science-spirit.com/highorp.html)

Whenever there is an 'expensive' (read: complex) way to do something, there is bound to be a simple way.

### sjoshua — 2015-06-09T19:50:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-orp-and-ph-of-water-drinking-oxidizing-vs-anti-oxidant-water.14195/post-167242>

::chirp chirp:: <- the crickets that took over since I took so long to post

Anywho, I've been running a some experiments with water and wanted to share some results. First, let's calibrate our meters:
ORP meter in 225mV solution:
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

pH meter in 7.0 solution:

Tap water (San Jose):

RO water (tap water ran through 5-stage RO system): You'll notice the ORP is slightly better than tap but not by much :\\ still 'stealing'. (Starting values: ORP: 248; pH: 9.0)

Now, let's add some stuff to RO water!

First, we will add ~1g of vitamin C (it was one of these ground up: [http://www.amazon.com/Foods-Vitamin-Sustained-Release-Tablets/dp/B0013P1GD6/](http://www.amazon.com/Foods-Vitamin-Sustained-Release-Tablets/dp/B0013P1GD6/)): You'll notice a nice drop in ORP, and also a sharp drop in pH. (running tally adjustments: ORP: -160 pH: -4.0)

Next, we will add in a 1-2g baking soda: You'll notice the pH corrects somewhat, and ORP also decreases a little more - yay no longer 'stealing'! (running tally adjustments: ORP: -254 pH: -1.8)

And now for the finale, we will add in a VERY small amount of [Sodium Borohydride](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sodium_borohydride) (10-15mg - one of [these](http://www.nootropics.co.uk/uploads/media/.thumbnails/Nootropics-Micro-Spoon_366x327.png) spoons worth, which is like a pinch with infant fingers): Boom shaka-locka, HUGE drop in ORP  (running tally adjustments: ORP: -730 pH: -1.8)

I neither support or suggest anyone else consume things they are not comfortable with, but I wanted to share these results from my own N=1. I have been drinking my morning bottle of water with this amount of Sodium Borohydride in it for a couple of weeks now (I rarely add the vit C and baking soda, but did so for the demo) and it gives me a little mental 'buzz'. I have no other 'case studies' to share on the results side.

### fitness@home — 2015-06-09T20:01:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-orp-and-ph-of-water-drinking-oxidizing-vs-anti-oxidant-water.14195/post-167244>

Thanks for posting the pics to go along with your story. Visual person here!

Interesting, very interesting...

# Thread: On the dangers of computer use (outline)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-dangers-of-computer-use-outline.14254/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thomas — 2015-05-01T16:07:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-dangers-of-computer-use-outline.14254/post-162973>

It's something I am thinking these days,
given that I feel best and most alive when I go camping and am outdoors all the time
Trying to figure what is wrong with using computers and modern technology (and civilisation to an extent). It's not a final version, mostly an evolving outline. Just a few random ideas.

The best obvious thing to do is to reduce usage. 12 hours a day on the computer is crazy and feel like hell. 3 hours seems more reasonable. Computers are like magic powers, awesome, but use it too much they kill you.

\- Light of the display. Too much high frequency blue light. And the vision too close.
possible solution: Use flux, Other types of display like e-paper. (you can use a kindle as a computer screen).

\- Electric/Magnetic fields.
possible solution: Computer well shielded. Put it on the other side of the room, or even of the house by buying extension cords. Have input devices low in emf. Have a display 1.5+ meters away.

\- Keeping us indoors.
possible solution: Use computer outdoors. well. Actually limit computer time.

\- Stressful Continus Overwelming flow of information consuming life and time. We rely on technology more and more and people&our tribe less and less.
Well that was a problem that existed with large libraries before too..
Instead of Focusing on few books at a time and living life. You are on the computer more and more. Information is like the worst drug ever. But actual life changing information is only a small fraction of information consumed. (like 1/1000). It's not really much better than tv (which people on average watch 5+hours a day I think). Maybe we're all bored and the burden of figuring better things to do is too much.
possible solution: Stop computers entirely and use libraries. Or get much better at navigating information. I am falling in love with the old ways of doing things to an extent.

Feel free to add ideas.

# Thread: Master cleanse....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/master-cleanse.14259/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2015-05-02T04:34:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/master-cleanse.14259/post-163033>

Some folks seem to think this is like fire water! .... but honestly - we don't find it to be at all. It will depend what peppers you use of course

24 oz. organic apple cider vinegar
1/4 cup finely chopped garlic
1/4 cup finely chopped onion
2 fresh hot peppers - I used long thin red hot peppers
1/4 cup grated ginger
2TBSP grated horseradish
2TBSP grated tumeric root - or powder I love turmeric root!

Combine all ingredients except vinegar and transfer to a mason jar.

Por in some apple cider vinegar and fill to the top. It is best if 2/3 of jar is dry ingredients and then fill with ACV

Close and shake well

Keep jar in a cool place for two weeks

Shake well several times a day

The original recipe says, after 2 weeks to squeeze well and strain .... and drink the firewater!

But we love all the bits in it - so I don't squeeze out and just leave in the fridge and mix it thru Tuna or an omelet or a salad or whatever your heart desires!

### Coldsnow — 2017-05-30T08:23:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/master-cleanse.14259/post-215074>

damn Caroline, this one would put hair on you. The garlic, onion, horseradish combo is a juicy one. Have you
ever not eaten garlic or onion for 3 weeks? It's a funny thing in that you begin to be able to smell everyone else
who eats garlic and onion; especially if your on an airplane. jus sayin

### JanSz — 2017-05-31T01:43:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/master-cleanse.14259/post-215146>

The 'Dirt on Clean' in an Oversanitized World
[http://www.npr.org/templates/story/story.php?storyId=16305411](http://www.npr.org/templates/story/story.php?storyId=16305411)

# Thread: Weight loss = freezing?!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-freezing.14308/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Andrea — 2015-05-04T18:30:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-freezing.14308/post-163274>

Ok... Something weird is going on here...

Brief summary: I haven't been well for a bunch of years (diagnosed with hypothyroidism) and my libido has been pretty non-existent for about as long. Thanks to stuff like caring for my circadian rhythm, ocean based diet and CT I've got a lot better and as of last fall I'm completely off all my medication - and I'm feeling well.

However, a couple weeks ago I met an intriguing man... I haven’t been able to stop thinking about him since and always look forward to seeing him again.  For some reason I quite lost my appetite and I eat just once a day and some days I even forget to eat (on average I eat about 250-300g of herring a day and that’s it). So over these few weeks I’ve lost about 7 kg/15 lb (which is nice but I still have about as much left to lose in order to get to where I was before I got sick).

The problem is that now I feel cold most of the time. My hands are cold. My feet too. The temperature outdoors is around 10C now but I feel soooo cold without a jacket (I walked around in a t-shirt and no jacket all winter!). After drinking a glass of cool water I freeze and shiver for hours. I haven’t used a blanket in bed since last spring, but now I couldn’t be without it. CT is tough, very tough. And my period has been unusually frequent this month. No brain fog as when I had my hypothyroid issues though.

What’s going on with me?

### Inger — 2015-05-04T22:15:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-freezing.14308/post-163292>

OMG.... Rerun I have worried about you!!!!

You are seriously in love.........
I do not think anything is wrong with you... when I eat that little and lose weight I am freezing too...... I did HCG and was sooo cold! I ate even more than you do now.
Man.. Rerun.... this seems to be serious..... I wish you anything good  Do not hide this long anymore, ok

### caroline — 2015-05-05T11:34:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-freezing.14308/post-163345>

intriguing man ...... tell us more! pleeeeeaaaase..........

Can you eat more fat? How are you sleeping?

### Andrea — 2015-05-05T15:20:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-freezing.14308/post-163361>

Thanks, Inger!  But that was sort of the obvious part...

Caroline, I might, one of these days... But not here...

Anyway, this post is about me feeling so darned cold all the time. I don't see how all those funny chemicals in my brain and everywhere would make me freeze, so I guess it may have to do with my sudden weight loss. But why? What can I do to stop freezing?

I've tried to eat a couple spoons of coconut fat, but no difference, I was still freezing.
Sleep is on and off. I tend to roll around and think a lot... Wonder why...  Although last week I spent some afternoons outdoors, walked in the ocean for a while and I slept outdoors on the ground; I slept like a baby from 8 pm to 5 am. Had to use my warm sleeping bag though, was feeling soooo cold otherwise.

# Thread: Is our favorite doctor consulting Hollywood?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-our-favorite-doctor-consulting-hollywood.14309/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Melanie Procter — 2015-05-04T19:53:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-our-favorite-doctor-consulting-hollywood.14309/post-163278>

Just saw another sci fi in which some of the principles we are learning this blog were used as the science behind the science fiction. Of course, like most sci fi, the science stretched and massaged a bit to justify the storyline.

Last night, I saw Age of Adeline. The heroine, played by Blake Lively, was a woman who never aged due to freezing in an ice lake which was hit by a bolt of lightening. They even mentioned something about D ribose or the ribose pathway. It was thrown out so loosely that I didn't catch their words. But this whole combination of events stopped her telomeres from "aging". This is a semi summary.

Another movie called "Lucy" about the epigenetic upgrade of the newest version of human, again using some of the science we see on this blog to create the science fiction.

It is fun to play with this material in the form of story. Though I know the science is not perfectly accurate in the movies, it is fun to see them playing with the hardcore science we are learning here. I like to think of this science in the form of story, as it makes it all a little playful.

Any other such movies like the ones I described?

Mel

# Thread: From 20% to 80% -- I'll call that success
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-20-to-80-ill-call-that-success.14312/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### WereBear — 2015-05-04T21:46:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-20-to-80-ill-call-that-success.14312/post-163289>

Ten months in… and tremendous progress. I’m not all the way back yet, but I can make a convincing show of being normal

I went from barely getting through my day, a mixed desk job/climbing mountains (not at a lot of that last summer) to having energy left over when I get home. And since it’s a lot of creative work (I write four blogs a week) and also physical activity (to do the activities to blog about) it’s always a challenge.

Right now the daily routine is a whey protein smoothie in the morning, (which seems to work better right now than my oily fish breakfasts all winter?) With D-ribose, dry gelatin, Spiru-tein which has an amazing effect, washing down 250mg chelated magnesium, 10k D3 in oil, 2k vitamin C, bio-available Curcumin (for inflammation,) and 500 mg plain niacin with a B50.

Then, mid-afternoon, I have a normal low carb meal, meat & veggies and a couple of squares of dark chocolate, and I’m good until breakfast the next day. Dr. Kruse said I would go to two meals a day, and it did happen.

Hope I’ll get these extra cortisol-mayhem inches off my waist, everywhere else has lost weight.

Sleep has become INCREDIBLE. I have a reminder chime on my iPod touch to signal the changing time of sundown, and off go the lights with only a salt lamp and maybe a lamp with an amber shade, I don my sunglass readers with the amber tint. Then bed by 10 pm, with 1g of niacin and 1g of liposomal C and 50mg of pregnenolone, and a sleep mask.

I get 8-9 hours reliably. Sometimes I wake up, but sometimes I don’t, and that’s amazing. When I do wake up in the morning, I have this incredible relaxed feeling in my muscles. My sleep goes so deep I have to lie there for ten minutes, slowly waking up. It’s great!

Will post more when I get a chance… there’s a lot to tell… but wanted to say, “I feel like a success!”

Dr. Kruse is a genius.

### fitness@home — 2015-05-04T22:49:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-20-to-80-ill-call-that-success.14312/post-163294>

It's great to hear how well you are doing. Nice improvement! Can't wait to hear more...

### WereBear — 2015-05-05T02:40:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-20-to-80-ill-call-that-success.14312/post-163305>

WIth my very first protein smoothie on the Leptin Reset Protocol I got through my day at work, back in July. This was fantastic, because I was used to feeling drained by 10 AM. On a good day, I could sorta make it to 2 PM. So this told me I was on the right track at last. But increasing cold kept my feet out of the grass, and by November last year it was getting dark at 4 PM and I was feeling it. Mind you, I have a lightbox on my desk at work. It came with a warning about "too much leading to mania" and I was all "Bring it on" and punching it for another session.

So I was not making any progress. Not exactly backsliding, but stuck. Then I got up one Saturday hoping I could work on my own creative projects for a change, but I was all feverish. When I knocked back some White Willow Tincture my husband had made (he's an herbalist) I felt so much more energetic. I decided the inflammation was blocking the cortisol from working on my cells so I kept taking small amounts all day. (It was originally what aspirin was derived from.) And it did wonders.

So for a couple of months I took a combo of White Willow Bark and Devil's Claw, which work synergistically so I don't have to take as much of either. It improved my sleep and my energy. I phased them out and switched to Niacin.

Which has been amazing. Next post so this one won't get too long.

### caroline — 2015-05-05T05:17:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-20-to-80-ill-call-that-success.14312/post-163313>

Just awesome Pam ....So very glad that you posted your successes! This will help so many

And Dr. K. is only scary some times!

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-05-05T07:59:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-20-to-80-ill-call-that-success.14312/post-163322>

really nice to read this

### WereBear — 2015-05-05T18:20:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-20-to-80-ill-call-that-success.14312/post-163374>

I stumbled onto the White Willow/Devil's Claw for sleep by missing a dose and doubling up before bedtime. And got GREAT sleep. Inflammation again. Is there nothing evil it won't do?

It seemed that I was doing everything I could but my thermostat was "stuck." In the old days, they would have said my "health was broken" and I would get sent away to a rest home with fresh air, low stress, you know, one of those places in the country. And then the lots of fish/homemade coconut oil mayo/grounding/circadian-nurturing stuff I was doing would have a better chance of gaining traction and resetting that thermostat.

However, in my case, no one believes in that anymore, and I couldn't recreate it with a full time job, disabled husband, and vital side project. So I was now knocking the inflammation back with herbs to let the cortisol in the cells, because when I kept up with it I had energy during the day and great sleep at night. So the herbs were a great short term solution, but I was wary of trying to make them a longterm solution. I moved it to one big dose at bedtime with good results, which demonstrated that great sleep would then give me more energy during the day.

Now I noticed that I felt almost normal much of the time, but I had a marked physical reaction to stress that would knock me back to my worst state. And how bad was my worst state? Well, I no longer have any of my little makeup sponges in my eyeshadow, because my hands had no fine control and I'd drop them on the floor, and there's no washing such things. I used my fingers. My hands just had no strength.

Early on, I screwed the top on my blender cup crooked and I just melted down in tears because I couldn't unscrew it from its crooked state. I finally managed to brace it against something in a way where I could use my large muscles, because my small ones just weren't reliable. And I couldn't open my car door the usual way. I had to put my shoulder against it and brace my legs against the transmission hump and use my torso to shove it open.

So when I got better it was obvious how much better because such superhuman adjustments didn't have to constantly be made. Which was its own form of stress relief! But a crisis at work, argument at home, or a glitch in my constant task juggling would create the sensation of strength actually draining from my body. I could keep going in the moment, but a serious crash was inevitable.

So it was now a mental state issue. My stress circuits were just burnt out or something; and that would influence how my body used cortisol. I'd dropped coffee at the beginning and it did help, but what helped even more was replacing it with roasted cocoa beans. No sugar, hardly any carbs, just a bit of heavy cream and some spices and I had a heavenly morning drink to replace it. And it was healing my glucocorticoid receptors. There's studies! So chocolate became a vital part of my daily routine. It's science, people!

I'm getting to the niacin. And the Brain Wave app.

### WereBear — 2015-05-05T19:21:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-20-to-80-ill-call-that-success.14312/post-163380>

So in the midst of this development plateau, I ran across an app called Brain Wave. It uses Binaural Brainwave Entrainment -- by sending two different frequencies to either ear, it encourages the brain to choose the "middle path," a pattern which we can choose with the programs on the app. I soon fell in love with the Reduce Anxiety setting, a 40 minute program. By running it on myself every day, I could FEEL my brain relax, and my muscles followed. On days I forgot it, I could feel a profound difference _in my head_ the next time I ran it.

I concluded **anxiety** was a large chunk of my remaining problems. Perhaps even previous anxiety; stress from the past. Because I had a pretty high opinion of my coping skills NOW. The Brain Wave App worked great for me NOW. But there was still something snagging my progress. So it was a great time to discover high dose niacin.

Turns out, Dr. Abram Hoffer of Canada had great results using 1g to 3g of niacin daily on patients with schizophrenia, anxiety, and PTSD. I figured if it helped survivors of Japanese POW camps, it would help me. I am _blown away_ by how well it works on this aspect of my issue.

I was able to phase out the herbs, it seems to regulate my appetite, I can take 1g at a time with only some skin tingles (I did have the full flush the first few weeks, but not anymore) and it took my sleep into yet better realms. My skin seems to love it, my dreams are in full color HD, and I have at least three other people try it with excellent results.

I take 500mg at three intervals during the day, with B complex and C to help it out, and then at bedtime a 1g dose with 1g of Liposomal C.

From Dr. Hoffer's experiences with the POW survivors, niacin seems to help reset the disordered endocrine system of PTSD sufferers. While I'm not saying my past was that bad, it was pretty bad for pretty long. And now, after all my success at meditation, philosophy, and structured coping skills, niacin works on my body as well as all that stuff I was doing for my brain.

Makes you wonder if there's a line separating them at all

### WereBear — 2015-05-06T03:34:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-20-to-80-ill-call-that-success.14312/post-163411>

As far as my EMG efforts go... to quote Dr. Evil, things got weird.

I ditched our cordless phones and got my husband his own cell phone, which he uses on speaker. I use my airtube headset, which is great — far more comfortable than holding the phone to my ear. We got rid of our boxspring and metal bed frame and got a wooden frame with drawers — more storage. But further elimination hit major snags.

We rent an old apartment with plaster and lathe walls so rewiring is problematic. In any case, my laptop can pick up between five and ten other WiFi signals passing through the house, from the college and the nearby motels, as well as the neighbors. So I installed a Schumann Resonance Generator device, the [Total EMF Shield](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00BQJ7BJC/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o08_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1). Which I found out about through this Forum!

Because I wanted a “control,” I didn’t tell my husband. I just smuggled into the house and set it up in the back of a bookcase. That first night, we each added two hours to our sleep. After a couple of weeks, I confessed. But since he had noticed the improvement in his sleep, he didn’t mind.  Now he fondly refers to it as our “NASA Space Device.”

It worked so well that I noticed when I left the apartment. My office is in a WiFi hot spot and when we got the heat fixed… we got a wireless thermostat. I have an open window by my desk, a wired Internet connection for my computer, and my office is farthest away from the router, so I was doing okay there. But sometimes I have to work in the lobby, and I started getting headaches. Also stores, such as the hardware store where people walk around with wireless headsets; I would get a headache as soon as I walked in.

So I got an [Earthcalm pendant](http://www.earthcalm.com/emf-protection-pendants-bracelets/) I always wear. And no more headaches.

I found a company who makes [shielding tiles](http://www.shieldyourbody.com/product/stick-on-6-inch-tiles-to-shield-emf-radiation/), and they are wonderful. I’ve got one across the back of my purse pocket for my cell phone, one over my charging brick for my laptop, and two across the bottom of my laptop case if I’m on the road. At home, I use a lap desk which has a sofa pillow added to the bottom, to give me six inches of space between the computer and my lap. Every electronic thing near the bed is on a powerstrip which I shut off when it's bedtime.

My iTouch is on airplane mode most of the time, as previously noted. That’s now my reading device of choice. I’ve even changed my writing process; I got Dragon Dictation for my computer so I can dictate drafts while keeping my distance!

I had previously thought the biggest trigger for my illness had been the new building my previous office was in, which had new everything and it was all outgassing formaldehyde. And I’m sure it didn’t help. But now I wonder if part of the blame should be from the banks of routers that blanketed the building.

Fortunately, I’m now in a more low-tech office. With a park across the street. Now that the weather is so nice, that’s where I will be having lunch!

### nicld — 2015-05-06T16:13:16+0300

I still do all I can to avoid EMF, but it's hard to make a living these days without exposure, that's for sure.

### WereBear — 2015-05-20T03:34:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-20-to-80-ill-call-that-success.14312/post-164751>

Had a retreat weekend by a lake, (mostly a work assignment. Mostly) beautiful spot with no WiFi and restricted cell phone usage. Just what the doctor ordered!

I managed even though I could not do my morning smoothie, and the food was very good, much of it cultured, though rather Weston Price -- bit light on the protein, but they always had gluten-free options.

Still, I did very well considering I couldn't control my environment, AND forgot my sleep mask! Though that was okay because there was no light pollution.

However, the one snag was when I woke up at 1 AM on my second day, midst of a panic attack, and couldn't get back to sleep. Things got worse and worse, to the point where I was ready to do anything to make it stop... and I realized I had brought my pregnenolone... and had not been taking it. Fool.

Took 150 mg, and in five minutes I felt much better. In fifteen minutes I was asleep again.

Amazing stuff.

Simple and cheap and no downside to any of it.!

### WereBear — 2015-05-25T17:31:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-20-to-80-ill-call-that-success.14312/post-165297>

Taking it easy on a three day weekend; I have so many creative projects going that unplugging is difficult, but of course it must be done!

Last weekend I went away on a three day wellness retreat. Most of it was very good; restricted Wifi, turned off cell phones, and a whole bunch of people who understand Airplane Mode. The food was delicious, very Weston Price and fermented and such, but protein light and starch heavy. Despite my good intentions, eating that way threw me off; especially since my environmental control was less than optimum. I needed the extra carbs, but have successfully gone back to my 2 meals a day this weekend.

And will be resting for the first time in 2 weeks.

# Thread: Native American Wisdom
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/native-american-wisdom.14335/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jeanne — 2015-05-05T18:55:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/native-american-wisdom.14335/post-163379>

I've been talking to my daughter about circadian signals, light and all I am learning from Jack and this site. Specifically, I am attempting to get her out in the sun more. She found this quote and sent it to me saying that Jack Kruse's words are way more intellectual but she thought this was saying the same thing in a more romantic way:
"The old Lakota was wise. He knew that a man's heart apart from nature became hard. He knew that lack of respect for living, growing things soon led to a respect for humans too. So, he kept his children close to nature's softening influence." Luther Standing Bear (Lakota indian chief)

### Josh — 2015-05-05T21:45:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/native-american-wisdom.14335/post-163392>

[http://www.amazon.com/Grandfather-Native-Americans-Lifelong-Harmony/dp/042518174X](http://www.amazon.com/Grandfather-Native-Americans-Lifelong-Harmony/dp/042518174X)

### nonchalant — 2015-05-07T18:08:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/native-american-wisdom.14335/post-163531>

> [Jeanne said:](/goto/post?id=163379)
>
> I've been talking to my daughter about circadian signals, light and all I am learning from Jack and this site. Specifically, I am attempting to get her out in the sun more. She found this quote and sent it to me saying that Jack Kruse's words are way more intellectual but she thought this was saying the same thing in a more romantic way:
> "The old Lakota was wise. He knew that a man's heart apart from nature became hard. He knew that lack of respect for living, growing things soon led to a respect for humans too. So, he kept his children close to nature's softening influence." Luther Standing Bear (Lakota indian chief)

I like that, Jeanne, thanks for posting. When we are sick, our world shrinks down until we get something like tunnel vision. Concern for nature goes away, then concern for others, and finally concern for our own selves.

### tjmackey1@hotmail.com — 2015-05-26T17:36:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/native-american-wisdom.14335/post-165429>
