# Thread: Is DHA an amplifier of the sun's power?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-dha-an-amplifier-of-the-suns-power.14911/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2015-05-31T02:08:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-dha-an-amplifier-of-the-suns-power.14911/post-165992>

I'm just wondering if I got this right:
The more photonic power from the sun we get (living closer to the equator or summer time between 10 AM and 4 PM approx when we get the most blue light), the less DHA we need. The sunlight (blue wavelengths) will destroy the DHA in the tissues because it is too much power. The more we get away from the equator, the less blue light we get from the sun, so we require more DHA to basically 'amplify' the sun's power that we do get from the other wave lengths (morning and evening light). That photonic power gets transferred to the electrons that DHA has collected to power life.
So modern life's 'artificial light' tells us that we don't need the DHA , destroys it, but since we are disconnected from the earth we cannot collect electrons either.(?)

### Jude — 2015-05-31T03:41:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-dha-an-amplifier-of-the-suns-power.14911/post-166003>

Hey Captain
Blue light=light signal=needs to be converted into electrical signal for optic nerve to send to SCN.
That ability requires DHA (in melanopsin( iirc) in the eye.)
DHA is degraded by nnEMF's... especially the blue light used by technology today!
Therefore, epi paleo addresses maintenance of DHA and avoidance nnEMF helps in consistent levels DHA . This protocol (plus other tools given by JK) gradually yokes our brain/gut axis, which is purpose of the Leptin Rx.

Check out what Yew posted in Matty's journal.
Yew explains in simple terms, that when the Brain /Gut axis is not properly linked it precipitates all the rest of ones issues -.
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...ould-use-some-advice.12816/page-9#post-164863](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/page-9#post-164863)

### yewwei.tan — 2015-05-31T03:57:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-dha-an-amplifier-of-the-suns-power.14911/post-166004>

It's the opposite actually, that the further you are from the Equator (given our current solar environment), the less natural blue and UV light there is. Thus the body becomes adapted to dealing with less blue light. See this post -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/how-much-sun-to-get-melanin-and-dha.14741/#post-165535](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/how-much-sun-to-get-melanin-and-dha.14741/#post-165535)

You need less DHA at the equator because skin can capture the light from the sun to energise blood plasma (you still need some DHA, and still need lots of sulfated cholesterol).

\----

Also, blue light doesn't just "magically destroy DHA". More accurate is that blue light stress stimulates DHA and EPA degradation into resolvins and protectins, as a protective mechanism -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...on-fish-oils-and-dha.14227/page-2#post-163657](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/some-perspectives-on-fish-oils-and-dha.14227/page-2#post-163657)

Sidenote: in the body we're mainly concerned about DHA in cell membranes and RBCs. Yes, free DHA found in something like fish oil pills will automatically degrade with photo-stimulation via blue and UV light, but the body's context is different.​

Net effect is still that you have less DHA, but this is also part of the natural response to the blue and UV wavelengths found in sunlight, and is thus also a form of signalling in the body. Note that this is also why DHA needs to be constantly replaced in the body via dietary consumption, regardless of how healthy you are.

\----

In general, artificial light at the wrong time provides a circadian mismatch, and increases systemic positive charge (remember that inflammation == positive charge), and demands a systemic anti-oxidative response. It's the circadian mismatch that is the key factor, not necessarily DHA in particular. Though you will probably find decreased DHA on cell membranes, alongside decreased Glutathione, lower cholesterol, etc ....

At the retina specifically, and to a slightly lesser extent in the skin, then yes, that's where direct photo-oxidation from UV light will directly stimulate DHA and EPA degradation.

....

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-05-31T08:05:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-dha-an-amplifier-of-the-suns-power.14911/post-166007>

Thanks guys for the responses. That means I'm moving in the right direction and some references to read.

# Thread: East European Theoretical Biologists Ervin Bauer and Dr. Vladimir Voeikov

### PaulG — 2015-06-01T03:58:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/east-european-theoretical-biologists-ervin-bauer-and-dr-vladimir-voeikov.14917/post-166065>

Ervin Bauer

-   [The Universal Principle of Biology: Determinism, Quantum Physics and Spontaneity](http://www.konkoly.hu/staff/grandpierre/Grandpierre2014Universal_Principle_of_Biology.pdf)
-   [2013 WAS THE 75TH ANNIVERSARY OF ERVIN BAUER’S DEATH](http://real.mtak.hu/21855/1/jav_215_226_u_103609.448362.pdf)
-   [Water Respiration - The Basis of the Living State](http://www.waterjournal.org/uploads/vol1/voeikov/WATER-Vol1-Voeikov.pdf) (by Voeikov VL and Del Giudice E)
-   [Ervin Bauer as Pathologist](http://www.orvostortenet.hu/tankonyvek/tk-05/pdf/5.1.1/2006_194_195_elek_muller_ervin_bauer.pdf)

Dr. Vladimir Voeikov

-   [Reactive Oxygen Species, Water, Photons, and Life](http://www.researchgate.net/profile/Vladimir_Voeikov/publication/50305420_Reactive_oxygen_species_water_photons_and_life/links/0912f5138a7e3d8c80000000.pdf)
-   [http://voeikov.com/](http://voeikov.com/)
-   [http://www.researchgate.net/profile/Vladimir\_Voeikov](http://www.researchgate.net/profile/Vladimir_Voeikov)

See also

-   [Biological Extension of the Action Principle: Endpoint Determination beyond the Quantum Level and the Ultimate Physical Roots of Consciousness](http://blog.one-in-all.org/wp-content/uploads/2008/02/wavefunctions.pdf)

# Thread: Pg cream and sleep disturbance.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pg-cream-and-sleep-disturbance.14961/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2015-06-01T17:18:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pg-cream-and-sleep-disturbance.14961/post-166128>

I have been realizing the past couple of months that my sleep is disrupted the second half of my cycle when I am on my Pg cream (or getting close to a full moon) and it never used to be like that. First half of the cycle, I sleep though the night and usually wake up naturally with the bladder needing to be emptied. Second half, the bladder is waking me up between 2:30 and 3 am to be emptied. I fall back asleep but it is really annoying.

This summer I will be doing some testing to figure out what my levels are at. I will take a month off and get the Pg tested and then have it tested while on the cream. I have tried switching when I apply the cream too. It all used to be at night but then the sleep issues started so I switched back to splitting it between am and pm like doc recommended. I have not tried doing it all in the morning yet but I have skipped some night doses with round.

My questions for those smart people out there are the following.

Is it better to get the Estrogen tested while on the Pg cream or off?

Is being off the cream for a cycle enough to see what is going on?

Should I also do the saliva cortisol test during the second half of the cycle when I am having sleep disturbances? While on the Pg or off?

### Penny — 2015-06-01T19:05:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pg-cream-and-sleep-disturbance.14961/post-166130>

Well, I'm no doctor and brain function has been a pachinko game for years but here's my take on labs in general - it's a complicated body with thousands of reactions happening - 100000/sec ? The point is, you are only going to be able to get a snippet in time and with so many variables in play \*constantly\*, it will only give you a rough "guesstimate" about what's going on - but, for that issue, here is what I am doing - I am doing the progesterone cream at night for a straight month, then getting a complete hormone panel - Life Extension had a blood test for $200.00 that literally tested \*everything\* I'm interested in - including thyroid, blood sugar stuff, hscrp, vit d, hormones - the more testing and the more frequently the testing is done, the more info you may glean, but alas, money does not grow on trees...

The idea being to get a menopausal pg/e ratio to give me a rough idea of how screwed up my hormones might be... the other bizarre thing to me is the body does not produce pg at the same rate each day, so why pray tell, do we put 1/4 tsp on our wrist every night? There is the Wiley protocol, which seems to attempt to address that, but how successful it is is anyone's guess... when we crowbar the body, we shouldn't expect stellar results because truly we are not smarter than the body...

Pg is required to make cortisol - which is why it is given to people with "exhaustion" - so it seems to me it depends on what you want to test - if you're testing cortisol without the "crutch" - do it without - optimally, a baseline test with nothing added would be good, then add a protocol and test again - like debugging a software program - except this one has a million inputs...

Dr. Kruse had something on the full moon which escapes me at the moment - my mind is like a sieve... but I find wearing black out shades helps somewhat when the light of the full moon is shining - I have also heard the yeast are more active during a full moon - I've also heard from a lab that's when parastites lay their eggs and it's a great time to test for them - I'm currently using Oregon Grape Root for menopause et al - interesting properties for a lot of things - blood sugar - cool stuff -

I have almost no menopause symptoms - maybe a little more of a pachinko game in my head, but nothing else - I am guessing it is because I don't break down estrogen at some level and what made me a hormonal mess in my youth is now saving me in menopause... I also take melatonin which is said to postpone menopause - I know it's not a hot idea, but I protect sleep at all costs - sleep+autophagy

You might try adding for grins and giggles P5P and some zinc to your nightime ritual as they are required to make serotonin - the zinc is a cofactor to make P5P from b6 - b6 can be toxic for you in high amounts, thus the p5p recommendation -

I have had amazing luck with reading by a red incandescant light bulb and using ice packs at night - going going gone

Waking up a 2:30 could be a spike in cortisol caused by blood sugar crashes - I use glycine before I go to bed for that - also, having carbs/alcohol at night puts my sleep in the crapper - according to ub4, I am ubiquiting like crazy - oh well, just another nail in the ol' coffin

### JanSz — 2015-06-01T21:27:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pg-cream-and-sleep-disturbance.14961/post-166137>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=166128)
>
> I have been realizing the past couple of months that my sleep is disrupted the second half of my cycle when I am on my Pg cream (or getting close to a full moon) and it never used to be like that. First half of the cycle, I sleep though the night and usually wake up naturally with the bladder needing to be emptied. Second half, the bladder is waking me up between 2:30 and 3 am to be emptied. I fall back asleep but it is really annoying.
> \~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
> \~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
>
> Should I also do the saliva cortisol test during the second half of the cycle when I am having sleep disturbances? While on the Pg or off?

while on progesterone
2:30 and 3 am when you wake up
test your saliva cortisol
preferably do that over few nights
likely high cortisol wakes you up

do not test if you do not wakeup voluntarily
,

### JanSz — 2015-06-02T00:11:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pg-cream-and-sleep-disturbance.14961/post-166153>

(insurance should cover the bill).

Salivary Cortisol, MS
Test Number: 500179 CPT Code: 82533
eight (8) serial samples
Salivette tubes (item # 51.1532) and
specialized patient collection kits
255.3
255.8
255.4

.............................

### nicld — 2015-06-02T15:56:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pg-cream-and-sleep-disturbance.14961/post-166251>

# Thread: To nicotine, or to not nicotine....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-nicotine-or-to-not-nicotine.14967/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KetoKatie — 2015-06-02T03:25:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-nicotine-or-to-not-nicotine.14967/post-166181>

I want to give 2 mg nicotine gum a try, esp because I was helped in my 20's by high doses of Wellbutrin and I have narcolepsy (N), aka nonfunctioning orexin neurons...in retrospect I think the Wellbutrin is what kept my N at bay enough to have some semblance of a career (for better or for worse depending on whether it would have been good to burn out earlier or when I did, after I had twins with special needs but am not working). Nicotine patches are a common stim for PWNs (people w narcolepsy), and some self medicate with cigarettes. Although I have been stim free for 2 years (was on Nugivil for 11 months til diet/lifestyle changes a la Jack took over what nuvigil could do for me), I need a boost, esp if I am supposed to pack up our place & move us (STILL nothing even worth looking at has come around but doing better about not stressing about it, overall).

Side note: I am SOO looking forward to the boost I'll get from being able to ground and CT all the time in a real tub (trough) and get early morning sun easily, all of that. And move out of nnEMF hell and it's affect on me. I know things will really start to gel at that point. But I need to last and get to that point for that to happen.

I got a virus in January I am not sure I fully got rid of. Because I am not in a great place physically, it took 8 weeks for acute symptoms to go away and 8 weeks for fatigue to mostly fade. So I afraid I am not well enough to try....

Which leads me to the question behind this message: what contra-indications are there, and besides the obvious Epi-paleo, CTing and circadian, what else might be good to consider having in place ie neurotransmitters, etc? I don't know enough to even know where my blind spots are in this...

But then again, if it's just a bad few hours, that's not the end of the world....

+++
My list of current and recent issues: N, POTS, Hashi's, PCOS, adrenal insufficiency that seems to be resolved, high blood pressure that seems to be resolved, 20 year history of depression & anxiety, which is all resolved for the last 3 years except when I get frustrated because of my health status...so any that comes around is short lived. I currently only take Armour. Magnesium. And probiotic...sometimes a whole food Vit C.)

+++
Never have even taken a drag off a cigarette, both because I wasn't cool in high school and because I am afraid of addiction, had a sense I would be instantly addicted. It's better now on the addiction front (other than disordered eating, I haven't had much) but those inclinations aren't all gone and probably never will be because of my genetics and my N.

### Inger — 2015-06-02T17:41:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-nicotine-or-to-not-nicotine.14967/post-166270>

Hi Katie! I cant really answer your question... but what I do have been thinking about regarding the nicotine gum use (I have been using them since I stopped smoking this year  )... is this.

It could be important to spit out the first "juice" after chewing a few times as that way you get rid of most of the xylitol/additives in the gum. The nicotine will be inside the gum anyways. I do that lately, I chew a few times, do not swallow and and then spit. Yeah... I know... a little TMI   but for the gut, especially if not optimal, less xylitol will be better....
then
to chew gum kinda produces some air i guess that you will naturally swallow... maybe not so good for the gut either? IDK for sure... just a thought... so if you have a sensible gut nicotine pads might be better option? But they you cant for sure use the same way as the gum as they release nicotine way slower..... I have used pads too.

### KetoKatie — 2015-06-03T20:32:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-nicotine-or-to-not-nicotine.14967/post-166451>

Thanks for the info, @[Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/). I am giving it a go, though started my first one late morning today because I was too antsy to wait til tomorrow. And today is a much better day for me to feel cruddy than tomorrow if it's not good (though so far so good). Normally will do it soon after waking. I spit some of the beginning stuff like you suggested, but maybe not enough. It lasted a lot longer than I expected but it could also be that I am not a fan of spicy stuff so I'm sensitive to it/sense it being there longer than a smoker might?

Hopefully I won't regret this down the road, like wearing on my adrenals or whatnot...but the more I analyzed what has helped me in the past and what I'm experiencing now and I can tell I need dopamine/catacholemine support, so hopefully this will help that.

Good for you for quitting! I don't know that patches come in 2 mg? I just wanted to see how I felt so I'll start with the 2 mg gum, only cost me $7 for a small pack of 20 pieces, not a lot of investment at 1 per day.

### Josh — 2015-06-03T20:49:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-nicotine-or-to-not-nicotine.14967/post-166453>

If you are hardcore, you can get liquid nicotine on Amazon. I take it straight. Absorbs quickly in the mouth. Be very careful with dosing.

### nicld — 2015-06-04T17:14:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-nicotine-or-to-not-nicotine.14967/post-166550>

Interesting. I will have to read more on what context that nicotine is beneficial.

### Shijin13 — 2015-06-04T17:24:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-nicotine-or-to-not-nicotine.14967/post-166551>

for now I'm holding on the nicotine... working on P450 pathways...and tightening my circadian cycles.....

### WereBear — 2015-06-04T20:07:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-nicotine-or-to-not-nicotine.14967/post-166566>

You might consider high dose Niacin (also known as vitamin B3 or _nicotinic_ acid). It might offer the effect you are seeking, and I find it an important part of my latest regimen.

My husband was concerned that my illness struggles could be alleviated with some SSRIs, but I was extremely wary, because I felt that part of my problem was the Premarin that I took for over a year. It alleviated my tormenting menopause symptoms but I think it contributed to my hormone problems in the end. SSRIs have a slow ramp up and a tapered end game; so if it was making me worse instead of better it could really set me back.

But my research led me to the work of Dr. Abram Hoffer, who treated POW survivors in Canada after WWII. He found high dose niacin did great things for PTSD and stress reduction, even schizophrenia and psychosis.

So I tried it, with a Bcomplex every day and vitamin C with each dose, and it's been amazing stuff. Great sleep, better stress handling, appetite more in line with what Dr. Kruse says should happen (I skip dinner half the time now.)

When I mentioned the diabetes risk with the SSRIs my doctor offered Welbutrin, but when I looked up the side effects it said it was contraindicated for eating disorders? And I was bulimic in my teens. (Just mentioned that because of your note about disordered eating.)

I definitely want to try that. I have a friend who takes 9 grams of tyrosine, so I hear ya on the high doses. My one problem is just figuring out which supps to try in what order and how long to leave between introducing new supps/major changes. I ended up with major cognitive decline (that thankfully reversed itself within 48 hours after stopping the supps in question, ALA {I think, it was a while ago and have it on my notes to self} and acetyl l carnitine...) A month just seems to drag things out, but might be required for things that have more ramping time like tyrosine and 5 HTP.

### KetoKatie — 2015-06-14T20:43:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-nicotine-or-to-not-nicotine.14967/post-168126>

> [WereBear said:](/goto/post?id=166566)
>
> You might consider high dose Niacin (also known as vitamin B3 or _nicotinic_ acid). It might offer the effect you are seeking, and I find it an important part of my latest regimen.
>
> My husband was concerned that my illness struggles could be alleviated with some SSRIs, but I was extremely wary, because I felt that part of my problem was the Premarin that I took for over a year. It alleviated my tormenting menopause symptoms but I think it contributed to my hormone problems in the end. SSRIs have a slow ramp up and a tapered end game; so if it was making me worse instead of better it could really set me back.
>
> But my research led me to the work of Dr. Abram Hoffer, who treated POW survivors in Canada after WWII. He found high dose niacin did great things for PTSD and stress reduction, even schizophrenia and psychosis.
>
> So I tried it, with a Bcomplex every day and vitamin C with each dose, and it's been amazing stuff. Great sleep, better stress handling, appetite more in line with what Dr. Kruse says should happen (I skip dinner half the time now.)
>
> When I mentioned the diabetes risk with the SSRIs my doctor offered Welbutrin, but when I looked up the side effects it said it was contraindicated for eating disorders? And I was bulimic in my teens. (Just mentioned that because of your note about disordered eating.)

I did Wellbutrin for 6-7 years at high doses. The Leptin Rx did wonders for my disordered eating, and my time in an eating disorder program (outpatient therapy I sought out after having twin \*girls\* that I didn't want to pass on my food issues to) also helped. But Bs in general would be a good idea....esp Niacin.... Thanks for the suggestions.

### KetoKatie — 2015-06-14T20:46:52+0300

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=166551)

> for now I'm holding on the nicotine... working on P450 pathways...and tightening my circadian cycles.....

I need to read more about these pathways (and catch up on the Ubi system, my printer isn't working right now)....

### KetoKatie — 2015-06-14T20:54:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-nicotine-or-to-not-nicotine.14967/post-168129>

> [KetoKatie said:](/goto/post?id=168127)
>
> I need to read more about these pathways (and catch up on the Ubi system, my printer isn't working right now)....

My circadian has been good the last two weeks, after recommitting to being diligent (had been staying up too late a few nights...interesting that when my motivation tanks, I stay up...I think it's motivation that goes first, but not always). My circadian rhythm doesn't get too far off these days, I just stay up later than I should sometimes, but otherwise good--after a day or two of that I pay and have to get back on track fast... Love the early morning sun that helps me wake up early these days.

Summer makes this easier to stay on rhythm, but being off of screens is the harder part for me to work on, even with blueblockers and f.lux turned WAY down, I need to be off earlier to make sure I can stick to it as a habit when the sun sets much earlier than 9:30 pm...

### KetoKatie — 2015-06-14T21:07:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-nicotine-or-to-not-nicotine.14967/post-168130>

So I tried 2 days, took 4 off and tried 1 more, of 1 piece of 2 mg gum ea day. The first 2, I thought it took the edge off my tiredness for most of day. I \*think\* I might have been able to push myself a little harder than normal, but it was also my highest energy days in my cycle. Basically, I didn't have the urge to nap residing in the back of my brain the way I do most days (due to this virus and narcolepsy...it's not overwhelming most days, just there). It went away for about 4 hours also the 3rd day I tried, but not all day...but that was also one of my most tired days.

I didn't try more because of the digestive upset it caused. The first day was a higher Bristol than my usual 4, but not bad. By day 2, it was obvious my digestive track didn't like it. On the 3rd try, after an hour or two I could feel the rolling through my intestines. It didn't speed things along too much, but there was a definite sense my body didn't want it, as is the case with certain foods, and I have worked too hard to rid myself of IBS that I don't want to revisit it. So that is a sign my body doesn't want this now, at least in this form.

I guess it's possible that a pure form of nicotine would be better, but my friend with similar IBS, N, and other issues gets the runs from stimulants (incl nicotine patch and she used to be a smoker), so I think it goes beyond the gum.

It was an interesting experiment to try.

# Thread: SSRI's and brain damage
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ssris-and-brain-damage.14969/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-02T06:49:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ssris-and-brain-damage.14969/post-166201>

I've been taking some kind of SSRI since 1991... that's 24 years!! (holy crap.)

At the time it was a godsend! It rescued me from the darkest depression, so many years went by before I even thought about getting off of it
I noticed that it made me tired chronically but that was better than the depression!
I am on a much lower dose nowadays but does anybody know about the long term effects of these drugs and whether they are permanent or not? I'm wondering if my dopamine system is completely trashed, making me depend on stimulants.
Oh, one other 'interesting' side note; when I came down with that serious depression back in '91, I was taking an avionics course at Vancouver International Airport, not far from the South Terminal control tower.....
...did I hear a 'boom'?

### Inger — 2015-06-02T18:17:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ssris-and-brain-damage.14969/post-166282>

You have the answers on this site
Just load on seafood, raw... and fatty... and oysters
do not forget to spend time in nature too
depression will be gone fast I predict

# Thread: Setting the SCN, BAB, then back to bed..is this okay?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/setting-the-scn-bab-then-back-to-bed-is-this-okay.15023/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-03T08:40:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/setting-the-scn-bab-then-back-to-bed-is-this-okay.15023/post-166386>

On the weekends I've been getting up when my alarm goes off just like I would on workdays. I go outside and look toward the sun for 2 minutes or so, then I have my BAB .

But then I go back to bed and sleep for another 1.5 -2 hrs.

I find that in the mornings I am soooo tired that I have actually phoned in sick occasionally because of this. But then I pick up a bit around 11 am and in the evenings I am 'ok'.
Still tired but not quit as bad.
I wonder if the reason that I am so tired in the AM is because of me going back to bed on the weekends after setting my SCN and BAB(?)

### JanSz — 2015-06-03T16:46:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/setting-the-scn-bab-then-back-to-bed-is-this-okay.15023/post-166434>

We sleep in cycles.
When you wake up to alarm clock, you are interrupting sleep cycle.
Consider going to bed 1-2 hrs earlier so you can wake up naturally (without alarm).

.

### Peertje80 — 2015-06-03T20:22:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/setting-the-scn-bab-then-back-to-bed-is-this-okay.15023/post-166449>

In the weekend I sometimes went back to bed for 1 or 2 hours after BAB. This made me feel totally drained. But I wanted/needed more sleep

2 weeks or so ago I decided to get more sleep and set my alarm half an hour later (and start work later) then I would normally do. Funny thing is I now wake 1 minute before the alarm goes. Pretty OCD hehe. But my energy feels some what better. No 2 o'clock crash this week.

### Lahelada — 2015-06-04T01:22:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/setting-the-scn-bab-then-back-to-bed-is-this-okay.15023/post-166470>

;So you put yourself in a vertical position,went outside maybe even barefoot. that means that your body has experienced gravity in an upright position and if you walk barefoot the daytime magnetic frequency of earth. And then you put yourself horizontal again....
Says one mitochondria to the other : "short day dat !"
Agree with the others . Better to add on the hours at the front end.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-04T04:17:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/setting-the-scn-bab-then-back-to-bed-is-this-okay.15023/post-166485>

Thanks guys for the replies. I'm gonna try keeping myself up in the weekend AM and not go back to bed. It will be tough but I've been pretty suspicious that not fully getting up early on the weekends is at least partly to blame for my fatigue.
A big cup of coffee and a bike ride should help.

> [http://www.greenmedinfo.com/blog/can-humans-photosynthesize-1](http://www.greenmedinfo.com/blog/can-humans-photosynthesize-1)

From article:
The authors pinpoint melanin as the central player in the drama of photosynthesis, arguing that melanin, a black substance prominent in certain tissues, absorbs all visible wavelengths. Those concentrated photons could then drive the photosynthetic process in the same way as photons do in green plants and many single celled organisms.
\=======================

I am white Caucasian with fairly white skin, but have enough melanin to get relatively nice tan.

For the last few years I am using Melanotan peptide (MT-2).
With that I am able to get much deeper brown skin (much more melanin), newer get even hint of sunburn, able to be exposed for many hours to sun with out feeling any stress.

I am also supplementing with vit D and the blood test indicate very good levels.

Always wondered how healthy it is to have deep dark tan?

..

# Thread: Crowd Source the Epi-Paleo Store
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crowd-source-the-epi-paleo-store.15112/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ColinGorham — 2015-06-04T20:23:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crowd-source-the-epi-paleo-store.15112/post-166569>

Crowd Source the Epi-Paleo Store

I think Members and Visitors/Guests to this site could play a more active role in a dialectic process of identifying, testing, and sorting products that could be sold through the Epi-Paleo Store to assist in implementing what Dr. Kruse graciously teaches us here. At present, we are often buying products outside the affiliate network of the Epi-Paleo store because it is not practical for one person (Jack) to identify, pick, test, all the potential products that could help people on the road to optimal. An amazon store could be a place where we could all add products to a list for sale, where we could rate each product introduced and Jack would have the ability to unilaterally exclude non-optimal/ potentially harmful products from the store. I envision a store that is a laboratory for testing, experiencing, analyzing, and vetting products where it is clear that the products are not endorsed but rather are in the process of being evaluated by the group. We all find products that help on the road to optimal but at present there is no central place to post them for group testing, analysis, and evaluation. I realize that the Circadian Biohackers Group on Facebook has a store but these same limitation exist with their small store. Perhaps we can create a "This product works for me thread" organized by protocol and specific product type. For instance: shoes that enable grounding, glasses that block blue light, etc....

# Thread: Saturated Fats Just Keep Getting Better
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saturated-fats-just-keep-getting-better.15115/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dennis Clark — 2015-06-04T22:24:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saturated-fats-just-keep-getting-better.15115/post-166586>

Julia Child loved to say that you can never have too much butter. That goes for coconut oil, too.

The main ingredient of interest seems to be myristic acid, which occurs in butter fat, coconut oil, palm kernel oil, and a low amount in some animal fats.

One of the coolest studies to come out recently involves stopping tauopathy with myristic acid. Lucky mice.

From the article sidebar on its significance:

_Neurodegeneration is tightly linked to tauopathy as a result of the overactivated cyclin-dependent kinase (cdk) 5 in the form of cdk5/p25. P35, another activator of cdk5, which is short-lived and myristoylated, is cleaved by calpain to the degradation-resistant activator p25. One way to tune down the aberrant, overreactive cdk/p25 is to reduce available p35. The sigma-1 receptor (Sig-1R) is an endoplasmic reticulum (ER) chaperone of unknown relation to tauopathy. Here we found that Sig-1Rs bind myristic acid and promote the degradation of p35. Further, the aberrant tau hyperphosphorylation and stunting of axon elongation in Sig-1R–KO neurons are mitigated by exogenously added myristic acid. Our results establish the role of ER Sig-1Rs in tauopathy and implicate the importance of myristic acid as a substance to fight against neurodegeneration._

Full article download here: [http://herbscientist.com/2015-myristic-acid-and-axon-extension.pdf](http://herbscientist.com/2015-myristic-acid-and-axon-extension.pdf)

This adds possibilities besides benefits of ketosis for brain health (a few cites here: [http://herbscientist.com/coconut-oil-and-brain-health/](http://herbscientist.com/coconut-oil-and-brain-health/)).

Put the lime in the coconut and drink it all up.

I want to order some labs for my friend that has a some health issues in the past and is concerned about making sure she is ok now. I'm slowly trying to influence her to changer her environment and eating habits.

She is 22 years old and when she was a teenager she had a cyst on one or both of her fallopian tubes and they removed them along with some of her ovarian glands.

She complains of mild anxiety and has acne on her back.

She eats a diet with a lot of carbs and snacks on high omega 6 nuts.

I know an essential lab to get is the hs-CRP. What other labs should I get for her? I want to cover all the bases. thyroid, kidney, hormones, ect.

Thanks for the help!

# Thread: Big ass breakfast in Montaigne
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-in-montaigne.15181/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-06-07T13:21:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-ass-breakfast-in-montaigne.15181/post-166922>

Here's Michel de Montaigne on what I think are the Caribs in his essay "Of Cannibals"

> They rise with the sun, and so soon as they are up, eat for all day, for they have no more meals but that: they do not then drink, as Suidas reports of some other people of the East that never drank at their meals; but drink very often all day after, and sometimes to a rousing pitch. Their drink is made of a certain root, and is of the color of our claret, and they never drink it but lukewarm. It will not keep above two or three days; it has a somewhat sharp, brisk taste, is nothing heady, but very comfortable to the stomach; laxative to strangers, but a very pleasant beverage to such as are accustomed to it. They make use, instead of bread, of a certain white compound, like Coriander comfits; I have tasted of it; the taste is sweet and a little flat. The whole day is spent in dancing. Their young men go a-hunting after wild beasts with bows and arrows; one part of their women are employed in preparing their drink the while, which is their chief employment.

[http://oregonstate.edu/instruct/phl302/texts/montaigne/montaigne-essays--2.html#IV](http://oregonstate.edu/instruct/phl302/texts/montaigne/montaigne-essays--2.html#IV).

Of course, Montaigne's informant (or his source if the informant is a literary device) may be mistaken. Nevertheless, it's interesting to learn that these people may have had no other meal than one very large one eaten just after dawn. And the description of the drink sounds very plausible to me. This sounds very much like some kind of lactic-fermented brew - perhaps made by that chew-and-spit method - and would doubtless be beneficial to the microbiome. And if it was "laxative to strangers" perhaps this was because it caused their own microbiome to adjust.

I also rather liked this comment in the same essay which starts with over-bred fruit and morphs into something rather sententious, a bit "philosophical" in the vague sense, and certainly unprovable, but still appealing:

> They are savages at the same rate that we say fruit are wild, which nature produces of herself and by her own ordinary progress; whereas in truth, we ought rather to call those wild, whose natures we have changed by our artifice, and diverted from the common order. In those, the genuine, most useful and natural virtues and properties are vigorous and sprightly, which we have helped to degenerate in these, by accommodating them to the pleasure of our own corrupted palate. And yet for all this our taste confesses a flavor and delicacy, excellent even to emulation of the best of ours, in several fruits wherein those countries abound without art or culture. Neither is it reasonable that art should gain the pre-eminence of our great and powerful mother nature. We have so surcharged her with the additional ornaments and graces we have added to the beauty and riches of her own works by our inventions, that we have almost smothered her; yet in other places, where she shines in her own purity and proper luster, she marvelously baffles and disgraces all our vain and frivolous attempts.

Interesting to find Montaigne musing on some of the same issues as our contemporaries.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Old Jack Tweets..

### thomas — 2015-06-09T18:02:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/old-jack-tweets.15267/post-167236>

For those of you wondering how to find old jack's tweets.(no this isn't the name of some whisky)
You first go to twitter. then you type this in the search:

> from:drjackkruse since:yyyy-mm-dd until:yyyy-mm-dd

y is year, m is month, d is day.. simple.
then you click on "direct" at the top.
2011 tweets:[https://twitter.com/search?q=from:drjackkruse since:2011-01-01 until:2012-01-01&src=typd&vertical=default&f=tweets](https://twitter.com/search?q=from%3Adrjackkruse%20since%3A2011-01-01%20until%3A2012-01-01&src=typd&vertical=default&f=tweets)

2012 tweets:
[https://twitter.com/search?q=from:drjackkruse since:2012-01-01 until:2013-01-01&src=typd&vertical=default&f=tweets](https://twitter.com/search?q=from%3Adrjackkruse%20since%3A2012-01-01%20until%3A2013-01-01&src=typd&vertical=default&f=tweets)

2013 tweets:
[https://twitter.com/search?q=from:drjackkruse since:2013-01-01 until:2014-01-01&src=typd&vertical=default&f=tweets](https://twitter.com/search?q=from%3Adrjackkruse%20since%3A2013-01-01%20until%3A2014-01-01&src=typd&vertical=default&f=tweets)

2014 tweets:
[https://twitter.com/search?q=from:d...2015-01-01&src=typd&vertical=default&f=tweets](https://twitter.com/search?q=from%3Adrjackkruse%20since%3A2014-01-01%20until%3A2015-01-01&src=typd&vertical=default&f=tweets)

# Thread: Here's the tiny human twig in the Tree of Life
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heres-the-tiny-human-twig-in-the-tree-of-life.15272/>
**Subforum:** Factor X
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gracelind — 2015-06-10T00:41:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heres-the-tiny-human-twig-in-the-tree-of-life.15272/post-167281>

_"Here's the tiny human twig in the Tree of Life
This is what 3.5 billion years of evolution looks like. Time to meet your relatives." { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/info-animalss_1024-jpg.1947/)

. . .

[Mark Wilson explains at Fast Company](http://www.fastcodesign.com/1670898/infographic-humans-are-just-a-twig-on-the-tree-of-life):

"As you look at the graphic, realise that time radiates outward and each kingdom’s appearance is also in chronological order from left to right. What you’ll discern then is a story of origins and mass extinctions, the way life almost bided its time through the Ice Age then hit the gas through the Cambrian Explosion. It was here when the protostomes (everything from trilobites to squids) simply went nuts, and the separation of plants vs. animals as we know them arose.”_

Larger image at:
[http://e.fastcompany.net/multisite\_files/codesign/slideshow/2012/09/1670898-slide-0-evo-large.jpg](http://e.fastcompany.net/multisite_files/codesign/slideshow/2012/09/1670898-slide-0-evo-large.jpg)

# Thread: Craving Carbs During Fat Adaptation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-carbs-during-fat-adaptation.15447/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty_M — 2015-06-14T00:01:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-carbs-during-fat-adaptation.15447/post-168000>

I am working towards optimal and one thing that is pretty challenging for me is remaining in ketosis. I have read many of the blogs regarding carb cravings and leptin resistance, however I am still struggling a bit. I am often pretty low on energy (while eating pretty low carb) and often find myself having carbs just to feel better, even when my goal is to remain in ketosis and work towards the PPP.

\- I am getting good sleep outside grounded in my tent (bottom cut)
\- seeing the sunlight for a few minutes (shirt off)
\- avoiding fake light/wearing my dark red glasses after sunset
\- grounding, getting fresh air, playing in dirt
\- CT (working on getting more ice)
\- mindfulness
\- avoding artificial light/using gunnar glasses
\- having a good time in general and doing things i like to feel great
\- red light therapy (20 min on lower back nightly)
\- epi paleo diet (leptin rx+ autoimmune protocol
\- ~gallon of ice cold spring water daily

I do still get a decent amount of fake light and nn EMF exposure (especially from school- should decrease over summer). I really want to be able to remain ketogenic but my body is so drawn to carbs after a week to a few weeks of pretty low carb (only from veggies/coconut/avocados).

Could it be methylation, minor EMF/fake light exposure, situational (family) stresses, still dehydration?

**What are the primary potential factors that would create this response in me?** This is something big to conquer on my quest to optimal. It has improved a lot, but it could get better- and the lack of energy affects my daily life a lot.

Any advice/thoughts/etc. are greatly appreciated.

Thanks,

\- Matt

PS I am getting a bunch of labs soon that could be revealing so I will post them and link that thread here.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/r-o-water-and-super-cooling-question.15450/post-168016>

Dr. Kruse recommends R.O. water because of its ability to be 'supercooled' which means that when the water temperature is dropped below 0 degrees celcius it continues to stay a liquid. It will eventually freeze at about -7 degrees celcius (Sorry, I grew up on metric ..sort of).
So the theory is that it works much better for CT.
My question is: When I do CT, I definitely do not go below 0 degrees! (32 degrees F).
When I CT its usually between 45 and 55 degrees F.
Am I still getting the benefit?

# Thread: Is R.O. water really best? Some doctors advise AGAINST it!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-r-o-water-really-best-some-doctors-advise-against-it.15451/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-14T03:06:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-r-o-water-really-best-some-doctors-advise-against-it.15451/post-168017>

There are a few people on the net that are advising against reverse osmosis water when trying to treat brain disorders saying that they have had bad experiences with it and that it doesn't hydrate the body very well.
Dr. Kruse says, "Question everything I say". To me, that's the sign of a true professional who is willing to put ego aside in the quest for the truth and willing to admit a mistake if need be (I'm not implying that he has made a mistake!). I'm just a little confused as to why other health care practitioners are against using it.

Here are the links:

1) [http://www.drlwilson.com/articles/water.htm](http://www.drlwilson.com/articles/water.htm)

**"Summary.** Water filtered by reverse osmosis or RO is not good drinking water, in my experience. Its effects can be terrible, including even kidney failure. I know that sounds extreme, but several cases have occurred that I know about.

It does not matter if someone has added minerals back to the RO water, boiled it, or done other things to it like praying over it or “energizing” it in any way that I have seen, so far. These methods do not seems to undo the damage to the water that occurs due to passing it through the plastic RO membrane. Please avoid all water made this way for best hydration."

2) [http://www.mosesnutrition.com/forms\_and\_articles/water-for-health-and-longevity/](http://www.mosesnutrition.com/forms_and_articles/water-for-health-and-longevity/)

-
    **"3.** Reverse osmosis also appears to damage the water significantly, for drinking purposes. The interaction of the water with the plastic membrane alters the spin and moment of the water. It does not appear to hydrate the body nearly as well, in our experience. This is unfortunate."

3) [http://www.shirleys-wellness-cafe.com/earth/water](http://www.shirleys-wellness-cafe.com/earth/water)

"I do NOT drink distilled water or reverse osmosis water because of the [demineralizing](http://www.shirleys-wellness-cafe.com/earth/water#minerals) effects on the body."

### bionaut — 2015-06-14T03:18:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-r-o-water-really-best-some-doctors-advise-against-it.15451/post-168019>

The advantage of RO water is

a) it contains no dielectric blockers
b) it can be supercooled below standard freezing temperatures

To get the best benefits the RO water needs to be as cold as possible this creates a massive exclusion zone and delivers a lot of protons to the gut.

I have been drinking it exclusively for about 3 yrs. Have not had any demineralizing effects that I have noticed, however I do cook with a mineral salt called "real salt" to get anything I may be missing.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-14T03:45:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-r-o-water-really-best-some-doctors-advise-against-it.15451/post-168022>

> [bionaut said:](/goto/post?id=168019)
>
> The advantage of RO water is
>
> a) it contains no dielectric blockers
> b) it can be supercooled below standard freezing temperatures
>
> To get the best benefits the RO water needs to be as cold as possible this creates a massive exclusion zone and delivers a lot of protons to the gut.
>
> I have been drinking it exclusively for about 3 yrs. Have not had any demineralizing effects that I have noticed, however I do cook with a mineral salt called "real salt" to get anything I may be missing.

Thanks for the reply. I started drinking R.O. water a few weeks back and I actually had a week of decent energy but in order to get a second 5 gallon water bottle I had to buy it pre filled with spring water. My energy has since waned but whether it was the water or not I really don't know. I don't know how many times that I have tried a supplement or done something different and had a surge of energy and good mood, only to have it disappear and leave me disappointed. I hope it was the water but I'm not holding my breath.
I'm going to refill both bottles tomorrow and see what happens.

### jwhb77 — 2016-01-14T20:40:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-r-o-water-really-best-some-doctors-advise-against-it.15451/post-186302>

### Tobiah — 2016-01-15T00:37:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-r-o-water-really-best-some-doctors-advise-against-it.15451/post-186323>

Include 'spin' to what water you use. Make or buy a water vortex trap.

### Cpt.Tired — 2016-01-15T20:57:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-r-o-water-really-best-some-doctors-advise-against-it.15451/post-186394>

> [jwhb77 said:](/goto/post?id=186302)
>
> Cpt. Tired, just wondering how your ongoing 'water' experiences have gone since your latest post here? Thanks...

I do tend to feel better if I consciously drink a LOT of water. Not right away but usually the next day or so. Right now my dopamine is trashed so I tend to forget a lot. Hopefully that will change when we start getting a bit more sunshine.
I drink R.O. as much as I can but on low redox days I still will default to my filtered tap water. I know its not great but its better than nothing I guess.

# Thread: The heart is not a pump
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-heart-is-not-a-pump.15454/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-06-14T08:26:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-heart-is-not-a-pump.15454/post-168049>

This, famously, is a statement once made by the Austrian philosopher Rudolf Steiner. I believe he added that we needed to learn what this meant.

Steiner was something of a mystic, and it's not clear that _he_ knew what it meant. Sometimes he would come up with these comments, and sometimes people would ask him questions and he'd say he'd get back to them and go away and do whatever he did and then come back and say something like that.

I should imagine if one were to google that phrase one would find people quoting that and offering it as proof that Steiner was a charlatan. Because … because there are a lot of unimaginative and rationalistic people around. It's not that we shouldn't be cautious when someone tells us something that hasn't come to him by the ordinary processes of thinking—as we should when it _has_ come that way, too, I'd add—but dismissing something purely on that account is less justifiable that it reflexively seems to a certain sort of person. And current belief—that the heart is a pump, for example—is not self-evidently true simply because that's what everyone currently thinks.

Be that as it may, this is a topic that has sometimes come up among the WAPF people. (There's an interest in Steiner in the WAPF.) Tom Cowan has given lengthy explanations here and there, trying to show that, if you actually understand hydraulics and look at what's going on you will see that actually the heart does not, and could not, function as a pump. I think he says it would be more accurate to say that it "is pumped" than that it "pumps". I'd have to look at the explanation again: hydraulics is not something we covered in A level physics all those years ago.

By coincidence I've heard the phrase again twice recently. The first time was when I came across and listened to an old podcast interview of Sally Fallon. Here she said that Dr. Tom had been thinking about that phrase for many years and added that she thought the bit about our needing to understand what this meant had quite wide social application. Her point was that the heart is in some sense at the centre, and that to see it as pumping is to see it as applying force. What it does, she said, is to _listen_, to listen and coordinate. That's what the centre, in her view, should do in social systems.

The second time was when I was listening to someone else whose name escapes me. I can't even remember where. (I think it may have been a guest on Evan Brand's podcast.) He just suddenly said, "The heart is not a pump". He then added that it's an electromagnetic structure more than it's anything else, and that the magnetic field from the heart can be detected up to 22 feet away. This, of course, made me think of this place, since one of the things it's associated with for me is "the body electric". ...

### yewwei.tan — 2015-06-14T10:30:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-heart-is-not-a-pump.15454/post-168052>

Paper: **A novel hypothesis for atherosclerosis as a cholesterol sulfate deficiency syndrome** \-- [http://www.tbiomed.com/content/12/1/9/abstract](http://www.tbiomed.com/content/12/1/9/abstract)

Notes: [http://tanyewwei.com/notes/cholesterol-sulfate-atherosclerosis/](http://tanyewwei.com/notes/cholesterol-sulfate-atherosclerosis/)

The heart exerts some mechanical pressure to push blood to regions whereby distances are small enough for electro-osmotic interactions to dominate (and likely quantum mechanical ones too).

There is also suggestion that the magnetic field emitted by the heart enhancers these distal electro-osmotic forces.

....

### Gagnrad — 2015-06-14T11:31:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-heart-is-not-a-pump.15454/post-168058>

> [yewwei.tan said:](/goto/post?id=168052)
>
> Paper: **A novel hypothesis for atherosclerosis as a cholesterol sulfate deficiency syndrome** \-- [http://www.tbiomed.com/content/12/1/9/abstract](http://www.tbiomed.com/content/12/1/9/abstract)

Yes, Stephanie Seneff again - mentioned in an earlier thread in connection with cancer. In her view the tumour is making lactate for the heart, because it can't use glucose efficiently when there's a deficiency of cholesterol sulphate.

It's seeing connections - or possible connections - between things that's interesting, isn't it?

# Thread: Eating Chinese Food, building bridges and making maps
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-chinese-food-building-bridges-and-making-maps.15531/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2015-06-16T15:23:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-chinese-food-building-bridges-and-making-maps.15531/post-168386>

I have been mulling over two quotes today..

1.  "A Picture is worth a thousand words but a map is worth a thousand pictures"
2.  "You don't need to speak Chinese in order to like Chinese food"

**Mapping the knowledge**
The [Quilt](http://www.jackkruse.com/the-quilthow-to-beat-agin/) is a picture that looks like a map but is not like the London Tube map which is designed to help people from various countries get from a to b across London.
I am a big fan of this [website](http://www.oscillatorium.com/id68.html) and think this visual hyperlink concept could be created for Jack Kruse website.

**Extracting the knowledge and building a bridge**
Although there is a [quick start guide](http://www.jackkruse.com/getting-started-guide/) I think many newcomers are overwhelmed by the sheer quantity and depth of knowledge on the blogs and in the forum. If I were to classify types of people here what most of us want is actionable steps plus acquiring various degrees of understanding (based on need, temperament, motivation). From my experience of being a long term forum member and blog reader the actionable stuff is perhaps 10% of the iceberg but the iceberg is not a good analogy as so long as there is light you can see the 10% easily but here and in the blogs it takes some (considerable) time, effort and reading to discover the actionable steps that are interspersed with the science or discussion (forum, Quantum Health Facebook group). It is of course dangerous and irresponsible to give blanket recommendations without context and without giving the why so I am not suggesting that but am suggesting that the information can be structured in a top-down way (like an pyramid)

**Actionable Steps**
The visual concept is similar to this [collapsible jquery plugin demo](http://www.snyderplace.com/demos/collapsible.html) in which top level are the actions and subsequent sections contain summary, detailed, cites, videos, images etc.

Some steps such as Eat an Epi Paleo Diet, Avoid blue light at night, get morning AM sunlight are broadly applicable to most people so would be classified as Level 1 Recommendations. Level 2 would be context-dependent and not so easy to summarise into an easily digestible form.

**Terminology Extraction**
Another idea I have had is to investigate the use of Terminology Extraction tools/api's that could be used on blog posts or the forum to auto-generate significant keywords or a glossary of terms.

[Terminology Extraction](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Terminology_extraction) or **Terminology mining**, **term extraction**, **term recognition**, or **glossary extraction**, is a subtask of [information extraction](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Information_extraction). The goal of terminology extraction is to automatically extract relevant terms from a given [corpus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Text_corpus).

Suggestion is to parse all posts using a terminology extraction algorithm/service and present to the user a Glossary of Terms. Clicking on the term returns ordered list of most relevant threads/posts.

As a test enter in the last JK Blog URL and see what happens??

[Demo 1](https://dandelion.eu/semantic-text/entity-extraction-demo/?text=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.jackkruse.com%2Fubiquitination-17-gears-of-the-eye-clock%2F&lang=en&min_confidence=0.6&exec=true#results)
[Demo 2](http://www.alchemyapi.com/products/demo/alchemylanguage)
[Demo 3](http://ultimate-research-assistant.com/)

**Hash Tagging**
This [add-on](https://xenforo.com/community/resources/tinhte-xentag.770/) for the forum software would allow tagging

### PaulG — 2015-06-16T15:44:03+0300

# Thread: Random Questions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-questions.15610/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lookingforanswers — 2015-06-18T16:11:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-questions.15610/post-168756>

I've been doing extensive reading lately and have just compiled a list of very random questions! I'm hoping someone can chime in on some of these and provide some answers or point me in a direction where I can find them myself.

1\. I find that any time I introduce a new food I just totally binge - whether it's a veggie, fruit, whatever. I feel like it has something to do with finally getting a new flavor or texture after being practically zero carb for about 2 years. DH thinks it's just because my body desperately NEEDS the carbs, but I could seriously just go crazy and consume so much of it that I end up bloating and sick. Thoughts on this?

2\. It seems like when I first start a new food (bok choy, cabbage, rice, etc) I do well with it for a day or two, but then it's like it builds up in my system and after that short time my body just can't handle it. S ome things will cause major stomach pain and ALWAYS I end up just burning up like I have a fever. This always gets worse at night. By the time it all happens though, it's like I'm totally addicted to these foods and just can't stop eating them.

3\. My urine has a very strong odor after eating carbs. I also end up with incomplete bladder emptying and wake in the middle of the night to pee. Sometimes it smells like rice/cereal, other times it takes on the smell of the veggies I ate. What's causing this?

4\. I can't digest fat at all either and end up with gallstones which also leads to the burning up - it's mostly the back of my neck where you can literally hold your hand about 6" away and just feel heat radiating from it. If I consume carbs and fat together - it's just unbearable! My entire body is like a furnace and it's so uncomfortable I can't sleep.

5\. Vegetables worsen my constipation and I thought this was from the fiber, but I tried some red lentils last week and they didn't have the same effect even though they are even higher fiber. They did cause the burning up and stomach pain though, so I'm not eating them anymore.

6\. I had two SIBO tests that were negative, but I just don't believe them at all. Nearly all of mysymptoms are consistent with SIBO and I have been told there's a 25% false negative rate, especially for the methane producing bacteria that cause constipation. I'm also wondering about more of a yeast overgrowth. A stool test didn't show any yeast issues, but that wouldn't show anything in the small intestine, right? Is there a good SIBO protocol you would suggest following?

7\. When I do consume carbs, I find they make tolerating CT so much harder, but I know I definitely need them. They help with the constipation, energy, mood, skin, etc, etc. With my limited diet and reactions to oxalates, salicylates, histamine, etc I'm really limited in the carbs I can handle. I've been trying some white rice, but after a few days that leads to the burning up too. Any other suggestions on foods to try? Not knowing what to eat is the hardest thing!

8\. I've been told to take Calcium Citrate before meals to bind to the oxalates, but after everything I've read about Ca efflux from EMF I'm nervous to do this. If it's binding to the oxalates though, then my body isn't storing it, right? So do you still see a problem with Ca supplementation?

I realize this is a long list - my mind has been spinning like crazy lately trying to apply what I'm reading to what I'm experiencing, so any help is so appreciated!!

### Lahelada — 2015-06-18T16:51:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-questions.15610/post-168759>

1.yes you do need carbs but not the ones you think and not as many as you think. Have you tried sweet potato,blueberry?

2\. how about cycling stuff? Carbs one day,fat the next.

Questions: how do you relate to vinegar? Just a hunch but try taking vinegar with your food. You may have to leave gourmet considerations aside and think medicinal recipes.

### lookingforanswers — 2015-06-18T19:34:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-questions.15610/post-168776>

Thanks for the help @[Lahelada](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6531/) ! I've been wanting to try sweet potato, but it's very high in salicylates, so I'm a bit nervous. I will make that my next new food attempt though and keep my fingers crossed!! Blueberries should be in season here very soon and I'm planning to give them a try too.

Cycling stuff is a good idea and something I keep telling myself to do. My problem is, like I said, I end up binging and just can't give these foods up once I start them! It's crazy! I need to somehow force myself to have better control.

As far as gourmet, ha! I haven't even thought gourmet for 6 years now. I just want variety! The most gourmet I get is having fresh lemon juice on my fish! Vinegar I haven't tried in quite a while because of histamine issues. It used to cause major burning up and a super red, hot face and neck with very blotchy skin all over. I have just recently been taking about 1/4 - 1/2 tsp of coconut water kefir, which before would also cause histamine issues and so far, so good, so hopefully soon I'll be able to try vinegar.

### seanb4 — 2015-06-28T15:38:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-questions.15610/post-169768>

I think you might be missing something in the foods you crave, but like you say, eating the foods then causes other problems, catch 22. It's similar for me, been low carb + restrictive for ages and I think it has lead to deficiencies.

I guess you could try different foods and hope they don't cause issue or just try to have them in small amounts. Maybe if you have them before eating a lot of meat and fat it will reduce the need to binge? Also are you getting any seaweed / leafy greens?

### Peertje80 — 2015-06-20T00:12:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/panic-attack-help.15651/post-168968>

I have no experience with panic attacks like you, but the moment is maybe somewhat similar. Have you tried breathing exercise without the bag? Go outside. Sit on the ground (earth). Straighten your back, close eyes. Focus on your breath and try to slow it down by inhaling and exhaling deliberately and deeply for at least a minute or ten. Even if you can't, just do it. Your mind takes slower breath as a key to slow down the panic. You sort of have to breathe through the panic moment. I used this when I had 'the night cramps/muscle spasms moment' or 'fear of having them moment' (of the 2, the last was the worst to have with the anxiety in anticipation of the coming pain and agony). After you calmed down you could try some light reading in red light to help you fall a sleep. I love red light they really make me relaxed and drowsy if I can't sleep.

### Peertje80 — 2015-06-20T14:31:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/panic-attack-help.15651/post-169034>

> [b612 said:](/goto/post?id=169027)
>
> Thanks  I tried to breathe like you said this morning... I think my diaphragm is blocked. I can't take deep breaths because my muscles start to spasm. Feels like my lungs have a very small capacity or something... Need to continue breathing deeply, probably it will help to unblock all those contracted muscles...

Buddha said: "What the mind regularly pounders on will become the inclination of the mind". But it becomes the inclination of the body too. If you have pain or fear the body reacts. Muscles become tensed. You adapt your posture to your emotion or pain. If pain and fear are chronic, then your body will have this all the time. After some time you (you body) won't remember the natural state or relaxed state. Of course all this has a physiological impact with stress hormones going through the roof and making havoc in your body (or maybe even vice versa). But it has a big psychological part too. It will take practise to relax mind and body again and to let go. When your chronic situation ends, you often have to learn your body and mind that there is no pain or fear any more.

To create space for your diaphragm and loosing up the muscles try pushing your belly out with some force (mimic pregnant belly) while inhaling, tense a bit and the relax while exhaling. It is important that your shoulders are relaxed and hanging down. If you pull them up, the focus of your breath is upward, meaning in the chest or even higher in the neck/upper chest.

You could maybe also try Wim Hof method (I have not read the CT series of Jack yet but I believe there is something in there about breathing. I'm not familiar with his instruction though), but I find this method to active for relaxation. But it is good for practising breathing muscles of course.

### shads — 2015-06-21T11:31:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/panic-attack-help.15651/post-169124>

> [b612 said:](/goto/post?id=168940)
>
> Could someone help me here, please...
> What to do when panic attack strikes? What is the best thing I could do?

What are you thinking when you have episodes of panic? Anything specific? I found that writing down the thoughts going through my head during or just after a panic attack was really helpful. You might find that there is a particular thought or belief that causes you to spiral into a state of panic. I think there is usually a secondary fear involved in issues with panic - ie you start feeling the physical symptoms of a panic attack coming on which which makes you feel anxious and start fearing having a panic attack, which makes the physical feelings worse, which makes you more anxious and so on until its worked up in to a full blown state of panic.

Maybe start writing down in a diary the activating event for the panic attack and also the beliefs that are going through you head at the time - ask yourself during and afterwards if they they rational and/or based on any evidence? Did what you were panicking about happen? Remember that the panic attack itself can't harm you.

Passionflower is really helpful with general anxiety, lemon balm is also good. Motherwort can help with palpitations. You can get each in a separate tincture and just squirt each one in a bit of water when you are feeling on edge.

Meditation and deep breathing is going to help big time as people have said. Try and do 10 minutes every day when you wake up, and check your breathing at several points throughout the day. Make sure its slow, quite and deep, through the nose. Check out Andrew Weil for some breathing techniques. Headspace is a good app for guided meditation.

### thomas — 2015-06-21T15:38:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/panic-attack-help.15651/post-169136>

I 'd propose to meditate even more. at least 20 min. I notice between 20 to 30 minutes my mind really calm down. I use breathing meditation. Do it outdoor if you can obviously!

It plays on one emotion that may be dominant in our life right now.An aria takes you through a progression culminating in something ,a flight of frequencies ,even if you do not like it intellectually speaking.

> We basically hate gmo's in france as a culture. I think they are banned. at least most of them.

Yes, pesticides are very much to the point. Essentially, that's what the GMOs are - "pesticide delivery systems" as it's been termed. The golden rice was a publicity stunt, seems not to do what it's been claimed to do and is a tiny side-issue. The big ones are Roundup-Ready crops, which are meant to sell Roundup pesticide, and the Bt technology, where the pesticide is built-in.

[http://thesymbiontfactorblog.com/tag/bt/](http://thesymbiontfactorblog.com/tag/bt/)

GMOs may be in the food-chain in European countries. They won't be grown there. Neither will they be for sale on the shelves, because they'd need to be labelled, and so no one would buy them. But they're likely used in animal feed: I know that's true of the UK, and I doubt France is any different.

> The global Animal Feed Industry, as of early 2011, was estimated at output of US$240 billion annually.[\[1\]](http://www.sourcewatch.org/index.php/Animal_Feed_Industry#cite_note-Panorama-1) In 2010, the industry produced an estimated 717.6 million metric tons of complete [animal feed](http://www.sourcewatch.org/index.php?title=Animal_feed&action=edit&redlink=1) globally. **The United States accounted for just under 155.3 million metric tons, or 21.6% of world production**.

[http://www.sourcewatch.org/index.php/Animal\_Feed\_Industry](http://www.sourcewatch.org/index.php/Animal_Feed_Industry)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/r-o-water-on-the-magnetico-questions.15673/post-169007>

I've heard Jack say a number of times, "Put your water on your Magnetico." to magnetize it.
Does this effect last a while after taken off the Magnetico or does it have to be drank immediately?
Does the effect last longer if put in the fridge?
My guess is that the rate of decay of the Magnetico's effect depends on how much it is disturbed by EMF, light,heat and vibration.
Since a piece of metal can hold its magnetism for a while after the real magnet is pulled away maybe water does the same?
How bad is the effects of the fridge's EMF on the structure of the water? Does the cold effects outweigh the benefits of the EMF?
What I do currently is I have two big bottles of R.O. water downstairs which I pour into a jug that I put in the fridge. I thought that maybe I should put a bottle on the magnetico during the day and just fill a jug when I get home and put it in the fridge.
I'm sure there are much better ways but as a bridge for now (?)
Is checking the pH a good idea?

### Lahelada — 2015-06-20T18:24:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/r-o-water-on-the-magnetico-questions.15673/post-169048>

I would make RO ice in teh fridge and then add it when you want to drink water. I would not put the water in the fridge. Unscientific opinion but if water has memory the ice will get assimilated . Hey everyone,

I have just found a discussion on youtube with a german physicist about electromagnetic waves and mobile phone dangers. He talks about scalar waves, about whom I read at wikipedia and are hypothetical waves because the shall travel faster than light.
Someone ever heard about that?
For friends of the german language I link it.

Interesting question: Why you can make a phonecall inside a microwave oven...

Ok, I googled and found something in the forums, but no big post. But it seems it is a big thing.

### Christos — 2015-09-05T04:26:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-ever-heard-of-the-term-scalar-wave.15685/post-176519>

If you wave an electron back and forth you get a transverse wave propagation through the aether, which is what light is. This is what is used for mobile phone communication. However two charged particles resonating together exert a longitudinal wave through the aether to communicate with each other. This is a scalar wave, and its speed is almost instantaneous. This is what Nikola Tesla was working on. Its possible this may be used for future mobile phones. Hope this helps.

# Thread: Hopi Prophecy and the End of the Fourth World - Part 1
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hopi-prophecy-and-the-end-of-the-fourth-world-part-1.15688/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-06-20T18:31:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hopi-prophecy-and-the-end-of-the-fourth-world-part-1.15688/post-169050>

I don't want to appear credulous, but … No, actually, I don't care. Readers are welcome to make of this post whatever they choose.

Do I know if the Hopi can, or could, see into the future - or, perhaps rather, see possibilities that may work themselves out in certain ways? No, of course not. Nor do I know if the Scottish Highlanders credited with second sight really had it, or if the many other accounts around one might come across are valid or not.

What interests me is the connection between prophecy and a sense of transgression against the natural order in these people's thinking:

> … global destructions were not the result of merely random earth changes or astrophysical phenomena but of humankind’s disregard both for Mother Earth and for the spiritual dictates of the Creator. In other words, cataclysmic events in the natural world are causally connected to collective transgressions, or negative human actions.

[http://www.ancient-origins.net/myth...i-prophecy-and-end-fourth-world-part-1-002280](http://www.ancient-origins.net/myths-legends-americas/hopi-prophecy-and-end-fourth-world-part-1-002280)

### Jonathin — 2015-06-20T23:33:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hopi-prophecy-and-the-end-of-the-fourth-world-part-1.15688/post-169087>

This is a common mythos of many ancient peoples. If we aren't connected to the world around us now in the present then then these 'old stories' don't have much value. Prophecy seems like it solidifies the future rather than allowing for innovation.

### Gagnrad — 2015-06-21T09:36:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hopi-prophecy-and-the-end-of-the-fourth-world-part-1.15688/post-169122>

Well, I guess what they call "walking the zig-zag path" might quite likely "solidify the future" … and ourselves along with it, unfortunately.

I bought a grounding plug and wrist strap to keep me earthed whilst I work on my computer. Last night I grounded myself by using it as I slept. And it was crazy. C-R-A-Z-Y. I had some of the most vivid dreams I think I've ever had - the kind I used to have when I was a teenager, and maybe even more realistic than those.

It was like actually being there. Like quad HD, or 8k even. I was in this antiques yard, looking at all these ornate fireplaces and marble statues. At one point the dream turned into a nightmare and was quite terrifying.
