I thought it interesting to read that about Crazy Horse. Also, there's the same writer making that a criterion for wondering about multi-story buildings.

Native Americans - the shadow people around on the fringes who, if one only listens, point to what mainstream American society misses.

### caroline — 2015-06-22T09:09:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crazy-horse.15697/post-169234>

interesting Mike......what book are you reading?

please - can we have a picture......

### Gagnrad — 2015-06-22T19:05:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crazy-horse.15697/post-169263>

I expect I can find one around somewhere.

I'm looking at _The Driving Force_ at the moment, since Jack recently recommended it.

I'm not very far into it yet. It's not exactly entertaining, although there are certainly some significant points made even in the little I've read of it as yet. I simply was not aware of how important Weissman had been for shaping (and distorting) people's view of what Darwin had said. It's amazing really the level at which much public discussion goes on at with quite radical misconceptions and huge gaps in people's knowledge. I'm reminded of someone's recent comment that most dentists have never heard of Weston Price and that he'd yet to hear an oncologist mention Otto Warburg's name.

_The Driving Force_ has very small print, too, which is tiring on the eyes.

### Gagnrad — 2015-06-22T19:13:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crazy-horse.15697/post-169264>

Actually, maybe you meant where is the comment about Crazy Horse coming from?

It's Bear Heart a Native American medicine man in _The Wind is My Mother_:

[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/0425161609/](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/0425161609/)

I suppose the story is accurate. It doesn't seem to me that Bear Heart is always accurate in the way a historian would be. In fact, I'm quite sure he's not. It's the significance of things that matters to him. But I do know Crazy Horse was known for wandering around alone, and I guess he might well have done so barefoot, on account it affected his energy.

### Gagnrad — 2015-06-22T20:29:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crazy-horse.15697/post-169267>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=169234)
>
> please - can we have a picture......

OK. There's a place-holder up there now till I can find a suitable snapshot and resize it.

### caroline — 2015-06-23T03:28:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crazy-horse.15697/post-169284>

Thanks Mike ....I have missed hearing about all the books you read and the follow up discussions.... I just came across the poetry book you recommended to me quite awhile ago!

### Gagnrad — 2015-06-23T10:33:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crazy-horse.15697/post-169322>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=169284)
>
> Thanks Mike ....I have missed hearing about all the books you read and the follow up discussions.... I just came across the poetry book you recommended to me quite awhile ago!

Was that Yeats? Quite an eccentric AFAICT and one for the dramatic gesture. I like the story of him being spotted carefully disarranging his hair in a glass. I think even people who didn't care much for most twentieth century poetry - didn't like Eliot, for example - could get drawn in by the cadences and the hypnotically beautiful images in Yeats. "When you are old and grey and full of sleep, And nodding by the fire, take down this book …"

_The Wind is My Mother_ is very good. Bear Heart was a Creek (Muskogee) and seems to have qualified as a psychologist as well as being trained by his people in their ways. And for him there seems to be no real discontinuity between mainstream American Christian culture - his mother helped run a Baptist church - and Native American spirituality. I think he managed to bridge across different ways of thinking and talk to all sorts of different sorts of people in language they could understand and accept. He comes across as someone who must have been very comforting to be around.

EDIT

Here's a story from down your way!

Burrup Peninsula rock art shows extinct megafauna and Tasmanian tigers in WA

[http://www.abc.net.au/radionational...sula-rock-art-shows-extinct-megafauna/6561788](http://www.abc.net.au/radionational/programs/backgroundbriefing/burrup-peninsula-rock-art-shows-extinct-megafauna/6561788)

# Thread: Sobering words from Rowan Williams
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sobering-words-from-rowan-williams.15729/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-06-22T19:29:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sobering-words-from-rowan-williams.15729/post-169265>

I think Rowan Williams didn't go down very well as Archbishop of Canterbury, and was happy to leave the position and go back into academia. He seemed often to have difficulty in making himself clear and also seemed too politicised to some.

He really is a very intelligent and thoughtful man, as well as a very nice one, though. And he certainly knows most of the key works in theology and philosophy (in a number of different languages) and usually has something relevant to say about them. I liked this on the environment. I think there are moral and even spiritual dimensions to be taken into account here - as the native Americans among others recognise - and it's the fact that those have no place in the thinking of the likes of Monsanto (and, actually, the USDA, FDA, etc.) that helps explains why we're where we are. So here's the passage:

> Is this – as some would claim – a failure to trust God, who has promised faithfulness to what he has made? I think that to suggest that God might intervene to protect us from the corporate folly of our practices is as unchristian and unbiblical as to suggest that he protects us from the results of our individual folly or sin. This is not a creation in which there are no real risks; our faith has always held that the inexhaustible love of God cannot compel justice or virtue; we are capable of doing immeasurable damage to ourselves as individuals, and it seems clear that we have the same terrible freedom as a human race. God's faithfulness stands, assuring us that even in the most appalling disaster love will not let us go; but it will not be a safety net that guarantees a happy ending in this world. Any religious language that implies this is making a nonsense of the prophetic tradition of the Old Testament and the urgency of the preaching of Jesus.

[http://www.theguardian.com/commentisfree/belief/2009/mar/26/religion-anglicanism](http://www.theguardian.com/commentisfree/belief/2009/mar/26/religion-anglicanism)

Here at any rate he's crystal-clear and quite compelling.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

**G4 (Severe) Geomagnetic Storm**
When the Kp-index measures Kp=8, a G4 (Severe) geomagnetic storm is in effect.

**G4 STORM OVERVIEW**

-   G4 storms occur approximately 100 times per 11 years (1 solar cycle);
-   Power systems may experience widespread voltage control problems and some protective systems will mistakenly trip out key assets from the grid;
-   Spacecraft may experience surface charging and tracking problems;
-   Satellite navigation degraded for hours.

### seanb4 — 2015-06-24T18:58:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/constant-headache.15756/post-169477>

@[Lahelada](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6531/)

In regards to the nicotine gum, I have noticed light headedness and weakness.

As for OP maybe the headaches are caused from dehydration but not the sort that could be cured by drinking water. I have always (4yrs) been dehydrated and dry mouth. I have been mostly very low carb and restrictive diet. Since starting CT showers my dry mouth got way worse even though I was drinking more water. The water felt like it wasn't doing any good, maybe even the oposite effect. I am in the proccess of fixing this by eating more leafy greens and fruits and it is working. I think it is something to do with increasing my potassium, the structured water in the plants, and possibly carbs.

Anyway, when you were smashing the rice crispies, etc, you could have been getting nutrients that you are now missing, like potassium, and possibly carbs helps hydration somehow.

What has been mostly working for me is adding spinach/kale/potatoes/fresh oranges to my diet. There is a massive difference in dry tounge when I eat a few oranges as oppose to when I drink the equivalent in water. Maybe it will work the same for your headache?

**Posts:** 1

### Gagnrad — 2015-06-24T10:29:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/our-brain-is-shrinking-but.15790/post-169451>

… our frontal lobe is growing.

> Endocasts are moulds of brains created from the imprints on the inside of the skull; allowing us to see what the brains of long extinct species looked like. They’re an invaluable resource when studying human evolution, allowing us to track how our brain has evolved over the past few million years. But few people have looked at endocasts of more modern individuals. ...

[http://www.evoanth.net/2014/03/13/our-brain-is-shrinking-but-our-frontal-lobe-is-growing/](http://www.evoanth.net/2014/03/13/our-brain-is-shrinking-but-our-frontal-lobe-is-growing/)

Apparently, sexual dimorphism as regards brain capacity is also increasing, having narrowed very much in the past. Nobody knows why.

# Thread: Sooooo - where do you want to be/ who do you want to be in ten years time???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sooooo-where-do-you-want-to-be-who-do-you-want-to-be-in-ten-years-time.15807/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2015-06-25T05:17:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sooooo-where-do-you-want-to-be-who-do-you-want-to-be-in-ten-years-time.15807/post-169526>

This has been on my mind lately because of JK's pre cruise party ....

I totally get the premise ........but.......

I really felt that I couldn't go to that party because I am very happy where I have gotten myself to NOW - right this minute...

On one level I feel that maybe my thinking is wrong - I should keep pushing ....shouldn't I???

But - here's the thing .........I have never settled my whole life - I have always pushed and pulled and did whatever it took to get forward motion. I always strived to be the best that I could be, and I did a pretty good job if I do say so myself!

I never, ever took "no" or "I can't" or "maybe you shouldn't" or "that is impossible" as vocabulary to listen to - I have always been very stubborn and insisted any thing was possible.

In a way - it was easy for me because I started with nothing and no one ...... so I needed to only listen to myself - and the world was my oyster!

I suppose, in some eyes , I wouldn't be thought of as a huge success - I am not a neurosurgeon, or an astrologer, or a PHD in anything whatsoever......

But - I am pretty happy with the person I have become ......of course I want to learn more - that goes without saying. I wouldn't be here if I didn't want to stretch and learn and grow.......

Everyday there are endless opportunities to learn and grow and be kinder and gentler ..... so this is what I want to be still doing 10 years from now!

I don't want to get complacent and lazy and take all the gifts I have been given for granted ...I want and need to pay all this forward.

So - if it is okay ..... I am okay being this me in ten years time - an improved and evolved version....

I keep thinking I have missed the point ....

What say you?

### cantweight — 2015-06-25T05:43:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sooooo-where-do-you-want-to-be-who-do-you-want-to-be-in-ten-years-time.15807/post-169534>

In 10 years Id like to be running a wellness center. Full of light, water, magnetism, and love. Sounds corny....but I want to fix people so they can be truly happy.

### b612 — 2015-06-25T13:25:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sooooo-where-do-you-want-to-be-who-do-you-want-to-be-in-ten-years-time.15807/post-169568>

I would like to be me again. Healthy, beautiful, wild and creative. All the rest is just a decoration.

### caroline — 2015-06-25T15:32:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sooooo-where-do-you-want-to-be-who-do-you-want-to-be-in-ten-years-time.15807/post-169570>

Kristi and b6 ..... what lovely and inspiring dreams for the future....... we all need to help you get there

We are all entangled...... how lucky we are!

### prAna303 — 2015-06-25T18:45:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sooooo-where-do-you-want-to-be-who-do-you-want-to-be-in-ten-years-time.15807/post-169578>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=169526)
>
> But - here's the thing .........I have never settled my whole life.

I really think we use this word wrong today.

You are never in the wrong place or time, it is all about how we take that moment and act upon it. Sure life can have some big bumps but it must have them, as soon as we try to flatline the world we do the same to us. Why would we want to have the same heartrate for the remains of our days?

Today i think you have gotten your sync where it should be, on time with you and the world, this means not that you have become lazy and lame. The other way around i say...

Back to my own world... I can not think in these terms, what where and who am i gonna be? I can guess how one likes to make goals but it does not resonate with me, at all. Perhaps why i am single as a term but the opposite in real life. Have no family to take care of but many who i love and give my time to.

So, i would do the same as i do today... Breaking many ties to a society gone wrong, not caring about age and time, not playing family because i can not... I can not lie.

I will be benched at every dance performance i can see, i will spend my last dime on a glass of wine on a cold and beautiful winters night, i will do my acting, i will give my love to a cigar, i will turn up the volume on my sound system to enjoy Arvo Pärt, i will not take my eyes of that wonderful girl...

And to sum it up, words are to small but together with other senses.

### caroline — 2015-06-26T02:51:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sooooo-where-do-you-want-to-be-who-do-you-want-to-be-in-ten-years-time.15807/post-169600>

in sync with the world ....... yes. yes, yes! That is exactly how I feel. Every moment seems so extraordinary ........

It sounds a little silly and lame and over the top to say that ...... but that is how I feel all the time - I truly feel like I am going to burst. I can't help laughing and jigging around .....

and ....... this really isn't because a special person has come into my life......

this is because I got there before ..... I became a whole person, warts and all - accepting of myself and those around me and now I really listen and I really see.

It is magic to just be quiet and really see everything and everyone ......and enjoy it all.

I told this lovely man the other day that he is perfect .... and he understood......

perfect isn't about not having any flaws or foibles or whatever .... we all have those in spades!

It is about being present ......

### nicld — 2015-06-26T17:58:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sooooo-where-do-you-want-to-be-who-do-you-want-to-be-in-ten-years-time.15807/post-169676>

Sitting outside, naked, with no neighbors!!!

### caroline — 2015-06-27T09:59:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sooooo-where-do-you-want-to-be-who-do-you-want-to-be-in-ten-years-time.15807/post-169724>

When I first read this Nicole I thought you said ...."naked with neighbours!" I thought to myself .....my, my Nicole has come a long way!

### nicld — 2015-06-27T17:25:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sooooo-where-do-you-want-to-be-who-do-you-want-to-be-in-ten-years-time.15807/post-169737>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=169724)

> When I first read this Nicole I thought you said ...."naked with neighbours!" I thought to myself .....my, my Nicole has come a long way!

### Gagnrad — 2015-06-26T17:38:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/old-photo-of-an-aboriginal-australian.15880/post-169675>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=169650)
>
> Is this a photo? or a painting?

It's a photo. Can't say why easily, but it just is.

But the slight fading to the right and somewhat along the top is indicative. It could be light-damage, but I'd guess it's where the processing chemicals weren't washed out fully, so that they've degraded the image over time.

Very little in the way of calves - I have heard that's usual for Aboriginal Australians, but I don't know if that's true. He looks wiry and tough rather than big, but I think he'd be strong.

### Da-mo — 2015-06-27T02:36:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/old-photo-of-an-aboriginal-australian.15880/post-169686>

### Da-mo — 2015-06-27T02:38:38+0300

# Thread: Summertime EMF alert! Test your fans!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/summertime-emf-alert-test-your-fans.15904/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-27T07:21:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/summertime-emf-alert-test-your-fans.15904/post-169712>

It's getting friggin' hot here in Vancouver, B.C. A lot of the homes here do not have air conditioning because the heat is really not that bad for very long. I set up some fans around the house and tested them with my Trimeter......
Holy shit!!! The EMF coming off some of these fans is just RIDICULOUS!! Completely pinning the needle on the largest scale!

But some of the fans weren't at all bad. So it pays to test your fan before you buy it- they vary widely!

Just letting people know before they place a fan by their head so they can sleep. (which I do..with a low EMF fan that is...)

### Martin — 2015-06-27T23:53:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/summertime-emf-alert-test-your-fans.15904/post-169741>

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-06-28T03:49:32+0300

I have kind of wondered about that myself. I too have the same situation as you - a pullout and no Magnetico. Upstairs there isn't a lot of EMF that I am aware of. I have tested the bedroom with both a Trimeter and an electrosmog meter, the latter is supposedly one of the best meters on the market according to Michael Neuart. I bought both meters so I have tested at all different times of the day and there seems to be very little EMF in our bedroom HOWEVER, - when I use my iPhone in the bedroom to look for wifi signals it will pick them up, but just barely.
So that shows that the meters are not totally dependable.
I do seem to feel more comfortable sleeping downstairs but could also be because I got the entire bed for myself!
I wonder what is better, upstairs with a Magnetico or downstairs without it but closer to the earth. Our basement is actually partially below ground...about 4 ft.

# Thread: This makes you think about heredity
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-makes-you-think-about-heredity.15919/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-06-28T14:30:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-makes-you-think-about-heredity.15919/post-169764>

I happened to stumble upon this article about guitar-legend Eric Clapton and the troubled past of himself and his mostly unknown family today:

[http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/art...heroin-addict-brother-slow-squalid-death.html](http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-3084642/One-lives-seedy-refuge-months-live-125million-Caribbean-drug-clinic-won-t-Eric-Clapton-rescue-heroin-addict-brother-slow-squalid-death.html)

Now I'm not sure I approve of what the _Mail_ is doing here. It's intrusive and involves some unnecessary finger-pointing. I heard there's an old Native American saying: "When you point, there are three fingers pointing back at you". (If you point with the index finger and then turn your hand over you'll see what's meant.)

This is a difficult situation that fallible people with a lot of pain they can't deal with have been thrown into. It doesn't affect the rest of us, and don't think it's anyone's business - least of all the media's - to pronounce on it. I'm not even entirely sure that I should link the piece anywhere. But FWIW I think it's interesting to compare the similarities between Clapton, the brother he didn't know about, and the father he never met.

I don't, of course, believe that genes are destiny.

(Let alone that they are manipulating us. I'll leave such thinking - if thinking it is - up to the likes of Richard Dawkins … who would, of course, angrily retort that he was only being metaphorical … and then, having as he thought disposed of the objection, would immediately return to the same kind of language, since actually he has no _other_ way of saying what he has to say and, in truth, does seem to think, and feel, in the way his language indicates. (C.f also _memes_ - more unseen, "demonic" manipulators.) )

No, the "conditions of life" matter. Nevertheless, so does heredity. And isn't it interesting that Clapton, who never touched a guitar before the age of fifteen but was a recognised master by twenty-one, should turn out to have had a musician as a father? It sounds like his brother has no mean musical talents either. There are several other similarities between all three, but the musical ability is the most striking.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: The Hispanic Paradox
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-hispanic-paradox.15920/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-06-28T18:37:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-hispanic-paradox.15920/post-169777>

I hadn't heard of this before. Here it is stated:

> It’s called the Hispanic mortality paradox. **In the United States, Hispanics die at slower rates than non-Hispanic whites. This is true even before accounting for differences in incomes and health-care access**, which put Hispanics at a disadvantage.
>
> One of the first people to point that out was Kyriacos Markides, a professor at the University of Maine who noted in a [1986 paper](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1477704/pdf/pubhealthrep00183-0027.pdf) that Hispanics in the Southwest were remarkably healthy. Compared to whites, they had lower rates of death from cardiovascular disease and cancer, and lower rates of infant mortality. The “accumulating evidence,” he wrote, “suggests that the health of Hispanics is much closer to that of other whites than to the health of blacks, with whom Hispanics share socioeconomic conditions.”

[http://www.washingtonpost.com/news/...in-the-u-s-live-longer-than-white-people-why/](http://www.washingtonpost.com/news/storyline/wp/2014/10/02/hispanics-in-the-u-s-live-longer-than-white-people-why/)

There's also another article from last year in the _Dallas Morning News_:

> In the face of higher rates of poverty and lower rates of education and employment, Hispanics live 2½ years longer than Anglos, and in some states, nearly eight years longer than African-Americans. **The effect is more pronounced in immigrants but also applies to Hispanics born in the U.S.** …
>
> One theme pops up in many of these studies but is often overlooked. Maybe because it doesn’t sound very scientific — and scientists like to be taken seriously — or maybe because it’s difficult to measure. But if you read between the lines of the articles published in esteemed medical journals, it’s there. Love.
>
> Love, however, may not be as unscientific as it sounds. In the same way that poverty is proven to be bad for your health, studies have shown that **loving family and social networks are good for your health**.
>
> Dr. Kyriakos Markides chuckles when I ask if love is the answer. “It could be,” he says. “But we don’t have the data to say that convincingly.” Markides, a professor of aging studies at the University of Texas Medical Branch in Galveston, coined the term Hispanic Paradox in 1986 while studying the health of Mexicans in the Southwest. **“It’s been many years since we discovered this, and people still haven’t figured it out,” he says**. …

[http://www.dallasnews.com/opinion/sunday-commentary/20140117-decoding-the-hispanic-paradox.ece](http://www.dallasnews.com/opinion/sunday-commentary/20140117-decoding-the-hispanic-paradox.ece)

The same source notes:

> Hispanics also seem to survive and recover from disease quicker than non-Hispanic whites, says Dr. John Ruiz, assistant professor of psychology at the University of North Texas. “We’re seeing a survival advantage across disease conditions.” ...

[http://www.dallasnews.com/opinion/sunday-commentary/20140117-decoding-the-hispanic-paradox.ece](http://www.dallasnews.com/opinion/sunday-commentary/20140117-decoding-the-hispanic-paradox.ece)

.

# Thread: A few questions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions.16040/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cap — 2015-07-01T07:47:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions.16040/post-170192>

Hi everyone, new to this community and very eager for knowledge and to get optimal! I am a young male 24 years old and have a strong desire to be a good knowledgable member to this "community." I and some family members have had some health issues and I really have been trying to study as much as I can from this blog, finished Jack's book and listened to all his podcasts/videos but I still have so much left to learn and comprehend (a lot makes my head hurt or I don't fully understand yet) so I apologize in advance if my questions seem dumb! Just looking for answers and possibly an area maybe I could direct my attention too as the the blog is enormous...

To Start I just have a few simple questions..

1\. I am confused about Carbs in general. Jack eats Carbs in the summer and is Ketogenic in the winter. I understand you are suppose to consume them seasonally depending on where you live? So if someone lives in say the Phillipines versus Southern California what is the difference in when one should be eating Carbs? Would the answer be that generally someone in the Phillipines can get away with eating Carbs year round due to being closer to the equator and having more photonic power from the sun and People in SoCal would benefit by having them only in the summer? And would eating Ketogenic year round in either place would just be 'extra credit' as a tool to help mitigate inflammation/nnemf exposure?
2\. I have read the dental health post=> Carbs Cause Tooth Problems...so if one has Cavities they should avoid Carbs at all costs?

3\. A bit off topic but I was wondering if I could get more of an explanation on how to "biohack" ones MRI?
as I have MRI's of my Brain and Lumbar Spine. I have had back problems and (hernia/bulging disc/inflamed facet joint) hormonal problems and from reading the blog post I did not comprehend exactly how to look at it and determine what I can learn from it unless I am Jack himself lol. What are some solutions to resolve the back issue? I have had it for almost a year now and seriously cannot sit down for more than 5 minutes without irritation.....I am tired of it and I want to fix this as it is not going away! All I have been recommended to do by Dr's is "don't sit" (I Agree but I do live in the real word), don't be hard on it, ice, and an epidural injection (I haven't decided to to that).

Thank you so much, if I am posting incorrectly I apologize, very new here!

# Thread: Urinalysis Results
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/urinalysis-results.16053/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### calishaye — 2015-07-01T21:32:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/urinalysis-results.16053/post-170245>

Hi all,

My last urinalysis showed some interesting things. My doc didn't seem too concerned, saying that it was probably just a contaminated catch even though they give 2 sterile wipes to wipe the area clean. Here are some of the results:

UA Leuk Est +1 (should be none)
UA Appear Hazy (should be clear)
UA WBC 5-9/HPF (should be zero)
UA RBC 1-2/HPF (should be zero)
UA Mucous 3+ (should be zero)
UA Squam Epi 4+ (should be zero)
UA PH 7.0

What are the chances of it being an unclean catch? I had a 24-hour Loomis urine test done a few years back that also came up with high leukocytes and low urine volume so I am wondering if there is an infection or something going on. My cholesterol was 282, LDL 182, HDL 92. Particle test revealed 95% was the big fluffy kind. BP always good. Glucose was 90. Vitamin D 48. TSH 1.628. Red and white blood counts in normal range. In the process of ordering more labs that Dr. Kruse recommends.

Should I be concerned about a possible underlying infection? I have not felt well for a very long time. Immense stress, too many nights working late on the computer and lack of grounding has screwed up my sleep cycle. Diet has been good for the past few years. I am tired but not chronic fatigue, depression, mood swings, food sensitivities to gluten/wheat and chocolate (possibly more), no motivation most days, almost 51 years old and heading into menopause. Edema in lower legs when sitting too long, horrible breath for as long as I can remember that clears up when I water fast for a few days or gargle with hydrogen peroxide, digestive issues, constipation. I just feel worn out physically and mentally and just the little task could set me off and feel way too overwhelming. Hair analysis revealed severe adrenal and thyroid fatigue but hormones good, slow metabalizer. Also a hidden copper toxicity and a "calcium shell". Low selenium and phosphorous in the hair. Not sure how much value there is in a hair analysis but it felt accurate.

Any insights would be appreciated.

Thanks.
Jill

# Thread: Lithium Orotate
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lithium-orotate.16054/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SpiritGuide — 2015-07-01T23:20:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lithium-orotate.16054/post-170253>

Hi All,

My wife and I have been researching bio-hacking on YouTube a lot lately and came across some online lectures presented by Dr. Tent from Diverse Health Services.

Unlike Dr. Kruse these guys actually do sell their supplements and products so some people may not like that, but I put them into the same category as a Dr. Mercola in that regard... We absorb the information and then buy the supplements from whoever it makes the most sense to do so.

Anyways one of the recommendations I found recently in a lecture on Minerals was for a supplement called Lithium Orotate, a much more easily absorbable form of Lithium than the standard pharmaceutical drug prescribed for bi-polar disorder.

We decided to give it a shot to see how it went, and for $8.95 a bottle on Amazon.com I have to say it's been one of the best (and most inexpensive) mental bio-hacks I've found yet so I thought I'd share it here: [http://www.amazon.com/Vitamin-Research-Products-Lithium-Orotate/dp/B005GWVRKE](http://www.amazon.com/Vitamin-Research-Products-Lithium-Orotate/dp/B005GWVRKE)

Alongside Bulletproof Coffee it's the best brain booster I've found, and I have a lot of compensating to do since my brain tumor surgery to get my mental clarity back... I hope this helps some of you, I've been taking it for about a week and have been in a near-constant state of flow every since, and with my brain that's saying a lot.

### SpiritGuide — 2015-07-02T17:16:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lithium-orotate.16054/post-170369>

One thing I wanted to add was that Lithium is an essential mineral and the lack of it causes a lot of brain fog for everyone, not just those with acute mental illness. I was shocked at how much clearer my own thoughts were after taking it... I suppose I may have been more deficient than I even thought.

I also recently added a Trace Minerals supplement since watching the lecture I mentioned by Dr. Tent since we're all basically mineral deficient due to the modern farming techniques stripping our top soil of it's nutrients... most of what we consider to be diseases are actually mineral deficiencies, and I already supplemented with Magnesium and also a mined salt, but I wanted to be sure to get all of the minerals that we're missing as well. Although the mental clarity is almost assuredly coming from the Lithium from what I'm hearing and reading.

Also that link I provided for Amazon is for a bottle with 100 pills, and I've only been taking 1 per day so it's a 3-month supply for under $9.

Again I can only hope that by posting this I'm helping one of you to also get this missing ingredient to "un-fog" your brain as I've been able to do this past week.

### SpiritGuide — 2015-07-06T20:33:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lithium-orotate.16054/post-171168>

Hi Quick update to this... I've been taking these for about a week and a half now and with some very good results... I'm not sure if this means I was undiagnosed bi-polar or not, but my wife reported "good" results as opposed to my "amazing" results.

I also wanted to point out that my wife informed me of where she first saw Lithium Orotate, we watch so many podcasts it's hard for me to keep up... The first person who recommended Lithium Orotate was actually John Grey during an interview podcast with Dave Asprey on Bulletproof Radio recently, although Dr. Tent did mention Lithium in his lecture on minerals.

Another fantastic gem to come from that John Grey interview was Calcium AEP for improved nerve and cell function. If you haven't seen this episode I'd recommend it also, Dave Asprey said that hardly anyone knows about Calcium AEP and it's actually on his advanced list of bulletproof supplements.

Dr. Tent is still one of my favorites, very entertaining and I'll absolutely never vaccinate myself or my family again after watching his episode on the "exploding autoimmune epidemic".

### SpiritGuide — 2015-07-07T20:11:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lithium-orotate.16054/post-171281>

By the way the price on this supplement has gone up twice in the past week on Amazon, it's now at $25.00 a bottle. Try this link instead for a better price at just $8.99 per bottle: [http://www.swansonvitamins.com/advanced-research-nutrient-carriers-lithium-orotate-100-tabs](http://www.swansonvitamins.com/advanced-research-nutrient-carriers-lithium-orotate-100-tabs)

I typically only see this happen when a supplement is mentioned on Dr. Oz... thanks for ruining the cheap Turmeric I used to buy Dr. Oz, geesh!

I do know that John Gray has been promoting his new book "Staying Focused in a Hyper-Focused World" (which I purchased) so maybe the word is really starting to get out on this now.

### Penny — 2015-07-08T00:11:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lithium-orotate.16054/post-171293>

I don't think Dr. K goes for lithium orotate, however, one day - God only knows why - I tried a lithium orotate plus 1/2 tsp of Concentrace mineral formula (disgusting) and I had focus the entire day - weirdly, I couldn't duplicate it again...

In Jerry Tennant's book, lithium actually has the highest negative charge: -3.04 volts/-3040 millivolts so I would have thought in the land of quanta, that would be a good thing - however, cesium also has -3.02 volts so maybe not such a hot idea -

it probably is indicative of something though I would not know what exactly...

### SpiritGuide — 2015-07-08T19:38:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lithium-orotate.16054/post-171400>

Thanks for the reply Penny and for giving me further information I didn't know about.

I did some research online and found that Cesium Chloride and Lithium Orotate were also both studied by Dr. Hans Nieper... so there's another connection.

Cesium Chloride was recommended by him for cancer treatment whereas Lithium Orotate was recommended for Alzheimer's... perhaps the charge has something to do with transporting the minerals through the cell membrane? From what I can tell the mineral transporters with regards to cell membranes was a key focus of Dr. Nieper's work.

As I mentioned in an update to this thread John Gray was the one who had mentioned Lithium Orotate in an interview with Dave Asprey, and I also began taking the Calcium AEP supplement recommended by him as well (apparently both were also recommendations of Dr. Hans Nieper which may be where John Gray found out about them)

I found a link on Dr. Ed Group's website that has some good details on the different mineral transporters that Dr. Nieper recommended for anyone who's interested in more of a backrground on that: [http://www.globalhealingcenter.com/calcium-orotate/orotates-mineral-transporters](http://www.globalhealingcenter.com/calcium-orotate/orotates-mineral-transporters)

I'd be interested to find out what Dr. Kruse had against it... it looks like he didn't elaborate in your thread just said it was "Not ask appropriate"

### Lahelada — 2015-07-08T20:02:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lithium-orotate.16054/post-171401>

This is relevant as to why not.

forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/lithium-could-help-reset-circadian-mismatch.12043/

### SpiritGuide — 2015-07-08T20:38:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lithium-orotate.16054/post-171405>

Hi Lahelada, thanks for posting that link... great find! Your google skills are far superior to mine

Now I'm left wondering what's recommended to take the place of Lithium for people like myself who are getting such a great mental benefit from it...

I see in that post he mentioned it's chemically and bio-chemically similar to Magnesium which I already supplement with daily. Magnesium is great, it helps relax me and helps me achieve more of an alpha brainwave state which I like, but it hasn't improved my mental clarity at all.

I really don't want to go back to the brain fog I had prior to the past week or two... does anyone have an answer to what can help in place of Lithium Orotate?

I also don't want to take any pharmaceutical drugs, so if there's a naturally occurring substance that will have the same desired effect I'd love to hear about it.... thanks for all the feedback guys, this forum is fantastic!

### SpiritGuide — 2015-07-09T21:00:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lithium-orotate.16054/post-171489>

I came across this on Dr. Ed Group's website: [http://www.globalhealingcenter.com/natural-health/is-lithium-orotate-good-or-bad-for-you/](http://www.globalhealingcenter.com/natural-health/is-lithium-orotate-good-or-bad-for-you/)

Here's a direct quote:

“Lithium is an essential micronutrient… It is present in all organs and tissues in the body. It has similar chemical properties to that of calcium and magnesium.” -Mark Hyman, M.D., best-selling author of “The UltraMind Solution"

Dr. Mark Myman is a very respectable source too... I'm hoping Dr. Kruse or another admin will chime in on this, I've been taking this supplement for about 2 weeks now with fantastic results, and while I respect all of you and thank you all for joining the conversation, I'm hesitant to stop taking it because I'm finally feeling really good for the first time in years.

Again perhaps that's because I was an undiagnosed Bi-Polar case, I may never know... queue the crazy person jokes

# Thread: Wacky story of the day
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wacky-story-of-the-day.16121/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-07-03T08:23:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wacky-story-of-the-day.16121/post-170492>

It looks like some Tibetans have disrupted the ecosystem in the waters off Brighton.

I'm starting to have sympathy with the Chinese now …

> A group of Buddhists have released £2,000 of crabs and lobsters into the sea as part of a religious ceremony - but now the newcomers are killing native species.
>
> Crustaceans from North America have been found in the waters off Brighton, which could threaten the sealife that lives in the area.
>
> The discovery of Canadian lobsters and Dungeness crabs was first announced by the Marine Management Organisation last week. ...

The MMO says the action could "constitute an offence under the Wildlife and Countryside Act 1981."

[http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/art...ee-foreign-crustaceans-eat-local-sealife.html](http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-3137547/Buddhists-bought-2-000-lobsters-crabs-set-free-foreign-crustaceans-eat-local-sealife.html)

### Gagnrad — 2015-07-03T08:49:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wacky-story-of-the-day.16121/post-170496>

By the way, Tibet is land-locked - so my guess is there is no way this food-wasting ceremony could be an historic Tibetan practice. Maybe it's the Hollywood influence ...

# Thread: This just seems wrong....  How to open oysters
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-just-seems-wrong-how-to-open-oysters.16169/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RobH — 2015-07-04T20:59:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-just-seems-wrong-how-to-open-oysters.16169/post-170819>

Ordering some fresh oysters .... Found this on website:

OPEN IN MICROWAVE:

Place oysters cup side down in a microwave safe dish. Look closely at an oyster and you'll see that the top shell is flat and the bottom shell is shaped like a cup. Cover oysters with a paper towel. Cup side down saves liquor used in recipes.

Less than a minute will open most oysters. Time depends on total number you´re opening. Just open, don´t cook. Be careful, oyster shells will be very hot when you take them out of the microwave.

With oysters open, insert sharp knife between shells and cut muscle from top and bottom shells. Discard top shell. With many recipes you will remove the oyster from the shell for preparation and later replace the oyster into its shell for decorative serving.

I have been working on my hand shucking technique. Finding I now kind of enjoy it.

RobH

### jackware — 2015-07-08T06:07:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-just-seems-wrong-how-to-open-oysters.16169/post-171332>

Who on here owns a microwave, really?

### thomas — 2015-07-08T14:44:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-just-seems-wrong-how-to-open-oysters.16169/post-171365>

With practice you can open one in three seconds. Google oyster shucking competitions, it's impressive!

My wife is a bit of an enigma in many ways !!!

She is frustrated because when she goes out in the sun and tries to get a tan... nothing happens.
She doesn't even get burned!
It's really weird because she really doesn't eat well (that is slowly starting to change) and she absolutely HATES fish.
Occasionally she will eat some shrimp but not enough to really get much benefit.

Here is the interesting thing - when she puts on suntan lotion, she will burn!
This leads me to believe that the lotion is highly toxic!

Anybody ever heard of this?

I don't know if this is toxic but it clearly makes the skin too sensitive to sun rays. which is not good.

I woke up at 2 AM with Montezumba's Revenge! It was bad!

and my body is just screaming for mercy when not getting enough oxygen.

# Thread: Should I be sleeping outside?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-be-sleeping-outside.16222/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Psyche — 2015-07-06T21:30:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-be-sleeping-outside.16222/post-171177>

Hello, all...
I have an EMF related question I was hoping to get some feedback from people who have been down this road. My question is, given the contect, what is the best place for me to sleep?

Current context:

I rent an older (1950's?) house in a rural area 50 miles east of Asheville, NC (Rutherfordton, NC). Closest neighbors are 2/10 mile either way. Street is on a mild ridge line compared with surroundings (1035 ft. above sea level).

EMF Sources:
Outside of the house- Smart meter on one corner of the house. Above it on the roof is the satellite for internet (no cable offered on my street). There is an antenna on top of the house and I have no idea why. DirecTV satellite in yard (next to my bedroom). There are power lines running across my yard, connecting to the house. The transformer (assuming it's for my house) is across the street. Also- I used the web to find out there are 9 towers and 63 antennae's within a 3-mile radius of my address. Even though I have no close neighbors (5 houses on a 2-mile stretch of road) all the houses have satellite dishes.

Inside the house- microwave, wi-fi router, 3 LED TV's, laptop computer.

Currently, I sleep in my bedroom with everything unplugged and darkened. Smart meter is located at opposite end of house. Bedroom is above the basement. When the sun goes down, my blue blockers go on and my little cave goes dark. I stay in there until I go to sleep. I sleep with a black lab doggie, too)

I would LOVE to sleep outside in my yard in a tent (perhaps taking out the plastic bottom), but I'm wondering if it would even be an improvement given all the EMF generating stuff around my house. I do not know enough about what is emitting from the smart meter and electric wires surrounding me, what direction and distance to be aware of, etc.

I feel like I have a ridiculous amount of EMF exposure for being in a rural area! (Also- I work in downtown Asheville on the fifth floor of my building!)

Comments?

I want to make it clear I have no affiliation with the creators of this documentary and am not a part of it  I do support the project, though, and will likely donate. Here is the teaser trailer to the new documentary film, "On the Back of a Tiger": [http://www.perceivethinkact.com/](http://www.perceivethinkact.com/)

Featuring:
Gilbert Ling - The A.I. Hypothesis
Harold Hillman - The Living Cell
Mae Wan Ho - Quantum Coherence
Gerald Pollack - Structured Water
Michael Persinger - Electromagnetic Fields
Raymond Peat - Unifying the Theories
Danny Roddy - Self Education and Experimentation

There is a Kickstarter page for those who would like to donate.

### shah78 — 2015-07-08T01:46:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-back-of-a-tiger.16224/post-171300>

Ray Peat PLUS Danny Roddy???????That's a large double dose of Ray Peat. They couldn't find any other "thinker" in the entire Universe to put on as the seventh person, so that brought on "THE PEAT WHISPERER". Very ONION like, but I imagine it's serious. Good times!

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-07-08T02:28:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-back-of-a-tiger.16224/post-171308>

Well, I don't know, I actually appreciate the addition of Dr. Peat. Maybe not Danny Roddy, but definitely Dr. Peat. On their website, they even have a picture of Dr. Gilbert Ling reading Dr. Peat's website, and it was apparently an "easter egg" as Dr. Ling had some very positive things to say about Dr. Peat's "view of the organism, disease, and aging". It's not the first time Dr. Ling has stated he shares many views similar to Dr. Peat.

Dr. Peat clearly has some ideas diametrically opposed to Dr. Kruse, and, since I'm part of this forum, I'd expect most people know where I tend to side; but, if anyone were to read Dr. Peat's books that he published in the 70's up to the early 90's, their idea of him would be drastically different. He has been speaking about the plasma and electric universe theories since their origins; Dr. Becker's work; magnetite in the human sinus and brain; "organism streaming" for regenerative purposes; etc. all for almost 50 years now. As I've said in other threads, there is no reason to throw the baby out with the bath water just because he has a different view on DHA or sucrose as Dr. Kruse. The points they agree on - blue light, red light, sunlight, EMFs, circadian zeitgebers, pregnenolone and progesterone and DHEA, quinones such as vitamin K2 and PQQ, etc - are far more important.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-back-of-a-tiger.16224/post-171398>

I believe that the film makers know Danny Roddy or something, if I'm not mistaken. I'm not sure why they chose Danny Roddy, though, as opposed to other well known Peat fans. For instance, Andrew Kim, who not only does self-experimentation, but has a science background and is continuing his education in the sciences, has been reading Gilbert Ling's work and the like, or Vladimir of Valtsu's ([http://valtsus.blogspot.com/](http://valtsus.blogspot.com/)), a science student, lecturer, etc. or "Prana-Rupa", etc. Danny Roddy has largely just regurgitated what can be found already online quite easily.

# Thread: Do I need extra vit K2 if I use topical D3 cream?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-need-extra-vit-k2-if-i-use-topical-d3-cream.16246/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### b612 — 2015-07-07T12:05:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-need-extra-vit-k2-if-i-use-topical-d3-cream.16246/post-171244>

I use topical vitamin D3 cream to repigment my vitiligo.
I do suntan a lot but I noticed that skin repigments faster if I use D3 cream.

I use Anumed D3 10 000 units.
Do I require extra vit K2 pills? I eat lots of raw grassfed butter and cheese.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-07-10T04:11:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-need-extra-vit-k2-if-i-use-topical-d3-cream.16246/post-171516>

> [b612 said:](/goto/post?id=171244)
>
> I use topical vitamin D3 cream to repigment my vitiligo.
> I do suntan a lot but I noticed that skin repigments faster if I use D3 cream.
>
> I use Anumed D3 10 000 units.
> Do I require extra vit K2 pills? I eat lots of raw grassfed butter and cheese.

Sounds like you may not be making enough Vit. D3. You have your cholesterol covered with the butter (which you need to make vitamin D3) but the liver makes most of your cholesterol anyway. Do you know what your cholesterol levels are?
Actually you probably already know this stuff.

### b612 — 2015-07-10T14:19:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-need-extra-vit-k2-if-i-use-topical-d3-cream.16246/post-171562>

I have high HCL and normal LDL

I have decided to supplement with K2 anyways... Won't hurt me since I have a little bit of teeth tartar.

The neighbors officialy know I am crazy. Those that are open minded become my friends. The close minded are left in their world.

# Thread: Eye doctor
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eye-doctor.16281/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2015-07-08T16:13:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eye-doctor.16281/post-171376>

I had my eye exam this week and low and behold the eye sight is changing. My prescription for my nearsightedness, that is only two years old, is now too strong. No wonder why I stopped wearing them.

My eyes are also super healthy. No signs of cataracts, macular degeneration, the optic nerve looks great, the blood vessels all were great (they took some really cool pictures), my glands were perfect and my macula was beautiful. Of course I had to tell them about all the seafood I eat and the doctor said that was great and that it probably helped due to all the fish oils and their anti inflammatory effects.

I will be getting specific glasses to work on the computer with BluTech lenses in them. Not cheap and I cannot believe how much a pair of freeking plastic frames are!!!!

I also talked with the person helping me get the glasses about the effects of reducing blue light and how just by putting on Uvex glasses at night has improved my sleep leaps and bounds. She said that she has to take a sleeping pill every night and I told her to get a pair of $9 glasses from Amazon and put them on as the sun is setting. Whether she does it or not is another story.

Should be getting them in about 2 weeks.

Thanks Jack. Sometimes health gains are not always on the outside!!!!!!

### nonchalant — 2015-07-14T03:20:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eye-doctor.16281/post-171879>

Sweet, Nicole!!
I'm looking forward to my next exam this autumn also. I'll have him give me two prescriptions again, one for regular and one for "computer use" (usually one diopter less). The lesser prescription will probably be the one I use to get a new glasses. Make my eyes work a bit again.

### Sean Ryan — 2016-06-02T23:03:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eye-doctor.16281/post-195894>

I put these glasses on outside in the sunshine and immediately started seeing blue dots everywhere in my field of view. Anyone experience similar?

### Nittygrittydanny — 2016-06-02T23:06:13+0300

# Thread: Thoughts on Addium
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-addium.16282/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty_M — 2015-07-08T20:38:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-addium.16282/post-171404>

If anyone has seen the movie limitless, then you know what I'm talking about. It's some kind of drug that "enhances brain function" with no apparent side effects in clinical trials....

Does anyone know about how this works? What are the side effects?

\- Matt

### prAna303 — 2015-07-08T20:44:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-addium.16282/post-171406>

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=171404)
>
> If anyone has seen the movie limitless, then you know what I'm talking about. It's some kind of drug that "enhances brain function" with no apparent side effects in clinical trials....
>
> Does anyone know about how this works? What are the side effects?
>
> \- Matt

I will forever think that manmade things will harm us no matter what. Instead i think we will see these things when we use nature...

Water, sun, fish, iodine, the list goes on... Load up on these and our limits slowly grow away.

The limits are set by society in large.

### Gagnrad — 2015-07-08T21:07:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-addium.16282/post-171407>

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=171404)
>
> If anyone has seen the movie limitless, then you know what I'm talking about. It's some kind of drug that "enhances brain function" with no apparent side effects in clinical trials....
>
> Does anyone know about how this works? What are the side effects?

No, haven't seen the movie.

It's probably what's known as a nootropic (pronounced _no-otropic -_ but frequently mispronounced _noo-tropic_). This often seems to mean something like caffeine or ginseng, but can also mean something synthesised. AFAIK it's supposed to be part of the definition of these that they don't have over-troublesome side-effects. In practice I expect they do, and anyway the term seems to be a little slippery … _caveat emptor_.

Evan Brand - who I think has worked with or presented with Jack in the past - has an interview with an academic at UCLA on these:

[http://notjustpaleo.com/dr-andrew-hill-on-nootropics/](http://notjustpaleo.com/dr-andrew-hill-on-nootropics/)

It's all interesting enough. Personally I'd be wary of this stuff, though.

### Penny — 2015-07-09T00:31:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-addium.16282/post-171421>

Not worth it Matt:
[http://selfhacked.com/2013/07/17/why-i-dont-supplement-with-piracetam/](http://selfhacked.com/2013/07/17/why-i-dont-supplement-with-piracetam/)

I've also read it's a fail if you are subject to depression - basically piracetam increases the uptake of choline (think: memory) in your brain - \*I think\* - and too much can increase "neurotoxicity"/"excess glutamates" - think migraines/epilepsy - add calcium efflux to that and you can see how it can go totally wrong - actually, piracetam does for choline what SSRIs do for serotonin - I think someone with a great memory would expect problems with this "nootropic" but someone who is a train wreck with not enough choline/methyl groups might experience a benefit - you do not strike me as being in the latter group...

Try glycine instead - no methyl groups, turns into TMG - great for increasing superoxide - fantastic for those middle of the night cortisol spikes to jack up your blood sugar as it comes crashing down at 2:00A.M. -
[http://www.life-enhancement.com/mag...ues-under-hypoxic-oxygen-deficient-conditions](http://www.life-enhancement.com/magazine/article/2913-glycine-protects-brain-tissues-under-hypoxic-oxygen-deficient-conditions)
[http://www.life-enhancement.com/magazine/article/2813-ingredients-in-sleepscape](http://www.life-enhancement.com/magazine/article/2813-ingredients-in-sleepscape)
[http://www.life-enhancement.com/magazine/article/1687-putting-more-power-into-your-life](http://www.life-enhancement.com/magazine/article/1687-putting-more-power-into-your-life)

**Effect of glycine on oxidative stress in rats with alcohol induced liver injury.**
[Senthilkumar R](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Senthilkumar%20R%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=14964423)1, [Viswanathan P](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Viswanathan%20P%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=14964423), [Nalini N](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Nalini%20N%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=14964423).
**[Author information](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/14964423#)**

**Abstract**
We studied the effect of administering glycine on tissue lipid peroxidation and enzymic and non-enzymic antioxidants in experimental hepatotoxic Wistar rats. Hepatotoxicity was induced by administering ethanol for 30 days by intragastric intubation. Glycine administered at a dose of 0.6 g kg(-1) body weight for 30 days significantly inhibited the severe oxidative stress as evidenced by the decreased levels of liver and brain thiobarbituric acid reactive substances (TBARS) and hydroperoxides compared to control. **The activities of enzymic and non-enzymic antioxidants such as reduced glutathione (GSH), glutathione peroxidase (GPx), superoxide dismutase (SOD) and catalase (CAT) in the liver and brain were significantly elevated on glycine supplementation as compared to the untreated alcohol fed rats**. The levels of serum vitamin E and vitamin C were also increased to near normal levels on glycine treatment. Microscopic examination of alcohol treated rat liver showed inflammatory cell infiltrates and fatty changes, which were alleviated on treatment with glycine. Alcohol treated rat brain demonstrated oedma, which was significantly lowered on treatment with glycine. Thus our study shows that administering glycine to alcohol supplemented rats, markedly reduced the oxidative stress and elevated the enzymic and non-enzymic antioxidants in the liver and brain, which a was associated with a reversal of hepatic steatosis and cerebral oedma.

I also like phosphatidyl serine complex with phosphatidyl choline for memory:
[http://www.lifeextension.com/magazine/2002/9/report\_ps/Page-01](http://www.lifeextension.com/magazine/2002/9/report_ps/Page-01)
[http://ajcn.nutrition.org/content/82/1/111.full](http://ajcn.nutrition.org/content/82/1/111.full)

What's great about this stuff is it's hard to hurt yourself with it and it's what your body needs... and glycine is cheap -

### Matty_M — 2015-07-09T14:27:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-addium.16282/post-171465>

> [Penny said:](/goto/post?id=171421)
>
> Not worth it Matt:
> [http://selfhacked.com/2013/07/17/why-i-dont-supplement-with-piracetam/](http://selfhacked.com/2013/07/17/why-i-dont-supplement-with-piracetam/)
>
> I've also read it's a fail if you are subject to depression - basically piracetam increases the uptake of choline (think: memory) in your brain - \*I think\* - and too much can increase "neurotoxicity"/"excess glutamates" - think migraines/epilepsy - add calcium efflux to that and you can see how it can go totally wrong - actually, piracetam does for choline what SSRIs do for serotonin - I think someone with a great memory would expect problems with this "nootropic" but someone who is a train wreck with not enough choline/methyl groups might experience a benefit - you do not strike me as being in the latter group...
>
> Try glycine instead - no methyl groups, turns into TMG - great for increasing superoxide - fantastic for those middle of the night cortisol spikes to jack up your blood sugar as it comes crashing down at 2:00A.M. -
> [http://www.life-enhancement.com/mag...ues-under-hypoxic-oxygen-deficient-conditions](http://www.life-enhancement.com/magazine/article/2913-glycine-protects-brain-tissues-under-hypoxic-oxygen-deficient-conditions)
> [http://www.life-enhancement.com/magazine/article/2813-ingredients-in-sleepscape](http://www.life-enhancement.com/magazine/article/2813-ingredients-in-sleepscape)
> [http://www.life-enhancement.com/magazine/article/1687-putting-more-power-into-your-life](http://www.life-enhancement.com/magazine/article/1687-putting-more-power-into-your-life)
>
> **Effect of glycine on oxidative stress in rats with alcohol induced liver injury.**
> [Senthilkumar R](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Senthilkumar%20R%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=14964423)1, [Viswanathan P](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Viswanathan%20P%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=14964423), [Nalini N](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Nalini%20N%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=14964423).
> **[Author information](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/14964423#)**
>
> **Abstract**
> We studied the effect of administering glycine on tissue lipid peroxidation and enzymic and non-enzymic antioxidants in experimental hepatotoxic Wistar rats. Hepatotoxicity was induced by administering ethanol for 30 days by intragastric intubation. Glycine administered at a dose of 0.6 g kg(-1) body weight for 30 days significantly inhibited the severe oxidative stress as evidenced by the decreased levels of liver and brain thiobarbituric acid reactive substances (TBARS) and hydroperoxides compared to control. **The activities of enzymic and non-enzymic antioxidants such as reduced glutathione (GSH), glutathione peroxidase (GPx), superoxide dismutase (SOD) and catalase (CAT) in the liver and brain were significantly elevated on glycine supplementation as compared to the untreated alcohol fed rats**. The levels of serum vitamin E and vitamin C were also increased to near normal levels on glycine treatment. Microscopic examination of alcohol treated rat liver showed inflammatory cell infiltrates and fatty changes, which were alleviated on treatment with glycine. Alcohol treated rat brain demonstrated oedma, which was significantly lowered on treatment with glycine. Thus our study shows that administering glycine to alcohol supplemented rats, markedly reduced the oxidative stress and elevated the enzymic and non-enzymic antioxidants in the liver and brain, which a was associated with a reversal of hepatic steatosis and cerebral oedma.
>
>
> I also like phosphatidyl serine complex with phosphatidyl choline for memory:
> [http://www.lifeextension.com/magazine/2002/9/report\_ps/Page-01](http://www.lifeextension.com/magazine/2002/9/report_ps/Page-01)
> [http://ajcn.nutrition.org/content/82/1/111.full](http://ajcn.nutrition.org/content/82/1/111.full)
>
> What's great about this stuff is it's hard to hurt yourself with it and it's what your body needs... and glycine is cheap -

Thanks Penny! I was never considering trying anything like this but I was just interested in the mechanism.

### Matty_M — 2015-07-09T14:30:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-addium.16282/post-171467>

> [Gagnrad said:](/goto/post?id=171407)
>
> No, haven't seen the movie.
>
> It's probably what's known as a nootropic (pronounced _no-otropic -_ but frequently mispronounced _noo-tropic_). This often seems to mean something like caffeine or ginseng, but can also mean something synthesised. AFAIK it's supposed to be part of the definition of these that they don't have over-troublesome side-effects. In practice I expect they do, and anyway the term seems to be a little slippery … _caveat emptor_.
>
> Evan Brand - who I think has worked with or presented with Jack in the past - has an interview with an academic at UCLA on these:
>
> [http://notjustpaleo.com/dr-andrew-hill-on-nootropics/](http://notjustpaleo.com/dr-andrew-hill-on-nootropics/)
>
> It's all interesting enough. Personally I'd be wary of this stuff, though.

I'm wary for sure also curious. Definitely worth avoiding this stuff IMO.

thanks!

### Gagnrad — 2015-07-09T21:12:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-addium.16282/post-171492>

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=171467)
>
> I'm wary for sure also curious. Definitely worth avoiding this stuff IMO.
>
> thanks!

Well, what I'd offered was more a guess at what might be involved that anything more. I'd never heard of Addium. But googling it, which I just did, wouldn't be likely to increase anyone's confidence I suggest.

[https://addium.com](https://addium.com)

Scroll to the bottom of that page and you find it includes a neurotransmitter. AFAIK, supplying those blind probably isn't a good idea and could dig you into a pretty deep hole. Plus that's one that supposedly can't cross the blood-brain barrier unless you have "leaky brain" ... and if you have then you've got bigger problems than lack of a stimulant.

There's also an enzyme inhibitor in there. That makes me wonder. If in the normal way of things there's an enzyme to break something down, should you be interfering with that process?

Slightly further down there's something that raised my eyebrows a little more. They actually compare it to adderall. Now that's a drug prescribed to treat ADHD (much of which is arguably down to disrupted sleep, poor diet, and the like evolutionary mismatches). It's not something I'd want to give to children (although that, apparently, doesn't worry many doctors):

[http://www.drugs.com/adderall.html](http://www.drugs.com/adderall.html)

It's kind of concerning to see this product compared to something that probably shouldn't be given to children with "attention problems" let alone taken on the offchance that it might "enhance" "performance" by some Wall Street whizz-kid that's not actually been diagnosed with anything.

I'm with you: it does raise curiosity, as you say - as so many things in the world do - but it's likely "definitely worth avoiding this stuff".

### thomas — 2015-07-10T15:38:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-addium.16282/post-171569>

I think it's pretty smart to ask^ So many people would just take it without second thoughts.
Drugs are like playing with fire. Eventually you get burned. It's not like we know everything in biology..
There's generally(not necessarily always) a price to pay for synthetic substances.

### Lahelada — 2015-07-10T15:59:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-addium.16282/post-171571>

I have a quite simple take on this. Taking brain enhancement drugs is like running Formula one races on a dirt track. You will have a great start but you will only get so far..... Stay away.

### Gagnrad — 2015-07-10T20:12:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-addium.16282/post-171592>

> [thomas said:](/goto/post?id=171569)
>
> I think it's pretty smart to ask^ So many people would just take it without second thoughts.
> Drugs are like playing with fire. Eventually you get burned. It's not like we know everything in biology..
> There's generally(not necessarily always) a price to pay for synthetic substances.

And anyway you eventually adapt. Even a man as intelligent as Oliver Sacks had to learn that the hard way:

[http://www.amazon.com/Awakenings-Oliver-Sacks/dp/0330523678/](http://www.amazon.com/Awakenings-Oliver-Sacks/dp/0330523678/)

But, I suggest, one could already see that kind of thing going on in De Quincey:

[http://www.amazon.com/Confessions-English-Opium-Thomas-Quincey/dp/0140439013/](http://www.amazon.com/Confessions-English-Opium-Thomas-Quincey/dp/0140439013/)

… or anything similar.

It seems to me you've got the same thing going on with antibiotics and with pesticides. They work at first, but everything tends to move back again to where it was over time.

I guess if one wanted to moralise the process one could say that the pharmaceutical or agrochemical or, indeed, technological "fix" tends to be a recipe to get around the give-and-take of things, and in the long run you can't. The interesting thing is that when you don't try to be "greedy" it tends to be better all round for everyone, including yourself. So, for example, Joel Salatin gets a _better_ profit per acre than industrialised farmers, who are misusing their livestock, hurting wildlife, poisoning the land, cheating consumers, and incubating antibiotic-resistant pathogens do. But it would be next to impossible to get most people to believe that. And, of course, what they do _is_ less work - and certainly takes less thought and less knowledge.

I guess if someone wants a quick boost in mental focus before an exam, or something like that, then an Optimal Coffee out of Jack's PDF would probably do the trick.

# Thread: "Raft out of Africa"
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raft-out-of-africa.16299/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-07-09T09:21:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raft-out-of-africa.16299/post-171453>

"Raft out of Africa: early hominids as ancient mariners: hand axes excavated on Crete suggest sea crossings."

> Human ancestors that left Africa hundreds of thousands of years ago to see the rest of the world were no landlubbers. Stone hand axes unearthed on the Mediterranean island of Crete indicate that an ancient Homo species--**perhaps Homo erectus**\--had **used rafts or other sea going vessels to cross from northern Africa to Europe via at least some of the larger islands in between**, said archaeologist Thomas Strasser of Providence College in Rhode Island.
>
> Several hundred double-edged cutting implements discovered at nine sites in southwestern Crete date to at least 130000 years ago and probably to much earlier, Strasser reported January 7. Many of these finds closely resemble hand axes fashioned in Africa about 800,000 years ago by H. erectus, he said. H. erectus had spread from Africa to parts of Asia and Europe by at least that time.
>
> Until now, the oldest known human settlements on Crete dated to around 9,000 years ago. Traditional theories hold that early farming groups in southern Europe and the Middle East first navigated vessels to Crete and other Mediterranean islands at that time.
>
> **"We're just going to have to accept that, as soon as hominids left Africa, they were long-distance seafarers and rapidly spread all over the place," Strasser said.** Other researchers have suggested that H. erectus took rafts across stretches of sea in Indonesia about 800000 years ago and that Neandertals crossed the Strait of Gibraltar perhaps 60,000 years ago. ...

[http://www.thefreelibrary.com/Raft+...s+as+ancient+mariners:+hand+axes…-a0218311378](http://www.thefreelibrary.com/Raft+out+of+Africa%3a+early+hominids+as+ancient+mariners%3a+hand+axes…-a0218311378)

### Danco3636 — 2015-07-15T18:09:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elite-athletes-ct-bath.16339/post-172019>

### shah78 — 2015-07-15T20:14:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elite-athletes-ct-bath.16339/post-172026>

> [Danco3636 said:](/goto/post?id=171781)
>
> Can you send me the link of how you made it?
>
> Thanks

70 gallon water trough from farm supply store (Home Depot/Lowes does NOT have them) + 8 cans of spray insulation on sides+ (for top)4x8 ' 1/2 in. styrofoam insulation cut in 3 pieces + duct tape to hold together + plastic covering (optional) . Optional spicket was $15 for parts (at Home Depot) Worker there put it together for me. four pieces. Going on three and a half years!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elite-athletes-ct-bath.16339/post-172059>

The Fred Flintstone method. I share my 12 cu.ft. of garage freezer space with gallon jugs of water/ice and frozen fish/meat. About six to eight gallons a day in the "winter" and up to 12 gallon a day in the summer. Florida, you kn0w.

# Thread: Is a low EMF cordless phone available in North America?

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-07-11T04:04:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-a-low-emf-cordless-phone-available-in-north-america.16341/post-171633>

I removed the cordless phone a while back and bought a regular corded phone system but now my wife is complaining that she hates it. She wants the old phone back .
I could put a timer on it so there is no pulsing while we are sleeping but a low (or no)EMF cordless phone would be better.
Can we even get them in North America? It looks like they are only available in Europe.

### Jenny S — 2015-07-11T10:59:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-a-low-emf-cordless-phone-available-in-north-america.16341/post-171659>

I just propose you be a dominant man and tell your wife it's gonna be like you want it. I am sure your wife impose her choices on you sometimes, it's gotta go both ways. That's my real solution, tho harder if you're not used to it.

>
> H All,
> Can anyone let me know about this. A few years back I read Robert O. Becker's work and Dr. Pawluk's blogs on PEMF. I bought one of these [http://medithera-usa.com/medithera-pemf-system/](http://medithera-usa.com/medithera-pemf-system/) and use it nightly for an hour. Is this a good idea or am I screwing myself over?
>
> Cheers,
> Kathy

Can't say that I am familiar with that.

PEMF can be a double edged sword, you are going to have to pull labs to see if you are getting a benefit from the device or if it is harming you.

In a similar vein I used a schumann resonator in the last city I was in. Had lots of dreams and restful sleep. After I moved to Fl it gave me sleep disruptions and made me feel antsy, so I quit using it.

### kvond — 2015-07-11T23:15:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/medithera-good-idea-or-no.16366/post-171716>

Hi bionaut,
Thanks for responding. The issue is there is no "before" value. I've been using this for about 4 years. I know at it's highest setting it reaches 0.75 gauss. I'm not ansy with it and wouldn't miss a night without it. But I'm not sure how it relates to the Leptin protocol.
Cheers,
Kathy
