# Thread: Towards a unified theory of injury treatment
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/towards-a-unified-theory-of-injury-treatment.16692/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### René Borg — 2015-08-07T18:09:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/towards-a-unified-theory-of-injury-treatment.16692/post-174193>

Starting this thread in the hope of harnessing the brain power on these forums to connect the dots between current best practices (and expose not-so-good practices) in the area of injury prevention and treatment with particular emphasis on so-called 'sports injury'.

It's obvious that Jack's overall theory sets the scene for how likely we are to pick up an injury and provides much of the information we need to map the full etiology of various injuries.

I am a movement and athletics coach so hope to get more underlying theory to provide the mechanism between what we 'observe works' and what we 'know doesn't work'. I have painful experience from my own long-distance running career having suffered 17 major injuries in a 4 year period including Achilles tendinitis, retrocalcaneal bursitis, plantar fasciitis, metatarsal stress fractures, torn meniscus, head trauma, osteitis pubis, heel spurs/pumps, sprained ankles, big toe calcification, shinsplints, midfoot sprains and subtalar joint sprain. I suppose you can call this 'first hand experience'.

I treat runners through firstly restoring natural movement patterns and optimal biomechanics after analysis (optimal meaning = 'maximal potential to create force' + 'avoidance of excessive joint torque' + 'avoidance of unnecessary and inappropriate muscle tone') and work with Barry Murray (known on these forums) to put the more important 'wrapping' environment, lifestyle and nutrition around this 'optimal movement'.

Most of my own injuries now largely healed, or in remission, through a system of natural movement rehabilitation (restoring motor skill stones) I consider the practical leaders in this field to be Lee Saxby (Born to Run system), Erwan Le Corre (MovNat), Ido Portal (Movement Culture) and dr Nicholas Romanov (Pose Method) and Gray Cook (Functional Movement Systems). But all of this sits within the shell of creating an 'optimal environment' and seasonal diet (as described on this page)

I'd like to have discussions here that links the best practices they have created and are creating to the underlying theory to get closer to the dream of a 'unified theory of injury'. Pain and injury are not synonymous, of course, and some athletes are in pain and not injured and viceversa (partly explained by the Pain neuromatrix hypothesis) and this would be an interesting topic to branch off into as well here.

### René Borg — 2015-08-07T18:28:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/towards-a-unified-theory-of-injury-treatment.16692/post-174195>

TOPIC #1 - Local energy crisis in muscles, trigger points and injury

SUMMARY: Local energy crisis in muscle cells, along with other factors, cause trigger points which causes the pain associated with many common sports injuries. How does Jack's theories help us understand the underlying mechanisms better and how should we modify our approach to 'symptom treatment' (the trigger point) and prevention (not getting trigger points) in light of this new understanding.

We can create great relief for most sporting injuries, albeit temporary, by using several types of conventional treatments for trigger points (self-massage, cold treatment, dry needling, laser, etc.) but a complete theory of the why trigger points occur is not yet set in stone. Symptomatic causes seem to be:

\- Motor end plate dysfunction
\- Upregulation of genes that cause muscle hyperexcitability
\- Spontaneous electric activity in trigger point site while adjacent muscle tissue is electrically silent
\- ATP energy crisis causing sensitisation of nociceptive nerves in the vicinity (including bradykinins, cytokines, serotonin, histamine, potassium, prostaglandins, leukotrienes, somatostatin, and substance P)
\- Demyelination of sensory nerves
\- Central sensitisation of the CNS from constant nociceptive signals caused by biomechanical and chemical stress

Central to biomechanical stress is Gary Ward's 'Flow Motion Model of Movement' or rule that 'joints act, muscles react' or more fully expressed: GRAVITY acts on JOINTS/SKELETON to which the BRAIN/CNS react by asking MUSCLES to act. So when a joint moves towards a position away from what the brain perceives as the 'stable safe centre', muscles are recruited to bring the 'joint back to safety'. So 'static' postural misalignment will cause biomechanical stress and trigger points because certain muscles will be constantly activated to 'pull' the joint back in 'safe position'.

The poorer the tensegrity of the body the more susceptible a person would be to biomechanical stress I would think. Apart from that I am interested in people's views on the linkage between what is known about the 'symptom' of trigger points and Jack's theories and how we might use this fuller understanding of the mechanism to modify the current approaches to treating these points.

# Thread: Glyphosate and breastmilk
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glyphosate-and-breastmilk.16695/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-08-08T15:17:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glyphosate-and-breastmilk.16695/post-174239>

Warnings delivered in Germany:

> The Green party made their warning after testing the breast milk of 16 women from a variety of German regions.
>
> Traces of glyphosate, a chemical used in weed-killers, amounting to between 0.210 and 0.432 nanograms per mililitre were recorded.
>
> In drinking water a quantity of no more than 0.100 nanograms of the substance is allowed. ...

[http://www.thelocal.de/20150626/concerns-over-safety-of-german-breast-milk](http://www.thelocal.de/20150626/concerns-over-safety-of-german-breast-milk)

Have we really plumbed the depths yet? No. We haven't.

In the U.S. levels in breastmilk are 1600 (sixteen hundred) times the level that concerns the Germans:

[https://twitter.com/zenhoneycutt/status/623120155078324224](https://twitter.com/zenhoneycutt/status/623120155078324224)

I sometimes wonder if the quantity of carbohydrate, or the increasing percentage of that that is refined carbohydrate, or even missed sleep is the factor that's most operative in American obesity (and many other American phenomena). The sheer quantity of the crap that is sprayed on food "Stateside" … What does this do to the gut microbiome? How do the various different pollutants inter-react? (Ans: no one knows.)

And is what people on the board of companies like Monsanto and Pfizer are prepared to do related to the process of socialisation and "education" in modern societies? I don't know. I do wonder, though. I really do. And I suspect some here - Gretchen and Josh, for example - might have some interesting thoughts on that.

> ‘Bitzer,’ said Mr. Gradgrind, broken down, and miserably submissive to him, ‘have you a heart?’
>
> ‘The circulation, sir,’ returned Bitzer, smiling at the oddity of the question, ‘couldn’t be carried on without one. No man, sir, acquainted with the facts established by Harvey relating to the circulation of the blood, can doubt that I have a heart.’
>
> ‘Is it accessible,’ cried Mr. Gradgrind, ‘to any compassionate influence?’
>
> ‘It is accessible to Reason, sir,’ returned the excellent young man. ‘And to nothing else.’

[http://www.gutenberg.org/files/786/786-h/786-h.htm](http://www.gutenberg.org/files/786/786-h/786-h.htm)

I doubt this what "Reason" most usefully means, but you know where Blitzer is - unfortunately - coming from.

And this is what Gradgrind has taught him coming home to roost.

She actually ended up coming down the aisle to the strains of:

### Gracelind — 2015-08-10T01:52:34+0300

### SeaHorse — 2015-08-10T15:58:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/music-for-a-wedding-%E2%80%A6.16700/post-174340>

OK...I can't resist putting in my organ story, except this was for a funeral on Prince Edward Island (where they have funeral co-ops) A neighbour of our was the organist for many services and sometime in the sixties was very happy to have a new electric organ to play with lots of shiny buttons and new fangled options. For her first funeral with the new organ she chose to play "Nearer my God to thee" as the service began. Unfortunately she had pressed the "Banjo" feature without knowing it and the hymn rolled out in a flurry of bluegrass banjo rolls!.....no one was amused as she madly pressed buttons to turn the damn banjo off.
Of course now it's a favourite story....

# Thread: CRAZY
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crazy.16704/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nittygrittydanny — 2015-08-10T17:00:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crazy.16704/post-174343>

I was at the beach this weekend. Saturday morning was good as I got my early AM sun and spent over 30mins in the surf.

Saturday afternoon was a total washout. Heavy thunderstorm, lots of lightning (sat on my covered front deck watching it, cool as hell).

Was watching an MLB game (Pittsburg vs Los Angeles). What caught my attention most was the # of fans setting in the HIGH $$$$ seats behind home plate that were spending most of their time looking down at a phone??? I lucked out at a spring training game a couple years ago and got the last seat behind home plate so close I was calling balls and strikes properly! How the hell can you spend a couple hundred for those seats and NOT pay attention???

At dinner the other night i noticed the same thing, whole tables (6-8 people) looking at phones. At the local pub, outside enjoying the live entertainment, 4 people sharing a table, ALL 4 were looking at their phones!!!

I bet the chaos that will ensue after an EMF pulse damages our country's cell phone services will be worse than drug rehab for those so addicted to their devices!!

### nicld — 2015-08-10T18:16:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crazy.16704/post-174348>

It appears to be even more addicting then drugs and is probably worse for the body too.

# Thread: Teeth grinding
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth-grinding.16707/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JMO — 2015-08-11T02:10:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth-grinding.16707/post-174377>

My 15 daughter has been in braces for over 4 years. Her first ortho had her in head gear then phase 1 and phase 2 braces. Then he wanted to do jaw surgery which found out was pretty much his standard protocol. Changed ortho to daemon braces and had a 2 year plan. When she had the braces removed recently, found out she had been grinding her teeth and did a lot of damage to her cuspids leveling them. He said the grinding was pretty significant for a 15 yr old. Her ortho wanted to get them rebuilt but the dentist wants to wait and have her wear a mouth guard. So...wondering if this is stress on her head from metal braces and nnemf. I'd like to try some oeseopathic treatments and see if that helps. I asked the dentist if there's a way to tell if she has stopped grinding and his response was to have a mouth guard ($1500) made and see if it has wear. I was wondering if the grinding was mostly due to braces. Anyone have any experience with this or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

### JessEvans — 2015-08-20T04:19:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth-grinding.16707/post-175176>

I grind my teeth while sleeping and I had my dentist make me a soft mouth guard. I KNOW there is no way he charged me that much. I think I paid around $500 which seemed like a lot to me. I would suggest shopping around for another dentist. As an interim measure, there are non-fitted mouth guards that you can buy at the drug store that she might be able to use, and there might even be home kits to make a mold and send away for a fitted one.

I was treated by an osteopath for the muscle strain and stiffness that was caused by my teeth clenching and grinding. It was very helpful but it didn't stop the behavior. Wearing the mouth guard not only stops the damage to the teeth but for me it changed the clenching/grinding and made it less aggressive. Now it is more like a gentle biting down on the mouth guard. I don't need to see the osteopath any more.

When he first treated me, my osteopath predicted that I might wear out the soft mouthguard and need to switch to a hard one but that hasn't happened. I do consciously yawn (to stretch the jaw muscles) and consciously relax my jaw at night before I go to sleep. Once you start night time bruxation (clenching/grinding) it is very difficult to stop. Even if the triggers aren't there any more, the behavior will often continue. For me, the trigger was taking a martial arts class where the teacher wanted me to bite down whenever I punched or kicked. So, that sort of entrained me to transfer any nervous tension to the jaw and to bite down any time I did anything. Like stand up from bed, for example. You can control it to some extent when you are awake, but not when asleep. For your daughter, the braces may have caused it but she getting out of the braces may not make it go away.

The most important thing is to get your daughter into some kind of mouthguard as soon as possible to protect her teeth from further damage.

### endless — 2015-08-20T06:30:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth-grinding.16707/post-175189>

Mine was about $300 from the dentist. I'd get another quote, or at least ask why so much. Problem for me was I would not keep it in, I would take it out in my sleep and find it under the bed! My dentist suggested wearing it in the daytime instead, sometime when it would not be in the way of eating, talking etc. So I did, and it helped a lot, broke me of the clenching habit (and grinding at night) and now my teeth naturally rest slightly open when I'm relaxed. I also made a point of taking a lot of magnesium. The osteopath is a good idea too.

### b612 — 2015-08-20T13:04:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth-grinding.16707/post-175199>

My osteopath fixed me.
I only needed one visit.

### Penny — 2017-09-28T00:20:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth-grinding.16707/post-224397>

> [JMO said:](/goto/post?id=174377)
>
> My 15 daughter has been in braces for over 4 years. Her first ortho had her in head gear then phase 1 and phase 2 braces. Then he wanted to do jaw surgery which found out was pretty much his standard protocol. Changed ortho to daemon braces and had a 2 year plan. When she had the braces removed recently, found out she had been grinding her teeth and did a lot of damage to her cuspids leveling them. He said the grinding was pretty significant for a 15 yr old. Her ortho wanted to get them rebuilt but the dentist wants to wait and have her wear a mouth guard. So...wondering if this is stress on her head from metal braces and nnemf. I'd like to try some oeseopathic treatments and see if that helps. I asked the dentist if there's a way to tell if she has stopped grinding and his response was to have a mouth guard ($1500) made and see if it has wear. I was wondering if the grinding was mostly due to braces. Anyone have any experience with this or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Go to Big Five - buy 2 mouth guards for $9.00 - made for martial arts - it's insane what they charge for a crappy little piece of silicone - I grinded my teeth for years - I think it's a COMT thing - maybe some lemon balm tea at night and taurine - anything that helps make GABA would be great too - I just ran into a guy who bit his fingernails for years, went to one crystal therapy session where the practitioner put a crystal at the bottom of his feet and he stopped... red light to the bottom of the feet might even help - any chiropractor or osteopath would help IMHO - they muscle test it -

Good point about head trauma.

I don't want to go into his history too much for privacy reasons, but I wasn't advising prolactin blood level testing, he did it on his own. He had done it for a long time as part of his TRT regimen, and his prolactin levels just reached low-normal for the first time ever after he added AM sun and other things (and incidentally stopped TRT).

# Thread: How to counteract against bluelight exposure during daytime
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-counteract-against-bluelight-exposure-during-daytime.16714/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2015-08-13T10:31:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-counteract-against-bluelight-exposure-during-daytime.16714/post-174547>

I am working many hours of the day in front of the screen. I try only to use the devices during daytime and have installed f.lux and set it to sunlight during daytime.
But I still face a non native blue-light source over the course of the day. Because I look directly into the screen and the screen has more bluelight than the sun.
Is it not so bad as I imagine or is it really an issue? What should I do do counteract if it is an issue? Should I were blueblockers sooner in the evening?
More DHA is needed for sure. I try to cover that. Is there a program for the screen for dimming the blue light even more during daytime for textwork?

### PaulG — 2015-08-13T12:49:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-counteract-against-bluelight-exposure-during-daytime.16714/post-174549>

Non native EMF in the form of blue light during the day is a poor cousin to getting the full spectrum frequencies of the sun during the day. We are designed by nature to get natural light during the day, so if possible, sit near an open window or get outside as much as possible during the day. Maybe you can get some full spectrum lighting at work. See [http://www.naturallight.com.au/styled/](http://www.naturallight.com.au/styled/)

# Thread: flu as an opportunity to rebuild mitochondria?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flu-as-an-opportunity-to-rebuild-mitochondria.16723/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### anglosaxon — 2015-08-15T10:07:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flu-as-an-opportunity-to-rebuild-mitochondria.16723/post-174724>

Ive had flu about 3 weks ago. it was bad for 4 days with loads of runny noses and coughing. sorry to be graphic. I had a temperature for 48 hours. I was reading that lots of mitochondria/ cells are killed off by the flu bug, especially cells that are not very healty, and are cleared by all that mucus.
So now Im recovering and want to know how do I best build back healthy cells. Im assuming that IF and doing a post exersice water fast is not a good idea at this time.
As this is the begginers area, please be kind.

### Peertje80 — 2015-08-15T13:55:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flu-as-an-opportunity-to-rebuild-mitochondria.16723/post-174728>

Well it is just one thing of many, but get some extra hours of sleep in. During sleep your body is doing its repair. So this is extra important when your sick.

### caroline — 2015-08-15T14:40:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flu-as-an-opportunity-to-rebuild-mitochondria.16723/post-174729>

drink tons and tons of good water and lots of outdoor time/grounding .....I am suffering the same fate right now!

I am thinking that food is not all that important..... my opinion.......

and lots of sleep as Peertje suggested.... I slept an enormous amount for a couple of days ...and I am still needing a lot more sleep than usual.

### anglosaxon — 2015-08-15T15:21:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flu-as-an-opportunity-to-rebuild-mitochondria.16723/post-174735>

Thank you, can you tell me what exactly is good water. I filter my water through a ceramic filter,it comes fromba resevoir on the hills.

### caroline — 2015-08-16T02:28:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flu-as-an-opportunity-to-rebuild-mitochondria.16723/post-174803>

spring water......

Does your water have fluoride?

### anglosaxon — 2015-08-16T06:04:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flu-as-an-opportunity-to-rebuild-mitochondria.16723/post-174814>

No, no flouride. Not much flouridation in UK only in some urban city which traditionally had bad teeth in kids.
its chlorinated, so we filter it.
Ive looked around locally for springs, but there are few which inspire any hope of a good test, as they are on farm land, we used to be on spring water but again we UV filtered it as it was always at risk getting E Coli from local sheep and cattle.

Interesting research on boy's penis size reflected in growing up in a city vs. the country. Baby boys who grew up in the country during puberty were found to have a larger penis after puberty. # Thread: Great books
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-books.16741/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lookingforanswers — 2015-08-19T17:14:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-books.16741/post-175112>

Just wanted to mention two good books I've recently read that you all may enjoy:

Blue Mind by Wallace J. Nichols - discusses how important water is to humans and why we are so drawn to it. I found this to be very interesting though I didn't necessarily agree 100% with everything the author says.

You are the Placebo by Dr. Joe Dispenza - I'm still reading this one, but it has blown my mind so far. It's making me question a lot of what I thought I knew.

In fact, it makes me wonder if I can heal my gut just by using my mind. What do you think? Is it possible to truly heal ourselves just by thought? I've been meditating a lot lately and am trying to just adopt a very positive state of mind. I usually do well, but eventually I crash. I'll start out truly believing I can use my mind alone to repair whatever damage has been done to my body. I'll start eating foods that I haven't been able to tolerate in a long time (good foods - veggies and such, not junk food!) and I'll do well for a few days and then all of the sudden my body can't handle anymore and I have a "flare." While I'm doing well, I will feel almost euphoric and truly believe "this time I'm on an upstreak and it's going to last and I'll just get better and better" and then suddenly BAM! I'm back to square one

I believe this to be an oxalate dump based on the symptoms I have, so then it makes me question whether my mind alone can really cause a lasting difference.

I'd love to hear anyone's thoughts on this.

# Thread: Magnetic Detector Smart Phone Apps
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-detector-smart-phone-apps.16743/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SpiritGuide — 2015-08-19T18:34:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-detector-smart-phone-apps.16743/post-175132>

Hello I just found out something very interesting and wanted to share it here. Has anyone else downloaded or used these apps, what was your experience?

My wife and I downloaded the "Magnetic Detector!" app on our iPhones to test the EMF field strengths in our home... what a shocker!

Not surprisingly the microwave oven (which we haven't used in years) put out a strong field, as did our refrigerator, we found a strong field by the head of the bed in our guest room which was right above the refrigerator so we moved the bed over a few feet and cleared it up.

The EMF output on my iPhone 5 was so much higher than my wife's iPhone 6 that I immediately ran to the Verizon store and upgraded mine to the iPhone 6 as well.

Another shocking thing I noticed about it was that putting the phone in airplane mode did NOT turn off the antennae, it actually made the EMF's worse on my iPhone 5 when it was in airplane mode! (The iPhone 6 also doesn't turn off the antennae in airplane mode, but it's the same EMF field strength as when it's in normal mode... so my suggestion is to test this for yourself to see how it affects your phone's EMF's too.

The App we use is called "Magnetic Detector!" by Daniel J. Perez, it's free on iTunes, I didn't see it on Android but a quick search just now came up with a few alternative options on Google Play.

Please post your findings or suggestions here, thanks for joining the discussion, My hope is that my posting this helps someone learn more about their EMF environment today.

### Starfish Prime — 2015-08-25T15:34:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-detector-smart-phone-apps.16743/post-175649>

I am using a similar app until I can afford a proper meter. Im uncertain what levels are acceptable though, I get very little variation location to location (the three places I've looked at span at 60 mile radius, plus the car) so accuracy is suspect, IMO. In the meantime I'm killing power to everything but the fridge/water heater overnight.

### SpiritGuide — 2015-08-27T00:03:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-detector-smart-phone-apps.16743/post-175817>

I used it on the road too, but the most eye opening use was inside the house... we actually moved our bed in the guest room a few feet over because the EMF fields were so high directly above the kitchen and it was really bad right where the pillows were located.

### Scompy — 2015-08-27T02:02:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-detector-smart-phone-apps.16743/post-175831>

Awesome info... I'm guessing you're talking about the Magnetic field of the Phone rather than the RF? Airplane mode should be turning off the RF.

This makes me wonder: is there an optimal Cell phone that best mitigates the Rf-EMF that comes through it and also has a low Mag-EMF too? Seems like a great company strategy in the future. When I measure my dumb-phone and put the meter right next to it, I can get the RF-EMF up to 400+ microW/Sq. meter. My wife's phone I've measured in the past at around 100 mW/Sq.m, but now you make me think that it's based on location to cell-towers, where the phone is currently at in the home, current signal strength, and other factors at the moment rather than the phone alone. I'll have to research this in full.

### SpiritGuide — 2015-08-27T17:34:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-detector-smart-phone-apps.16743/post-175874>

That's a great question Scompy, I downloaded a magnetic field detector app, didn't consider that there was a RF component to consider as well.

I found a few apps on iPhone and will play around with it today.

The reason I was using a magnetic detector was because of the recent shows by David Wilcock on his show Wisdom Teachings on GaiamTV that just happened to be about how magnetic fields affect our health, where natural ley lines cross (they're spaced just about 10 feet apart) you will experience negative effects on your health and we wanted to check the magnetic field strengths over our beds and couches to be sure we weren't sitting/laying in bad areas.

So I was just focusing on magnetics.

It looks like there aren't any free apps on iPhone for RF measurement but it's an important topic so I'll probably buy one soon to see if I can get this sorted out.

### FutureVision — 2015-08-27T23:51:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-detector-smart-phone-apps.16743/post-175951>

I went looking for an app like this for Android. I learned not too long ago our phones have a ton of sensors in them we don't even know about. Would be great to leverage them for our own good. I've been knocking around the idea of buying a dedicated meter but I'm not completely sure which one. If my phone can get me started why not? At least until I get a dedicated meter. Which meters are you looking at buying?

hi guys
I had ordered the 50ft cat 5E flat ethernet cable and hard wired the laptop but I am hearing a lot of RF EMF coming from the length of the wire. I am using a simple Am radio for this. # Thread: HCG plus CT plus CO plus more calories- WORKING!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-plus-ct-plus-co-plus-more-calories-working.16761/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### npc3 — 2015-08-22T03:41:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-plus-ct-plus-co-plus-more-calories-working.16761/post-175354>

For the past two weeks I have done 200 U HCG, 2+ TBSP CO/day, and half my body for 30 min 65 degree water (trying to get upper body into the cold water but taking it slow). I don't count calories and simply eat something small when hungry like a 80 calorie sausage or tbsp of CO. I do have a large protein breakfast, a tuna wrap for lunch with a wrap made out of veggies, and huge piece of fish for dinner with sometimes a very small salad with Coconut oil as my dressing with balsalmic vinegar. No exercise. The first week I lost 9 lbs and a whole inch off my butt! This week the weight loss has slowed down but I have also eaten out probably 5 times since I started the diet (always try healthy options fish, chicken). One time included a day where I ate a burger with bun, sweet potato fries and had spoonfuls of my husband's brownie sundae. That particular day the next day I actually LOST a whole pound when I was hitting a plateau and weight loss had stalled for a few days.

I feel differently on this round of HCG compared to any other round I've done in the past. I feel normal, not hungry, no cravings, the brownie sundae was more like "mehh" as opossed to OMG. I have energy, feel great, sleep great, good sex drive, positive mood. The CT makes my skin nice and soft and I don't feel bloated or like I am retaining water all the time. I'm sure if I cut my calories down I'd lose faster but seems to be like 2-3 lbs a week with very little effort. I'll take it!! Before I could never stay on the 500 calorie diet and would give up to temptation, or be so hungry at the end of P2 that I'd go nuts in P3. This time I can really feel my body changing. My appetite is starting to regulate itself- I find my self not finishing meals. Plus I am losing a ton of inches, 1.5" off my butt, 2" off my waist, 2" off my belly. This is easy! I've struggled a very long time with my weight and I feel this combo is actually fixing whatever hormonal/mictochondrial disfunction I have. Thank you Dr. K!

# Thread: Help, my Ask Jack thread from 3 years ago.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-my-ask-jack-thread-from-3-years-ago.16765/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### growltgc — 2015-08-23T09:54:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-my-ask-jack-thread-from-3-years-ago.16765/post-175426>

I made this thread in 2012:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/rash-on-my-chest-might-be-prurigo-pigmentosa.4263/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/rash-on-my-chest-might-be-prurigo-pigmentosa.4263/)
It's an "Ask jack" so I can't reply there now because of permissions. Since that time there has been a lot more written on the web about the arising of prurigo pigmentosa in ketosis venturing man. Very impressive is the theketorash.com blog which has pulled together both academic and n=1 research. A round of antibiotics either Doxycyline or Minocycline puts the rash into remissions, but none is sure why? I used Minocycline last time to treat mine. I only used it when the rash flared up though... which happened 4-5 times in 9 months. I probably should have taken it straight for 2 weeks. I am probably going to do that. I wonder why I have to take Antibiotics. And what to think about this? And might Jack know anything more? it's been 3 years, come on!!

And the long story short: I just started restricting my carbs 7 days ago to probably to around 40g. And I have not done this since 2012. And the rash has returned tonight in its symmetric form under my collar bones with its familiar itch.

Here is maybe the best explanation(s) I have found... I am thinking related to number 3? maybe explains why antibiotics work? Although I have read its some anti-inflammation mechanism of the antibiotic.
[http://bjjcaveman.com/2013/08/17/prurigo-pigmentosa-thoughts-on-pathogenesis/](http://bjjcaveman.com/2013/08/17/prurigo-pigmentosa-thoughts-on-pathogenesis/)

> My ideas fall into 2 main categories:
>
> **Ketones Directly Cause the Rash**
>
> 1\. Something about ketones is causing an inflammatory reaction in our skin. The ketones react to our skin like a drop of acid causing damage and inflammation. I don’t think this is actually it because it should affect EVERYONE in ketosis.
>
> 2\. Or maybe because our bodies are so unaccustomed to high levels of ketones, our immune systems haven’t had the chance to acclimate and thus over-react to them, like an allergic reaction. This is possible since everyone’s immune system is unique and reacts to things differently.
>
> **Ketones Alter Our Physiology Which Indirectly Cause the Rash**
>
> We know for sure ketones change our physiology because once we are ketogenic, our insulin levels go down, our blood sugar goes down, we begin to lose weight, the overall body water goes down since we have less glycogen, other hormones are affected such as our thyroid hormone levels ([Parts 1](http://bjjcaveman.com/2013/04/28/the-effect-of-a-ketogenic-diet-on-thyroid-hormone/), [2](http://bjjcaveman.com/2013/06/15/the-effect-of-a-ketogenic-diet-on-thyroid-hormone-part-2/)), and our cholesterol can either stay the same, go down, or go way up (Parts [1](http://bjjcaveman.com/2013/03/10/the-effect-of-a-ketogenic-diet-on-cholesterol-part-1/), [2](http://bjjcaveman.com/2013/03/14/the-effect-of-a-ketogenic-diet-on-cholesterol-part-2/), [3](http://bjjcaveman.com/2013/04/06/the-effect-of-a-ketogenic-diet-on-cholesterol-part-3/), [4](http://bjjcaveman.com/2013/06/23/the-effect-of-a-ketogenic-diet-on-cholesterol-part-4/)), among many other things. It could be any of these things.
>
> 1\. A high LDL-P can possibly either directly cause the rash (not likely since not everyone with high LDLs have the rash) or elicit an indirect response. The same goes for lowered blood sugar and lowered thyroid hormone levels. Not everyone with low blood sugar and low thyroid levels have the rash.
>
> 2\. Lowered levels of insulin. This actually has some traction because one of the ways to make the rash go away is to reintroduce carbs into the diet, which raises the insulin. Also in a few of the papers I reviewed ([here](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/8832962) and [here](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/12692369)) the rash went away after insulin was injected!
>
> 3\. Another idea I had is that being in ketosis can alter our skin flora and our gut biome. There is less glucose for organisms to feed on, so theoretically the ones that are more dependent on glucose for metabolism start dying off allowing others to grow. Having altered flora can elicit an immune response. I know there’s a lot of talk out there about ‘candida die off’ with ketogenesis or coconut oil intake. I think this is also a real possibility because the current medically recommended treatment is to use antibiotics as I discussed in my [prior post](http://bjjcaveman.com/2013/05/09/prurigo-pigmentosa-a-ketosis-associated-rash/) (my own feeling is still to try adding carbs back, and only turning to antibiotics if carbs don’t help).
>
>
>
> I want to emphasize that all of this is pure speculation on my part. I tried to include the supporting papers where I could but there are only a handful of papers on prurigo pigmentosa (and I reviewed almost all of them in my prior post) and the current scientific consensus is basically: **we don’t know why this happens.**

# Thread: Sometimes, one just has to not try and fix things.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sometimes-one-just-has-to-not-try-and-fix-things.16780/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2015-08-25T20:17:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sometimes-one-just-has-to-not-try-and-fix-things.16780/post-175676>

Lesson.......it is not good for one’s own health.

The SIL who is in poor health and lives in the EMF hell hole of Denver called and said she is about the become homeless and was wondering if she could stay with us for a bit. Of course I said yes but that made my cortisol levels go through the roof that night, sleep took a bit hit and the heart rate got messed up and the BP did too.

Most women, when faced with something like this, want to swoop in and try to fix things and make is easier on someone they love and care about, which is what I wanted to do. It is in our nature to care and try and fix much to our detriment (raise of hands of women here who have tried to do that). But that is NOT good for US.

I took a step back and realized I did not get her into this mess, I do not have to rush in and fix it. We will offer her support and help but I am NOT dropping everything to make sure that this is as painless as it can be (and she was not asking that we do this).

She mismanaged her money, not me. If she loses most of her stuff, she did this by buying too much stuff. My health and well being is more important than her materialist comfort.

So it was a good lesson for me and a success story. I am NOT responsible for fixing anything or anyone but ME!!!

### Da-mo — 2015-08-25T21:11:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sometimes-one-just-has-to-not-try-and-fix-things.16780/post-175697>

This is not what I was expecting from the title but it is a good lesson nonetheless.

What I was expecting was something along the lines of fixing individual health problems rather than the conditions of existence that led to problems.

When I first started on this journey I was chasing down health issues one by one with this supplement - that protocol etc. Now I have learned that if you change the conditions of existence, this releases the doctor within and most of the individual problems (or symptoms) melt away.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sometimes-one-just-has-to-not-try-and-fix-things.16780/post-176010>

Went through this last winter with my mom. Destitute again her and all of her stuff and all of her problems landed on my front door step. I let her in and she had the fucking nerve to become abusive towards me every time I brought up the subject of her finding permanent residence...not in my living room. I started to fall apart fast and furiously. Made the hardest decision of my life to kick her out....gut wrenching. But it was right. It's hard when you try to love someone that doesn't love themselves. A losing fucking battle. Glad you recognized it early

### nicld — 2015-08-29T23:52:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sometimes-one-just-has-to-not-try-and-fix-things.16780/post-176047>

Next week after the DD goes back to school the hubby will start helping her get stuff filled out and make calls to get her back on her feet. She does not have any intention on staying here too long and the DH will not allow that.

She is sleeping in the bed and around 9 pm the TV goes off and we go to bed and so does she. Her sleep is so fucked up it is not funny. She can sleep 18 hours a day plus has sleep apnea. The only thing that is annoying right now is that she will not get herself something to eat. We make her a plate and take it to her. That WILL stop as she was able to make her own food when she lived on her own, she can get up and fix her own plate.

Just don't know if I want to put forth the energy to help her try and get better. I am still working on me and the DD.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-dhea-s-bad-environment.16784/post-175801>

So I got my DHEA-S lab result back, and my DHEA level is 77.

Normal is 65-380
Optimal is 250-380

I don't understand Tanner stages, but it seems related to age and sex- however, I don't get the table below.

Anyway, I have heard Jack say he uses DHEA as a proxy for the person's redox potential, and especially sleep:

> DHEA is the middle of the hormone chain, and tells me about androgen power and the immunity switch, so it gives me a decent idea of how metabolically efficient one is.......it tells me about the proximal chain (free T3 and pregnenolone) an it tells me what I should expect from the distal sex steroid hormones if this one is low........the most important thing DHEA tells me is sleep efficiency because of its 98% correlation to IL-6 production in the brain....DHEA is the second most powerful antioxidant in the brain..........from its level and the persons age I can extrapolate what their IGF1 and salivary melatonin are doing to circadian cycles.

I need a little more insight about the sleep part because my sleep is REALLY good. I get to sleep easily, stay asleep, and dream a lot. Sleep is actually the one thing I'm NOT worried about) Perhaps my context changes things- would "perimenopausal" indicate hormonal imbalance by nature...(??)

It's funny, because I tend to want to dissect this, but another part of me knows it's really simple- hormone levels are the reflection of the environment your cells are subjected to. I already know I have too much nnEMF and blue light in my environment. I need to get busy fixing that instead of getting bogged down in my results. The results will change when my environment changes. I really do believe that...

### Psyche — 2015-08-26T19:41:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-dhea-s-bad-environment.16784/post-175807>

**THE DESTRUCTION OF THE GUT: CORTISOL**

To optimize the handling of these subatomic particles naturally from food requires proper circadian signals from cortisol and melatonin within the brain. Both of these rely deftly on the Vitamin A and D cycles in the brain and body, respectively. Cortisol in excess destroys the collagen triple helix everywhere in the body. When cortisol is raised in the stroma of tissues there is a loss of electric charge. This chronic charge loss leads to an alteration of the serotonin supply in the gut to convert to melatonin. This is due to the loss of charge or the redox potential in the gut. It is tied to a defect in water chemistry due to a smaller exclusion zone of water in the central nervous system. This is the link to CSF, I mentioned earlier in the blog. When this happens their is a lowered charge carried in the DC current within the system. _When a person is more oxidized for any reason at all, there is a shift in our immune systems’ T helper cells, favoring the TH1 arm of the immune system over the TH2 system._ This is a thermodynamic effect due to a loss of electrons.

When the TH1 arm dominates, the protein structure of zonulin is ionized and is altered on a molecular basis. A new emergent protein is the result and it allows for a loss of gut barrier integrity. This exposes food in the gut lumen to the GALT. Eventually the proteins from gastric contents makes contact with cell mediated immune arms allowing for the formation of new emergent proteins that also can activate the same arms of the immune system, and the autoimmune battle begins. Eventually, this mismatch develops into most of the auto immune diseases you all have heard of. TH1-dominated responses are involved in the pathogenesis of organ-specific autoimmune disorders, Crohn’s disease, sarcoidosis, acute kidney allograft rejection, and some unexplained recurrent abortions.

**Where you see food as metabolic fuel, I see it as electrons and protons. The organs in our body take the thermodynamic energy and information in electrons and protons and turn it into a biologic signal called hormone information.** This process itself is also a phase transition thermodynamically. The symmetry breaker in any organ is its water content. If you are dehydrated this organ will be under thermodynamic pressure. These electrons and protons allow the proper coordinated actions of our brain to work with the adaptive nature of our current environment to decipher the signals for our immune system to properly engage and yoke with the environment signals it receives. This is why we must stop thinking about food as fuel, and understand how subatomic particles determine the molecular gymnastics that the proteins of biology can organize around and sculpt our tissues and determine their function. We are all quantum beings whether we want to believe it or not.

# Thread: nnEMF Causes Excess Calcium causing excess Nitric Oxide which (can) cause a potent oxidant
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-causes-excess-calcium-causing-excess-nitric-oxide-which-can-cause-a-potent-oxidant.16790/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Frank Peters — 2015-08-29T03:24:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-causes-excess-calcium-causing-excess-nitric-oxide-which-can-cause-a-potent-oxidant.16790/post-175997>

Here's the context of my question. It is coming from Dr Martin Pall's work:
(See YouTube -

)

In 14 minutes, Dr Pall lays it out as follows but do listen to his YouTube. At 4 minutes 30 seconds into it, he gets to this point.

-   nnEMF (hyper)activates L-Type Voltage Gated Calcium Channels in the cell
-   causing an increase in intracellular calcium
-   increased calcium activates two enzymes, nNOS and eNOS, which are nitric oxide synthases

These nitric oxide synthases are calcium dependent and can't activate unless there is increased levels of calcium in the cell and when calcium levels increase so too do levels of nitric oxide.

Nitric oxide can work along two different pathways; one pathway is normal but the other pathway is pathophysiological.

-   on the pathophysiological (bad) pathway
    -   the elevated levels of nitric oxide combine with superoxide to form peroxynitrite
    -   peroxnitrite (a potent oxidant) causes oxidative stress

HERE IS MY QUESTION.
Wouldn't this phenomenon show up in blood work? Increased calcium? Increased nitric oxide? Peroxnitrite?

### PaulG — 2015-09-03T13:12:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-causes-excess-calcium-causing-excess-nitric-oxide-which-can-cause-a-potent-oxidant.16790/post-176307>

Good question.

See also [http://www.oscillatorium.com/id19.html](http://www.oscillatorium.com/id19.html) - there are a lot of papers linked there.

[https://www.healthyhabits.com/emf-calcium-electrosensitivity/](https://www.healthyhabits.com/emf-calcium-electrosensitivity/)

> Weak electromagnetic fields cause calcium ions to detach from cell membranes, a well-documented effect that has significant consequences. That “highly conductive salty fluid” that we call blood makes the human body an efficient antenna for picking up electromagnetic radiation, says Goldsworthy. “If an alternating electrical field is applied across the membrane, the negatively charged membrane and the positively charged \[calcium and potassium\] ions move in opposite directions so that the ions tend to bounce on and off the membrane.”
>
> Calcium ions with a double charge are more easily removed from the membrane than the single-charge potassium ions. Because potassium is more abundant in living cells than calcium, potassium ions are more likely to replace calcium, however, calcium ions give cell membranes a stability that potassium ions cannot. Weakened cell membranes tear more easily.
>
> The body has mechanisms for repairing torn membranes, but, before healing occurs, weakened membranes allow cell contents to escape, causing inflammation and free calcium to enter the cell. Calcium triggers metabolic changes in cells, including enzyme activity and gene expression. Its effects depend upon the cells involved.
>
> “Leakage in the hair cells of the cochlea give false sensations of sound (tinnitus),” explains Goldsworthy, “and those in the vestibular system (which sense rotation, linear acceleration and gravity) give false sensations of motions, which result in dizziness and symptoms of motion sickness, including nausea.” Too much calcium in brain neurons causes the cells to release neurotransmitters more easily.
>
> Goldsworthy hypothesizes, “It is possible that at least some forms of electrosensitivity could be due to the victims having their natural blood calcium levels bordering on hypocalcaemia.”
>
> Hypocalcaemia is a medical condition, usually caused by a hormone imbalance, in which the concentration of ionized calcium in the blood and in extracellular fluid is abnormally low. People with hypocalcaemia have fewer ions to complete with the potassium ions rushing to cell membranes disrupted by electromagnetic currents.
>
> Hypocalcaemia and electrosensitvity share many of the same symptoms: skins disorders, paresthesias (pins and needles, numbness, sensations of burning, etc.), fatigue, muscle cramps, cardiac arrhythmia, and gastrointestinal problems. Goldsworthy says, “If this \[hypothesis\] is correct, conventional treatment for hypocalcaemia may relieve some if not all of these symptoms.”

[http://www.hese-project.org/hese-uk/en/papers/goldsworthy\_bio\_weak\_em\_07.pdf](http://www.hese-project.org/hese-uk/en/papers/goldsworthy_bio_weak_em_07.pdf)

### Frank Peters — 2015-09-04T22:14:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-causes-excess-calcium-causing-excess-nitric-oxide-which-can-cause-a-potent-oxidant.16790/post-176495>

> [PaulG said:](/goto/post?id=176307)
>
> Good question.
>
> See also [http://www.oscillatorium.com/id19.html](http://www.oscillatorium.com/id19.html) - there are a lot of papers linked there.
>
> [https://www.healthyhabits.com/emf-calcium-electrosensitivity/](https://www.healthyhabits.com/emf-calcium-electrosensitivity/)
>
>
> [http://www.hese-project.org/hese-uk/en/papers/goldsworthy\_bio\_weak\_em\_07.pdf](http://www.hese-project.org/hese-uk/en/papers/goldsworthy_bio_weak_em_07.pdf)

Hi PaulG. I than you for taking the time to refer me to these. But when I looked at them, I found them troubling. The first link, osillatoruim.com, is bogus because it does not link to a lot of papers. Rather clicking on any link on the web page downloads a screen shot of that web page in pdf format. So, nothing there links to a study.

The second link, healthhabits.com, is to a business that wants to sell stuff like vitamins. At this point, I am not interested in buying stuff because I am looking to learn more, first.

And the last link to Andrew Goldsworth's "The Biological Effects of Weak Electromagnetic Fields" is a link to his review and conclusions on some of the literature. I only saw little mention on calcium levels in the blood and these were levels going in the opposite direction, lower levels of calcium not higher. He makes no mentions in his review of the literature of VGCC's, nitric oxide synthases, nitric oxide or peroxnitrite. I am therefore ruling out his work in trying to answer my question.

I thank you again as I am hoping to find a definitive answer to my question above.

### PaulG — 2015-09-05T01:51:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-causes-excess-calcium-causing-excess-nitric-oxide-which-can-cause-a-potent-oxidant.16790/post-176505>

This is the pdf from the oscillatorium site, links do work
[http://oscillatorium.com/sitebuildercontent/sitebuilderfiles/calciumefflux102014.pdf](http://oscillatorium.com/sitebuildercontent/sitebuilderfiles/calciumefflux102014.pdf)

Also try Pubmed, quick search found this article
**[Electromagnetic fields instantaneously modulate nitric oxide signaling in challenged biological systems](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22940137)**

> This study shows that a non-thermal pulse-modulated RF signal (PRF), configured to modulate calmodulin (CaM) activation via acceleration of Ca(2+) binding kinetics, produced an immediate nearly 3-fold increase in nitric oxide (NO) from dopaminergic MN9D cultures (P < 0.001). NO was measured electrochemically in real-time using a NO selective membrane electrode, which showed the PRF effect occurred within the first seconds after lipopolysaccharide (LPS) challenge. Further support that the site of action of PRF involves CaM is provided in human fibroblast cultures challenged with low serum and exposed for 15 min to the identical PRF signal. In this case a CaM antagonist W-7 could be added to the culture 3 h prior to PRF exposure. Those results showed the PRF signal produced nearly a two-fold increase in NO, which could be blocked by W-7 (P < 0.001). To the authors' knowledge this is the first report of a real-time effect of non-thermal electromagnetic fields (EMF) on NO release from challenged cells. The results provide mechanistic support for the many reported bioeffects of EMF in which NO plays a role. Thus, in a typical clinical application for acute post operative pain, or chronic pain from, e.g., osteoarthritis, EMF therapy could be employed to modulate the dynamics of NO via Ca/CaM-dependent constitutive nitric oxide synthase (cNOS) in the target tissue. This, in turn, would modulate the dynamics of the signaling pathways the body uses in response to the various phases of healing after physical or chemical insult or injury.

### Frank Peters — 2015-09-05T17:52:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-causes-excess-calcium-causing-excess-nitric-oxide-which-can-cause-a-potent-oxidant.16790/post-176543>

>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-moon.16791/post-176011>

As someone who is very sick I'm exponentially more sensitive to things than normal people. For a few days prior and during a full moon my disease goes haywire. Particularly my adrenaline levels go through the roof and therefore my sleep gets even worse than normal. I'm talking 30-60 minutes a night which leads me into even further disease.

Sometimes it will go as far as psychosis (which I am close to daily regardless) but whatever the symptoms may be, it's always a revved up situation.

Is there anything that can be done to counter this aside from the regular protocols? Nothing I can do seems to have any effect.

I've been feeling as though I'm watching the things around me from a perspective other than my own; almost movie-like. In other words, my normal dissociation is greatly heightened. This induces brain fog because everything is so "weird", and then I become anxious about that, which exacerbates everything.

I think anything that would enhance one's own personal magnetism/stored charge in cells would help. Ground as much as possible, perhaps all day. Stay cold all day. Be ketotic. If possible, us various biohacker technologies such as CES, TENS, MET (microcurrent electro-therapy), etc. especially if combining it with other things. Example -> CES @ an alpha wave frequency while grounding and using the cool fat burner+cool gut buster and/or the CryoHelmet while also running a MET program at specified muscles/tissues.

# Thread: Coconut Oil & Weight Loss
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-weight-loss.16794/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Frank Kaplan — 2015-08-31T04:26:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-weight-loss.16794/post-176088>

I've been eating quite a lot of coconut oil - and plenty of Coconut Manna. I've been losing weight - especially with a lot of seafood and very low carbs.

I just read this on Hyperlipid and how eating it can cause an increase in blood insulin levels (albeit for chow fed lab rats) was curious about anyone's take on it:

Anyone reading [Chris](http://conditioningresearch.blogspot.com/2008/04/its-not-just-carbs.html) or [Emma's](http://blog.plantpoisonsandrottenstuff.info/2008/04/30/aspirin-like-compounds-increase-insulin-secretion-in-otherwise-healthy-obese-people/) blogs will realise that aspirin, and possibly other related salycilates from plants, cause the pancreas the secrete extra insulin. Avoid. Gluten and wheat germ agglutinin (both from wheat, barley and rye) are (or contain) insulin mimetics, avoid. Casein stimulates insulin secretion, avoid. Pharmaceutical NSAID probably do the same as salycilates, avoid if possible.

**Coconut oil** is interesting. It has a reputation for assisting weight loss, but if gavaged in to the stomach of a chow fed lab rat it will decrease blood glucose and increase blood insulin levels. You don't want to increase your insulin levels if you want to loose weight. There are other plus and minus sides to coconut oil, but I'd keep life simple and avoid it.

### lookingforanswers — 2015-09-03T18:41:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-specs.16798/post-176343>

Thanks for the clarification @[fitness@home](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5474/) ! I get so confused and sometimes I feel like I'm doing everything wrong. I'm glad to hear that maybe I am starting to understand something for once

### Da-mo — 2015-09-04T01:48:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-specs.16798/post-176446>

-   Water itself is a good EMF attenuator

and

-   Think about what you will treat your hot tub water with . . . we went away from chlorine/bromine (for obvious reasons) and now use a product that is 595g/l hydrogen peroxide and 0.55g/l silver as silver nitrate.

### lookingforanswers — 2015-09-04T14:05:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-specs.16798/post-176460>

Thanks for that info @[Da-mo](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9712/) . I've been looking into water treatment options as I definitely don't want chlorine. I was reading about using hydrogen peroxide, but we have well water and most of what I read said you should only use that with municipal water. Can I ask where your water is sourced? And would you mind sharing the name of the product you use? Thanks again!

### Da-mo — 2015-09-04T22:14:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-specs.16798/post-176494>

We have well/bore water too. Ours has a bit of iron in it so discolors at first as the iron oxidizes but that eventually gets taken out by the filter.

The peroxide acts as the oxidizer and the silver nitrate as the algacide. Residual peroxide levels may have some health benefits.

The product we use is Poppits [http://www.poolpoppits.com.au/about-poppits/chlorine-free-pool-spa-maintenance/](http://www.poolpoppits.com.au/about-poppits/chlorine-free-pool-spa-maintenance/)\- maybe you have something similar available locally.

# Thread: Invasive yeast growth as an argument against LCHF
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/invasive-yeast-growth-as-an-argument-against-lchf.16803/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### René Borg — 2015-09-01T22:37:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/invasive-yeast-growth-as-an-argument-against-lchf.16803/post-176192>

It was interesting I saw this study used as an argument that LCHF and ketosis are dangerous allostatic states that should be avoided for any length of time: [http://www.pnas.org/content/97/25/13619](http://www.pnas.org/content/97/25/13619)

Now there seems to be a number of issues with the study:

1\. it was done on agar not humans or other test animals thus presumably the findings may not transfer
2\. since a healthy individual can create glucose in the liver, you can presumably benefit from the observed suppressive effect of glucose even on a LCHF or ketogenic diet so this may only be a problem for people with reactive hypoglycemia to LCHF (done badly)
3\. My feeling is this article is looking at a simplistic cause-and-effect relationship between low glucose and candida albicans growth which does not necessarily need to become detrimental if the host's internal and external environment is set up differently
4\. The causal link, even as listed in the article, is not with glucose depletion per se but with increased activity of the protein kinase SNF1. Other things than just glycogen depletion may cause increased activity of this protein kinase
5\. Many other causal relationships have been suggested (stress, copper deficiency, zinc etc. etc.)
6\. The true environmental causes of the candida morphogenesis cannot be explained purely at the biochemical level because it is hidden at the level of physics (currents, light etc.)

Curious to Jack and everyone else's take on this argument being used as another rod to bash LCHF as irresponsible.

### thomas — 2015-09-01T23:04:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/invasive-yeast-growth-as-an-argument-against-lchf.16803/post-176194>

I am not a man to go deep in scientific terms. However I'll use the simplest science of them all which comes from observation then analysis:
You have no choice but to do lchf for a good chunk of the year if you live at high lattitudes. that's how your biology is supposed to work.

### Inger — 2015-09-02T09:18:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/invasive-yeast-growth-as-an-argument-against-lchf.16803/post-176233>

True! I cant find any much carbs here at all... just some berries in the woods.............. what to do...
and I am doing great
But I lie down in the morning sun.... nude.. or in my bikini... and suck in the light..... on the wet grass in the morning... under that beautiful tree in the garden... and it feels like heaven
having a raw lamb ball smoothie and fermented lamb hearts for breakfast........... life is so good

### René Borg — 2015-09-02T13:31:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/invasive-yeast-growth-as-an-argument-against-lchf.16803/post-176243>

Thomas - yes well that would be a good strategy - simply pose that question. There is no need to convince me - Jack's blogs do that already and I find his view on evolution convincing. But some of the anti-LCHF brigade does not agree on the fundamental assumptions. I get the feeling they believe that since we evolved as 'opportunistic omnivores' close to the equator, they also assume we evolved in climates where fruits were generally available year-round. They do not see the connection between light imprinting food and would likely need to be taken on a long journey to accept that notion and they would see human adaptations to a cold climate as an allostatic state not a homeostatic one - i.e. they consider our 'winter metabolism' pathological and something to continue working around (again because they do not see the connection between metabolism and light hours etc.).

What I am really interested in is the ammunition to help win the battle of ideas that is taking place - reading the above my theory would be that it is not the glycogen depletion 'per se' that makes candida albicans more aggressive - but it is something else. I know we can make the right choices without understanding the underlying mechanisms (the practitioner and our patients can rest with that) but I like to understand the mechanisms for when I get taken to task by aggressive reductionist scientists. We all know how they react Inger to 'n=1' and 'personal anecdotes' (they will shout 'confirmation bias', 'naturalistic fallacy', delusion etc. etc.)

### Inger — 2015-09-02T13:55:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/invasive-yeast-growth-as-an-argument-against-lchf.16803/post-176244>

> [René Borg said:](/goto/post?id=176243)
>
> We all know how they react Inger to 'n=1' and 'personal anecdotes' (they will shout 'confirmation bias', 'naturalistic fallacy', delusion etc. etc.)

Well... Rene.. if you want try to convince them... we have all the science in Jacks blogs... especially the latest ones... tells us everything about light... and goes deep!
So go ahead...  if you want to do it.. I do not..lol I just do not have the nerve to try to convince anyone..lol
I am thinking.. who seek will find.. and if your mind is open, you will find the truth.. with the rest I just do not bother
in my life, as I travel along.. I share with people, live, if they want to hear it or not lol but I just share. They can take it or leave it. Because it is my duty to share the light. I am forever deeply thankful to Jack that he shared it with me.......

### René Borg — 2015-09-03T13:50:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/invasive-yeast-growth-as-an-argument-against-lchf.16803/post-176308>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=176244)
>
> Well... Rene.. if you want try to convince them... we have all the science in Jacks blogs... especially the latest ones... tells us everything about light... and goes deep!
> So go ahead...  if you want to do it.. I do not..lol I just do not have the nerve to try to convince anyone..lol
> I am thinking.. who seek will find.. and if your mind is open, you will find the truth.. with the rest I just do not bother
> in my life, as I travel along.. I share with people, live, if they want to hear it or not lol but I just share. They can take it or leave it. Because it is my duty to share the light. I am forever deeply thankful to Jack that he shared it with me.......

It would have been convenient because the people who need convincing are doing important work in another area but sadly cannot see beyond current prejudices once they step outside that specialty. It would have been good to have influential individuals onboard with this because they will now go and spread the opposite memes to people who trust them. Thus the battle of ideas shifts a bit away. But what you said did remind me of Stephen Covey - you have to focus on what is in your circle of influence rather than your circle of concern. I've realised this case is in the circle of concern - so I'll leave it be and focus on influencing people closer to me.

# Thread: Collagen I vs. III and what to do about it...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/collagen-i-vs-iii-and-what-to-do-about-it.16817/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### René Borg — 2015-09-04T13:13:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/collagen-i-vs-iii-and-what-to-do-about-it.16817/post-176458>

When my clients come to be to get their motor skills 'reprogrammed' old tendonosis can be one of the most frustrating obstacles to deal with as a movement coach.

So I am interested in what the underlying physics on this site can tell us about what goes haywire when collagen III becomes more abundant in tendons after they suffer microtrauma and how it can help me advise people better rather than necesarily sending them straight to current 'fixes' for collagen degeneration (such as surgery, stem cell infusion, blood platelet injection, laser etc.).

So current research (one link below) seems to show that collagen 3 becomes more abundant in damaged tendons instead of the 'better' collagen 1 which is normally more abundant in connective tissue. The reason is speculated to be the ability to form cross-links quicker. All this is speculation at the molecular level. I wonder do we know what the more fundamental difference is between collagen I and collagen III and what aspects of the Rxes on this site may incite the body to remove the collagen III again and replace it wth the original collagen I? Can we even achieve this without a modicum of intervention (such as perhaps laser or light therapy) to support the body's own catatoxic response to this change.

Would movement reprogramming along with the principles on this site be enough for this change or do we need to go a bit more specific. Is light also a 'scalpel' for this type of tendon change? Could the change in collagen be due to constant exposure to the wrong type of vibration frequencies from the surfaces we walk on? (i.e. because we wear cushioned footwear so the body misinterprets its habitual adjustment because it cannot pick up the correct frequency from the surface it is contacting because the foot proprioceptors cannot pick up frequency of the force wave in time for the CNS to respond optimally).

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/7671527](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/7671527)

### prAna303 — 2015-09-04T13:48:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/collagen-i-vs-iii-and-what-to-do-about-it.16817/post-176459>

Hi.

What i have done to my own body and mind thru the last ten years resonates a bit with the above.

Since i had some old injuries that i managed quite well i sensed that i had to fix them before they show up as a limit, both physical and mental. It goes very well with Jacks work combined with a deep passion with some other areas.

\- I have fixed my neck, been hit by cars four times, riding bike in training and competition.

\- I have fixed my hip, a result from that neck.

\- I have fixed my elbow, connected with the above.

\- Working on the impossible according to my old doctor (25 years ago), my eyes. This one is going to take time but the change is big.

This all boils down to one simple little thing, let go!

Since my very early days i had my own master, a teacher that has been with me every day, pushing and pulling me apart, demanding my response within seconds, always ready to pull the mat away... Nature.

Light is just like Jack says, fundamental and most of us miss the point... We are built upon that, not just in a goofball kind of way but way deeper and higher. This means that we are made to use it 24/7, 365 days / year, forever as long as we live. You say something about walking and wearing shoes. Yes this is a big issue but you need to connect it deeper. The feet is optimal without any technology applied, yes any kind of protection BUT that demands that you are connected in nature the whole time. I think it is not just about magnetism, it is the light that you should be able to transfer to higher states, your palm of your hand and the sole of your feet do not get a tan, can this be a light clue?

Why do we build extra material inside of our bodies like crazy today? Is it a healing process that is going haywire? To heal is today to steal but what happens when you do not have anything to steal from because you have technology that robs you 24/7. With strange ideas on correction to our bodies we put that extra stress to points in our bodies that leak... Light.

I have taken some athletes this summer and changed their abilities just by making them think different, some at quite high levels and everywhere the science is so static, do this and that and you should see results, bang and crash they burn down. Also the rest of us is under that stress.

I feel that we need before everything else in helping others with just say, gait and posture, we need to understand what we call redox potential here. When you are constantly transfering energy badly, your brain will loose some of its most detailed systems, those who makes things vivid and sublime. Fixing a gait issue without adjusting this first only makes the object more stressed because she can not perform like we tell her to do. Get those basic three things in place and the above issue often just, do not exist anymore, why? Loose you brain and you go back to our most basic systems, survive. In that mode, your ability to walk and make others look is not important.

What i used to fix my problems was simply, tune up my potential. Learn again that the things i thought was impossible is possible, apply it to my movement and motion, forcing myself into places where i have been told that i have no chance at my age. It all comes down to each person to make the changes by themselves, we others can only plant a virus in them.

Everything is possible, just not predictable... This is where most people who works with people fail. We who learn here should accept that fact and change the world at our place where we are.

### René Borg — 2015-09-04T14:19:23+0300
