Hi prAna, thank you for the long and thought out reply. I definitely recognise the question of 'defensive mechanisms' going haywire. I remember an athlete from Mali once telling me he never ran in a 'highly reactive state' which meant in a state of stress where he assumed his body would overreact to further stressors. I also see again and again this 'overcompensation' especially on the foot - my favourite example the bunions and other bony deformities that are created when the big toe has been displaced or when too much tension is drawn across the ankle bone. No doubt a thicker Achilles is an ill-informed attempt to protect the body just as the bunion is a last ditch attempt to try and build 'a new big toe' where the real one has 'gone missing'.

There is no choice to reflect on your words and take my own experiments to the next level. I walk barefoot all the time, swim in the local river etc., but cannot be outside as much as I want nor do I do as much 'pure barefoot' running as I want because I live next to gritty tarmac and rocky trails - but this is something I will have to work around. Greater exposure to nature, more protective measures from and then also fix some fundamental problems I have (such as my contact lenses - cue Jack's recent post) and then continue the 'good movement'. I will see where this takes me - and if it works then it is my obligation to communicate it to future clients.

Another clue that we cannot be 'biomechanics' or 'athletics' coaches - merely 'coaches', focusing on one part of the picture is a sin of omission by default.

### Linz — 2015-09-04T16:21:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/collagen-i-vs-iii-and-what-to-do-about-it.16817/post-176470>

Rene, I see big parallels with the equine industry here. We have huge problems with tendon/ligament injuries that never regain strength properly alongside a vast increase in metabolic syndromes, joint inflammation, and many investigations showing as many as 90% of working horses with gut ulceration. We are also seeing horses dying from toxins in sycamore trees that have grown alongside their fields for many years without a problem.

Working with principles similar to Peter to boost cell redox potential I have seen many good changes in my own very low DHA herbivores (some my vets cannot understand) but I'm still treading water to stay afloat. Makes me think very hard about my own environment when keeping the animals outside in a rural place with little artificial light or food and in constant contact with the ground/only shoeing in a few cases when it can't be avoided, isn't enough here. Most work is done on natural surfaces. Providing RO water isn't a viable option - clean and cold with Cl but no Fl (and human approved levels of nitrate) is the best I can do. My biggest progress has been made by replacing all the conventional stretches and other 'biomechanics' advice with cranio-sacral treatment and very small controlled movement in place of the 'stretching'. I'm only an amateur but this very much involves down-regulation of stressed SNS - and I think real healing occurs with upreg (or perhaps just better balanced?) ANS.
My preferred artificial interventions that seem to have helped are low photon red and IR light pads/ice packs/hydro-therapy and turmeric and I would like to know more about lasers. I think you need to start by improving redox using good water, cold therapy and natural light alongside a clean DHA and cysteine- rich diet. These would all give the collagen a better chance to re-establish with proper movement.

### prAna303 — 2015-09-04T16:33:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/collagen-i-vs-iii-and-what-to-do-about-it.16817/post-176471>

Also, there is a reason why i always have my student walk for me.

Words can say somethings but if your redox is shot, the words are quite off. Walk around and you tell me many stories, even the darkest ones.

### René Borg — 2015-09-04T20:04:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/collagen-i-vs-iii-and-what-to-do-about-it.16817/post-176485>

Linz, those observations definitely point to the truth. Optimal biomechanics is obviously desirable and we can create a fairly objective measure of optimal if we define it as 'minimising unnecessary joint torque' and 'maximing the ability to harness and create force' (power). Originally, this led me to think that the sedentary development in our population, the sitting and the over-engineered footwear was the main driver of sports injuries and the decline of Western athletics since the early 1980ies.

When I first got in contact with Bruce Lipton's thesis on the primacy of the environment (rather than the primacy of the genome) it did begin to ring some silent alarm bells coupled with the fact the poor biomechanics does not account for all injuries - we see injuries in sedentary populations that should not be occurring at the minute amounts of physical exercise we see. and then there was the many who have great mechanics and are still injured or those with poor mechanics who are not. The first could be explained partly by cohort of runners who move well but are unconditioned. But then you read the descriptions of how the Kenyans live today, how the Antipodean athletes of the 1940ies to 1970ies lived and there's a pretty big red flag being waved that indicates what you see. Take the 'Flying Kiwis' of the 1960ies - minimal technology, hardworking outdoor culture, lots of barefoot walking and running, regular exposure to the elements, eating mainly local produce that was not yet industrialised agriculture, low stress levels/good work life balance and a stoic attitude to life.

Just reading the autobiographies suggest people with a much better balance between ANS/SNS before you consider anything else. They ran 100 mile weeks and were barely ever injured.

I am going to take down your points in the last line and see how best to implement it. Where have you found it easiest to obtain low photon red and IR light pads? In Ireland we have a cryochamber as well as a very famous surgeon who works with laser (Dr Patrick Leahy, one of the pioneers of key hole surgery) who seems to be doing interesting and effective work and accelerating the healing response of tissues manyfold.

### Linz — 2015-09-04T21:06:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/collagen-i-vs-iii-and-what-to-do-about-it.16817/post-176489>

My light pad came from equinelighttherapy.com for what seemed a very inflated price for a set of LED lights and some velcro. Tax and shipping to the UK didn't help. It's for animal treatment although I have used it on a few of my own injuries too. I couldn't find anything suitable here but I think there may be some German ones. Language made it difficult to research.

I have always disliked over-engineered footwear too and the synthetic over padded boots designed to keep riders' feet warm. At least we still have some leather-soled boots although they are becoming harder to find.

### Penny — 2015-09-04T21:26:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/collagen-i-vs-iii-and-what-to-do-about-it.16817/post-176491>

I "cured" my achilles tendonitis - can anyone say dehydration - with magnesium oil (mag chloride) spray twice a day like Jack told someone on a thread here eons ago - for me, it was like a miracle - I could finally walk without pain, braces, and shoes

It's important to use magnesium throughout the day for dehydration - I just stick the mag citrate powder in my ice water - with a mint sprig - and hydrate away...

I just buy it in bulk, I use so much -

Because, at the time, I was addressing this as a simple 'cause and effect' and not as a more complex picture (as I try to know) I don't think I got the results I needed but I would like to give it a try now that I have the epigenetic ducks in a row.

I definitely think EMF and bluelight exposed me to this at the same time as I ramped up barefoot running. The problem was that I was not aware of the connection until after reading 'Lights Out' prompted by a recommendation here and I was (and am) working two jobs - IT consultant during the day - and trying to run two business in the evenings. This for a long time meant being near an electronic device working over 12 hours per day. I work for a major player in the IT world and the EMF readings you get in their offices are through the roof! ### Penny — 2015-09-06T21:23:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/collagen-i-vs-iii-and-what-to-do-about-it.16817/post-176623>

I just spray it a few times on the back of my legs and rub so it gets all the way down to my heals - in the shower, I take a handful of epsom salts and rub them all over my body - even over my heart - I get no heart weirdness anymore either - I think I put around 1/2 tsp mag citrate into a glass - but the water is alkaline, iced, has apple cider vinegar and has a sprig of mint - it would be the total bomb to add potassium bicarbonate to this, but that would make it taste vomitous -

I also - when I remember - use something called Fibro Response because it has magnesium malate in it which I think makes me more energetic because I am guessing my aluminum load is probably insane... but mag malate pills are so huge, I damn near choke on them, so that's why I go with the Fibro Response - but the epsom salts make my skin quite smooth and soft - truly a no brainer IMHO

The other thing I've been doing - as I am 100% sure at this point calcium is effluxing like crazy, is spray epsom salts all over my body when I go sunbathing in the morning - I hiked for 5 hours at Yosemite this summer in the middle of the day with nothing but epsom salt spray and never even got sunburned -

# Thread: Need some help/advice on resetting sleep
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-help-advice-on-resetting-sleep.16824/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SpiritGuide — 2015-09-05T06:47:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-help-advice-on-resetting-sleep.16824/post-176526>

Hi,

My wife and I did 23 and me a few months ago and started following Dr. Kruse shortly after that.

It winds up my wife's "clock genes" are all wired for insomnia...

Are there any strategies to help overcome some of these natural night owl tendencies?

She's been a night owl since she was a baby so she's having problems converting to sleeping at a normal time ... 2:00am would early for her to get to bed.

We typically stay away from pharmaceutical drugs so any alternatives would be great if you have any recommendations in addition to adding seafood and cold thermogenesis for us... Thank you for your help.

### Lahelada — 2015-09-05T12:42:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-help-advice-on-resetting-sleep.16824/post-176535>

I was never a night owl but could not stay asleep. Dr K does not recommend it but I took melatonin for a week. It helped me to rebalance then. I am not one to take anything in pill form lightly so there was no danger for me to want to continue it for my convenience sake psychologically speaking. I wanted the Leptin reset to provoke healing rather than relying on a chemical aid forever and it worked

### nonchalant — 2015-09-05T16:50:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-help-advice-on-resetting-sleep.16824/post-176542>

Hi Spirit. Does she get sunshine in the morning and darkness at night?

### nicld — 2015-09-06T21:20:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-help-advice-on-resetting-sleep.16824/post-176621>

Blue blocker glasses as soon as it get dark and keep the lights low and sun right away in the morning. That has worked for me after years of crappy sleep.

### Penny — 2015-09-06T21:30:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-help-advice-on-resetting-sleep.16824/post-176624>

Her zip code is probably wired for insomnia as well...

### cantweight — 2015-09-07T06:51:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-help-advice-on-resetting-sleep.16824/post-176652>

Make her start catching the sunrise, expose eyes and skin. So important to yoke circadian rhythm. Once the sun goes down block artificial light from eyes and skin. Develop a nighttime routine, relax/unwind. No food after dark.

It shouldnt take long for gears to switch if adhered to.

### SpiritGuide — 2015-09-10T20:06:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-help-advice-on-resetting-sleep.16824/post-176936>

Hi all, thanks for the feedback... yes she has blue blocker glasses which she uses in conjunction with the f.lux app on the computer...

she doesn't really get morning light since she's not waking up very early right now....

She's taking melatonin currently just trying to jump-start a reset in her sleep, it's been working (to an extent) recently as she's been getting to bed before 2am which is an improvement over the typical 4am bedtime.

Any other advice would be fantastic... thanks!

### JanSz — 2015-09-11T01:59:25+0300

she doesn't really get morning light since she's not waking up very early right now....

She's taking melatonin currently just trying to jump-start a reset in her sleep, it's been working (to an extent) recently as she's been getting to bed before 2am which is an improvement over the typical 4am bedtime.

Any other advice would be fantastic... thanks!\[/quote\]

**Dx:** Brain tumor (Glioma)
**Surgeries:** Brain tumor removal surgery in 2011

\------------------------------------------------------------
Lets start with adjusting waking up time first.
Going to bed comes second.

Today
sunrise in Charlotte NC --- 07:02 AM====>>>**wakeup time**
sunset 19: 38

Assume 8 hrs sleeping time, bed time =11PM
dinner finished at least 4hrs before bed time =7PM

7:40PM shut off all electronics, tv, ect

to see around use red light and black light (available at amazon or Home Depot).
candles, fire, .
\============================================
Optimize your Steroid Hormone Panel.
Hell or high water.
at least:

cortisol (levels and shape)
DHEAs
progesterone

Look what Jack is saying

[https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-7/](https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-7/)
These mechanisms are mediated by the hormone DHEA which is the major antioxident in the brain and correlates directly with effective sleep by lowering IL-6 levels. Progesterone is another critical hormone for brain homeostasis and learning as well. Sleep is vital to mammals, but it is supremely vital to humans, because they have shrunk the benefits of hibernation into 2 short critical hours of their sleep cycle because of the massive growth of their brains extinguished the need to sleep through the winter months.

\============================================
There is a problem with blue blockers (I think).
There is a problem with the name blue blockers.
The glasses that we are usually using are designed to block UV light.
My "blue blockers" have UV400 label on them.
By design they are meant to block UV light of 400nm down.
Any blocking of light frequency of more than 400nm is incidental.
Blocking UV400nm and down is hurtful and should be avoided.
Any glass or plastic stops UV light.
That includes lenses implanted during cataract surgery.

All above is new and just came from Jack with UBIQUITINATION #24
[https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-24-are-myopia-light-and-aging-linked/](https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-24-are-myopia-light-and-aging-linked/)

but we also benefit from them in all other times,
see the 20 year long experience of the restaurant crew in Chicago **Sherman Hotel Well of the Sea.**

Dr Kruse in UB#24 posted picture of his daugter's bedroom with (black light)=(UV-A)+(UV-B) in it.

# Thread: 'Port wine stain' (nevus flammeus) and epigenetic questions...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/port-wine-stain-nevus-flammeus-and-epigenetic-questions.16828/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### René Borg — 2015-09-06T22:33:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/port-wine-stain-nevus-flammeus-and-epigenetic-questions.16828/post-176632>

My 14-week old was born with a port-wine stain birthmark on the side of his left leg. He's a healthy and contented toddler and it gives him no issues but I am still curious as to whether it can be removed/reversed and what him having this tells me about his epigenetic expression and future.

current research seems to suggest that the port wine stain is caused by a mutation in the GNAQ gene expression in the later stages of foetal development (when this mutation happens earlier it is linked to the much more serious Sturge-Weber syndrome).

The gene is responsible for many things including making a protein important for cellular signalling and skin pigmentation so my layman's understanding is that this switch basically causes some of the capillaries in the skin to develop differently.

Now what I wonder is manifold: 1) can it be reversed through the 'optimal protocols' and are any in particular important for reversing this, 2) would laser therapy be the obvious choice later on to remove it and 3) is there anything in particular that we know triggers these changes in GNAQ gene or is it the random quantized world at action - i.e. this was just how that gene got knocked around by modern environmental signals and 4) does having this mutation link to any benefits or weaknesses later in life?

### nonchalant — 2015-09-07T00:49:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/port-wine-stain-nevus-flammeus-and-epigenetic-questions.16828/post-176638>

> [René Borg said:](/goto/post?id=176632)
>
> My 14-week old was born with a port-wine stain birthmark on the side of his left leg. He's a healthy and contented toddler and it gives him no issues but I am still curious as to whether it can be removed/reversed and what him having this tells me about his epigenetic expression and future.
>
> current research seems to suggest that the port wine stain is caused by a mutation in the GNAQ gene expression in the later stages of foetal development (when this mutation happens earlier it is linked to the much more serious Sturge-Weber syndrome).
>
> The gene is responsible for many things including making a protein important for cellular signalling and skin pigmentation so my layman's understanding is that this switch basically causes some of the capillaries in the skin to develop differently.
>
> Now what I wonder is manifold: 1) can it be reversed through the 'optimal protocols' and are any in particular important for reversing this, 2) would laser therapy be the obvious choice later on to remove it and 3) is there anything in particular that we know triggers these changes in GNAQ gene or is it the random quantized world at action - i.e. this was just how that gene got knocked around by modern environmental signals and 4) does having this mutation link to any benefits or weaknesses later in life?

I am also interested in this, Rene'. A relative had several (scary for a child) laser sessions, which only reduced the strength of the stain, theoretically. I suppose it might be less vivid.

### brent-next — 2017-09-03T02:28:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/port-wine-stain-nevus-flammeus-and-epigenetic-questions.16828/post-222776>

He might grow up to be president of his country.

[https://www.google.com/search?q=mik...v04fWAhVM7yYKHS1-D1IQ\_AUICigB&biw=832&bih=451](https://www.google.com/search?q=mikhail+gorbachev+port+wine&source=lnms&tbm=isch&sa=X&ved=0ahUKEwjV3ruv04fWAhVM7yYKHS1-D1IQ_AUICigB&biw=832&bih=451)

### René Borg — 2017-09-03T12:19:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/port-wine-stain-nevus-flammeus-and-epigenetic-questions.16828/post-222785>

> [brent-next said:](/goto/post?id=222776)
>
> He might grow up to be president of his country.
>
> [https://www.google.com/search?q=mikhail gorbachev port wine&source=lnms&tbm=isch&sa=X&ved=0ahUKEwjV3ruv04fWAhVM7yYKHS1-D1IQ\_AUICigB&biw=832&bih=451](https://www.google.com/search?q=mikhail+gorbachev+port+wine&source=lnms&tbm=isch&sa=X&ved=0ahUKEwjV3ruv04fWAhVM7yYKHS1-D1IQ_AUICigB&biw=832&bih=451)

Looks like that is now out of the question - the port wine stain simply faded. He is today 27 months old and there is barely any of it left despite it being quite substantial. We haven't done anything very active to remove it but he does live a healthy life in our mountain home and get's outside a lot (obviously without sunscreen), so perhaps we just get lucky or perhaps running around barefoot outside was the way to go

### brent-next — 2017-09-04T21:48:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/port-wine-stain-nevus-flammeus-and-epigenetic-questions.16828/post-222871>

Great!

Approximately where in the world is your mountain home and what altitude are you at?

### René Borg — 2017-09-05T20:08:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/port-wine-stain-nevus-flammeus-and-epigenetic-questions.16828/post-222922>

It's not particularly epigenetically ideal where we live really apart from being rural and with no immediate neighbours, own waterwell and fresh air. We are in Ireland on the 53 degrees N line and our mountains are really low hills - the house lies on 350 metres with the surrounding national park going no higher than 928 metres. We do get a lot of sunshine when it's available but it's only truly good between April and September.

# Thread: The Changing Nature of Ice Cubes: EZ demonstration?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-changing-nature-of-ice-cubes-ez-demonstration.16832/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2015-09-08T00:39:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-changing-nature-of-ice-cubes-ez-demonstration.16832/post-176695>

Now that I'm using ice-cubes 24/7, a scientific property has occurred to me that I haven't realized my entire life until just yesterday. The nature question has to do with this method:

1\. Half-fill ice cubes in a plastic tray
2\. Freeze fully for 3 hours
3\. Top off the ice-cube tray with water
4\. Freeze fully again for another 3 hours
5\. Attempt to twist the ice cube tray to break the ice free

Expected Results: The ice cubes break free with normal pressure/force as if the ice-cubes had been filled fully the first time.

Actual Results: The ice cubes require extra pressure/force to break free when they are first half-filled and then topped off with water again.

So my question would be: Why do ice cubes in trays, when half-filled and frozen, then fully-filled later, seem to cling stronger to the sides of the ice-cube tray?

Possible explanation? Is this result have to do with EZ lattice formation?

Another question comes to mind about biohacking water... What about 1/10th filling the tray in 10-stages... would refreezing somehow increase the EZ formation because of surface-area lattice formations? Get me thinking about glaciers in nature if surface areas increase the EZ formation.

# Thread: Hack your cordless phone!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-your-cordless-phone.16834/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2015-09-08T06:58:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-your-cordless-phone.16834/post-176717>

Since my wife INSISTS on having a cordless phone in the house I had to start brainstorming.

I bought an old 900Mhz cordless phone from Value Village for $3.99 since they do not transmit between calls and the handsets are approx. 1/20th less powerful during calls (I used my meter to compare). I wanted to use the current answering machine since it has some good features but stop it from transmitting.
I opened it up and removed the two little antennas with a soldering iron. (Just snipping them off at their base may work too.)
The reading went from 8 mW/meter squared down to 0.00uW/meter squared (at approx 5 feet away.)
Perfect! (The meter was a TES 593. A good meter according to Mike Neuart )
Just for reference, Dr Klinghardt wants his patients sleep location to measure no more than 5uW/meter squared.

But what really surprised me is that ....THE PHONE STILL WORKED! Even in the backyard!

Of course it's not perfect..there still is some radiation at close range (within 4 ft) and the 900Mhz phone is still better EMF wise. I would still use the speaker phone mode on the DECT phone handset.

So there's an idea for someone who lives with family members that just won't give up that thing!
Hope this helps someone..

### nicld — 2015-09-08T21:13:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-your-cordless-phone.16834/post-176781>

I just put mine on a timer to be off at night. Told the DH that it was to conserve energy. He said what if we get a call in the middle of the night. We still have a corded phone in the kitchen plus we cannot hear the phone in the bedroom.

### Penny — 2015-09-08T22:20:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-your-cordless-phone.16834/post-176792>

That was brilliant What about one of those headsets with the air tubes for whoever is still hell bent on using that type of phone?

I did something really stupid - I got what I thought was a corded phone and the EMF reading on the handset was quite hideous - but at least it doesn't act like a cell phone tower in your house -

What is the model number of your 900 mhz phone?

We got a trendnet router at Fry's for $24.00 over the weekend - totally wired and it works great -

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-09-09T02:07:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-your-cordless-phone.16834/post-176817>

> [thisbirdhaswings said:](/goto/post?id=176785)
>
> Just going through this, too. In my case, telling my neighbors I shut the phone off after sunset so if there's an emergency and I can't be reached by phone, come pound on my door.
>
> Of course, I keep a plain old fashioned phone plugged into a phone line but I don't give that number out.
>
> At the end of the day, we don't really \*need\* a phone 99.99% of the time (at night).
>
> I'm curious...I stopped pluging in/out my cordless because I started thinking when it's not plugged in but charged, it might be sending out a searching signal. Does it?

There was very little coming from the base unit/handset when I measured. At 5 ft. it was not measurable (0). When the unit is not plugged in there is nothing transmitting.
The thing is that if you want complete protection you really cannot use a cordless phone because even though the reading is zero, there still has to be something coming from it for me to be able to use the handset in the backyard.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-09-09T02:11:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-your-cordless-phone.16834/post-176819>

> [Penny said:](/goto/post?id=176792)

> That was brilliant What about one of those headsets with the air tubes for whoever is still hell bent on using that type of phone?

> I did something really stupid - I got what I thought was a corded phone and the EMF reading on the handset was quite hideous - but at least it doesn't act like a cell phone tower in your house -
>
> What is the model number of your 900 mhz phone?
>
> We got a trendnet router at Fry's for $24.00 over the weekend - totally wired and it works great -

Sony cordless telephone..model no. SPP-D900. The air tubes are a great idea too,especially if someone is on the phone for a long time. But the 24/7 pulsing I think is the big one to watch out for because it is constant.

There are two places i traveled where my acne/rosacea and digestive issues completely vanished even thoug i ate like crap. One of this places was extremely low in nnEMF. No cellpohne signal, no wifi, weak radio signal... But the other place wasnt so low in EMF, so ¿why did i have such an improvement? (there was wi-fi, some cell signal, and radio signal as well.) Well the thing is that this place was a village in a kind of a valley between two mountains FULL of trees... My question is. Even thoug there where nnEMF there, could it be that the soil (paramagnetic materials in the mountains maybe) and the trees provided a stable magnetic field, i dont know much about the topic, but could it be that so much nature was countering those nnEMF?

Thanks

# Thread: Converting nnEMF to IR
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/converting-nnemf-to-ir.16843/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### digital — 2015-09-09T23:52:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/converting-nnemf-to-ir.16843/post-176882>

[https://www.google.com/patents/US20030162007](https://www.google.com/patents/US20030162007)
**Energy converting article and method of making**
US 20030162007 A1
ABSTRACT

A graded composite energy converting article having a working surface of **carbon-bonded carbon-fiber** (CBCF) material that **can absorb light, RF, microwaves, or other forms of energy and convert this energy to sensible heat**. Conversely, CBCF can take sensible heat and convert it to radiative emissions of IR energy. Energy is conducted through the article by carbon foam to a secondary surface. The graded composite is made by first heating pitch under pressure to create the foam, which is then pyrolyzed and graphitized to create graphitic, open-cell foam. A slurry of chopped carbon fibers and organic binder in water or solvent is prepared and deposited on a working surface of the foam, preferably infiltrating the foam to some depth. The green body is dried, cured, and carbonized, creating a graded body of carbon foam in conductive thermal contact with a working surface of CBCF. Secondary surfaces of the foam may optionally be metal-infiltrated for added strength, brazability, or other purposes. The article may be used for optical absorbers, baffles, beam stops, RF and microwave loads, radiators, electron collectors, and other uses where energy is passively converted from one form to another and superior thermal transport is required.

\[0001\] **The United States Government has rights in this invention** pursuant to contract no. DE-AC05-00OR22725 between the United States Department of Energy and UT-Battelle, LLC.

At home, I've found this **weird pulsing sound** coming out of my Cornet's sound-RF filter. I want everyone to **watch my vids below and speculate**.

Is this a neighbor's WiFI, or something else than the Smart Meter on the side of my house? Has anyone heard this type of pulsing before? I'm about 75% thinking it's Smart Meter microwave transmissions, but it's hard to know for sure; it seems like the most likely candidate. I recorded 3 videos about RF I'm getting in my son's room.

Part2:

Part3:

Thanks all!

### av8r — 2015-09-11T13:19:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rf-emf-in-bedrooms.16848/post-176990>

Cover the bedroom wall with lengths of aluminum foil that the dumb meter is on. Create a z-bend between the runs of foil so shinny ends are in contact. Then for aesthetics cover over the foil with a poster collage using a theme your son is into.

### Scompy — 2015-09-11T17:34:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rf-emf-in-bedrooms.16848/post-177002>

Yeah, I have that option on the table. I did a test with huge piece of mylar too and the pulsing diminished almost to nothing. In theory though, my electric company is going to allow me to remove it and replace with an analog smart meter. But then I'm also thinking, it's best anyway to block off the walls from any RF regardless of the source.

# Thread: Benefitting Children: Collecting Data on Schools
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/benefitting-children-collecting-data-on-schools.16853/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2015-09-13T18:56:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/benefitting-children-collecting-data-on-schools.16853/post-177066>

I was thinking about meta-data on schools asking the questions:

1\. Do children at your school get early morning light: daily Recess?
2\. Do you have fluorescent lights in your classrooms?
3\. Do you use WiFi or other RF-EMF in your schools?

Map these three elements against the Absentee-Rate of the children in the school for an overall idea of sickness in the school. Also we could look at their grades too.

I'm wondering if getting data back from schools is possible or collectible over the internet right now.

Anyway, I'd love to get data that shows what happens to children's health and performance when you get them out into the sunlight in the morning while eliminating fluorescence (and LED) and RF.

The big question to ask is: Why can't I find a study that at least shows the difference between incandescent in one school and fluorescent lights in another school and then compare the health of the children in both schools over time? This issue reminds me of adding flouride to the water supply. Someone about 60+ years ago thought it was a good idea with very little scientific study and then it becomes status-quo. Someone thought it was a good idea to add fluorescent lights to classrooms, and then no one notices. These are all issues of 'seeing is believing.' You can't see the added flouride in your water and you can't see the interaction of light across the skin and retina, so everyone continues to go on blissfully naive. RF is another one too... unless someone has a meter to measure it.

### Scompy — 2015-09-16T22:06:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/benefitting-children-collecting-data-on-schools.16853/post-177241>

Had a great conversation at my son's school today with the school's Principal. A half-hour later talking about RF-EMF and biological intersections, she undestands enough now that she's pushing the issue forward. For her own benefit, I also showed her how her 'wired' setup on her laptop was still giving off 2 milliWatts/Sq.m because her WiFi wasn't turned off on the laptop. She was so pleased to get the information that now I'm going to be meeting with their IT-engineer soon. Fingers-crossed. One school at a time, right?

### Scompy — 2015-09-20T20:41:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/benefitting-children-collecting-data-on-schools.16853/post-177458>

The Principal talked to me again saying that she's talking with the IT person about ways to reduce the milliWatts/Sq.m of the RF at my children's school. That's a great start to say the least that they want to see about taking action. However, they need an education. Their first tactic was to move the WiFi to other locations higher up. I've already told them that moving of the WiFi may not eliminate how RF reflects off of surfaces, and actually could make the problem worse in certain locations... that meters are needed. I offered the school to have them borrow my meter so they could get better measurements overall. It will be interesting to see how this pans out in the next few weeks. At least I've got them aware, but I may need to go in and help EMF-hack their school for them.

# Thread: Cravings - Glycogen Stores Used Up?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cravings-glycogen-stores-used-up.16859/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2015-09-14T19:04:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cravings-glycogen-stores-used-up.16859/post-177103>

Please tell me if this makes sense.

I realize there can be different reasons for having cravings, but I'm wondering if there might be one primary reason - glucose. Let me use this analogy I found on [wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glycogen):

> **Glycogen depletion and endurance exercise**
> Long-distance athletes, such as [marathon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marathon_%28sport%29) runners, [cross-country skiers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cross-country_skiing), and [cyclists](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cyclist), often experience glycogen depletion, where almost all of the athlete's glycogen stores are depleted after long periods of exertion without enough energy consumption. This phenomenon is referred to as "[hitting the wall](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hitting_the_wall)".
>
> Glycogen depletion can be forestalled in three possible ways. **First**, during exercise, carbohydrates with the highest possible rate of conversion to blood glucose (high [glycemic index](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glycemic_index)) are ingested continuously. The best possible outcome of this strategy replaces about 35% of glucose consumed at heart rates above about 80% of maximum. **Second**, through endurance training adaptations and specialized regimens (e.g. fasted low-intensity endurance training), the body can condition type I muscle fibers to improve both fuel use efficiency and workload capacity to **increase the percentage of fatty acids used as fuel,[\[12\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glycogen#cite_note-12)[\[13\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glycogen#cite_note-13)\[_[citation needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed)_\] sparing carbohydrate use from all sources. Third,** by consuming large quantities of carbohydrates after depleting glycogen stores as a result of exercise or diet, the body can increase storage capacity of intramuscular glycogen stores.[\[14\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glycogen#cite_note-14)[\[15\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glycogen#cite_note-15)[\[16\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glycogen#cite_note-16) This process is known as [carbohydrate loading](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Carbohydrate_loading). In general, glycemic index of carbohydrate source does not matter since muscular insulin sensitivity is increased as a result of temporary glycogen depletion.[\[17\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glycogen#cite_note-17)[\[18\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glycogen#cite_note-18)
>
> When experiencing glycogen debt, athletes often experience extreme [fatigue](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fatigue_%28physical%29) to the point that it is difficult to move. As a reference, the very best professional cyclists in the world will usually finish a 4- to 5-hr stage race right at the limit of glycogen depletion using the first three strategies.
>
> When athletes ingest both carbohydrate and [caffeine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caffeine) following exhaustive exercise, their glycogen is replenished more rapidly.[\[19\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glycogen#cite_note-19)[\[20\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glycogen#cite_note-20)\[_[unreliable medical source?](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Identifying_reliable_sources_%28medicine%29)_\][\[21\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glycogen#cite_note-21)

So is that what happens when we get cravings - we "hit the wall"? I'm thinking that if we are on a low carb diet, but not in ketosis, we don't have quite enough glucose to handle all our body's needs. Eventually the glycogen stores in muscles and liver get used up and our body tells us it needs glucose - NOW. So we get cravings and fill up on ice cream, cookies, chocolate, potato chips, etc. We're good for a couple of weeks until the same thing happens again.

Comparing to the endurance athlete from above, this is similar to third - consuming large quantities of carbs after glycogen stores are depleted. Some people avoid cravings by increasing carbs and never getting into ketosis (First). But it looks to me that getting into ketosis (Second) is the option we want and it should eliminate cravings.

Is this right? All these years all I had to do was decrease my protein and increase my fat and I wouldn't have cravings? If it is that simple, I could have reached goal weight 30 years ago!

### Penny — 2015-09-14T21:33:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cravings-glycogen-stores-used-up.16859/post-177114>

you might look into the wonders of glycine... don't remember a hell of a lot about that particular rabbit hole, but I think it helps with gluconeogenesis - like when you wake up in the middle of the night because you're out of sugar - it kicks in - I could be remembering this totally wrong though - sometimes I take it before bed (1/4 tsp) and it helps with sleep - have 50 grams of carbs divided into morning and lunch - get it in the form of greens/cabbage/broccoli - it also spikes superoxide -

### Huck — 2015-09-15T22:42:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cravings-glycogen-stores-used-up.16859/post-177208>

This post should be labeled **Fat Guy on Lawnmower!**

Thinking is my favorite hobby, but for most of the last 20 years, I couldn't seem to keep a thought in my head long enough to do much thinking. For the last 2 1/2 weeks I have been eating lots of fat and the brain is starting to fire. I spent about 3 hours on my lawn tractor cutting the grass, in the sun wearing just a pair of shorts and actually did some thinking. Probably faulty, as I'm out of practice.

Back to the glucose question. Assuming a climate that experiences a cold winter, would it make sense for ancestral man to crave carbs in the middle of winter? No, because there would be very limited carbs available. Would it make sense for him to crave carbs in the summer or fall? And when I say crave, I'm talking about those that make you get up and drive to the store at 10 pm for some ice cream. So would ancestral man get cravings in summer and fall and why?

I can think of 2 possible reasons:

1.  He's not eating enough carbs and his body is warning him that there is not enough glucose for his organs and his muscles. Eat some cabs now.
2.  He's not storing enough fat for the winter. Eat some carbs now.

From this, I made some assumptions:

-   Ancestral man ate a ketogenic diet with limited carbs part of the year (cold/dark) and ate a higher carb diet (warm/light) the rest of the year.
-   When on a ketogenic diet, there was no reason for the body to crave carbs, because carbs would throw him out of ketosis - he was using ketones for fuel and burning body fat

-   When on a higher carb diet, it made sense to crave carbs, because his body required high levels of glucose to both function and store fat.

So I believe the answer is, if one is in ketosis, one will not crave carbs.

### Penny — 2015-09-15T23:57:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cravings-glycogen-stores-used-up.16859/post-177210>

One needs to add the cold to emulate evolution - plus, that lawn mower of your is sending much EMF to your body - just like a motorcycle would... so, embrace the cold and skip the lawn mower

# Thread: How to measure EMFs on piece of land

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lady Eve — 2015-09-17T03:53:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-measure-emfs-on-piece-of-land.16872/post-177259>

Hello everyone,

My husband and I are considering buying a lot in Santa Rosa Beach, FL. It's along the Gulf between Destin and Panama City Beach in Florida. I've checked for cell towers and there are only 10 in a 3 mile area. This is much improved from our 66 in the city! The closest one is about 1.3 miles away and the others are +2 miles away. There is a small antenna (about 35 feet high) that is 1/4 mile away.

I took my Trimeter and checked the property and the levels were 1-2. I'm thinking this is good, no? Is there anything else you'd recommend me checking before we purchase the land. Would love your thoughts.

The property is 1/2 mile from the beach so plan to swim in the Gulf often. Feeling very blessed indeed!

### Coreena — 2016-02-17T00:13:17+0200

# Thread: Posting photos online
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/posting-photos-online.16897/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-09-23T20:45:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/posting-photos-online.16897/post-177687>

I wonder does anyone here ever do that. I guess a few people have done that on this forum, and probably elsewhere as well.

The problem I see is the EXIF data. I'm not sure that everyone knows about that. It's a metadata (metadata = data about data) of a kind. Some devices insert it into image files. So a camera might do that, so might a internet-enabled phone with photographic capabilities (and far more likely), and so will some programs.

I see it as breach of privacy. Some forum software is sophisticated enough to scrub the EXIF data out of image files on upload. But most isn't.

What can it include? Quite a lot actually. Here is a list (not by any means exhaustive, and not meant to be): Information about the device the photo was taken on, information about the settings used (who cares?), a timestamp (getting more uncomfortable), GPS coordinates (i.e., latitude and longitude … more uncomfortable yet for those who value their privacy).

My advice: if you're uploading a photo you've taken anywhere, check what EXIF data is in the image file. If there's anything significant there, scrub that out before you upload it.

Here's an example of mine. On Mac OS X you can view this data simply by opening an image file in Preview.app and then using the keypress Command + I. (On Windows, IIRC, you'd want to right-click on the file and choose "Properties" and then go from there.)

Anyway, have a look. You can see where I took this, can't you? Not always a good idea ...

# Thread: Questions re: Mitigating wired PLC technology (TWACS)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-re-mitigating-wired-plc-technology-twacs.16902/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Psyche — 2015-09-25T17:55:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-re-mitigating-wired-plc-technology-twacs.16902/post-177868>

I recently learned my smart meter is a "pulsing" TWACS meter. The TWACS smart meters communicate with the utility by adding low frequency signals to the electrical lines. My utility company promotes such power line carrier (PLC) systems as a positive alternative to wireless smart meters.

The TWACS system creates powerful dirty electricity which is a constant presence, possibly 24/7.

-   **The dirty electricity turns all wires throughout the house into antennas.**
-   **Switching to an analog meter will not help much**
-   **The signals cannot be blocked or filtered (in the US)**

The issue with TWACS is referred to as "the antenna effect" which the utility company denies. The electrical wires inside a house and along the street were not designed to carry communication signals. When you put communication signals on them, it creates transients in the lines, effectively making the wires antenna. If wires on the poles are separated from each other, it enhances the antenna effect. The miles of electrical wires in a neighborhood can act as a very large antenna. It transforms all the wires in your home into antennas as well, so it no longer matters where you are in the house, you cannot reduce your exposure. Evil.

Here is an article describing all of this:
[http://www.eiwellspring.org/smartmeter/twacs.pdf](http://www.eiwellspring.org/smartmeter/twacs.pdf)

**LOTS OF QUESTIONS:** _Hopefully, someone out there has some experience they can share!_

1.  Has anyone out there who has this type of meter been able to measure the dirty electricity and mitigate it successfully? If so, what are the things you did?
2.  What EMF meter should I rent? I have heard the best is an oscilliscope with a filter to remove the 60Hz
3.  Does shutting off the breaker to the house at night mitigate things much?
4.  What is required to disconnect the house entirely at night?

### Scompy — 2015-09-25T23:57:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-re-mitigating-wired-plc-technology-twacs.16902/post-177891>

Wow, this is getting insideous now. That's gonna be the problem with this issue you brought up because it doesn't appear we have the correct types of meters to measure it at this time. Shutting off the breaker seems like a great idea. I'm wondering if using sound-EMFs could help determine intensity by the volume of the sound produced.

They do make products to reduce RF-EMF that you can plug into a wall here, but this sounds more like an electrical EMF issue rather than an RF one ([http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00JM8POXE?psc=1&redirect=true&ref\_=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o03\_s00](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00JM8POXE?psc=1&redirect=true&ref_=oh_aui_detailpage_o03_s00))

# Thread: Any tips for a Faraday cage?

**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Flawed — 2015-09-27T12:49:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-tips-for-a-faraday-cage.16910/post-177966>

I want to make my sleeping environment a haven for autophagy to take place like Jack suggests we do.

As of now my sleeping environment is simply terrible (a story for another post). I'm weighing up my options for a remedy and i'm thinking about a Faraday cage.

Anything i should bare in mind while devising one?

Note: i cant move house for another year

### Martin — 2015-09-27T16:45:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-tips-for-a-faraday-cage.16910/post-177976>

Unless it's built to _exacting_ standards with NO way to allow ANY nEMF signals to enter, don't expand the time, money or energy trying. If a signal gets in, it will bounce around the walls... and through you. No bueno. Default question is always: Would a cave man build \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ ? (fill in blank)

### Scompy — 2015-09-27T22:02:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-tips-for-a-faraday-cage.16910/post-177985>

However, if you can pinpoint a huge soure of RF-EMF outside the house, some of it can be mitigated/reduced down. For instance, I have a nearby house with WiFi that I can pinpoint, so blocking an inside wall of my house with silver-threaded curtains appears to back-reflect most of it in the sleeping area. Some people also use silver-threaded mosquito-like canopies over the bed while sleeping too like this image: [http://www.bedbathandbeyond.com/1/1/36142-siam-bed-canopy-mosquito-net-ivory.html](http://www.bedbathandbeyond.com/1/1/36142-siam-bed-canopy-mosquito-net-ivory.html)

### Penny — 2015-09-28T04:47:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-tips-for-a-faraday-cage.16910/post-178005>

Measure twice, cut once...

Actually, I heard a cheap test is to get one of those space blankets and sleep with it one night and see if you improve -

What should i look for in a tent? Can the Schumann resonance reach me through the tents ground layer?

### Andrea — 2015-09-27T14:19:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-be-sleeping-in-a-tent.16911/post-177971>

A normal tent doesn't stop a magnetic field. But you might not want to sleep in something made of synthetic, oil based materials...

I prefer to sleep under the stars, lying just on a reindeer skin. The downside is that when I wake up in the middle of the night and open my eyes, the view is so stunningly beautiful that it's difficult to fall asleep again.
Having said that, I do sleep in a crappy tent when it's too wet outdoors. A completely dark place, far away from electricity and cell towers is far better than my home - even in a tent.

# Thread: NF kappa beta and the Sun
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nf-kappa-beta-and-the-sun.16912/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Melanie Procter — 2015-09-28T06:15:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nf-kappa-beta-and-the-sun.16912/post-178011>

Hi,

I was designing some juice and soup recipes today. I own a health retreat in Thailand and thus am the head chef. I was looking at Indian herbs and spices to put into soups. I came across this blog when I looked up healing spices.

[http://www.anti-agingfirewalls.com/2009/06/14/spices-of-life/](http://www.anti-agingfirewalls.com/2009/06/14/spices-of-life/)

After looking at their reference to NF Kappa Beta, I thought I would search our blog here to see what Jack has said about it in respect to the latest blogs. I came up with this interesting tidbit that should stimulate some discussion. Can you name this blog?

"Most influenza outbreaks disproportionately kill juvenile, elderly or already weakened patients; in contrast the 1918 pandemic killed predominantly previously healthy young adults. Modern research, using virus’ taken from the bodies of frozen victims, has concluded that the virus kills through a cytokine storm (overreaction of the body’s immune system). The strong immune reactions of young adults ravaged the body, whereas the weaker immune systems of children and middle-aged adults resulted in fewer deaths among those groups. These cytokine storms are mediated via NF kappa beta and IL-6 levels in the brain and body. You learned about them in my [early leptin blogs](https://www.jackkruse.com/hormone-cascade-101/). During the solar cycle 14 of 1918, there was a tremendous change in the Sun’s spots activity that dramatically altered the the Sun’s magnetic field, the solar wind, and the Earth’s magnetic field simultaneously. What was found when 1918 epidemiologic data was married with data NASA scientists found about the event later on, was that as the sun power decreased because less activity was seen those in **the coolest climate sustained the lowest death rates from influenza**. Those who lived in hotter climates died at much higher rate from the cytokine storm. This strongly pointed to a direct effect of the sun’s magnetic field in the pandemic. Physicists found the Earth had a weakened magnetic field in the solar maximum 14 as well. The pandemic of 1918 showed the direct alterations on human beings’ immune function worldwide. Why is this important for this blog? The exact same set of circumstance is now happening on the sun since 2009. In 2013, we should be at a solar maximum but the sun’s activity has been recently troubling NASA since March of 2013."

Mel

### Melanie Procter — 2015-09-28T06:28:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nf-kappa-beta-and-the-sun.16912/post-178012>

Actually, I feel the latest helps me to understand NF kappa beta more than anything else. Do you see the connection? [https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-25-uv-light-and-poop-plants/](https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-25-uv-light-and-poop-plants/)

# Thread: Sleep reversed / Farady cage bed
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-reversed-farady-cage-bed.16919/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thomas — 2015-09-30T17:35:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-reversed-farady-cage-bed.16919/post-178278>

I've experimented with how I lie in beds.
Most people sleep with their bed touching a wall.
In that wall there's usually some version of low frequency nnemf.
My advice, is put your pillow on the other side of the bed. (opposite to the wall)
Sleep with your feet pointing at the wall.
Simple advice, but works as I constistently sleep better when I do that and there's electricity running..

\----

So somehow I've got the idea a building a farady cage around my bed. It's one of the simplest hacks to do.
I can't believe it's not something that's discussed much.
Costs less than 100$ and few hours of time, and pretty much destroys rf-emf.
What are the pros and cons, yay or nay?

### thomas — 2015-09-30T17:40:13+0300

# Thread: How to intelligently increase my times in cold baths/ how long should I be working up to?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-intelligently-increase-my-times-in-cold-baths-how-long-should-i-be-working-up-to.16934/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### E_than — 2015-10-03T18:37:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-intelligently-increase-my-times-in-cold-baths-how-long-should-i-be-working-up-to.16934/post-178538>

All,

I have been doing cold baths with temps ranging from 50-55 degrees. I started at 5 minutes and am up to 15 minutes. I always eat a huge fatty meal beforehand and do them when my wife is home so she can check on me.

Naturally I would like to avoid hypothermia/injury. I am curious about how others progressed their times. How long should I work up to?

Thanks in advance for any insights others may share.

Ethan

### caroline — 2015-10-06T02:46:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-intelligently-increase-my-times-in-cold-baths-how-long-should-i-be-working-up-to.16934/post-178689>

Hi Ethan ....

There is a huge C/T thread here somewhere - and please re read the C/T protocol again.

Stay in as long as you can - monitor how you are feeling - use your intuition.

Sometimes we can stay in for longer than other times - do what feels right.

Do you drink lots of cold water before and after?

Did you start the protocol as written?

### CTforlife — 2015-11-18T05:08:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-intelligently-increase-my-times-in-cold-baths-how-long-should-i-be-working-up-to.16934/post-182250>

Until you think you are super human.

### seanb4 — 2015-11-18T15:18:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-intelligently-increase-my-times-in-cold-baths-how-long-should-i-be-working-up-to.16934/post-182260>

I'd say the most important thing is to stay disciplined with it and adaption will come with time, even if it's really slowly. If you stay at 15 mins for the next 2 months that's okay, as long as you keep doing it.

I personally think how fast you adapt depends on a lot of factors but a big one is how healthy you are to begin with. For me I am not healthy so it is taking much longer than others have reported however I am seeing benefits, use these to motivate you to stay disciplined.

For time reference I started pretty much daily CTing march this year and am now up to a point were shivering is minimal and it has stopped feeling like torture, however I am still far from being able to read a book whilst CTing.

Things that helped me: Making sure you eat enough the day before. A big one is also recently I have been sitting under a full spectrum reptile light in daytime whenever possible. This heats up my body thanks to IR. I then think (though I'm not sure) my body saves some of this IR and uses it when I CT, that or the full spectrum light is increasing my overall energy / hormones making CT easier. Either way this has had a big effect recently on making CT easier.

### caroline — 2015-11-19T03:24:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-intelligently-increase-my-times-in-cold-baths-how-long-should-i-be-working-up-to.16934/post-182302>

Sean - do you eat something high fat before C/T?

### seanb4 — 2015-11-19T09:38:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-intelligently-increase-my-times-in-cold-baths-how-long-should-i-be-working-up-to.16934/post-182314>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=182302)
>
> Sean - do you eat something high fat before C/T?

Not particularly, I like to eat fish meals before CT, usually boil in the bag kippers + can of mackerel / mussels.

If I eat high fat, when I lay in the tub I get reflux making the whole experience much more unpleasant...

# Thread: Heliophobics transitioning into The Unliving
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heliophobics-transitioning-into-the-unliving.16940/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2015-10-05T21:43:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heliophobics-transitioning-into-the-unliving.16940/post-178665>

Do you know or have known a heliophobic? Someone with a real phobia to the sun? I do. Many actually. I think we have a large segment of the population that believes: 'UV light is bad.' And it's worse for many women that have been told by the mainstream, "UV light = wrinkles." We now have a generation that fears the sun, slathers on sunscreen and hasn't seen a sunrise or sunset in years. Sad, I know.

But they need serious education. They have little to no understanding that daily timing matters greatly as well as seasonal patterns, the latitude where someone lives, etc. So have any of you watched the behaviors of people that omit and mitigate as much UV light in their lives as possible? Then, watch what happens to them over the next several years?

I could write a book about a 'Jane Doe' in my life on this subject. The phobia is like this as a past example:

1\. Shove material into door cracks that had morning light coming through it.
2\. Put up rugs or other material up over windows to prevent light coming in.
3\. Do all activities at night when the sun is down.

The Jane Doe I noticed doing this was tired/sleep all day long and never wanted to get out of bed. It's usually starts with depression that manifests, then into something else...Seeing is believing. I've seen the lack of UV + circadian mismatches in-person and seen the damage up-close and personal. Then disease manifests along with the behaviors that literally converts them slowly into the walking dead: the unliving.

# Thread: Hot Ladies......

### caroline — 2015-10-07T05:51:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-ladies.16951/post-178788>

I have been having some discussions here lately with ladies of a certain age .....

I/we are trying to understand what makes us so "Hot"

other than our obvious "hotness" lol ..... why do we tend to sleep so hot?

Even when trying to do everything else right ........why do we wake up in the middle of the night so hot???????

Have we reset our thermostat after all the C/T and walking around half naked? and then we don't do well on a warm nite?

I sleep so much better when it is icy cold.

I have gotten rib of my latex mattress, use only cotton bed linens, sleep naked etc.

I always assumed it is a hormone problem .... but I feel great - and not sure about using BHRT just for this issue.

What are we missing ...... probably a lot Dr. K. will say!

tell me.......

### fitness@home — 2015-10-07T16:27:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-ladies.16951/post-178813>

This is me at night too. Fall asleep very quickly but at some point during the night I wake up overheating. Not sweaty like a hot flash - - just plain hotness. I throw off the sheet until I cool back down.

Have been going to bed with the ice pack designed for around the neck/upper back use. It has a cover and velcro for holding it in place. Really helps me fall asleep quickly.

### Curves — 2015-10-07T19:44:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-ladies.16951/post-178829>

For me the shift in internal body temperature started with CT. Before CTing in the ocean I was always bundled up and cold. After a short while of CTing it felt like my internal body thermostat shifted from wanting 80s temps to the low to mid 50s (same as the ocean).

At night, my sleep is awesome! 8 1/2 hours, little tossing and turning, rarely getting up to pee, waking up rejuvenated -- EXCEPT in the heat. I can't sleep as well in the heat. @[fitness@home](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5474/) I totally like an ice pack around the neck and upper back too Do you have a brand you love? I haven't found the perfect fit yet.

Most of the time, I'm warm while everyone else is cold. This past weekend, I had half a dozen ladies to my home for a meeting and I set up chairs outside. Well, the ones in mid-life were way too cold to sit outside and we built a fire inside instead.

I'm curious to know more . . .

Is there something that you haven't understood ....and then it is said in a different way by Dr. K. or someone else - and magically - we get it?

What has changed your perception? What has made a huge difference? what simple phrase finally did it for you???

I fully understand the more we read the better we understand ...and the farther along in this journey Dr. K's words become clearer - words that made no impact the first few times.

But - sometimes, Dr. K. will say something seemingly very simple and I will go wtf - I totally don't get it!
How stupid am I! that is rhetorical btw Dr. K.

Please share what has made a difference for you .....

### cantweight — 2015-10-07T17:33:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/re-interpreting-dr-ks-words-of-wisdom.16952/post-178820>

In Ubi 17 Jack wrote high fat diet in bold print...I felt like that was a big slap upside the head from the universe....but one thing Ive noticed over the years is what Jack says and how I interpret it can be very different....then I do it wrong and wonder why it doesnt work

@[Dextery](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/881/) posted a video that married this concept perfectly for me. Its a silly video, I started watching it because it was so funny....but I kept listening because it broke down how to really implement a high fat diet and get control over insulin resistance in perhaps an oversimplified way...but it was just what i needed to marry to all the complicated shit I read.

# Thread: Newbie here - question about past CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-question-about-past-ct.16955/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### earthwalker — 2015-10-07T18:15:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-question-about-past-ct.16955/post-178823>

I am recovering from severe environmental illness which I had for about 15 years. During that time, there was a period of a few years where I could barely tolerate clothing, could not use any bedding, could not tolerate heating systems, etc. So I essentially lived a CT lifestyle but without the water part. I was living in northern Arizona where the nights were cold and winters could go down to -20 deg Fahrenheit at times. I lived through 5 winters wearing just a very thin long sleeved t-shirt and thin leggings, sometimes without socks, and flip flops. I never wore a winter coat or used a sheet or blanket. The result of this, I discovered after some time, was that my body had adjusted to the cold temperatures quite well. I remember standing outside at a party one day (I lived in a community of others with environmental illness) in my thin t-shirt and leggings. It was cold and windy but I felt quite fine temperature wise. A couple that had just moved there had their winter coats on and had to leave the party because they were too cold to be outside. That was the moment I realized how well my body had adjusted to the cold temperatures.

My question is... does that type of adaptation stay over time even if you stop exposing yourself to cold temps like that? I no longer have to live that way because I have recovered enough to wear clothes and use bedding and heat. Although in the winter here in Sedona, Az I don't use heat while sleeping as I am quite warm with my down comforter - many times I wake up sweating and have to push the covers off. I don't wear a winter coat normally either. I feel that the adaptation has stayed with me but I am wondering if I still need to do CT. I take hot showers nowadays but always finish with a burst of really cold water at the end and get my torso chilled, front and back.

Any comments appreciated. Thank you!

### nonchalant — 2015-10-07T18:47:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-question-about-past-ct.16955/post-178825>

Hi Earthwalker, welcome. Adaptation to cold is good, but one of the main purposes of CT is to actually get your skin chilled, so you can benefit from more ultraviolet light.

### earthwalker — 2015-10-07T23:09:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-question-about-past-ct.16955/post-178840>

Ah, thank you so much for clarifying that. Very helpful! So would one want to do CT and then directly go into the sun?

### nonchalant — 2015-10-08T01:37:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-question-about-past-ct.16955/post-178846>

> [earthwalker said:](/goto/post?id=178840)
>
> Ah, thank you so much for clarifying that. Very helpful! So would one want to do CT and then directly go into the sun?

Yes, that would be a good strategy. But also CT before bed, or anytime, is good.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: NadoRo's High Altitude Labs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nadoros-high-altitude-labs.16957/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NadoRo — 2015-10-08T21:50:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nadoros-high-altitude-labs.16957/post-178881>

I couldn't decide if I should post in the labs thread or Hormones 101 thread, so please advise if I need to repost elsewhere.

I have a journal for context, but here's a rough sketch:

\--Live at 5,000 ft near Denver

\--Have been working on bringing up sex hormones since last July w/Functional Medicine Practitioner who is governed by diet dogma. Diagnosed w/Stage 2-3 Adrenal Fatigue a year ago...Hormones were in the toilet all around, with elevated cholesterol and high LDL. Classic leptin resistance. She seems to think the magic of a gluten free diet will cure all issues...my experience as a perimenopausal 42 year old suggests otherwise. What you see below is the result of aggressive supplementation and experimentation on my part, which she's (mostly) gone along with.

\--Diagnosed w/SIBO Spring 15 by Genova Stool test. Practitioner cited excess growth of one type of bacteria and HS CRP levels as reason. I'm not convinced...but I do feel better on a very low carb diet.

\--7 years of bad sleep caused by 2 babies then toddlers then preschoolers who didn't sleep, stress from multiple moves due to husband's job (military) , little to no support system because of said moves..Both kids are in full time school as of late August and stress levels are lower as a result.

\--started leptin rx two months ago. Have lost 8 lbs (20 more to go to a 20-24% body fat range rec. by JK), eat at least one seafood meal a day, get lots of natural daylight, eat BAB outside, never wear sunglasses, red blockers go on at sunset. Sleep still very inconsistent. I wrestle mostly with night wakings and being awake for long periods of time during the night, despite fatigue and a strong desire to fall asleep. My current body fat is around 34-38% based on a Dexa scan last February, and subsequent measurements at my doctor's office. I HAVE NO HISTORY OF OBESITY OR HIGH CHOLESTEROL. This all started after the birth of my second child at age 36. That's why I know Leptin Resistance is a real state of being!

The labs: (drawn 8/18/15)

Vit D: 41 (so bad! that's what happens when you stop supplementing at altitude, even in the summer)

HS CRP: 1.4

LDL-P 2501

Small LDL-P 911

HDL-P 44.3-67 (I had two cholesterol tests performed with in a week of each other)

TRG: 63

Fasting Glucose: 82

Fasting Insulin: 13 (Optimal range 3-9)

A1C: 4.8

BUN/Creatinine ratio 18 ( I know! I'm deydrated)

DHEA-S: 371 (provider's range is 200-300)

Estradiol pg/ml: 75-125 (her range is 75-125)

Estrone pg/ml: 45 (13-135)

Progesterone: 23.38 ng/ml (her optimal range is 10-20???)

Testosterone: 26 ng/dl (her optimal range is 12-82) Still waiting for that sex drive to come back..

Pregnenalone: 174 ng/dl (her optimal range is 100-150)

If you've made it this far, here are my questions:

1) If my hormones are high, too high for DHEA-S & Pregnenalone according to my provider, WHY IS MY SLEEP STILL BAD?

2) Is my Progesterone high enough for living at altitude?? Should I press for it to go higher? Should all my hormones be higher, living at altitude?

I'm not going to post thyroid, except to say that I'm on 90 Naturethroid daily to raise a low T3 of 78 ng/dl (80-200). My TSH is 1.84, and I know that needs to be at 1.00 or lower. My Reverse T3 ratio was somewhere around 18 when I calculated it.

I have started the CT protocol and am taking cold baths every day or so as I can fit it in. I also started yoga classes and Relora to work on cortisol levels along with continuing the Leptin RX. I know I've got pregnenalone steal going on, and I'm trying to contain my stress levels. Haven't done an ASI in a couple of years. Thanks to Leptin Rx and light exposure, I've got the best energy levels I've had in a looong time. Just not sure why bad sleep persists and feel that it's linked to my hormones.

Thoughts and input are greatly appreciated!!

# Thread: Circuit breaker off = Dreams on
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-breaker-off-dreams-on.16960/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Psyche — 2015-10-09T17:13:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-breaker-off-dreams-on.16960/post-178928>

I've been cutting the circuit breaker to the house at night and I can say for sure at this point there is a huge difference in my sleep. The thing I notice the most is how incredibly detailed and vivid my dreams are, and how I remember them well in to the next day. I also feel like the quality of sleep is better, like I'm getting sleep that is more restorative.

Has anyone else experienced this as well?

### caroline — 2015-10-10T00:47:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-breaker-off-dreams-on.16960/post-178964>

What about your fridge? I found better sleep when i turned off the power to my bedroom.

### Scompy — 2015-10-10T02:37:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-breaker-off-dreams-on.16960/post-178974>

I just got myself a Stetzerizer Microsurge Meter and discovered my computer room had a 1000+ coming in off my power. The recommended is 50 and under. Bedroom plug on one side was around 250 and with a stetzer plug adaptor, I got it down to 45

I'll be posting some picutres or a video of my new Stetzerizer and how I'm mitigating in my journal some point this weekend. Making this a hobby these days

### Psyche — 2015-10-10T18:19:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-breaker-off-dreams-on.16960/post-178994>

@[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/). Yes, power to everything shut down at night, not just bedroom. For one thing our house has everything tied to one master breaker. But really I would do it anyways given how the smart meter acts to make all the house wires an antennae(power line communication-TWACS). All power off is the best I can do and still be sleeping inside. Still getting the smart meter and if line voltage is unbalanced it could be an issue. However the fact I have dramatically improved sleep says my little rural house is probably not getting ground voltages back inside. Even talking about this shit totally pisses me off.

You're an inspiration, Scompy. I have a 15 yo son with wifi and Xbox and I look to your posts a lot for info/advice. Thanks

### RobH — 2015-10-11T07:40:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-breaker-off-dreams-on.16960/post-179053>

had switch installed for bedroom, work likes a charm. Tell her about my two trees. I have an oak tree with power line running to light mounted on tree -- almost never turned on. Tree is dead. Tree next to it, same water, same sun, same soil, etc, is very much alive. Just the presence of the potential voltage -- not even current flowing, has killed the tree after 15 years. Every oak tree around my property (4 total) with conduit running up it and light on it (nearly never on, like twice year for a few hours) is dead compared the the thriving ones. I will try to post picture of it soon. It made a huge impression on my wife and kids when I pointed it out and now my wife switches off electricity in bedroom even if I'm in bed first. Looking at getting switching installed for kids bedrooms also. It wasn't that expensive, and avoids me having to run outside and around the house to breaker box.

RobH

# Thread: D-Ribose Ok for Leptin Rx?

**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NadoRo — 2015-10-10T03:24:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-ribose-ok-for-leptin-rx.16966/post-178979>

I'm wondering if D-Ribose is ok while on the Leptin Rx?

If I take it 2-3 times a day, that averages out to an extra 10-15 grams of carbs in addition to what's in my food..likely bringing my carb count higher than the recommended 30 grams.

Does its benefits for mitochondrial function outweigh the carb concerns in this case?

### NadoRo — 2015-10-10T04:42:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-ribose-ok-for-leptin-rx.16966/post-178981>

After getting further along in my reading of the D-Ribose thread...I'm going to answer my own question and say that, Yes, it is OK. Consensus seems to be that the body doesn't treat D-Ribose as it does other sugars, so not a concern.

### DavidL — 2015-10-26T17:44:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-ribose-ok-for-leptin-rx.16966/post-180490>

D-Ribose is great after a workout in the afternoon outdoors.

# Thread: Google Trends: Albert Einstein vs. Britney Spears
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/google-trends-albert-einstein-vs-britney-spears.16989/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2015-10-13T21:21:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/google-trends-albert-einstein-vs-britney-spears.16989/post-179398>

If you don't know about this feature on Google, it's a way to track popularity about various people and topics. I did a few on "Jack Kruse" and "DHA" to see what people know about it and general public interest based on searches.

[https://www.google.com/trends/explore#q=Jack Kruse&date=3/2011 56m&cmpt=date&tz=Etc/GMT+7](https://www.google.com/trends/explore#q=Jack%20Kruse&date=3%2F2011%2056m&cmpt=date&tz=Etc%2FGMT%2B7)

[https://www.google.com/trends/explore#q=DHA&cmpt=date&tz=Etc/GMT+7](https://www.google.com/trends/explore#q=DHA&cmpt=date&tz=Etc%2FGMT%2B7)

You're able to enter in time-ranges and such, and also overlay the results to see comparisons of searched-terms against each other like this one with Einstein and Britney Spears:

[https://www.google.com/trends/explore#q=/m/0jcx, /m/015f7&cmpt=q&tz=Etc/GMT+7](https://www.google.com/trends/explore#q=%2Fm%2F0jcx%2C%20%2Fm%2F015f7&cmpt=q&tz=Etc%2FGMT%2B7)

As expected, Britney is the Hare doomed to lose to Einstein--the Turtle that will win the race.
