# Thread: Sleeping directly on the magnetico
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-directly-on-the-magnetico.16994/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BradTheBreather — 2015-10-14T03:34:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-directly-on-the-magnetico.16994/post-179417>

I'm planning on purchasing a magnetico soon and I was wondering if its safe to sleep directly on the magnetico, I'm assuming this will be great, but just wanted to ask to be sure. I've slept on the floor without a mattress for a couple years now, usually with just a couple blankets under me or a thin pad. When I start using the magnetico I'm not planning on buying a mattress, so I'm guessing being 8in closer to the pad than the design intend will mean a much bigger magnetic field because of the inverse square law.

Just wondering if anyone has tried this or would recommend against it for any reason?

### JMO — 2015-10-14T04:13:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-directly-on-the-magnetico.16994/post-179425>

I don't think they recommend sleeping directly on it.

### endless — 2015-10-14T04:14:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-directly-on-the-magnetico.16994/post-179426>

They recommend being a minimum of 4 inches above the magnetico to avoid the positive return field spikes. This is pretty clearly diagrammed on their website.

> [https://magneticosleep.com/why-were-better/comparison-diagram/](https://magneticosleep.com/why-were-better/comparison-diagram/)

### JMO — 2015-10-14T04:15:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-directly-on-the-magnetico.16994/post-179427>

Don't forget to ask for the kruse discount. Hopefully they still give it.

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-14T05:25:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-directly-on-the-magnetico.16994/post-179429>

They do still offer the discount. I just used it!

### Just Breathe — 2015-10-15T17:14:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-directly-on-the-magnetico.16994/post-179563>

I used the discount in August and it was still valid. You need a mattress with at least 4 in of thickness between you and the magnetico.

# Thread: Shielded power cable for eliminating monitor electric fields ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielded-power-cable-for-eliminating-monitor-electric-fields.17002/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2015-10-15T06:53:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielded-power-cable-for-eliminating-monitor-electric-fields.17002/post-179543>

In the EMF bootcamp series Michael Neaurt said that you can remove a lot of the computer monitor electric fields with a shielded power cord. I couldn't find one anywhere local so I just wrapped my existing one in aluminum foil and grounded it. Bad mistake. The fields INCREASED dramatically.

I tried more and more foil to increase the mass of the shielding so it would start to absorb and not reflect. I tried grounding on different locations on the cord, all kinds of stuff.

My best result was just clipping the grounding cable straight to the cord rubber casing (?) It reduced the fields by almost half just doing that. Adding a second grounding cable made it worse again.

Interesting that the best absorber of electric fields is the human body

Anybody else had luck with shielding a power cable? I don't want to order one and find out it doesn't work.
This stuff can be really finicky, even for an experienced electrician like me!

### Scompy — 2015-10-15T07:09:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielded-power-cable-for-eliminating-monitor-electric-fields.17002/post-179544>

Yes, you have to constantly monitor with a meter, then remonitor again to take measurements. Same with Faraday cages and RF. You may think everything works or works better, but only the meter reveals the truth.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-10-16T04:26:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielded-power-cable-for-eliminating-monitor-electric-fields.17002/post-179631>

Today I went to Home Depot, bought a few feet of metal clad (armored) house wiring and a three pronged 120v plug. I snipped off the connector on the monitor side of the existing cable and soldered it to the new wiring and plug. I tried to ground the armor to the ground wire but it just would not stick to the solder no matter how much I buffed up the clad.

So I just plugged it in and grounded the clad with my alligator to ground plug cable.

Well.. it totally did not work..

I am hesitant to purchase a shielded AC power cable for the monitor because I don't want to end up with the same result.

I kind of got the impression from Michael Neaurt's bootcamp that it was an easy fix - like you could just go down to Radio Shack and buy a shielded AC power cable for a computer monitor. I went to three different computer stores and they all looked at me like I was from outer space!

I dunno...I guess the tinfoil hat stays until I can figure something else out

I found one site online that was raving about a cord that they have but I did not see any information on how to buy it anywhere:[http://www.lowemfoffice.com/shielded\_power\_cord.htm](http://www.lowemfoffice.com/shielded_power_cord.htm)

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-10-16T05:12:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielded-power-cable-for-eliminating-monitor-electric-fields.17002/post-179632>

I just found a place that sells them. I'm not sure about the quality but its something -
[http://www.slt.co/products/ShieldedPowerCords/ShieldedPowerCords-6Foot-12Foot.aspx](http://www.slt.co/products/ShieldedPowerCords/ShieldedPowerCords-6Foot-12Foot.aspx)

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-12-01T04:14:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielded-power-cable-for-eliminating-monitor-electric-fields.17002/post-182935>

Just updating- I got myself a shielded power cable for my monitor but it really didn't do much. The readings came down a little bit but hardly worth the hassle. But who knows...maybe my house has a weak ground.

### Christina Gagnon — 2018-08-03T15:41:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielded-power-cable-for-eliminating-monitor-electric-fields.17002/post-247379>

> [Cpt.Tired said:](/goto/post?id=179543)
>
> In the EMF bootcamp series Michael Neaurt said that you can remove a lot of the computer monitor electric fields with a shielded power cord. I couldn't find one anywhere local so I just wrapped my existing one in aluminum foil and grounded it. Bad mistake. The fields INCREASED dramatically.
>
> I tried more and more foil to increase the mass of the shielding so it would start to absorb and not reflect. I tried grounding on different locations on the cord, all kinds of stuff.
>
> My best result was just clipping the grounding cable straight to the cord rubber casing (?) It reduced the fields by almost half just doing that. Adding a second grounding cable made it worse again.
>
> Interesting that the best absorber of electric fields is the human body
>
> Anybody else had luck with shielding a power cable? I don't want to order one and find out it doesn't work.
> This stuff can be really finicky, even for an experienced electrician like me!

### Christina Gagnon — 2018-08-03T15:43:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielded-power-cable-for-eliminating-monitor-electric-fields.17002/post-247380>

[@Cpt.Tired](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/12957/) Maybe we can wrap our power cords in bacon  I wonder if you placed a thick pane of lead glass in front of your monitor if that we cut down on some of the radiation emitting from the monitor.

### Scompy — 2015-10-15T19:25:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/virtual-reality-coming-soon.17007/post-179582>

I personally see that we are on the doorstep of having easy-access to the replacement of reality. No one questions the physics behind the technology they place on their bodies and how it negatively interacts and intersects with their biology.

First 2mins...

Strapping Cellphone onto your head...
[https://www.kickstarter.com/project...rtual-reality-headset-for-sma?ref=fundedtoday](https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/1040005785/dscvr-a-roadworthy-virtual-reality-headset-for-sma?ref=fundedtoday)

Completely 'untethered' = No hardwiring to mitigate RF and magnetic EMF

UGH!!!!

The public has 0% awareness about RF-EMF, magnetic EMF and how this impacts the eyes. For one, someone needs to champion EMF reduction and blue-light blocking settings into these devices. The reality is when a person gets ahold of VR and has a typical lifestyle of gaming, that person will spend hours on end each day using this tech on their bodies.

I don't know the answer to this question yet, but the primary one that's hitting me is:

**What poison is worse:**
1\. Playing on VR tech for an hour?
2\. Talking on a cell phone up to the head for an hour?"

**The next question to ask is about exposure time when combining tech:**
Play on your VR tech for an hour. Then go talk on the cell phone with your friends for an hour. 1+1=2.

### Scompy — 2018-02-22T08:39:07+0200

This is about all of the biology they understand about the human eye...

"A surprise inclusion on the system is the addition of eye-tracking, styled for the sake of utilizing a technology that focuses high-resolution graphics on the center of your vision to account for the biology of how your eyes work. Foveated rendering will be included in the specs for the reference design and will allow a pair of cameras to track where your eyes are looking and will adjust the resolution to lighten the load on the graphics processing. This can allow for increased battery life or higher resolution displays."

[https://techcrunch.com/2018/02/21/q...-its-vision-for-the-near-future-of-mobile-vr/](https://techcrunch.com/2018/02/21/qualcomm-shares-details-on-its-vision-for-the-near-future-of-mobile-vr/)

# Thread: UV Bulbs to biohack awakening? / Magnetic sleeping mat?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-bulbs-to-biohack-awakening-magnetic-sleeping-mat.17008/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Daniel — 2015-10-15T22:04:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-bulbs-to-biohack-awakening-magnetic-sleeping-mat.17008/post-179614>

Hi all, since winter is coming, and I live in Budapest, Hungary, we will soon have very short days, when the sun comes up after 8am. I usually wake up around 5-6am to work or exercise, and I was thinking if I buy one of those UV lightbulbs what they use for reptilians living in captivity might help to wake up. What do you think?

It's not possible to sleep with the sunset, cause it goes down at 5-6pm in the winter, but I usually go to bed around 8:30-9pm, and I have a fairly good sleep.

Also cuould you link me a few stuff to read about magnetic sleeping mat, and why is it good for? I heard Jack Kruse talking about it in a podcast, but couldn't manage to find information on the website. Thanks a lot!

### Daniel — 2015-10-19T10:43:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-bulbs-to-biohack-awakening-magnetic-sleeping-mat.17008/post-179853>

Ok, so I found the following lightbulbs available over here:
\- Mercury UV lightbulb called SYLVANIA HSW 125W E27 BLACKLIGHT 0023970
\- PHILIPS IR RED R95 100W 230V E27 (this one is red)
\- TUNGSRAM INFRASEC 150R/IR/CL/E27 245V TU 509699 (transparent)

Are these good? Which of the IR do I need, the red or the transparent?

I work in office / on business trip, but mostly driving in car... and it's almost winter over here, which means less and less sunlight

### Monica W. — 2015-10-30T21:43:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-bulbs-to-biohack-awakening-magnetic-sleeping-mat.17008/post-180804>

Many of us have Magnetico sleep pads. They're made here in the US so I'm not sure if you'd even be able to get one in Hungary, but you can probably find something similar where you are. I have a 20 gauss Magnetico and I LOVE it! I sleep like a baby and within 2 weeks of sleeping on it my chronic neck and back pain was gone.

Here's the Magnetico website for reference. Lots of great info there. I also suggest you search for "Magnetico" on the forum and you should find more info: [https://magneticosleep.com/](https://magneticosleep.com/)

Do not use a grounding mat if your house is not grounded. I tried using one last year and experienced all sorts of weird feelings and I was grounded to the outside. Turned out my house had old old wiring and nothing was grounded. By me grounding myself to the outside I was a magnet for all of the bad electricity from the house. All of the nnEMF;s were going though me. We just had the house rewired and it is much much better but still will not ground myself inside. I either go outside or sit in my basement which is bare concrete.

# Thread: Dancing and red dresses and hugging and loving and looking hot and being a girl!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dancing-and-red-dresses-and-hugging-and-loving-and-looking-hot-and-being-a-girl.17011/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2015-10-16T11:01:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dancing-and-red-dresses-and-hugging-and-loving-and-looking-hot-and-being-a-girl.17011/post-179643>

Awhile back we had a great thread about "dresses" ..... I learned so much about life and living and we had so much fun and lots of guys contributed too!

This is partly in response to something JK said ..... He thinks it is pretty easy to do it all if you get enuf lite and DHA. ....... I don't disagree - Of course lite and DHA are huge - no argument there.

It is hard work being a woman! We have to look good - preferably really hot, be a great cook, be sensitive, supportive and caring and loving, be great in bed , take care of the kids, the house, the shopping, contribute financially ..... and lets not forget to try to keep up with Jack and the blogs and the webinars and FB and all the reading etc. et. etc.

Holy smokes!

Being a woman is not for the feint hearted! We need to be tough and strong and at the same time gentle and caring...... and take care of our family and their needs as well as everything else.

My guy will sometimes remark that a woman we pass when out walking is "high maintenance". That ruffles my feathers a bit!

Guys love looking at a woman who is well dressed and in great shape and manicured and pedicured and coiffed within an inch of her life........ and why not???

But .... that takes a lot of money and time and self focus - well, unless you are young and a natural beauty.

How are all you girls juggling all of this?????

tell me.....

### Inger — 2015-10-16T15:10:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dancing-and-red-dresses-and-hugging-and-loving-and-looking-hot-and-being-a-girl.17011/post-179647>

Well... I have no issues dressing like a girl... I love that  I almost always wear skirts and stuff.....
but.....
My nails are so not cute!!! OMG as I dig oysters and open them with my hands.. it makes for no pretty nails!!! That is my issue.........
I need to make me manicure and pedicure way more often! And I have no money to let someone else do it.....
My hands and feet (as I go barefoot a lot in dirt) are so not pretty..........
what to do

### fitness@home — 2015-10-16T16:51:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dancing-and-red-dresses-and-hugging-and-loving-and-looking-hot-and-being-a-girl.17011/post-179657>

Haircut and pedi/mani every 5-6 weeks. Since I am rough on my hands (like Inger) I only get fingernails trimmed & buffed.

Have to wear dress clothes all week for work, so no problem there with my feminine side. Nights my husband really likes my attire of just a simple tank top and panties .

_Love_ wearing sundresses on the weekends.

### nicld — 2015-10-16T16:55:27+0300

Some guys like the "dressed to the nine" girl but some love the all natural girls. I am the all natural girl on the weekends and put together during the week for work.

I do agree that some women put off the vibe of high maintenance.

### thomas — 2015-10-16T20:45:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dancing-and-red-dresses-and-hugging-and-loving-and-looking-hot-and-being-a-girl.17011/post-179675>

As a guy I donj't care about manicure and pedicure LOL. Nails aren't the first thing I will look at for sure.
A great tan that I look for lol. If you're super white It's kinda repelling.

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-17T03:23:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dancing-and-red-dresses-and-hugging-and-loving-and-looking-hot-and-being-a-girl.17011/post-179693>

During the week I have to dress nice but dresses are not what I wear. I'm a skinny pant wearing girl with girly blouses and jewelry. Pedi every 4 weeks but I'm with Inger - my hands are kinda beat up. Unfortunately, I've been a nail biter. I like wearing a dress to dinner when we go out. But I have that hippy natural side to me so I go ala naturale on weekends. Sundresses and no makeup and my guy thinks this is a great look!

### Inger — 2015-10-17T10:37:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dancing-and-red-dresses-and-hugging-and-loving-and-looking-hot-and-being-a-girl.17011/post-179708>

>
> As a guy I donj't care about manicure and pedicure LOL. Nails aren't the first thing I will look at for sure.
> A great tan that I look for lol. If you're super white It's kinda repelling.

This post made me happy....... that there are guys like you
My dear brother once said to me, as I complained how bad my nails and hands looked (every women I have seen have better looking nails..uh)
he told me he thinks it is sexy when women have hands looking like they are doing something... like dirty..... he said he does not care one bit about that women have so manicured nails.. he said he loves wild looking women...
maybe he said it only to make me happy... because he loves me.. but I do think he is like that. He is very special for sure.. too bad he is my brother so I cant marry him...!

Every time you pick oysters your nails and hands get scratched up real bad...
and the dirt is so hard to get off

I think a real man loves the women with her dirty nails and all....
One day one will come in my way

### caroline — 2015-10-17T11:00:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dancing-and-red-dresses-and-hugging-and-loving-and-looking-hot-and-being-a-girl.17011/post-179710>

I think a real man is looking for a real woman for sure.......we maybe don't need all the window dressing so much - but we do need to be healthy and strong and happy in ourself and then we glow from within ...and that is impossible to ignore!

But that still doesn't mean you get to be a sclumpadink!

I wasn't okay for someone else until I was okay for me ..... that made the most huge difference. Loving yourself is so powerful and it can't be denied.
I think you become relaxed and easy and fun - and that is transmitted by quantum Jazz!

# Thread: Insurance Negotiation

### Raiken3712 — 2015-10-18T04:20:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insurance-negotiation.17017/post-179769>

I've heard that supposedly you can get insurance to cover more. I've read numerous people talk about their insurance covering labs...mine doesn't seem to cover anything except for 20 chiropractor visits and one physical a year.

The rest is all me....They do have a member rate for everything else so I don't pay full price, but considering what I'm paying in I think they could pay a bit more.

If possible I'd like to get them paying more. They are getting 4,872.60 a year from me and if they paid for everything I've done this year it would amount to only 400 dollars total.

Some of it is subsidy from my employer, but they are getting paid regardless. I'd like to get more out of this if possible. How do I go about getting them to cover more. I want them to cover all my labs, and the doctors visits. They'd still be making a profit.

Sure insurance is also about the possibility of a catastrophic happening..and if that happened than the 8,000 dollars I've put in over 2 years would be nothing, but I don't see that happening..anyway I'd like to get more out of this.

Also insurance renewal is soon...I'm betting my out of pocket next year will go up...it went from 0 I was paying to 432 dollars...I can only imagine what it's going to increase to.

Thanks for any info

### fitness@home — 2015-10-18T15:42:54+0300

# Thread: Jan Baalsrud
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-baalsrud.17018/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-10-18T16:25:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-baalsrud.17018/post-179780>

Has anyone heard about this man before?

His seems to be yet another remarkable survival story. He was a member of the Norwegian Resistance in WWII and while chased by the Nazis survived swimming through icy water, snow blindness - and even frostbite, amputating nine of his own toes after gangrene set in.

[http://www.scotsman.com/news/jan-baalsrud-the-other-great-escape-1-786223](http://www.scotsman.com/news/jan-baalsrud-the-other-great-escape-1-786223)

This seems to be a book about him:

[http://www.amazon.co.uk/We-Die-Alone-David-Howarth/dp/1847678459/](http://www.amazon.co.uk/We-Die-Alone-David-Howarth/dp/1847678459/)

### Optimalbound — 2015-10-18T19:02:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-baalsrud.17018/post-179786>

Definitely heard of [Baalsrud](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jan_Baalsrud). I remember that his incredible story made quite an impression on me when I read it in my early teens. Especially the part where he amputates his own gangrene-ridden toes... (Yes, you CAN do too much CT! )

There's a movie from 1957 that depicts his espace from the German army. Not exactly an Oscar-winning production, but I believe it is historically quite accurate.
[https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL5017C6434C63875C](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL5017C6434C63875C)

### Gagnrad — 2015-10-19T19:08:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-baalsrud.17018/post-179878>

Thanks! He sounds like the real deal. I bought the book.

### caroline — 2015-10-20T06:36:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-baalsrud.17018/post-179940>

Please tell us how you liked the book Mike.....

### Optimalbound — 2015-10-20T13:48:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-baalsrud.17018/post-179945>

> [Optimalbound said:](/goto/post?id=179786)
>
> <snip...>
>
> Not exactly an Oscar-winning production, but I believe it is historically quite accurate.
> [https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL5017C6434C63875C](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL5017C6434C63875C)

Correction: [The film](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nine_Lives_\(1957_film\)) was actually nominated for an Oscar for Best Foreign Language Film in 1958.

# Thread: Sister Kenney - a remarkable story
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2015-10-19T02:23:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-179821>

Does anyone know this story of a remarkable woman who was ostracised In Australia - but found huge success in the U.S.?
She was a nurse with a very innovative treatment for Polio that was not accepted by the orthopaedic Docs in Aus. She was shunned and very abused by the medical community.

There is utube stuff that I couldn't paste here for some reason....

### Gagnrad — 2015-10-19T19:15:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-179879>

Hadn't heard of her.

According to Wiki:

> **Elizabeth Kenny** (20 September 1880 – 30 November 1952) was an unaccredited [Australian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Australia)[nurse](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nurse) who promoted a controversial new approach to the treatment of [poliomyelitis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Poliomyelitis) . Her findings ran counter to conventional medical wisdom; they demonstrated the need to exercise muscles affected by polio instead of immobilizing them. Kenny's principles of muscle rehabilitation became the foundation of [physical therapy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Physical_therapy), or physiotherapy.[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elizabeth_Kenny#cite_note-1)

May have been "unaccredited" but that doesn't sound so far out. I think medics always used to think that bedrest was better than exercise for pretty much any condition … but is it always?

What I can see when searching under "video"

[http://www.wnyc.org/story/interview-with-patients/](http://www.wnyc.org/story/interview-with-patients/)

Will have a listen.

### caroline — 2015-10-20T01:39:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-179908>

Thanks Mike.....
A woman told her story Sunday morning on a radio show we listen to .....

Her Dad had lived in the U.S. and then came to New Zealand and met and married her Mother. When she was 11 she got polio and the Doc's wanted to treat her in the conventional
way - complete hospital bed rest and the legs completely immobilised in irons. Her Dad said no way and she was treated by massaging for 5 hours a day \[ 3people\].

She is now 80+ and has never had any signs of polio.

Nurse Kenny was treated terribly here by the medical profession and so went to the U.S. I think maybe she has a connection to Roosevelt?
I am looking for her biography and am having trouble finding it unless I want to pay $165.00

### caroline — 2015-10-20T01:49:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-179909>

Mike - if you have a chance - and are so inclined ....listen to a program here Sunday mornings called Australia all over with Ian McNamara. People call in from all over the world.

Every Sunday morning I get the most incredible geography lessons and a huge lesson in caring, committed, strong people doing the most wonderful things for others and for nature and the environment.

Every Sunday morning I am blown away by the goodness and mental strength and kindness in people and I want to get in the car and see all this for myself!

### Jude — 2015-10-20T02:49:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-179910>

iirc Sr Kenny was active in southern Queensland. Think there is a street dedicated to her name in ? Clifton?. Heard lots of stories from oldies who loved and respected her therapies. Had no idea she could be still living.

### caroline — 2015-10-20T06:29:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-179938>

Jude she has passed away. There is a museum etc.

### Gagnrad — 2015-10-20T09:38:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-179944>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=179909)
>
> Mike - if you have a chance - and are so inclined ....listen to a program here Sunday mornings called Australia all over with Ian McNamara. People call in from all over the world.
>
> Every Sunday morning I get the most incredible geography lessons and a huge lesson in caring, committed, strong people doing the most wonderful things for others and for nature and the environment.
>
> Every Sunday morning I am blown away by the goodness and mental strength and kindness in people and I want to get in the car and see all this for myself!

That's so nice.

I tried googling under "images" and found pictures of her shaking hands with him:

["sister kenny" roosevelt](https://www.google.co.uk/search?q=%22sister+kenny%22+roosevelt&client=safari&rls=en&source=lnms&tbm=isch&sa=X&ved=0CAkQ_AUoA2oVChMImf_TsrXQyAIVAoMaCh3VrQf1&biw=1270&bih=600)

This also turned up:

A Nurse Gains Fame in the Days of Polio:

(By Barron H. Lerner, a professor of medicine and population health at New York University School of Medicine)

[http://well.blogs.nytimes.com/2013/12/26/a-nurse-gains-fame-in-the-days-of-polio/?\_r=0](http://well.blogs.nytimes.com/2013/12/26/a-nurse-gains-fame-in-the-days-of-polio/?_r=0)

Among other things it mentions her as a pioneer of physical therapy in general:

> Her emphasis on early mobilization has come to be a mainstay of not only polio treatment but of [physical therapy](http://topics.nytimes.com/top/news/health/diseasesconditionsandhealthtopics/physicaltherapy/index.html?inline=nyt-classifier) more broadly.

I was quite interested in this:

> Plus, Ms. Kenny believed that patients needed to play an active role in their recovery, learning the names of affected muscles and how they worked.

I did a lot of Alexander Technique at one time and while a lot of that is hands-on and experiential there's also somewhat of an emphasis of teaching people the mechanics of their own bodies. AT teachers seem to believe that some people carry out certain movements badly, because they have a false notion of how particular joints work and of the mechanics of movements. Maybe there's a kind of connection there. She also seems to be seeing a link between proper _understanding_ and proper functioning. (Not that theoretical knowledge is the point here: it's practical knowledge that counts at the end of the day: like riding a bike.)

Here's the book that Barron H. Lerner was wiring about:

[https://global.oup.com/academic/pro...613C5B1AC504DF0C9115C4A7ED229D?cc=us&lang=en&](https://global.oup.com/academic/product/polio-wars-9780195380590;jsessionid=63613C5B1AC504DF0C9115C4A7ED229D?cc=us&lang=en&)

As cheap as $9 secondhand on Amazon:

[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/0195380592/](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/0195380592/)

### caroline — 2015-10-21T02:31:49+0300

### Gagnrad — 2015-10-21T09:06:01+0300

### Gagnrad — 2015-10-21T09:19:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-180058>

By the way, on polio - notice the academic writing in the Times there says:

> The successful development of a [polio vaccine](http://health.nytimes.com/health/guides/specialtopic/polio-immunization-vaccine/overview.html?inline=nyt-classifier) in the 1950s made these debates much less pressing.

Hmmm … that's the story, but I wonder. Maybe @[patgrif@hotmail.com](mailto:patgrif@hotmail.com) has some comments on that.

There's recent comment on this from Sally Fallon:

> The most persuasive arguments for vaccinating children have to do with polio. Didn’t the polio vaccine wipe out this truly tragic epidemic of viral disease? **Seems like an open-and-shut case for vaccination**. However, the campaign to eradicate polio is littered with accidents, contradiction, confusion and fraud. Health officials introduced the live attenuated Salk vaccine in the U.S. in 1955. The Sabin oral polio vaccine followed in 1961. At that time, the diagnostic criteria for polio were changed. Before introduction of the vaccine, patients diagnosed with paralytic polio had to exhibit symptoms of paralysis for twenty-four hours; after the vaccines they had to exhibit symptoms of paralysis for sixty days. In addition, before the polio vaccine, coxsackie virus and aseptic meningitis were lumped in together with polio with no lab confirmation, but after the introduction of the vaccine, coxsackie virus and aseptic meningitis were separated out and lab confirmation was required. So naturally the rates of polio seemed to come down. But worse ...
>
> \[My emphasis\]

I'll let anyone who wants to see where the "but worse …" leads look up the original article:

[http://www.westonaprice.org/health-topics/caustic-commentary-summer-2015/](http://www.westonaprice.org/health-topics/caustic-commentary-summer-2015/)

But I shall add that she concludes:

> Polio has disappeared, case closed. Or has it? In 2014, neurologists began reporting cases of paralysis and death in children fully vaccinated against polio. So of course they don’t call it polio. “Acute flaccid paralysis” has occurred in dozens on American children and tens of thousands of children in heavily vaccinated India.

Well, I don't know. I'm not a doctor. But this does look like smoke and mirrors. And I'd certainly not like to say _for sure_ that Sally's chasing a will-'o-the-wisp. Further, I suspect anyone who did say that would have economic - or ideological - motives for their certainty.

If you keep changing your definitions and criteria, you certainly can make things seem however you want them to - at least in the short term.

“Acute flaccid paralysis” … well, well.

### caroline — 2015-10-21T11:28:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-180061>

That quote seems quite appropriate to these times we live in Mike.

I have spent way to much time looking for this book - will get back to it tomorrow.

This must be something I am totally not getting that they won't ship that particular book ....but I can buy it for $360.00 if I choose! lol

### caroline — 2015-10-21T11:29:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-180062>

BTW - I can buy the other book from Amazon - no problem .....?????

### Gagnrad — 2015-10-21T12:24:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-180063>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=180061)
>
> That quote seems quite appropriate to these times we live in Mike.

You know I feel quite angry about these things.

I don't think most of us have begun to realise to what an extent commercial entities have manipulated our view of so many things in their own interests and regardless of the harm they do to people. My uncle was a world-famous New Left academic at one of our two oldest universities over here - I'll say no more or people will be able to work out who he was - anyway, he bought hook, line and sinker into the low-fat garbage. I myself was always cautious of the Left - I think they've done a hell of a lot of damage around the world (The Great Leap Forward in China alone!) - but ain't it morbidly funny that a New Left academic, a kind of quasi-Marxist, should believe what Big Food wanted him to and never have an inkling. It killed him. Early heart attack.

Tim Noakes:

"Ten companies produce 80% of processed foods in the world, and they're creating billions and billions of dollars in profits by poisoning people. I'd tax them out of existence."

It's not just Big Food, though. Big Pharma is up to the same tricks.

### caroline — 2015-10-22T01:18:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-180107>

I always think that big companies influence us in such subtle ways that we are so very unaware.

I was shocked to learn that department stores turn up the heat so that you get too warm when shopping and you just buy random things as well as what you are looking for quickly so you can get out!

I don't know how we can ever be smart enuf and on top of it enuf to figure out all the subtleties of what all these high powered companies and brain power do to twist us into submission.

I understand we must be a sceptic - to the max I guess ........but it seems to me that some people who are sceptics - it overflows into every area of their life and is kind of a lousy way to live .... in my opinion anyway.

That is part of the reason I love Macca's radio program on Sunday mornings. Macca regularly does his program remote from flooded and ravaged areas and people drive for hundreds of miles to show up at 5:00am to help and donate in any way they can. There is a huge heartbeat out there in the heartland - people that don't have much and helping others.
Macca lives and loves and promotes wildlife and nature and always at least trying to do the right thing. He also loves and promotes local musicians/talent. He has a small band and he sings and they do benefit concerts and often sing about the outback and all the old timers and their songs.....

We saw him here in town a few months ago and Tony bought one of his albums ....in vinyl of course!

# Thread: Sunscreen Linked To Coral Destruction
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunscreen-linked-to-coral-destruction.17048/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-10-25T11:10:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunscreen-linked-to-coral-destruction.17048/post-180406>

Think of all the peoples who have been diving for pearls and shellfish over millenia and never a one put any toxic chemicals on their skin to get in the water to poison the coral!

These unnecessary and actually counterproductive products that have been pushed at the public so assiduously - for a parallel, see also statins, see also Gatorade and the sugar-laden sports-drink "rehydration" mania ... and hand-sanitisers, anti-perspirants and a plethora of other products - have now been linked to the destruction of coral reefs:

"Researchers conducted the study in the U.S. Virgin Islands and Hawaii — areas that attract large amounts of tourists each year to swim in reef areas. They found that the chemical oxybenzone affects coral in three different ways: it alters its DNA, makes coral susceptible to potentially fatal bleaching, and acts as an endocrine disruptor, which causes baby coral to encase itself in its own skeleton and leads to its death.

To make things worse, it does not take a large amount of this chemical to upset coral. According to the research, concentrations of oxybenzone as low as 62 parts per trillion — equivalent to a drop of water in six and a half Olympic-sized swimming pools — are deemed harmful.

Between 6,000 and 14,000 tons of sunscreen lotion make their way into coral reef areas each year, and much of that sunscreen contains oxybenzone. ..."​

Article:

Almost Every Chemical-Based Sunscreen In The U.S. Linked To Coral Destruction:

[http://thinkprogress.org/climate/2015/10/21/3714544/sunscreen-coral-enemy/](http://thinkprogress.org/climate/2015/10/21/3714544/sunscreen-coral-enemy/)

### RobH — 2015-10-25T12:28:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunscreen-linked-to-coral-destruction.17048/post-180408>

somehow this doesn't surprise me.... probably not so good for your skin eeither!

RobH

### omegajosh — 2015-10-25T11:28:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raising-progesterone.17049/post-180407>

It is obvious that my adrenal issues are holding back my recovery at the moment and are at the crux of my problems. Jack says that for chronically depressed cortisol one of the most important steps is to re-establish progesterone levels. Can anyone (including Jack) tell me the best way of doing this? Would pregnenelone supplementation be preferred to progesterone?

### JanSz — 2015-10-27T17:13:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raising-progesterone.17049/post-180561>

> [omegajosh said:](/goto/post?id=180407)
>
> It is obvious that my adrenal issues are holding back my recovery at the moment and are at the crux of my problems. Jack says that for chronically depressed cortisol one of the most important steps is to re-establish progesterone levels. Can anyone (including Jack) tell me the best way of doing this? Would pregnenelone supplementation be preferred to progesterone?

cortisol, pregnenolone, progesterone, DHEAs
are steroid hormones.
There is many other steroid hormones.
But if you would take healthy person,
measure his commonly measured steroid hormones,
took all of them and treated as 100% by weight
you will find big differences in their participation
DHEAs -- 94.5%
cortisol(AM) -- 4.25%
all the rest take what is left
It makes good sense to mind this two hormones with special care.

Cortisol is made in adrenals, so it becomes first suspect.
Most often it is declared fatigued, sick.
That is bad assumption most of the time.

To nourish adrenals you must provide them with:
numerous micronutrients
and
proper light

I you live
above or below subtropics there is no sufficient light there (even if you are naked)
If you wear clothes, glasses, sunscreen is even worst.

I think you live in UK, say London, latitude 51.5072° N
even on cloudless sunny day at noon, there is not enough light there
subtropic ends at 38th parallel, it is around Gibraltar in your area.
36.1430° N

There are discussions on how to go about artificial lighting.
Some here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-18](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-18)

Remember, lighting is about
proper frequencies
proper intensity
proper timing

all that lead to proper Circadian Rhythm

\-----------------------------------
Lighting is just one item.
The other are large variety of micronutrients.
We are not what we eat, we are what we assimilate.
So
you want to check your micronutrients
I do that using this test:

Nearest blood draw site for you is here:
[http://www.spectracell.com/find-a-draw-site-results/?branch\_country=GB&search\_by\_other\_go=Search](http://www.spectracell.com/find-a-draw-site-results/?branch_country=GB&search_by_other_go=Search)

# Thread: Looking for a solid MSDS on DHA
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-solid-msds-on-dha.17052/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2015-10-26T03:28:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-solid-msds-on-dha.17052/post-180461>

So back in the mid-90s, I was a biologist at Stanford University. I mostly worked in the Bio-sciences department helping in the pre-med labs during the main school year, but also had several summer jobs when the students were off-campus. At the Environmental Health and Safety Dept, I worked a job catalogueing chemicals and materials on campus to help construct a database of info. For this reason, I became extremely familiar with MSDSs (Material Safety Data Sheets) at that time to denote what hazards existed in various locations.

After learning from the wise Dr.K about the extraordinary significance of DHA and the mitochondria for electron tunneling, it was instinctual for me to understand the full details of DHA (Docosahexaenoic acid). Knowing we can curse DHA into a transfat with heat like other fats and oils, I expected to see find better info on DHA on MSDSs. But most data on DHA I've found poking around today looks to be 'unknown' for DHA. I'll have to search for better info.

My main curiosity has to do with how it denatures. Generally, anything over 105F is a standard for denaturing in living tissues. It's why we see survival rates plummet if you get a fever for 105F. That alone is making me believe we want to take in DHA at a very low-temp if possible, like body-temp or lower to be assurred the DHA is holding up as a molecule for use.

### Lahelada — 2015-10-26T12:23:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-solid-msds-on-dha.17052/post-180470>

[https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&sou...T\_IAzM&usg=AFQjCNG4y6E2zwrotbhRX2OYLs1HMnkrLg](https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&source=web&rct=j&url=http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/ejlt.200500290/pdf&ved=0CCEQFjAAahUKEwj5mIDB8t_IAhWLOogKHT_IAzM&usg=AFQjCNG4y6E2zwrotbhRX2OYLs1HMnkrLg)

[https://www.omega3innovations.com/blog/does-frying-bakinfalse](https://www.omega3innovations.com/blog/does-frying-bakinfalse)
\-canning-fish-affect-the-omega-3-content/

[https://books.google.com/books?id=vcbSBwAAQBAJ&pg=PA62&lpg=PA62&dq=Dha degradation temperature&source=bl&ots=WFtGI41An2&sig=i7FEh5kR\_w-DSZQwDudGSsBVJ18&hl=en&sa=X&ved=0CCgQ6AEwADgKahUKEwiO1sCf89\_IAhVYNYgKHaJrBU0#v=onepage&q=Dha](https://books.google.com/books?id=vcbSBwAAQBAJ&pg=PA62&lpg=PA62&dq=Dha+degradation+temperature&source=bl&ots=WFtGI41An2&sig=i7FEh5kR_w-DSZQwDudGSsBVJ18&hl=en&sa=X&ved=0CCgQ6AEwADgKahUKEwiO1sCf89_IAhVYNYgKHaJrBU0#v=onepage&q=Dha) degradation temperature&false

# Thread: Masai diet low in Dha but Dha blood values higher than intake?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/masai-diet-low-in-dha-but-dha-blood-values-higher-than-intake.17058/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lahelada — 2015-10-27T18:39:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/masai-diet-low-in-dha-but-dha-blood-values-higher-than-intake.17058/post-180570>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21854590](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21854590)

It is not a very well rounded study but it made me wonder.
Humans have a very low,negligible capacity to synthesise DHA.
Did we lose the ability as we moved away from the equator?
Is the type 6 skin colour /extreme sun exposure combo somehow a trigger? Another study diagnoses low d3 due to same said skin colour.
If it is can it be reawakened in those of us who left?
Interesting.

### Shijin13 — 2015-10-27T18:43:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/masai-diet-low-in-dha-but-dha-blood-values-higher-than-intake.17058/post-180572>

> Did we lose the ability as we moved away from the equator?
> Is the type 6 skin colour /extreme sun exposure combo somehow a trigger? Another study diagnoses low d3 due to same said skin colour.
> If it is can it be reawakened in those of us who left?
> Interesting.

Interesting question.... Love to hear other peoples thoughts....

# Thread: TheraBlue lenses?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/therablue-lenses.17070/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Eilee — 2015-10-29T20:21:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/therablue-lenses.17070/post-180736>

I asked my opticians to be in touch with Blotch about helping to get me a pair of their lenses and they contacted this company instead. Wondering people's thoughts ...

[http://www.luzerneoptical.com/top-w...ical-brand/therablue-uv-hev-lenses.html?sl=EN](http://www.luzerneoptical.com/top-wholesale-optical-labs/new-products/exclusive-optical-brand/therablue-uv-hev-lenses.html?sl=EN)

### Lahelada — 2015-10-29T20:47:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/therablue-lenses.17070/post-180737>

It filters UV too...

### Eilee — 2015-10-29T21:53:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/therablue-lenses.17070/post-180742>

Ok, I misunderstand that. Confused about harmful UV's. What is the type that causes cataracts?

### Lahelada — 2015-10-30T02:12:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/therablue-lenses.17070/post-180752>

SorryEilee, I confused matters. The diagram is from their website and they are saying that UV is harmful. I will try to delete the diagram.
Essentially though these glasses block UV as per conventional wisdom,which we do not share.

### Eilee — 2015-10-30T02:48:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/therablue-lenses.17070/post-180753>

Got it. Thanks so much!!

### RobH — 2015-11-09T18:03:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/therablue-lenses.17070/post-181508>

Just got some BluTechs for my every day glasses and like them very much. They do have a slight yellow tint and a few people at work (and mostly my kids who love to tease me) have noted the tint but it isn't an issue. The TheraBlue look like they would be reasonable for indoor use around screens / fluorescent lights, etc, but remember how important it is to get them off and get your eyes out in the sun when you can! Like all lenses they block UV, and of course advertise that. Would be nice if somebody would design a lens that would just block the blues but pass UV, but that seems unlikely given the conventional wisdom...

Interesting how they break down the blue spectrum into "good blue light" and "bad blue light" sort of reminds me of the "good cholesterol / bad cholesterol" meme that is perpetuated in medicine

RobH

### Summer — 2017-06-20T20:50:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/therablue-lenses.17070/post-216650>

Blutech is better protection. ( There are different materials in each brand that differ in filtering) but on average blutech filters 95% 400- 420nm, 73-90% of 400-440nm, 31-46% of 459-484nm. The Therablue filters 57- 99 % at 415nm, 15- 41% at 430nm and 10% at 450nm. I personally wear blutech max but right now does not come in a prescription.

### JoeT — 2017-06-20T22:59:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/therablue-lenses.17070/post-216655>

> [Summer said:](/goto/post?id=216650)
>
> Blutech is better protection. ( There are different materials in each brand that differ in filtering) but on average blutech filters 95% 400- 420nm, 73-90% of 400-440nm, 31-46% of 459-484nm. The Therablue filters 57- 99 % at 415nm, 15- 41% at 430nm and 10% at 450nm. I personally wear blutech max but right now does not come in a prescription.

Due to what you state above, I went with BPI 500 tints. They block 100% of 350 - 500. I think Jack has the 600s; I just couldn't pull those off in my office though!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Sleeping Masks.. are they bad ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-masks-are-they-bad.17072/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### vangirl — 2015-10-30T01:16:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-masks-are-they-bad.17072/post-180750>

This may sound like a bizarre question but in the past 3 weeks I started using a sleeping mask to try to make my sleeping environment as dark as possible. W hat I found was that my eyes got scratchy and dry. I started out with a mask that laid right on my eyes and then switched to newer model that was formed so that the material does not touch the eyes, but both do the same thing. I can't help wonder if these are ruining my eyesight (although I have no signs of that.. yet) by cutting off circulation or exposure to air/ oxygen ? Does anyone know or have had the same experience ?

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-30T03:32:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-masks-are-they-bad.17072/post-180754>

I wear a mask most nights to keep the room as black as possible. I even sleep with a fan going to keep the room as cold as possible. I've not had the dry eye issue and do like that my eyes are kept completely in the dark. I know others who use masks and had not heard that issue.

### vangirl — 2015-10-30T03:34:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-masks-are-they-bad.17072/post-180755>

Thanks Patty Cakes for sharing! But how odd then .. I wonder what is wrong with me ?

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-30T03:36:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-masks-are-they-bad.17072/post-180756>

Get some more sun and get more DHA through food (fish). Both should help with dry eyes.

### vangirl — 2015-10-30T03:55:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-masks-are-they-bad.17072/post-180758>

Thanks Patty Cakes. I am doing both of those things you advise, but the thing is that my eyes were feeling fine before I started with the sleeping mask. Sometimes I wake up in the middle of the night now and my eyes are burning and scratchy. But once I take it off in the morning they get better. Im starting wonder if I am allergic to the materials...!

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-30T04:04:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-masks-are-they-bad.17072/post-180761>

I use the Bucky lite or something like that for travel. It's a slightly different material than the regular Bucky and it's thinner. Maybe try a different brand and see what happens.

### Penny — 2015-10-30T18:02:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-masks-are-they-bad.17072/post-180793>

Yes, maybe the material - I wear some neoprene ones and they soothe my eyes actually - maybe try reading with a red incandescent lamp before bed? And more water/seafood/grounding - you also might be glycating - eye drops with l-carnosine might be helpful for that - if your blood sugar is high - or your ubiquitination rate is high, you are glycating...

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19487926](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19487926)

### vangirl — 2015-10-31T22:16:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-masks-are-they-bad.17072/post-180851>

Patty Cakes, thanks, I looked at the Bucky brand on Amazon. It says it is latex free. I then looked at mine (also bought at Amazon) and it doesn't say! I then checked the mask myself and was shocked there was no label that lists the materials used. I think I will have to chuck it.. : (

Thanks Penny! I will look into some made from neoprene, as well! Hopefully I will make a better buy this time!

Thanks for both of your input! Really appreciate it ! : )

p.s. Penny , I dont think I have high blood sugar as I eat only a small amount of carbs, but I do check it and it's most of the time in range. But I will keep this in mind ! thank you !


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Please advise on my emf shielding efforts
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-advise-on-my-emf-shielding-efforts.17081/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Liam Taylor — 2015-11-02T12:46:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-advise-on-my-emf-shielding-efforts.17081/post-180969>

I live in a house where I do not have the option to turn the wifi router off, it currently sits in the room below where I sleep and I am conscious of this.

Two efforts I am making to negate against the nnemf are to put my phone and ipad in a aluminum lined shoebox with airplane mode on, and to wrap my pillow in a foil space blanket underneath the pillowcase. Do these sound like sensible and useful moves?

### Lahelada — 2015-11-02T13:09:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-advise-on-my-emf-shielding-efforts.17081/post-180971>

Not sure. There is an I phone app you can download to measure. I am dubious about the actual values it gives butg it may help you to ascertain where exactly the router is and move your bed away . I would be more interested in getting a barrier under my bed.

### Scompy — 2015-11-02T22:03:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-advise-on-my-emf-shielding-efforts.17081/post-181017>

> [Liam Taylor said:](/goto/post?id=180969)
>
> I live in a house where I do not have the option to turn the wifi router off, it currently sits in the room below where I sleep and I am conscious of this.
>
> Two efforts I am making to negate against the nnemf are to put my phone and ipad in a aluminum lined shoebox with airplane mode on, and to wrap my pillow in a foil space blanket underneath the pillowcase. Do these sound like sensible and useful moves?

Do you have an RF meter and what #s are you getting? It sounds like you don't have control over the WiFi router, else you'd just unplug it for the night. If I were in your place, I'd get a silver netting/cloth to put under the bed. But you'll see that with RF, it's insideous. Even if you block off just one wall or floor, RF can still be getting in because it doesn't behave like a square-root falloff from the source exactly. For this reason, you won't know unless you have a meter. Magnetico makes a lot of sense too.

### SpiritGuide — 2015-11-04T20:11:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-advise-on-my-emf-shielding-efforts.17081/post-181158>

Jack will probably tell you to move.

Why can't you turn off the wifi router out of curiosity?

# Thread: NASA's new EM Drive (Electromagnetic Drive) - The electormagnetic force is primordial
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nasas-new-em-drive-electromagnetic-drive-the-electormagnetic-force-is-primordial.17096/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jeff Power — 2015-11-05T22:58:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nasas-new-em-drive-electromagnetic-drive-the-electormagnetic-force-is-primordial.17096/post-181231>

Interesting article, if proved true, it appears once again science is lacking in understanding the laws of the universe in many respects. This EM Drive is defying the laws of the universe as it is classically known in mainstream science. Maybe this some how ties back in to what Jack has said about the only true primordial force being the electromagnetic force. Gravity is only emergent.

**
EM Drive is reportedly still producing thrust after another round of NASA testing
The "impossible" engine continues to surprise.**

FIONA MACDONALD
5 NOV 2015
[the past few months about the EM Drive](http://www.sciencealert.com/independent-scientists-confirm-that-the-impossible-em-drive-produces-thrust) – an "impossible" engine that, if functional, could supposedly power a spacecraft to Mars in just [10 weeks, without any rocket fuel](http://www.sciencealert.com/nasa-has-trialled-an-engine-that-would-take-us-to-Mars-in-10-weeks).

It's pretty easy to understand the appeal. Science can't explain how the engine could possibly work and yet, somehow, throughout a range of tests at NASA's Eagleworks Laboratory, the engine reportedly continues to produce thrust. But here's the catch – so far none of these results have been peer-reviewed, and it can't be ruled out that the thrust isn't the result of some type of experimental error. So for now, we remain skeptical.

But in the meantime, Paul Mach, one of the principal investigators at the Eagleworks Lab, has provided the first public update about their tests on the EM Drive in months, and has admitted that the team has upgraded their experimental protocol and mitigated some of the errors that people were concerned about in prior tests.

"And yet the anomalous thrust signals remain..." Mach [wrote on the NASASpaceFlight forums.](http://forum.nasaspaceflight.com/index.php?topic=38577.msg1440938#msg1440938)

His comment was in response to an unpublished paper, which claimed that the propulsion seen in earlier trials of the EM Drive were a result of Lorentz force – which means the force generated by interactions between the EM Drive and the Earth's magnetic field.

[Mach explains](http://forum.nasaspaceflight.com/index.php?topic=38577.msg1440938#msg1440938) that he can't comment on this work in detail or provide any photos, as his lab is in the process of getting a peer-reviewed paper published, but that he can shed some light on the issue.

“I will tell you that we first built and installed a 2nd generation, closed face magnetic damper that reduced the stray magnetic fields in the vacuum chamber by at least an order of magnitude and any Lorentz force interactions it could produce,” [wrote Mach.](http://forum.nasaspaceflight.com/index.php?topic=38577.msg1440938#msg1440938)

[He also admits](http://forum.nasaspaceflight.com/index.php?topic=38577.msg1440938#msg1440938) that there are still traces of contamination caused by thermal expansion in the system, and the team is now developing an advanced analytics tool to try to work out where this is coming from.

The Em Drive stands for electromagnetic propulsion drive, and the reason it's so controversial is that it defies one of the fundamental concepts of physics – the conservation of momentum, which states that for something to be propelled forward (like a spacecraft), some kind of propellant (like fuel) needs to be pushed out in the opposite direction.

But the EM Drive, which was invented by English researcher Roger Shawyer in the early 2000s, works [in theory by bouncing microwaves back and forth inside](http://www.sciencealert.com/independent-scientists-confirm-that-the-impossible-em-drive-produces-thrust)an enclosed chamber in order to generate thrust.

And so far a range of scientists, including the NASA team, have shown that the engine "is producing a force that is not attributable to any classical electromagnetic phenomenon," as the Eagleworks researchers [wrote in an unpublished paper last year](http://ntrs.nasa.gov/search.jsp?R=20140006052). In other words, it seems to work, but they can't explain why.

So far, this thrust has only been produced in incredibly small amounts, but given the fact that the theoretical system – once scaled up – would be able to power a spacecraft without the need for heavy rocket fuel on board, that's not an issue. The EM Drive is still predicted to be able to carry humans great distances in[incredibly short periods of time.](http://www.nasaspaceflight.com/2015/04/evaluating-nasas-futuristic-em-drive/)

Of course, that's all dependent on the thrust the scientists are detecting being an actual result of the EM Drive's bouncing microwaves, and not an experimental artefact. And despite reports that more and more stringent testing is being conducted to rule this out, we still need to see some results in peer review.

So please, Eagleworks scientists, while we love your interaction on the forums and the feedback, get cracking with the journal articles and those replicable results, we beg of you, because we really would love something concrete to go on sometime soon!

[http://www.sciencealert.com/the-em-...ious-thrust-after-another-round-of-nasa-tests](http://www.sciencealert.com/the-em-drive-still-producing-mysterious-thrust-after-another-round-of-nasa-tests)

# Thread: TV bands for Wifi
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tv-bands-for-wifi.17118/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2015-11-13T08:55:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tv-bands-for-wifi.17118/post-181876>

Came across this one that made me go, 'Crap!'

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2014/11/141124092402.htm](http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2014/11/141124092402.htm)

Takeaways:

"...propose to extend the frequencies for free communication to include lower ranges and even **increased transmission power**."

"These bands are being used less and less for the transmission of TV signals. They are **highly suited for penetrating obstacles such as walls**. Depending on the ambient conditions and by automatically adapting transmission power to prevent interference, such WLAN networks might even reach communication partners at a distance of several kilometers. Even in cities where the transmission capacity will be limited due to the large number of transmission stations, **the range of wireless networks could be extended significantly**."

"Examples besides wireless computer networks are wireless loudspeakers and cameras, remote controls for garage doors, transponders, baby phones, and Bluetooth."

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yes-so-im-a-horses-ass.17129/post-182090>

The importance of reading all blogs!
So, I realized last week that I missed a few very important blogs posts by Jack...and now I know that contact lenses and RX glasses block UV light. I've been gazing at the sun every day in the morning and spending so much time outside for the past 3 years with my contact lenses on. Well, every morning now I drive to the beach wearing my old RX glasses, take them off when I get there, and allow my eyes to get a few hours of sun. What a difference! For the first few days I could hardly even look at the sky! I feel like my whole world has opened up, thank you Jack.

Jack's recent blogs have really been blowing me away...it's all about the sun...so simple. When I think about so many people walking around with RX glasses and contacts in, coupled with EMF, not grounding, being in office environments all day, no wonder everyone is getting so sick!

My new goal is to get RX glasses without any UV coating (does that even exist? I haven't been able to find any companies stating so) and work on improving my eyesight...

Has anyone found any companies with making glasses without the UV coating?

# Thread: Labs and Reactions to Everything
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-and-reactions-to-everything.17147/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### krw2586 — 2015-11-21T22:39:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-and-reactions-to-everything.17147/post-182486>

I was hoping someone could share their thoughts on my labs. Sorry in advance for the long post. I'm just at a loss for what to do!

I've had health issues since 2012 including Lyme, severe chemical sensitivity, toxic mold exposure, methylation issues, heavy metals, etc. I have seen many amazing functional medicine doctors over the years and they have helped some issues, but not most of it. This past February, I had a sudden crash and have not been able to recover. It followed a ketogenic diet experiment, which was suggested for some neurological issues. Ironically, it helped a lot of things so I wish it didn't crash me. At the same time I had also been on way too high of a dose of T3 for supposed "thyroid resistance". I think it damaged something... Anyway the keto diet made me sicker than I have ever felt, I believe due to dumping an excessive toxin load. Since then, my adrenals are completely shot, my POTS has returned, my energy is basically zero, my gastroparesis / paralytic ileus is the worst it's ever been, I don't even tolerate simple vitamins and minerals, and I've been gaining weight rapidly (45 pounds since February after weighing the same (about 105) basically my whole life). I truly don't know what to do. I have even tried a ketogenic diet again, hoping it would undo whatever it did in the first place, but I have a hard time getting into ketosis despite eating the same as I did before. I'm also staying very puffy on a low carb diet - I'm not letting go of excess fluid like I usually do when I enter ketosis. Fluid is a big issue in general - every morning I wake up swollen and my lymph nodes hurt. Once I'm up and moving around, it drains down to my legs and then they feel swollen... if I lay flat my legs feel better, but then my face and underarm lymph nodes swell again. The fluid just shifts. It's also noticeably worse if I react to something, like a food, chemical, or EMFs. I know inflammation is a problem. I can't take electrolytes because they give me instant low blood sugar (I think it's the potassium) and a ragey, burst-into-tears type of adrenaline surge. Minerals do the same thing. Earthing also does the same thing, and so do ionic foot detox baths. I think there is a problem with electrons?? It's a fairly severe and immediate response - within 30 seconds of all of these things.

I also react with major adrenaline, rage, feeling overwhelmed, worse POTS to anything that is supposed to be energizing - like ubiquinol, pqq, vitamin d (and major fluid retention on that one), amino acids, coconut oil, coleus, B6, etc. I've been trying to follow the Leptin Rx, but eating first thing in the morning completely wipes me out even with zero carbs. I feel almost drugged/drunk from it, even taking high dose enzymes and ox bile. And my attempts at Cold Thermogenesis are equally as difficult. I actually like the cold (heat makes my BP way too low and my POTS much worse), but I tend to react sort of extreme after being very cold. Once I'm warm again, I get way too hot, flushed, sweaty, anxious, irritable, worse POTS.... my system tends to overreact to everything. And sunlight gives me migraines with lots of neuro sx like dizziness, slurred speech, vision loss, etc. I feel high from it. Help!

Anyway, here are some labs with their ranges and with notes. Does anything make sense? I have been reading/researching for years and am more than ready to learn. I've just reached a point where I need help. I did recently discover mold in my apartment, but I've lived here for almost 2 years so I'm not sure why it took about a year to crash.... could that be causing all of this?

TSH: 1.31 \[0.45-4.5\] This was suppressed (about 0.04) for about 2 years from thyroid meds. I'm still taking the same dose, but it's suddenly not suppressed anymore... why? I take 1 grain of WP Thyroid plus 25mcg T3 SR. Thyroid labs were done fasting, before taking meds.
Free T4: 0.76 LOW \[0.82-1.77\]
Free T3: 3.0 \[2.0-4.4\]
Reverse T3: 8.9 LOW \[9.2-24.1\]
Cortisol am, fasting: 21.2 \[4.0-22.0\] This is higher than my norm
Aldosterone: 19 \[<= 28\] This used to be high, over 50. Suddenly it dropped and cortisol went up.
Estradiol: 22 \[19-144\] On day 7
Progesterone: 0.1 \[<= 2.7\] On day 7. Very low for me, but I react to progesterone supplementation
Prolactin: 16.6 \[4.8-23.3\] This is a bit higher than normal
Pregnenolone: 55 \[7-188\] I get migraines/seizure activity from pregnenolone supplementation
hs-CRP: 0.2
Insulin: 6.7 \[2.0-19.6\]
DHEA-S: 63 \[18-391\] This is lower than normal
A1c: 5.7 High \[<5.7\]
Vitamin D3 1,25 (OH)2: 53 \[no range given\]
Vitamin D, 25 OH, total: 18 LOW \[30-100\] I've never had low D before
Sodium: 139 \[135-146\]
Potassium: 4.5 \[3.5-5.3\]
Chloride: 104 \[98-110\]
Carbon Dioxide: 21 \[19-30\]
Glucose: 95 \[65-99\]
BUN: 10 \[7-25\]
Creatinine: 0.87 \[0.5-1.1\]
Calcium: 9.9 \[8.6-10.2\] My calciums is frequently a little high
Protein, total: 7.3 \[6.1-8.1\]
Albumin: 4.5 \[3.6-5.1\]
Globulin, calculated: 2.8 \[1.9-3.7\]
Bilirubin, total: 0.3 \[0.2-1.2\]
AST: 34 HIGH \[10-30\] Both AST and ALT are often high
ALT: 23 \[6-29\]
Alkaline Phosphate: 65 \[33-115\]
VIP: 29 \[<75\] This used to be 41.8, it has gotten worse
VEGF: 44 \[31-86\] This used to be 10, so it has gotten better
Leptin: 34.8 HIGH \[4.7-23.7\] This used to be 4.4! Much worse?!
ADH: 0.9 \[0.0-4.7\]
MSH: 18 \[0-40\] This has been low for years

Saliva Cortisol:
6-8am: 8 LOW \[13-24\]
11-1pm: 4 LOW \[5-10\]
4-5pm: 1 LOW \[3-8\]
10-midnight: 1 NORMAL \[1-4\]

THANK YOU THANK YOU THANK YOU to whoever was patient enough to read all of this

### seanb4 — 2016-10-15T11:27:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-and-reactions-to-everything.17147/post-202836>

Any luck with the POTS?

I have been on mirtazapine with limited success ...

### kyrakitty — 2016-10-15T18:52:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-and-reactions-to-everything.17147/post-202853>

I had these exact same issues as you quoted below:

Mine started in 2009 "I've had health issues since 2012 including Lyme, severe chemical sensitivity, toxic mold exposure, methylation issues, heavy metals, etc."

In 2014 like you "I also react with major adrenaline, rage, feeling overwhelmed." It would feel like someone injected epinephrine into me and other times the rage was occurring with little cause and not under my control. It wasn't a mental state something in my environment was messing with the part of the brain that controls rage.

Keto did the same to me "Anyway the keto diet made me sicker than I have ever felt"

It's something in your environment at home or work. The way to test it if you are able is to avoid your home and work area for a minimum of a week by staying somewhere else that is clean. You could try to book a space for a week or two that is specifically for people with mold, EMF and chemical sensitivities. If you feel better then you have your answer.

When the above was happening to me it was my environment. When I would leave for many days I would get better when I returned I got worse.

CT, keto or any diet, supplements, medications won't help if your environment is bad.

Also I had... and still struggle with too much blue light exposure from computers and phones and hardly spent enough sun. I am currently working on figuring out how to get more sun during the right times for my latitude and how to use supplemental lights to give my body more uvb, uva, red and infrared light spectrums. I haven't figured it out yet but this is necessary along with living and working in a safe clean environment.

### Huck — 2016-10-15T20:08:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-and-reactions-to-everything.17147/post-202862>

Keto was making me sick, although I didn't realize it for years. I recently found in my 23andme genetics lab, that I likely had a problem digesting saturated fat and metabolizing fat. What was happening to me was that after a few weeks on keto diet, I would start having blood pressure spikes and heartburn which mimicked heart problems. Docs kept prescribing more and more meds.

If you are not digesting food properly, all kind of bad things can happen, with inflammation at the top of the list. Plus it can lead to bad bacteria getting into the blood stream.

I decided on my own to cut way back on saturated fats. Immediately the heartburn stopped, blood pressure spikes completely disappeared over the next 3 weeks, inflammation went way down, back pain went away, I started losing weight at a rapid pace, I reduced my bp meds, my PSA dropped from 33 to 10 in 3 weeks (which is an indicator of reduced inflammation.

I have taken thousands of dollars worth of supplements over the years with no improvement in my health. When you throw in insurance and doctors fees, labs, etc I'm guessing the amount is close to $75,000 without a single doctor having a clue.

Now that I have reduced sat fat, I can tell some of the supplements are doing what they are supposed to do. I find I can't eat much coconut oil, eggs, bacon, etc that are high in sat fat or symptoms start coming back. I can use a little coconut oil to cook, but no more eating 2 or 3 tbsp a day.

I'm not saying your problem is saturated fat, but that looking at your genetics might lead to the clue you need to figure out what is causing your problems. For example, my 23andme report also showed I likely had trouble converting sunlight to vitamin d and might have to supplement at higher doses than most people. I already knew that - I take 10,000 IUs in summer and 15,000 IUs in winter, plus get as much sun as I possibly can. If I don't do that, my vitamin d drops below 30.

Hopefully, when we regain our health, our epigenetics will overrule our genes. But when we are in an unhealthy state, our genes may be running things.

ETA: The keto diet and Adrenalin were what got my attention in op. When I was having the BP spikes, my heart rate would jump up to 135, which I assumed was accompanied by a surge in Adrenalin. It would stay there for hours and then slowly come down. Now my heart rate varies from 65 to 85 over the day - higher when exercising.
