>
> I've had health issues since 2012 including Lyme, severe chemical sensitivity, toxic mold exposure, methylation issues, heavy metals, etc. I have seen many amazing functional medicine doctors over the years and they have helped some issues, but not most of it. This past February, I had a sudden crash and have not been able to recover. It followed a ketogenic diet experiment, which was suggested for some neurological issues. Ironically, it helped a lot of things so I wish it didn't crash me. At the same time I had also been on way too high of a dose of T3 for supposed "thyroid resistance". I think it damaged something... Anyway the keto diet made me sicker than I have ever felt, I believe due to dumping an excessive toxin load. Since then, my adrenals are completely shot, my POTS has returned, my energy is basically zero, my gastroparesis / paralytic ileus is the worst it's ever been, I don't even tolerate simple vitamins and minerals, and I've been gaining weight rapidly (45 pounds since February after weighing the same (about 105) basically my whole life). I truly don't know what to do. I have even tried a ketogenic diet again, hoping it would undo whatever it did in the first place, but I have a hard time getting into ketosis despite eating the same as I did before. I'm also staying very puffy on a low carb diet - I'm not letting go of excess fluid like I usually do when I enter ketosis. Fluid is a big issue in general - every morning I wake up swollen and my lymph nodes hurt. Once I'm up and moving around, it drains down to my legs and then they feel swollen... if I lay flat my legs feel better, but then my face and underarm lymph nodes swell again. The fluid just shifts. It's also noticeably worse if I react to something, like a food, chemical, or EMFs. I know inflammation is a problem. I can't take electrolytes because they give me instant low blood sugar (I think it's the potassium) and a ragey, burst-into-tears type of adrenaline surge. Minerals do the same thing. Earthing also does the same thing, and so do ionic foot detox baths. I think there is a problem with electrons?? It's a fairly severe and immediate response - within 30 seconds of all of these things.
>
> I also react with major adrenaline, rage, feeling overwhelmed, worse POTS to anything that is supposed to be energizing - like ubiquinol, pqq, vitamin d (and major fluid retention on that one), amino acids, coconut oil, coleus, B6, etc. I've been trying to follow the Leptin Rx, but eating first thing in the morning completely wipes me out even with zero carbs. I feel almost drugged/drunk from it, even taking high dose enzymes and ox bile. And my attempts at Cold Thermogenesis are equally as difficult. I actually like the cold (heat makes my BP way too low and my POTS much worse), but I tend to react sort of extreme after being very cold. Once I'm warm again, I get way too hot, flushed, sweaty, anxious, irritable, worse POTS.... my system tends to overreact to everything. And sunlight gives me migraines with lots of neuro sx like dizziness, slurred speech, vision loss, etc. I feel high from it. Help!
>
> Anyway, here are some labs with their ranges and with notes. Does anything make sense? I have been reading/researching for years and am more than ready to learn. I've just reached a point where I need help. I did recently discover mold in my apartment, but I've lived here for almost 2 years so I'm not sure why it took about a year to crash.... could that be causing all of this?
>
> TSH: 1.31 \[0.45-4.5\] This was suppressed (about 0.04) for about 2 years from thyroid meds. I'm still taking the same dose, but it's suddenly not suppressed anymore... why? I take 1 grain of WP Thyroid plus 25mcg T3 SR. Thyroid labs were done fasting, before taking meds.
> Free T4: 0.76 LOW \[0.82-1.77\]
> Free T3: 3.0 \[2.0-4.4\]
> Reverse T3: 8.9 LOW \[9.2-24.1\]
> Cortisol am, fasting: 21.2 \[4.0-22.0\] This is higher than my norm
> Aldosterone: 19 \[<= 28\] This used to be high, over 50. Suddenly it dropped and cortisol went up.
> Estradiol: 22 \[19-144\] On day 7
> Progesterone: 0.1 \[<= 2.7\] On day 7. Very low for me, but I react to progesterone supplementation
> Prolactin: 16.6 \[4.8-23.3\] This is a bit higher than normal
> Pregnenolone: 55 \[7-188\] I get migraines/seizure activity from pregnenolone supplementation
> hs-CRP: 0.2
> Insulin: 6.7 \[2.0-19.6\]
> DHEA-S: 63 \[18-391\] This is lower than normal
> A1c: 5.7 High \[<5.7\]

> Vitamin D, 25 OH, total: 18 LOW \[30-100\] I've never had low D before

> Potassium: 4.5 \[3.5-5.3\]
> Chloride: 104 \[98-110\]

> ALT: 23 \[6-29\]

> VIP: 29 \[<75\] This used to be 41.8, it has gotten worse
> VEGF: 44 \[31-86\] This used to be 10, so it has gotten better
> Leptin: 34.8 HIGH \[4.7-23.7\] This used to be 4.4! Much worse?!

> 6-8am: 8 LOW \[13-24\]
> 11-1pm: 4 LOW \[5-10\]
> 4-5pm: 1 LOW \[3-8\]
> 10-midnight: 1 NORMAL \[1-4\]
>
> THANK YOU THANK YOU THANK YOU to whoever was patient enough to read all of this

**ADH: 0.9 \[0.0-4.7\]**

Also known as: Vasopressin; AVP
Formal name: Antidiuretic Hormone; Arginine Vasopressin
Related tests: Osmolality, BUN, Creatinine, Sodium, Urinalysis

ADH deficiency is often seen with one of two types of diabetes insipidus. Central diabetes insipidus is a decrease in the production of ADH by the hypothalamus or in the release of ADH from the pituitary while nephrogenic diabetes insipidus is characterized by a decrease in the kidney's response to ADH. Both types of diabetes insipidus lead to the excretion of large quantities of dilute urine.

diabetes insipidus results from a lack of production of arginine vasopressin (also called antidiuretic hormone or ADH) by the pituitary or
a lack of response of the kidneys to ADH, .......

**DDAVP® Nasal Spray** (desmopressin acetate)

**DDAVP Nasal Spray (desmopressin acetate nasal spray)** is provided as an aqueous solution for intranasal use.

\====================================================

Saliva Cortisol:
6-8am: 8 LOW \[13-24\]
Cortisol,serum am, fasting: 21.2 \[4.0-22.0\]

High totalcortisol when free cortisol is low=high TBG
Thyroid Binding globulin

Elevated TBG levels are associated with influences such as pregnancy, genetic predisposition, oral contraceptives, and estrogen therapy. TBG levels can decrease with androgenic or anabolic steroids, large doses of glucocorticoids, hypoproteinemic states, liver disease, nephrotic syndrome, and congenital TBG variants.

\======================================================

### krw2586 — 2017-04-05T20:22:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-and-reactions-to-everything.17147/post-211822>

Thanks for the responses! I wasn't getting alerts until today so I'm sorry for missing some of the older replies.

Since the first post I moved out of that apartment and have slowly (painfully slowly!) improved. I think my body had an actual nervous system breakdown. I weaned off my thyroid meds and that helped a lot. I basically had to take away all stress, including meds and supplements. My new home has low RF/EMF readings and I still shut everything down at night. There are lots of windows so my artificial light exposure is much less. And I have been taking extremely small doses of cholestyramine for mold exposure (it binds to toxins). It has helped a lot, but I don't want to stay on it for a long time because it can lower cholesterol and thus interfere with hormones and fat soluble vitamins.

I am now drinking only spring water (so no chlorine or fluoride) and I add sea salt to it, which helps the extreme thirst. I drink about 80-90 oz a day. But last time my ADH was checked it had dropped to 0.5.

I actually feel better eating LESS protein, and it has helped with the ammonia smell in my sweat. I still eat one serving a day and it's usually seafood (mostly shrimp because fish causes histamine reactions).

I still have issues with electron donors. Sun, IR light (mostly just near IR), grounding, and certain supplements can crash me quickly. I'm going to try the very very gradual sun approach.

The saturated fat thing is interesting. I realized I also wasn't handling fat well and cut back on it. I need to look at my 23andMe results and see if I can figure out why. Coconut oil seems to be extremely hard for me to take. It gives me immediate low blood sugar and after some research I discovered that it actually triggers the release of insulin (somewhat strongly). So I think my whole issue has something to do with the interplay between insulin and cortisol. Just recently I tried taking Lauricidin (an MCT) for a gut infection and even an extremely low dose (2 pellets with each meal) messed with my blood sugar and insulin and made my weight jump up. A few years ago I took Lauricidin with no problems, and I was taking 30 pellets with each meal! So it's very odd.

I still cannot take any hormone supplements or vitamin d supplements (vit d supps shut down my kidneys and I stop peeing- even as low as 40 ius a day). And my weight/puffiness is still being stubborn. Oddly, coming off of high dose thyroid meds made no change in my weight at all. Whatever is going on feels bigger than thyroid, like there is an even greater controller of metabolism.

Hopefully if I can start to slowly get more sun and maybe do some very light strength exercises to help any possible insulin resistance, more of this will turn around.

Like you,...I have been plagued by Biotoxin mold illness, lyme and its coinfections for over 40 years and I just learned that this was the root cause of my chronic fatigue all these years.

I also have the CBS mutation which affects my sulfur metabolism and my detox pathways. Like you , i cannot handle coconut oil or coconuts. I believe the reason why you cannot handle them is because you may have sulfur metabolism issue as well that will negatively affect your detox pathways. Because of my CBS mutation I need to limit sulfur containing foods (ketogenic diet makes me ill because of the over consumption of sulfur rich foods) and limit supplements like MSM, NAC, Milk thistle, chlorella, ALA, Garlic, Glutathione, HCL.

By what you mentioned, I believe that you may have the same sulfur issue because you mentioned in a previous post that your sweat smells like ammonia and that is a good sign of sulfur metabolism issues as well.

First off, I believe that the Mold illness needs to be adressed first and foremost. In my opinion, my mold symptoms have been worse than the lyme. The mold issue needs to be addressed ASAP with a super agressive approach especially if you have the HLA Dr1 gene, which I also have. You should have had the full labs done from both Labcorp and quest, and the Marcons should have been competed as well. You should know your VCS (vision contrast test) results so you have that benchmark to go by.

One reason you may feel ill when you eat fat is because of the release of bile will be loaded with mycotoxins. If you don't bind them, as you know they are going to be reabsorbed and cause a cascade of inflammation throughout the body. You could also do the NeuroQuant MRI like i have to see the extent of inflammation in the brain. this can tell you if its Mold related or lyme related.

I believe that you need to follow the protocol to the fullest extent with labs along the way to chart your progress. Just found a good place that seems to have all the right things...price is excellent and would allow me to stay a member, homeschool my kids next year like I hope to, while paying down our bills (student loans mostly) and it's low nnEMF, great yard for grounding/CT/fire pit/garden etc, low stress for hubs commute.

# Thread: Sea water - deadly or just another myth?

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-11-27T08:31:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sea-water-deadly-or-just-another-myth.17161/post-182736>

It seems to me that there is a pattern emerging -almost everything that we are taught about in health ends up being vague or just dead wrong. (Lack of UV light for instance seems to be at the root of almost all chronic illness.)
Since our ancestors in the East African Rift lived off of seafood and probably spent most of their time in the water wouldn't it make sense that they probably could drink it too (?). I really can't see them hunting for a fresh water creek everytime they got thirsty.
Or maybe they just absorbed water through their skin(?) I've seen sea gulls gulp the shit down like no tomorrow..and that really got me thinking that , just like the ability to tolerate cold is in us, maybe our ability to handle salt water is too.

Maybe people die because of an intense Herx reaction. Any thoughts? Possible biohack?

(Or is this idea just ridiculous)

Bad idea.
Go on the boat, at least few miles away from the shore, get your water there.
The idea is to collect clean sea water from large depths, because, hopefully, it is clean.
\-----------------------------------------------------------------------
There is a skimming effect in places where water meets land.
Sea is getting rid of its debris that way.
Aquarium skimmers works using this premise.

\====================================

### Lahelada — 2015-11-30T12:53:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-challenged-opportunities-for-winter-sailing.17174/post-182875>

For people reasonably mobile this might be an opportunity to get away from EMF and closer to UV latitudes. If you have no sailing experience there are still opportunities but you need to look a b. it harder. If you can't get away you may think about a weekend course/charter to maximise the UV that you can still obtain.

[www.sailingnetworks.com/boats/needed](http://www.sailingnetworks.com/boats/needed)

[https://www.crewseekers.net/sailingopp.aspx](https://www.crewseekers.net/sailingopp.aspx)

You can also just approach your local yacht club. Rail meat is often needed and yes you will need to learn what that means...

PS: a shameless plug If you are coming to Buenos Aires we can arrange Kruse compliant outings for you

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/concern-about-cold-showers.17176/post-182894>

I have been taking three minutes cold showers in the morning's and evenings. I seem to be developing very mild Raynaud's syndrome and not sure if it's related or not. Many times after a cold shower, a couple of my fingers are totally white and feel ice cold and the other fingers are red. Not sure if that's something I should be concerned about or not.

Also, my skin in general is quite a bit drier and wrinkled. Again, I'm not sure if this is some negative effect from the cold showers or something different that's going on with me altogether.

Thanks so much.

# Thread: Transcendental Meditation and lifestyle modification increase telomerase, new study finds
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transcendental-meditation-and-lifestyle-modification-increase-telomerase-new-study-finds.17191/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lilreddgirl — 2015-12-03T03:40:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transcendental-meditation-and-lifestyle-modification-increase-telomerase-new-study-finds.17191/post-183123>

"Increased telomerase associated with decreased hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and cellular aging

MAHARISHI UNIVERSITY OF MANAGEMENT

A new study published in [_PLOS ONE_](http://journals.plos.org/plosone/article%3Fid=10.1371/journal.pone.0142689) found that the Transcendental Meditation technique and lifestyle changes both appear to stimulate genes that produce telomerase, an enzyme that's associated with reduced blood pressure, cardiovascular disease, and mortality.

Specifically, these approaches were found to activate two genes that code for telomerase, which adds molecules to the ends of chromosomes, or telomeres, protecting them from deteriorating.

May explain mechanism for known health benefits

"The finding that telomerase gene expression is increased, and that this is associated with a reduction in blood pressure in a high-risk population, suggests that this may be a mechanism by which stress reduction improves cardiovascular health," said Robert Schneider, MD, FACC, coauthor of the study.

Earlier research on the Transcendental Meditation technique found lower rates of high blood pressure, heart attack, stroke, and death, as well as slowing of biological aging. The new study examined what was happening at the level of DNA, showing that the Transcendental Meditation technique increases telomerase gene expression and suggesting that this may contribute to the cardiovascular and aging benefits.

Both groups show improvement after 16 weeks

For this pilot trial, the subjects included 48 men and women with high blood pressure who were recruited and studied at Howard University Medical Center. Half were assigned to a group that learned the Transcendental Meditation technique and received a basic health education course. The other half were assigned to a group that focused on achieving significant lifestyle modifications such as weight reduction, reducing salt intake, engaging in regular physical activity, and moderating alcohol. They also participated in support groups and group exercises.

After 16 weeks, both groups showed significant increases in telomerase gene expression and reductions in blood pressure. There was no significant difference between the changes in the two groups. The results also showed that the lifestyle modification group made a larger number of changes in their lifestyle behaviors.

"These findings are very encouraging for prevention," said Dr. Schneider, director of the Institute for Natural Medicine and Prevention at MUM. "They show that both the Transcendental Meditation technique and active lifestyle modification can contribute to heart health."

Coauthor Otelio Randall, MD at Howard University College of Medicine concluded, "This pilot study in African Americans suggests stress reduction and lifestyle modifications may reduce blood pressure with an increase in telomerase."

Encouraging findings for heart health

"The result is valuable new information, relevant both to cardiovascular disease and to the molecular mechanisms involved in Transcendental Meditation," said John Fagan, professor of molecular biology at Maharishi University of Management and senior author on the study.

###

This research was part of a larger clinical trial funded by the National Institutes of Health--National Heart Lung and Blood Institute and by the Howard University College of Medicine General Clinical Research Center.

Collaborating on the study were the Howard University College of Medicine, the Center for Natural Medicine and Prevention at Maharishi University of Management Research Institute, and the Department of Physiology and Health at Maharishi University of Management.

Duraimani S, Schneider RH, Randall OS, Nidich SI, Xu S, Ketete M, et al. (2015) Effects of Lifestyle Modification on Telomerase Gene Expression in Hypertensive Patients: A Pilot Trial of Stress Reduction and Health Education Programs in African Americans. _PLOS ONE_ 10(11): e0142689. doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0142689"

[http://www.eurekalert.org/pub\_releases/2015-12/muom-tma120215.php](http://www.eurekalert.org/pub_releases/2015-12/muom-tma120215.php)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

### Lahelada — 2015-12-07T14:23:49+0200

I think ylou should read with red. Candles seem bright for their yellow light. Yellow is "brighter" than red. Look up colour temperatures.your eyes are optimised for the yellow green spectrum but also for hitting the ground like a log and go to sleep when it gets dark. Reading is a neolithic activity,its only saving grace being that it does not use the same intensity of light insult as for example a videogame on a mega led screen. It is an insult nevertheless. Hence why I read with red ,visible but closest to the frequency my eyes should be exposed to at night,so as to interfere as little as possible with its repair such as there is.
TIME 4 says this :

1.  **_Don’t work on bio-hacking sleep._ Bio hack your light environment……….you are missing darkness at night with too much blue light and allowing way too much blue during day then you have red light to balance it. Perspective change is in order. I want you to focus in on eliminating your “alien sun’s” at night 100% of the time. Once completed, your sleep returns and your regeneration begins. UV with IR are the frequencies of rebuilding and regenerating your eye clock.**

**If you fail in getting natural sunlight could you augment yourself artificially like a hydroponic plant does? Could you consider using a quantum wearable device like [the Quantlet](http://thequantlet.com/)? Yes you could."**

### Scompy — 2015-12-07T19:53:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-bad-for-your-eyes-to-read-in-light-that-is-not-full-spectrum.17206/post-183627>

> [Paleodocteur said:](/goto/post?id=183530)
>
> Red incandescent I used until I realized the unpleasant feeling I had was actually correlated to voltmeter readings
>
> I prefer candle but I'd leave the window open (VOCs).
> However one candle now feels really bright and in itself seems to be enough to delay sleep onset. But I got readings to do, so I chose to phase delay a bit ( about 2 hours) in winter.

This is a great point of about VOCs. My son has a reactive lung issue where if we light a fireplace, light candles, if he's around anyone with heavy shampoo, hairspray or if we wash his clothes in phthalates, it can trigger coughing. The cure is sunlight for him and this condition, as even in the past 3 months getting him into more light, his lungs have shown a great deal of improvement.

### WereBear — 2015-12-07T22:27:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-bad-for-your-eyes-to-read-in-light-that-is-not-full-spectrum.17206/post-183640>

On those rare occasions I get caught up with something and forget my sunset sunglasses, I put them on and within 30 minutes I'm naturally sleepy for the time period. Plus sleeping more now that we are so close to the solstice.

# Thread: Merry Christmas
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/merry-christmas.17234/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-12-12T22:03:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/merry-christmas.17234/post-183969>

Well, I had my first two Christmas cards this morning—and an unexpected present from a very kind friend. She had visited the States and brought back a bottle of General Biotics Equilibrium probiotic (which has 115 different organisms in it):

[http://www.generalbiotics.com](http://www.generalbiotics.com)

So that got me thinking that maybe it was time that I wished others a Merry Christmas and Happy New Year. And so why not the people here?

I'm not sure about that. I think I can be abrasive, and I probably wore out my welcome here a long time ago.

Something else I realise I don't know: how to convince anyone who is not already convinced of the uses of poetry. But …

But here is my seasonal gift. It's Alec Guinness reading a medieval lyric. Maybe it can speak for itself. I like this partly for the way he shapes the words—I think they fall softly and slowly like snow on a perfect winter's day and I hardly know how anyone could do anything like this better. The lyric is "I syng of a mayden".

_makeles_ is Middle English for "matchless"

and

_ches_ is "chose"

The video is from YouTube. The graphic is a pre-Raphaelite painting of Ophelia (by Millais) and is actually of the Hogsmill River in Worcester Park, Surrey. IOW, the painting is totally irrelevant. But the audio is good, very good.

### fitness@home — 2015-12-13T00:07:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/merry-christmas.17234/post-183972>

Merry Christmas to you Gagnrad!

I somehow missed the drama

### caroline — 2015-12-13T00:14:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/merry-christmas.17234/post-183974>

Merry Christmas Mike !..... I have been missing you and , I guess, I missed all the drama too!

How are you? And you do know that I love poetry - you helped me buy a book long time ago....

### Gagnrad — 2015-12-13T10:18:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/merry-christmas.17234/post-183987>

Thank you that's so kind, Audrey.

I wish you, fitness@home, and anyone else reading this a happy, peaceful and rewarding Christmas and New Year. And since you like poetry, too, here's a curiosity I found and photographed yesterday. It's a copy of Shakespeare's Sonnet 116 and it's been printed on handmade paper by a tiny little printing shop and put in their window.

What beautiful language!

"… an ever-fixed mark,
That looks on tempests, and is never shaken …"

(See uploaded attachment.)

### caroline — 2015-12-13T22:45:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/merry-christmas.17234/post-184035>

That is so lovely Mike .....thanks for posting/attaching that beautiful poem.

### notsoperfick — 2015-12-20T15:21:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/merry-christmas.17234/post-184575>

I didn't think anyone else in the world except me tried their best to avoid killing anything. Life is precious even to the lowliest and tiniest of creatures, we would all do well to remember that.

### Yowanas — 2015-12-15T17:47:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-management.17248/post-184202>

nnEMF effects management for those of us who can't change zipcodes yet

First identifying nnEMF effects. Per Becker, emf induces a stress response, read cortisol. So, I teach in an electronics lab with wifi repeaters, cell phone emitters, and dozens of computers and blue light insanity. I go into the lab calm and relaxed. Then one day I noticed coming out that I am totally stressed. But it was a good day. Congrats, my boy, you are your own builtin trifield meter.

So now i have a measure for emf overload, how do I reduce it. First i was hungry for chocolate, good chocolate (Mg). Hmmmm, smart body. Calcium channel blocker. Then add gaia adrenal health combination, and dried adrenals. Sit on 30 gauss magnetico for the evening and hug my heartmath Hrv sensor. Read more printouts of my favorite QED blogs. System calms down and blood pressure improves.

Please springtime return. Need sun and grounding.

Destin Beach anyone? After Christmas day? Camping, oysters and quantlet talks?

# Thread: Breast screening. Is ultrasound a good way? Pls help with some ideas!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breast-screening-is-ultrasound-a-good-way-pls-help-with-some-ideas.17255/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Daniel — 2015-12-16T13:21:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breast-screening-is-ultrasound-a-good-way-pls-help-with-some-ideas.17255/post-184269>

My wife found a knot in her breast today which wasn't there before and we'd like to make sure what it is. We have an appointment to have ultrasound inspection on 8th January. Is there anything in the meantime which can help us figure out something more?

I was thinking to have a bloodwork, and check CEA, CA 15-3 and CA125 (unfortunately CA 27-29 is not available here).

Is there anything else we could check?

Since it's on the sides of her breast close to the bones and muscles, it feels like a muscle but not in the proper position. I don't know much about this, can you please help with some ideas?

Regardless of what it is, I got some ideas from here [https://www.jackkruse.com/what-to-do-with-new-cancer-diagnosis/](https://www.jackkruse.com/what-to-do-with-new-cancer-diagnosis/) to boost her immune system. A little background info. We eat a primal kind of diet, mostly beef, mutton, wild meat and trying to eat more and more fish/seafood, plus green vegetables, squash, avocadoes etc... Pretty much real food, very few processed stuff. I'm in ketosis, she sticks with low carb and eats a little rice here and there. We live in Budapest, winter time, no UV or any other light outside. EMF is 40-ish everywhere (radio towers, 3G-4G). Exercise 3-4 times a week (weight training, crossfit and yoga for her), and go hiking on weekends.

# Thread: Co Q10 - caps or liquid form? + Best natural sources...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/co-q10-caps-or-liquid-form-best-natural-sources.17258/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Daniel — 2015-12-17T18:14:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/co-q10-caps-or-liquid-form-best-natural-sources.17258/post-184342>

What is better form of Q10 supplementation, capsules or liquid? Any brand you suggest, you trust?

Seems like Chicken heart and Beef heart is the best source, but does cooking destroys it? Not sure I could convince my wife to eat it raw

### Inger — 2015-12-17T18:32:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/co-q10-caps-or-liquid-form-best-natural-sources.17258/post-184346>

It is pretty heat stable so your wife will still get Q10 even if she heats it  but many other benefits will diminish... like the B vitamins.... and bacteria..... etc etc
I would try to cook it as gentle as you can to save the most nutrients. I mostly eat lambs hearts raw but sometime I cut them up in slices and heat them gently in a little butter and heavily spiced with curcumin and black pepper and a little sea salt.. I just fry them on low heat for a minute or so.... totally delicious.. tastes like amazing sausage to me but just better...lol

She could cook them moist with cabbage too.... lamb with cabbage like a stew is delicious too

I mostly make lamb heart carpaccio

### Inger — 2015-12-17T18:34:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/co-q10-caps-or-liquid-form-best-natural-sources.17258/post-184347>

I think eating gently cooked hearts of grass fed animals wins over supplement any time
I personally like lamb hearts the most

### nikita — 2015-12-18T15:15:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decreased-hunger.17265/post-184428>

I am about to enter my third trimester and astonishgly i have very little hunger.
i eat breakfast at 8.00am and lunch at around 1.00pm, usually shellfish and rice,since we don't get oysters here  and after that i just don't feel like eating anything.
The uv index in navi mumbai india is 7 and i am getting direct exposure from 11 to 12am.
This is amazing.

### TerrierMom — 2015-12-19T02:13:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decreased-hunger.17265/post-184483>

I, while not pregnant, have also found that when I get serious sunlight my hunger is greatly diminished. When I am indoors all day or exposed to too much blue light (indoor office environment) I want to eat sooooo much more. Even then, if I have my big seafood breakfast, I am usually only mildly hungry by 4 pm

### John Smith — 2015-12-21T05:53:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-start-hiit-now.17278/post-184663>

i have been eating a low carb paleo for the last 6 months and have done the leptin reset (not strictly-I could not finish dinner before 8 pm for quite a few days) for about two months. I was underweight since starting off the leptin reset and now I have a BMI of 21. (I gained weight very quickly).

Currently
I have no carbohydrate cravings even in the middle of summer.
My face skin tone and color looks nice
I do not sweat that much. Never profusely.
I eat lunch although I do not feel hungry at that time.

Since I have gained weight I also gained quite a bit of body fat (Some fat in the belly region)
I have been taking cold showers (10 minutes) as well but I have not done full CT yet.

Do I need to first do a reverse t3 test and hs-crp before doing HIIT body weight training? I am in Australia so it is hard to do these tests without a doctor. I am worried that my AMPk pathways are still not working and starting HIIT training will make things worse.

# Thread: quervain's tenosynovitis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quervains-tenosynovitis.17311/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sami — 2015-12-29T17:31:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quervains-tenosynovitis.17311/post-185077>

Hi Dr Jack,

Recently I was diagnosed with the quervain tenosynovitis, i went to seek a second opinion and came with a new diesease called as Carpal Tunnel syndrome,
however my problem was that i could not fold my thumb into the wrist and move the wrist towards down.
the pain is horrible i couldnt tolerate and then i read some of your threads and started CT, initially it did help, but whenever the arm temperature returned normal, acute pain used to start.
I got some blood tests also done, ESR is around 25 uric acid is high, but my diet does not include too much fruits, i eat mainly sea food , then i found that the ratio of BUN/creatinine was around 15, i had a history of kidney stones \[colic\], i increased my water intake, i did less work with my laptop\[ unfortunately backlog started\].
until one day I stumbled upon a friend who knew Sujok, i told him my problems, he took my hand that had pain, he took my index finger and in the first joint of the finger from the tip, did a kind of massage for around 40 seconds, and then he asked me if there is pain in the wrist? to my astonishment the pain was gone and i felt like there was some tenderness in the area where it was paining.
I noticed that as the hour was passing by, my pain slowly was returning, now it has come to a state of pain which can be managed 40% still remains, iam continuing with CT, loads of Bcomplex, curcumin, sea food, vitamin D3, .
will update once i become better.

### Sami — 2015-12-29T17:33:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quervains-tenosynovitis.17311/post-185078>

But what wonders me is how does Sujok work and where does it fit in the quilt?

### Sami — 2016-01-01T09:31:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quervains-tenosynovitis.17311/post-185219>

Hi Dr Jack,

any suggestions, any thoughts !!!

Regards

# Thread: specific bands of UV best?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/specific-bands-of-uv-best.17317/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aerose91 — 2016-01-01T00:15:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/specific-bands-of-uv-best.17317/post-185185>

The sun being best is a given, but are there particular bands of UV that are most beneficial, such as particular wavelengths of red? I know black lights are a very specific wavelngth of UVA, are there better bands of UV to "supplement" with during times of less sun?

### twilightkid — 2016-01-01T01:17:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/specific-bands-of-uv-best.17317/post-185187>

for supplementing there is no one "best" range of light. It comes down to context and what it is "best" for what your looking for.
UV-B 280-315nm is for making sulfated vitamin D
UV-A 315-400nm is good for thyroid
Water in the cell acts as both a battery and a motor (the work of biochemistry)
UV is what makes the motor in water – Red light makes the battery (ez).
It would be "best" to use both UVB,UVA, red light and infrared with correct timing in my opinion.

### Aerose91 — 2016-01-01T01:31:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/specific-bands-of-uv-best.17317/post-185189>

I know theres no substitute for that, im just wondering which bands come to the top when used artificially; such as the quantlet or use of black lights

### lilreddgirl — 2016-01-01T05:24:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/specific-bands-of-uv-best.17317/post-185209>

> [twilightkid said:](/goto/post?id=185187)
>
> for supplementing there is no one "best" range of light. It comes down to context and what it is "best" for what your looking for.
> UV-B 280-315nm is for making sulfated vitamin D
> UV-A 315-400nm is good for thyroid
> Water in the cell acts as both a battery and a motor (the work of biochemistry)
> UV is what makes the motor in water – Red light makes the battery (ez).
> It would be "best" to use both UVB,UVA, red light and infrared with correct timing in my opinion.

Thank you for your clarity! Could you please give an example of correct timing of use...

### SpiritGuide — 2016-01-04T19:54:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/specific-bands-of-uv-best.17317/post-185407>

After researching this recently my wife and I just purchased the Duracell CFL Ultraviolet light bulb at Batteries Plus along with several full-spectrum bulbs to use throughout the house.

The guy at Batteries Plus was shocked we would spend so much on just a handful of bulbs, so we told him what we were doing... very interesting conversation indeed. Hopefully we opened his eyes to what is possible... good luck!

### thomas — 2016-01-05T21:30:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/specific-bands-of-uv-best.17317/post-185490>

No better band, just get both uva and uvb consistently. consistency is king to me.

# Thread: Blacklights far away due to nnEMF... but does Solar Glo have similar nnEMF issues?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blacklights-far-away-due-to-nnemf-but-does-solar-glo-have-similar-nnemf-issues.17323/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lilreddgirl — 2016-01-02T21:37:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blacklights-far-away-due-to-nnemf-but-does-solar-glo-have-similar-nnemf-issues.17323/post-185296>

I've read on forum Dr. Kruse recommending the use of blacklights from across the room, as the desired effect is still going on, yet it avoids nnEMF generated by the bulbs...

here: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/uva-uvb-light.17231/#post-184585](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/uva-uvb-light.17231/#post-184585)

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=184588)
>
> The distance from the light is immaterial for the effect.......but to get away from light for nnEMF reasons is get the light as far from you as possible. To get your specific answers Eilee you need a journal and likely to get on the member Q & A's.

A member mentioned that nnEMF is around 100mG AC magnetic I assume right close to the bulb...

Is this EMF an issue with blacklights in particular?

Is it ok to use the other bulbs such as Solar Glo 160's, Exo Terra's 200 (a mainly UVB bulb that one could try to hack at close distance for Vitamin D production) close up? Or is it just a trade-off between Vitamin-D production and EMF exposure ?

I assume there is not an EMF issue with non CFL bulbs like LED's...

### nonchalant — 2016-01-03T01:06:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blacklights-far-away-due-to-nnemf-but-does-solar-glo-have-similar-nnemf-issues.17323/post-185312>

LED's have transformers to change the voltage. They bother me, but I am able to tolerate an LED blacklight when it is on the far side of the room.

### lilreddgirl — 2016-01-04T03:23:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blacklights-far-away-due-to-nnemf-but-does-solar-glo-have-similar-nnemf-issues.17323/post-185376>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=185312)
>
> LED's have transformers to change the voltage. They bother me, but I am able to tolerate an LED blacklight when it is on the far side of the room.

Thanks! Gosh complications... can you tell me more, I'm not very tech savvy on this. What are the implications of the LED's having transformers? Does that mean they put out more nnEMF?

How does that compare to a CFL bulb ?

In what way do they bother you?

Is this exclusive to the blacklights or are the Red LEDs also a problem? I think I read of some using the Red LED's directly on skin to heal...

### nonchalant — 2016-01-06T01:43:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blacklights-far-away-due-to-nnemf-but-does-solar-glo-have-similar-nnemf-issues.17323/post-185513>

They put out some electrical and magnetic EMF, like an appliance would. They cause ringing in my ears, but not as bad as the CFLs do. The CFLs cause huge spikes on the meter. I also use red LEDs, but only for a few minutes close up. The blacklight LEDs tend to stay on for hours.

### lilreddgirl — 2016-01-06T05:16:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blacklights-far-away-due-to-nnemf-but-does-solar-glo-have-similar-nnemf-issues.17323/post-185540>

Thanks! So the LED's are still better for you than the CFL's as far as triggering symptoms...

I am noticing some symptoms (a body tension I have) from using the Solar Glo, I am EMF sensitive in this way... but it could also just be the strong light (the blue? or just strong light asking my body to create hormones and thus causing some 'stress' ? ) that triggers the symptoms. I'm still using it as I'm also noticing benefits...

I also noticed EMF symptoms from using the CFL blacklight bulb close up, no symptoms when I moved it over 3 feet away.

# Thread: Eating RAW fish from your own freezer?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-raw-fish-from-your-own-freezer.17350/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lilreddgirl — 2016-01-08T07:58:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-raw-fish-from-your-own-freezer.17350/post-185734>

Is anyone here eating their fish raw?

Are you buying it frozen and then thawing it out ?

I eat sushi and sashimi when out, but because I've never done it at home I feel hesitant to eat the fish raw from my own freezer. I've been reading online and am concerned about parasites...

I'm buying supermarket salmon that has already been cut and vacuum sealed. It's in my freezer which says that the temperature is -6 Farenheit.

Is this safe to thaw and eat raw? Is anyone else doing this? Are parasites a concern?

### cygnario — 2016-01-08T09:58:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-raw-fish-from-your-own-freezer.17350/post-185736>

For me, I've always eaten most of my fish either raw or smoked/fermented, never had any problems.
I really think raw is the way to go, also keeps the most important nutrients intact.

### Inger — 2016-01-08T12:10:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-raw-fish-from-your-own-freezer.17350/post-185738>

Well I do it all the time... freezed or not.... I enjoy it

### fitness@home — 2016-01-08T12:37:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-raw-fish-from-your-own-freezer.17350/post-185740>

I have eaten fish from my freezer that's been thawed many times.

Whether you eat raw fish at home or at a restaurant there is always going to be a chance of parasites.

The parasite thing...if your stomach acids are strong enough it shouldn't be an issue. It was one of my questions in a consult with Tim Jackson.

### Martin — 2016-01-08T15:10:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-raw-fish-from-your-own-freezer.17350/post-185745>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=185740)
>
> I have eaten fish from my freezer that's been thawed many times.
>
> Whether you eat raw fish at home or at a restaurant there is always going to be a chance of parasites.
>
> The parasite thing...if your stomach acids are strong enough it shouldn't be an issue. It was one of my questions in a consult with Tim Jackson.

I was going to answer the same way. I LOVE my wild salmon warmed up with water - very mild and sweet tasting. Some capers... then down the hatch with betaine HCL caps.

Martin

### lilreddgirl — 2016-01-08T20:40:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-raw-fish-from-your-own-freezer.17350/post-185781>

Thank you for the answers!

I'm going to try it  I love raw sashimi when out... so now I'll get to have my own...

### TerrierMom — 2016-01-08T20:51:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-raw-fish-from-your-own-freezer.17350/post-185783>

My understanding is that freezing kills the parasites. I am moving to a raw diet for dogs and the the PaleoPet vet that I see (he has a great book too, you can find it on Amazon, Dr Coghlan), in his book he says always freeze the meat you get for them and thaw it, to protect from parasites....seeems as though it should work for us too. There are countries where they are never allowed to serve fish raw that has not been frozen and thawed (Canada, for example, last time I was there anyway). And I believe it is for this reason..... I eat tons of sashimi. I also can buy "sashimi grade" fish at the local Asian markets (gotta love southern CA for those markets alone), but honestly am not sure what the difference is between that and regular fish.

### TerrierMom — 2016-01-08T20:53:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-raw-fish-from-your-own-freezer.17350/post-185784>

Here's some info : For fish that contain parasites, the [FDA](http://www.fda.gov/Food/GuidanceRegulation/RetailFoodProtection/FoodCode/ucm186451.htm#part3-4) provides guidance under their Parasite Destruction Guarantee. This states in part that fish intended to be consumed raw must be “frozen and stored at a temperature of -20°C (-4°F) or below for a minimum of 168 hours (7 days)”. So.... hope that helps.

### SandyD — 2016-01-08T21:10:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-raw-fish-from-your-own-freezer.17350/post-185785>

Perhaps this is the reason I don't get food poisoning from bought sashimi, but I get it from anything else raw, with regards to HCL, is this something one needs to do all the time? Or would the body heal the HCL levels increases gradually and one can come off them? I don't want to be relying on HCL for a prolonged period of time.

# Thread: Black light - directions of use
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-directions-of-use.17352/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Flawed — 2016-01-08T14:15:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-directions-of-use.17352/post-185742>

Hey,
Today i tried my black light setup for the first time. A few things i'm not sure about:

How long should i spend under them? (of course that's dependent on amount of am sun i got and skin colour, but approx how long?)

Look directly at them?

Distance from them?

Time of the day they're acceptable to use?

Thanks

### lohd2015 — 2016-01-08T19:03:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-directions-of-use.17352/post-185774>

> [Flawed said:](/goto/post?id=185742)
>
> Hey,
> Today i tried my black light setup for the first time. A few things i'm not sure about:
>
> How long should i spend under them? (of course that's dependent on amount of am sun i got and skin colour, but approx how long?)
>
> Look directly at them?
>
> Distance from them?
>
> Time of the day they're acceptable to use?
>
> Thanks

I have my black lights on all day in the living room where I do most of my 'work' when there it is a cloudy day outside. At night, before I go to sleep, I generally spend at least 45 mins to one hour under one situated right above my bed (replacement of the ceiling fan light). I find that during the day, it gives me extra energy and at night, it helps me sleep better.
The first time I used it, I looked directly at it for over 10 minutes and it gave me a slight headache. Now I look at it from time to time with no ill effects. I have found it unnecessary to focus on the bulb directly. This is just my personal experience.
Hope it works for you too, Flawed.

### Pebbles — 2016-02-01T18:37:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-directions-of-use.17352/post-187866>

I have one in living room and 2 around my office desk- I can look directly at them w/o a problem. At office desk runs as long as the computer is running.

# Thread: Apps that can be used to listen to the blogs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apps-that-can-be-used-to-listen-to-the-blogs.17361/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rubicon — 2016-01-10T18:18:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apps-that-can-be-used-to-listen-to-the-blogs.17361/post-185911>

Typically I generally prefer listening to audiobooks vs reading text, and I wanted to share a method I've been using to listen to the blogs which involves using the app "Pocket":

1.  Download Pocket to your phone ([Android](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.ideashower.readitlater.pro&hl=en)) ([iPhone](https://itunes.apple.com/us/app/pocket-save-articles-videos/id309601447?mt=8))
2.  Login to Pocket and browse to the blog page you'd like to listen to. On mobile, you should have an option to "Share to Pocket". Alternatively, a method I use is to get the [Pocket Chrome extension](https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/save-to-pocket/niloccemoadcdkdjlinkgdfekeahmflj?hl=en) and save the blogs I want to listen do via desktop.
3.  Once you've saved some blog posts to Pocket, open the Pocket app and you'll see a list of saved blogs. You can now put your phone into airplane mode, view the the blogs, and select, "Listen (TTS)"
4.  Pocket will now read the blog to you in a computer voice using Text To Speech (TTS)

In general the computer voice & pronunciation in the app is pretty good. I like to go for a 30-60 min walk as the app reads a blog to me.

# Thread: A new serotonin modulating antidepressant that does NOT inhibit dopamine!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-serotonin-modulating-antidepressant-that-does-not-inhibit-dopamine.17401/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2016-01-17T02:15:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-serotonin-modulating-antidepressant-that-does-not-inhibit-dopamine.17401/post-186536>

First off I am not condoning the use of antidepressants. Nature is our true antidepressant by far. But for those people that are sick and tired of the dopamine suppression caused by these f$%^%^g SSRI meds, there may be an option.
My doctor just prescribed me an antidepressant called "Brintellix" (vorioxetine). It is a serotonin 'modulator' but it also boosts dopamine, norepinephrine, glutamate and acetylcholine. Boosts cognitive function but most importantly- DOES NOT SUPPRESS DOPAMINE!!
You know how much we all need our dopamine!
I just started it a few days ago but I will keep you posted. I need the dopamine so I can actually APPLY the info that I learn here to get optimal. The drug is just a 'stepping stone' NOT a permanent solution.

Hopefully this helps someone struggling on those shitty SSRI's
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4296590/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4296590/)

# Thread: Thyroid feels funny after not eating
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-feels-funny-after-not-eating.17409/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### marlowe — 2016-01-19T07:34:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-feels-funny-after-not-eating.17409/post-186711>

Hey, first time poster here, so im not sure if im posting in the right place, but im definitley a beginner. I have been following a ketogenic diet for a few months now, and the last couple weeks I have been eating more seafood, shellfish, and sea weed. Ive never had any major health issues, but always been high carb eater and I am just changing diet to live better. So far I feel great!

I have been reading through a lot of the blogs on this website as well as the forums, what a wealth of knowledge!

I have had one thing that has come up that i am interested in learning more about. Sometimes when I dont eat for a while, I start to feel pressure in my thyroid. Its not painful, its just like slightly inflamed maybe, its almost like I feel its there where as normally I dont even notice it. My energy levels are good, no brain fog, and this only happens if I have been without food for 6-12 hours. I figure it might just be my thyroid becoming adapted to my new eating habits, maybe detoxing from the years of carb eating. I am curious, has anyone else had this happen and know what causes it, or knows where I can read more about this?

Thanks!

### marlowe — 2016-01-21T17:54:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-feels-funny-after-not-eating.17409/post-186996>

I think I may have figured out what happened in case anyone searches for this in the future. I got some of the Lugols 2% drops but didnt really pay attention to the proper dosing so I took a full dropper 3-4 times over the past 7 days. I believe I got the wolff-chaikoff effect from too much iodine. I am not sure why this was more noticeable when I havent eaten, and Its wierd because I have no other symptoms besides the inflamed feeling thyroid, but it seems like too much iodine is the culprit. I got some selenium to try and help balance it out, and it says this usually clears up in 10 days.

### nonchalant — 2016-01-21T20:32:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-feels-funny-after-not-eating.17409/post-187009>

OMG marlowe! Glad you're ok.
Welcome!

# Thread: Lichen Planus, Glaucoma
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lichen-planus-glaucoma.17417/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Pebbles — 2016-01-21T11:40:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lichen-planus-glaucoma.17417/post-186958>

My journal still waiting for an update, however now I want to give an update on MDH....
MDH's Oral Lichen Planus/OLP is GONE in one year!
For those who are not aware - docs do not know the WHY of OLP, there is NO research on the topic and no SNP's connection - some Vit A is given and patients are struggling 10-20 years or even more - some of them life long - frankly not find ANY healing story on internet. Regular control is needed, since these eruptions can get cancerous. He is checked ( can not say treated) by the Univ. prof. of paradontology who does not understand how is gone in one year. He got checked by a Univ prof of rare diseases - all labs done, all NEG/in the range, so conclusion was that OLP is on psycho basis - like anything else which does not have a reason or a cure in allopathic medicine.
In Sept 2014 MDH started with a Leaky Gut RX near a strict keto-epi-paleo lifestyle.
Than beginning of 2015 when I got deeper in learning JK, I got to the light connection of OLP confirmed by Jack. That gave a big kick - so we changed dramatically all light sources home and in his office and fixed all gadgets (blocking blues). We regularly go to tropics during winter time, however he never took off his glasses! - no more glasses in the sun protocol is alive now.
Daily DHA is strictly followed near seasonal eating. Cold was added as well, near sun-gazing and grounding.
He has myopia and glaucoma too - his eye pressure readings were as follows: March 2015 - 19, July 2015 - 16, November 2015 - 12! Most probably during winter this will go up a bit by the next Feb-March control, however now HE IS AWARE he can influence all this. Again, his doc did not understand how this can happen....
There is still a long way to go, to improve his myopia and glaucoma, to fix his disc hernia as well, and to avoid OLP coming back, but we are sure we are here in the right place to be on the right track.
If we missed anything what we could add to the healing process, open to hear your suggestions/advice!
THANK YOU!

# Thread: How to use a single IR bulb lamp for therapy... an informative article
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-use-a-single-ir-bulb-lamp-for-therapy-an-informative-article.17425/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lilreddgirl — 2016-01-23T05:57:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-use-a-single-ir-bulb-lamp-for-therapy-an-informative-article.17425/post-187145>

**_INSTRUCTIONS AND CAUTIONS FOR USE OF A SINGLE RED HEAT LAMP_**

by Lawrence Wilson, MD

© March 2014, The Center For Development

A single reddish 250-watt “heat lamp”, which is really a near infrared lamp, is an excellent healing device. This article details where to get a lamp and clamp-on socket, how to use the lamp, and cautions that one must observe for its safe and effective use.

**SINGLE LAMP PROTOCOL**

Clamp the socket to a chair, shelf, headboard of a bed, or other convenient location where you can move your body close to the lamp. If the spring-loaded clamp is very stiff, place a towel or glove on your hand when opening the clamp.

Always set a timer for your session. Infrared lights can cause deep relaxation and you could fall asleep under the light and overtreat yourself.

Sit about 8" to 24" from the lamp. Move the lamp or your body slightly during the lamp session as otherwise it may cause a slight burn.

Allow the area to become as hot as one can comfortably tolerate. A slight reddening of the skin is common and is not a burn. It will go away within an hour, and is not harmful.

Use the lamp five to twenty minutes per session, one to about six times per day. Be sure to observe all the cautions listed in this article.

**BENEFITS OF AN ISOLATED INFRARED LAMP**

An isolated reddish infrared heat lamp directed at an area of the body provides the following excellent and often surprising benefits:

·Improved circulation.

·Deep penetration, up to several inches inside the body.

·Improved oxygenation and even hydration of the tissues.

·Infrared effects, which may include faster tissue healing and others.

·The energy of the lamp is often very relaxing for muscles and for the nervous system.

**CONDITIONS THAT MAY BENEFIT**

**Joint and muscle conditions.** Joint pain, muscle strains, nasal polyps, ear aches, possibly eye problems, possibly toothaches, skin rashes, acne, boils and perhaps some other conditions of the eyes, nose, ears and sinuses. Neck, shoulder, elbow, knee and back pain may also respond.

Relief in one or two days has also occurred in cases of low back pain, knee pain, nerve root irritation and other local conditions. One can safely shine the lamp on any area of the body in need of healing. However, one must not shine it near the head for more than 5-10 minutes about every two hours.

**Infections.** For a general treatment that can be surprisingly effective, shine the red lamp at the thymus gland, moving the body or the lamp as close as possible without causing a burn. The thymus gland is located at the level of the breast bone.

A treatment should last about 15-20 minutes, and can be repeated three or four times daily for a severe infection.

The lamp can also be used for ‘spot treatment’ of any infected area of the body. For example, one patient had a sinus infection that did not respond to antibiotics or to natural healing methods. She was weak and feverish every evening and had constant headaches. By shining an infrared lamp on the sinuses for 5-10 minutes at a time, four times a day, relief occurred in two days with complete elimination of all symptoms in five days. The patient continued to use the lamp for another two weeks because she said the red light felt wonderful.

**Nail fungus.** One gentleman completely controls his toenail fungus on many toes by placing a red heat lamp near the floor. Each day he moved his toes as close as he could to the lamp until he felt it start to burn. He needed to do this every day, once daily at least, for 6 months. The treatment does not seem to be a cure, as he must repeat it every few months or so or the condition will return, but it keeps it under control.

**Young children.** For a baby or infant, one can shine the lamp on the abdomen for about ten minutes, preferably while the baby is lying comfortably in your lap or on a table. Use caution and keep the lamp at least 1.5-2 feet from the baby. This can help colic, constipation, anxiety, irritability and other problems. Do not shine the light near a baby’s eyes or at a baby’s head.

**For older children.** Skin rashes, blemishes, and other skin conditions may respond. Teenage or adult acne often responds beautifully, often in a few days with just one or two treatments for 5-15 minutes per day.

**For the eyes, ears, teeth and sinuses.** The single lamp can be very helpful for conditions involving these areas. However, because they are on the head, do not use the light more than 5-10 minutes at one time. Then wait two hours or more before doing another session. Also, keep your eyes closed when using the light near your eyes.

**Pets.** It can also be used on pets and large animals such as horses.

**CAUTIONS WITH A SINGLE INFRARED LAMP**

·Do not use the lamp on any area of the head, such as the ears or sinuses, for more than 5-10 minutes in one session, and sessions should be at least two hours apart.

·Do not look at the lamp at close range. Always close your eyes if you shine it at your head area.

·Always use a timer when using the lamp so that you do not overtreat yourself.

·Avoid using the lamp on an injury for the first 24 hours following the injury. The lamp might increase inflammation, which is not helpful. However, the lamp is excellent for most injuries and accidents.

·Move the lamp or your body a little during a session. Remaining in one spot too long could cause a burn.

·Avoid applying any kind of essential oil, lotion or cream to your skin before using the heat lamp. Oils can heat up and cause a burn.

·Avoid touching an infrared lamp when hot.

·Avoid placing the lamp on any surface when hot to avoid igniting or burning the surface.

·Treat the lamp gently. Do not screw the lamp in too tightly. Also avoid banging the lamp or splashing water on it, as these could cause it to break.

·Rosacea or some skin cancers may become irritated by the light.

**WHERE TO BUY A SINGLE REDDISH HEAT LAMP**

These are found at most hardware stores. Ask for a 250-watt “heat lamp”. It is not called infrared. Any brand will do, and these include General Electric, Philips, Sylvania, Havel-SLi, Westinghouse, Feat or perhaps another.

To get a clamp light socket, here are two links:

[http://www.walmart.com/ip/Bayco-10.5-Brooder-Clamp-Light/14003468](http://www.walmart.com/ip/Bayco-10.5-Brooder-Clamp-Light/14003468)

[http://www.amazon.com/Alert-Stampin...96498122&sr=8-2&keywords=Clamp-on+lamp+socket](http://www.amazon.com/Alert-Stamping-RLB-6MC-Reflector-10-5-Inch/dp/B000WEPJD2/ref=sr_1_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1396498122&sr=8-2&keywords=Clamp-on+lamp+socket)

\_\_\_\_\_\_

from [http://www.drlwilson.com/Articles/SINGLE LAMP.HTM](http://www.drlwilson.com/Articles/SINGLE%20LAMP.HTM)

### seanb4 — 2016-01-23T10:24:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-use-a-single-ir-bulb-lamp-for-therapy-an-informative-article.17425/post-187153>

I ordered one of these yesterday to go with the vitamin D lamp. I figure since the vielight has helped so much I could try increasing infrared without side effects if I add a decent amount of UV. We will see...

### lilreddgirl — 2016-01-23T23:34:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-use-a-single-ir-bulb-lamp-for-therapy-an-informative-article.17425/post-187206>

Talk to me about Infrared and UV synnergy... I've read that 'you need both' and also that there's a danger of fibrosis with just IR without UV' but not sure if there's a specific way to use them timing wise... do you know more details?

Is the way to do some IR in a dark room at some point in the day then at a different time of the day you get some UV?

### seanb4 — 2016-01-24T11:45:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-use-a-single-ir-bulb-lamp-for-therapy-an-informative-article.17425/post-187256>

It seems to me the best way is to get both UV and IR at the same time.

I have also read about overusing IR leading to fibrosis and dehydration. There is a study somewhere and I think yew worked it out that 2 sessions of IR from vielight is the cut off dose. But obviously no studies have been done and there is very little info on combining it with UV. Also the cut off point will vary depending on health / etc. I'm still unsure of the mechanism that UV will stop IR fibrosis. The only way to find out for me will to be use my 2 doses of vielight and then see how much IR + UV-A-B I can take without feeling worse.

If i sit with the 250w ir lamp close to were i'm shining vit d lamp hopefully that will bring the blood to surface and allow both frequencies to interact in blood. I will try 5 mins x4/day + vielight and see what happens. Slight concern of overuse of d lamp as it recomends less time. But tbh i dont even know if its working as its second hand and my skin hasn't gotten any redness from it....

### Lahelada — 2016-01-24T12:25:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-use-a-single-ir-bulb-lamp-for-therapy-an-informative-article.17425/post-187263>

I second the idea of both at the same time. We do not live under a cold sun,I.e IR and UV go hand in hand. My solution would be different to yours though. I would have a heated room preferably heated by some combustion fire be that open fire place, wood burners and least gas heating .No electric for obvious reasons. Then I would add red light.
This thinking that I am trying to simulate conditions of a certain time of day. Don't get stuck on the science so much but think about the goal. You want to pretend that you are in a different t latitude or at a different point in the day. You are in effect lieing to your SCN.

### Lahelada — 2016-01-24T12:37:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-use-a-single-ir-bulb-lamp-for-therapy-an-informative-article.17425/post-187265>

This is also the reason why to take the instructions for IR bulb with a pinch of salt. These are Kinesiology recs which have some application with regards to not burning yourself but you will need to think out the details within your context. Let's say you are a shift worker and want to supplement the frequencies you miss by still sleeping g at 4 pm . Your requirements in terms of frequence ratio and time of exposure are different in terms of dose and times than a person like DR K who is trying to undo the blue light /EMF exposure from the OR.

That is how I understand it anyway.

>
>
> A single reddish 250-watt “heat lamp”, which is really a near infrared lamp, is an excellent healing device. This article details where to get a lamp and clamp-on socket, how to use the lamp, and cautions that one must observe for its safe and effective use.
>
>
> **SINGLE LAMP PROTOCOL**
>
>
> Clamp the socket to a chair, shelf, headboard of a bed, or other convenient location where you can move your body close to the lamp. If the spring-loaded clamp is very stiff, place a towel or glove on your hand when opening the clamp.
>
> Always set a timer for your session. Infrared lights can cause deep relaxation and you could fall asleep under the light and overtreat yourself.
>
> Sit about 8" to 24" from the lamp. Move the lamp or your body slightly during the lamp session as otherwise it may cause a slight burn.
>
> Allow the area to become as hot as one can comfortably tolerate. A slight reddening of the skin is common and is not a burn. It will go away within an hour, and is not harmful.
>
> Use the lamp five to twenty minutes per session, one to about six times per day. Be sure to observe all the cautions listed in this article.
>
>
> **BENEFITS OF AN ISOLATED INFRARED LAMP**
>
>
> An isolated reddish infrared heat lamp directed at an area of the body provides the following excellent and often surprising benefits:
>
>
> ·Improved circulation.
>
> ·Deep penetration, up to several inches inside the body.
>
> ·Improved oxygenation and even hydration of the tissues.
>
> ·Infrared effects, which may include faster tissue healing and others.
>
> ·The energy of the lamp is often very relaxing for muscles and for the nervous system.
>
>
> **CONDITIONS THAT MAY BENEFIT**
>
>
> **Joint and muscle conditions.** Joint pain, muscle strains, nasal polyps, ear aches, possibly eye problems, possibly toothaches, skin rashes, acne, boils and perhaps some other conditions of the eyes, nose, ears and sinuses. Neck, shoulder, elbow, knee and back pain may also respond.
>
> Relief in one or two days has also occurred in cases of low back pain, knee pain, nerve root irritation and other local conditions. One can safely shine the lamp on any area of the body in need of healing. However, one must not shine it near the head for more than 5-10 minutes about every two hours.
>
> **Infections.** For a general treatment that can be surprisingly effective, shine the red lamp at the thymus gland, moving the body or the lamp as close as possible without causing a burn. The thymus gland is located at the level of the breast bone.
>
> A treatment should last about 15-20 minutes, and can be repeated three or four times daily for a severe infection.
>
> The lamp can also be used for ‘spot treatment’ of any infected area of the body. For example, one patient had a sinus infection that did not respond to antibiotics or to natural healing methods. She was weak and feverish every evening and had constant headaches. By shining an infrared lamp on the sinuses for 5-10 minutes at a time, four times a day, relief occurred in two days with complete elimination of all symptoms in five days. The patient continued to use the lamp for another two weeks because she said the red light felt wonderful.
>
> **Nail fungus.** One gentleman completely controls his toenail fungus on many toes by placing a red heat lamp near the floor. Each day he moved his toes as close as he could to the lamp until he felt it start to burn. He needed to do this every day, once daily at least, for 6 months. The treatment does not seem to be a cure, as he must repeat it every few months or so or the condition will return, but it keeps it under control.
>
> **Young children.** For a baby or infant, one can shine the lamp on the abdomen for about ten minutes, preferably while the baby is lying comfortably in your lap or on a table. Use caution and keep the lamp at least 1.5-2 feet from the baby. This can help colic, constipation, anxiety, irritability and other problems. Do not shine the light near a baby’s eyes or at a baby’s head.
>
> **For older children.** Skin rashes, blemishes, and other skin conditions may respond. Teenage or adult acne often responds beautifully, often in a few days with just one or two treatments for 5-15 minutes per day.
>
> **For the eyes, ears, teeth and sinuses.** The single lamp can be very helpful for conditions involving these areas. However, because they are on the head, do not use the light more than 5-10 minutes at one time. Then wait two hours or more before doing another session. Also, keep your eyes closed when using the light near your eyes.
>
> **Pets.** It can also be used on pets and large animals such as horses.
>
>
> **CAUTIONS WITH A SINGLE INFRARED LAMP**
>
>
> ·Do not use the lamp on any area of the head, such as the ears or sinuses, for more than 5-10 minutes in one session, and sessions should be at least two hours apart.
>
> ·Do not look at the lamp at close range. Always close your eyes if you shine it at your head area.
>
> ·Always use a timer when using the lamp so that you do not overtreat yourself.
>
> ·Avoid using the lamp on an injury for the first 24 hours following the injury. The lamp might increase inflammation, which is not helpful. However, the lamp is excellent for most injuries and accidents.
>
> ·Move the lamp or your body a little during a session. Remaining in one spot too long could cause a burn.
>
> ·Avoid applying any kind of essential oil, lotion or cream to your skin before using the heat lamp. Oils can heat up and cause a burn.
>
> ·Avoid touching an infrared lamp when hot.
>
> ·Avoid placing the lamp on any surface when hot to avoid igniting or burning the surface.
>
> ·Treat the lamp gently. Do not screw the lamp in too tightly. Also avoid banging the lamp or splashing water on it, as these could cause it to break.
>
> ·Rosacea or some skin cancers may become irritated by the light.
>
>
> **WHERE TO BUY A SINGLE REDDISH HEAT LAMP**
>
>
> These are found at most hardware stores. Ask for a 250-watt “heat lamp”. It is not called infrared. Any brand will do, and these include General Electric, Philips, Sylvania, Havel-SLi, Westinghouse, Feat or perhaps another.
>
> To get a clamp light socket, here are two links:
>
>
> [http://www.walmart.com/ip/Bayco-10.5-Brooder-Clamp-Light/14003468](http://www.walmart.com/ip/Bayco-10.5-Brooder-Clamp-Light/14003468)
>
>
> [http://www.amazon.com/Alert-Stamping-RLB-6MC-Reflector-10-5-Inch/dp/B000WEPJD2/ref=sr\_1\_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1396498122&sr=8-2&keywords=Clamp-on lamp socket](http://www.amazon.com/Alert-Stamping-RLB-6MC-Reflector-10-5-Inch/dp/B000WEPJD2/ref=sr_1_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1396498122&sr=8-2&keywords=Clamp-on+lamp+socket)
>
>
>
> \_\_\_\_\_\_
>
>
> from [http://www.drlwilson.com/Articles/SINGLE LAMP.HTM](http://www.drlwilson.com/Articles/SINGLE%20LAMP.HTM)

Thanks lilreddgirl for the incredible specificity and resource links. This is very close to what I've been doing (without much research) with a 250 w Therabulb NIR-A bulb. And now I'll make a few adjustments. Awesome post.

### Sheddie — 2017-08-22T11:25:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-use-a-single-ir-bulb-lamp-for-therapy-an-informative-article.17425/post-222029>

> [seanb4 said:](/goto/post?id=187256)
>
> It seems to me the best way is to get both UV and IR at the same time.
>
> I have also read about overusing IR leading to fibrosis and dehydration. There is a study somewhere and I think yew worked it out that 2 sessions of IR from vielight is the cut off dose. But obviously no studies have been done and there is very little info on combining it with UV. Also the cut off point will vary depending on health / etc. I'm still unsure of the mechanism that UV will stop IR fibrosis. The only way to find out for me will to be use my 2 doses of vielight and then see how much IR + UV-A-B I can take without feeling worse.
>
> If i sit with the 250w ir lamp close to were i'm shining vit d lamp hopefully that will bring the blood to surface and allow both frequencies to interact in blood. I will try 5 mins x4/day + vielight and see what happens. Slight concern of overuse of d lamp as it recomends less time. But tbh i dont even know if its working as its second hand and my skin hasn't gotten any redness from it....

Ditto thanx to lilreddgirl!

If nnEMFs lead to fibrosis (as in tough microwaved meat) then it seems reasonable using light frequency bulbs (as with sunlight) would have more, or less, side effects depending how dehydrated the body is. This is intriguing; I wonder what goes on with say, a pulsing red Vielight up the nose and an IR and/or UV lamp light simultaneously directed at the face or some other body area? Of course, starting slow and gradually increasing light (with enough hydration) goes without saying.

Looking forward to more reports on experiments, especially if some idea of hydration (self-assessed maybe on a scale of 1-10?) at the time of exposure and noticed results like skin appearance and mood changes... Am I insensitive or are we not likely to feel UV on skin? IR's heat is easier to feel. If one aimed for 'balanced/equal UV & IR' from bulbs would that be possible from matched wattage in the bulbs? If not matched would a 'stronger' IR lamp let the UV lamp's rays come through? Would the exposure time be the same for both, or would one be best to turn off first, or place at different distances from the target area?

# Thread: Anyone made crab broth?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-made-crab-broth.17429/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tff — 2016-01-24T10:38:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-made-crab-broth.17429/post-187244>

Has anyone made crab broth? I've googled and didnt find much.

Im thinking to just throw 3-4 crabs in a pot and simmer for 1-2 hours, not sure if shell will disintegrate or be somewhat edible!
Its the whole animal(eyes, organs etc) so should contain far more nutrients than cow/lamb bone broth.

Any crab tips? Im a crab newbie. Here's what i found:

1 Apparently blue crabs have 'dead mans fingers'(gills), but they are not poisonous.
[http://msbayoulady.blogspot.sg/2012/07/the-dreaded-dead-mans-fingers-strike.html](http://msbayoulady.blogspot.sg/2012/07/the-dreaded-dead-mans-fingers-strike.html)

2 Only buy live crabs, due to potential bacteria in dead ones.

3 Many people love the strong-tasting, yellow stuff inside a Crab. It is a gland that branches out on both sides of the Crab's stomach to function as its liver and pancreas. In areas where the water may be doubtful, it's best not to eat this, as part of its function is to filter impurities, so any chemical contaminants will be concentrated in the mustard. (I'd ignore this tip and eat it!)

Thanks!!

### Inger — 2016-01-24T11:12:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-made-crab-broth.17429/post-187247>

Well.... a a waitress.. I know many chefs (the good ones ..) make crab broth for the base of their crab soups. They cook the shells many hours. Tastes delicious!
My boss makes broth of shrimp shells that he cooks the whole night.
I imagine the broth must have lots of minerals in it for sure!
I would maybe to it that way.. cook the crab shortly, save the water.. eat the meat... put the shells back in the pot and let simmer for hours
