# Thread: Antidepressants can make you WORSE!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antidepressants-can-make-you-worse.17449/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2016-01-30T07:44:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antidepressants-can-make-you-worse.17449/post-187660>

I just want to give a heads up to anybody who is currently taking an antidepressant that might not know this fact.
The 'therapeutic window' is different for everyone so if you are feeling shitty on an antidepressant or maybe even feeling WORSE, it is possible that the dose is too high for you..even at a dose that is considered 'low' by your doctor.

This has happened to me twice now.

A long time ago my doctor (back then-1991) wrote me a prescription for me to titrate up to 300mg of Fluvoxamine. When I got up to 100mg I felt much WORSE! I dropped it down to 75mg and that worked.
My Dr. was very surprised that I got benefit from such a low dose stating that I was "the only person that he had ever seen get benefit from such a low dose".
This could be one of the reasons that there is a suicide link to antidepressants. The therapeutic window can vary hugely from person to person and I honestly do not think that there is enough awareness of this fact.

Hopefully this little bit of info will help someone who is struggling out there....
Better yet-meditate, sunlight on your RPE, deep breathing, DHA, CT, epi-paleo gives much more than an antidepressant will ever give...
..freedom from Big Pharma is such an awesome feeling!

### BrainWhisperer — 2016-02-08T05:39:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antidepressants-can-make-you-worse.17449/post-188509>

Especially for the younger cohort...

[http://www.bmj.com/content/352/bmj.i545](http://www.bmj.com/content/352/bmj.i545)

# Thread: Can epigeneitcs help me?

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1

### Tobiah — 2016-02-03T05:04:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-epigeneitcs-help-me.17468/post-188036>

I know I can change my biology, but how? Two Dr's both believe I have Gilbert's syndrome. But these doctors don't know about epigeneitcs.

From what research I have done, ive come to a conclusion that the break down of red blood cells can cause the billirubin to increase. This would make sense because I have a long history of full contact martial arts and a lot of brusing. Aswell as that, I get cupping done to extract the stagnet blood that is interfering with my joints.

I have had my liver looked at and its not from that. I'be increased my fat intake and digestive enzyme to help my body adapt. But my levels are not going down, my eyes are always yellow and friends and family worry about me. Aparenttly it's not harmful but the doctors said I should avoid eating fats and I know that is piss poor advice. So does anyone have any advice for me?

With thanks Toby

Hello again everyone!
Thank you for all your help with my previous questions! Now I have a few concerning my dad! He was/has been very skeptical about everything I'm doing, but hasn't refused to let me, so I can't really complain. However he recently inquired about my Leptin Reset and how he wouldn't be able to do it, so I really want some tips on how he can do the Leptin Reset. The first problem is that he works shift works, so he is the opposite of optimal on that, but I would like to help him as much as possible.
He wakes up at either 4:45 AM or PM depending on his shift. He eats and goes to work. He eats lunch at around 2-4 PM or AM and the works until around 7:30, when he goes home and eats, then goes to bed around 9:00 or 9:30.
He understands that he can't eat so soon before bed, but he needs a lunch that he can take in his lunch box that won't smash or go bad. He currently takes things like hard-boiled eggs but that's not going to work if he only eats 2 meals a day. Any suggestions about foods or tips he can use? If I can give him some tips so that he thinks it's doable, I think he may be able to take control of his health and get on the road to optimal. I think if he has success losing weight with the Leptin Rx he may see how all of this works together and take more steps to improve his health. Thanks to anyone who answers in advance!

# Thread: Free water deficit - U.M conversion
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/free-water-deficit-u-m-conversion.17484/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Pebbles — 2016-02-04T20:19:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/free-water-deficit-u-m-conversion.17484/post-188213>

I am lost.... - in EU, so have to convert Na u.m

Formula:
mg/dL = (mmol/L \* gmw ) / 10
Where
gmw = Gram Molecular Weight
Example:
Convert 200 mmol/l of Sodium to mg/dL . The gram molecular weight of Sodium is 22.99 g/mol.
mg/dL = ( 200 \* 22.99 ) / 10
\= 20 \* 22.99
mg/dL = 459.8

My serum Na is 139mmol/L - lab range is 135-190
Converting it to mg/dl gives me 319 mg/dl - which is almost double vs the 140 optimal.

With this number, I am getting to +61L free water.
Using this calculator [http://www.medcalc.com/freewater.html](http://www.medcalc.com/freewater.html)

I am doing something wrong, or if not, where I am screwed up?
Thanks!

### Rubicon — 2016-02-07T02:17:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creating-a-custom-title-on-the-forum.17502/post-188451>

I noticed Yew had a clever title under his profile nickname and dug through the settings till I found how to customize mine. Just go to **[Personal Details](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?account/personal-details)** and modify the **Custom Title** field

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

# Thread: Tell me what I can do better (psoriasis sufferer)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-what-i-can-do-better-psoriasis-sufferer.17503/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lawrencep93 — 2016-02-07T02:44:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-what-i-can-do-better-psoriasis-sufferer.17503/post-188455>

So what I have been trying to do this year:

-   Trying to get sunlight with minimal clothing before 10am
-   Use red lights in my room when I am inside, Flux and twighlight on my PC and Android
-   Eating a keotgenic diet, minimum 1 dozen oysters per week + seafood like raw tuna
-   Sleeping 8 hours
-   Using cold where possible, mainly sitting in my room cooled and cold showers
-   Fast and eat dinner post work out mainly for appetite control and ease of lifestyle

So basically I live in Melbourne Australia.
I have Psoriasis which has gotten better the past few months (it is summer here=more sun)
Also short sighted, which I find is at it's worse at night or in places that are lit with artificial light, but my vision is much better in sunlight.
Have lost over 66lb but still wanting to lose a further 25lb
Thinking of doing UVB therapy over winter as the UV from the sun has greatly helped my psoriasis over winter and a friend of mine with the same condition has seen amazing results, considering lack of sun here in winter.

I have been reading Jacks blogs and I really like how he recently spoke about psoriasis, quite a few things go over my head, but I am constantly trying to learn more and more to try and keep up with the vast knowledge provided

**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluoridated-water-better-than-nothing.17517/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Allanah — 2016-02-09T20:30:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluoridated-water-better-than-nothing.17517/post-188644>

Good morning everyone! I have a particular situation I need help with today..
I live in South Dakota, USA. (It's freezing out right now here!) Anyway, I live in two different places, one during the week (Monday-Friday) and one during the weekend. At my place during the week I have access to RO water at all times, and I drink up to 2 gallons daily. There is no issue here. However, where I live on Saturday and Sunday I have only access to tap water, which I'm sure is fluoridated.
My question is this: On those two days should I drink the fluoridated water at all? Is it better than no water? Or should I drink only a little, etc.? Thanks again!

### shah78 — 2016-02-09T20:44:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluoridated-water-better-than-nothing.17517/post-188647>

Get some used glass gallon apple cider bottles (or smaller glass bottles )and bring them to your week end home. Weak,Second choice ,fill plastic bottles and bring them to your weekend home. Don't drink floride.

### Mystic Rose60 — 2016-02-09T20:54:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluoridated-water-better-than-nothing.17517/post-188648>

Hi Allanah, I think that it's important to understand "why" it's important to not drink fluoridated water, or, use products that have the fluoride in them.

Also, two gallons of RO water per day is a lot of water. Are you also urinating a lot as well? If so, you may be dehydrated. Excessive thirst and urination indicates a less than optimal environment

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...t-fluoride-dielectric-blocker-in-water.17369/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/what-mds-need-to-learn-about-fluoride-dielectric-blocker-in-water.17369/)

### nonchalant — 2016-02-09T22:00:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluoridated-water-better-than-nothing.17517/post-188649>

Glad you have access to all the RO water you want, Allanah. Drink up!
Carrying water for the weekends would be best. But I don't think restricting water on weekends is a good idea at all. Hope you can work something out.

# Thread: another olden text about heliotherapy

**Subforum:** The Cave

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lahelada — 2016-02-10T20:10:23+0200

[https://archive.org/details/b23983565](https://archive.org/details/b23983565)

Air, light and sun baths in the treatment of chronic complaints

by [Monteuuis, A. (Albert), 1861-](https://archive.org/search.php?query=creator%3A%22Monteuuis%2C+A.+%28Albert%29%2C+1861-%22); [Rothwell, F. (Translator)](https://archive.org/search.php?query=creator%3A%22Rothwell%2C+F.+%28Translator%29%22); [Rothwell, Fred, 1869- (Translator)](https://archive.org/search.php?query=creator%3A%22Rothwell%2C+Fred%2C+1869-+%28Translator%29%22); [Royal College of Physicians of London](https://archive.org/search.php?query=creator%3A%22Royal+College+of+Physicians+of+London%22)
Published [1907](https://archive.org/search.php?query=date:1907)
Topics [Phototherapy](https://archive.org/search.php?query=subject%3A%22Phototherapy%22), [Heliotherapy](https://archive.org/search.php?query=subject%3A%22Heliotherapy%22)

# Thread: Impact of oral melatonin on the electroretinogram cone response
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impact-of-oral-melatonin-on-the-electroretinogram-cone-response.17531/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2016-02-10T23:06:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impact-of-oral-melatonin-on-the-electroretinogram-cone-response.17531/post-188719>

But what if it is administered at night when you are supposed to make melatonin? The next day, are your cones photoreceptive again? I wonder how long the effect lasts?

I stopped taking it a couple months ago - but if I have trouble sleeping, I wonder if lack of autophagy due to lack of sleep is worse than this cone effect and by taking an exogenous mel, you clean up cells -

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2785757/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2785757/)

**Abstract**
**Background**
In the eye, melatonin plays a role in promoting light sensitivity at night and modulating many aspects of circadian retinal physiology. It is also an inhibitor of retinal dopamine, which is a promoter of day vision through the cone system. Consequently, it is possible that oral melatonin (an inhibitor of retinal dopamine) taken to alleviate circadian disorders may affect cone functioning. Our aim was to assess the impact of melatonin on the cone response of the human retina using electroretinography (ERG).

**Methods**
Twelve healthy participants aged between 18 to 52 years old were submitted to a placebo-controlled, double-blind, crossover, and counterbalanced-order design. The subjects were tested on 2 sessions beginning first with a baseline ERG, followed by the administration of the placebo or melatonin condition and then, 30 min later, a second ERG to test the effect.

**Results**
Following oral melatonin administration, a significant decrease of about 8% of the cone maximal response was observed (mean 6.9 μV ± SEM 2.0; P = 0.0065) along with a prolonged b-wave implicit time of 0.4 ms ± 0.1, 50 minutes after ingestion.

**Conclusion**
Oral melatonin appears to reach the eye through the circulation. When it is administered at a time of day when it is not usually present, melatonin appears to reduce input to retinal cones. We believe that the impact of melatonin on retinal function should be taken into consideration when used without supervision in chronic self-medication for sleep or circadian disorder treatment.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: The Heart as a Bi-Directional Scalar Field Antennae
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-heart-as-a-bi-directional-scalar-field-antennae.17538/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Psyche — 2016-02-11T17:49:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-heart-as-a-bi-directional-scalar-field-antennae.17538/post-188772>

This amazing paper is dedicated to the memory of Francisco Torrent-Guasp (1931-2005).
[http://www.omicsonline.com/open-acc...field-antenna-2090-8369-1000121.php?aid=60866](http://www.omicsonline.com/open-access/the-heart-as-a-bidirectional-scalar-field-antenna-2090-8369-1000121.php?aid=60866)

> _Conventional thinking is that the brain is the seat of consciousness and memory. Evidence summarized here opens the possibility that as a scalar field antenna the heart might be able to tap into so called “empty” space, which appears to contain structure and energy and information in coherent holographic form \[[30](http://www.omicsonline.com/open-access/the-heart-as-a-bidirectional-scalar-field-antenna-2090-8369-1000121.php?aid=60866#30)\-[32](http://www.omicsonline.com/open-access/the-heart-as-a-bidirectional-scalar-field-antenna-2090-8369-1000121.php?aid=60866#32)\].Many modern thinkers are drifting away from purely mechanistic explanations of how the universe and those within it function, as well as the nature of consciousness_.

> _Further research by McCraty et al. led to extraordinary and compelling evidence that **the heart’s energy field is coupled to a field of information that is not bound by the limits of time and space.** Rigorous experimental studies demonstrated that the heart and brain receive and process information about an event before the event actually happens \[[19](http://www.omicsonline.com/open-access/the-heart-as-a-bidirectional-scalar-field-antenna-2090-8369-1000121.php?aid=60866#19),[20](http://www.omicsonline.com/open-access/the-heart-as-a-bidirectional-scalar-field-antenna-2090-8369-1000121.php?aid=60866#20)\]. Moreover, the physiological data showed that the heart appears to receive “intuitive” information a few seconds before the brain. The researchers suggested that the heart is directly coupled to an ambient subtle energetic field of information that surrounds the body, which, in turn, is entangled and interacts with the multiplicity of energy fields in which the body is embedded, presumably including fields present in the quantum vacuum_.

### Psyche — 2016-02-11T17:53:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-heart-as-a-bi-directional-scalar-field-antennae.17538/post-188773>

[http://www.ecocardionet.com/entrevista.pdf](http://www.ecocardionet.com/entrevista.pdf)

The paradigmatic change in ventricular anatomy and function prompts research in every field of cardiology. In the words of Dr. James L. Cox, ‘‘It is such a dramatic change from the understanding of the heart’s physiology that we can almost go back and start repeating the experiments of the last 50 years with this new knowledge and see how we interpret these experiments now."

# Thread: Traveling Tips.. India
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/traveling-tips-india.17539/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Allanah — 2016-02-11T20:49:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/traveling-tips-india.17539/post-188791>

Hello! I need some advice! I am a 15-year old girl currently doing the leptin reset among other things. I may have the opportunity to travel to India this summer through an exchange program, so my question(s) relate to food. I have been doing some research and they eat a lot of rice, legumes, nightshades, etc. They don't tend to eat much beef or pork which is fine and in some places they eat seafood. When they make dishes (a sweet one for example) they use things like molasses, etc., not sugar. They also eat a lot of fresh yogurt. When on this trip, if I am able to go, should I try to follow every rule I can? Or since it is not the same processed garbage we have here is it okay? Should I do something in-between? Is the yogurt actually healthy in this case? Or is it likely made from bad milk too? Ultimately I'll make my own choice but any input is greatly appreciated!

### Lahelada — 2016-02-11T22:21:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/traveling-tips-india.17539/post-188798>

I would live the experience to the full, food included.

Depending where you travel you are very close to the equator so a higher carb diet would not be a circadian mismatch.
Mumbai to Equator 1300 miles
Chennai 905 miles
Delhi 2000 miles

For comparison New Orleans is 2100 miles away,North Dakota 3200 etc
What does this have to do with anything?
The closer you are to the equator the more carbs your body can handle but step off the gas on fats and brake proteins a little. The yogurt should be fine as due to the tropical conditions the cattle breeds are different and the casein issue goes away. Just bear in mind normal tropical hygiene rules.
HOWEVER,if you get little time outside all bets regarding carb restriction are on again. In order to live like the locals you must spend a lot of time outdoors if you can. I personally would avoid the sweets.They are very very sweet and I think the info about Molasses is now outdated but I could be wrong.
Have you ever stuck a hot wet spoon into sugar and the sugar congealed on it ? That is the sweetness level you can expect. Indian ice cream I would eat on the rare occasion.

### Just Breathe — 2016-02-11T23:19:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/traveling-tips-india.17539/post-188806>

Do your best to enjoy the food. There is nothing like traveling to a place and experiencing it fully. I feel like the US has the most compromised food system with GMO's and other poisons in it. Whenever I travel elsewhere, even if I'm breaking the rules, I feel fine!
Having said that, I worry about gut health more than the food when I travel. I take emergen-c packets and put a few drops of biocidin in my daily emergen-c glass and I'm good to go. Enjoy your time out there.

### Allanah — 2016-02-13T06:09:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/traveling-tips-india.17539/post-188931>

Thanks to both of you! Those were all the same things I thought but I'm glad to know I wasn't wrong. Thank you!

# Thread: EMF Analog Signal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-analog-signal.17544/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kevin W — 2016-02-12T04:31:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-analog-signal.17544/post-188832>

Hey all,
We moved recently, worked with Michael Neuert on fixing this house with the correct wiring.

Our Magentic readings at the old place were between 1.50 and 3. Now they are .06. PERFECT. We have cutoff switches to cut power to the room at night, all seems good.

Our EMF reading great. No cell phones, no WIFI, etc. However, the house of course has steel wrapped wiring, so the North side of the home all rooms (on 2 acres) as I get close to that wall will pick up a signal, EMF about .015 uW/cm2. I was told to not hold the tester and place it on some wood or cardboard and check, reading was then fine. He said this is an Analog signal. Not Digital. He said this can happen.

However, my wife is experiencing extreme joint pain (worst she has ever had) at night. Now she has major join issues, they are literally all stuck, long story there.

Can the analog signal still cause issues. Moving to the new home amazingly her migraines were cut in half within a week of leaving that electrical nightmare of a home. I am just worried that analog signal may be hurting her. Is there a way anyone knows to reduce it? Lots of harmonizer products out there, anyone with experience here?

Thanks all...
Kevin

Nailbot : prints designs from your smartphone onto your nails
Selfie spoon : take pictures of you eating
Batband : a headband that transmits music to your private inner ear. Playlists come from your smartphone naturally

I have been making a serious effort to up my hydration. I've been buying spring water at the store in 1 gallon plastic jugs. We need to be drinking more and I hate that we are having to recycle all this plastic. I finally convinced DH to get home delivery water service because he needs to be drinking it too. Problem is we live outside of the delivery area for Eldorado Water. My only other option is Deep Rock but they only offer Artesian Water. I've checked the water analysis and levels are as follows:

Fluoride - ND (not detectable)
pH - 6
Conductivity - 45
Chloride - 2.6
Total Dissolve Solids - 30
(All other Inorganic Chemicals and VOC levels are ND)

I want optimal so is this a good option?

# Thread: Seafood in order of "optimality.." help!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-in-order-of-optimality-help.17581/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 40
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Allanah — 2016-02-20T02:19:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-in-order-of-optimality-help.17581/post-189418>

Hello!
I know "optimality" is not a word,  however I need some help figuring out which types of seafood are more superior in how optimal they are over other seafood. Here's a list of some of the seafood which I have access to or would like to eventually acquire. In order of most to least optimal, which of these are the best? (I know oysters are the best, and tilapia is inferior to salmon, etc., but I'm still including everything I have access to so that I can reference it later. )

Canned oysters in water
Canned clams
Canned pink salmon (wild caught)
Canned mackerel in water
Canned sardines in water
Canned tuna in water
Canned crab in water

Frozen salmon (wild caught)
Frozen calamari
Frozen octopus
Frozen lobster tails
Frozen crab
Frozen tilapia
Frozen flounder
Frozen catfish
Frozen smoked oysters
Frozen swai
Frozen raw shrimp
Frozen Ahi tuna steaks
Frozen mahi mahi

Fresh salmon (farmed)

Taking into account DHA, mercury, and anything that affects health overall, which of these are the best and worst?
Are there any other really good seafood options that I am missing and should try to find?
Should I significantly limit any of these sources? I know tuna steaks are usually recommended to be consumed less than once a month for my age (and I will probably never have them anyway) due to mercury, but from my understanding that changes depending on redox? How can I figure out my redox? (sorry I know that's off topic) Other than tuna and mercury, though, are there any reasons to limit any of these?
Lately I've been eating salmon twice daily (canned salmon for salmon patties for breakfast and a frozen salmon fillet for supper) until I can get more seafood variety, is this too much of one thing? Is it okay until I can get other sources?
Anything else I should know about seafood? (the next thing to find on my shopping list is sea vegetables!!)
Thanks in advance for any tips!

### Marbear — 2016-02-22T18:16:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-in-order-of-optimality-help.17581/post-189578>

Here is what I use:

DHA Charts
[http://www.health.gov/dietaryguidelines/dga2005/report/html/table\_g2\_adda2.htm](http://www.health.gov/dietaryguidelines/dga2005/report/html/table_g2_adda2.htm)
[http://seafoodhealthfacts.org/seafood\_nutrition/practitioners/omega3\_content.php](http://seafoodhealthfacts.org/seafood_nutrition/practitioners/omega3_content.php)

Regarding mercury:
[http://medicalxpress.com/news/2015-01-fatty-acids-fish-shield-brain.html](http://medicalxpress.com/news/2015-01-fatty-acids-fish-shield-brain.html)

Search Dr. Kruse's site for Redox Rx. There is a list of things you can check for your redox.

I'm pretty new here too but the bottom line is eat as much seafood as you can - raw is always better but hard to access in some places. With that being said, make sure you are blocking blue at night and you are getting the morning and afternoon UV you need as well so that all the DHA you are eating can turn that UV light into a DC electric current.

### Jenelle — 2016-02-22T19:11:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-in-order-of-optimality-help.17581/post-189583>

Marbear, the article you posted is great ~ and also just makes me feel awful, because I did not consume fish during my pregnancies. Maybe tuna ... maybe occasionally. I don't recall being so afraid of the mercury (although I'm sure it was on my radar at the time) ~ I just didn't **know** of the importance for their neurological development. I wasn't raised on seafood, so I was not familiar with it. ...So many things I would do differently if I were having more children now...

Anyway, thanks for the links. I was just thinking to myself this morning, that I would like to work on a project with the kids: Making big posters to hang on the fridge, with all of the benefits of DHA... and then a list of all the best sources. They are old enough that they are doing some of their own meals now, and I have been emphasizing to them that I want them to eat some fish _every day_.

### Penny — 2016-02-22T20:11:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-in-order-of-optimality-help.17581/post-189585>

> [Allanah said:](/goto/post?id=189418)
>
> Hello!
> I know "optimality" is not a word,  however I need some help figuring out which types of seafood are more superior in how optimal they are over other seafood. Here's a list of some of the seafood which I have access to or would like to eventually acquire. In order of most to least optimal, which of these are the best? (I know oysters are the best, and tilapia is inferior to salmon, etc., but I'm still including everything I have access to so that I can reference it later. )
>
> Canned oysters in water
> Canned clams
> Canned pink salmon (wild caught)
> Canned mackerel in water
> Canned sardines in water
> Canned tuna in water
> Canned crab in water
>
> Frozen salmon (wild caught)
> Frozen calamari
> Frozen octopus
> Frozen lobster tails
> Frozen crab
> Frozen tilapia
> Frozen flounder
> Frozen catfish
> Frozen smoked oysters
> Frozen swai
> Frozen raw shrimp
> Frozen Ahi tuna steaks
> Frozen mahi mahi
>
> Fresh salmon (farmed)
>
> Taking into account DHA, mercury, and anything that affects health overall, which of these are the best and worst?
> Are there any other really good seafood options that I am missing and should try to find?
> Should I significantly limit any of these sources? I know tuna steaks are usually recommended to be consumed less than once a month for my age (and I will probably never have them anyway) due to mercury, but from my understanding that changes depending on redox? How can I figure out my redox? (sorry I know that's off topic) Other than tuna and mercury, though, are there any reasons to limit any of these?
> Lately I've been eating salmon twice daily (canned salmon for salmon patties for breakfast and a frozen salmon fillet for supper) until I can get more seafood variety, is this too much of one thing? Is it okay until I can get other sources?
> Anything else I should know about seafood? (the next thing to find on my shopping list is sea vegetables!!)
> Thanks in advance for any tips!

seafood with the highest DHA is herring,mackeral,sardine - but oysters have 200% of the daily zinc requirement plus shellfish feeds the distal flora better than say a salmon steak - evolutionarily speaking, it would have been easier to "score" shellfish as it is on the seashore, so there is that eating for your biology thing...

A great test is to stand on one foot prior to eating seafood and then do it after to check any change in balance - if there is,m think "mercury" - it is at this point you should eat some cilantro or pop an alpa lipoic acid pill and chase it with 200 mcg of selenium - I think wasabi has some mercury chelating properties also - really, the Japanese have it down with wasabi and ginger with their raw seafood -

One more thing - search for estrogen and BPA's - Dr. Kruse posted something about canned seafood and its BPA affecting estrogen - I think it binds to the estrogen receptors or something...

You can also try smoked salmon as well - never eat tilapia or catfish and flounder is also a bottom feeder so not sure that's such a hot idea either...

Skipjack tuna I've heard has less mercury than albacore - the bigger the fish, the more mercury it probably contains -

### Marbear — 2016-02-22T20:39:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-in-order-of-optimality-help.17581/post-189586>

> [Jenelle said:](/goto/post?id=189583)
>
> Marbear, the article you posted is great ~ and also just makes me feel awful, because I did not consume fish during my pregnancies. Maybe tuna ... maybe occasionally. I don't recall being so afraid of the mercury (although I'm sure it was on my radar at the time) ~ I just didn't **know** of the importance for their neurological development. I wasn't raised on seafood, so I was not familiar with it. ...So many things I would do differently if I were having more children now...
>
> Anyway, thanks for the links. I was just thinking to myself this morning, that I would like to work on a project with the kids: Making big posters to hang on the fridge, with all of the benefits of DHA... and then a list of all the best sources. They are old enough that they are doing some of their own meals now, and I have been emphasizing to them that I want them to eat some fish _every day_.

Jenelle - don't feel awful about something you did when you didn't know any better!! Like Dr. Kruse says when we know better, we do better. I think it's more important to focus your energy on moving forward and I think a perfect example is the poster project with your kids. Teach them now and then they can teach their kids. Plus you never know what they may teach other kids as well. It's the ripple effect. I don't have kids of my own but everyday I am inspired from reading about so many parents here who have taken the steps and made the changes to make their own lives and the lives of their kids better.

A lady named Lisa posted this on Facebook recently and I think she sums it all up perfectly:
"I think it's better to accept that we all wanted optimal for our children, even if we only know now that we were on the wrong track. We don't know everything; even if we did we would be imperfect in our execution. All you can change now is the future - I don't accept any guilt for this and I move forward with my family doing better now."

### Jenelle — 2016-02-22T21:26:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-in-order-of-optimality-help.17581/post-189587>

Thank you, Marbear. I really appreciate that. It's easy for me to tell _others_ not to feel bad about things... "We do the best we can with the knowledge we have at the time." I've said that a lot, in fact. You're right... all I can do is move forward and teach them better _now._

Thankfully ~ we have been a Paleo family for 3+ years... so the addition of quality fish daily is not a huge deal. We already do not eat like anyone else we know in real life.

I did have a small moment of panic when I saw the post above about BPA. We just went to Costco yesterday and stocked up on canned fish!  But I was happy to see that the sardines and the tuna both say no BPA. Whew. The canned salmon... we will eat it, and then I may have to look for another source. There is no mention on the can about BPA for that one.

With that being said, make sure you are blocking blue at night and you are getting the morning and afternoon UV you need as well so that all the DHA you are eating can turn that UV light into a DC electric current.

You are missing context and useability. When you start out with a low redox you can even eat ketogenic in summer. Complex 1 needs to recover.
Complex 1 is the most sensitive in the ECT as pertaining to macro nutrient input (leaving aside deuterium for a minute) The carb load in a steak is minimal so the stress on complex 1 is less. Local or not local does not come into it for that reason as the photonic charge of the electrons received is way lower than if you were to eat the plants the animal ate for you.makes sense?

Canned: You lose DHA through the canning process, probably B12 too. However you preserve the DHA /EPA ratio and the "high B12. You maybe lose the 1H
There is the BPA argument. Possibly but detox does not depend on the ingestion of some dandelion variety but on the methylation process working. The methylation process is now presumed not to be working because of malabsorption / reduced content of B vitamins. B vitamins are light sensitive and destroyed by light ( a good thing if it happens in context,a bad thing if it happens in your house or the supermarket ) it is a good thing because B vitamins are aggressive oxidants if we keep them too long.

This is why I responded pointedly earlier. It takes a heck of a lot of study when you are not a member and even if you are a member. The paradigm thinking is alive in everyone of us and is recreated every day by easy solution marketing.

"The levels found were too low to be harmful however, according to Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution.
Ken Buesseler, a senior scientist at the centre, told USA Today: "To put it in context, if you were to swim everyday for six hours a day in those waters for a year, that additional radiation from the addressed cesium from Japan... is 1000 times smaller than one dental x-ray."
[https://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2...ected-fish-caught-off-fukushima/#.XP0OIIhKjIU](https://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2019/02/02/national/science-health/limit-cesium-detected-fish-caught-off-fukushima/#.XP0OIIhKjIU)

"Levels have been particularly low among invertebrates like oysters, squid and octopus. The highest levels have been among bottom-feeding fish, such as sand-lance, cod and flounder. These may occasionally feed at a hotspot of accumulated radioactive sediment. Flatfish such as slime sole were previously a lucrative local product, as the prefecture’s proximity to Tokyo allowed fresh shipping." from 2014

### Lahelada — 2019-06-09T18:19:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-in-order-of-optimality-help.17581/post-272335>

JanSz, post: 272332, member: 933"\][@Lahelada](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6531/)
If someone have given you a deal,
free with no attachment,
available any seafood, from any place, all wild, all best.

Would you eat only seafood? **NOOO.**

Or, would seafood be only part of your diet? **YESSS**

How big part, please describe in percentages of total food eaten.
**NO IDEA**. It would vary according to availability, signalled need observing my well being. A percentage would be of no use anyway as my figures will not be yours. Don't tempt me to write a book of the one size fits all style  Neither would I eat the same at different stages of my journey.

Better, compare a variety of food in their calories or their weight. **BETTER compare omega 6/ omega 3 by natural occurence in relation to season ! theer is a progression as the year passes. Calories you can safely forget as a measure as the usable output varies from person to person according to redox or metabolic order if you prefer. Weight of food is a better general measure. My personal interest is micronutrients under a different LIGHT, especially the B vitamins... Mitochondrial nutrition if you will. Fascinating stuff when you take light into account. Strange that I should have become a defender of seafood,though. I still do not like the stuff all that much. This means to me that my dopamine can still do with optimising. It is not logical for any being to have food aversions..**

How much would you care about Omega6 food? **Not much once I dealt with the excess of years of fake food or onesided alimentation. I would care about the hidden processed oils always.**
Or would you even care about omega6? **Omega 6 like other things is not a poison. We made it a problem by or willy nilly combination of ingredients in processed food.

Leading questions but also reductionist, Jan. Everyone who comes here needs to eat to put the toothpaste back into the tube as much as possible and very few foods are available that can help in our altered environment. A leaky gut and senescent enterocytes are quite indiscriminate about what goes in, where it goes and what comes out unchanged. What helped the Chinese in 1800 may not help now. This is my point. People come here with the idea that food may be your healer and quote Hippocrates and Paracelsus, giants of THEIR time. The functional world lives of that nostalgia. Some do note that we live in an altered environment but then revert right back to pointing out the benefits of some obscure herb/substance from an area only accessible by canoa that will kiss it all better using short term studies that have no follow up or epidemological studies that in a court of law would be considered hearsay. I know who says what in the world of alternative but longterm results are outstanding everywhere. This is not only a reflection on the theories,the doctors, but also a reflection of the people employing the methods here as well as there. I spoke before about the mambo nr 5 of doctors,the "i like this that sounds right" facts taken out of context of the complete protocol of whatever doctor. Do I believe that only Dr Kruse's protocol is successful? No, there are others that have some success. It is the one with the widest clinical scope /reach in my opinion though. Playing pick a mix without clinical context waters it down in my opinion, an opinion I have rePEATedly stated.

Over and out. I only provided fodder, whatever you eat ...

### Saichi — 2019-06-09T21:05:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-in-order-of-optimality-help.17581/post-272348>

SST,

I've abstained from all top feeder Pacific fish since two weeks after 3/11/2011. I've abstained from all small local Pacific fish since April 2012 coincides with the deep ocean turnover rate. Also the first two years after March 2011 I abstained from all local grass-fed meats since they too bioaccumulate the fallout. At that time in California I was buying all my meat from Organic Prairie based in the midwestern states.

My grandfather lived a few miles away from the Hiroshima bomb and had health complications throughout his life and died with Alzheimers in his late 80s. He fled to Iwate just north of Fukushima where his children have now died from the largest industrial disaster in history. I don't recall the exact number but Arnie Gundersen said the reactors ongoing release was/is equivalent to thousands of Hiroshima bombs in terms of radiological release, THOUSANDS. I have two cousins who fled to Hokkaido after March 2011 for five years but after my uncles died they had to return to Iwate to handle the estate and family grave. They are only in their 20's/early 30's and I pray the sixth great extinction takes us all out before they have to suffer the inevitable very premature cancer and death from radiation poisoning.

Wine is only as old as agriculture, basically a blip in human history. California wines also tested positive for radionuclides. Wine's not paleo so I don't recommend that either even if it weren't contaminated.

Salmon roe was my favorite food ever.

They are only in their 20's/early 30's and I pray the sixth great extinction takes us all out before they have to suffer the inevitable very premature cancer and death from radiation poisoning.

Yes the east Coast of all North America has been heavily radiated last several years and is still being.

The Americans tried holding it back for a few years after 3/11 happened but the weather modification equipment failed or they just couldn't hold it back forever. Now it's all over North America radiation even showing up on west coast and the top part of South America.

Atlantic seafood is not as bad, but still I think you want to eat local and in season.

I have a small fishing boat for netting, trapping and long lining. My family only eat local and organic, wild caught in season.

The waste from gutting fish, even the shells and waste from any seafood goes into my soil all around my fruit and nut trees.

I also have several pole dovecotes on my small holding, never ending supply of doves and dove eggs. All the waste that I don't eat either go back into the soil or I use as bait in my fish traps.

Fishing and farming go so well together, I have such nutrient rich dark soil.

> **NO IDEA**. It would vary according to availability, signalled need observing my well being. A percentage would be of no use anyway as my figures will not be yours. Don't tempt me to write a book of the one size fits all style  Neither would I eat the same at different stages of my journey.
>
> Better, compare a variety of food in their calories or their weight. **BETTER compare omega 6/ omega 3 by natural occurence in relation to season ! theer is a progression as the year passes. Calories you can safely forget as a measure as the usable output varies from person to person according to redox or metabolic order if you prefer. Weight of food is a better general measure. My personal interest is micronutrients under a different LIGHT, especially the B vitamins... Mitochondrial nutrition if you will. Fascinating stuff when you take light into account. Strange that I should have become a defender of seafood,though. I still do not like the stuff all that much. This means to me that my dopamine can still do with optimising. It is not logical for any being to have food aversions..**
>
> How much would you care about Omega6 food? **Not much once I dealt with the excess of years of fake food or onesided alimentation. I would care about the hidden processed oils always.**
> Or would you even care about omega6? **Omega 6 like other things is not a poison. We made it a problem by or willy nilly combination of ingredients in processed food.
>
> Leading questions but also reductionist, Jan. Everyone who comes here needs to eat to put the toothpaste back into the tube as much as possible and very few foods are available that can help in our altered environment. A leaky gut and senescent enterocytes are quite indiscriminate about what goes in, where it goes and what comes out unchanged. What helped the Chinese in 1800 may not help now. This is my point. People come here with the idea that food may be your healer and quote Hippocrates and Paracelsus, giants of THEIR time. The functional world lives of that nostalgia. Some do note that we live in an altered environment but then revert right back to pointing out the benefits of some obscure herb/substance from an area only accessible by canoa that will kiss it all better using short term studies that have no follow up or epidemological studies that in a court of law would be considered hearsay. I know who says what in the world of alternative but longterm results are outstanding everywhere. This is not only a reflection on the theories,the doctors, but also a reflection of the people employing the methods here as well as there. I spoke before about the mambo nr 5 of doctors,the "i like this that sounds right" facts taken out of context of the complete protocol of whatever doctor. Do I believe that only Dr Kruse's protocol is successful? No, there are others that have some success. It is the one with the widest clinical scope /reach in my opinion though. Playing pick a mix without clinical context waters it down in my opinion, an opinion I have rePEATedly stated.
>
> Over and out. I only provided fodder, whatever you eat ...
>
> **

Love this, La. Thank you.

### Solidsilverteeth — 2019-06-10T20:17:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-in-order-of-optimality-help.17581/post-272400>

Saichi are you not concerned about the prevailing wind pushing the worst of the radiation over the Americas? Some say the worst of it is going away from Japan and all ending up all over the Americas but north and north of South America. Like revenge for the two nukes dropped.

I wonder how the harm from Fukushima radiation compares to the harm from other things?

### Saichi — 2019-06-11T08:42:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-in-order-of-optimality-help.17581/post-272428>

> [Solidsilverteeth said:](/goto/post?id=272400)
>
> Saichi are you not concerned about the prevailing wind pushing the worst of the radiation over the Americas? Some say the worst of it is going away from Japan and all ending up all over the Americas but north and north of South America. Like revenge for the two nukes dropped.
>
> I wonder how the harm from Fukushima radiation compares to the harm from other things?

The aerial fallout was almost entirely in the first few months after the accident before they could begin recooling the freshly spent storage pools and melt-through reactor cores. Yes the radiation went all over the states but it was mostly a concern for the northwestern states, I mentioned the precautions I took which I believe were sufficient. After the first few months all the rads have been flowing directly into the ocean. Before a year or two after March 2011 we used to see families of seals on the beach during every coastal afternoon walk. Since about 2013/14 we've seen none on the sand bars, literally none. I followed the independent news reporting the die-off of seals all along the western coast so it came as no surprise to me. I have to say I did see my first seal in years little over a week ago when I was doing CT in the ocean around 7am, about 15 meters from me had the head up out of the water staring at me like an alien. I really cherish any moments with wildlife now.

On a slight tangent we used to have a swarm of non-migrating birds in our backyard every morning chirping up a frenzy every day of the year for almost a decade. We noticed in hindsight about the time PG&E installed the 5G cabling in our neighborhood couple years ago the birds all disappeared. We used to get annoyed about not being able to get back to sleep in the early mornings, before we knew of circadian natural law, but now it's eerily quiet. Only semi-resident birds we have left is a bluejay and one woodpecker which is nothing compared to the amazing chorus we used to have. I have friends who've also noticed the disappearance of songbirds from their yards.

The sorrow can be overwhelming at times.

### mrc — 2019-09-21T23:24:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-in-order-of-optimality-help.17581/post-278508>

**[Dr. Jack Kruse](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/?__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARA2IHE4mJPX8yPUhv2Lhwq1esp-VY96aq0hrJ1nZJThlQHAM2XUJWbVI96MLKEiMnhBTgqduGuV1JE85BeswSg1duXDrm9vbXNN7tdgmhV4LEMj_Bf5bc1kUrs6lPez6msRJ8I8ccorp20S_-Y8adUvqCHT9GvvWQJ86szGLSvHRNYnl4s86SOxSbl4NgsBy656KfEHbEktFeqI5qMvH8p6rOk7NzeAY_LfcRTf28iz1XH4HJumaY0jkLdihc__I3dirKTewDmlZPzWng_2eENmmIG0ASEdj7p616g0znBldmAbwgyTXKIJ7cwVG55BKvrWHQFR-FlrqHyZNEwZjUpJEw&__xts__%5B1%5D=68.ARBsubVfnnv7e0sFS3huePa5o_NarZN5wKxfo16PBIrSeAP9sZIHg348yav_UuPtrKCwCZAQS3NWPMRiKZQxUVYVtEVwJOv_gSRPEtUQzPNnq2HEaWiVMHCCP5ZE4CZvHNc3fazFrpZmm3zHYHOpLT1RNsb-a_HlcDruJzbJ8O2lHNxb-ysvTozrqrtTD4trjJOVUs8buPW2GfExgbO0aKccXORymelnt16ySPQNeaRPIia9D88ONfsWCmU_x95PKM2-dQcqVMP9van7q2WMs19B7DMJx4JyaVh0LJrhX13IX9i6snTT7aYrYWcicJnz5geBeZg3fR9TTg9J5BUsrgmyzg&__tn__=kC-R&eid=ARDzjn7beyI_GLm0jDibwN6jSV8ncM5G8dfjiOeDdfggoAhigyDvLnyJUa078rDbvCLzUe_eFmvWIAd8&hc_ref=ARR0NxL7ljsvtlT1lO8pUcyH11ioAGTkIxMi1_a4VKfNF4oSSKsQPJj4A4KwBTl56wo&fref=nf)** [1 March 2016](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1191766344221051?__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARBsubVfnnv7e0sFS3huePa5o_NarZN5wKxfo16PBIrSeAP9sZIHg348yav_UuPtrKCwCZAQS3NWPMRiKZQxUVYVtEVwJOv_gSRPEtUQzPNnq2HEaWiVMHCCP5ZE4CZvHNc3fazFrpZmm3zHYHOpLT1RNsb-a_HlcDruJzbJ8O2lHNxb-ysvTozrqrtTD4trjJOVUs8buPW2GfExgbO0aKccXORymelnt16ySPQNeaRPIia9D88ONfsWCmU_x95PKM2-dQcqVMP9van7q2WMs19B7DMJx4JyaVh0LJrhX13IX9i6snTT7aYrYWcicJnz5geBeZg3fR9TTg9J5BUsrgmyzg&__tn__=-R)
The topic of radiation in seafood is overrated and misunderstood (few know about hormesis and transcription factors like PARP1 and Ku70). Sharing this for people who say, "I'm not sure about eating seafood due to Fukushima."
[https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1191766344221051](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1191766344221051)

"Science evolves even if your thinking remains stagnant. We must expand our grip of science…………or risk falling from the cliff.
I tell this to my patients and all the alternative functional medicine doctors who keep telling their patients seafood is bad.
It is really bad advice based on outdated thinking.

Radiation has been condemned by the entire scientific community as being a “dangerous bar”.
This is surprising, however, since there is strong scientific evidence for the molecular mechanisms of how transient “radiation drinks” could promote health.
I tell my patient's seafood radiation has many surprising benefits. Today we know that low doses of radiation stimulate DNA repair through the activation of four transcription factors, PARP-1, PARP2, ATM, and Ku70.
In response to low dose, sublethal radiation (or xenohormetic compounds). PARP-1 and PARP-2 activate two DNA repair pathways called “Single strand break repair” (SSBR) and one called “Base Excision Repair” (BER).
Both of these repair single-stranded DNA breaks. In response to low dose, sublethal radiation (or xenohormetic compounds), Ku-70 activates the DNA repair pathway called “Non-homologous end joining” (NHEJ).
[https://scholar.google.com/scholar?...HN68hO6ZS3W9Rxrh790od9\_cMqMeBwE-9dNElXxR3Ar78](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=radiation%20DNA%20repair%20parp1%20parp2&btnG=&hl=en&as_sdt=0%2C22&as_vis=1&fbclid=IwAR2RH0-rfHOLTSHN68hO6ZS3W9Rxrh790od9_cMqMeBwE-9dNElXxR3Ar78)

# Thread: Going to sleep early
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-to-sleep-early.17615/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thomas — 2016-02-28T16:21:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-to-sleep-early.17615/post-189918>

Something I wonder...
Why If I go to bed (relatively) late, I am destroyed the next day..
If I go early,(that's around 8pm..) then yeah, I can have the best sleep I have in my life, it almost feels like a superpower, But go 2 hours later and the next day it's like I am sick or something. At the same time others seem to have no problem going late everyday and loving life. I kinda did that in the past but I don't think I slept very well..but I wasn't destroyed either. Is it all due to using tons of stimulation and artificial light at night and avoiding the outdoors during the day?

You see, I love nightlife, it's great. And I would absolutely love being able to go out at night and have fun. But I absolutely hate being tired, and not having my general feeling of wellbeing during the day.
Is there a way to actually go out in the evening/night and somehow be fine the next day? Or is it just a forever choice of .. go out have fun and hate the next day.. or go early to bed and enjoy the next day.

**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2016-03-01T23:05:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bluelight-on-the-skin-during-nighttime.17623/post-190038>

Hey guys. Jack mentioned blue light has an effect even on the skin at night.
But I cant find any study that is supporting that.
He mentioned one study, in which they shined light on the backside of the knee. But all studies I found said, there is no effect on melatonin.

Can you help me out?

### Lahelada — 2016-03-01T23:15:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bluelight-on-the-skin-during-nighttime.17623/post-190039>

(had heard a long time ago some metahphysical info on how someone had one and it cleared the aura of anyone in the room... but also that it had to be 'washed'/charged every few days - well now knowing about DC current and Kirlian photography it doesn't sound so irrelevant...

Also after I become EMF sensitive I also noticed I was 'crystal sensitive,' meaning I could go in a crystal shop and holding different ones in my hand would feel a marked qualitative difference in my energy level/state. But these are small crystals...

Does anyone have any extra info on if this could work and how you could measure impact, and what exactly one would DO to use a big crystal like that to feel better (preferably not so metaphysical - but if it's all about clearing your aura, but you did it yourself and felt a difference, that's relevant too)?

# Thread: Organic Laundry Detergent that actually cleans?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-laundry-detergent-that-actually-cleans.17625/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lilreddgirl — 2016-03-02T04:35:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-laundry-detergent-that-actually-cleans.17625/post-190062>

Does anyone use organic laundry detergent? What brands actually clean!?

The ones I had didn't do a good job... I seem to have to go back to using Tide for the clothes that are stained...

### Lahelada — 2016-03-02T12:57:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-laundry-detergent-that-actually-cleans.17625/post-190072>

Have you tried hydrogen peroxide as a stain remover ? Bicarb is a good allround clothes detergent.

### lilreddgirl — 2016-03-03T02:33:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-laundry-detergent-that-actually-cleans.17625/post-190113>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=190072)
>
> Have you tried hydrogen peroxide as a stain remover ? Bicarb is a good allround clothes detergent.

Thanks Lahleda, you're using Hydrogen Peroxide as stain remover? Do you just pour some to the stain and let sit, or rub it in?

It doesn't cause a fade spot?

### Lahelada — 2016-03-03T02:51:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-laundry-detergent-that-actually-cleans.17625/post-190114>

I do a staged attack
bicarb
bicarb and vinegar
hydrogen peroxide

You will need to know your stains . H,Peroxide can leave a stain .If your stain is too old it may leave a discolouration . Bllod leaves a green stain if you do not catch it quickly enough. I have used it neat ( the weak solution) directly on the fabric ,never used cream of tartar as suugested as in the link below. A large percentage of "Vanish " is Hy Perox..#
[http://www.geeksonhome.com/how\_7405276\_use-peroxide-laundry-stains.html](http://www.geeksonhome.com/how_7405276_use-peroxide-laundry-stains.html)

### lilreddgirl — 2016-03-03T04:14:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-laundry-detergent-that-actually-cleans.17625/post-190117>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=190114)
>
> I do a staged attack
> bicarb
> bicarb and vinegar
> hydrogen peroxide
>
> You will need to know your stains . H,Peroxide can leave a stain .If your stain is too old it may leave a discolouration . Bllod leaves a green stain if you do not catch it quickly enough. I have used it neat ( the weak solution) directly on the fabric ,never used cream of tartar as suugested as in the link below. A large percentage of "Vanish " is Hy Perox..#
> [http://www.geeksonhome.com/how\_7405276\_use-peroxide-laundry-stains.html](http://www.geeksonhome.com/how_7405276_use-peroxide-laundry-stains.html)

Ahhh you are so thorough! I hardly ever pretreat but regular detergent seems to get oils out, while my last few organic detergents didn't.

I am remembering I will have to deal with some old blood stains. I do the wash in ice water method but this is way old, so may not work anymore as you mention.

I just bought this LOL! it seems quite a few reviewers are impressed with it more so than other products...

[http://bandnlaundry.com/felted-goat-milk-soap-stain-stick-ships-2-15/](http://bandnlaundry.com/felted-goat-milk-soap-stain-stick-ships-2-15/)

It's a GOAT MILK STICK! and then you rub it out with the WOOL part of the stick  ! Apparently it works... just had to try it

### Starfish Prime — 2016-04-03T19:45:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-laundry-detergent-that-actually-cleans.17625/post-191967>

I'm dying to know how your goat milk stick works!

Have you tried soap nuts? Eucalan is another lanolin-based cleanser/wash for wool/delicates that is mild enough to use on animals sans rinsing (which doesn't make a lot of sense...why would you leave dirty water/cleanser on that you're trying to clean? But perhaps for those areas a thorough rinse is difficult or sick/elderly critters.....anyway, I digress)

I have several woolen baby wraps (highly, highly recommend them! Life saver for fussy babes!) that are quite valuable. Eucalan is quite effective at removing poo and milk stains with the soak&ignore method of washing woolies (dump it in a bucket of cleanser&water, ignore for an hour or so, flip it, repeat, drain, lay out to dry)

### lilreddgirl — 2016-04-04T01:22:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-laundry-detergent-that-actually-cleans.17625/post-191982>

> [Starfish Prime said:](/goto/post?id=191967)
>
> I'm dying to know how your goat milk stick works!
>
> Have you tried soap nuts? Eucalan is another lanolin-based cleanser/wash for wool/delicates that is mild enough to use on animals sans rinsing (which doesn't make a lot of sense...why would you leave dirty water/cleanser on that you're trying to clean? But perhaps for those areas a thorough rinse is difficult or sick/elderly critters.....anyway, I digress)
>
> I have several woolen baby wraps (highly, highly recommend them! Life saver for fussy babes!) that are quite valuable. Eucalan is quite effective at removing poo and milk stains with the soak&ignore method of washing woolies (dump it in a bucket of cleanser&water, ignore for an hour or so, flip it, repeat, drain, lay out to dry)

Wow Eucalan sounds great if you can just leave the wraps in there and ignore!

The Goat stick works amazingly well! No wonder it was getting all the rave reviews. I was even able to get out a 6 month old blood stain by applying and blotting with Goat milk stick and then washing by hand (in cold water of course for blood stain). That even impressed my mom.

It has lye as a listed ingredient. But it seems very mild to my hand and smell.

### Starfish Prime — 2016-04-04T02:39:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-laundry-detergent-that-actually-cleans.17625/post-191983>

Lye is used to saponify the fat; it's very common in soap making and it is consumed in the process, much like baking soda + vinegar in the proper ratio uses up all of both. (Maybe you knew that, idk)

I'm a big fan of Eucalan...esp for lingerie and the like... But I'll definitely be ordering one of those goat milk sticks (the name cracks me up...bc apparently I'm easily amused!) for grass stains and whatnot for summer! Glad you mentioned it and even more glad it actually works

Thanks that's reassuring about lye being used up since I thought it might be an irritant. And I didn't know !

I will look into Eucalan myself and also love calling my goat milk stick the "Goat stick" hehe

# Thread: Online meetup
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/online-meetup.17628/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rubicon — 2016-03-03T06:46:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/online-meetup.17628/post-190122>

I was thinking it might be a lot of fun to have a meetup for members of this community to chat about the latest blog, share biohacks, and socialize. Unfortunately, it's not practical to regularly do so in person, but why not some kind of group video conference online? To that end, I propose the following:

An online meetup at a time that will work for as many people as possible. Lets say, 3:00PM Eastern Time this Saturday. Via [WorldTimeBuddy](http://www.worldtimebuddy.com/?qm=1&lid=2147714,2643743,5128581,5368361&h=5368361&date=2016-3-5&sln=12-13), here's when 3:00PM Eastern is at various locations around the world:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

If you're in Sydney, meeting time will be 7:00AM Sunday morning. London is 8:00 PM Saturday evening, LA is 12:00 noon on Saturday, etc.

Where to meetup? There are lots of possibilities, but I did a bit of research and found tinychat.com is an option that's very easy to use, has reasonably good features, and it's free. It's basically like Skype's group chat, but no need to have an account or login.

Ok, so on Saturday at 3:00 Eastern, click here to enter a specially created chat room and join the meetup: [**http://tinychat.com/OptimalHealth**](http://tinychat.com/OptimalHealth)

Hope to see you there

### lilreddgirl — 2016-03-03T06:52:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/online-meetup.17628/post-190123>

ok, keep telling us though!

### Rubicon — 2016-03-04T22:38:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/online-meetup.17628/post-190200>

Ok, so in roughly 23 hours from now, if you want to hangout online go to **[http://tinychat.com/OptimalHealth](http://tinychat.com/OptimalHealth)**

When you click that link you'll be able to instantly join the chat where you can just hang out and listen-in, type in the group chat, or stream your audio/video. Hope to see you there

### Rubicon — 2016-03-18T01:25:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/online-meetup.17628/post-191108>

Is there any interest in having another meetup this weekend? At roughly the same time as the previous Saturday (but now with daylight savings), the meeting time in various timezones could be:

1:00PM in LA
4:00PM in New York
8:00PM in London
7:00AM in Sydney (Sunday)
etc

Come share progress on your latest biohacks, chat about the latest blog/podcast, ask or help answer each others' questions, or just chill out.

The video chat room at [**http://tinychat.com/OptimalHealth**](http://tinychat.com/OptimalHealth) is always open and anyone can join it without registering. Skype group chat or Google Hangouts are some alternatives if there's interest in that instead.

### Lahelada — 2016-03-18T04:08:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/online-meetup.17628/post-191117>

How long are you online for ? I might be able to drop in a bit later.

GREAT idea by the way !

### Rubicon — 2016-03-18T05:05:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/online-meetup.17628/post-191121>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=191117)
>
> How long are you online for ?

Not sure exactly how long...but I can stick around. Assuming the sky is clear, I might actually be a little late joining while I finish up on getting some mid-day sun.

# Thread: Solar Glow Lizard Lights Question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solar-glow-lizard-lights-question.17630/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthy2015 — 2016-03-03T16:37:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solar-glow-lizard-lights-question.17630/post-190139>

Hi
I wonder if anyone can help?
My friend and her partner have started using the Solar glow lizard lights and they have noticed a massive decline in his sex drive but when he had bloods taken his T, vit D and Oestradiol had all increased?
Any ideas as to why?
Thanks

### healthy2015 — 2016-03-04T17:26:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solar-glow-lizard-lights-question.17630/post-190186>

Does anyone have any ideas?
Thanks

### Penny — 2016-03-05T04:47:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solar-glow-lizard-lights-question.17630/post-190214>

That is bizarre - a low estrogen would cause low sex drive - even in a man - so here is my wildest guess - the lamp is emitting mercury - this screws up zinc which screws up libido - has his balance gone to crap? That's a definite tell...
[http://www.drkaslow.com/html/mercury-s\_influence.html](http://www.drkaslow.com/html/mercury-s_influence.html)
I would have him eat many oysters or take zinc and see if there is an improvement - but that's not going to bind the mercury - he could eat cilantro and chase it with selenium, but you need to do some research on how to get rid of mercury - no tfun - definitely stop the lamp

Also, if his sense of taste/smell are horrible, that is low zinc - you can also use this stuff called "zinc status" to test your levels of zinc - it's a taste test -

I also think Dr. K mentioned that if you are already mercury toxic, then these lights would be a bad idea - amalgams, being first born (you inherit mom's metals) and living in Seattle or Pittsburgh where there were coal plants or where the coal dust blow over from China...

### Penny — 2016-03-06T03:05:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solar-glow-lizard-lights-question.17630/post-190255>

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/mercury-testing.17261/#post-190246](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/mercury-testing.17261/#post-190246)

### healthy2015 — 2016-03-13T12:21:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solar-glow-lizard-lights-question.17630/post-190745>

Penny - thank you so much for the reply and my apologies for not thanking you sooner
That is really interesting - I have told my friend to get her hubby of the lights - I know he eats oysters and will get them to investigate how to get rid of mercury
Could this be the only reason for the lights affecting sex drive?

### Karl K — 2016-03-05T04:17:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-so-far.17638/post-190208>

So I've replaced my wi fi with ethernet adapted setup (finally)... no more headaches at home which is nice. Have amber bulbs in my bedroom, himalayan salt lamps throughout the house and a cheap 250w IR light setup next to my work desk. Tried getting black light bulbs from Home Depot but for some reason they hardly emitted any light, very dim. Did my first trip out to the park to lay in the sun on the ground for a bit in the morning a few days ago. Seemed to make a noticeable difference at work. Better energy throughout the day. I've set up an account through wwoofusa to go work on a farm eventually- got some great responses from wonderful hospitable people. Still waiting tables which is hardly the ideal environment but it is the best I can do for now to sustain myself. I try to keep it to one shift per day. Anyway how can I best use IR light exposure given my situation? What exposure time and proximity works best? Does IR exposure help strengthen the body against nnEMF? Most blogs I've read suggest at most 15 minutes of IR exposure a day but what does this accomplish? Thanks for any feedback.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-ls-yet.17644/post-190313>

Hey guys!
I have a dilemma. (surprise, surprise
I've been doing the leptin reset for over two months, and II have absolutely no cravings, everything's going well, but I'm pretty sure I should be leptin sensitive by now and I just don't know. I sleep well but I'm not energetic upon waking. I know I need more sleep and I'm trying to get to that point, but I don't know if my leptin is fixed. I've been itching to start with some weights, but if I start before my leptin is fixed will that create a problem? Is it possible that I'm leptin sensitive but still tired?
Any advice or tips are appreciated! Thanks!

I sleep well but I'm not energetic upon waking. I know I need more sleep and I'm trying to get to that point, but I don't know if my leptin is fixed. I've been itching to start with some weights, but if I start before my leptin is fixed will that create a problem? Is it possible that I'm leptin sensitive but still tired?
> Any advice or tips are appreciated! Thanks!

### Robert Haas — 2016-03-26T22:07:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-ls-yet.17644/post-191564>

Itching to start with weights is a good sign. It means your dopamine levels are coming up but still need to come up more. Weight training properly will help but don't over do it. Fixing leptin means fixing melotonin.
get UV light during the day, block blue light later in the day. Sleep when it is dark, as dark as possible. This should help making more melotonin which activates leptin and will raise morning cortisol.

# Thread: European Overharvesting in the New World and coastal defences - New York

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2016-03-07T19:52:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/european-overharvesting-in-the-new-world-and-coastal-defences-new-york.17648/post-190349>

> A recent study of past disturbance of the oyster beds in New York Harbor led by geoscientist Jonathan Woodruff and his doctoral student Christine Brandon of the University of Massachusetts Amherst is the first to link Europeans' overharvesting and disturbance of the ancient shellfish beds to loss of natural coastal defenses against floods and storm waves.
>
> Read more at: [http://phys.org/news/2016-03-york-harbor-oyster-beds-severe.html#jCp](http://phys.org/news/2016-03-york-harbor-oyster-beds-severe.html#jCp)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kombucha-success.17657/post-190658>

My mom (78yrs old) has been having skin issues for a few months (says she's allergic to the sun, a Dr. told her that 50yrs ago???)

Anyway, I have been giving her about 8-16ozs per day for the past 4 days and the skin issues seem to be clearing up. She had red itchy spots on her arms and her head should be in a Head and Shoulders (American dadruff shampoo) commercial.

She has had 1/2 of her stomach removed as well as her gall bladder. She suffers from what she calls panic attacks daily. takes meds for panic and thyroid (neither helps IMO).

We'll see how well she is in a couple weeks of simple common sense to health approach.
