# Thread: Staph infection success
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/staph-infection-success.17670/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Simon Wright — 2016-03-14T14:21:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/staph-infection-success.17670/post-190816>

Hi All

I run every morning along the beach with my dogs and this sometimes takes me through rivers. Its rainy season here in at the moment and the rivers here in Bali leave a bit to be desired (polluted).

Anyway i hit my shin and there was small cut and i went running with this cut not thinking too much about it. I noticed last Wednesday my leg was getting infected. I woke Thursday and i could see this infection was taking off.....by thursday arfternoon i could see that it was heading toward Staph ( I have had this 4 times before). By Thrsday evening after work my shin was going a dark purple colour with oozing pus and the heavy red colour around the edges. I had seen this before and got a bit worried.

I went and saw the local doctor and he said i should take antibiotics (oral ceftriaxone). As a rule i dont take oral antibiotics and asked for the injection of this to the site. He didnt have this injectible in his surgery (we are talking a developing world doctor here...that is local to my area). So i sat there and thought about what i would do.

So anyway I took this disinfectant wash he gave me called Oxoferin and some gauze and tape and went home. I then decided to markedly increase my intake of sulfur via homemade suerkraut at every meal, take 2 garlic cloves finely chopped and left for 15 minutes on the chopping board then add to my salad, have 1/2 teaspoon in a 1/2 glass of water of this very sulfurous salt a friend gave me. But the relevance to the group is what i was doing during the middle of the day. I own my own business and can juggle my day about. I have been coming home at midday and having a 20 min ice bath that has 660nm and 620nm led panels above it (not directly at my eyes) and then i go out into the midday sun which at the moment has a UV rating of 12-15 (as per d-minder). I have been doing this a few months to combat a heavy injury i incurred last year (30% of my humerus head was replaced from my hip)

I do front and back naked (in my garden)for 15-20 mins (each side) depending on cloud cover .

On last Friday i also exposed my infected shin to the direct light , and then covered it back up and went back to work. When i awoke on Saturday i noticed it had halted the spread (Usually Staph is voracious so halting it was impressive)

I then CT'd and exposed my shin twice per day on Saturday and Sunday and also exposed my shin (removed all bandages ) to the high UV midday sun.

Today I awoke and the skin has pretty much returned to normal....yes there is still a definite sore but all the infection has cleared up. I admittedly was amazed as I have been through hell with staph infections before and they are usually about 3 weeks battles....and not very pretty.

So the combination of the increased sulfur(via all above mentioned ), the high UV, The CTing, lots of water and being grounded most of the day with heaps of morning sun has help me beat an infection in 4 days which would usually last weeks.

A short note to say thanks to Jack and all here, I dont post so much as am usually run off my feet ....but am lurking sucking up all of this great information. Thank you all!

Simon

### lilly antic — 2016-03-14T17:34:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/staph-infection-success.17670/post-190828>

that is awesome, Simon!!!!!
Bacteria cannot survive in UV light, so good move.
I am also using it it for many purposes, only on a smaller scale
3 times so far after long distance traveling I got a fierce cold this winter. Very bad with watering eyes, sore throat etc, whereas the watering of the eyes is the worst and usually required heavy medication in the past.
All three times the cold was completely gone ( like in never existed) after several hours of sunlight. For me and my heavy cold symptoms nothing short of a miracle.
Then this weekend I came back from another long distance trip from NYC (second transcontinental flight this month-too much for me) and on top of the cold I got a fever and flulike symptoms.
Although we had an overcast day, I sat there for hours (with my mouth slightly open, so that light can enter and kill the throat infection)-the cold disappeared the same day completely, the fever was gone the next day. My body still feels beaten up-but another day of light and some seawater CT will surely fix this ....
What can I say.... for me my miracle drug!!!!

### Joe Gavin — 2016-03-14T19:56:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/staph-infection-success.17670/post-190840>

Cool stuff. I read that 460-470nm blue light is good for Staph as well. I read a bunch of stuff on pubmed/nih etc.

### Woodstock — 2024-10-10T19:15:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/staph-infection-success.17670/post-340764>

> [Jingpaw said:](/goto/post?id=340757)
>
> Ive had toe fungus for a few years. One toe now looks quite infected, now oozing yellow and the area around the big toe nail is red. astonishingly, no pain. I’m guessing this is potentially an ingrown toenail issue (I can’t see or feel typical pain of an ingrown) that is causing the infection. Anyway, I’m looking for good home Treatment recommendations, it seems pretty urgent now. I’d love to rip off the nail but I don’t think it’s ready to do that! My plan is to soak in Epsom salts and cut away at the nail to see if part of the nail is digging into my skin causing infection. I’m also going to use colloidal silver topically after soaking because tea tree oil is too strong!
> I am in northern latitude at the moment, and there’s very little UVB now, so I’m wondering if Sun is as effective as it would be otherwise. I know getting infected area in Sun is good any time of year, but with UVB near absent I’m wondering if there is something else I can do too.

I have had success applying Thyme essential oil to toenails that had fungus issues. I used Doterra but I would guess any high quality brand would work. Also sunlight of course. If you can wear sandals all the time. with Socks and shoes our feet rarely see the sun, so it makes sense that fungus would grow easily there.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-10-10T19:43:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/staph-infection-success.17670/post-340767>

My wife got a staph infection in the incision after a knee replacement years back. The Dr explained how serious this was and what would happen if the staph got into the joint, the new knee would be removed and she would spend a year in a wheel chair with no knee. He opened the incision and packed it with silver impregnated tape. Staph cleared up very quickly and did not get into the joint.

I actually asked him why all the expensive $300 creams and other treatments for infections when something as inexpensive as silver does the job . . . He looked at me like he had been caught and said “good question”.

### rtd — 2024-10-10T22:00:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/staph-infection-success.17670/post-340778>

> [Jingpaw said:](/goto/post?id=340770)
>
> Wow!! Amazing recovery.
> I’m going for some colloidal silver, thanks! And trying the thyme or oregano too.

My family has been using the following with good outcomes: [https://www.vermontcountrystore.com/personal-care/category/silver-savior](https://www.vermontcountrystore.com/personal-care/category/silver-savior)

### Annelieuk — 2024-10-13T10:51:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/staph-infection-success.17670/post-340918>

> [Jingpaw said:](/goto/post?id=340757)
>
> Ive had toe fungus for a few years. One toe now looks quite infected, now oozing yellow and the area around the big toe nail is red. astonishingly, no pain. I’m guessing this is potentially an ingrown toenail issue (I can’t see or feel typical pain of an ingrown) that is causing the infection. Anyway, I’m looking for good home Treatment recommendations, it seems pretty urgent now. I’d love to rip off the nail but I don’t think it’s ready to do that! My plan is to soak in Epsom salts and cut away at the nail to see if part of the nail is digging into my skin causing infection. I’m also going to use colloidal silver topically after soaking because tea tree oil is too strong!
> I am in northern latitude at the moment, and there’s very little UVB now, so I’m wondering if Sun is as effective as it would be otherwise. I know getting infected area in Sun is good any time of year, but with UVB near absent I’m wondering if there is something else I can do too.

I typically recommend bathing the tow in a bowel of warm water and 2 teaspoons of bicarbonate of soda for 10 mins and then applying iodine and keep it exposed to fresh air. At night put a small amount of bicarb onto the affected area, wrap it in a small bandage and after 2 nights the ingrown toe nail will soften and you can pick it out easily. The iodine works wonders to keep infection at bay.

# Thread: Ides of March:Four years of CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ides-of-march-four-years-of-ct.17689/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### shah78 — 2016-03-17T00:12:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ides-of-march-four-years-of-ct.17689/post-191024>

Four years and nearly 2000 hours in my green tub between 50-55 degrees. Best body composition(despite only thirty minutes of weight lifting/mo)/best erections/ least amount of anxiety/depression of my life, at age 59. No one really believes, it except my wife , and even she "forgets" sometimes.  Went from around 20 % body fat to around 10%bf. Eat about the same, just less hungry. Still don't enjoy the "WINTER" (FLORIDA) , but I find myself wearing shorts /tank tops, when friends and relatives are wearing sweaters. It all seems like science fiction..... Long live Jack Kruse and long live his memory!

### jwhb77 — 2016-03-21T17:35:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ides-of-march-four-years-of-ct.17689/post-191253>

Fantastic, shah78, thanks for sharing!

### Hunger4More — 2016-03-22T14:50:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ides-of-march-four-years-of-ct.17689/post-191286>

What does your CT schedule look like? How long and how often? Cheers!

### shah78 — 2016-03-22T18:10:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ides-of-march-four-years-of-ct.17689/post-191299>

i'M SORT OF A WIMP STILL. I admit that I CT more in the summer than the winter. I shoot for three to four half hour sessions a day. then I sun bath. I eat my meals and use the computer, read the newspaper and make phone calls on my land line during the session. In the summer I shoot for twenty hours a week

### lilreddgirl — 2016-03-23T07:12:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ides-of-march-four-years-of-ct.17689/post-191337>

I've always forced myself to go fully submerged apart from my head and it seems like getting the arms/shoulders/neck in the cold water is always the hardest part for me. I'm starting to think maybe i should spend more time just going up to chest height, and then i might actually spend a bit more time in my cold tub and it would seem less daunting on days i dont feel like getting in the cold.

# Thread: Made My Own DIY Quantlet!

### jackware — 2016-03-19T06:05:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/made-my-own-diy-quantlet.17697/post-191178>

During my 90 minute commute to work between 1pm and 2:30 pm each day I rest my left hand on the window sill, wrist facing up. The sun hits those veins and the wind cools my hand and arm real quick. After about 5-10 minutes I bring it inside the truck to warm back up and then repeat the process. Not bad, huh?

# Thread: Green space may make city kids smarter
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-space-may-make-city-kids-smarter.17707/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2016-03-21T21:27:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-space-may-make-city-kids-smarter.17707/post-191261>

[http://www.world-science.net/exclusives/150615\_greenspace.htm](http://www.world-science.net/exclusives/150615_greenspace.htm)

Of course, the only thing not mentioned in this article is sunshine...

**Exposure to green spaces may help boost children’s cognitive development, according to a study, which attributed part of the effect to lower pollution.

Researchers monitored changes in cognitive measures every three months between January 2012 and March 2013 among 2,593 grade-school age children ages 7 to 10 in Barcelona, Spain.
**
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

****Over the course of a year, exposure to greenness within and around schools—as determined by satellite data—was linked with enhanced mental ability to continuously manipulate and update information, according to the investigators. These abilities are called working memory and superior working memory, respectively.

Reduced inattentive was another benefit, according to the study.

The results were independent of ethnicity, maternal education, and parental employment, said the researchers, Payam Dadvand of the Center for Research in Environmental Epidemiology in Barcelona and colleagues.

The results linked every increase in total surrounding greenness by a quartile to a 5 percent increase in working memory, 6 percent increase in superior working memory, and 1 percent reduction in inattentiveness.

A computer modeling analysis suggested that elemental carbon, a common traffic-related air pollutant, might account for 20 percent to 65 percent of the estimated links between school greenness and cognitive development.

No significant link turned up between exposure to greenness at home and cognitive measures, the researchers said. Given the soaring rates of global urbanization, expanding green spaces at schools might be a step toward improving academic attainment among children, according to the authors.

Dadvand and colleagues said previous studies had linked green space to better mental health, but whether this might influence cognitive development specifically was unclear.

“Contact with nature is thought to play a crucial and irreplaceable role in brain development,” the researchers wrote, reporting their findings this week in the early online edition of the research journal _Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences_.

“Natural environments including green spaces provide children with unique opportunities such as inciting engagement, risk taking, discovery, creativity, mastery and control, strengthening sense of self, inspiring basic emotional states including sense of wonder, and enhancing psychological restoration, which are suggested to influence positively different aspects of cognitive development.”****


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Replacing windows with UV transmitting plastic
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/replacing-windows-with-uv-transmitting-plastic.17785/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lilreddgirl — 2016-04-07T23:28:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/replacing-windows-with-uv-transmitting-plastic.17785/post-192276>

I remember someone asking about this but can't find the member's name or initial question. They had a picture of themselves and family including kids as a gravatar.

Well I found these mentioned elsewhere online

**Rohm and Haas' UVT Plexiglass, or American Cyanamid's Acrylite UVT**

These are plastic that can be used to replace glass windows.

A source also suggested to use the thinnest plastic (as they come in varied thicknesses) to further improve UV transmission and also because even thin plastic would be a superior insulator to glass (for temperature reasons). Get ready to bleach your colored furniture with that incoming UV!

### thomas — 2016-04-09T21:27:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/replacing-windows-with-uv-transmitting-plastic.17785/post-192435>

# Thread: "Fasting Shrinks ECT and Lowers Heteroplasmy" JK
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-shrinks-ect-and-lowers-heteroplasmy-jk.17787/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Danny — 2016-04-07T23:58:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-shrinks-ect-and-lowers-heteroplasmy-jk.17787/post-192284>

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...-start-a-thread-on-fasting.17779/#post-192248](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/in-which-forum-should-i-start-a-thread-on-fasting.17779/#post-192248)

### Danny — 2016-04-08T00:14:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-shrinks-ect-and-lowers-heteroplasmy-jk.17787/post-192288>

47 hr fast : Started at 130P and Ended at 1230P 2 days later

Ending the first 1/2 day w/o food was my normal for about the last 3 weeks.

2nd day: I decided to skip my one meal and fast. Got a good amount of sunshine this day. Brain was functioning a bit higher and nothing seemed to rock me even though I had, for me what would have been, a stressful work situation later in the day. Got tired early... went to bed at 9P.

3rd Day: Had nausea in the morning. Felt as though was going to vomit. BM relieved feeling a bit. Took 1 charcoal tab, drank water, and got out in sun naked. 2 hours later felt better. A little lack of energy and maybe a tiny bit of muscle soreness. Lower back and upper back/neck usually bother me. On this day the discomfort was almost completely gone. Finished the last day strong after the meal. Over ate a bit, but not feeling it too much.

Ended my fast in a sloppy manner: 2 doz oysters, 5 - 6 oz of ground beef, 1/2 cup of rice w/ salmon roe, approx 1 lb raspberries, banana, 2 dates, and 6 marshmallows (I know that was stupid... but sue me... I was feeling good and it's important to be honest)

### shah78 — 2016-04-08T00:24:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-shrinks-ect-and-lowers-heteroplasmy-jk.17787/post-192289>

MARSHMALLOWS?????? A hallowed 3,000 year old TRADITIONAL AMAZONIAN FAST BREAKING FOOD. Practiced only by the High Priests and Kings.

### Danny — 2016-04-08T01:51:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-shrinks-ect-and-lowers-heteroplasmy-jk.17787/post-192297>

I should have known you'd roast me on that one!

### caroline — 2016-04-08T02:42:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-shrinks-ect-and-lowers-heteroplasmy-jk.17787/post-192303>

Danny ....were the marshmallows roasted over an open campfire with your son????? that would be excellent in m opinion!

sometimes shit happens! How did you feel after? the day after? That was a great biohack!

You have felt like crap for way too long......

### Jenny S — 2016-04-09T05:18:48+0300

### Danny — 2016-04-11T02:17:32+0300

> [Jenny S said:](/goto/post?id=192390)
>

breaking a fast w/ marshmallows is way better than not fasting and not eating marshmallows in my brief experience

### Danny — 2016-04-11T02:31:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-shrinks-ect-and-lowers-heteroplasmy-jk.17787/post-192484>

Finished 2nd 48hr fast: A few notes

This time no nausea at all. I realized during my last fast that I had salt and water around hour # 32. Woke up the next morning w/ salty mucus and nausea. Didn't put 2 + 2 together apparently.

I didn't seem to be counting the hours this time. I just went about my day in a more productive way than normal I might add. I ended my fast around 230P this time. I wanted to be fasted while getting sun exposure around solar noon. My energy felt steadier this time... probably b/c no salt induced nausea My mental functioning is definitely higher when fasting. I am way more productive and better mood.

Ended my fast in a little more cautious manner: 20 oz frozen raspberries, 4-5 oz of rare salmon w/ dill, few raw beet greens, cooked kale and onions, 3-4oz rare burger w/ dill, rosemary, green onions, few slices of bacon, 2 heirloom carrots, few mulberries from my mulberry tree , a little dandelion from the yard, an apple and 1 lb of marshmallows (ha! just kidding about the marshmallows- none this time).

Next, maybe a three day fast. Will see how it goes as the UV index climbs higher here.

### caroline — 2016-04-11T10:06:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-shrinks-ect-and-lowers-heteroplasmy-jk.17787/post-192494>

How do you feel Danny when eating that much after fasting? In my experience with fasting - they tell you to introduce food slowly ....and marshmallows not until the 5th day!

I have fasted for 12 days and loved it .... would have gone longer but for some reason had to end it - but not because of how I felt.

I had so much energy and I may have been able to understand Jack's blogs if I would have had them way back when!!

### Danny — 2016-04-11T15:34:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-shrinks-ect-and-lowers-heteroplasmy-jk.17787/post-192503>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=192494)
>
> How do you feel Danny when eating that much after fasting? In my experience with fasting - they tell you to introduce food slowly ....and marshmallows not until the 5th day!
>
> I have fasted for 12 days and loved it .... would have gone longer but for some reason had to end it - but not because of how I felt.
>
> I had so much energy and I may have been able to understand Jack's blogs if I would have had them way back when!!

I feel totally fine. As a matter of fact my stomach can handle things, like raw greens, that would ordinarily be a little tough. Now, I've also only fasted for 2 days not 12. That's awesome by the way! Don't know much about the veracity of stomach shrinking phenomenon when fasting.

My MO for now is to find out what works best for me... either chipping away at my % HeteroplasmY w/ 2 and 3 day fasts or going for the longer fasts for maybe quicker results. Is one 12 day fast like 50 two day fasts or does this depend on the individual (assuming you are getting UV)? My hunch is telling me that the health benefits of fasting are exponential. The 2nd day builds upon the first, 3rd day builds upon the second, 4th upon the 3rd... and so on in a non-linear fashion. I'm trying to understand all of this HeteroplasmY stuff in my own way. I have so many questions. Fasting while getting doses of the strong Sun (UV 9) is working well for me at the moment.

Do you remember what time of year you did your fast? Were you getting strong UV? Btw on the 2nd night of fasted sleep, my body feels electric! I have these feel good sensations coursing throughout w/ like a whole body armor of goosebumps. Can't wait to do the 3 day.

### Danny — 2016-05-08T17:14:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-shrinks-ect-and-lowers-heteroplasmy-jk.17787/post-194366>

Hour 42 of fast. Hunger subsided, but been fatigued ever since I woke up today. Sun helped some and ACV helped a little more. Gonna try a little nicotine.

# Thread: If UV and IR are invisible what do we see from blacklights and heat lamps ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-uv-and-ir-are-invisible-what-do-we-see-from-blacklights-and-heat-lamps.17793/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lilreddgirl — 2016-04-09T05:48:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-uv-and-ir-are-invisible-what-do-we-see-from-blacklights-and-heat-lamps.17793/post-192393>

If UV and IR are invisible what do we see from blacklights and heat lamps ? There is certainly "light" to be seen...

### thomas — 2016-04-09T21:11:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-uv-and-ir-are-invisible-what-do-we-see-from-blacklights-and-heat-lamps.17793/post-192433>

you see visible light. lamps aren't perfect and there is always unwanted unefficient "light".note.. experiments have shown that some people can see ir up to about 1000nm if i recall.
But yeah it's uneficiencies in the design of lamps basically.

# Thread: A quick trip to beach before surgery?

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### michellehp — 2016-04-12T17:24:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-quick-trip-to-beach-before-surgery.17803/post-192559>

hi- I'm a real newbie at this but I figured I'd ask anyway. My husband (40 yr old contractor/ recreational tennis player) has a torn hip labrum (injured 10 months ago) that he is having surgery to repair in 2 weeks. I was trying to talk him into a quick trip to Gulf Shores this weekend as I believe it will be beneficial for grounding, water, sunlight and all. We get plenty of sun here in New Orleans and actually spent this whole past weekend outside and in the pool. We eat a good amount of seafood, grass-fed, etc. but was thinking this could give him an edge for surgery. I am also trying to convince him to visualize the whole process as a positive one and draw it, wrote it down. I think he's starting to look at me like he doesn't need "all that". Btw- I have all the peri-operative suggestions from Jack that I could find as far as sleep, light, and supplements.

# Thread: CT with aquarium chiller
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-aquarium-chiller.17814/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### omegajosh — 2016-04-15T09:48:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-aquarium-chiller.17814/post-192717>

I have been looking around at the commercial CT baths. They are extortionately priced. Trying to find a cheaper alternative I stumbled across aquarium water chillers. At a 10th of the price does anyone think they might be a decent alternative rigged up to a bath?

### fitness@home — 2016-04-15T13:01:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-aquarium-chiller.17814/post-192729>

I use a chiller that keeps my CT tank at a steady 55 degrees. Was lucky enough to find a good one on Craigslist and only paid $150 for it.

There are a couple of others on the forum that have used them too.

### omegajosh — 2016-04-15T13:16:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-aquarium-chiller.17814/post-192730>

Great. Can you send me a link for the one you use?
Do you leave it chilled 24/7? I was thinking about just switching it on for an hour before CT to chill down to save electricity/environment.

### fitness@home — 2016-04-15T15:58:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-aquarium-chiller.17814/post-192744>

I think it is an Aqua Logic Delta Star. Paperwork is at home, so not positive. We have it set on a timer. It comes on about 2 hours before I get home from work. It only runs 3-4 hours per day. I always turn it off before getting in - don't want any extra nnemf field around me.

My stock tank is mounted in a cabinet that my husband built for it. There are pictures somewhere in my journal (On the Pathways to Methylation, post #650). I keep a foam cover on it when not in use to keep it cool and to also keep out dust, pet fur, etc... Can't stand when the bottom or sides start to feel "slimy". I do put Epsom salts in when we fill it, in the hope it stays clean longer.

Usually drain/replace the water about once per month. In fact, it will be refilled this weekend. We drained it before going on vacation recently.

### QuantumRadical — 2016-04-15T22:40:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-aquarium-chiller.17814/post-192786>

I have been using this one for a few years. [https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000TMC2CI/ref=oh\_aui\_search\_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000TMC2CI/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

Depending on the temp of the water, the desired temp, and the amount of water to be cooled, you can determine rate of cooling. My tub size allows a 6 degree F decrease per hour. Ymmv.

### omegajosh — 2016-04-16T13:57:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-aquarium-chiller.17814/post-192825>

6 degree F per hour sounds fine. I am only looking to cool my tap water by a further 10-15 degrees.

### travolttt — 2020-06-23T12:43:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-aquarium-chiller.17814/post-290456>

hey, I'm just starting using a chiller, and can't find the right one. Any suggestions? Also, I'm new at this and wanna help other newbies like me. I have been looking for the right decorations for my aquarium for a veery long time and came across this site that helped me a lot. It has all the information about [live rocks](https://arcreef.com/live-rock/live-rock-guide/) beginning with what are they and ending with helping you choose the right ones for you! This site is a true salvation!

# Thread: So stupidly, I recently had cataract surgery
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-stupidly-i-recently-had-cataract-surgery.17817/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### joangtaylor@gmail.com — 2016-04-15T17:16:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-stupidly-i-recently-had-cataract-surgery.17817/post-192755>

Last December.
I am a 62-year-old female. Right after my cataract surgery, I read here about the deleterious UV blocking in the implanted lenses. I'm prediabetic and my fasting blood sugar rose consistently about 10 points after the surgery.
How to cope? Get morning sun on my skin; cold baths; minimize EMF exposure; install some UV light in my house? Anything else? I apologize if the answer to my question is elsewhere in this forum or on Dr. Jack's blogs. I have zero scientific background and get totally befuddled in the blogs.

### nonchalant — 2016-04-15T19:19:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-stupidly-i-recently-had-cataract-surgery.17817/post-192770>

Hi Joan, don't fret. You still have UV-sensitive neuropsin in your cornea and skin. You need to be serious about exposure to sunlight all day. It will eventually fix the diabetes also. As for the UV lights, where do you live?
Have you seen these posts?
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...ses-implanted-fear-not-neuropsin-helps.16972/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/if-your-patients-have-had-lenses-implanted-fear-not-neuropsin-helps.16972/)
[https://www.jackkruse.com/the-dark-knight/](https://www.jackkruse.com/the-dark-knight/)

My parents have both had cataract surgery and their health has really deteriorated afterwards. Wish I could convince them to get more than a few minutes of sunshine daily.

# Thread: Is cold smoked salmon raw?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-cold-smoked-salmon-raw.17820/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cmegan — 2016-04-15T19:46:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-cold-smoked-salmon-raw.17820/post-192775>

Sorry for the possibly stupid question, but is cold smoked salmon considered to be raw? Im assuming so but just want to double check, try to eat as much raw fish as possible! Thank you!
Chrissy

### Inger — 2016-04-20T17:13:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-cold-smoked-salmon-raw.17820/post-193074>

Yes it is
You can tell on the texture when something is raw or cooked... the proteins get a different texture........very different

I can even tell pretty well by taste and texture now if a cheese is raw or pasteurized.... they too get different when heated
but way less than meat or seafood... or veggies

### Benny Hammond — 2017-06-23T21:27:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-cold-smoked-salmon-raw.17820/post-216931>

I have been picking up this pack of cold smoked salmon twice a week at my local Whole Foods. Is this a good source of DHA? Is there any loss and/denaturing of the DHA in the cold smoked process? I just started eating fish in February. I'm also taking Mitolife's DHA. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### drezy — 2017-06-23T23:44:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-cold-smoked-salmon-raw.17820/post-216936>

Any cooking process, even the cold smoking temperature lower than 80F, is going to change the proteins and DHA.

### Benny Hammond — 2017-06-24T01:34:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-cold-smoked-salmon-raw.17820/post-216948>

I was going to pick that one up but didn't because it said it was farmed. Maybe that doesn't matter. These guys who have fish at my local farmers market use a giant circular containment net which they move around in the sea. It seems that the fish would still be eating wild. The fishermen don't feed them anything.

### drezy — 2017-06-24T01:57:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-cold-smoked-salmon-raw.17820/post-216958>

> These guys who have fish at my local farmers market use a giant circular containment net which they move around in the sea.

Oh I do like that method too. My boy did a 5th grade report on this technique and got an Oxford professor to reply and add content to the report! Consequently he's the author of my favorite ever food history book AND took the time to respond to my then 5th grader. If you're a foodie it's worth checking out I promise:
[https://www.amazon.com/Near-Thousan...743227409/ref=mt\_paperback?\_encoding=UTF8&me=](https://www.amazon.com/Near-Thousand-Tables-History-Food/dp/0743227409/ref=mt_paperback?_encoding=UTF8&me=)

After sun and CT my son is still mystified that I, a former goose stepping paleo person, don't care what he eats at least for one item. He's shocked when I tell him the item isn't shitty enough and he should probably find a shittier item. Here's why:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...g-importance-of-vegetables.19622/#post-215970](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/for-jack-raw-food-tcm-yin-yang-importance-of-vegetables.19622/#post-215970)

Obviously I don't eat crappy or encourage my child to do so all the time. I just need to teach him that it's about the light and not about the food. This is the best way that I've found.

# Thread: Resetting the Eye Clock
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resetting-the-eye-clock.17834/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael Polito — 2016-04-18T18:24:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resetting-the-eye-clock.17834/post-192976>

How long does it take (days, weeks) before i can get back on track (Circadian) . My sleep sucks, I just started getting out in the sun between 8-9 am (sometimes longer) a few days ago. Eating seafood everyday. Sometimes grass fed beef, and pastured chicken, Dark Leafy Greens, dont eat starch and sugar carbs. Im in great fitness, but cant seem to sleep through the night. I get to bed by 11pm, but up between 2:30 and 4am and toss and turn for 2 hours. I also shut off the lights when it gets dark outside, and use yellow/amber classes if watching tv. Will this take some time before my body kicks in and gets back on track? Thank you for any help.

### Joe Gavin — 2016-04-18T20:24:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resetting-the-eye-clock.17834/post-192978>

It will depend on how long you lived your life in an asynchronous manner, how strict you are with minimizing ambient light at night, and all the other shit going in your life. Play around with it. Try to go to bed earlier and wake up earlier. Eat breakfast while the sun is out (the light will entrain your central clock, and the food will entrain your peripheral clocks--yes, there clock genes everywhere!). Perhaps cut back on working out, and keep it to daylight hours as well (working out at night can mess you up). And definitely do NOT eat after dark; that's when your body is supposed to be resting and regenerating--those programs can't work if you are still digesting food. Unplug your wifi at night and shut off the electricity to your bedroom at a minimum. Then get your barefeet on the ground in the morning, and the AM sunlight in your eyes. Try all that and see how you feel. Doesn't cost a thing except thinking it through a bit.

### Michael Polito — 2016-04-18T20:40:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resetting-the-eye-clock.17834/post-192980>

Good stuff here Joe. I do eat late at night, sometimes because of my schedule. I will try eating before i go to teach my classes.. I love my coffee, but have eliminated that after 12pm. I'm getting it now. I'm a bare footer, so that is cool. Thanks so much Joe.

### Joe Gavin — 2016-04-18T20:49:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resetting-the-eye-clock.17834/post-192982>

and where your skypers/blueblockers to any evening event.

### Mover — 2016-04-18T21:05:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resetting-the-eye-clock.17834/post-192984>

Yes. Just starting doing that 3 days ago. Found a real nice pair of yellow tinted lenses. Shooting glasses color. Round frames. Very cool looking. Also have my blue blocker Amber/orange.

### Michael Polito — 2016-04-19T16:12:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resetting-the-eye-clock.17834/post-193018>

Hey Joe, took your suggestion and did not eat in the evening. Had my last meal at 6pm. Cup a herb tea at night. Kept my yellow lens glasses on while watching the game. Lights off. I slept right through the night till 6:30 am. Perfect ! Thanks man ! I think that was my major problem. Eating too late when the body is supposed to be resting.

### Joe Gavin — 2016-04-21T17:16:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resetting-the-eye-clock.17834/post-193163>

> [Michael Polito said:](/goto/post?id=193018)
>
> Hey Joe, took your suggestion and did not eat in the evening. Had my last meal at 6pm. Cup a herb tea at night. Kept my yellow lens glasses on while watching the game. Lights off. I slept right through the night till 6:30 am. Perfect ! Thanks man ! I think that was my major problem. Eating too late when the body is supposed to be resting.

That's awesome Michael! Its the details that matter!

# Thread: Baby Steps...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17843/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HopingMom — 2016-04-21T14:49:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17843/post-193151>

Small success, but just had to share.
My son has old man syndrome - we can't keep him awake past 7:30 and he is up at 4:30am-always.
We just started night light blocking and some CT (disguised as dares ) about 6 weeks ago for my teenage son.
He half-assed used the glasses for the first 1.5 weeks...then he started putting them on...then he started asking that I get them for him closer to sun-down.
The last 2 days I have had to wake him up for school...this is not something that happens...

I am sincerely grateful for this group, Dr Kruse and this site.

### Scompy — 2016-04-23T02:57:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17843/post-193283>

This time last year, my 7-year old was playing video games at night and was getting allergies, colds and flus like no tomorrow. Very electrosensitive last year. Nearly all of his conditions are totally gone now. The major change is cycling with the sun and wearing blue-blockers, but also that he's not playing on game consoles and I've mitigated and hacked the entire home for nnEMF, and I've been making a point to pump a lot of water into him every day. It's night and day. You can read my timeline over at my journal to see what I did.

### HopingMom — 2016-04-30T02:16:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17843/post-193863>

Thanks Scompy, I will check it out!

# Thread: Making a Kerrygold Butter run...Canadian style
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-kerrygold-butter-run-canadian-style.17862/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rubicon — 2016-04-25T19:13:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-kerrygold-butter-run-canadian-style.17862/post-193457>

I really like Kerrygold Butter. I could eat around a stick a day if I had easy access to it. Unfortunately Kerrygold is unavailable in Canada (the local dairy industry is very protective), and there are no ideal substitutes that have the same quality and affordability.

As a result, anytime I go to the US, I load up my suitcase with Kerrygold and cross my fingers that the customs agents are in a good mood. Canadians can bring 20lbs of butter, but anything over a few sticks may be subject to high ~300% tariffs. A recent trip back from Hawaii worked out great. 70 sticks of unsalted, and 10 sticks of salted Kerrygold!

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Whole Foods had unsalted on the east side of Oahu. A few hours before my flight I went to Whole Foods, transferred the butter to one of their freezers (I stashed it in with the frozen vegetables), and returned a few hours later to purchase it. When checking out, the clerk was pretty amazed but mentioned that it wasn't the first time she'd seen someone buying such a large quantity. Perhaps I'm not the first Canadian to make a mass purchase of Kerrygold...

I packed a few ziplock bags of ice into the luggage to help cool it and all the butter made it back to Canada nice and cold 14 hours later. Luckily, no questions asked by customs.

### Jenelle — 2016-04-25T21:34:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-kerrygold-butter-run-canadian-style.17862/post-193464>

That is some serious Kerrygold dedication, man.

### fitness@home — 2016-04-25T21:57:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-kerrygold-butter-run-canadian-style.17862/post-193465>

I guess we Americans should be really thankful for the easy access at COSTCO (and the good prices)

### shah78 — 2016-04-25T22:00:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-kerrygold-butter-run-canadian-style.17862/post-193466>

How much did you pay? My seat of the pants guess: $5/half lb.

### Rubicon — 2016-04-25T22:31:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-kerrygold-butter-run-canadian-style.17862/post-193471>

> [shah78 said:](/goto/post?id=193466)
>
> How much did you pay?

Unfortunately they were $4.69 per stick in Hawaii...a little more than the usual ~$3 per stick in California.

There's actually a sortof black market here where people occasionally sell the stuff for $20 per stick. If I'm able to build up enough of a cache before winter, I might try selling a bit and see if I can make enough to pay for my flight. Lol

### Inger — 2016-04-26T13:33:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-kerrygold-butter-run-canadian-style.17862/post-193529>

Oh.. that is expensive! I get kerrygold butter here regular price 1,70 euro/stick
and often they are on sale for 1 euro/stick......
But I do better with raw organic grassfed butter..... which is more expensive tho so kerrygold is my choice if I need butter and I have to save money

I buy my raw butter from here (Austria), when I can spend more money....

[https://schaetzeausoesterreich.at/rohmilchbutter](https://schaetzeausoesterreich.at/rohmilchbutter)

but it is 20 euro / 1 kg! But excellent quality... like a dream.....

### endless — 2016-05-01T20:11:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-kerrygold-butter-run-canadian-style.17862/post-193972>

I buy Organic Valley Pasture Butter in the U.S., which is only available from May to September (the rest of the time the cows get grain). Luckily I am close to the border so can stock up.

### jenaf — 2016-05-01T20:23:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-kerrygold-butter-run-canadian-style.17862/post-193975>

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=193972)
>

The organic store Pomme (poco) here has it year round. Kerry Gold. If you want to stock up while here.

### jenaf — 2016-05-01T20:25:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-kerrygold-butter-run-canadian-style.17862/post-193976>

I think it was Kerry gold. I saw it and sighed... Wish I could eat butter. My body is just not ready for it yet.

### Rubicon — 2016-05-01T21:19:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-kerrygold-butter-run-canadian-style.17862/post-193984>

> [jenaf said:](/goto/post?id=193975)

Could it have been Kiwi butter you saw at Pomme? [https://twitter.com/helenavan/status/688257332304728066](https://twitter.com/helenavan/status/688257332304728066)

I've searched in Canada a lot and have never found Kerrygold sold at any store. Kiwi butter is not bad, but it sells for $12 per stick here in Alberta and it's not always in stock. Right now for example the supermarket shelves are empty. I would consider switching to Kiwi if it were a little more reasonably priced, but I do prefer Kerrygold.

I had a difficult time with butter until I started taking Betaine HCL. Now I seem to be able to digest it reasonably well.

### endless — 2016-05-03T06:37:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-kerrygold-butter-run-canadian-style.17862/post-194059>

Holy shit, $12? Is that for a pound, at least? That's crazy..... I've seen the Kiwi here but haven't tried it.

### Rubicon — 2016-05-03T07:42:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-kerrygold-butter-run-canadian-style.17862/post-194060>

$12 for a little 250g stick

I think it's maybe down to $10.99 these days. I took the above pic the first time I found the stuff in 2015. 3-days later when I went back to the supermarket, the shelf was empty:

I guess people were pretty desperate. Luckily it was re-stocked a few weeks later.

### Purple Haze — 2016-06-09T01:02:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-a-kerrygold-butter-run-canadian-style.17862/post-196189>

> [Rubicon said:](/goto/post?id=193457)
>
> I really like Kerrygold Butter. I could eat around a stick a day if I had easy access to it. Unfortunately Kerrygold is unavailable in Canada (the local dairy industry is very protective), and there are no ideal substitutes that have the same quality and affordability.
>
> As a result, anytime I go to the US, I load up my suitcase with Kerrygold and cross my fingers that the customs agents are in a good mood. Canadians can bring 20lbs of butter, but anything over a few sticks may be subject to high ~300% tariffs. A recent trip back from Hawaii worked out great. 70 sticks of unsalted, and 10 sticks of salted Kerrygold!
>
>
>
>
> Whole Foods had unsalted on the east side of Oahu. A few hours before my flight I went to Whole Foods, transferred the butter to one of their freezers (I stashed it in with the frozen vegetables), and returned a few hours later to purchase it. When checking out, the clerk was pretty amazed but mentioned that it wasn't the first time she'd seen someone buying such a large quantity. Perhaps I'm not the first Canadian to make a mass purchase of Kerrygold...
>
> I packed a few ziplock bags of ice into the luggage to help cool it and all the butter made it back to Canada nice and cold 14 hours later. Luckily, no questions asked by customs.

That's awesome! I do the same thing, but I'm only a 20 minute car drive from the US border/Traders Joe's so I can stock up once every few months with out going to Hawaii. : )
...and yes, I saw Kiwi Pure in the grocery store for $12 too! Insane. I did find a local cheese company here in the Fraser Valley (British Columbia) that makes grass fed butter. It tastes absolutely amazing...I could eat it like ice cream...but it isn't cheap either. Less expensive than Kiwi Pure but better tasting.

# Thread: CALLING ALL STARFISH!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calling-all-starfish.17866/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Curves — 2016-04-27T01:50:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calling-all-starfish.17866/post-193628>

A few weeks ago I posted a thank you to Dr. Jack Kruse for being the catalyst to my rebirth in the Quantum Health FB Group.
The number of people who shared their own stories and thanks to Dr. Kruse was profound.
That gave me an idea.
Now, I know there are a lot of mixed feelings about technology in this community but for anyone who'd like to share their story live, I thought it might be fun to host a live Blab session.
If you're unfamiliar with Blab it's a live streaming format that allows up to four people to video stream at the same time, while an unlimited number of people can attend in the audience. You can check it out here at [https://blab.im/](https://blab.im/).
The format of this particular Blab won't be super sciencey but rather a place where we can share our stories and biohacks in a supportive friendly environment, and learn from each other.
If this resonates with you and you'd like to share your story live or just hang out in the audience, please let me know in the comments or direct message me.
I'll post the date of my first Blab shortly.
In love and appreciation for this community,
Elaine

### caroline — 2016-04-27T03:15:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calling-all-starfish.17866/post-193639>

Great idea Elaine! I have been thinking something similar lately.....

Dr. K. gets so frustrated with us because we miss important stuff sometimes.

I am finding over and over in conversations I have with others here ......It is easier to see the gaps in what someone else is doing/or not doing/missing - than for ourselves.

That is what Dr. K. sees clearly - all our weak spots that we aren't seeing for ourselves - for lots of reasons I think.
Everyone is so busy with life. There are kids to take care of, partners to support, a job to go to, bills to pay, keep the house clean, do the laundry, cooking......how endless is this list????

Dr. K. has support staff in his clinic, in his surgery and at home..... he can concentrate on what he does best.

I have no problem with seeing the forest .... but I do have a problem with missing the trees. Is anyone else like that!

I do get the overall picture ....but often can't get out of my own way to make necessary adjustments ....unless someone \[Dr. K.\] clubs me over the head! often by saying "the lights are on - but is anyone home?

### Rubicon — 2016-04-27T03:26:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calling-all-starfish.17866/post-193640>

# Thread: grounding mats
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-mats.17871/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hello — 2016-04-27T14:57:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-mats.17871/post-193690>

Hello all,

I was just watching this youtube:

It shows a guy whose voltage goes way down when he is on a grounding mat near his computer gear. He's making the point that you need one when you are working on a computer or surrounded by EMF.

I decided not to get a grounding mat because I read here and elsewhere that it makes you an antenna for EMF.

What does it mean to be an 'antenna' for EMF? If the guy in the video above - if his voltage in his body is going low, does that mean his body isn't acting as an antenna in this instance?

I normally ground for about an hour outside each day and preferably in the sun but I'd love to ground for longer if I can find a way. is there something on this I should be reading?

any thoughts let me know!

### caroline — 2016-05-01T08:16:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-mats.17871/post-193930>

Grounding mats are kind of iffy ..... When you plug it in you could be getting dirty electricity from the outlet.

I am sure lots of folks here have hacked this for themselves.

You need to consider a lot of things .....like overhead power lines and towers near you and smart meters....

### jwhb77 — 2016-05-01T18:14:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-mats.17871/post-193954>

As Caroline said, there are a number of factors to consider... be careful, pay attention to your experiences, & think things through before experimenting...

Personally, I'd never use an electrical outlet to ground an earthing mat or blanket... much better to run a covered wire outside & ground it to a copper grounding rod that is planted 10 or more feet deep into the earth. This avoids the dirty electricity that can be lurking in your home's wiring, as well as any transient currents near the earth's surface that could get transmitted via a shallower grounding rod. Most big-box home improvement-type stores carry these kinds of rods. Note: You might need some 'muscle' to help you drive it into the ground, though, depending on the composition of your soil.

If you want to take more extensive precautions, you could also build a mini Faraday cage over the exposed end of the grounding rod (though not touching it), to prevent the possibility of any antenna-like reception & transmission of ambient currents. This is probably an extreme measure, but could be relevant depending on your local EMF conditions.

Another possibility is using a body voltage meter to test your setup & its various configurations.

Above all, whatever you do or don't do, I recommend keeping a detailed, running log of what you've done & the effects that any or all of these measures have on you personally. Generally speaking, if you're not testing, you're just guessing. Hope this helps

### ZenBeach — 2019-06-30T00:04:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-mats.17871/post-273462>

# Thread: Which light bulb looks most like sunlight?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-light-bulb-looks-most-like-sunlight.17872/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Optimalbound — 2016-04-27T19:01:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-light-bulb-looks-most-like-sunlight.17872/post-193702>

This video compares the spectrum from incandescent, fluorescent and two different kinds of LED lights. No halogen lights, unfortunately, but informative nonetheless. It also shows how badly some LED lights pulsate with the a/c frequency.

The peaks in the blue parts of the spectrum from LEDs and fluorescent lights are clearly visible, but they seem a bit smaller than I have seen in other analyses, for some reason. My understanding of the Bluetech lenses is that they narrowly filter out most of these peaks but leave the rest of the blue largely intact, which is why they appear only slightly tinted, and which is why they make sense to wear in artificial lighting during daytime but don't really replace blueblockers for use after sunset.

### Curves — 2016-04-27T19:19:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-light-bulb-looks-most-like-sunlight.17872/post-193706>

> [Optimalbound said:](/goto/post?id=193702)
>
> This video compares the spectrum from incandescent, fluorescent and two different kinds of LED lights. No halogen lights, unfortunately, but informative nonetheless. It also shows how badly some LED lights pulsate with the a/c frequency.
>
> The peaks in the blue parts of the spectrum from LEDs and fluorescent lights are clearly visible, but they seem a bit smaller than I have seen in other analyses, for some reason. My understanding of the Bluetech lenses is that they narrowly filter out most of these peaks but leave the rest of the blue largely intact, which is why they appear only slightly tinted, and which is why they make sense to wear in artificial lighting during daytime but don't really replace blueblockers for use after sunset.
>

The slowmo pulsing light finale at the end was truly eye opening to watch. Very dramatic.
What do you make of the low spike in blue light in the LED bulbs? From his analysis it seems like only fluorescent bulbs fail to mimic natural sunlight.
Thanks for posting this!

# Thread: A Visual Model of a particle... honey dreamed this up, thought I'd share it
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-visual-model-of-a-particle-honey-dreamed-this-up-thought-id-share-it.17878/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2016-04-29T05:45:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-visual-model-of-a-particle-honey-dreamed-this-up-thought-id-share-it.17878/post-193805>

RFC: proposal - model for teaching: Medium Mechanics

thank you in advance for the time you spend in reviewing this proposal.
I will summarize in outline form periodically so that you can gauge your
interest in the various sections. To whit:

Proposal: reinterpret current knowledge of atomic and sub-atomic
particle dynamics to be consistent with a more intuitive model.

The intent is not to invent new science, but to provide a model which is
easier to visualize and grasp.

Summary description of proposed theory / model:

The last completely intuitive model for particle physics is the Bohr
atom: a depiction of large point nucleus with a single small point
electron orbiting in a perfect circle. A horribly simplistic
description, but food for the thought of a generation - because it was
visual.

I am proposing a new model for particles which can be more readily
visualized. In it's simplest terms, I propose that the dynamics of
particles can be described in terms of the universe expanding. From
this notion, I am producing a visual model of the universe's basic
particle.

Outline of model:

Part 1
\- Description of model
\- Visualizations To Aid in Explaining the Model
\- Is the Model Justified
\- The model is reasonable because...
\- Does the Model Add Anything
\- Tetrahedral Reduction

Part 2
\- Theory of an electron
\- Theory of an positron
\- Theory of an proton

Part 1

\- Description of model

As stated in the brief introduction, the basis of this model is
that the expanding of the universe and the dynamics of particles is
fundamentally the same thing.

I mean by "expanding universe" the doppler red shift seen in the
solar radiation of all of the stars in the sky (with a small number of
exceptions in nearby stars). The mapping that these red shifts imply is
the basis for the big bang theory of cosmology. Most interestingly, the
mappings imply that the universe is expanding.

\- Visualizations To Aid in Explaining the Model

The first visualization of this model is a fundamental particle as
a microscopic reflection of the universe expanding. The universe is
expanding in all of the directions we normally think of, that is in 3
dimensions. When we picture particles, we think of them as having 3
dimensional properties, like a ball. The electrical field of a
electrons and protons also projects equally in all 3 standard dimensions
(all 3 of the cartesian coordinates - also known as the X, Y, and Z
axes). This three dimensional aspect applies to other forces of nature
as well, such as gravity.

The next set of visualizations helps to picture the phenomenon.
Imagine a balloon filled about 1/2 way with air inside a special room
where all the air was slowly being removed. The balloon would begin to
get larger and eventually explode. Before it exploded, the air in the
balloon would be becoming less dense; the air would be becoming thinner
or more sparse. So at any place within the balloon it would appear that
the air was leaving the vicinity of that point. It would appear that
air was leaving that point. This flow from every point is what I
propose as the basis for describing what occurs in particles at the
atomic and sub-atomic levels. From another point of view, what we
experience as particles could be described as the turbulence created
by this flow so that the very existence of the particle is due to the
flow (or flux) described in this model.

\- Is the Model Justified

This is the first 1/2 of what is typically called "the apology".
It is typically presented at the beginning of a writing, before one has
a clue what is being justified.

\- The model is reasonable because of the following:

\- The vectors of all the forces of physics and the vector of
the expansion of the universe all point in the same direction.
This is at least prima facie evidence that the model has some
basis in fact.

\- A process leading to conditions described in this model has
already been described in terms of "Flatland". If "A sphere"
passes through "A plane", "A square" would perceive at first a
point which would grow over time to a circle having the same
radius as "A sphere", then shrink back to a point, then vanish.

By extrapolation then, if a 4 dimensional ball were to pass
through a 3 dimensional semi permeable space, the perception
of "A person" might well be the following: a point which would
grow over time to a sphere having the same radius as the 4
dimensional ball, etc. If the expansion of the universe is
viewed as the sphere which is growing to some maximal radius,
then the implications of that model would add weight to the
notion that the same process which is seen on the macroscopic
level as the expansion of the universe would be also felt
directly at the microscopic level.

\- Does the Model Add Anything

This would be the second 1/2 of "the apology". Yes, it works and
why should I bother to consider it?

\- Everything I have presented so far can be visualized. As
vision is the most acute sense in humans, presenting data in a
form which allows visualization allows significantly more of
our perceptive paradigms (brain power) to be used.

\- This model can at least partly replace some of the paradigms
of more complex models such as strings: in string theory the
fundamental pieces of matter and energy are tiny fragments or
vibrations resulting from the "Big Bang"; this theory would
describe the same phenomenon as resulting from an ongoing
process rather than echoes from the distant past.

\- This model naturally leads to a geometric simplification
which I describe later as "Tetrahedral Reduction". This
simplification allows the model to be even more readily
visualized and may explain how the electric force derives
directly from the process of the universe expanding.

\- Tetrahedral Reduction

This is a purely mathematical reduction. A tetrahedron is a
4-sided pyramid. If the tetrahedron is composed of equilateral
triangles then it is an equilateral tetrahedron. If lines are drawn
from the center of the tetrahedron to each of the 4 corners, then these
lines could be thought of as an inverse or inside-out tetrahedron. I
claim without presenting a proof that for purposes of this model this
inverse tetrahedron provides an accurate representation or mapping of
the model. That is, instead of using spherical mathematical methods to
describe the behavior of the flow from a point, we can instead apply our
reasoning to the 4 lines of an inverse tetrahedron.

I have proven that this reduction is an accurate mapping using
geometric and trigonometric arguments, and I expect it is intuitively
correct and not worthy of a formal proof here.

The criteria I have for this mapping is that fundamental particles
are capable of remaining at rest as they are in nature. So for this
reduction to be accurate, an identical force placed along each of the
lines of the inverse tetrahedron must balance at the center such that
the object would not be put into motion by the forces. Also, identical
torque or rotational rotational forces applied to each of the lines of
the inverse tetrahedron must also cancel at the center. This reduction
does satisfy this criteria, and so finally, we have our visual model of
a basic particle. It looks somewhat like a surveyor's tripod.

|
|
|
/i\\
/ I \\
/ \\

# Thread: The Trivium Method
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-trivium-method.17882/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dan E — 2016-04-29T18:57:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-trivium-method.17882/post-193827>

I would like to introduce a method of learning to those that may have never heard of it. It is claimed by many people I respect to be the proper and natural way to learn any subject in half the time. I have found it very useful and it seems to be essential to learning the incredible information here.

It is a process that can be used as a weapon against half truths and lies. Jack has a platform and following because he is a lighthouse above the sea of marketing strategies we live in that corrupt our ability to make good decisions. One minute cholesterol causes heart disease and the next minute the sun causes cancer. With these repetitious claims, sales of poison skyrocket and our health diminishes.

What can we use to protect ourselves from these fallacious appeals to fear? If you arrived on this site chances are you tried many remedies that fell short of there promises. I have myself but kept searching and experimenting. I found instant success with some basic bio hacks. If I were to pass this on to someone else, I would have to know why it worked if I hoped to convince them. In this case, the required reading is staggering. This doesn't intimidate me anymore.

Why is it important to learn quickly and efficiently? In the current state of affairs, as discussed on this site, it seems our survival depends on it. Being a complete newb to physics, chemistry and biology, the tsunami of new theories on the blog helped me realize how important it was to sharpen up my learning skills. It compelled me to get a better understanding of the trivium method.

It is the use of the first three subjects of the seven liberating arts; sequentially grammar, logic and rhetoric. Grammar is the study of identification - who, what, where, when. Logic answers the question why and brings you to a level of understanding. Rhetoric is the act of telling you how in simple terms and demonstrates wisdom. With these principles passed down from an unknown time, along with the quadrivium; scholars, scientists, and philosophers have been able to find truths in nature and communicate them to others. A truth in nature is one without contradictions. There are no contradictions in nature, only in the mind of man.

As we develop an understanding of disease and supreme health on this site, we encounter many conflicting ideas that oppose beliefs we once held. If brains were built like computers, and we wished to store only truthful information, we would have to erase the hard drive. We might want to upgrade the operating system to the trivium method, take in new data, and run the information through a logical sorting process, one that removes contradictions from the most valid arguements. This process run many times leads to a state of wisdom.

We are here to lift ourselves up to Jack's level of understanding and apply it to our way of living. I believe the way to do it properly, is by this method, which will be quite different from what is given to us in school. It is not memorization and parroting, it is critical thinking.

If this sounds worthwhile, I recommend any and all audio with Gene Odening. Gene was a featured guest on many podcasts beginning in 2009. triviumeducation.com is the go to place for resources, books and essays. They will give you a nice introduction and a community of people helping to spread the "lost tools of learning." How to Read a Book by Mortimer Adler would be my first read along with frequent reviews of the logical fallacies. This book alone will take you to a new level. I am confident you will find them invaluable as a foundation for the education you will find here on this blog and other required reading. I hope this post does the liberating arts some justice and generates some interest. I believe it is a requirement to free our minds from the shackles of dogma and inferior information if we are to attain peace, clarity and well being.

A warning from Alvin Toffler,
"The illiterate of the twenty first century will not be those who cannot read and write, but those who cannot learn, unlearn and relearn."

Hello, today I am announcing the launch of my first Health and Wellness eBook called [My Natural Life](http://www.mynatural.life)

**What is this eBook?**
**{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

**
It is a collection of over 1,500 health, wellness and bio-hacking tips based on laws of nature, light, water and magnetism and that are in alignment with latest cutting edge scientific research. In addition the eBook has a habit-forming approach based on the [Seinfeld chain method](http://lifehacker.com/5886128/how-seinfelds-productivity-secret-fixed-my-procrastination-problem) that recognizes that health and longevity can be easily achieved by adopting some new simple behaviors that get performed each day.

I have acquired many of these tips and recommendations through reading, researching and being an active member of Dr. Jack Kruse Optimal Health Forum and the [Quantum Health: Light, Water and Magnetism Facebook Group](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/1198693316810227/).

The eBook is low cost and high value, for example you could spend hours visiting various websites and reading a number of books to help improve sleep and get a handful of relevant tips or you could pay the small amount for My Natural life eBook and get a convenient compilation of over 65 sleep tips without the sermon. Multiple this value proposition by the over one hundred categories that are in the eBook and you have a **WIN-WIN**.

**How can I help?**

-   Go to the [My Natural Life](http://www.mynatural.life) web site
-   Download the free [15 Daily Habits for Optimal Living Infographic poster](http://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fpayhip.com%2Fb%2Fx1nr&h=5AQGzED9x)
-   Buy It Now on [Amazon US](https://www.amazon.com/My-Natural-Life-Habits-Optimal-ebook/dp/B01FBKDXU2) or [Amazon UK](https://www.amazon.co.uk/My-Natural-Life-Habits-Optimal-ebook/dp/B01FBKDXU2) or [Amazon AU](https://www.amazon.com.au/My-Natural-Life-Habits-Optimal-ebook/dp/B01FBKDXU2) and leave a review
-   Go to the [12-Week challenge Public Trello board](http://www.mynatural.life/12-week-challenge-trello-board.html)
-   Like the [Facebook Page](http://www.facebook.com/MyNaturalLifeEBook) and [Join the Facebook Group](https://www.facebook.com/groups/MyNaturalLifeEBook/)
-   Forward this note to family, friends and colleagues

Thanks for reading and supporting this initiative...

Paul

### LouiseAngela — 2016-06-16T19:02:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/announcing-the-jack-kruse-inspired-ebook-my-natural-life.17918/post-196712>

### PaulG — 2016-06-17T08:17:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/announcing-the-jack-kruse-inspired-ebook-my-natural-life.17918/post-196761>

> LouiseAngela said:
>
> Can you explain more about Trello board?

When you buy the My Natural Life eBook (on [Amazon US](https://www.amazon.com/My-Natural-Life-Habits-Optimal-ebook/dp/B01FBKDXU2) or [Amazon UK](https://www.amazon.co.uk/My-Natural-Life-Habits-Optimal-ebook/dp/B01FBKDXU2) or [Amazon AU](https://www.amazon.com.au/My-Natural-Life-Habits-Optimal-ebook/dp/B01FBKDXU2)) and email me I will create your own personal Trello board which contains all the images, goals, checklists and templates from the eBook. This has a number of advantages:

-   You can visually see how much progress has been made (e.g. Implemented 10 out of the 60 sleep tips)
-   You can add your own health and wellness goals and checklists or remove ones not relevant to you
-   You can invite others to your board for accountability
-   You can view/edit on you mac, PC, tablet or phone
-   You can enter your own comments, how you are responding, what are your roadblocks, what was easy, what was hard etc.

Best thing to do Louise is to play with the example **[12 Week Challenge Trello Board](https://trello.com/b/TmPpNNOg/my-natural-life-ebook-12-week-challenge),** this will give you a good idea of what it looks like and how it works.

Paul

# Thread: Is my diet at odds with my environment?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-my-diet-at-odds-with-my-environment.17963/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### pascale ahmad — 2016-05-17T12:25:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-my-diet-at-odds-with-my-environment.17963/post-194946>

I have been on the leptin Rx for the past 6 weeks, eating a strict keto paleo diet. We are at the peak of our summer here in India which is very hot (46c in Delhi today). Lately I find myself craving fruit, which are in season right now (mangoes, water melons, lichees pineapples) A nice crispy green salad would be nice too but we don't get salads and very few vegetables at this time of year. I suffer from heartburns after meals, difficulty digesting food and my skins is extremely dry, even though is eat lots of fats (coconut oil and ghee) and drink at leat 4L of RO water per day. This is making me think that perhaps my diet which is very high in protein dense food might be at odds with the climate I live in. Normally at this time of year I live mainly on fruit, yoghurt and butter milk.
While I don't want to do that now, I am wondering if I should eat a bit less protein and aff a bit of fruit to my diet.
My sun exposure is about 2 hours a day, an hour and a half in the morning at sunrise and half and hour at sunset.
Could anyone give me advice?

### seanb4 — 2016-05-17T13:06:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-my-diet-at-odds-with-my-environment.17963/post-194948>

Take what I'm saying with a grain of salt but I should think that you should be eating far more carbs in your enviroment. The key would be to get a lot of sun/uv.

### Lahelada — 2016-05-17T13:25:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-my-diet-at-odds-with-my-environment.17963/post-194950>

What do you currently live on? Are you doing the Leptin reset ?

### pascale ahmad — 2016-05-17T13:58:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-my-diet-at-odds-with-my-environment.17963/post-194952>

I am indeed on the leptin reset.
Unfortunately my diet is very restricted because of where I live. We do not have very sophisticated food distribution in India, so we have to eat what grows or is produced near us (I guess not a bad thing). I do not eat the standard Indian diet which consist of mainly grains and pulses, so I have little to fall back on.
I eat only canned tuna and sardines because I do not easily find sea fish in Delhi (sometimes in the winter a bit of sea bass and pomfret) because it's not near the sea. I eat goat and buffalo meat only (I stay away from chicken), eggs, kefir (home made from raw buffalo milk) and a lot of coconut in all its forms (water, meat and oil). Not very many vegetables at the moment, they do not grow in this heat, we get the good veg like spinach, green beens, radishes and so on only in the winter. Right now all we get are the Indian vegetables which are various gourd type of veg. I do eat some but I do not like them very much and they need to be cooked Indian style with a lot of masala to be palatable.
Sun exposure is possible only mainly at sunrise (5.30 to 7am) because of the extreme temperature, it is quite dangerous, in fact I got sun stroked years ago when I first moved here. It made me so ill!!!!!

### Jenelle — 2016-05-17T17:39:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-my-diet-at-odds-with-my-environment.17963/post-194958>

Local fruit is in season and you are getting lots of sun. Seems to me you are craving it for a good reason.

### pascale ahmad — 2016-05-18T06:10:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-my-diet-at-odds-with-my-environment.17963/post-195000>

Wow, that would be my first healthy craving ever!!!!
