# Thread: First eukaryotes found without Mitochandria
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-eukaryotes-found-without-mitochandria.17972/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jenc288 — 2016-05-19T23:26:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-eukaryotes-found-without-mitochandria.17972/post-195099>

Any response to this?

[http://www.sciencemag.org/news/2016/05/first-eukaryotes-found-without-normal-cellular-power-supply](http://www.sciencemag.org/news/2016/05/first-eukaryotes-found-without-normal-cellular-power-supply)

### NeilBB — 2016-05-19T23:42:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-eukaryotes-found-without-mitochandria.17972/post-195102>

> [jenc288 said:](/goto/post?id=195099)
>
> Any response to this?
>
> [http://www.sciencemag.org/news/2016/05/first-eukaryotes-found-without-normal-cellular-power-supply](http://www.sciencemag.org/news/2016/05/first-eukaryotes-found-without-normal-cellular-power-supply)

Interesting. Not too surprising. Life adapts to its environment. This is a single celled organism living in a monkey gut. It's a eukaryote that sacrificed its mitochondria and is now living like an anerobic prokaryote because it inhabits an environment without oxygen.

# Thread: The timing of full spectrum UVB/UVA/IR
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-timing-of-full-spectrum-uvb-uva-ir.17973/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cmegan — 2016-05-20T01:11:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-timing-of-full-spectrum-uvb-uva-ir.17973/post-195106>

Hi! Can anyone answer this question for me, I am a bit confused and can't clarify it in my reading....

I am a bit confused as to the timing of full spectrum sunlight UVB/UVA/IR...is it from 8AM to 10AM?

Thank you!

Christine

### PaulG — 2016-05-20T03:43:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-timing-of-full-spectrum-uvb-uva-ir.17973/post-195113>

Depends on your location and what season but early morning sun say 6-10am is a mixture of red and blue and is good to get in order to keep the SCN synchronized which helps production and regulation of various hormones and blood glucose/insulin metabolism and Leptin. After 10am UVB levels are higher and is more optimal for Vitamin-D production. Get the d-minder app if you have a smart phone to check best times for uvb for your location. Sunsets are in a way the reverse of sunrise and signal the brain for rest and recovery and sleep. Pretty simple

### cmegan — 2016-05-20T16:34:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-timing-of-full-spectrum-uvb-uva-ir.17973/post-195129>

Hi thank you for answering me! I do have a dminder and I understand the reasons behind getting morning sun, which I do everyday! My confusion came upon reading an answer from Jack to a member on the forum who has psoriasis. Jack told him that it is important for people with psoriasis to make sure they are getting "full spectrum sunlight UVB/UVA/IR" everyday. So, I got confused because I thought that full spectrum was with the solar sun, midday when we most the most Vit D. I have psoriasis also, so I wanted to make sure that I should continue with the morning sun as opposed to midday with the solar sun. So I'm assuming I should still just take in the morning sun!

As I understand stomach acid either too low and too high produce the same symptoms. I't's been 9 years and I **never returned to study HCL until now and came here to see what Dr.Jack Kruse thinks of HCL and if an when it might be needed.** I was thinking if your body produces it we should not supplement with it...
According to two GI Effects test over the past 7 years I do not digest fats well but I've very rarely have acid reflux but did suspect silent acid reflux once. But think it turned out to be due to high histamine, high lectin, high oxalate foods I was eating during season allergy months. When I lowered those foods during those seasons symptoms of serve sore throats went away. I rarely have any digestive upset perhaps because I have practiced natural hygiene eating since the early 70's. I felt better not eating starches and meats together and eating fruit alone so except for some SAD eating years I've mostly eaten that food combining way. greens and veggies with meats or vegetables with starches avoiding starches with meats and not eating fruits after a meal.

Anyway, for the fat absorption I've sometimes used ox bile 125 mg and 500 mg doses but metabolic counselor Michael McEvoy warned me not to take ox bile ongoing as it can cause the body to become dependent. With heavier meals especially with red meats I now take 1 tiny Himalayan pink salt pebble or eat a bit of watercress or arugula as bitters to help produce my own HCL. Remember I've had a _Foodie nutrient perspective_ focus for my entire life. Anyway, I ran across a fellow who had articles I liked that were full of natural ways to eat rather than taking enzymes or HCL and I latched onto those for digestive health.
I've been leaning that way for some time. One article he had was titled, **_Why I don’t recommend HCl and Digestive Enzymes…Right Away_... at one time I knew the author's name but now when I went to a link I had to that article my Malwarebytes software popped up a warning so I did not go any further.
**
**Maybe a mute point as I am now interested in Dr. Jack Kruse's perspective of digestion.**

**Got to get down to brass tacks and study the basics instead of just entertaining myself by
_Goggling_ filling in one of hundreds of _subjects_ I've studied and wondered about for the past 10 years plus _Jack Kruse_ to see what comes up.**

**Having to tear down my health ideology to build on a better foundation. Moving at a snails pace but still moving.**

> I had difficulty ordering the Premier HCL in Canada so I proposed getting [Bulk Supplements HCL](http://www.bulksupplements.com/betaine-hcl.html) or other common HCL supplements. However, Josh advised to avoid anhydrous HCL or any that contain pepsin, fenugreek, etc. In the end I was able to get a big order of the Premier stuff.

Ten years ago I took _HCL, Premier_ ( Betaine Hydrochloride) [https://prlabs.com/hcl-e2177-1.html](https://prlabs.com/hcl-e2177-1.html) and _HCL Activator_ [https://prlabs.com/hcl-activator-prlabs.html](https://prlabs.com/hcl-activator-prlabs.html) and promptly gave myself an duodenal ulcer that took me over a year to heal taking about 15 different things beginning with rounds of Pepcid. I now try and carefully weigh what I take as like with food all supplements have pros and cons for each person....sometimes I feel like just sticking with my FOOD RESEARCH supplements that are 100% food and or herbs and herbal tinctures. **One reason I am attracted to Dr. Jack Kruse's work as he is not overly 'supplement' oriented.** I am unsure if folks should take HCL without knowing if they are high or low acid..if that is even possible... as they have the same symptoms. I just don't know for sure ways to test...although when I did at home testing with baking soda I think it was it did indicate I'm on the acid side and also my twin brother was also prone to heartburn, GERD before he cleaned up his diet. The activator product has nightshades and high oxalates ingredients. About 8 years ago I eliminated nightshades and my joint pain was greatly improved and just last year I discovered a genetic connection to my being sensitive to oxalates. I have 3 Variated Oxalate SNPs including **homozygous for GRHPR**. Based on that and concurrent with symptoms of pain and disrupted calcium scores my Metabolic Counselor suggests a greater that 50% probability of oxalate related issues. So I am in the process of learning and slowly (as not to cause oxalate dumping) reducing my oxalate intake by 50 %.

I did find some stellar advice online but the link to the article **brings up a Malware warning**
which was titled '_Why I don’t recommend HCl and Digestive Enzymes…Right Awa_y' **(I can't find a good link to it)**
A few basics from the article were:
**\-Increase the amount of natural enzymes-- eat more raw food
\-Juice
\-Chew or puree food
**\-Take a teaspoon of apple cider vinegar 15-20 minutes before you eat
**\-Use bitters
\-Ginger******
******\-Eat more fermented foods

******The Author said,******
**_“The bottom line:_** In dealing with and healing from IBD naturally, we already buy so many supplements—everyone has their favorite, THE supplement without which you cannot heal. Improving your digestion is one area in which you can build good habits and experiment without ever purchasing a single pill.”

I will try and find my notes and find his name to give him credit ... I liked his thinking!

All uncommon 'common sense'

******

### NadoRo — 2016-05-25T04:57:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/experience-with-leaky-gut-higher-dose-vitamin-k.17986/post-195299>

Background: I've been following the Leptin Rx (again) for around 5-6 weeks. I have a leaky gut, adrenal issues, trashed hormone panel, and cholesterol numbers that reflect all of these issues. I added high dose Vitamin K 15 mg (along with my 5,000 IU D3 daily) to my supplement regimen about a week ago and am noticing an uptick in gut issues, specifically more frequent bowel movements and more diarrhea. I understood that Vitamin K helps with gut health, which is why I'm taking it.. Is this a good sign or a sign that I moved up to a dose that is too high for me? Curious to hear about any experiences with high dose Vitamin K..

# Thread: Kala Namuk - High Sulfur Pink Salt

**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Simon Wright — 2016-05-29T16:26:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kala-namuk-high-sulfur-pink-salt.18008/post-195579>

Hi All,

I have been recently using a salt called 'Kala Namuk' which is very high in sulfur. I am not sure if it is the original salt or if its blended himalayan salt with extra sulfur but it has had a great effect on me.

It has definitely helped with energy levels and also with my capacity to withstand exteneded periods of high UV sun. I live in Indonesia and can surf during the day for 3 to 4 hours with no burning (UV 12-14). Admittedly i have a propensity to go rather tanned.

I take it 30 mins before meal (1/2 tespoon) morning and evening. I also make a sauerkraut with all the crusciferous veg i can get seasonally, and also all the allium veg i can get hold of and take this just before i eat as well. I was thinking that with the abundance of extra sulfur with also the cruciferous / allium sauekraut it has really given my sulfation capacity a massive boost.

Anyone else used this salt as well? It has been ticking boxes for me.

Simon

Thanks for your help.

### healthy2015 — 2016-06-02T18:35:02+0300

Can anyone point me in the right direction please?

### Allin — 2016-06-02T23:30:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/methylene-blue.18013/post-195898>

**No data on** **pregnancy and your dose is your dose.

I started off with one drop and I did not notice anything. I went to two drops after eating a can of oysters and drinking 750 ml spring water and walking the dogs out in the sun. I will continue tomorrow with 3 drops, etc until some brain fog goes away.**

**Nootropic are also called smart drugs or cognitive enhancers.**
**Nootropic Effects of Methylene Blue**

Studies in animal models have established methylene blue as a neuroprotective compound with nootropic properties. Methylene blue’s mechanism of action involves enhancing mitochondrial respiration by supplementing the action of ubiquinone as an electron carrier. Low-dose methylene blue enhances cytochrome c oxidase (complex IV) activity, oxygen consumption, cerebral blood flow, brain glucose utilization and ATP production while concomitantly reducing oxidative stress.

**Methylene Blue As A Novel Nootropic**
Methylene blue has been demonstrated to have nootropic effects in diverse conditions in animal models, including: inhibitory avoidance, spatial memory, fear extinction, object recognition, open-field habituation and discrimination learning(1).

Methylene blue prevents cognitive impairment induced by mitochondrial dysfunction(2), anticholinergic drugs(3), cytochrome oxidase inhibition(4), and experimental models of Alzheimer’s disease(5).

Methylene blue is a potent antioxidant that readily crosses the blood brain barrier and enhances mitochondrial function by behaving as an artificial electron donor and acceptor.

Methylene Blue’s ability to support REDOX (reduction-oxidation) reactions in the mitochondrial matrix is hypothesized to enhance ATP synthesis and improve the energetic status of neurons.

In principle, Methylene Blue would have a synergistic effect with creatine supplementation, since creatine phosphate has an extremely high-energy phosphodiesterase bond that allows creatine phosphate to phosphorylate ADP to ATP and thereby regenerate the available pool of cytoplasmic ATP. Note that genetic X-linked disorders which result in a defect in the creatine transporter (necessary for the efficient uptake of creatine into cells), results in extraordinary intellectual disability, highlighting the importance of the ATP status of neurons to optimal functioning.

I am looking to increase mental clarity. First I am going to use it straight; then, I will try it with some creatine and see if I feel any synergistic effect. If you catch me drooling, make me stop….

> Can anyone let me know is this okay for a pregnant woman to take? or if there is a thread on here where I can read up on this?

A good guideline for any drug during pregnancy is this. Unless there is a TON of evidence showing it to be safe, then don't use it. I wouldn't want to be in the first million pregnant women using any drug.

# Thread: Blood sugar on keto - insight?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-sugar-on-keto-insight.18020/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lisarwaite — 2016-06-02T23:27:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-sugar-on-keto-insight.18020/post-195897>

Any insight into this?
I have been keto for 5 months, and finally bought a glucometer and my fasting insulin numbers were in diabetic range! When I started I had them drawn and they were fine (I was on a real foods paleo diet for 2 years and they have always been fine.)
In order to help a brain trauma, I went high fat and low carb and cut out ALL sugar including fruit and all veg except greens and cruciferous. I am grain free, diary free, and food sensitivities free. And my glucose number went through the roof on keto. ANY INSIGHT AT ALL? I'm thinking of adding back in resistance starches and some low glycemic fruit to see if I can reverse what I've done, but that make NO SENSE AT ALL. I'd love input.
I have enough wrong, I can't deal with high glucose numbers on top of it.
Thx.

### Allin — 2016-06-02T23:44:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-sugar-on-keto-insight.18020/post-195899>

If your numbers were fine BEFORE you went on your specific KETO diet, I would go back to it.

I do not know if you are doing any of these.
1\. Spring water or RO water only
2\. Protein - shrimp, oysters, fish
3\. AM sun

### lisarwaite — 2016-06-02T23:48:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-sugar-on-keto-insight.18020/post-195901>

Yes to all of the above.

### Scompy — 2016-06-03T05:49:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-sugar-on-keto-insight.18020/post-195915>

You may not be shuttling insulin into your cells is one possible guess. I had issues in the past with a lack of potassium (K). That helps as the gate with the sodium/Potassium pumps, and if they are not working well, insulin-resistance can get compromised. I had some hearty arrhythmia in the past along with a lot of muscle cramping.

Also another side of this could be protein at the wrong times... increasing the protein way too much in the evening (for me in the past) resulted in some possible gluconeogenesis. So I flipped my protein to the morning and minimized the protein in the evening...these days, I actually am intermittent fasting again, so I'm actually skipping dinner altogether. Shrimp is ultra high in protein, though also really good in potassium. Do you know your blood ketones as well as blood glucose? I've also cycled out of ketosis already and I'm integrating more carbs from veges (like squash and spinach which are also high in K+) and some lower-carb fruits like cherry.

### kathylu — 2016-06-03T07:13:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-sugar-on-keto-insight.18020/post-195917>

Same thing happened to me.

Physiological insulin resistance at the muscle level. Perfectly ok as long as your HbA1c is good and your post prandial glucose readings don't spike. What is happening? Well, the first thing is that LC eating rapidly induces insulin resistance. This is a completely and utterly normal physiological response to carbohydrate restriction. Carbohydrate restriction drops insulin levels. Low insulin levels activate hormone sensitive lipase. Fatty tissue breaks down and releases non esterified fatty acids. These are mostly taken up by muscle cells as fuel and automatically induce insulin resistance in those muscles. Not to worry.

Peter at Hyperlipid talks about this in detail.

### NateToup — 2016-07-01T02:11:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-sugar-on-keto-insight.18020/post-197648>

Jack up your fats. Consider a two day fast to get into ketosis to efficiently produce more ketones. Be aware of electrolytes. Please research.
Also given the many interviews I have listened to of Jack he advocates seasonal carbohydrate consumption, and it is now June. I have not read the Epi- Paleo Rx, but my notion of exceptional carbohydrates are local fruits and vegetables and another source that barely sees the light of day, fermented grain and legume flours. Almost everything bad about grains and legumes is resolved by fermentation and baking. I believe there is some truth to " the good _shepherd_ " bringing bread and fish to the people.

# Thread: Hypoglycemia causes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypoglycemia-causes.18042/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aerose91 — 2016-06-11T01:09:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypoglycemia-causes.18042/post-196337>

This was my first symptom ever, over 10 years ago, long before i ever got sick. Since falling ill 5 years ago it's become the bain of my existence.

I know the typical explanations for it, be it pacreas, liver or low cortisol, but are these the only reasons? I tend to not think its cortisol for me because i had it before hitting AF when my cortisol levels were probably really high.

My father has had hypo for 40+ years but not as bad as me. The longest i can ever go without eating is 4-5 hours and sometimes less than an hour. I can make it through the night without it waking me up maybe 50% of the time. Amt of sun exposure or grounding doesn't make a difference. I'd love to know if its something that can be targeted.

### Purple Haze — 2016-06-11T02:22:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypoglycemia-causes.18042/post-196342>

I have similar Adrenal Fatigue/ Hypoglycemia issues. I started going low carb several years ago, then started intermittent fasting and ketosis, but hit a wall while working out a few years ago. My blood sugar was low and started having heart palpitations, insulin resistance issues and major sleep problems. I realized I needed to have more carbs in my diet, so I stayed somewhat Paleo but added in more low GI carbs when it felt necessary.
It seemed ketosis with IF was not for me. Not long term anyways. Everyone is different.
I noticed improvement when I started getting more sun and added a ton of seafood to my diet. It's a work in progress, but improvement has been made. Like you, I need to eat every few hours too. You may find that seafood for breakfast is helpful. I did. Read Jack's articles on Leptin and DHA.
I thought high cortisol was part of the issue, but I think there are different versions of Adrenal Fatigue. Do you get restless leg syndrome too?
Jack will tell you it's blue light toxicity, so less time at the computer and TV under artificial light is recommended….and more morning sunlight.
I hope something here helps.

### jenaf — 2016-06-11T16:32:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypoglycemia-causes.18042/post-196361>

I had "adrenal fatigue" and I got worse while being "paleo" and training a lot.
For me - blue light toxicity.
Once I drastically limited my blue light - I got better. The Magnetico mattress pad also helped a lot. But even with the pad, if I expose myself to blue light, especially at the wrong times and excessively, I crash and burn.

### jenaf — 2016-06-11T16:33:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypoglycemia-causes.18042/post-196362>

And getting rid of my contacts was also a game changer.

### jenaf — 2016-06-11T16:35:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypoglycemia-causes.18042/post-196363>

And I should say that my hypoglycaemia started in childhood. But immediately went away when I restricted blue light. #lightismydrug

### Purple Haze — 2016-06-11T21:53:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypoglycemia-causes.18042/post-196391>

> [jenaf said:](/goto/post?id=196361)
>
> I had "adrenal fatigue" and I got worse while being "paleo" and training a lot.
> For me - blue light toxicity.
> Once I drastically limited my blue light - I got better. The Magnetico mattress pad also helped a lot. But even with the pad, if I expose myself to blue light, especially at the wrong times and excessively, I crash and burn.

Yes, looking back, I can see my problems started while working out a lot and going low carb, but also at the same time I started working in an office in front of a computer for long hours, but I forgot… that's when I started wearing contacts too. They bothered my eyes after a few years so I trashed them. I also started going outside less and less. It was a perfect storm brewing that led to my eventual crash.

I still eat paleo (meaning more natural food, less process industrialized food), but I've allowed some good carbs and not so focussed on the low carb thing. Cutting out process sugar is still important to me, because I feel like crap when I have it.
Getting lots of sun, eating loads of seafood and getting out of fake blue light has been the key for my road to recovery. Thanks Jack!

### Aerose91 — 2016-06-13T03:24:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypoglycemia-causes.18042/post-196469>

I do all of those things and have been for over a year, yet i still progress downward. I live outdoors and the only source of blue light i have is my phone which i keep a blue light filter on and dont use after sunset anyway. Also, i never had a desk job where i stared at a screen all day, i was training clients on the beach or a park lots of the time

The only things i know are effecting it for sure is humidity makes it worse and having a small bit of cold exposure a few months ago dropped me down and i haven't recovered from it. Also, since the cold incident I've had a persistent flu feeling and greatly reduced tolerance for energy expenditure which is why i think humidity effects me so much more now

### Purple Haze — 2016-06-13T08:33:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypoglycemia-causes.18042/post-196477>

Hopefully Jack or some of the other more experienced members can have a better answer for you. I can only speak from my own experience and acknowledge everyone is different.
Sounds like you're living the healthy outdoor lifestyle, which is the opposite of my situation and cause. Just wondering if you work out while eating very low carb or in ketosis. That was what revealed my hypoglycaemia.

### Aerose91 — 2016-06-13T17:14:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypoglycemia-causes.18042/post-196510>

I appreciate tue input, purple haze.

Certainly no workouts or overdoing it for me. Standing upright for a couple minutes a day is the most my body can handle

# Thread: DIY sleep mask with magnets
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-sleep-mask-with-magnets.18048/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Amund — 2016-06-12T22:18:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-sleep-mask-with-magnets.18048/post-196458>

Hello Jack and forum!
I was wondering about making a sleeping mask with magnets inside since I can't get myself a magnetico pad. That way I will still get _some_ magnetic field during the night.

However, the strength of the magnets I'm not so sure about. I can get hold of neodymium magnets the size of a big coin with a strength of 13000 gauss! But that sounds ridiculous considering the magnetico has a strength of 20 gauss. It's larger though.

How many gauss in total would you recommend to put in a sleep mask, if you would recommend it at all? What would happen if I had a coin-sized 13000 gauss magnet attached to my forehead during the night (with the correct side against my forehead)?

### bionaut — 2016-06-12T22:32:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-sleep-mask-with-magnets.18048/post-196459>

The magnetico is designed to expose you to the negative portion of the field and keeping you away from the positive portions. Having a strongly concentrated magnet on the side of the head is not advised as a DIY hack. This sort of therapies are more in the realm of a doctor performing trans cranial magnetic stimulation. A doc would be using a precision instrument that is tested, targets a specific depth and structure for a desired effect. This is a type of hack I would completely avoid since the application would be lacking knowledge and specific controls on an extremely complex sensitive organ and you would be lucky if you didn't harm yourself.

More gauss is not better. Connecting to your local natural environment constantly is key.

If you want to sleep connected to the magnetic field have you thought about whipping out a tent in your backyard? The magnetic field you want to connect to is our planet's. Have you made any changes to your light environment?

### Lahelada — 2016-06-12T22:51:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-sleep-mask-with-magnets.18048/post-196461>

I second what Bionaut said. Magnets seem harmless but used wrongly they cause more harm than good. The tent is a great idea.

### Amund — 2016-06-12T23:19:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-sleep-mask-with-magnets.18048/post-196464>

Thanks bio!
After some searching I absolutely agree; when so close to the head the south pole of the magnet will also affect the brain to a large degree. It was worth a shot!

# Thread: The Human Heart – The Origin of Light and Life
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-human-heart-%E2%80%93-the-origin-of-light-and-life.18057/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2016-06-15T07:55:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-human-heart-%E2%80%93-the-origin-of-light-and-life.18057/post-196604>

[http://wakeup-world.com/2015/02/22/the-human-heart-the-origin-of-light-and-life/](http://wakeup-world.com/2015/02/22/the-human-heart-the-origin-of-light-and-life/)

June 15, 2016
**The Human Heart – The Origin of Light and Life**
[Leave a comment](http://wakeup-world.com/2015/02/22/the-human-heart-the-origin-of-light-and-life/#comments)

 [](http://wakeup-world.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/02/The-Human-Heart-The-Origin-of-Light-Life.jpg)By Dr. Fahad Basheer

Guest Writer for [Wake Up World](http://wakeup-world.com/2015/02/22/the-human-heart-the-origin-of-light-and-life/)

DNA is an extremely long chain of molecules that contains all the information necessary for the life functions of a cell. It helps in carrying information from generations to generations and also helps in the production of proteins that are essential for growth and development of the human body.

The individual molecules that make up DNA are called nucleotides. There are only four nucleotides that are ever used: Adenine, Thymidine, Guanine and Cytosine. These nucleotides are made up of atoms like Carbon, Hydrogen, Nitrogen, phosphorous and Oxygen.

The quantum physics have discovered that our universe is made 99.999% energy and 0.0001% matter. It is now proven that even the atoms in our DNA are nothing but energy. Each atom of the DNA is made up of unique energy which codes a specific set of information within it.

It was found that every atom has its own energy signature, and that the molecules collectively radiate that unique energy signature. This energy signature is otherwise known as the “intelligence” that determines the behavior of the DNA in terms of production of proteins by transcription and translation, which in turn determines the physiology of a living cell.

Photons are tiny little particles of light. In physics a photon is a bundle of electromagnetic energy. They carry energy and momentum which are dependent on the frequency. They have both particle nature and a wave nature. Photons, when they behave like particles, interacts with matter.

In some cases the energy of the photon is absorbed by the matter and when the energy from photons is absorbed by matter, the matter can emit electrons. This process is known as the photoelectric effect.

Photons interact with electrons in the matter to excite the electrons to a higher energy level by transferring the energy from photons into the electrons. The energy that the photons carry is not mere energy, it is a set of information that contains all of what it must do during its life time.

This information is known as “intelligence.” Not only is it light that is made up of photons, but all electromagnetic energy is made up of photons. This forms the wave nature of photons. Photons are always in motion and travel at a speed of 2.998 x 108 m/s in vacuum.

We are about to know the marvelous interaction between our DNA and the photons which travels at the speed of light. We have now understood the existence of DNA in terms of energy that contains a set of information and the ability of the photons in transferring energy that contains information to the electrons.

At this point you might be wondering what that miracle would be that happens between the DNA and a particle that travels at the speed of light.

Russian scientist, Dr. Vladimir Poponin, discovered photons scattered and running inside the vacuum when he completely evacuated and created a vacuum inside the container. He then inserted DNA into the container and he then measured the location of Photons. What he found was amazing. This time he saw the photons that were scattered previously in the vacuum had now perfectly arranged in alignment with the DNA that was kept in the vacuum.

This was totally shocking for the researchers. He then removed the DNA from the container and then he tried again to locate the position of photons. This time the results of the experiment were more shocking than the previous one: the photons had aligned in the same positions where the DNA was before and it looked exactly like a DNA made of photons.

This was then known as the “phantom DNA.” Finally it was then established that the physical DNA had an effect on the non-physical photon, and the DNA is communicating with these photons through a new field of energy. This proved the existence of a connection between DNA with the photons (1).

This doesn’t stop here; these photons not only interact with DNA, but also play a master role in determining the health of our body. Amazed? Yes, in another experiment a new shocking truth was leaked that brought a new light to the world of health.

A German biophycist, Dr. Fritz Albert Popp, who did exclusive research in biophysics discovered the presence of bio-photons in tissues. He discovered that the DNA in a living cell stores and releases photons creating “bio-photonic emissions” that hold the key to illness and health.

These bio photons are not mere photons, they play a significant role in the communication within and between cells to determine the health of tissues. The coherent emission of bio-photons is connected to energy and information transfer processes in the biological organisms, and it is also linked to the function of DNA and to gene regulation. And in his research it was found that the healthy cells had more bio-photons, while the cancerous and the diseased cells had a decreased amount of bio-photons (2, 3, 4).

In conclusion the bio photons are the means by which cells in our body communicate. The human DNA is storing and releasing photons (bio-photons) that determine the health of living tissue in the human body. The bio photons carry a set of information known as “intelligence” that gives instructions to the DNA regarding the production of proteins that are vital for our life. The higher number of bio-photons in the cell, the more healthy the cell becomes, and the less amount of bio-photons in the cell the more diseased or cancerous it becomes.

The Light is in truth an electromagnetic energy that is linked to the human consciousness, which is the fundamental source of human life. The most powerful electromagnetic generator in the human body is our heart. Our heart is 5,000 times magnetically more powerful than the brain. At the same time our heart is 100 times electrically more powerful than the brain (5, 6).

In sum, the heart is five times electromagnetically more powerful than the brain, which constantly keeps on generating electromagnetic quantum packets that carry a set of information known as “intelligence” to the whole body, which is immersed in the field of the heart. This field is even detectable three to four feet away from our heart.

By altering the electromagnetic fields of the heart we are able to change the whole electromagnetic field that is immersed in the human body. The human heart in truth is the source of light, and the power of this light determines the health of the human body. The people who generate the most positive emotions are able to create high quality cardiac coherence which, in turn, determines the quality of electromagnetic field generated by the heart. This then determines the bio-photons that are emitted into the electromagnetic field of our body, which determines our health.

At the Institute of HeartMath, scientists started to test the effect of emotions on DNA. In this experiment some human placental DNA were placed in a container from which they could measure the changes in the DNA. Twenty-eight vials of DNA were given (one each) to 28 trained researchers. Each researcher had been trained on how to generate and feel different emotions. The results of the experiment showed that DNA could change its shape according to the different feelings of the researchers.

These outstanding outcomes showed that whenever the researchers felt gratitude, love and appreciation, the DNA responded by relaxing, the strands unwound and the length of the DNA became longer. And when the researchers felt anger, fear, frustration and stress, the DNA responded by tightening up and switching off many DNA codes (7).

Later HeartMath applied practical interventions of generating positive emotions on HIV positive patients and they discovered that the feeling of love, gratitude and appreciation created 3,000,000 times the resistance to HIV than they had without feelings of love, gratitude and appreciation (8).

Truly, by harboring positive emotions in the heart a person is able to generate a highly coherent electromagnetic field that can even change DNA, which can change our health and our lives.

**References:**

(1) P.P.Gariaev, K.V. Grigor’ev, A.A. Vasil’ev, V.P. Poponin, and V.A. Shcheglov, “Investigation of the Fluctuation Dynamics of DNA Solutions by Laser Correlation Spectroscopy,” Bulletin of the Lebedev Physics Institute (1992), no. 11-12, p. 23-30, as cited by Vladimir Poponin in an online article “The DNA Phantom Effect: Direct Measurement of a New Field in the Vacuum Substructure” (Update on DNA Phantom Effect: March 19, 2002)
Rattemeyer M., Popp F. A., Nagl,W. (1981) Evidence of photon emission from DNA in living systems, Nature Wissenshanften, 68 (11): 572-573.

(2) Cohen, S., Popp, F.A. (1997) Biophoton emission of the human body. Journal of Photochemistry and Photobiology B: Biology 40(2): 187-189

(3) Motohiro; Kobayashi, Masaki; Takayama, Mariko; Suzuki, Satoshi; Ishida, Takanori; Ohnuki, Kohji; Moriya, Takuya; Ohuchi, Noriaki (2004).

(4)”Biophoton detection as a novel technique for cancer imaging”. Cancer Science 95 (8): 656–61

(5) Rollin Mc Carty. Director of research institute of heartmath. BBC Documentary Catalyst 2007

(6) Rollin Mc Carty director of research institute of heartmath. Shift cv the frontiers of consciousness

(7) Rollin McCraty, Ph.D. Mike Atkinson, and Dana Tomasino, b.a modulation of DNA conformation by heart focused intention, Institute of heartmath, (2003)

(8) A Pilot Intervention Program Which Reduces Psychological Symptomatology in Individuals with Human Immunodeficiency Virus: Deborah Rozman, PhD, Rupert Whitaker, PhD, Tom Beckman, BS and Dan Jones; Complementary Therapies in Medicine. 1996; 4 (4): 226-232.

# Thread: Wifi from space???....getting wierd
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-from-space-getting-wierd.18066/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Simon Wright — 2016-06-17T09:56:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wifi-from-space-getting-wierd.18066/post-196763>

Hi All,

I just recieved this in a financial newsletter. Dumb just got dumber:

**The genius behind PayPal, the Hyperloop, electric cars and tourist space travel unveils his biggest feat yet:**

****Cheap, lightning-fast
wireless internet
beamed to every inch
of the planet****

****‘…on the North Pole, the middle of the Pacific
Ocean, the top of Mount Everest — anywhere.’****

****— Rajeev Badyal, SpaceX Vice President of Avionics****

****And one tiny ASX-listed tech company holds the key
to wirelessly connecting Earth’s entire population****

****As an early investor, you could pocket 14 times your money, starting this year…****

**From the desk of Sam Volkering,**
**Editor, _Australian Small-Cap Investigator_**

Dear Simon,

The greatest mind of the modern era is about to embark on his greatest and most ambitious project yet...

A revolutionary, digital network designed to beam superfast internet to every inch of the planet.

This network will be entirely different to the archaic internet services you’re familiar with today.

Not a single fibre optic cable will be laid to bring this globe-stretching network to life.

**This will be super-fast internet beamed direct from space**.

Satellite internet isn’t anything new. Companies have been doing it since the early 1990s.

But what makes this plan unlike anything you’ve seen before is the sheer scale of the network.

As technology blog Tympani.com puts it: ‘_…\[he\] intends to launch upwards of 4,000 satellites into space to provide internet for the entire planet.’_

That’s more than double the total number of satellites in orbit right now!

**Once operational, and if successful, this satellite array** — **_comprising thousands of small internet-ready satellites_** — **will be capable of wirelessly connecting billions**.

Imagine the ability to ‘jump online’ whether you’re tracking along the dusty plains of the Saharan Desert, navigating through Amazonian Brazil, or scaling the snow-capped peaks of remotest Canada…

**Anywhere**.

Business Insider said (emphasis is mine): ‘_Bringing cheap, high-speed internet access to every inch of the Earth would **change the world as we know it**_.’

Tech Insider calls it ‘_game-changing_’

And Fortune says: ‘_the advent of reliable, space-based Internet service \[marks\] a second coming of the first internet boom.’_

Technology behemoths like Google agree.

In January last year, the tech giant poured $1 billion into making this ‘space internet’ dream reality.

As you’ll see, they’re not the only ones investing big money into this technology right now.

A host of tech’s biggest players are joining Google by investing a small fortune in this arena too.

Now, saturating the Earth with wireless internet sounds like an outrageous goal.

It IS an outrageous goal.

Bloomberg called this genius’ satellite internet plans ‘_hugely ambitious_’_._

But no matter how ambitious or audacious this plan sounds, the man behind it has a proven history of making the seemingly impossible become reality.

As the _Washington Post_ puts it: ‘_\[His\] track record of disruptive innovation make this a really attractive business_’

**And for you — as an investor — it could mark the start of an epic 14-fold profit run…if you’re willing to take a calculated risk with a small portion of your capital**.

Because I’ve found a small ASX-listed company with a breakthrough technology vital to the long term future of this global satellite internet project.

Let me give you the full story…

.......it goes on but you get the point.

Simon
**[http://click2.portphillippublishing...NQzImdmlkPUx5cHM1MCZnPTAjY29udGludWU./AQ/3nvv](http://click2.portphillippublishing.com.au/t/FA/Etc/IcQ/AAEVqA/JVM/MjQzODU5fGh0dHA6Ly9wcm8xLnBvcnRwaGlsbGlwcHVibGlzaGluZy5jb20uYXUvNTE5OTI4Lz9lbWFpbD1zaW13cmlnaHQxMDAlNDB5YWhvby5jb20uYXUmYT0yMCZvPTQ4MjMmcz04NjQ0JnU9NzEwODAmbD0yNDM4NTkmcj1NQzImdmlkPUx5cHM1MCZnPTAjY29udGludWU./AQ/3nvv)**

# Thread: Little wacky but my family is used to it, but if others see/know...?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/little-wacky-but-my-family-is-used-to-it-but-if-others-see-know.18071/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Joeakim — 2016-06-18T00:01:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/little-wacky-but-my-family-is-used-to-it-but-if-others-see-know.18071/post-196844>

Okay, my family always, initially, rolls their eyes when I started taking cold showers, face dip in ice water before known as CT, drinking butter coffee, lowcarb/highfat, intermittent fasting IF, various types of vitamins, walking outside for sunlight upon getting up, unplugging wifi (without telling them), etc., so this additional wacky stuff is not new to them. But others? lol ...I guess only in here, I'm okay, sorta, no? I am seeking corrective comments/advisements on what I am doing so I won't be off the tangent. I started with three legged stool: getting far far more sunlight, especially when I first get up, drink spring water and more of it, and work to lessen the artificial light.
\- Wifi off before sleep or when not needed.
\-Since I practice bit of Feng Shui, I've always had our bedroom pitch dark when sleeping.
\-Just ordered the blue blocker glasses.
\-Using the sheet of aluminum foil to ground myself often when in house, and at night while calming down (will be enhanced by adding copper on top?).
\-Eat shrimp and/or smoke oyster (fresh if possible) daily.
\-Diet mostly ketogenic/Lowcarb already.
\-Drink plenty of un-floudated water, the challenge is to buy/get them in glass.
\-Use headphones most of the time on my iPhone and away from my body whenever possible.
\-While using PC & iPad, disconnect from the charge and run on battery only (does it make a difference?).-Will do after more reading: Nicotine. Please feel free to add suggestions.

Thanks in advance.
\-

### caroline — 2016-06-19T07:57:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/little-wacky-but-my-family-is-used-to-it-but-if-others-see-know.18071/post-196909>

What health issues do you have?

Please start a journal in the journal section and give Dr. K. more specifics about your location, job, Mom's and grandma's health etc. etc. etc.

Are you reading the blogs? listening to webinars? podcasts?

Have you read the suggested books? Dr. K's book?

> Please start a journal in the journal section and give Dr. K. more specifics about your location, job, Mom's and grandma's health etc. etc. etc.

Thanks Caroline for your reply.Yes, I have been listening/watching to Dr. K's podcasts more than once and continue to listen/watch over and over until the message fully sets in. There are so much to read in blogs and forums, sometimes the information is overwhelming.
As far as my health, I got the clean bill of health in May other than low-T, all my lab results came back in check.
I am still struggling to read so much info, I have not thought about journaling yet.

### caroline — 2016-06-21T01:15:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/little-wacky-but-my-family-is-used-to-it-but-if-others-see-know.18071/post-196976>

in this new lifestyle, I keep explaining these things to my husband. He had a hip labrum tear repaired 7 weeks ago. So he's been watching me while u clean the house while listening to all the webinars. I've been cooking great meals and walking around by our pool half naked and in explaining everything that I'm reading and hearing to him I'm thinking that it comes down to laws of attraction? When we are full of life/ light/ energy/ electrons, people see the spark of life in us and are attracted. Those that are in need are attracted to those that are abundant. Whether even if it's on tv or the phone or in print or in person. The more light you have the more people want you or want to be with you. We say it's a spark in some ones eye. We feel it. There's a reason why we go looking around for another human- when something is missing inside us. We need the people who have what we lack. Opposites attracting...

Thanks for the link. Just for the sake of digging - JK said that equatorial living is very beneficial then he lists the ice (eat like a great white, live like a polar bear).
The issue I see is that when the majority of the world population used to live in the tropical climate, back then, there was no way to get ice baths - no ice machines.
One of the issues I have down here (18N, 64W coordinates) is no ice cold water unless one makes it. Our record low temperature is in the 60s F.....we are always hot down here unless surfing in the winter.
So is Jack saying that Good light is number one prerequisite, Ice is number two and if we can combine them = ideal conditions? I am prepared to be yelled at.

### Lahelada — 2016-06-21T18:10:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/book-experience.18081/post-197025>

The point is not the question. You are asking questions that have been covered extensively.

If the list is clear to you then you would not ask if ice is Number 2 and you have not considered the seemingly unrelated lumping together under point 3 of "The best healers"
I will point you in the right direction. Living at the equator is point 1. Using ice is point three. Eating like a great white shark is point 4 a progression in your healing and ongoing process if you will. I mentioned aussies and the donut hole as it refers specifically to water issues in Australia that go beyond hot and cold. And yes if you cannot get cold water you have to make it cold. An ice vest is also an option.

You cannot compare our ancestors conditions to today, you cannot live our ancestors conditions today, try as you might. Our ancestors did not need CT . They were plugged into the earth. We have now created an artificial bluelit world that does not support our physiological needs. Look for the least damaging conditions of existence and use the five healers to optimise yourself using the five above.

I am really not trying to discourage you from asking questions...

### Lahelada — 2016-06-21T19:00:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/book-experience.18081/post-197030>

No schooling no weeding .Suggesting that you need to read more and stop wondering about the culture here. We have all been where you are at the beginning of learning here but not in your personal place. If I can give you a ride in my car I will but initially one cannot.
I answered your question,though. Light and ice combined does not make for ideal conditions. You are not putting the two seemingly contradictory statements in context. Equator living is as ideal as it gets at this moment and it is still not ideal , living like a great white shark refers to what you need to do to reverse conditions that you may or may not have in the current conditions of existence that you are facing or to point blank avoid them.

Try asking your questions more specific to your personal situation. Big picture questions like yours cannot be answered with just a couple of links.

### hello — 2016-06-22T04:53:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-water-oz-style.18084/post-197060>

Hey guys,

Wondering if anyone had thoughts on putting water on the magnetico in Aus? After a lot of fretting about finances I got a magnetico, 10 gauss and I LOVE it. As soon as I lay down on it I'm out of fight-or-flight immediately, and I fall asleep like a log. If it cost five times the price I'd still buy it.

Anyway, I know people put their water on the magnetico - as far as I can tell this is a simple operation - put water in container, put container on magnetico, drink water.....

but I'm wondering whether magnetico-water mediates the bad effects of ozone on Australian drinking water? Any thoughts?

### Toby King — 2016-06-22T08:09:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-water-oz-style.18084/post-197065>

I dont have all of the facts, and I think it will probably have some pretty good effects, but I seriously doubt it will have any effect on the halide ion issue in the water from the chlorofluorocarbons in our ozone layer. Concisely, undoubtedly will have and effect, but not on the most important matter at hand.

### hello — 2016-06-22T08:13:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-water-oz-style.18084/post-197066>

thanks! Sorry to be slow: what is it that is bad about the halide ions? Presume this must be an even bigger problem for New Zealanders

### Toby King — 2016-06-22T08:22:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-water-oz-style.18084/post-197068>

Bottom line: reduces the dielectric potential in a way similar to fluoride. Prevents the ability to hold electromagnetic energy (As I understand).

### michellehp — 2016-07-05T22:09:56+0300

Hi all,

I got some of these cheap uvex orange glasses, they pass the test here: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/are-your-blue-blockers-working.13985/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/are-your-blue-blockers-working.13985/)

and I'm wondering, should I be using them ALL times I'm looking at the computer screen, like in the morning, etc, or is it only really necessary at night?

I'm pretty sure I have screwed up neurotrasnmitters and hormonal systems already from over usage of technology (working on the computer a lot the last 8 years).

So if there's any risk at all of blue light causing damage at ANY time of the day I'd rather not risk it.

Yes, I'm also working on cutting down on having to use a computer at all, but for now It's my income source

Thanks guys

> Hi Dan,
> I referred your situation/ set up to Dr.Bonlie...
> The model you have would be our 5 gauss Magnetico pad. Dr Bonlie does not recommend using a 5 gauss strength under a crib mattress as these mattresses tend not to be thick enough.
> The return (+) positive peaks from the magnets are very high and may extend through the crib mattress.
> If you place the crib( without a Magnetico pad) 3 feet or more away from the side of your bed then the twins would not be within the return positive field off the edges of your 5 gauss . ( We don't want to be within the area of the return (+) field for extended periods of time nor when we are sleeping.
> If you were to get a 10 gauss crib pad then it could not be placed beside your 5 gauss bed. Plus once the babies moved to a larger bed, the crib pads would not be used.
> In order for the twins to be on a Magnetico they should have the 10 gauss Booster pad. You would then also need a 10 gauss pad ( Core or booster)

### JoeBranca — 2016-07-26T07:20:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-for-children-babies.18155/post-198934>

congrats!!!!

For our second baby he co-slept for only a couple of weeks on our king size with 20Gauss pads. For the crib transition I had ordered a custom fitted 10Gauss, and also a 4-5 inch non-metal-coil crib mattress. Almost every morning after he's out for nursing I pull the magnetico out and roll it up, so he's not napping on it in daytime hours.

We grade his overall sleep patterns as "phenomenal", more than just "good" and the vast majority of parents we know would kill for a "good" sleeper. In terms of ease of going down for the night, sleeping through the night (once night time feedings waned), ease of transfer during sleep, and ease of adapting to new environments (which is rare but he doesn't have a hard time with the changes).

Also the magnetico is for only night sleeping not daytime sleeping or naps.

In the UK you're lucky to have that much room on one or two sides of a bed. # Thread: Is 65% blue blocking enough?

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DPH — 2016-07-13T22:04:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-65-blue-blocking-enough.18158/post-198320>

I am looking into buying some blue blocking glasses prescription glasses, and the ones I have found so far are from Gunnars who have an amber version that filters 65% of high-intensity blue light at 450 nano-meters. Is that enough or should you try to find a pair with 100% filter? (I have a pair of regular uvex though, but they are just protection wear without any prescription on them so I can't really use them besides when I am reading at night)

What filters and brands are people using here? And do you use the same for the whole day or might it also be good to get a pair where you perhaps have a lesser filter in the first half of the day and then switch to a stronger one in the evening? Or is it best to just have as strong of a blue blocking filter on as possible for the whole day?

# Thread: Finally my Acne, intestinal issues, & anxiety are gone!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-my-acne-intestinal-issues-anxiety-are-gone.18160/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Arsim — 2016-07-13T23:40:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-my-acne-intestinal-issues-anxiety-are-gone.18160/post-198324>

Hi! A long journey it was. I started getting acne & intestinal issues very early on like sixth grade. Then it exploded like freshman year and got even worse when i started lifting weights. I knew little things here and there about health like fluoride... I slowly started changing my diet too. I began to work at a health food store and kept learning more. However i was antibiotics (solodyne-minocyclne) for long periods of time. It even made me lose my personality some what and changed my mood (gut flora?). I took a second course eventually and took a strong probiotic this time (renew life 90 billion) helped a lot. After learning so much I called a ND doctor in Ann Arbor Michigan. Got blood work done (ALCAT, hormone, panels), i stopped eating gluten and dairy got off the antibiotic. My blood report was not good high CRP, high triglycerides, elevated liver enzymes etc... All he told me to do was eat paleoish which i did gluten and dairy free... and he gave me three supplements one was an intestinal repair herbal powder. After a month i noticed a big improvement we got blood work again and all my levels went back to normal range which was awesome. After about two months it really did help my acne lowered by like (95%), my stomach felt way better i used to get severe stomach pain once in a great while. I also noticed much more normal bowel movements. However I still could tell something wasn't right. I was hungry for more knowledge i tried ketosis felt very good and slept great but always fell off got sick of the food i was eating/craved carbs. Around this time i discovered dr jack kruse and ordered blue blocking glasses. I noticed such a difference with them, so i kept doing more seafood, CT, walking barefoot, sunlight exposure at the right times, turning off wifi etc... It was so much information I wasn't really doing well at balancing it all i did make good progress tho and kept feeling better. I then really realized what you can feel like... My mood got way better I was having crying spells constantly before seeing the ND, my skin got even better and like a thousand other things. Also i forgot to mention being active in the gym and lifting weights helped me health wise, first it probably made it worse but it taught me discipline. The leptin RX has helped a lot i dont always stick to it but i notice how much it helps.

Hi everyone
Can I ask what people are doing to limit EMF and still being able to read Dr K's blogs? I thought about saving the blogs for offline reading and reading it on cell phone while its in airplane mode. Is there a better way without printing and killing trees?

### caroline — 2016-07-14T12:54:46+0300

print off JK's blogs and go sit in the sun and on the grass ...... and read away!

### Scompy — 2016-07-16T06:36:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/limiting-emf-when-reading-blogs.18161/post-198412>

Frequency of use and reference....I've gone back to the same blogs over and over now that I'm finding it better to just have them printed out because I've also scribbled and put stuff in the margins. If someone created a JK encyclopedia of blogs, i know a lot of us would buy them for the ease of having them/reading them...just saying.

### smart cat — 2016-07-19T11:32:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/limiting-emf-when-reading-blogs.18161/post-198580>

> [Scompy said:](/goto/post?id=198412)
>
> Frequency of use and reference....I've gone back to the same blogs over and over now that I'm finding it better to just have them printed out because I've also scribbled and put stuff in the margins. If someone created a JK encyclopedia of blogs, i know a lot of us would buy them for the ease of having them/reading them...just saying.

Yep!
I never read longer text on a screen.
First problem : screen reading strains your eyes more than the already eye straining book and journal reading.
Second: I can't understand things well, can't remember the important points and the strucure after reading, if I don't see it on paper. Which I can read everywhere without fake light & EMF.

I highlight it , make remarks , read it again and again, see the structure of the text, the whole thing.
I started reading the blog when it went online in June 2011. And I printed out every post. And the comments (nuggets). This stuff which goes way over my head (I am no physicist, biochemist, biologist....... )
has to be read multiple times. And if I go back to the older posts I see/understand what I could'nt see/understand before. But that's normal experience with every text/book you read. The text doesn't change but the reader changes.
I have a lot of "Jack Kruse" leitz folders now. And some more for important stuff from the forum, Quantum Health group, JKs linked in posts.

JK encyclopedia? What do you mean? Could you explain?

# Thread: Artificial morning sunlight bulb by Mega Ray UVA/UVB/IR 290-400nm is it a good idea?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artificial-morning-sunlight-bulb-by-mega-ray-uva-uvb-ir-290-400nm-is-it-a-good-idea.18164/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Arsim — 2016-07-14T17:47:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artificial-morning-sunlight-bulb-by-mega-ray-uva-uvb-ir-290-400nm-is-it-a-good-idea.18164/post-198351>

Living in Michigan is not the best for sunlight... Even tho this summer so far has been excellent. Luckily I avoided this previous winter and was in Tempe Arizona for school and got lots of sun. I bought this Mega Ray reptile mercury vapor light bulb ($45) after lots of research, I called the guy who owns the company and he was pretty knowledgeable on the light spectrum and I asked him if this truly mimics morning sun and has 290-400nm wavelengths... He explained in detail all the wavelengths the light bulbs emits and says it will produce vitamin D3 on the skin. The light bulb was specially designed for this purpose in mind for the health of reptiles. I use it on cloudy days and use it on my junk.. It feels amazing! I notice it works very well, using it on my face it makes my whole day different like I actually got my morning sun. Is this a good idea? Especially for winter so I can get the stimulus needed for the circadian rhythms I also plan on being very cold adapted and sticking to a ketogenic eating style (hopefully). Here's a couple pictures of it. There's also a coupon code right now for 20% off on their website if anyone is interested in buying this it seems to be one of the best full spectrum bulbs out there. I know Dr. Kruse recommends natural sunlight as much as possible but this light and a true Infrared bulb I have really work well for me. First pic is the mega ray bulb, second pic is the infrared bulb (therabulb) amazon $20, third is the box the mega ray came in, fourth is my moms IR session. Im slowly having my parents implement all of this in its not easy lol.. they're doing a good job tho.

# Thread: 2x/day vs one long session

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sfast — 2016-07-15T10:10:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2x-day-vs-one-long-session.18170/post-198387>

I have a hard time to commit to a longer CT session in the morning. Last winter I was at 30min at below 0°C (32°F). Biggest problem: Boredom and my daily schedule.

I figured that I could easily commit to two smaller sessions. 20min AM as part of my morning routine and 20min evening as a topping of my work day.

Do you see any disadvantages of more but shorter session vs. longer session?

Best of Luck
Sascha

### caroline — 2016-07-15T12:12:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2x-day-vs-one-long-session.18170/post-198394>

what ever works for you .....just do it!

What about taking Jack's blogs into the bathtub with you? listen to music? what ever works!

### sfast — 2016-07-15T14:35:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2x-day-vs-one-long-session.18170/post-198396>

Boredom is not the bottle neck.

I am rather talking about any disadvantages on on splitting a longer session up into two.

# Thread: Dry-eyes upon waking...due to Lasik?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-eyes-upon-waking-due-to-lasik.18171/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Joeakim — 2016-07-15T17:17:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-eyes-upon-waking-due-to-lasik.18171/post-198401>

Hey folks,

Shortly after my Lasik surgery 10 years ago(?), I started waking up with very dry eyes.
When complained during follow ups, they gave me very little info but advised me to use eye drops.
So for years and even currently I use Refresh upon waking. For a while, I thought it was
the ceiling fan in the bedroom but my dry eyes persisted when the ceiling fan was off.

Two questions: 1. Due to my lasik, am I not getting the full spectrum UVA/UVB rays?

2\. Any advise on what or how to better my dry eyes? At some point I thought it
was a hormonal issue.

Thanks

### Lahelada — 2016-07-16T02:33:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-eyes-upon-waking-due-to-lasik.18171/post-198406>

This is not to do with dry eyes per se but may help you get some more context with regards to Lasik

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/laser-eye-surgery.17336/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/laser-eye-surgery.17336/) Ubi24 helps too

### Joeakim — 2016-07-16T05:33:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-eyes-upon-waking-due-to-lasik.18171/post-198410>

Thanks Lahelada!

I've read that before, and after reading for another time, I am still confused or not totally understand the damage it may have caused for light absorption.

I still have corks in my tear ducts to prevent tears from draining. Still, dry eyes continue.

Luckily, unlike some of the friends who did Lasik in same period who now wears glasses again due to regression, I still have 20/20.

I do wear readers now. It's annoying as heck. Can't fight mother nature! Or is it something that I can do?

# Thread: Any bio-hacks that ditched the reading glasses?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-bio-hacks-that-ditched-the-reading-glasses.18209/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Joeakim — 2016-07-24T20:44:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-bio-hacks-that-ditched-the-reading-glasses.18209/post-198872>

Hello all,

I've read and heard JK talk about his sight improved via morning sun gazing and more sunlight absorption. However, he is near sighted. I was nearsighted until I got Lasik 10+ years ago and I'm still 20/20 although some of my friends who had Lasik went back to glasses again.

I have been in denial for years as my eyes flickered and squinted to see the small print. A couple of years ago, I finally broke down and got my readers at 1.25. My optometrist told me just wear those until I needed a Rx.
Slowly I realize it's getting worse with time.

So, my question is that any biohack on ditching the readers? If I need to try, what would I need or how do I get started?

Thanks

### Videha — 2016-08-31T10:58:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-bio-hacks-that-ditched-the-reading-glasses.18209/post-200558>

>
> Hello all,
>
> I've read and heard JK talk about his sight improved via morning sun gazing and more sunlight absorption. However, he is near sighted. I was nearsighted until I got Lasik 10+ years ago and I'm still 20/20 although some of my friends who had Lasik went back to glasses again.
>
> I have been in denial for years as my eyes flickered and squinted to see the small print. A couple of years ago, I finally broke down and got my readers at 1.25. My optometrist told me just wear those until I needed a Rx.
> Slowly I realize it's getting worse with time.
>
> So, my question is that any biohack on ditching the readers? If I need to try, what would I need or how do I get started?
>
> Thanks

Great Q.i started having troubles with fine print...no outside help sought..been using 1.5 glasses (people say not to use them as they weaken the eyes) 1 needed to, over time the 1.5 not working so well. Thanks for asking this Q joeakim-would be fantastic to not need any glasses at all, bb's is enough! poor nose

### fitness@home — 2016-08-31T16:08:36+0300

Glad to hear @[Melanie Procter](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/10090/)'s success and by reading her biohack (putting icepack over the eyes = CT, and sunlight) it gave me ideas for me to open my eyes when doing the Face-Dunk in ice water.

It's quite amazing when reading another's N = 1 journey and their success/failures with added comments leads you to different paths...to ponder.

Will report back my n=1 journey on this as well.

### nonchalant — 2016-10-26T17:37:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-bio-hacks-that-ditched-the-reading-glasses.18209/post-203339>

@[Joeakim](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18463/) you should also eat lots of seafood in your hack. DHA does amazing things to your eyes. My optometrist that I've had for many years was amazed at my eyes new focusing abilities after I increased my seafood consumption. I can see well through several prescriptions and I can now focus up close much better. Sure helps, when removing tiny thorns and splinters from DH's hands!

### Joeakim — 2016-10-28T18:39:41+0300

# Thread: Good Book Rec
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-book-rec.18228/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jeff Power — 2016-07-28T16:44:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-book-rec.18228/post-199048>

For anyone looking for a good read, I recommend the book Magicians of the Gods - Graham Hancock. Sounds woo from the title but it is a fact based journey through recent archaeological discoveries and geological discoveries. A couple highlights:

1\. Recent discovery in Indonesia using new ground penetrating radar found that a site discovered in the 70's and left alone until 2013, is actually the tip of a huge pyramid that is underground. Dates back as old as 20,000 years ago.

2\. Site in Turkey that is being escavated is a city covered/entombed on purpose at around 12,000 years ago.

3\. Strong evidence that a massive meteor struck the North America ice sheet about 12,000 years ago melted the whole ice sheet instantly and caused sea levels to rise 400 feet on parts of the planet. Evidence that another fragment hit the planet again about 9,600 years ago heating the planet back up.

4\. Like many disciplines, the book chronicles how commonly held believes about history are wrong and based on half truths.

It is pretty fascinating stuff, that is the tip of the proverbial ice berg. Enjoy!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Fish vs Shellfish? (And Oysters)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-vs-shellfish-and-oysters.18230/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DPH — 2016-07-28T21:43:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-vs-shellfish-and-oysters.18230/post-199069>

I have listened to many interviews with Jack and one thing that is always recommended is to eat a lot of seafood. But when naming "seafood" I have not heard "fish", but rather shellfish and above all Oyster.
From what I understand the main reason is the DHA content of these foods.
But I am a little confused by this when looking up DHA content in different kinds of seafood. From the lists it seems fish like Salmon and other fish has the highest DHA?

According to the list I found here: [https://health.gov/dietaryguidelines/dga2005/report/HTML/table\_g2\_adda2.htm](https://health.gov/dietaryguidelines/dga2005/report/HTML/table_g2_adda2.htm)

In 100g Salmon there is 1.457g DHA
In 100g Oyster there is only between 0.2 and 0.5g DHA (?)

Actually the most DHA content rich food on the list here seems to be caviar with 3.800g DHA in 100g

I am not sure if I am confusing or missing some things here though (probably?)

Is there some other hidden reason shellfish is better than fish (like Salmon)?
And if Oysters are the king at the top, what would be the hierarchy of the most optimal seafood to eat after the Oyster?

### JCDC — 2017-02-12T22:14:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-vs-shellfish-and-oysters.18230/post-208480>

I am wondering this too. Zinc content is the only thing I can think of. Anybody have anything else to add?

### PaulG — 2017-02-13T01:30:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-vs-shellfish-and-oysters.18230/post-208485>

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...ional-data-on-high-dha-seafood-sources.11276/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pdf-nutritional-data-on-high-dha-seafood-sources.11276/)

**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### primalmofo — 2016-07-30T05:50:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-7-myo-cancer.18238/post-199139>

[http://news.nationalgeographic.com/2016/07/oldest-human-cancer-disease-origins-tumor-fossil-science/](http://news.nationalgeographic.com/2016/07/oldest-human-cancer-disease-origins-tumor-fossil-science/)

### Lahelada — 2016-07-30T13:00:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-7-myo-cancer.18238/post-199140>

Conditions of existence still come into it. The bone was found in a cave. Did they see the sun ? What sun did they see? Social pressures existed even then. Keep the ill out of sight hence out of the sun ? Who knows. While a valid research project I found the side mention of the Paleo diet interesting. I was also thinking that any research based on one individual in other matters would be considered incomplete to put it mildly. When it confirms the current thinking though it is much easier to accept.

# Thread: August 2016 Webinar
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/august-2016-webinar.18254/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2016-08-05T05:51:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/august-2016-webinar.18254/post-199326>

Help me better understand significance of [Fraunhofer Lines](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fraunhofer_lines)

**Questions**

-   How much variation is there in the lines width/depth per day, per season, per generation? How related to Chronobiology?
-   Are the missing frequencies always missing?
-   What during about K-T event? and other extinction events?
-   How are they affected by our atmosphere? Ozone, O2, CO2 levels? and the implications of this??
-   Artificial light does not contain Fraunhofer lines - how significant is that?
-   Are they affected by nnEMF?
-   Do birds for example read this barcode for purposes of migration/navigation?
-   Hotter temperature=spectral lines more spread out and less well defined, Colder=smaller and more well defined, explains why CT is more effective in colder latitudes.
-   Fraunhofer originally counted 574 thin black lines but modern astronomers have discovered tens of thousands.

In [Time 17](https://www.jackkruse.com/time-17-melatonin-insulin-solar-metronomes/) you said "All molecules have their own spectrum of vibrational energies. Everything protein in life is a vibration, a color, a frequency. If the molecule's spectrum contain a frequency matching that of the water coherent domain, it would get attracted to the coherent domain, and become a guest participant in the coherent domain's coherent oscillation, and settle on the coherent domain's surface. Furthermore, the coherent domain's excitation energy would become available to the guest molecules as activation enery for chemical reactions to take place."

So, this is what Ling was missing but I am wondering what is the significance of the missing frequencies? How does the eye quantise something that is missing? If there is a dark line this means the energy gap is greater and spectral line dispersion explains why periodic table is quantised.
Are they like the black notes on a piano? Spectra Lines=Rossetta code.
