I will remind you that each element from the periodic table is organized in quantized fashion, like oxygen and hydrogen, and each has a dominant spectral wave (color/frequency), and a corresponding action on the body because frequencies control atoms in a quantized fashion. There is clearly a hidden “Rosetta code of frequency” that light uses to control hydrated proteins for living cells to keep them as far from equilibrium as possible. This quantum link has transfixed me for a decade now. These spectral lines of atoms correspond to similar to the Fraunhofer emission lines in the solar spectrum. By applying the spectral waves of elements directly to the body, I believe we can control substrate biochemistry in distinct ways that offer control that life requires to keep us far from equilibrium. I call this a quantum radar system that allows for coherence and entanglement. Color therapy has shown many therapeutic results but no one has developed the precise quantized code life uses because there is a lot more qualities to light than just color. We have many clues these days because of newer data. Over a period of time therapeutic consistencies were noted and recorded in a 1500 page authoritative treatise on color therapy, the Spectro-Chrome Metry Encyclopedia. Spectro-Chrome color filters go over a light source. The light behind the filters, I think is hugely important to success of failure of the therapy. These filters are broad band filters and not mono-chromatic colors because the emissions of ionized elements in the solar spectrum are in fact a broad band fingerprints. I have a sense that the source of light must be unpolarized and we can use light at special anatomical sites to access imperfect black box radiator sites in the body. Humans have one perfect black box radiator and many sites where imperfect black box radiators can be taken advantage of. The only perfect BB radiator is the pupil at the edge of the iris. It is here where we can effect the central retinal pathways directly. It turns out meridians in acupuncture are another rudimentary way for light to enter our body but most modern humans put tarps over these areas. The best way to add light energy and data is where blood comes to the surface to allow light to interact with it. I took advantage of these findings to actually co-invent Quantlet to take advantage of these abilities. You can watch the video’s we do at [http://www.thequantlet.com](http://www.thequantlet.com/). There is some research that was being done in Italy and France that I reviewed years ago on the iris of the eye that really got my attention about the pupil. Here is where I locked into the eye. After reading that I found Dr. John Ott’s work. I found a few MD’s were applying pulsed and colored light to the iris/pupil and into the retina in Europe. The iris and retina are essentially extensions of the brain – and noting various responses in the iris as related to the Autonomic Nervous System, specifically the PVN in the brainstem. This is when I began to realize adrenal fatigue was acute to chronic electromagnetic punishment of vibrations to the PVN in the brainstem that stopped fat burning in the mitochondria in the grey matter neurons from altered light frequencies delivered to the eye from our environment. There were also certain implications from Ott’s early work that the whole body might be accessed via the visual system both positively and negatively. This was great information for a clinician to take advantage of. I wanted to experiment on myself and I did. My June 2016 webinar gets into this hacks in some detail. To do this type of citizen science, I needed to build a device that could deliver pulsed and colored light to the iris/pupil via optic fiber which would not be damaging to the eye and would change and transform what my mitochondria could do. Laser light is monochromatic but our cell surfaces did not evolve with monochromatic light. The interesting part of this story is that all stressed cells release monochromatic ELF-UV. This light creates some advantages inside a cell but it has other effects outside the cell. The body must also “scatter” and polarize light to make it incoherent from the ELF-UV light. This is called the “Monte Carlo Effect” and it happens in most biological systems. For example: the epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) pathway is critically important to growth, survival, proliferation, and differentiation of all mammalian cells. Membrane-bound EGFRs can bind diffusing extracellular ligand molecules, dimerize, autophosphorylate their tyrosine residues. Tyrosine is an aromatic amino acid that absorbs all frequencies of UV light. Considering that stressed cells make ELF-UV begins to make sense because it can subsequently trigger a host of intracytoplasmic and intranuclear signaling network modules that use calcium as a secondary messenger system to amplify the effect of electromagnetic radiation from the environment. Every one's energy can be transmuted or transformed to create specific frequency vibrations that work optically optimally in different tissues to determine specific tissue level physiology.

Why doesn't the [Sun](http://science.jrank.org/pages/6606/Sun.html) emit these missing colors? Or, if the Sun does emit these colors, what happens to the light before it reaches [Earth](http://science.jrank.org/pages/2214/Earth.html)? The answer lies at the surface of the Sun. I have a deep sense what we believe about this part of the Sun will change with the Safire project.

When we look at a picture of the Sun, the surface that we see is called the photosphere. The photosphere is a region, several hundred kilometers thick, in which the Sun changes from opaque to transparent. SO what did evolution do with this transition of light from the sun?

Cells are transparent for one reason. The optics of sunlight. Stars that emit UV light clear space........star clouds or nebula were opaque and they begin to clear when starlight illuminates these gases clouds. All stressed cells emit uncolored ELF-UV. All transparent substances show some dispersion in the visible spectrum, and, if uncolored, have a higher index of refraction for purple (UV) then the red light. This difference implies that higher visible frequencies are slowed down more by the medium than are the red frequencies. This explains why cells are designed to release UV light, it also explains why they EZ has a higher refraction index than bulk water, and why red light penetrates tissues 10-30 cm’s to work directly on the ATPase to run it as a quantum torque nanomachine. As light is slowed down it can be transformed to other forms of matter with mass because energy and mass are equivalent in Einstein’s equation. The key to their transformation is found within the size and shape changes the energy undergoes.

Evolution touches us via the photoelectric effect while we are connect to Mother Nature’s tuning fork called the magnetic field. The magnetic field tunes the tuning fork buried in your thalamus to improve sensory relays of the Earth and sun and unify them with the 5 senses we have developed. One is a bolt of lightening to our sensory station in the thalamus and the other a careless whisper to the magnetic dipoles in our thalamic cells. Without both present, water becomes a useless quantum fluid. With both present water becomes the perfect quantum fluid that opens the door to just about any possibility. The Fraunhofer line-depth principle enables the simultaneous measurement of solar-excited fluorescence and CO2 assimilation rate in individual leaves, but also at larger scales in plants. Since photosynthesis is the basis of all food webs on Earth, Fraunhofer line depth principles are the key link to the quantum yield for all living things. Thus, it may contribute significantly to field studies of the relationship of fluorescence to photosynthesis.

Now we know that each absorption line is caused by a transition of an electron between energy levels in an atom. Each element has a distinct pattern of absorption lines. # Thread: A little bit of inspiration....

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2016-08-07T05:59:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-little-bit-of-inspiration.18256/post-199385>

I want to share a bit of an email I got from a long time member.....

He has always been extremely supportive of JK and me and everyone really.....

He takes all of this on board and applies it to his life every single day. His family has benefited enormously.

They have a wonderful backyard that he has worked really hard on .....so they all get daily grounding and sunshine - and C/T.

His parents are also benefitting hugely from his example ..... They eat a lot more seafood, get outside in the sunshine etc. .....and consequently are a lot clearer /healthier and with better brain power.

At times he has wondered if they were paying attention ..... They were and Are!

Everyday what we do speaks volumes ...... way more than words - in my opinion.

# Thread: Involuntary calf/feet muscle spasm after cold baths
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/involuntary-calf-feet-muscle-spasm-after-cold-baths.18260/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Catalin — 2016-08-08T09:28:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/involuntary-calf-feet-muscle-spasm-after-cold-baths.18260/post-199417>

Hello everyone!
During my cold baths with tap water (~16-18 \*C at this time, 20-25 minutes), after 2 months, 4-5 times a week, now I get some calf (feet) muscular involuntary spasm (no pain and it tooks a day or long) (later:muscle twitching is better term) even I don't submerge my calf in the cold water?

Can you tell me what's going on? I stopped for couple days now, to read more about it, but I failed to get an answer related to this. I suspected also a Mg deficiency and I take as supplements 400 mg Mg a day but I can't say I see more then just a temporary help.
I guess I should mention I have hepatitis C also.
Cold tap water baths were helping me to sleep better so I was sure it is fine for me, but listening Ruben few days ago saying that it could lead to muscular fibrosis by doing it wrong (related to my not so good redox state?) I stopped until I will understand more.

Thank you!

### Catalin — 2016-08-17T18:19:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/involuntary-calf-feet-muscle-spasm-after-cold-baths.18260/post-199727>

My problem still not solved with supplements. If I expose myself to cold the muscles starts to spasm involuntary for days, no pain. CT is stopped. Short cold showers only...

### Scompy — 2016-08-18T01:33:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/involuntary-calf-feet-muscle-spasm-after-cold-baths.18260/post-199741>

For my N=1, this would be a potassium deficiency for me... Mg can also help at times, but it was upp-ing the avocado daily because it's a huge K+ source..., shrimp and yellow squash also have a lot of K+. I was also getting heart arrythmia/palpitations at times, especially 14+ hours into an Intermittent Fast. I typically do a lot of harder exercising at times like soccer, 'lumberjacking' and gardening stuff here and there; this can really promote muscle twitching or an occassional heart-skip for the lowered Potassium just after a harder workout. For these reasons, I typically end my last meal with an Mg spray application and an avocado to get me to the next morning to prevent any losses.

### Catalin — 2016-08-18T09:32:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/involuntary-calf-feet-muscle-spasm-after-cold-baths.18260/post-199753>

@[Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) Thank you for answer. I use KCl salt and I eat avocado, K supplements here in my country are mostly placebo (due to low % like 76 mg/pill for example). No way I can find here shrimps or yellow squash... or seafood. I can get some shells but not available too much. Maybe need to use more KCl salt and too eat more avocados. I will take next month some blood analysis maybe I can convince my dr to run some tests on electrolytes too.
Thank you!

### Da-mo — 2016-08-18T15:19:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/involuntary-calf-feet-muscle-spasm-after-cold-baths.18260/post-199759>

watching - I get this too . . . will try upping the avo and see what happens

### Joeakim — 2016-08-18T17:59:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/involuntary-calf-feet-muscle-spasm-after-cold-baths.18260/post-199774>

For my n = 1, adding potassium helped greatly. I always get this occasional spams on my right inner thigh and calf muscles but not same time. What's scary is that the spasm on my left ribcage that runs like snake. Magnesium alone does not do the trick but daily potassium has had great impact.

Best of luck.

### Scompy — 2016-08-18T21:17:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/involuntary-calf-feet-muscle-spasm-after-cold-baths.18260/post-199791>

I have weaved in IF (Intermittent Fasting), strong execise with a Keto diet for a good 6 years now off/on. My quick observation would be that a ketogenic diet depletes salts fast, Keto+IF+exercise ultra-fast, especially the K+ ... that little dark egg known as the avocado is now a must in my daily life and more seafood really helps too. KCl probably isn't that great because it just doesn't deliver enough K+ compared to an avocado, plus the crazy # of added benefits and the delivery method. My best method is to mix in a little tahini or hummus with the avocado. Sometimes, it's the only thing I eat if I decide to have a dinner. Back when I ate a lot of 85% dark chocolate, I think the caffeine was also depleting even more salts out of my system, leading to a lot of K+ loss.

### Catalin — 2016-08-19T12:55:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/involuntary-calf-feet-muscle-spasm-after-cold-baths.18260/post-199832>

@all Thank you for your advice.
@[Joeakim](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18463/) yes, inner thigh not so much but sometimes it spasm a bit too, started to add more K+
Yes @[Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) lately I used more cocoa and dark chocolate thinking about supplementing Mg. Thanks for tahini + avocado idea.
I use avocados but they are not so many in my country (all imported and missing during large periods of time) and they doubled the price in one week this year... Seafood is rarely found here. I can add more fish but the provenience of it is hard to know. I must rely on the seller word for it.
I'll keep you all informed.

### Catalin — 2016-08-24T08:34:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/involuntary-calf-feet-muscle-spasm-after-cold-baths.18260/post-200121>

Increasing K+ helped a lot. The frequency of spasms lowered and some days it disappears. Looks like is solving. Keep you updated.

### Catalin — 2016-09-08T12:36:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/involuntary-calf-feet-muscle-spasm-after-cold-baths.18260/post-201102>

Weird stuff... My brother in low started a ketogenic diet for few weeks by now and he has same involuntary muscular spasm in his calf muscle while he is exposed to cold due to the fact he works outside and the weather started to be a bit colder.
I did some blood tests and K is 4.3 mmol/L and Na 137 mmol/L, Ca 9.3 mg/dL. I didn't get all results right now. And I think they did't run the test for Mg.
So, it is not K, but now while the tap water is colder, near 16.8 \*C then a month ago (19\*C), the involuntary spasm after cold showers is worst them last month.
I will go tomorrow to get my results from dr and I will post it here.
I was thinking I do not eat something essential but my brother in law lives in UK and he eats different...

### Catalin — 2016-11-08T13:29:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/involuntary-calf-feet-muscle-spasm-after-cold-baths.18260/post-203712>

Updating my status:
Everything I did on that time related to this calf spasm not worked even with supplements Mg, K, Na, Ca both sourced from pills and from natural sources.
The solution revealed to me unespectedly: due to 1 day hospitalisation on 5 September (which came with all blood test very nice even with my HCV related: GGT 30, AST 16 ALT 18) I left hospital with clostridium difficile infection, on september 15 bad dream started. That leaded to 1+ month of hospitalisation because 2 weeks they did'nt knew what it is (abdominal enormous pain, bloted, diarheea, 22 episodes / 24 h, near death experience overall...they wanted to make surgery on me..pain, and pain) and during that, because they sent me over 18 different doctors from a lot of specialities, meds where always changing and I noticed that muscular spasms lower when they gave me a lot of i.v. liquids. Of course, they said my diet is the fault for my condition until the 18 doctor understood what it is, and they were blind and deaf to my questions about spasms.
That's why I increased the hydration and problem solved now even the CT temperature dropped to 14.7 degree Celsius.
Tested few time by reducind hydration a bit and spasm pop up in max 2 days.

Another thing worth to be mentioned here:

Before diet
GGT 90-145 AST -ALT 60-90 TG 254
ketogenic diet reach on fish (<20g carba/day)
GGT 30 AST 16 ALT 18 TG 66 AFP 6
During 1 month+ hospital diet(750g carbs/day diet)
GGT 105 AST 29 ALT 52 TG 98 AFP 13

Thank you all!

# Thread: Most benefit for time/Rx spent to wellness?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/most-benefit-for-time-rx-spent-to-wellness.18263/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Joeakim — 2016-08-10T17:50:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/most-benefit-for-time-rx-spent-to-wellness.18263/post-199475>

Wife took a walk this morning, of course not wearing sunglasses or makeup but only to have her sunglasses on her head for the aesthetic reasons. However, she did have iPhone in her hand while listening to music via headphone.

Me? I chose to sit on the bench while listening to one of Jack's podcast. I sat bare footed touching the concrete, topless, drank my cold aspirin spring water, watching the sun rise higher and alternatively its reflection on the water. I was tempted to sit on the concrete wall dipping my feet into water thinking it's more beneficial.

As I am continuing to educate myself and wife seeking optimum health, I wonder what would be the best time spent?

1\. At the beach, minimum clothing, walking in the water as waves come and go.

2\. Hike through pine tree forest.

3\. CT ( I doubt my wife is ready ).

# Thread: Curious about my Saliva Labs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curious-about-my-saliva-labs.18271/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kathy1967 — 2016-08-12T20:23:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curious-about-my-saliva-labs.18271/post-199562>

I'm hoping someone can suggest for me how to naturally raise my progesterone. My saliva results show that I'm low in progesterone but high in DHEA. I attached recent saliva test results as well as some old tests for reference. I've been following a ketogenic diet, and doing a lot of important lifestyle things:

-   amber glasses at night
-   trying to be in bed by 10pm
-   working out mid morning (& not lifting to the point of failure anymore)
-   walking barefoot
-   no caffeine
-   ketogenic diet (80% fat, 15% protein, 5% fibrous carbs) with TONS of fat and (grass fed beef, seafood, kerrygold butter, avocados, cruciferous veggies, herbal tea, spring water)
-   Sun on the eyes in the am every morning between sunrise and 10am (minimum of 5 minutes, max at 30 minutes.
-   In front of computer 4 days a week for 10 hours per day from 7:15am - 5:15pm but I go outside every hour for stretch and sunshine barefoot & sometimes walk down & put my feet in the lake (I work at a park)
-   Not sure if this is good or not, but I've been going to a tanning salon a few times a week for 10 minutes in the morning - I started this a few years ago when I found out my vitamin D was 16! Natural sun got it up to 32 but with work I couldn't stay outside long enough to raise it any higher so I started tanning - & my level now hovers around 40

I wonder if I should consider bio-identical progesterone? I hoped to fix everything without exogenous hormones. The day after I did my saliva tests I started taking 2 tsp of Maca daily and plan to test hormones again after 3 months (should I do blood or saliva?). A few health things:

-   I'm 48 year old female
-   Gained about 15lbs in the last couple years
-   Waist seems to keep getting bigger
-   Foot surgery in Feb of 2016 (still in pain from that - better BEFORE surgery!)
-   Raynaud's

Thanks for any help I receive!!! NOTE - files won't upload, I'll try again but here are some highlights:

-   8/3/16
    -   Estradiol (Saliva), 2.6 pg/ml, Range 1.3 - 3.3 Premenopausal (Luteal)
    -   Progesterone (Saliva), 40 pg/ml, Range 75-270 Premenopausal (Luteal)
    -   Ratio: Pg/E2 (Saliva), 15 Range - Optiaml 100-500 when E2 1.3-3.3
    -   Testosterone (Saliva) 27 pg/ml, Range 16-55 (Age Dependent)
    -   Cortisol, Morning (Saliva) 3.4 ng/ml, Range 3.7-9.5
-   2/5/16
    -   BUN/Creatinine Ratio 16
    -   Calcium, Serum, 9.1 mg/dl
    -   Cholesterol, Total 198 mg/dl
    -   Triglycerides, 41 mg/dl
    -   HDL Cholesterol, 73 mg/dl
    -   LDL Cholesterol Calc, 117 mg/dl
    -   VLDL Cholesterol Cal, 8 mg/dl
    -   Vitamin B12, 572 pg/ml
    -   Folate (Folic Acid), Serum, 9.3 ng/ml
    -   ANA Direct, Negative
    -   Antiscleroderma - 70 Antibodies, <0.2 AI
    -   T4, Free (Direct), 1.11 ng/dl
    -   TSH, 1.890 uIU/ml
    -   Smith Antibodies, <0.2 AI
    -   Vitamin D, 25-Hydroxy, 39.8 ng/ml
    -   Triiodothyronine, Free, Serum, 2.6 pg/ml
    -   Antinuclear Antibodies, IFA, Negative
-   3/25/15
    -   Progesterone (not saliva), .4
    -   Cortisol (not saliva), 24.0
-   4/8/14
    -   T3 Uptake, 36%
-   7/18/11

    -   Estradiol (Saliva), 2.1 pg/ml, Range 1.3 - 3.3 Premenopausal (Luteal)
    -   Progesterone (Saliva), 73 pg/ml, Range 75-270 Premenopausal (Luteal)
    -   Ratio: Pg/E2 (Saliva), 35 Range - Optiaml 100-500 when E2 1.3-3.3
    -   Testosterone (Saliva) 41 pg/ml, Range 16-55 (Age Dependent)
    -   DHEAS (Saliva), 5.0 ng/ml, Range 2-13 (Age Dependent)

    -   Cortisol, Morning (Saliva) 12.5 ng/ml, Range 3.7-9.5
    -   Cortisol, Night (Saliva) .06 ng/ml, Range 0.4-1.0

    -   IGF-1 (Blood Spot) 217 ng/ml, Range 100-300

### Kathy1967 — 2016-09-05T01:17:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curious-about-my-saliva-labs.18271/post-200869>

Hi - thanks for your response! I had some of the labs done through labcorb (covered by insurance) and the others with ZRT. The Omega 6/3 ratio was purchased through Vital Choice (sell seafood and some supplements)

I don't take many supplements - just started taking Maca the day after I did my saliva hormone tests because I was hoping to raise my DHEA. I THOUGHT I was low in DHEA but it turns out I'm not really but I AM low in Progesterone. I want to raise that naturally without exogenous hormones. I'm not really sure what supplements I would take to support the HPA axis (as I'm not really sure what that is!!!). Does it look like I should take something after reviewing my labs? I'm pretty sure I have an autoimmune condition (raynauds and just found out the name of an odd appearance to my tongue is called "Geographic Tongue" and it's an autoimmune disorder symptom.

Newbie so go easy
I am probably at the "fit" end of the scale - about 10-12% body weight and been following a lean gains weights and fasting type protocol for several years now - I come from a competitive endurance sports background, but also long term weight lifting.
I've been doing CT for 3-4 weeks now and seem to have adapted very well (doing morning and pre-bed CT - as an athlete i participated in a cold immersion study back in 2004 so was aware of some of the benefits), However I have noticed after the morning session that i develop a lot of jaw /neck tension for 3-5 hours post the CT.Any ideas?
I have done a search of the forum and google but can't seem to find anything relating to this.

### caroline — 2016-08-19T03:36:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee-coffee-coffee.18286/post-199820>

I have gone back to having a BPC lately. And I have been using MCT oil. All of a sudden the smell of coconut oil is so not appealing.

Dr. Mercola has an interesting article on MCT oil today ........ Refreshed my memory about the benefits.

I have been buying it recently at our local grocery store .....less expensive than Dave's. But - I wonder about the quality......

I am a little worried about having coffee ... but I have it because I really enjoy it .

Sitting outside with a cuppa in the early morning is priceless......especially with as few clothes as possible and just enjoying the morning with my favorite guy .....no talking necessary. Such peace and contentment.
No amount of money can buy that.....

Thoughts on coffee???

### Videha — 2016-08-22T14:04:44+0300

### Videha — 2016-08-23T12:45:15+0300

### Videha — 2016-08-23T17:12:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee-coffee-coffee.18286/post-200074>

This is the one i got> EarthTone© Flute – Key of "G" Minor – 432Hz Frequency Tuning
In the world of music there has been a revival of tuning instruments to lower frequencies of sound vibration. Until recently, most musical instruments have been tuned so that the “A” note (of the middle octave on a piano) is tuned to 440Hz (Hz = cycles per second). This is known as Concert Pitch and was established as a standard at the beginning of the 20th century.

This flute features Rainbow Moonstone inlaid cabochons, a fetish symbolizing the circle of life, and it is engraved with a geometric pattern representing the 432Hz frequency. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### thisbirdhaswings — 2016-08-31T19:35:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee-coffee-coffee.18286/post-200593>

Last time I tried to give up coffee entirely was just before (maybe a week or two) before my move from the gulf coast. I didn't miss it much, but then around the third day I started to have a strange withdrawal symptom of chest pain. I even called on a neighbor for aspirin late at night when the kids were asleep because God forbid I'd end up with a heart attack in the middle of the night. Anyway, I decided it wasn't the time to give it up. In these past few weeks as I've been dealing with the UTI fallout, I naturally found I didn't want it so much. I think it's interesting how our body communicates with us like that. I have in the past done bulletproof, but haven't wanted so much fat lately. I have been putting organic, grass fed cream, but have been pulling back from that too. My taste for it is disappearing. I'm surprised the taste of black coffee is just fine. One or two cups a day.

### caroline — 2016-09-01T04:23:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee-coffee-coffee.18286/post-200614>

Life seems to be a moving target these days! Hard for others in our life to keep up!

Just go with the flow .....and trust our bodies......

### Hunger4More — 2016-09-01T04:43:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee-coffee-coffee.18286/post-200618>

Coffee seems to be a huge player in my leaky gut... cutting down on it, while increasing nutrition to heal it is making some positive changes.

Even one per day is enough to set me off, and yes, I would even stick to organic coffee.

Transitioning off has been difficult. Coffee and caffeine gives me such a large mental boost that I'm trying to ween off them with brain specific nutrients (ALCAR, alpha GPC, etc.)

Wonder if anyone else has any tips they can recommend. MB and nicotine do nothing for me.

### caroline — 2016-09-01T06:56:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee-coffee-coffee.18286/post-200626>

### Hunger4More — 2016-09-01T14:14:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee-coffee-coffee.18286/post-200638>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=200626)
>
> What kind of nicotine are you using? How much? How often?

I've used lozenges. Played around with a lot. From 2mg - 16mg. I've gotten the "buzz" before, but never really felt a mental boost as I do from coffee. Never take it on consecutive days, and yes, I get plenty of strong sun light.

### Matthias — 2016-09-01T18:19:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee-coffee-coffee.18286/post-200650>

Coffee is great, both black and in BPC.

Coffee, for me, acts as something of a supplement, so I try and be diligent to abstain for it for at least 3 days between every... ~3-5 when I consume. Something I've noticed is that I get a slight bone ache if I've abstained from coffee for too long.

I have a minor casein sensitivity, so when I make BPC, I use I use ghee, coconut oil, a cacao butter wafer, and an egg. The cacao butter and egg help emulsify the ghee in addition to making it more nutritious. (I had mistakenly been using MCT oil that came in a PETE bottle, so I stopped. If I had to do it again, I would get [**this one**](https://www.amazon.com/NOW-Foods-MCT-100%25-Oil/dp/B00KPZXER6/ref=sr_1_1_a_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1472742640&sr=8-1&keywords=NOW+MCT+oil) because it comes in a glass bottle).

When I'm on coffee/caffeine, I've tested myself to have faster reaction times and more concrete logical reasoning. (My testing battery is timed chess puzzles). But I'm a little less patient, and I move and think more quickly.

### thisbirdhaswings — 2016-09-01T19:29:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee-coffee-coffee.18286/post-200653>

> [Hunger4More said:](/goto/post?id=200618)
>
> Coffee seems to be a huge player in my leaky gut... cutting down on it, while increasing nutrition to heal it is making some positive changes.
>
> Even one per day is enough to set me off, and yes, I would even stick to organic coffee.

>
> Wonder if anyone else has any tips they can recommend. MB and nicotine do nothing for me.

Chlorophyll in water maybe a great morning pick me up in lieu of coffee. That's how I started my day today. We have also been making spirulina smoothies in the morning. Spirulina is high in chlorophyll, so that might be another option for you.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-while-in-the-water.18292/post-199877>

Wow Dr. Kruse! That's awesome!!!! Thanks so much! Every hour I try to walk outside and spend at 2 - 10 minutes with my feet in the water and today I was thinking that if I stand farther out, say with the water up to my knees maybe I'm getting more grounded than if only my feet are in the water. Thank you and all the people on your site!

# Thread: "catching" a virus
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/catching-a-virus.18302/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2016-08-21T00:27:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/catching-a-virus.18302/post-199929>

How is it that we can "catch" a virus that is already on our genome? For instance, the herpes virus is supposedly already on our genome. So how do we catch something that we already got?
Confused on this subject...

### Cpt.Tired — 2016-09-23T23:54:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/catching-a-virus.18302/post-201829>

Is anyone else curious about this one?

### nonchalant — 2016-09-24T01:56:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/catching-a-virus.18302/post-201833>

Viral shedding. The immune system fails to keep it in check.

### Cpt.Tired — 2016-09-24T22:25:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/catching-a-virus.18302/post-201864>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=201833)
>
> Viral shedding. The immune system fails to keep it in check.

I understand that part but thanks Nonchalant for confirming it for me.
But I still don't understand how we can catch it if we already have it. My best guess is through resonance. If your immune system is low but still strong enough to just keep the viruses in check, and you are exposed to a "powered up " virus, they will resonate giving the suppressed virus the strength to overcome our immune system. This is just my "theory" until someone gives me the correct answer.

**Demographics**
As a suburb that is being converted from small farms to residential lots, the population of Piara Waters has grown significantly, from 94 people in 2006 to 2,589 in 2011.[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Piara_Waters,_Western_Australia#cite_note-1) The population is further expected to grow to over 8,000 by around 2021.[\[2\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Piara_Waters,_Western_Australia#cite_note-2) To accommodate this growth, there are three new community and sporting facilities planned for the suburb. Piara Waters Primary School was opened in February 2012, with classes to cater for students from Kindergarten to Year 7. A government high school is planned to open in neighboring [Harrisdale](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Harrisdale,_Western_Australia) in 2017.

1- last period approx 25 yrs ago..can ask gp if needed

3-get all three estrogens (E1) Estriol, (E2)estradiol, (E3)estriol
...is E1 &E3 spelt exactly the same, with only the upper/lower case being the difference? Just to clarify thanks.

4\. HRT(hormone replacement therapy ) skin PATCH -(not tablets)-ESTALIS-CONTINUOUS 50/140.. OESTRADIOL+ NORETHISTERONE ACETATE (NETA)
Started 9 aug 2016 apply 2 patches pr week. According to the pack insert the NETA is a progesterone.

5\. Not taking any thyroid hormones (mother had large goitre & thyroid issues)
6\. No diagnosis of adrenal fatique from gp... oops..that was me feeling adrenals & thyroid,hormones were out...

7\. Not taking any cortisol raising medication.. (waiting room heated to 23 degrees C-was feeling uncomfortably hot) cold,wet outside-home always between 10-15 degrees C..i did wonder whether being in hot room would've caused cortisol to rise.

8\. Cortisol lab comments: Current result makes adrenal insufficiency unlikely. If cortisol excess suspected clinically suggest either 24 hour urine free cortisol or overnight dexamethasone suppression test.

9\. Vit D: year 2008- 26 Low
year 2013 - 18 Low gp forgot to mention Vit D was low- i started supplementing myself, started with D3 1,000 IU pr day, upped it to 4,000 daily
year 2016 - 65
\*since stopped D3 supplementation

Using sun as often as possible.-i still need to learn more about australia's donut hole issue, i burn easily/have many large raised moles/some flat & some look suss....so taking sun with sensible use... til i find out more..

10\. Lighting; I agree! Been avoiding all artificial light at night, still looking for uv & ir lights to replace globes, still not fully understand the correct types to buy..have looked at reptile lights,black lights,uv lights........all bit confusing to me...seen some say reptile lights are good, some say no.
I was clear to avoid fluorescents, leds, cfl, then i saw some of the uv lights were fluoro, some with mercury...i recall seeing somewhere here to avoid the mercury ones if you know you are mercury toxic...i have never been tested,do have mercury fillings & broke a mercury thermometer last year.
i still need to learn more to prevent wasting money by purchasing the wrong type..just need/want to get it handled asap..

> coming out of winter now-more sun avail. Been sunning daily between 7am-10am & 2pm-sundown, with breaks inside to get stuff done.
> No sunnies, no sunscreen,no windows, feet on bare earth,pavers. Working on getting body used to sun, i burn easily,pacing myself..afternoon sun feels scorching so am finding the afternoons the hardest with the gazing.. i still need to learn more about the oz donut hole issue...

Sunlight have variable light frequency content during the day.
We are mostly concerned with UVb because it is most scarce.
There are also variation per latitude
but
UVb on average is only present within two hours before and after solar noon.
In USA, we have daylight saving time.

Daylight Saving Time (United States) 2016 began at 2:00 AM on
Sunday, March 13
and ends at 2:00 AM on
Sunday, November 6

During daylight saving time clock is pushed one hr ahead.
During daylight saving time UVb is present from 11AM till 3PM

In other times solar time and actual time are close.

From UVa we produce vitamin A (need to eat caretinoid containing food, carrots, peppers, night shades (but skip potatoes or other high GI)
From UVb we produce vitamind D(sulfate) (need to eat sulfur containing food, garlic, onions, leeks)

We also need sunlight to build and constantly reinforce our Circadian rhythm.
Without light our pineal gland produces melatonin.
Among other things, presence of melatonin is suppressive to cortisol.
Ideally you want to be weaken up by raising sun.
Practically,
sleep in (totally) dark room
when getting of the bed look at sun or (blue) light, brighter better

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) just posted September webinar.
I am still in the process of watching it,
but
my short takeout is:
skin cancers are not due to UV exposure
skin cancers are due to man made light (that is devoid of UV and IR)(and is otherwise unbalanced)

People exposed to sunlight are healthy or can be cured from sickness that was caused by lack of light (rickets, tuberculosis...)

Moles, freckles, etc will (definitely) get darker,..... get used to it.

Skin on scrotal area or breasts is different that most of other skin.
It needs sun exposure.

////

### JanSz — 2016-09-05T18:03:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-blood-test-results-cause-for-concern-or-acceptable.18306/post-200918>

> [Videha said:](/goto/post?id=200888)
>
> thanks for that...was tad confused on why. will look up EPA to learn more.. i knew about rancidity of fish oil caps, so i was using orthoplex bio-omega liquid fish oil...expensive...
> You are being very helpful, thank you!!

Rancidity or freshness are other issues that may be real but I do not even consider them.
EPA is one of the omega3 fats.
Seafood and fish/krill oils have high amount of them, much higher than people should have (some or most are able to self regulate desired levels).
If I had it right, people process more omega3 when they farther from equator.
Eventually, everybody responds differently, plus within last centuries lots of people migrated around the world.
Also, we see lots of neolitic diseases. Many of them result in excess of omega3.
For some time I was observing lab test results of fatty acids analysis that people here are posting.
I noted that about half of those who posted had excess of omega3.
I am one of them.
Relatively to omega6 the omega3 fats are stronger. Excess of omega3 is suppressive to omega6 (but the reverse is not true).
I ended up dropping (most not all) of seafood from my diet.
You may want to ask your doctor for this test:

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

If you get it, post results, I will help you in evaluation,
but
it is rather self explanatory.
Ideal level=mid range of laboratory range for each fat
acceptable level=plus-minus 25% of range

////
Omega3 but specially DHA are very important to our well being.
The good news is that we need minute amounts of it and the one that we have, have very long half life, so the need for replacement is not frequent. Other that seafood there are other foods that contain omega3's.

//

### JanSz — 2016-09-05T18:40:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-blood-test-results-cause-for-concern-or-acceptable.18306/post-200920>

> [Videha said:](/goto/post?id=200886)
>
> Thanks for video JanSz.. my flutes are part of my healing regime, i am becoming the **semi-naked** flutist in backyard....wonderful feeling, more & more birds come daily, beautiful eagle yesterday circling.

Try totally naked at least for some time.
The skin that semi-naked covers also needs sun.

Hormones- skin.

Fat under skin and elsewhere contains aromatase (CYP19).
Aromatase is an enzyme that converts testosterone or androstenedione to estradiol or estrone respectively.
Fat people have more aromatase, they tend produce estrogens.
(they may need more progesterone to balance that excessive estrogen load)

Skin contains 5Ar and 5Br (reductase) enzymes.
There is significantly more 5Ar and 5Br in scrotal skin (and I think in breast skin specially aureola and nipples).
Those enzymes assist in conversions of
progesterone
androstenedione
testosterone
cortisol
cortisone
to downstream metabolites
[http://imageshack.com/a/img923/1937/AhxmtP.jpg](http://imageshack.com/a/img923/1937/AhxmtP.jpg)

Like anything else, sun exposure can only help.

////

### Lahelada — 2016-09-06T12:45:57+0300

Videha, at the risk of confusing you, not everything you read here is what Dr K recommends. A lot of people build their own n-1 which is fine it it works for them. Dr K recommends selenium and iodine in its natural package ,seafood in other words. His view is that we cannot eat DHA as fast as blue light destroys it. He does not recommend supplementing,heck he does not even recommend eating these days... I am facetious about the latter of course, but just to make a point that diet is the least of your worries . Cook like your Great gran did, add seafood, done. Follow the seasons once well, stay ketotic until you are. Whatever you put in your stomach has a slim chance of getting where you want it to go, at least at the beginning of your journey as your gut may be leaky. It is very tempting to want to speed things along . I got focused on DHEA supplementation in the first days of my journey for no better reason than it seemed to fit the bill . How little did I know and how little do I know still. Knowing little keeps the thirst for knowledge awake though.

### JanSz — 2016-09-06T19:34:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-blood-test-results-cause-for-concern-or-acceptable.18306/post-201004>

> [Videha said:](/goto/post?id=200969)
>
> Re: Lugols link you posted>[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00E3B49R2?redirect=true&ref\_=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o04\_s00](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00E3B49R2?redirect=true&ref_=oh_aui_detailpage_o04_s00)
> i checked amazon>**International Shipping:** This item is not eligible for international shipping.
> Will speak to health food store manager & see if any way to get it into oz.where there's a will,there's a way..i won't quit. I need it. thanks J.

It does not have to be exactly the same product.
It is about content.
I have seen (unfortunately lost) information on how to make it home (gallons) from easily accessible ingredients.

Possibly right person will see this.

///
The idea is that adult person benefits by taking
50mg/day of iodine in form of Lugol's solution.
(Sometimes even 100mg/day)

By other standards this is very large dose.
Body will not use that amount,
but
it is easily discarded in urine.

That large amount of iodine/iodide will hopefully protect from assaults from other halides that are unfortunately very common.

Only iodide is halide (beneficial) to our health
the other (first three)
fluoride, chloride, bromide, astatide,
are common in our households

fluoride water, toothpaste, added to multivitamins (when prescribed by doctor)
bromide is added to flour, spa water
chloride added to pool water
\----------

A **halide** is a [binary compound](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Binary_compound), of which one part is a [halogen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halogen) [atom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Atom) and the other part is an [element](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chemical_element) or [radical](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radical_%28chemistry%29) that is less [electronegative](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electronegative) (or more electropositive) than the halogen, to make a [fluoride](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fluoride), [chloride](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chloride), [bromide](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bromide), [iodide](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iodide), [astatide](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Astatide),

\==
Nuclear fallout contains radioactive iodine.
Doctors use radioactive iodine to destroy cancerous thyroid gland.

/////////////

on small budget.

1\. no plans to move. I am afraid that my new location may not be any better than what I have now. It is easy to overlook important data. Changes to my new location may have already been planned. The new discussed locations are hurricane/tornadoes prone. In present location I have lost one small tree due to bad weather within 40 years I am here.
Now I have family all around me, some few houses away.
population density increased within last 40 years, but it is still about 2100/sq-mile,
but significantly less near my residence.
I am originally from 10degree higher latitude (Berlin, Warsaw), I have moved significantly south already.

2.Spend maximum time outside, specially during day time. Barefoot, naked, no glasses.....

3\. 24hrs divided into (14hr day) + (10hr) night, that goes 365/24/7

4\. 8hr sleep + 2hrs down time

5.usually that is
(9PM-11PM)downtime (red+IR(NIR-A) light only) + (11PM-7AM)sleep (no light)
(7AM-9PM) day, sunlight or
(Exo Terra Solar Glow 160W(two)

### JanSz — 2016-09-06T20:42:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-blood-test-results-cause-for-concern-or-acceptable.18306/post-201008>

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Videha](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Videha)

**Videha** was an ancient Vedic Kingdom established by King [Janaka](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Janaka) whose trans-boundary is presently located in [Mithila](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mithila,_Nepal) federal state of eastern [Madhesh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Madhesh) in [Nepal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nepal) and the northern region of [Indian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/India) state of [Bihar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bihar).

During the late [Vedic period](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vedic_period) (c. 1100-500 BCE), Videha became one of the major political and cultural centers of South Asia,

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vedic\_period](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vedic_period)

The **Vedic period** (or **Vedic age**) (c. 1500 – c. 500 BCE) was the period in [Indian history](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/History_of_India) during which the [Vedas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vedas), the oldest [scriptures](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Scriptures) of [Hinduism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hinduism), were composed.

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Historical\_Vedic\_religion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Historical_Vedic_religion)

The **religion of the [Vedic period](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vedic_period)** (also known as **Vedism**, **ancient Hinduism**, **Brahmanism** and **Vedic Brahmanism**[\[note 1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Historical_Vedic_religion#cite_note-5)) was the religion of the [Indo-Aryans](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indo-Aryan_peoples) of northern India.[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Historical_Vedic_religion#cite_note-FOOTNOTESamuel2010-6) It is a historical predecessor of modern [Hinduism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hinduism), though significantly different from it.

//

### JanSz — 2016-09-07T00:35:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-blood-test-results-cause-for-concern-or-acceptable.18306/post-201013>

I came across this site that sells internationally Lugol's solution.
(Very expensive)
[http://www.artpharmaceutical.com/pages/lugol-iodine-solution](http://www.artpharmaceutical.com/pages/lugol-iodine-solution)
I normally buy
J.CROW'S¨ Lugol's Iodine Solution(2 oz.)
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01FWM4J84/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o00\_s00?ie=UTF8](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01FWM4J84/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o00_s00?ie=UTF8)
I use 1 dropperfull=0.8cc a day, put it into my coffee
the label says
60mL
2% iodine
4% iodide
I do not know how much iodine is in potassium iodide, so i bunch the two and have 6%
dropperfull contains 0.8cc=0.8mL=0.8mg
6% of that is 48mg
so I assume that I use 48mg/day iodine
pretty close to doctor recommended 50mg/day (I thought)(see slide from presentation below)
now
on this artpharmaceutical.com site I see that
2.4mg potassium iodide = 1.8348mg iodine
so my 4% of iodide becomes
(4\*1.8348/2.4)=3.058
(2+3.058)\*0.8/100\*100=40.464mg
so I am not taking 48mg/day iodine
it is closer to 40.464mg/day
I am not changing my dose, one dropper-full is convenient to dispense,
but it is good to know about this.

### caroline — 2016-09-07T06:20:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-blood-test-results-cause-for-concern-or-acceptable.18306/post-201028>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=200975)
>
> Videha, at the risk of confusing you, not everything you read here is what Dr K recommends. A lot of people build their own n-1 which is fine it it works for them. Dr K recommends selenium and iodine in its natural package ,seafood in other words. His view is that we cannot eat DHA as fast as blue light destroys it. He does not recommend supplementing,heck he does not even recommend eating these days... I am facetious about the latter of course, but just to make a point that diet is the least of your worries . Cook like your Great gran did, add seafood, done. Follow the seasons once well, stay ketotic until you are. Whatever you put in your stomach has a slim chance of getting where you want it to go, at least at the beginning of your journey as your gut may be leaky. It is very tempting to want to speed things along . I got focused on DHEA supplementation in the first days of my journey for no better reason than it seemed to fit the bill . How little did I know and how little do I know still. Knowing little keeps the thirst for knowledge awake though.

Love this^^^^^

Dr. K. Always, always tells us to question everything .....even him.

Just because something is written here by someone - doesn't always make it correct or true.....

Dr. K. Is giving us the science .....read, decipher, learn, cross reference, read ......wash, rinse and repeat

Dr. K. Couldn't be more clear about supplementing .....he has been beating this drum for a long time now.

### JanSz — 2016-08-23T22:04:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-how-to-get-started.18308/post-200102>

> [Joeakim said:](/goto/post?id=200098)
>
> Thanks JanSz!
> I found an article by D Asprey. Will have to consider Risk/Reward factors for my n=1.
>
> [https://www.bulletproofexec.com/is-nicotine-the-next-big-smart-drug/](https://www.bulletproofexec.com/is-nicotine-the-next-big-smart-drug/)

These downsides are the reason I recommend that, if you decide to use nicotine, you do it at very low dose **(1-2mg), and only occasionally** as needed. I’ve used nicotine a handful of times over the past four years and I’ve tried out many different forms, but it’s certainly not something I do regularly. It’s useful for an occasional boost.
\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[
The problem with nicotine gum is that chewing gum fires your trigeminal nerve more than it should be firing. Save your chewing for eating, and your jaw (and nervous system) will be healthier.
'''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''
**Nicotine lozenges mostly suffer from the same problem as nicotine gum;** they’re full of crappy chemicals and sweeteners like aspartame, acesulfame-potassium (Ace-K), and sucralose. **The safest one I’ve found is the Nicorette mini-lozenge, which is very small, and contains no aspartame**. You do get a small dose of unsafe sweetener, but it’s so tiny that it probably doesn’t matter. **I take ½ of the smallest 2mg lozenge, and feel a cognitive shift in about 15 minutes.** These are easy to find in the US. Go for the mini lozenges; large Nicorette lozenges are full of chemicals you don’t want.

.......................
**Nicorette mini Nicotine Lozenge Mint 2 milligram Stop Smoking Aid 81 count**
from [Nicorette](https://www.amazon.com/Nicorette/b/ref=bl_dp_s_web_2597365011?ie=UTF8&node=2597365011&field-lbr_brands_browse-bin=Nicorette)
[https://www.amazon.com/Nicorette-Ni...1-spons&keywords=nicorette+mini+lozenge&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/Nicorette-Nicotine-Lozenge-milligram-Smoking/dp/B0036WTVH2/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1471979156&sr=8-1-spons&keywords=nicorette+mini+lozenge&psc=1)

### JanSz — 2016-08-26T14:13:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-how-to-get-started.18308/post-200261>

**[https://www.drugs.com/dosage/methylene-blue.html](https://www.drugs.com/dosage/methylene-blue.html)**

**Renal Dose Adjustments**
No dosage adjustment recommendations available; however, should be used with caution in patients with severe renal impairment. Methylene blue is metabolized in the body to leukomethylene blue which is excreted primarily in the urine. Some unchanged drug is also excreted in the urine.

### Penny — 2016-12-15T00:04:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-how-to-get-started.18308/post-205278>

> [Joeakim said:](/goto/post?id=200022)

> Hey all,
>

In my humble experience, do some face dunks prior to nicotine and be in the sunshine - good water with some methylene blue is also a hit - I have used the gum and just chew the first part and spit it out - after about 15 chews, I stick it between my bottom teeth and gums - I think even that wrecks my teeth though - this placement of the nicotine gum I got from nonchalent (member on this forum) - I have found research that states it rots your teeth/gums, so I do this instead:

I take BH4 to make dopamine (dopamine+tyrosine (found in any protein)=melanin)
sunshine hits melanin and splits water into hydrogen and oxygen -

However, I don't think you get the small superoxide burst with just BH4, so I'm doing fermented papaya for that... the superoxide burst is the signal for your mitochondria to repair - which is also what nicotine does -

The stuff made me nauseous without the face dunks - more good news for my biology

I think you can also use berberine (Oregon Grape root) for the ROS burst :
upperroomwellness.com/.../Berberine\_use\_in\_Stimulating\_Caspase\_Apoptotic\_Pathw.ay
"There are numerous other studies that may be cited, however, taken together, berberine may exert an antitumor activity in cancer cells by reactive oxygen species generation and mitochondrial-related apoptotic pathways. These findings provide an insight into the potential of berberine for cancer therapy."

In fact, here is a great article on regenerating melanin:
[https://doctorglen.wordpress.com/2013/07/18/regenerating-melanin/](https://doctorglen.wordpress.com/2013/07/18/regenerating-melanin/)

and here is one on glycine and ROS:
[http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1567576908002889](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1567576908002889)

and here's a nice article on why you would want a superoxide burst
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4197410/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4197410/)

and here is an article on nicotine and periodontal issues:
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2933534/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2933534/)

# Thread: Reading ......
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading.18333/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2016-08-29T02:42:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading.18333/post-200412>

We all have soooooo much to read ......there just doesn't seem to be enough hours in the day!

Dr. K. Is giving us so much .....so how are you getting a handle on all this?

Dr. Mercola talks about walking at the ocean everyday and reading on his kindle. Great multi tasking!

Are you reading on a kindle???

I had gone back to paper .....but it is so hard to get books here in Australia - ebooks are so much more efficient.

So - if you are reading on your kindle outside in the sunshine ....?Do you wear your blue lockers?

I bought a cheap pair of half readers at the drug store and have been using those. - pushed way down my nose.

Is this a good idea?

What is everyone doing to absorb all these amazing gifts we are given?

Especially on the days that Dr. K. Has verbal diarrhoea???

### Sue-UK — 2016-08-29T12:34:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading.18333/post-200439>

I don't use technology to read outside, it has to be a book or a printed out blog, and only after I have felt a "shift" .... Because of our lower quantum yield I spend time grounding, scanning the skies, until I sense that my mitochondria have read the sun's barcode, and tuned in to my environment....on a good day that takes 10 to 20 minutes, the longest was 2 hours after a difficult visit to mum in the nursing home. I do download books onto an iPad, not ideal but I use airplane mode, and have replaced the clear plastic in the cover with an amber one. I also have a tray for it, and it sits on a bag filled with crushed oyster shells, putting something else between it and the table my legs are under. Not sure what difference it makes .. I also use it when I want to pop indoors and check out a link mentioned in a printed out blog. I removed the speakers from my office computer because of the emf readings, so I also listen to the webinars on it, which is why I've kept it and not bought a kindle. My office screen also has an amber cover so I only use blublockers for artificial light in the evening, or if the TV is on.

### Lahelada — 2016-08-29T13:03:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading.18333/post-200441>

my modus operandi :
\- skim a new blog for keywords and match with older blogs that fit the current subject at hand when back reading. While everything is connected some blogs have more nuggets than others depending on what the subject of the week is.
\- copy paste everything into mail messages creating my own "book" with pithy titles like "Ferritin the iron ferry". The titles really only really have to make sense to you
\- I have also printed off catch up blogs for reading at night by red light, on the bus or when sunbathing. Basically multitasking.
\- I don't like using a computer outside. I have done when listening to webinars but it disturbs me on an "esthetic" level, feels too incongruous with its surroundings.
\- Talk to the dog about the about matters at hand. I find I need to read less as hearing myself expound crystalises where I need to delve deeper or where surprisingly I have a reasonable grasp already. No idea what the dog thinks about it..

### Greenie — 2018-01-31T09:11:47+0200

### Jan Christer — 2018-03-26T00:03:15+0300

### Greenie — 2018-03-26T00:41:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading.18333/post-237957>

### karenr — 2018-03-26T00:43:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading.18333/post-237958>

I use an old Kindle Touch, e-ink, no backlighting, and it has text to speech, which I like when I'm tired and don’t feel like reading. The speech isn’t terribly robotic sounding, either.

The newer ones don’t have text to speech - now they don’t want to give you too many features on the lower priced Kindles, so you'll buy the higher priced tablets with bells and whistles and blue light and wireless. You do need to use wifi to download books onto the device initially, but then I turn the wifi off.

I also use my iPad now without WiFi, so it’s more like an e-reader, but I just don’t know the effects of looking at that screen, even though I have it so heavily blue-blocked. An e-ink screen definitely looks more natural than any back lit screen no matter the color temperature.

Been trying to find a way to hook my phone to an ethernet connection, though it seems that many phones don't recognize a Micro-USB to Ethernet connection.

# Thread: 10 Gauss Magnetico Daily Progress Report
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/10-gauss-magnetico-daily-progress-report.18338/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KalosKaiAgathos — 2016-08-29T15:52:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/10-gauss-magnetico-daily-progress-report.18338/post-200448>

Started using the 10 Gauss Magnetico since yesterday. I am extremely tired today. The problem is, that I'm working from my bedroom, as I'm still living with my parents. I do not get within 4 feet of the positive magnetic field that is projected to the sides however.

Yesterday, I was also very tired. I was working security in the weekend, and only sleeping for one hour between Friday and Saturday, and five hours between Saturday and Sunday. Quit the job, as it is not worth it. Bedtime was 4:30 (AM!) during both days. These days ruin me. I only used to work this job during the summer holidays, but I consider it no longer worth it. For two years now, all year round, I have blocked all toxic blue light after sunset, except during the time I work this job. Then, when some of these days come around, it takes a few additional days to recover. My mental performance is shot.

So, the first night on the Magnetico went amazing. Very deep sleep and I remember some very good dreams. Immediately after getting up at 6:15AM, a 30 minute CT session in my 400 gallon tub outside. Water temperature was 60F, so very hot. It will get colder soon in the Netherlands.

Does anyone know whether the Magnetico projects north and south in addition to the 4 feet it projects a positive magnetic field sideways? And does anyone know whether the Magnetico also leaves a diagonal positive magnetic field behind? I need to know this in order to make the choice whether to study in my bedroom during the day.

Another option for me would be to fold the Magnetico EVERY day, and to move it in a rolled-up state another room. The only reason I'm considering _not_ doing that, is that it will damage the magnets in the long run.

I'll keep you updated.

### KalosKaiAgathos — 2016-09-01T08:30:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/10-gauss-magnetico-daily-progress-report.18338/post-200631>

Second day went oke. I was still pretty tired in the evening, but no longer in the afternoon as the first day. On the third day, there was no tiredness during the day. That night I had the best sleep that I can remember in my life, except maybe sleeping in to 13:00 in my teens

It surprises me HOW much quicker I fall asleep, and how much deeper the sleep is. This thing is worth every penny IMO. And I'm using only 10 Gauss. Imagine what 20 or 40 Gauss will do, or even more!

Edit: Still a bit tired during the day. My mind is not yet working optimally.

### KalosKaiAgathos — 2016-09-02T19:47:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/10-gauss-magnetico-daily-progress-report.18338/post-200738>

Had two very good days, with great sleep. Still the same in terms of benefits: Deeper sleep, more dreams, falling asleep easier. No benefits (yet) during the day. No more tiredness either! That probably means that I'm almost adapted.

Thinking about how to get to 60 Gauss in the future. These things rock

### Danco3636 — 2016-09-03T02:47:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/10-gauss-magnetico-daily-progress-report.18338/post-200755>

Two weeks ago upgraded my 5 to a 10. Notice I am having some fun dreams lately. Before that been on the 5g for about 3 years.

### KalosKaiAgathos — 2016-09-03T08:25:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/10-gauss-magnetico-daily-progress-report.18338/post-200766>

Sounds fun! 99,999999% of people would rather spend 20.000$ on a bigger car than a better Magnetico. I guess that's what an expensive 60 or 80 Gauss Custom made Magnetico would cost.

I rather drive a worse car, or a borrowed one, and have the best Magnetico. But before that: Graduate College

### KalosKaiAgathos — 2016-09-05T10:06:50+0300

# Thread: If One (1) hour a day keeps the doctor away...what is your choice?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-one-1-hour-a-day-keeps-the-doctor-away-what-is-your-choice.18360/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Joeakim — 2016-09-01T23:44:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-one-1-hour-a-day-keeps-the-doctor-away-what-is-your-choice.18360/post-200673>

I have asked this same question to a friend recently: "If you had just one hour a day, what would you do: sunlight exposure or CT?

He said, he would sit in the CT tub while getting the sunlight.

Mind you when I asked him that question, I was thinking of a busy 9 - 5 working folks and my family especially my son who works and lives in downtown Chicago.

So, I thought the answer would be getting sun for an hour during lunch and do CT later in the day or early early. Furthermore, for the working folks the Fournier Affect may not be applicable.

And of course mitigating nnEMF and artificial light as much as possible is given in the formula.

### Scompy — 2016-09-02T00:42:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-one-1-hour-a-day-keeps-the-doctor-away-what-is-your-choice.18360/post-200679>

This

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/)

### caroline — 2016-09-02T06:57:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-one-1-hour-a-day-keeps-the-doctor-away-what-is-your-choice.18360/post-200694>

For me ..... A deep and loving relationship gives me wings - and that is 24/7 if you are very, very lucky.

And hugging ...... After I left Dr. K. In Orlando I ran into this extraordinary young man - a bell hop at the hotel.

He gave me the biggest and bestest bear hug I have ever had. I asked him about it and he told me his whole family were huggers. I asked about his girlfriend - if she was a hugger? And he said "yes". "I would never be with anyone that wasn't a hugger - that didn't love hugging"

That told me so much about this gorgeous young man and how he handled himself.

### Lahelada — 2016-09-02T13:26:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-one-1-hour-a-day-keeps-the-doctor-away-what-is-your-choice.18360/post-200700>

I have been wondering recently if your haplotype and percentage of heteroplasmy dictate the ratio of sun , grounding etc. that you need. I think my answer for now is that it would vary throughout the year and how my journey goes.

Example in spring I would give priority to sunbathing to get sun callus going for summer.

Summer I would have the full hour of sun exposure, autumn a gradual shift to more grounding again winter grounding as in most places you have insufficient quantum yield. Grounding can then easily be combined with CT as you can undress even in the city in your lunch hour and throw yourself into the local park. It is the old hibernation period and so seems apropos for more grounding to reduce inflammation acquired during summer.

Never leave the sun out of the equation though. However, starting out follow the order of the six healers _as written_ .

As I said my musings and I am a Nordic type so the answers maybe different for someone with a different haplotype.

@[Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) thanks for the reminder. I was ketogenic before getting into JK's protocols including much sunlight, water, and cold shower (7+ years) and recently Face-dunk (sort of CT) while getting more seafood intro. I guess i was trying to maximize time and effort in our busy day...but sometimes there is no shortcut.

> I wonder if massage might do you some good? If you can get one in the sun might be ++. Or why not start with self massage in the sun? <--I may try this soon as we get our sun back from the storm.

You would be wrong! I was never much of a hugger - and didn't share anything of myself with anyone except my DH.

I have learned a ton the last couple of years ...And yes, I think hugging is very healing.

I always hated messages - I would get very hyper. It probably felt like an invasion of my private space.

Now that I know better ....

# Thread: sun gazing in Australia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-gazing-in-australia.18380/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hello — 2016-09-06T02:44:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-gazing-in-australia.18380/post-200952>

Hey guys, I've just realised that the right time to get sun exposure is in the morning and that looking at the sun is important. I've been sitting in the sun in the middle of the day with my glasses on, but just realised through reading here that actually the thing to do is to look at or near the sun, glasses off, early in the morning........

For anyone who knows, anyone in Aus, etc., - is it safe to look at or near the sun? What time of day should it be done? I assume its just dangerous to do after mid morning.

if anyone has any thoughts or has experimented with it would love to know! thanks!

### Jenny S — 2016-09-06T05:20:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-gazing-in-australia.18380/post-200956>

Hi & welcome. Yes you do need the sun in your eyes first thing - preferably before you get any artificial light in your eyes. It's ok to look at sun or near up toabout 20 secs altho I find I can look at it longer when I get up - definitely no glasses when outside whenever possible. Morning sun is optimal for all body but afternoon still good. There is a UV checker which I will put link - handy to find max UV times so best time to spend in sun if time limited [http://www.arpansa.gov.au/uvindex/realtime/bri\_rt.htm](http://www.arpansa.gov.au/uvindex/realtime/bri_rt.htm)
Somewhere on forum there is a blog to read first for newbies - can't see it at moment but am sure someone else will link it for you.
Where are you located? I'm on Bribie Island - moved up here from Vic for more sun. You're off to a good start finding Jack's work anyway - good luck

### caroline — 2016-09-06T07:29:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-gazing-in-australia.18380/post-200971>

Welcome.....

There is lots of stuff on the home page on how to get started here.....

Do you listen to Dr. k's podcasts? They are a great way to get information

Do you have Dr. K's book? It is the first couple of years of the blog

Please get John Ott's book .....health and light

### Lahelada — 2016-09-06T12:57:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-gazing-in-australia.18380/post-200976>

The link Jenny mentions is in my signature. There is an error though in thinking that you need to stare into the sun. Don't make me look for the quote but Dr K says to look 15-20 degrees east of the sun rather than practice direct sungazing. The sunning in the am should be done at sunrise or as close to it as possible. This is done for resetting your clock. Sunning yourself for Vitamin D happens in another window. Afternoon sun between 3-5 is healing due to the shift of frequencies towards red.

# Thread: Buying blue blocking glasses for the office.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-blue-blocking-glasses-for-the-office.18401/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Juha — 2016-09-09T12:02:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-blue-blocking-glasses-for-the-office.18401/post-201175>

I'm in the process of purchasing a pair of blue blocking glasses for my office job. My intention is to protect myself from the blue light emitted from computer screens and fluorescent lighting.

At home I've been wearing a pair of amber tinted Uvex Skyper Safety Glasses a few hours before bedtime. They have worked wonderfully for me! However, these safety glasses aren't very stylish, to say the least. I can wear them in my own apartment but I wouldn't consider using them in a public setting!

I've done my best to research what the most important characteristics of blue blocking glasses are for an office environment:

-   I think the tint of the lenses should be as close as possible to an amber color. (Many of the gaming/computer glasses on the market are closer yellow than amber. I think these yellow lenses won't block as much blue light as amber lenses.)

-   The build of the frame should be such that too much light doesn't come in through the sides of the glasses. In other words, it doesn't matter if the lenses block blue light, if blue light gets in through the sides.

On top of these two characteristics, **what else would you consider to be important in a pair of blue blocking glasses, for an office environment?**

### lawrencep93 — 2016-09-09T13:03:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-blue-blocking-glasses-for-the-office.18401/post-201176>

I bought "PAVOSCREEN BLUE LIGHT BLOCKER COMPUTER GLASSES" for the office, because the glass colour is close to clear, I know it only blocks 50% blue light.
However I pair them with the UVEX blue light blockers once the sun sets, also keep the lights down low at home once the sun sets.

Take all glasses off when going outside

### Juha — 2016-09-09T13:24:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-blue-blocking-glasses-for-the-office.18401/post-201178>

Thanks for the tips lawrencep93!

Did you have any other reasons for buying the Pavoscreen glasses other than that the glass color is close to clear?

### lawrencep93 — 2016-09-09T14:08:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-blue-blocking-glasses-for-the-office.18401/post-201183>

Price and availability.

Hard to get something good here in Australia at a decent price with out paying large sums for shipping.

I had a pair of yellow gaming glasses but they have a slight zoom in effect which gives me a head ache if I am looking at anything from a distance.
But mainly they are super yellow compared to Pavoscreen which look more like every day glasses

### Juha — 2016-09-09T15:40:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-blue-blocking-glasses-for-the-office.18401/post-201185>

Yep, got it!

I know how you feel with the expensive shipping costs. I live in Finland and this has been an issue for me for a long time.

Luckily, it looks like the market of blue light blocking glasses is expanding. There's plenty of different models of blue light blocking glasses on UK's Amazon these days.

# Thread: Optimal use of the device for IR
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-use-of-the-device-for-ir.18415/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### smith5 — 2016-09-11T20:51:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-use-of-the-device-for-ir.18415/post-201314>

Hi,
I've been following dr. Kruse's recommendations for some time in drinking more cooled spring water, eating more fish and trying to get at least 10-20 min or more sunlight a day and got quite not bad results although some better results would be possible for myself at least by other means. Now i would like to tune a device i have for optimal usage. It's a device generating vibroacoustic and infrared frequency patterns for a variety of disorders, as mentioned in its operation manual. It's manufactured in Russia and named "Vitafon-2". (Probably better than nothing). As i would like to tune for optimal use only it's IR emitter, so would briefly describe only the framework it is used in as it's described in it's operation user manual.

"Therapeutic effect of infrared radiation is linked to microcirculation enhancement in tissues, activation of physical and biochemical processes in an organism and reduction of inflammation intensity.
The power of IR-radiation changes automatically and continuously according to a preset programmed cycle from a minimum of 3 mW to a maximum of 40 mW. This produces temperature microoscillations in the area of exposure. Also, the working surface of the IR emitter is a metallic disk and its contact with the body causes a short-term reduction in temperature down 2-3 °C on the surface of the exposure area. This enhances muscle microtremor and therefore lymph efflux and venous drainage are improved.
IR radiation of the emitter has an average wavelength of 0.95 micrometers. The effect of such radiation has an anti-inflammatory nature."

So IR radiation (in conjunction with vibrophone generated preset acoustic frequency patterns) generated by this device is used for combined treatment of prostatitis, cystitis, trophic ulcers, pyelonephritis (including remission stage), arthritis, maxillary sinusitis, chronic hepatitis, lower limb lymphostasis and diabetic angiopathy. Vibroacoustic patterns alone are also used for other disorders, but i would not mention them as am going to use only IR part. Approximate IR duration for these treatments is 10 (3-15) min a day for 14-21 days (i can provide more details if necessary). IR radiation is preprogrammed by the device to be emitted in 7 different modes (not in a steady state mode) with IR power varying at various time cycles. Detailed IR power timing is depicted in the graphs attached, there's no explanation in the manual for this specific timing and i didn't search for relevant studies. The diameter of the IR emitter disk is ~5cm, and there are 3 IR radiation points at it's perimeter line at 120 degree angles (they are activated all at once).

I would like to use it in the framework of regeneration and general function improvement and I heard there may be some precautions and contraindications for IR usage.

Another optimization for IR is making more EZ water with this emitter (just radiating the IR onto the water) and drink it or use freezed. And there are several questions in relation to that:
1) what (spring) water volume should be used (i have 500, 720 ml and 3 l glass volumes for now)
2) IR exposure time (i would suppose 15-30 minutes)
3) should i drink the irradiated water immediately or can store it cooled in a dark fridge or make ice
4) can i test the irradiated water by smartphone magnetic field sensors (or maybe there are other means to see the features changed by IR).

Any ideas, suggestions?

Thanks.
