**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/20w-uv-black-light-advice-please.18416/post-201326>

Noob question..go easy on me pls

'Brilliant' B22 20W UV Black Light Spiral Globe $14.95
Model Name UV Black Light B22 20W CFL
Model Number 19184
Material Glass Colour Black
Product Dimensions (mm) W:60 H:160 L:60
Package Dimensions (mm) W:62 H:175 L:62
Features 20W max B22 globe Black UV spiral globe
Average Life (hours) 6000
Commercial No
Dimmable No
Indoor Use Yes
Outdoor Use No
Package Quantity 1
Wattage (watts) 20

Unclear as to the whole issue of what is correct & am asking for some clarification please.
I realize there is no substitute for natural light.. i started the 'no artificial light at all'- needed some form of light to prevent accidents etc.
I had it set in my head to avoid all fluoro,LED,CFL's.... am wary this is a CFL style, i do have mercury fillings (not been tested)

I know i still need to obtain red light for nightime...\*working on that

My questions are....
Appropriate timings to use it? Evenings only? Restrict how long it's on?
Been turning it on only briefly to navigate through house..whilst still having blueblockers on & face covered ( if comp on)
Is this bulb acceptable or am i doing more damage?
I have 2 more days if i want to return it.

Thank you for any input.

### Lahelada — 2016-09-12T04:26:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/20w-uv-black-light-advice-please.18416/post-201328>

Videha ,do you know the nm specifications ? My view is thsi in general
If the light is Uva and a bulb then it is ok. If it is the spirally kind. The round type they sell is a bit weak .
UV LED I do not recommend as they go very near or even into the blue spectrum
. What kind of light does your give?Is it really brilliant or just a help when finding your way around?

As far as timing is concered ,have it on when you would have UVA pesent naturally ,that is during the day and maybe an hour in the evening. Use it when you use the PC during the day

Hi Videha

My computer screen has its own amber glasses .... Its a stage lighting gel, two names I know of are Rosco [https://www.rosco.com/filters/roscolux.cfm#colors](https://www.rosco.com/filters/roscolux.cfm#colors) and Lee Filters. [http://www.leefilters.com/lighting/colour-list.html](http://www.leefilters.com/lighting/colour-list.html). They show the spectral energy distribution, but it might be worth getting a swatch (I have the Lee Filters one), before buying a piece. I bought a range of colours years ago (making my own light therapy unit), but have cut one up for devices, spare bits go over torches etc ... I've just raked my swatch out from the depths of the drawer ... you have inspired me to check if the colour I picked for my long gone DIY colour therapy unit could be improved on for the purpose I want it for now....

>
> Videha ,do you know the nm specifications ? My view is thsi in general
> If the light is Uva and a bulb then it is ok. If it is the spirally kind. The round type they sell is a bit weak .
> UV LED I do not recommend as they go very near or even into the blue spectrum
> . What kind of light does your give?Is it really brilliant or just a help when finding your way around?
>
> As far as timing is concered ,have it on when you would have UVA pesent naturally ,that is during the day and maybe an hour in the evening. Use it when you use the PC during the day

\*Reply from the Product Engineer

The band is UVA.
Wavelengths from about 345 to 400 nM are used for "Blacklight" effects (causing many fluorescent objects to glow).
These longer UVA wavelengths emitted by our blacklight and general purpose high pressure mercury lamps are harmless. Shorter UVA wavelengths from 315 to 345 nM (not emitted by our blacklight) are used for suntanning.

That’s correct, the UV 20W globe contains mercury.
In fact all fluorescent lights contain a small amount of mercury which is sealed within the glass tube.

Regards,
Peter Strajner | Product Engineer

### Sue-UK — 2016-09-13T13:08:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/20w-uv-black-light-advice-please.18416/post-201380>

> [Videha said:](/goto/post?id=201378)
>
> Hi Sue, Thanks for the links-briefly looked at them...all a bit over my head at this stage. I do like the idea of your diy light therapy unit- did you get plans off internet,or did some handy person help you? I am interested in the diy idea, sounds really useful...
> Did you get good results from yours? Thanks

It was about 18 years ago ..... I was working long hours in a small office with lots of computers and printers, fluorescent lighting,and with that and an underwire bra I developed a skin infection under one of the girls. I had read about colour therapy and tried a standard coloured bulb, not a good idea, it blew and I got a nasty burn from the hot glass .... So DH built a box to house a full spectrum light, mesh in front, and we made up the filters so that they slotted in. But it dawned on me that although it "felt" better, my problem wasn't caused by lack of shining an artificial light source ...... My first subtle energy course introduced me to Becker, I left the job and the unit disappeared to the garage ..... Later complex homeopathy taught me that if all treatment does is relieve a symptom, without removing the cause, it can drive the disease process deeper. A seemingly unrelated problem can later develop. (I now relate the complex homeopathy system to mitochondria, heteroplasmy and the 3 legged stool). The greatest benefit to my skin was leaving the job ....changing my environment ...... I have a commercial light/colour therapy unit, a vertical tanner and an FIR sauna now that I do occasionally use. But only if weather conditions outside make it a better option than doing nothing. Used occasionally its "different", an acute stimulus, used too often I think it becomes "same" chronically, using unnatural light sources and NNemf. The context becomes different ...... It tests my discipline .....  Sitting in a sauna or using the tanner would be soooooo much more comfortable than spending the same time out in the CT tub in the pouring rain ......

# Thread: Optimal from the start on
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-from-the-start-on.18417/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2016-09-12T10:35:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-from-the-start-on.18417/post-201340>

I am fresh and greenhorn father since the day before yesterday.
I wonder how to start the optimal journey for my daughter from now on. For nighttime a redlight environment is set for the mother and the child when she must breastfeeding.
But what other things we have to do? I read about grounding the kid through skin to skin contact. Is that a good idea?
Do we have to be careful with the sunlight, because its very warm for september in germany this year? I read that a newborn must not get direct sunlight.

### fitness@home — 2016-09-12T16:07:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-from-the-start-on.18417/post-201345>

Congratulations! Picture please...

### Butters — 2016-09-12T22:04:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-from-the-start-on.18417/post-201359>

No picture here. It is all open to see, sorry.

### Rubicon — 2016-09-13T03:37:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-from-the-start-on.18417/post-201368>

Congratulations!

I haven't done extensive research, but my understanding is that babies born when there's a lot of sunlight typically develop healthier and do better in the long-term. It seems unlikely that babies born in the East African rift zone area while humans were evolving would have been kept from sunlight, and we are likely optimized to receive sunlight from birth.

If I were in your position my instinct would probably be to try and get that kid as much safe sun exposure for the skin type as possible before the sun continues to fall towards a weak winter solstice in Germany. I'd probably look into using artificial UV/IR in winter too. Interested to see what others have to say.

### caroline — 2016-09-13T09:51:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-from-the-start-on.18417/post-201376>

congratulations! how wonderful .....

### NaturalHealthForLife — 2016-10-06T08:01:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-from-the-start-on.18417/post-202375>

Baby carry/snuggle all the time. Mom eats fish/dha foods. Lots of walks with baby outside. Read to and talk to baby. Imagine what Indian tribe would have done with baby while camping outside. No Emf in baby's room. No blue/artificial light. Fresh air open windows.

They used to hang baby's outside the window in a cage playpen to get fresh air (yes!). In Sweden they leave baby strollers outside the store while mom shops.

They are tougher than you think. Skin to skin is good for warming/bonding. Then bundle up and get them outside!

# Thread: Refitting my factory.  Replacing CFL lights.  Suggestions?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/refitting-my-factory-replacing-cfl-lights-suggestions.18472/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Simon Wright — 2016-09-26T03:14:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/refitting-my-factory-replacing-cfl-lights-suggestions.18472/post-201894>

HI All,

I am refitting my factory and want to replace all the CFL lights with broad spectrum lighting. I wanted to ask if anyone has had any experience with this but on a commercial and not residential level. (even what you have done residentially would be appreciated as i can pick some specs or products out of there)

I live in the tropics (thus have not looked into this before) and whilst my light habits are pretty good I have a whole factory of workers who spend all day under CFL's and I want to 'share the love'. I prefer to do this on a discreet basis and replace all the lights rather than giving a speech on biophysics will be met with blank looks and some gaping of mouths.

Any websites or specs would be greatly appreciated.

Many thanks

Simon

### Rubicon — 2016-09-26T04:45:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/refitting-my-factory-replacing-cfl-lights-suggestions.18472/post-201897>

I'm imagining your factory floor having workstations with hanging lamps that contain cfl bulbs. Something like these:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

If that's that case, it seems the best fix might be to change out the cfl bulbs with 60 or 100W incandescent.

I'm actually in the process of getting my wifes office renovated. The rooms are 9ft high, have suspended ceiling tiles, and fluorescent tube lights. I've been looking around at various offices and found a design that replaces the tube fluorescent with 4 recessed pot flood lights that hold [incandescent bulbs](http://www.homedepot.com/p/Philips-65-Watt-Incandescent-BR30-Flood-Light-Bulb-12-Pack-per-Case-248872/100560688?MERCH=RV-_-rv_nav_plp_rr-_-NA-_-100560688-_-N). It produces a nice atmosphere. I was thinking of augmenting that with a reptile light that would run on a timer for a couple of hours every day at solar noon, and/or a UVA light.

Another option might be LED lights. I've heard some discussion about systems that adjust color temperature based on time of day to try and mimic the sun. Those would likely not contain the infrared component of light...so perhaps incandescent is still the best option for now.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Miss Lizzie — 2016-10-01T02:53:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-my-ice-baths.18500/post-202147>

I started doing ice baths, cold showers and using an ice vest as part of the Leptin Rx. I have to say, I look forward to my icing every night!

When I take my ice bath, I find it relaxing and even my heart rate starts to lower (not into a bad range).

I'm a person has always been cold. I'd be in my office with my space heater on complaining about the A/C, Now since doing CT, I'm warm. It feels way too hot when I'm near my coworkers with their space heaters on.

Has anyone else had a similar experience?

### caroline — 2016-10-01T08:47:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-my-ice-baths.18500/post-202154>

There is a huge C/T thread......

### Valerie — 2016-10-01T15:47:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-my-ice-baths.18500/post-202159>

Prior to CT I needed to wear a sweater or jacket all the time unless over 75 deg F.

Now I love 65 deg F weather and can tolerate colder.

CT built up BAT and I run much warmer, and it didn't seem to take that long to build up. Maybe within 1-2 months of CT.

What is interesting even if I stop CT over the summer I have maintained by BAT tissue quite well

### DLO — 2016-10-05T07:32:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-my-ice-baths.18500/post-202308>

How long do you guys shiver for after? Sometimes I shiver so bad i can't type or write or do much of anything.... Except exercise to warm myself up but that kinda defeats the purpose doesn't it?

### Valerie — 2016-10-05T12:36:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-my-ice-baths.18500/post-202311>

For the first 5 month I would shiver about half hour, maybe 45 minutes. But after that I no longer shivered.

### NaturalHealthForLife — 2016-10-08T17:37:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-my-ice-baths.18500/post-202509>

Wow! I am impressed!

I am up to 5-7 minutes of cold water at the end of the shower maybe 3 times a week. Ice vests/belt on other days - but with too many other clothes on, I guess I just found out... I suppose I have to be mostly naked to get the best out of the ice vests.

It's good to hear you say these positive things about CT. This has been the most challenging thing for me to implement.

Emily

### Valerie — 2016-10-08T22:36:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-my-ice-baths.18500/post-202521>

Cold showers are harder. Although two weeks facing dunking is recommended, I just jumped into an ice bath. I hyperventilated for about 1.5 minutes and then was fine. What I told my DH to do was to get into room temperature bath water (20 deg C) and add the ice. This worked well for him and he was able to adapted fairly easily without hyperventilating.

You get far more benefit from sitting in the ice water. Being outside in the sun is even better.

# Thread: L1 injury
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/l1-injury.18522/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Linz — 2016-10-04T16:37:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/l1-injury.18522/post-202269>

Three weeks ago I fell backwards and caused 10% compression fracture of L1 and severe bruising to my right hip.
Medical response was 6 hours on my back looking up at LED and fluorescent tubes (asked for them to be switched off most of the last two hours), codeine, no ice packs available except the one I was able to hold onto from home, and not a single glass of water. Discharged with more codeine and follow up 4 weeks later.
Before going to hospital I had used a cold hose, ice packs and IR/red light pad on the injury. First week regular cold showers (bath once steady enough), ice packs, sunrise outside, red light, UVA light, outside in autumn mini heatwave for long hours, fish and fish broths. A partial cranio-sacral treatment, to head only, after a week made an enormous difference towards restoring comfortable pelvic movement and better sleep, Painful nerve spasms in right leg after two weeks meant a return to ER for another xray but no further damage.

This morning, still with nerve twinges through the night I did brief ct, red light, outside in sunlight and attended a spine clinic (managed to bring forward a week!). What pain medication was I taking? Two codeine in ER. One diazepam for muscle relax on second visit (big mistake!). Leptin sensitivity/ct/red lights/uv = no acute pain!!

Interesting examination when I could lean well back, drop hands right forwards down legs, no pain at all with pressure over injury and full rotation/ lifting of legs (not sure where the nerve spasms went. I felt rather a fraud). A huge appreciation Dr K. Not least for the confidence to take responsibility for myself.

### Lahelada — 2016-10-07T02:02:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/l1-injury.18522/post-202427>

Wow ,Linz ,what a story. So sorry it happened but exemplary how you turned the story around.

### caroline — 2016-10-07T09:05:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/l1-injury.18522/post-202440>

Hi Linz ..... Thank goodness for all you have taken on board here - and then you could apply it all to yourself.

well done! Hopefully your fast recovery has impacted on the medical people who saw you.

How are you progressing?

### Linz — 2016-10-07T18:33:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/l1-injury.18522/post-202462>

Thanks Lah, Caroline,

Making good progress (but perhaps not as good as the young gymnast!). I think one of the young medics will be thinking hard about Leptin and pain and I'll be armed with a blog or two when I go back.

### Linz — 2016-10-08T11:35:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/l1-injury.18522/post-202486>

Sleeping well. Very dark and we have rewired recently with armoured cable and breaker switches. Really makes everything feel very still at night.

# Thread: Nicotine + Alzheimers/Dementia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-alzheimers-dementia.18531/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DLO — 2016-10-05T07:26:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-alzheimers-dementia.18531/post-202306>

[http://www.naturalnews.com/055531\_nicotine\_brain\_health\_dementia.html](http://www.naturalnews.com/055531_nicotine_brain_health_dementia.html)
**Texas A&M: Nicotine can protect aging brains against Parkinson's, Alzheimer's disease**
Wednesday, October 05, 2016 by: Ethan A. Huff, staff writer
_Tags: [nicotine](http://www.naturalnews.com/nicotine.html), [brain health](http://www.naturalnews.com/brain_health.html), [dementia](http://www.naturalnews.com/dementia.html)_

(NaturalNews) [Cigarette angst](http://cigarettes.fetch.news/) and all the associated media hysteria over the health effects of smoking have deterred millions of people from using tobacco in any way, shape or form. But new research out of Texas A&M University in College Station has revealed something about the tobacco plant that many people don't know: When used properly, tobacco can actually help _promote_ health rather than destroy it.

It's true: Tobacco contains a compound known as nicotine that, aside from its use in addiction patches, has been shown to help protect the brain against the age-related onset of degenerative conditions like [Parkinson's disease and Alzheimer's](http://www.dementia.news/). So, while smoking tobacco may not be the best approach for maintaining vibrant health, consuming its constituents in extract form just might have a place in the supplemental regimens of health enthusiasts seeking optimal cognitive function.

Dr. Ursula Winzer-Serhan, Ph.D., an associate professor at the Texas A&M College of Medicine, collaborated with some of her colleagues to come to this conclusion, as shocking as it might be. They tested the effects of nicotine on animals, and found that heavy consumption of the compound causes changes in the brain that, believe it or not, are overtly positive.

At low and medium doses, nicotine was found to have minimal effects on the animals' brains, and showed almost no substantial presence in their blood. But at high doses, [nicotine](http://www.naturalnews.com/nicotine.html) was found to not only curb the animals' appetites, effectively minimizing excess weight gain, but it also triggered an increased number of receptors in the brain associated with anti-aging.

Though the precise mechanism of this action has yet to be uncovered, the team believes that nicotine somehow acts in such a way as to minimize the degenerative factors that lead to early-onset memory loss and the eventual progression of more serious conditions like [dementia](http://www.naturalnews.com/dementia.html). And the best part is that it does this without producing any noticeable side effects.

"Some people say that nicotine decreases anxiety, which is why people smoke, but others say it increases anxiety," Winzer-Serhan explained in a statement. "The last thing you would want in a drug that is given chronically would be a negative change in behavior. Luckily, we didn't find any evidence of anxiety: Only two measures showed any effect even with high levels of nicotine, and if anything, nicotine made animal models less anxious."

**Does natural tobacco leaf have any health benefits? Science suggests yes**
Winzer-Serhan and her team were quick to note that people shouldn't [go out and grab cartons of cigarettes and start lighting up](http://www.stopsmoking.news/) simply due to these findings. Isolated nicotine is obviously very different to dried tobacco leaves wrapped in paper – especially when those leaves are intentionally [spiked with hundreds or even thousands of other added chemicals](http://science.naturalnews.com/chemicals_in_cigarettes.html), as is the case with most commercial cigarettes.

Still, nicotine extract and potentially even natural tobacco _could_ have some health benefits that get overlooked or ignored because the science behind them doesn't align with the popular, and mostly negative, narrative surrounding tobacco. Tobacco shouldn't be completely written off, in other words, as it's not entirely evil – [and the science shows this](http://science.naturalnews.com/).

"I want to make it very clear that we're not encouraging people to smoke," Winzer-Serhan warns, noting that further research is needed on tobacco and nicotine in order to gain a better understanding of all the unknowns surrounding its effects on the body.

"Even if these weren't very preliminary results, smoking results in so many health problems that any possible benefit of the nicotine would be more than cancelled out. However, smoking is only one possible route of administration ... and our work shows that we shouldn't write-off nicotine completely."

**Sources for this article include:**
[MedicalXpress.com](http://medicalxpress.com/news/2016-09-nicotine-aging-brain.html)

[DiscoverMagazine.com](http://discovermagazine.com/2014/march/13-nicotine-fix)

[Science.NaturalNews.com](http://science.naturalnews.com/chemicals_in_cigarettes.html)

# Thread: Red film over lights?

### NaturalHealthForLife — 2016-10-06T05:40:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-film-over-lights.18540/post-202366>

My house has LED light panels under the kitchen cabinets for countertop lighting.

I was wondering if anyone had tried putting a red film over this type of lighting strips to make them red.

Would this be effective in blocking blue light do you think?

I am thinking of some type of cellophane, maybe like what the crew guys would put over a spotlight in play. I looked for red LED lightbulbs to install in the fixtures, but did not see them on Google, Amazon or a few specialty lightbulbs stores.

### Sue-UK — 2016-10-06T13:05:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-film-over-lights.18540/post-202382>

I've got a gel filter, two names I know of are Rosco [https://www.rosco.com/filters/roscolux.cfm#colors](https://www.rosco.com/filters/roscolux.cfm#colors) and Lee Filters. [http://www.leefilters.com/lighting/colour-list.html](http://www.leefilters.com/lighting/colour-list.html). They show the spectral energy distribution, I picked which one I wanted and got a sheet off Ebay. I've currently got it on a 2 computer screens, 3 leds on a floor standing vertical light DH wants on in the evening, and covered a pair of clear safety glasses for if the tv is on.

### NaturalHealthForLife — 2016-10-06T21:55:36+0300

I've got a lee filters swatch so picked a couple of very deep Amber ones that I can function with, and then bought the gel with most blue blocking. Sleep is better ...

> Does this mean you avoid artificial lighting... or how do you use the atmosphere to filter light?

I rearranged the majority of my life around solar redox. Other than when I shop for groceries, I'm outdoor all day long.

At any given time I'm either:

-   Sun baking (whilst writing software)
-   Cold tubbing (in the sun)
-   Working on a construction site (usually with some clothing on for that one)
-   Seated under a peach tree if I need a break from the direct sun

Things I still need to change:

-   Set myself up to cook breakfast outside (that's a big one)
-   Make some more basic improvements outdoors to make it brainlessly convenient to be outside

> It's kinda fun to watch the system slowdown dramatically in the shade when I feel just fine in the sun.

This made me chuckle! This is what I do also. There's a spot by my old crappy tall table that I can shove most of the laptop in the shade of the closed umbrella and I'm still in the sun. It's as good as it gets I guess. I considered putting ice packs underneath a tray beneath it the other day.

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=277678)
>
> I love the sound of your setup and how you are raising your best and ultimate investments in the world's future.

Thanks, that's such nice encouragement! It's far from pretty and certainly not perfect, but it's still good.

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=277678)
>
> I love to hear that dads are cooking. Traditionally guys are raised up to embrace the role of provider. More often than not providing means going somewhere else and doing something away from family. Cooking is such a fun thing for me as a guy because I get to provide directly and be there in that moment.

D was a bachelor before we started dating and living on his own. I very much appreciate his developed skills in all things. It's so nice to be able to share tasks. I have plenty of my own power tools and it's not threatening to D if I take up projects myself. Sometimes we fall into traditional roles. It's always changing.

I'm currently torn between wanting my kids outside 24/7 and having them come and help with cooking. In fact, I'm heading in to make dinner, while 2 of the 3 keep swimming!

# Thread: Pop Corn Muscles?

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HopingMom — 2016-10-07T03:45:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pop-corn-muscles.18547/post-202429>

I've noticed that after exercising (just briskly walking-not strenuous) that all of my muscles all over my body will be twitching. It is short fast twitching like feeling (not visible, just feel it) - the best I can describe is it is kind of like popping corn? It is mainly in my legs/thighs and will last for a good 10 minutes.
My legs/thighs are my fat-est parts now so I feel like that may have some play.
Anyone have any thoughts on what this is or what is going on?
Thanks much!

### DLO — 2016-10-07T16:41:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pop-corn-muscles.18547/post-202454>

Mild muscle spasm. Try some hot and cold contrast 3 mins warm 1 min cold 10 deg difference( the bigger the gap u can stand the better) in the shower. Idea is to get circulation flushing in there.

### HopingMom — 2016-10-09T03:41:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pop-corn-muscles.18547/post-202530>

Thanks much DLO! Yes, circulation is poor. Do you have any idea what is causing this or the mechanism for what is going on?

### DLO — 2016-10-09T06:32:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pop-corn-muscles.18547/post-202536>

How many gallons of water are you drinking? What kinds of magnesium are you taking and what types at what time?

**The CDC medical police state: the right to detain anyone**

Americans traveling between states can be stopped, detained, and even quarantined, if they seem to have indications of an illness that could impact public health.
If this sounds vague, it is. The CDC has even coined a new absurd category: “precommunicable” illness. It means that with few or no symptoms—but merely the probability of having contacted someone who had or could have had an illness—a traveler in America can be picked up and held against his will.
The CDC asserts it has police powers to do this.

# Thread: Winter light schedule
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-light-schedule.18556/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NaturalHealthForLife — 2016-10-08T08:09:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-light-schedule.18556/post-202482>

I have been reading a lot about optimizing indoor and outdoor lights. Definitely a lot to learn!

My question is about how to optimize light exposure in a low light area: specifically (Seattle) the cloudy and rainy Pacific Northwest of the United States during the winter.

Are usually wake up at seven and go outdoors for grounding and sunlight. I have noticed that it is no longer a light when I wake up. I am wondering if I should postpone my sunlight exposure until sunrise or if I should be working with some sort of full spectrum indoor light as soon as I wake up.

Wake up:
My current plan is to continue to wake up at seven and eat my BAB breakfast first in relative darkness. At 7:30, 8 AM, or whenever the sun rises, go outside for half an hour of grounded send gazing and walking.

I homeschool my kids so we are very flexible on optimizing, if I can just figure out what to do.

How does sunrise and eating (I am working through Leptin RX) tie in to wake up time.

Perhaps the ideal is to ignore the clock and stay in bed til sun up?

Day time:

Goal to be outside doing school work at a picnic table barefoot all morning for grounding, cold, and sun exposure.

Inside it is very dim while overcast, so it sounds like I should get red light, black light, and full spectrum light indoors.

Sauna in afternoon with full spectrum IR and red lights on.

Evening: it will start to get dark around 4-5 pm my goal is to be done with dinner before it is dark and then go for a sunset walk. After we return we are wearing our orange uvex glasses and keeping the lights dim in the house. Kids go to sleep at 8 and adults at 10 pm.

Does this seem like a good schedule or should I tweak something?

# Thread: Multiple Sessions a day work great
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/multiple-sessions-a-day-work-great.18559/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sfast — 2016-10-08T14:32:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/multiple-sessions-a-day-work-great.18559/post-202490>

Hi Freezers,

I try a couple of protocols that divide a bigger session into some smaller sessions. Me and my clients make great result. Instead of one big session, try:

\- Get 20-30min light movement in the cold air.
\- Get two shorter sessions of ice bath (depending on your level) per day in.
\- Reduce gradually the amount of clothes, bedding and temperature in your home environment.

I will try to add a once or twice weekly bigger session (right before the point of intensive shivering) for further experimentation.

Overall, this approach looks promising especially for people with a lower tolerance of the associated stress response.

Best of Luck
Sascha

# Thread: postpartum depression, low dopamine?, fibromyalgia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/postpartum-depression-low-dopamine-fibromyalgia.18582/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dar9903 — 2016-10-13T00:10:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/postpartum-depression-low-dopamine-fibromyalgia.18582/post-202704>

Hi -
I am pretty new here and have to admit that my brain fog makes it hard for me to follow some of what is discussed on the blog and the forums (my problem, not an issue of the content!) I'm looking for help on where to start.

Background on me: I am just crawling out of a long bout with post-partum depression (I'm 21 months PP). I concurrently have had digestive slow-down and what seems like a total tanking of my sex hormones--no sex drive, no period (just stopped breastfeeding to try to see if I'll restart). I have achy joints and muscle fatigue/wasting (labeled fibromyalgia/CFS by rheum).

I have what seems like a way overactive sympathetic nervous system--easily startled, constipation when stressed, etc. I have low basal body temp, low BP, and I also have Raynaud's, so the idea of cold thermogenesis is totally scary to me! (Although I want to try it...) I have high iGG titers for a bunch of infections (parvo, EBV, mycoplasma) and had an active mycoplasma infection till recently.

I have restless sleep. I've had a couple hyperthyroid flares in life, but not since daughter was born. If anything, my thyroid is sluggish -- conversion of T4 to T3 is on the low side. I basically feel much older than my 35 year old self.

Oh, I should also mention that I've always been super fair-skinned and my family inculcated in me a real fear of sun exposure, which I'm working to get over because I know my vit D is low. I'm not sure I'm great at absorbing it from the sun, though--what's going on with me seems pretty consistent with low a-MSH. My job requires me to sit behind a computer -- but I did reduce my hours to 15 a week after I (my adrenals?) tanked out postpartum. I have also spent a ton of time on the internet searching for answers to my issues (tried parasite cleanses, candida diet, autoimmune paleo, etcetcetc), so I have probably been exposing myself way too much to EMF.

I know that my hormones are out of whack. I just don't know where to start. I saw on one of the threads that the leptin reset is not for people with BMI of 25 or lower, that they should proceed directly to the post-leptin reset time. Is that true? Because I have an average BMI (21) but I crave food from 2 pm to 6 pm basically. I ate strict paleo for a while and then realized it was making me shaky and sad. So I've added some grains back in.

Sorry for the long post. I guess I'm just hoping someone who's been in a similar spot can break down for me where to begin with all this.

Thanks for reading!
Dar

# Thread: Temporary moving to tropics...escaping the European winter - what to change, what to consider?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/temporary-moving-to-tropics-escaping-the-european-winter-what-to-change-what-to-consider.18617/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Pebbles — 2016-10-19T14:39:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/temporary-moving-to-tropics-escaping-the-european-winter-what-to-change-what-to-consider.18617/post-203043>

Background: Floxxed 2014 Oct and 2015 March.
During my March-April Sri Lanka vacation - almost 12 hours a day in sun - I had 50% improvement of my hearing damage caused by FQT.
About May-June I had the heavy detox reactions and my sweating returned. During summer spent minimum 6 hours a day in the sun, often 8-10 hours ( naked grounding in my garden - village w 350pers/sqkm, low nnEMF) but no further improvement happened. Even 3 weeks Mediterranean vacation with all day sun+sea was enough only to counteract the nnEMF damage caused by the mobile power tower close to our villa.

Actual status: LBBB, continuous low volume high pitch sizzling still present in my ear, Myoclonus ( Tympani Stapedius, Eustach, Rhino, Palate) getting more rare - even missing for days (Hurray!), EHS is strong ( causing pressure in my head and ears, increasing HBP, lymphedema, dehydration, etc), and still did not start to loose weight (+20kg right after FQT, -7kg down only during 2 weeks water fasting) - latest Somato/thermograph shows still high flairs along ears-nose-sinuses (cipro burned), slight flairs along all tendons and ligaments, and hiatus hernia.

In a few days moving to Sri Lanka, to Kalpitiya and will stay until April-May next year- very low density population, will stay at the beach, at an eco lodge and focusing on my hobbies - swimming with whales.
My sun and grounding satiety will be fulfilled on a daily basis.

As a haplotype- J1B1 and 1/2 mediterranean genes and 1/3 Eastern Eur genes, in perimeno ( thrown due to FQT, at 51yo don't think will be able to turn it back) it will be OK to continue the daily sea-beasts + local seasonal fruits and veggies? Eating morning only will be OK?

As for the flight, beyond full Jet lag RX, should I wear silver anti wave underwear? I know the pros and cons mentioned by DrK in several blogs, webinars and Q&A's, however the trip is 2x 6,5 hours flight. But in 6 hours I am reaching 200/100 HBP due to nnEMF.
Unfortunately Quantlet not arrived yet, I have only Vielight and Valkee available to carry with.

Anything else to take care, take with, etc?

Thanks for inputs!

### kovita — 2016-10-19T15:03:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/temporary-moving-to-tropics-escaping-the-european-winter-what-to-change-what-to-consider.18617/post-203044>

you might see some turn in your menopause, Eniko. Light seems to have capacity to maintain you out of it despite of the first signs. I think you should just do some spiritual exercises before your flight, concentrate on what do you want to achieve during the flight. I am convinced you can manipulate your body with great success in any way if you are connected enough. There are many methods, all of them leading you to the same result.

# Thread: UV-A and far-infrared wavelengths have an energizing or preservative effect On food
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-a-and-far-infrared-wavelengths-have-an-energizing-or-preservative-effect-on-food.18620/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kyrakitty — 2016-10-20T00:53:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-a-and-far-infrared-wavelengths-have-an-energizing-or-preservative-effect-on-food.18620/post-203069>

****The 7 Month Long Cherry Tomato Storage Test:**
**{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

****
A microbiological experiment with cherry tomatoes was done to confirm, more simply, that violet glass better protects the bio-energy of food. As had become clear in the previous study where the herbal samples had been exposed to the sun, the most significant differences were detected when the products were stored in clear glass versus violet glass. Therefore, Dr. Hugo Niggli bought cherry tomatoes and bottled them individually in a clear-glass and violet glass apothecary jar on the 22nd of June 2007. The tomatoes were then stored at room temperature in an apartment.

On the 22nd of December, exactly half a year later, a microbiological change judged as mold was observed in the cherry tomato stored in the clear glass bottle. A month later, after 7 months, on January 21st 2008, the result of this biological experiment was photographed. For this purpose, the cherry tomatoes were taken out of the bottles and the microbiological changes of the tomatoes stored in both bottles were photographed. The cherry tomato stored in violet glass retained its red color and had not dried out, contrary to the tomato stored in clear glass.

[http://www.vitalitygoods.com/science-miron-vitality-glass](http://www.vitalitygoods.com/science-miron-vitality-glass)
**
WHY MIRON JARS?**

_From Dr. Cowan:_ I heard about Miron (or purple jars) years ago but never tried them, mostly because they are very expensive. More recently, I revisited the issue as a way to properly store many of the common foods I use (oils, water, crispy nuts) and found that there are some interesting things about these jars. Normal sunlight consists of three "types" of light, which scientists call UV-A, UV-B and UV-C. UV-B and UV-C stimulate plants to grow, but they also cause decomposition of the plants after they die. UV-A does not provoke the same decomposition, or, at least, it affects decomposition to a much lesser extent. Early research also suggests that UV-A and far-infrared wavelengths have an energizing or preservative effect on whatever it encounters, although this is a very complicated subject. The color and thickness of the Miron jars are intended to screen out as much of the UV-B and UV-C wavelengths as possible, while admitting as much of the UV-A and far-infrared wavelengths as possible. These properties allow for prolonged storage and, possibly, an "energetic" enhancement of the contents of the jar.

[https://www.power-plugs-sockets.com/en-us/netherlands-holland/](https://www.power-plugs-sockets.com/en-us/netherlands-holland/)

From what I understand the Magnetico pad will have a socket type A (male, not grounded) or more likely B (male, grounded) and the Dutch wall socket is C (female, not grounded) and F (female, grounded).

USA
Voltage (120 V)
Frequency (60 Hz)

Dutch
Voltage (230 V)
Frequency (50 Hz)

**Question:**
Are there any negative (health) effects from frequency converting like this?

_I would think converting energie results in some "loss" of energie via heat and other radiation? And having an adapter near the bed might also be harmful to the goal of sleeping well/better?_

### Alain Dwight — 2024-12-22T03:11:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-posts-pdf-in-one-zip-file-for-download.18666/post-344239>

I ran a script to combine these all into a single PDF so I can drop it in chatGPT and get a budget Kruse LLM

Obviously not a replacement for reading the blogs, but can help with bouncing ideas and searching (if you ask for direct quotes). It seems to push the edge of the context window so I've gotten a bit better responses when directing it to a specific series eg. "Review Time series and provide one sentence thesis as well as supporting evidence."

### [kruse\_all\_free\_blogs.pdf](https://drive.google.com/file/d/14aHlyThxAyQpZerNN5XM-fL2JJLzd2xS/view?usp=sharing)

 drive.google.com

### Alain Dwight — 2024-12-23T02:01:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-posts-pdf-in-one-zip-file-for-download.18666/post-344266>

> [Michalis said:](/goto/post?id=344262)
>
> Are you going to make it available online? Would be fun but all these LLMs are hallucinating a lot. Every thing needs to be double checked. It would be enormously helpful to feed them them papers too..

There are definitely serious limitations. To be clear, by "budget LLM", I mean just chatGPT with this PDF uploaded into it. With ~300 separate PDFs you can't get them all in the conversation, but as a single PDF anyone with chatGPT can upload the file and ask questions about the contents of the PDF

I might share a conversatio/starter later if I get a really good one rolling, would be cool to hear from anyone else too if they get good results

# Thread: Hemochromatosis - HPA Axis Dysfunction
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hemochromatosis-hpa-axis-dysfunction.18670/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NateToup — 2016-10-27T22:34:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hemochromatosis-hpa-axis-dysfunction.18670/post-203376>

Jack, how important do you think blood donation is? I've heard Mercola on a podcast with Greenfield claiming it is highly recommended for males.

For awhile now I have been neurotically questioning where has my energy gone, what am I missing? And it just might be a possibility of me missing what I have too much of. I've been through the ringer of hypothyroid symptoms including hypogonadism. And it could all be due to hemochromatosis, depending on what my iron levels are. You could name this Triple H.

I'm looking to DIY a home "bloodletting". Seems easy and practical, don't know where I can donate blood. If symptoms don't give up, then I'll most likely go to a useless urologist or endocrinologist to get some tests done. Any recommendations for what I should get tested? Maybe I don't need to get any tests done, and see a pelvic floor specialist, or see a sleep doctor to see if I have sleep apnea. I'd like to think hemochromatosis is my answer or maybe it's just a lower protein diet (unemployed and living at home).

Overall, I have had some good days and bad days, but no support to finding some real answers.

### prime1 — 2016-10-28T05:26:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hemochromatosis-hpa-axis-dysfunction.18670/post-203393>

.
I found an amazing Neurosurgeon this week who talked about how Hemochromatosis puts you into a chronic hypoxic state. I have 2 units of blood drawn every 3 to 4 months.

George Washington died from blood letting - whoops....

### JanSz — 2016-10-28T17:10:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hemochromatosis-hpa-axis-dysfunction.18670/post-203401>

Get
Complete Blood Count (CBC)
look up
hemoglobnin(12.6 - 17.7)g/dL

if your hemoglobin is above 17.7 you should donate blood
if you are on the lover side, careful with donations.

Some people must get rid of some blood but are not allowed to donate.
Ask doctor for script for therapeutic phlebothomy.

Someone who let the blood out should monitor ferritin.

### Echosyn — 2016-11-11T05:57:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/led-light-bulbs-vs-led-diodes.18672/post-203815>

Jack told me that LED was a bad choice. ### JmanGigHarbor — 2019-07-16T03:48:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/led-light-bulbs-vs-led-diodes.18672/post-274367>

I have copied over a post with useful info on led's that I have researched. This is a way to use LEDs with DC power.

I have been researching this lately and would like to make it simpler for some new people.
So the overall problem with "LED's" is that when they run on "AC" current, it causes them to flicker from the way AC current runs through a normal US house. This AC current will pulse as it is used. In order to get around this issue, you can use LED's that run directly from "DC" current. Because this is a direct line of current and does not pulse like AC current does. This can be easily tested using your smart phone on video record, setting it to "slow motion" mode you are able to see the LED light pulse on AC current. Now, if you start looking around there are actually AC to DC wall adapters for running off a standard US home power supply. What I have found is on amazon you can find a UV 12v Led flood light, that is 10w. this can then be ran off an AC-DC power supply. And by doing this it has a perfect, constant light output with no flickering.
There are also easier to test devices that are 5v DC UV light bars, that come with there own wall adapter. I have also found a red light LED that uses this same 5v DC adapter method. I will provide links below to all the devices, I hope this helps someone out

UV Flood Light (395-400nm)
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B012CUL180/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o02\_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B012CUL180/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o02_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

AC to DC power supply
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B073QTNF9F/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o04\_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B073QTNF9F/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o04_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

Low power 5v UV Light Bar (395-400nm)
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B071CQF7V5/ref=oh\_aui\_search\_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B071CQF7V5/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

5v 20w Red Light Bar (650nm)
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B07D16NQMV/ref=oh\_aui\_search\_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B07D16NQMV/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

Standard Light for lighting room (2700k) "These are powered off the same AC to DC power supply as above"
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B077Q2G7NZ/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o01\_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B077Q2G7NZ/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o01_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

# Thread: Vagus Nerve Stimulation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vagus-nerve-stimulation.18673/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### prime1 — 2016-10-28T05:37:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vagus-nerve-stimulation.18673/post-203394>

.
Ran into an amazing Neurosurgeon this week - electrical stim on my tongue to activate the vagus nerve. What an amazing exeperience and a huge help with improving my health. I'm surprised it's not used more widespread to help patients out.

Forbes Magazine

Is Your Tongue The Key To A Neuroscience Breakthrough?

Nov. 2015

The team at [Helius Medical Technologies](http://www.heliusmedical.com/) believe combining physical therapy with stimulation of the tongue may improve impairment of brain function and associated symptoms of injury. “We have already seen that stimulation of various nerves can improve symptoms of a range of neurological diseases. However, we believe the tongue is a much more elegant and direct pathway for stimulating brain structures and inducing neuroplasticity. We are focused on investigating the tongue as a gateway to the brain to hopefully ease the disease of brain injury,” said Dr. Jonathan Sackier, CMO at Helius.

### seanb4 — 2016-10-28T15:09:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vagus-nerve-stimulation.18673/post-203400>

# Thread: Porfiriy Ivanov - CT guru
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/porfiriy-ivanov-ct-guru.18674/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Anton Chigur — 2016-10-28T10:52:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/porfiriy-ivanov-ct-guru.18674/post-203398>

Hey Guys,

I would like to share a nice example of what we are capable of. Actually this guy initiated Ice bucket Challenge long ago. There was a guy in Russia called Porfiriy Ivanov who was born around 120 years ago from now. Once he got sick with terminal cancer, he decided to commit a suicide by freeze himself, so he began pouring called water on himself in minus 30 Celsium and recovered quickly. He realized that all problems are from disengagement from nature and he gave all clothes for 50 years, he left only underwear. Once in winter he made a walk in his only underwear from Saratov to Moscow (around 1300km) and returned back. Now he is considered a God of seals (ice smimmers) in Russia. His method is simple - every morning go outside and pour a bucket of cold water on yourself
A few photos for you to realize how literally cool this guy is:

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sujoe123 — 2016-10-28T20:35:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.18677/post-203408>

I live in far south Texas in a rocky Bay. It is an unsafe area to walk. Will I ground adequately sitting on a dock with my feet in the salt water? I would say yes! Comments??

### Sujoe123 — 2016-10-28T21:34:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.18677/post-203411>

> [Sujoe123 said:](/goto/post?id=203408)
>
> I live in far south Texas in a rocky Bay. It is an unsafe area to walk. Will I ground adequately sitting on a dock with my feet in the salt water? I would say yes! Comments??

Also another question. Why does my avatar appear twice ...a larger and then a superimposed smaller image and what about the eyeglasses on my post!

### fitness@home — 2016-10-28T23:44:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.18677/post-203414>

I only see one avatar. The glasses usually mean you are following a thread.

### Sujoe123 — 2016-10-29T15:06:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.18677/post-203423>

Thanks...dont know how to undo it

### Echosyn — 2016-11-11T06:08:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.18677/post-203816>

> [Sujoe123 said:](/goto/post?id=203408)
>
> I live in far south Texas in a rocky Bay. It is an unsafe area to walk. Will I ground adequately sitting on a dock with my feet in the salt water? I would say yes! Comments??

Moisture wicking footwear such as leather sole moccasins serve to protect the feet and ground you. Due to MCS I wear only natural fabrics like cotton and wool which ground well when slightly moist. A twin size cotton bed sheet serves well as a grounding ground cloth when you want to lay on a grassy area or sandy beach. If things are too dry just dampen the sheet a bit. Polyester, nylon and rayon are non-conductive.

### Sujoe123 — 2016-11-11T21:27:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.18677/post-203848>

Thank you for your reply Echosyn, but I am referring to a blog or podcast where Dr. Jack suggested standing in the ocean and getting Sun at the same time. There is no beach in this area just a tiny rocky harbor. I live in a condo that has a pier and I can sit in the Sun and dangle my feet in the salt water without having to traverse the rocks to get in the ocean. My main point was: am I grounded just dangling my feet in the saltwater? I would guess, yes I am grounded.

### Echosyn — 2016-11-12T00:55:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.18677/post-203851>

> [Sujoe123 said:](/goto/post?id=203848)
>
> Thank you for your reply Echosyn, but I am referring to a blog or podcast where Dr. Jack suggested standing in the ocean and getting Sun at the same time. There is no beach in this area just a tiny rocky harbor. I live in a condo that has a pier and I can sit in the Sun and dangle my feet in the salt water without having to traverse the rocks to get in the ocean. My main point was: am I grounded just dangling my feet in the saltwater? I would guess, yes I am grounded.

"I see." said the blind man. Yes indeed we are like mobile trees that are rooted in the earth and our bodies bathed in water, sun and air. Remove any of them and the tree dies. There is a low rock promontory beneath aTrinity Willow where I would often sit with my feet in the river water, notebook in hand and the evening sun at my back. Minnows would nibble at my toes. Can it get any better?

But, looking at it through my spectrograph (not sure of exact name- handheld prism device that allows me to look in and see various frequencies), I see a decent amount of **blue frequencies**.

# Thread: Where to go if I leave home as a mimor?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-go-if-i-leave-home-as-a-mimor.18690/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty_M — 2016-11-03T03:56:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-go-if-i-leave-home-as-a-mimor.18690/post-203542>

Probably shouldn't post this on the internet... but

I'm 17 for 9 months, almost an entire year. I'm in my senior year in high school. I set my schedule with all AM classes free until 10:30am so I spend all this time outdoors.

As winter comes it will be more difficult to get anything done outdoors but I'll bundle up if I must. I can handle a lot of cold but at a certain point I cannot.

I'm 12 miles Northeast of Philadelphia so my environment sucks... 87 cell towers within 4 miles. I want to get the hell out of here, drop out of high school, so I can focus on my studies and actually helping our species- not dealing with all of the constricting, distracting, illusion of importance of shit like homework, grades, college applications (probably not going but Dad wanted me to apply...), and all the other stuff that steals my ability.

The upside is that I started The Optimal Club at my high school and some 20-30 kids are pretty interested... because everyone is really tired and feels like shit all the time (school gives each student a macbook air to take home- FLUX not allowed though I'm pushing for it).

I want to get the hell out of here, but I'll need food and and a place to stay and these are difficult without an income. Of course, I'm trying to figure out how to "get rich quick" (ie make a lot of money somehow without losing so much time nor health).

**Any ideas, suggestions? Where could I go live for minimal money? How could I maybe convince my dad to support me? If I were to "run away" where would I go?**

One side of me wants to stick it out- it's just 8 months till I graduate. The other thinks that's way too long to be in this shitty an environment. I can biohack it a lot but only so much when I spend 3 hours a day in a Wi-Fi shithole and the rest in a cell and smart meter bath (PA suburbs).

### caroline — 2016-11-03T07:54:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-go-if-i-leave-home-as-a-mimor.18690/post-203550>

Please do stick it out ....look at it as an investment in your future. Spend as much time as possible reading Dr. K.'s blogs and learning.

Family is important ........ don't throw the baby out with the bathwater if you can help it!

The group you formed at school is a great tool for you ....you are all learning and bouncing ideas off of each other.

Don't underestimate networking and quantum entanglement - it is very important to achieving what you want in life.

Dr. K. has been on this optimal path for about 12 years now ....slowing building a tribe and asking them to pay it forward....

That is what you can do as well.

Everyday make connections, everyday smile at people and show you care.

Dr. K. always tells us .... people don't care what you know - until they know that you care.

Please always remember that.

Care about others, especially your family ..... and build your knowledge, and build your tribe.

BE PATIENT! - you can't force feed people but you can arm yourself with knowledge and kindness ...and a smile speaks volumes.

Good Luck!

### caroline — 2016-11-03T08:00:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-go-if-i-leave-home-as-a-mimor.18690/post-203551>

BTW ....I spent almost a week with Dr. K. in Orlando a few years ago.

Would you be surprised to learn that JK is actually kind of quiet \[sometimes -lol\] and a phenomenal listener?

well, yes he is .... but when some one is interested and has questions for him he will repeat things endlessly and with kindness and patience - like he does for us.

He always tells us - when the student arrives - the teacher in him appears.

### Matty_M — 2016-11-04T00:49:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-go-if-i-leave-home-as-a-mimor.18690/post-203586>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=203544)
>
> don't do that.

Biohack it out? It's just 8 months...

### Matty_M — 2016-11-04T00:50:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-go-if-i-leave-home-as-a-mimor.18690/post-203587>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=203551)
>
> BTW ....I spent almost a week with Dr. K. in Orlando a few years ago.
>
> Would you be surprised to learn that JK is actually kind of quiet \[sometimes -lol\] and a phenomenal listener?
>
> well, yes he is .... but when some one is interested and has questions for him he will repeat things endlessly and with kindness and patience - like he does for us.
>
> He always tells us - when the student arrives - the teacher in him appears.

Thank you. I'll stay here, biohack it out, keep learning, connecting with people, and keeping doors open for my future. Really appreciate your advice.

\- Matt

### JanSz — 2016-11-04T15:52:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-go-if-i-leave-home-as-a-mimor.18690/post-203598>

**Bill Gates, world's richest college dropout, says stay in school**.
[http://money.cnn.com/2015/06/03/pf/college/bill-gates-college-dropout/index.html](http://money.cnn.com/2015/06/03/pf/college/bill-gates-college-dropout/index.html)
\-------------------------
You are one smart kid.
Why Bill Gates dropped out of college?
[https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Bill-Gates-drop-out-of-college](https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Bill-Gates-drop-out-of-college)

I would say, listen to your dad, go to college, study diligently,
when you get real good idea about something (that you think is worthwhile of your life commitment), go for it, college or not.

So, until you get idea that you are passionate about, stay in college.

### Huck — 2016-11-04T16:13:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-go-if-i-leave-home-as-a-mimor.18690/post-203599>

### Lahelada — 2016-11-04T17:03:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-go-if-i-leave-home-as-a-mimor.18690/post-203601>

Don't go. Select your career wisely. Nine months seems incredibly long but it is not. Mitigate as much as you can. Ask your parents for a birthday present ,a trip to the Yucatan. Ask for an early birthday present and head for the meet up in December if finances allow.
You are young but you have a great brain. You can make this work.

# Thread: nnEMF meter
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthy2015 — 2016-11-03T13:13:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203556>

Can anyone tell me what the below could possibly be
We have a TriField Meter to check our house and surrounding areas and a constant reading 2 (0-100 milligauss) when putting it on the Radio/Microwave setting. Could this be from a cell tower or what could it be from and is this a high reading?
Thanks

### healthy2015 — 2016-11-03T19:36:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203574>

Any ideas please?
Thank you

### Penny — 2016-11-03T22:20:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203579>

> [healthy2015 said:](/goto/post?id=203574)
>
> Any ideas please?
> Thank you

Not sure about the radio setting, but 2 or under is pretty good - at least when checking dirty electricity on a wall...

### healthy2015 — 2016-11-05T15:03:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203631>

> [Penny said:](/goto/post?id=203579)
>
> Not sure about the radio setting, but 2 or under is pretty good - at least when checking dirty electricity on a wall...

Thank you once again penny, you are so helpful and I am always grateful of your replies!!

The 2 reading is everywhere in the house, garden and down the street which I assume is very bad??!

### Penny — 2016-11-06T00:42:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203649>

I don't think it's that bad - if you were super hyper electrically sensitive, you would want to get it under 1 - like a Michael Neuert type of person - who is incidentally being interviewed by Lloyd Burell here:
[http://www.electricsense.com/11948/emf-shielding-protection/](http://www.electricsense.com/11948/emf-shielding-protection/)

google: Michael neuert and you get a lot of hits - he is quite an expert on EMF and how to use these meters -

### Scompy — 2016-11-06T02:45:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203652>

The Trifield is not sensitive for RF... it really has to hit ultra-high radio frequency to register. Cornets and other meters that are more sensitive help to pick up more in the 1 to 100 milliWatt per Square meter (or cm) and they're great for focusing sources to know the causes/locations. I.e., milliGauss is not for measuring RF.

### endless — 2016-11-06T09:04:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203655>

Use your Trifield for magnetic and electric fields.... Get another for RF.

### Inger — 2016-11-07T10:20:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203683>

I just tested my home with my TriField.. in the Radio/Microwave setting to compare. I got 0,5-0,6 in the 100 milligauss range. And I get 3 weak wifi signals from neighbors in here. So it could be better. But we have no smart meters at all.
I think 2 is not too good... there must be something in the environment..?

But you sure should get you another meter for RF as suggested above.

### Butters — 2016-11-13T00:56:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203908>

Should one start with a trifield first? I looked the RF-meters up and they are horrible expensive for a student.
How is the trifield - meter from trifield?

### Scompy — 2016-11-13T03:25:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203910>

Magnetic and electrical EMFs should not be discounted. The TriField is great for them; don't expect the RF meter to do much because it's not very sensitive. I say this because I get issues in all types of fields, and some magnetic/electrical fields in the past have been brutal to my visual spots and tinnitus. In a weird way, I'm a walking meter because I know when I'm in a strong field. Sadly, even in a weaker field, I don't know what my mitochondria think, but I'm sure they are yelling at me to stop.

### Butters — 2016-11-13T12:52:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203921>

Ok. Most things you can control easily with WiFi etc.. At least what is in your own responsibility. So I will go for the TriField.
But is low ELF important? Or is 50Hz for europe enough?

### Dean6789 — 2017-12-16T23:54:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-230442>

> [Scompy said:](/goto/post?id=203652)
>
> The Trifield is not sensitive for RF... it really has to hit ultra-high radio frequency to register. Cornets and other meters that are more sensitive help to pick up more in the 1 to 100 milliWatt per Square meter (or cm) and they're great for focusing sources to know the causes/locations. I.e., milliGauss is not for measuring RF.

Hi Scompy,

I'm now looking into a tri-meter. The two that I've come across the most so far are:

1\. The Trifield 100XE EMF Meter and
2\. The Tri-Mode High + Low Frequency Meter - Cornet ED88T

A guy that wants to sell me the Cornet says that it reads RF, magnetic1 & 2, and electric fields.

Do you know which one is best (or if some other one is more highly recommended)?

I'm trying to keep it as simple (and still effective) as possible and get an all-in-one meter.

Thanks.

### Scompy — 2017-12-17T06:27:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-230471>

Cornet 88T will probably be a better choice, since the Trifield's RF/Microwave is very poor. The Cornet's magnetic and electrical EMF detection is not as precise as the TriField, but pretty close. And the Cornet has a built-in sound mode for RF.

### Dean6789 — 2017-12-17T19:45:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-230511>

> [Scompy said:](/goto/post?id=230471)
>
> Cornet 88T will probably be a better choice, since the Trifield's RF/Microwave is very poor. The Cornet's magnetic and electrical EMF detection is not as precise as the TriField, but pretty close. And the Cornet has a built-in sound mode for RF.

Cool. Thanks a bunch!

# Thread: First Day!

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lvr413 — 2016-11-04T23:31:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-day.18696/post-203618>

Hello, I am Jessalee and today is my first day starting my leptin reset! I am open to any advise or tricks if you have them? I have struggled with my weight my entire life I also have an underactive thyroid condition and am on medication for hereditary high blood pressure. I also struggle from anxiety and am currently on medication for it.

### caroline — 2016-11-05T00:41:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-day.18696/post-203619>

Welcome Jessalee .....

There are no tricks! honestly just do your due diligence - read everything you can and apply!

Have you read the C/T protocol? start with face dunks ....just take baby steps. Rome wasn't built in a day. You didn't get sick overbite - it is going to take time to turn your titanic around.

Start a journal in the optimal journal section. It is great to keep track of what your are doing and fun to read back at a later date and see your progress.

Give Dr. K. some health history of you and your Mom and Gran...

where do you live? work?

Do you have blue blockers?

How is your sleep? libido?

nnEMF? Is your bedroom free of electronics??? do you limit computer time?

How much outdoor time do you get? You need lots and lots of sun and grounding and as much mother nature as you can manage.

This is a light story!

Have you read Dr. K.'s book? The Epi-Paeo RX ....it is the first couple of years of the blog and a lot easier to get the info.

Please read John Ott's book ... Health and Light. It is a very easy read.

Listen to all Jack's podcasts .... He has a very earthy way of speaking and distills down all the info.

Good luck on this amazing journey ..... your amazing journey to optimal health and wellness.

# Thread: Trying to get what the deal is with latitude
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-get-what-the-deal-is-with-latitude.18706/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DC Brown — 2016-11-08T05:41:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-get-what-the-deal-is-with-latitude.18706/post-203703>

I just listed the top 20 countries for longevity, then added for kicks the blue zones (five areas where people on average live longest). I then added factors: average age, latitude, and population density.

There is absolutely no correlation between (average) age and latitude.

There is absolutely no correlation between (average) age and population density.

So why the message being close to the equator is better.

Blue zones (average age > 90)

Okinawa: Lat 26.21, pop/sq mi 1084

Sardinia: Lat 40.12, pop/sq mi 178

Nicoya: Lat 10.14, pop/sq mi 208

Loma Linda: Lat 34.05, pop/sq mi 3094

Ikaria: Lat 37.6, pop/sq mi 86

So these five 'shining examples' (well documented and now under intensive scrutiny) of mean longevity cover a latitudinal range from 10 - 40 degrees, and population densities range from 86 - 3094/sq mile.

The rest of the data (top 20 countries for longevity from CIA factbook) is similar - covering a broad range of both latitudes and population densities with higher and lower extremes for both.

### PaulG — 2016-11-08T10:05:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-get-what-the-deal-is-with-latitude.18706/post-203705>

missing piece to your puzzle = nnEMF

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sperti-vitamin-d-lamp.18707/post-203724>

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

That's the UV Wavelength for the Sperti D above. The Sperti D is a Vit D lamp. You make Vit D when the wavelength is between 280 and 315 nm, see the range above. The Sperti Fiji is predominantly UVA , 70 - 75% UVA and 25 - 30% UVB (315 to 400 nm), so you will still make some Vit D with it. Sperti Fiji is mostly used for tanning because of the high UVA. UVA also has it's numerous benefit, but if it's D you're after, then the Sperti D is more effective at that.

I spent a lot of time in the sun in the summertime, but now that we don't have anymore natural Vit D here, I have been using the lamp. I use the lamp more frequently than my daughter. Right now I'm using it five days a week, 10 minutes a day, because I'm dark. My daughter uses it about three time a week, ten minutes a day with better results, because she is fair. We go somewhere warm twice in the winter time as well to get some real sun. I would like to see my Vit D over 50/ng/ml, but I find it hard to get there. I am currently at 47/ng/ml. My daughter is currently at 85/ng/ml.

21ng/mL is very low level per the chart I posted and also Sperti have (the link I posted), so something does not sound right.
The chart says that such a low level invites breast cancer, MS and other.

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) is fond of Hs-CRP and many people do it and many have it high.

I wonder what would be Jack's opinion on ESR?

to help detect inflammation associated with conditions such as infections, cancers, and autoimmune diseases
Sedimentation Rate, (ESR), Modified Westergren
(ESR) Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate
Test Number: 005215 CPT: 85652

> But he says there is no set amount of supplementation that can bring someone up to 21 nanograms per milliliter because the way people process vitamins varies. In research published in January 2012 in the American Journal of Cardiology, Amer and Qayyum found that increasing levels of vitamin D in the blood are linked with lower levels of a popular marker for cardiovascular inflammation -- c-reactive protein (also known as CRP). Beyond blood levels of 21 nanograms per milliliter, any additional increase in vitamin D was associated with an increase in CRP, a factor linked to stiffening of the blood vessels and an increased risk of cardiovascular problems.

They start with talking about vit D deficiency causing rickets.

until

It cost a cash, can be ordered over internet.

> The Solaris D890 Therapy Probe incorporates one laser diode emitting a wavelength of 875 nm and 3 red diodes emitting a wavelength of 660nm. The D890 Probe has a maximum power output of 625 mW.
>
> D890 Probe Specifications:
>
> Laser Source: 1 **Watt infrared laser diode**
> Red Light Source: 3 red 660nm diode
> Wavelengths: Infrared laser diode 875nm
> Red Diodes 660nm
>
> Duty Cycle: CW
> Power Output: 625mW (+/- 20%)
> Dose: 6 J/cm2 (48 second treatment)
> Imbedded Laser: 1 Watt 875nm CW un-collimated
> \----------------------------------------------------------

Yes. I assume that the UV exposure from the Spertis, at recommended distance, is much stronger than that from the sun, since the recommended exposure time is much shorter.

My personal view about supplementing with Lugols.
It is about halides.
Halides are:
fluoride, chloride, bromide, **iodide** and astatide

We are overloaded with a variety of halides.
We need a large excess of iodide to possibly deal with that excess.
You do not need to test for iodine.
Any excess will be peed out.
Actually, the proper test for iodine is to take a certain measured amount of iodine, test urine and have all that iodine peed out (there is a number, it is in high 90%of excreated iodine after certain amount of time). Otherwise, you would be considered iodine deficient.

PG/E (ZRT) test you can buy on amazon.com

..

I wonder if the benefits of increased vit D production is eroded by exposing skin to UV out of natural diurnal rhythm? Thoughts? Maybe AM would be better than evening? Or UV in AM, IR in the evening? Thanks!

I figured it's best around noon, because those lights give their full power with no change.
The sun light changes frequency and intensity every second, with full power and range around noon.

But indeed anytime is better than never, but I would always catch the real sunrise because nothing can mimic it.

### Ibrahim — 2020-03-27T01:33:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sperti-vitamin-d-lamp.18707/post-288025>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=283609)
>
> My personal view about supplementing with Lugols.
> It is about halides.
> Halides are:
> fluoride, chloride, bromide, **iodide** and astatide
>
> We are overloaded with a variety of halides.
> We need a large excess of iodide to possibly deal with that excess.
> You do not need to test for iodine.
> Any excess will be peed out.
> Actually, the proper test for iodine is to take a certain measured amount of iodine, test urine and have all that iodine peed out (there is a number, it is in high 90%of excreated iodine after certain amount of time). Otherwise, you would be considered iodine deficient.
>
> PG/E (ZRT) test you can buy on amazon.com
>
> ..

How much Iodine should one use for gut issues like candida? I know 2 drops is about 5 mg .

> [Ibrahim said:](/goto/post?id=288025)

> How much Iodine should one use for gut issues like candida? I know 2 drops is about 5 mg .

For the last few decades
I am squeezing the rubber top and pouring the content into my morning coffee, then I drink the coffee.
Assume about 1cc volume.

Note;
most often those rubber tops degrade and become hard, not flexible.
When you find a good one, cherish it.
Prevent lugols from wetting rubber.
