# Thread: way to avoid PPD testing ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/way-to-avoid-ppd-testing.18710/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### motivatedhealthseeker — 2016-11-08T21:21:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/way-to-avoid-ppd-testing.18710/post-203730>

Is there a way to get out of PPD testing for tuberculosis that is required for healthcare workers ? It has live antigens in it and has the possibility to wreak havoc on the immune system. One other option might be a chest x ray which isnt that great of an alternative either. Push comes to shove, what is the lesser or the two evils? Any other ways around it? They also require flu shots which is not requiredddd but then its said you have to wear a mask all the time.

### motivatedhealthseeker — 2016-11-16T23:01:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/way-to-avoid-ppd-testing.18710/post-204002>

A blood test called quantiferon gold could be done in lieu of ppd but ofcourse its not as cost effective.

### Jenny Young — 2020-01-09T18:42:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/way-to-avoid-ppd-testing.18710/post-284365>

Late to the game, but this might help someone else. Just tell them you already tested positive. I tested positive as a teen and work in healthcare and never had to get it redone. They used to recommend a yearly chest x-ray, but now they don't even do that unless you're having symptoms.

### PaulG — 2016-11-09T02:34:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/book-list.18711/post-203736>

-   [**Epi-Paleo RX**](http://www.amazon.com/Epi-paleo-Rx-Prescription-Disease-Reversal/dp/0989057739) by Dr. Jack Kruse
-   [**Cross Currents**](http://www.amazon.com/Cross-Currents-Robert-O-Becker/dp/0874776090) by Dr. Robert O. Becker
-   [**The Fourth Phase of Water**](http://www.amazon.com/The-Fourth-Phase-Water-Beyond/dp/0962689548) by Dr. Gerald Pollack
-   [**The Body Electric**](http://www.amazon.com/The-Body-Electric-Electromagnetism-Foundation/dp/0688069711) by Dr. Robert O. Becker
-   [**Light Medicine of the Future**](http://www.amazon.com/Light-Medicine-Future-Heal-Ourselves/dp/1879181010) by Jacob Liberman
-   [**Health and Light**](http://www.amazon.com/Health-Light-extraordinary-Affects-emotional/dp/0898040981) by John N. Ott
-   [**The Healing Sun – Sunlight and Health in the 21st Century**](http://www.amazon.com/Healing-Sun-Sunlight-Health-Century/dp/1899171975/) by Richard Hobday
-   [**Going Somewhere**](http://www.amazon.com/Going-Somewhere-Truth-about-Science/dp/0981854915/) by Andrew Marino
-   [**Power, Sex, Suicide: Mitochondria and the Meaning of Life**](http://www.amazon.com/Power-Sex-Suicide-Mitochondria-Meaning/dp/0199205647) by Nick Lane
-   [**Light in Shaping Life**](http://www.amazon.com/Light-Shaping-Life-Biophotons-Medicine/dp/9081884328) by Roeland Van Wijk
-   [**Life on the Edge**](http://www.amazon.com/Life-Edge-Coming-Quantum-Biology/dp/0307986810/) by Johnjoe McFadden and Jim Al-Khalili
-   [**The Vital Question**](http://www.amazon.com/The-Vital-Question-Evolution-Origins/dp/0393088812) by Nick Lane
-   [**Cosmosapiens**](https://www.amazon.com/Cosmosapiens-Human-Evolution-Origin-Universe/dp/1468312448) by John Hands

### Sun Disciple — 2017-03-15T22:36:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/book-list.18711/post-210431>

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/img_0881-jpg.4522/)
I just finished reading the healing sun. It does a great job at laying out the history of Sun therapy. It chronicles Its use in ancient times from the Egyptians through to Greece and Rome. Also Its disappearance through the dark ages and finally with strong resurgence at the turn of the twentieth century sadly to be again suppressed after the advent of antibiotics. I was intrigued at the traditions of morning sunrise exercise among eastern cultures. I would like to look to find a group that practices tai chi, chi gong or yoga in the traditional way grounded outside at dawn. The one thing is when it comes to diet it fails somewhat as it pushes low fat. Other than that it should be on every mito hackers reading list. A good quote that stuck out from the book is from an old Italian proverb -'Dove non va il sole,va il medico' or 'where the sun does not go, the doctor does.'

Just about to start Cosmo sapiens. Thanks for the list Im almost half way through the above titles.

### Dania Nour — 2019-01-07T12:51:24+0200

# Thread: Functional endo says to convert T4 to T3 lots of vegetables/fruits activate Deiodinase. Leptin rx?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/functional-endo-says-to-convert-t4-to-t3-lots-of-vegetables-fruits-activate-deiodinase-leptin-rx.18723/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Stanislava — 2016-11-11T17:04:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/functional-endo-says-to-convert-t4-to-t3-lots-of-vegetables-fruits-activate-deiodinase-leptin-rx.18723/post-203833>

Hello
Ive seen my functional endo. And I told him Ive tried cynomel T3 100mcg with T3/T4 medication he prescribed me.
And I have felt great and healthy for a first time in 3 years.
I know I know I dont convert well.
He freaked out with dose of 120mg of T3 in total.
And said to activate Deiodinase we need lots of vegetable and fruits. 80% veggies for 20 %.
And high fat is not needed.
Not very epi paleo leptin reset friendly.
And it was going against what I was trying to with leptin reset.

He changed my meds. Instead of T3/T4 mix. Ive started on levo. He increased my T4 with levo from 100 slowly to 150 and later try 175.
And 30mcg of T3 cynomel.

Only problem with high levo I get
dizzines, black spots fainting episodes. Basically my Postural tachycardia syndrome comes back.
Not on T3 higher dose.

Then later in the night got horrible constant adrenaline surges with hot feeling on my head and whole body tremors which lasted whole night when too much thyroid hormones lowered my cortisol.

Now he wants me on betablockers for 2-3 days to stop adrenaline surges.

But my functional endo is trying to convince me that high T4 is not issue when I get Pots episodes.

Im on Hydrocortisone 20mg in 2-3 doses for last 2 years with thyroid meds. And adrenal adaptogens.

And lots of supplements for those who would be interested
Like coq10 200mg, pqq 20mg, magnesium malate 800mg (threonate and all others give me nausea and closing throat)vitamin A betacarotene complex
Vitamin B complex
B12methyl
B9 methyl
pregnenolone 50mg
selenium 200mcg
vitamin C 3gr
vitamin D3/K2 8000UI
N acetyl cysteine 1800mg
biotin 5000mcg
zinc copper 50mg
quercetin
ginger
glycine 1gr
b6 as p5p 100mg
Fish almost daily cant do seafood with histamine intolerance mcas
progesterone cream 200mg in 2 doses
No iodine bit apprehensive with histamine intolerance.

### JanSz — 2016-11-12T15:04:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/infrared-light.18728/post-203863>

Possibly others will chime in too.
From what I recall infrared is about two frequency ranges
IR-A
heat
Near-infrared NIR, IR-A DIN (0.75–1.4 µm) (750-1400nm)
heat frequencies (I think) were not described
\---
Quantlet uses IR-A but the frequency is not disclosed (AFAIK)

# Thread: Best dopamine-building-vacation locations
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-dopamine-building-vacation-locations.18744/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Darleen — 2016-11-18T17:49:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-dopamine-building-vacation-locations.18744/post-204060>

Hi All,
Looking to go on a vacation and get some sun during the months of December-February.
Any suggestions are greatly appreciated!

### michellehp — 2016-11-22T16:57:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-dopamine-building-vacation-locations.18744/post-204211>

We recently went to Tortola, BVI. Stayed in a villa through AirBnB. They stocked the kitchen with all of our requests- seafood, grass fed beef, water, wine, etc. We had a chef come cook for us, rented a boat (with driver) for some days to visit other islands, 18th parallel, no one around... except goats, chickens, roosters, the beach, water and sky. I had never come home from a vacation feeling that good and its effects lasted for weeks. We never turned on a television the whole time and the electricity went out for a day and it wasn't missed.

### Rubicon — 2016-11-22T23:00:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-dopamine-building-vacation-locations.18744/post-204219>

# Thread: Worms in raw fish
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worms-in-raw-fish.18746/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sujoe123 — 2016-11-18T19:23:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worms-in-raw-fish.18746/post-204062>

Does anyone know if Jack freezes his fresh fish before he eats it to kill the worms. There are many species of fish here in the Gulf that carry worms and parasites in their flesh at one time of the year or another.... I do use lime juice to make ceviche but the acidity can also change the conformation or folding of proteins.

### Rocky — 2017-01-30T18:07:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worms-in-raw-fish.18746/post-207579>

Assuming you are eating raw fish? If there were worms our parasites in the fish wouldn't chewing and stomach acid take care of them in short order?

### Samuel Brunic — 2017-02-26T06:55:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worms-in-raw-fish.18746/post-209295>

I asked Trader Joe's about their fresh Alaskan salmon filets and they told me they freeze them on the ship at -40 F fot at least 15 hours to kill parasites. I have been eatin raw oysters, salmon and tuna for months with no issues.

Dr. Kruse also mentioned in his Twitter account in early 2017 that he does not worry about worms from fish.

# Thread: Chronically elevated cortisol found finally. Is 7keto dhea and progesterone right?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronically-elevated-cortisol-found-finally-is-7keto-dhea-and-progesterone-right.18753/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Stanislava — 2016-11-21T12:18:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronically-elevated-cortisol-found-finally-is-7keto-dhea-and-progesterone-right.18753/post-204136>

Hello

Ive had one car accident at 8 rolling over car and another minor head trauma when fainting and hittinh my gead on door frame at 34 years old.
Since that night Ive develloped adrenaline surges, extreme anxiety palpitations, bloating diarhea, heavy digestion. And started fainting only for 3 weeks one hour after falling asleep waking with a jump horrible tachycardia, loosing urines and it felt like pseudo seizures whole body tremors.
Then this fainting episodes stopped and Ive started to have histamine issues. Couldnt sleep and anxiety was uncontrolavle for hours.
Doctors put me in antidepresants, benzos and sleeping pill but my muscles would become mire and more stiff and painful.
Til 8 months later I had pheochromocytoma like symptoms without tumor thats what doctors said.
Functional doc put me and thyroid T3T4 hydrocortisone and progesterone pill. And few vitamins Bs, D, pregnenolone, nac, ala, zinc, adaptogens formula etc
But after 2 years we still couldnt explain why without progesterone I had this horrible adrenaline surges. And in Paris France I would always get constantly high adrenaline sweating light sensitivity, tachycardia.

Then 10 days ago doctors put me on betabkockers to stop adrenaline surges and it burned my head.
So Ive taken 500mg phosphatidylserine and it stopped burning and adrenaline.
I took Gaba supplements like l theanine and slept like a baby for a first time after 3 years with phosphatidylserine.

And light went on chronically elevevated cortisol.
Head trauma or even activated virus and estrogen dominance?

Well doctor told me to get on 7 keto dhea to balance cortisol and progesterone vit E orally to lower estrogen dominance.
Is it right path?

And how high cortisol can create histamines intolerance mcas?

Ive have been treated for hashi and low cortisol and mast cells till now.
Looks like I might not even need thyroid meds maybe.
And my leaky gut and leaky gut brain barrier can finally start to heal with balanced cortisol and no hydrocortisone.

Even if in this state without thyroid medications I was in dark room and it felt like every cell of my body was burning.

Im getting those 2 this week.

# Thread: Plastic free ice cubes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plastic-free-ice-cubes.18766/>
**Subforum:** Community Cookbook
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sujoe123 — 2016-11-23T15:19:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plastic-free-ice-cubes.18766/post-204246>

Use Ramekin cups to freeze ice. They can be found in glass or ceramic and they stack nicely on a cookie sheet. You have to allow for a few minutes of thawing to dump them out to store in a glass bowl. These are the little cups that creme brulee and puddings are made in individual serving sizes. You can purchase them cheaply at a restaurant supplier. I think I paid about $30 for 50 of them.

### Sujoe123 — 2016-11-23T15:23:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plastic-free-ice-cubes.18766/post-204247>

> [Sujoe123 said:](/goto/post?id=204246)
>
> Use Ramekin cups to freeze ice. They can be found in glass or ceramic and they stack nicely on a cookie sheet. You have to allow for a few minutes of thawing to dump them out to store in a glass bowl. These are the little cups that creme brulee and puddings are made in individual serving sizes. You can purchase them cheaply at a restaurant supplier. I think I paid about $30 for 50 of them.

It seems that with all the warnings of plastics that someone would have created an impact-resistant glass reusable ice cube that is partially filled with water to allow expansion for freezing. My daughter and I look for these online every few months and so far nobody has manufactured them! I think it would be a great idea!

### Sujoe123 — 2016-11-24T00:19:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plastic-free-ice-cubes.18766/post-204265>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=204253)
>
> Pyrex works too

Pyrex was the word I was looking for but I could not remember it so I said impact-resistant)

### Sajid Mahmood — 2018-04-02T21:36:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plastic-free-ice-cubes.18766/post-238546>

Silicone must be okay to use

# Thread: Low - Secretory IGA - Anyone know about this?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-secretory-iga-anyone-know-about-this.18785/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### __D__ — 2016-11-28T02:24:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-secretory-iga-anyone-know-about-this.18785/post-204399>

Hello I recently had a stool test done and my Secretory IGA was on the low end @ 3. I looked up the potential cause and it could be allergies, stress, primary immune dysfunction, celiac, chrons, etc....

I am hoping to see an immunologist as the rheumatalogist said he still could not make a diagnosis other than auto immune phenomenom. I am also thinking Low Secretory IGA could be a clue for me, as if it were because of primary immune dysfuntion, then it would make sense why I get sick for longer, more frequently etc...

However, I have been unwell after a severe gut infection in Indonesia - I never fully recovered to a baseline I was used to. In fact, I am pretty sure I was having some immune problems prior to all of that as well. I have tried Paleo AIP, an elimination diet, and copious amounts of blood tests -

What has helped was cutting gluten. My sinus infections went away quickly. But at times, I have unusual autoimmune markers that pop up and go away (ANA, Low C3, C4, DNA Antibody, RNP), along with consistent low platelets.

After years of experiencing painful joints, stiff muscles, and cognitive problems (memory issues), chronic fatigue, and now neurological issues (balance problems), I am still at a loss of what is causing this kind of situation in my body. If its primary immune dysfunction, then it would make sense that I cant fight pathogens (i have dysbiotic ones in my stool) / viruses not matter how hard i try. At least this is my theory after looking back over the years of countless sinus infections, low energy, hormonal disruptions, and illnesses that seemed to drag on much longer than my peers.

As mentioned, I am hoping to find more answers with an immunologist, but I was wondering if someone has more expertise or has come across this themselves?

I came across this site after researching a man named Wim Hof and cold therapy. I have not tried this yet as something that could help me.

Thank you

# Thread: Hands in or Hands out?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hands-in-or-hands-out.18816/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sfast — 2016-12-04T06:33:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hands-in-or-hands-out.18816/post-204746>

Hi folks,

I am progressing with my CT and fluctuating around the 20-30min at the moment. The water temperature is 32°F. So it is cold and perhaps colder than recommended but as I have just a barrel on my balcony I cannot regulate it properly. In winter, I am making a Wim Hof, meaning that I have a lot of ice.

I have a problem with my hands. After a while they start to hurt which makes my progress slow down. Are there any disadvantages for keeping (just) the hands out of the water? I am thinking of the great density of receptors in the hands and their special role as sensors of the environment.

Thanks for the help
Sascha

### Danco3636 — 2016-12-04T07:05:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hands-in-or-hands-out.18816/post-204747>

Your in freezing water 32°F for 20-30 minutes submerged? Is this in the AM or PM?

### sfast — 2016-12-04T07:41:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hands-in-or-hands-out.18816/post-204748>

Yes. Just a bit of shoulders and the head are out because I am pretty tall.

I do my CT second thing in the morning after my mobility and some movement practice. Which is around 6:30-6:45 AM

### Carole traole — 2016-12-04T10:38:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hands-in-or-hands-out.18816/post-204751>

Hi there, I am experiencing a similar problem with my hands. I am swimming every morning in an outdoor pond that is currently at 4 degrees Celsius and I am only managing to swim for 10 minutes because my fingers get really numb and painful. I see most of the other women swimming there wearing gloves and socks but I don't think I want to go down that path. I am hoping that my resilience will improve with repeated exposures but this hasn't happened just yet.

### Lahelada — 2016-12-04T13:27:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hands-in-or-hands-out.18816/post-204755>

Gloves and socks if you can swim with them are ok. Read these [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/wearing-socks.667/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/wearing-socks.667/) [https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-easy-start-guide/](https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-easy-start-guide/)

By teh way,if ever in doubt , a google search with Jack Kruse and then the term of enquiry works wonders. In this case I asked Jack Kruse socks . Not all queries have to me multisyllabic !!  I am saying this as it makes learning faster.

### Sue-UK — 2016-12-04T15:50:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hands-in-or-hands-out.18816/post-204757>

> [sfast said:](/goto/post?id=204746)
>
> Hi folks,
>
> I am progressing with my CT and fluctuating around the 20-30min at the moment. The water temperature is 32°F. So it is cold and perhaps colder than recommended but as I have just a barrel on my balcony I cannot regulate it properly. In winter, I am making a Wim Hof, meaning that I have a lot of ice.
>
> I have a problem with my hands. After a while they start to hurt which makes my progress slow down. Are there any disadvantages for keeping (just) the hands out of the water? I am thinking of the great density of receptors in the hands and their special role as sensors of the environment.
>
> Thanks for the help
> Sascha

I keep my hands out, or sometimes dip my wrists in with my fingers stretched upwards. I'm never fully submerged to the top of the neck because of my bath set up, so I will sometimes splash exposed torso/leg parts with the cold water or dip them in and out and when my hands get too cold I warm them on my carotids, or massage my scalp and face. Cools them down and my hands warm up ... Win win ...

### sfast — 2016-12-04T22:07:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hands-in-or-hands-out.18816/post-204778>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=204755)
>
> Gloves and socks if you can swim with them are ok. Read these [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/wearing-socks.667/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/wearing-socks.667/) [https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-easy-start-guide/](https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-easy-start-guide/)
>
> By teh way,if ever in doubt , a google search with Jack Kruse and then the term of enquiry works wonders. In this case I asked Jack Kruse socks . Not all queries have to me multisyllabic !!  I am saying this as it makes learning faster.

Thanks for the links. I found them. But I am not referring to the practical issue of adapt them to the cold but rather to their role as one of the main sensors during ct. The question is: Is submerging the hands more important than a faster progress without them submerged?

### Sue-UK — 2016-12-05T13:51:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hands-in-or-hands-out.18816/post-204801>

> [sfast said:](/goto/post?id=204778)
>
> Thanks for the links. I found them. But I am not referring to the practical issue of adapt them to the cold but rather to their role as one of the main sensors during ct. The question is: Is submerging the hands more important than a faster progress without them submerged?

I don't currently think so .... My feeling is that as a bipedal mammal, in a natural environment, our feet adapt differently using the gradual lowering of temperature and light stimulus as the season changes to slowly adapt, including perhaps a change in the fat ratios in our "hooves." They become less sensitive to the cold, and help endurance.

Being upright, not connected to the possibly frozen earth, there is less advantage to the hands, and more sensitivity there might have advantages, such as the collecting of food. A bipedal wading through cold water looking for food might be constantly dipping their hands in and out to harvest, but because its not chronic, they can retain sensitivity, and are still receiving information from the natural environment. Hands trailing in the water means faster heat loss and
might mean less food harvesting time ...?

### sfast — 2016-12-11T09:15:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hands-in-or-hands-out.18816/post-205123>

Thanks for your reply, Sue.

This is a good argument but I don't think it is valid. Now, we are bipedal. But we inherited many traits of our non-human ancestors. The spine is developed in the 3D-World of the water and still benefits from water like actions (e.g. spinal waves) and we can find residues of quadruple locomotion in our brain, which can explain, why quadruple locomotion is oddly beneficial in ones movement practice.

I apply this thinking to the hands. Yes, we use them in a different way, but the question remains: Could it be oddly beneficial to adapt the hands while submerged in water? When we talk about cold adaption we no longer talk about being exposed to a natural environment. We talk about ice baths which is a artificial way to have a connection to nature again.

### Sue-UK — 2016-12-11T18:36:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hands-in-or-hands-out.18816/post-205161>

> [sfast said:](/goto/post?id=205123)
>
> Thanks for your reply, Sue.
>
> This is a good argument but I don't think it is valid. Now, we are bipedal. But we inherited many traits of our non-human ancestors. The spine is developed in the 3D-World of the water and still benefits from water like actions (e.g. spinal waves) and we can find residues of quadruple locomotion in our brain, which can explain, why quadruple locomotion is oddly beneficial in ones movement practice.
>
> I apply this thinking to the hands. Yes, we use them in a different way, but the question remains: Could it be oddly beneficial to adapt the hands while submerged in water? When we talk about cold adaption we no longer talk about being exposed to a natural environment. We talk about ice baths which is a artificial way to have a connection to nature again.

Quantum Biology 6 - Bipedalism has made me think differently about the hands compared to the feet.

For me there is a "temperature window of opportunity" for cold adaptation for the hands, and that when the temperature drops to a certain level, and dependent on other environmental factors, the risks can outweigh the potential benefits. But its a constantly changing N=1. Shrinking my respiratory proteins and reducing inflammation systemically is more important to me. I aim to be outside in the water just before sunrise. I can tolerate 0C water with a clear sky and good view of the rising sun much better than 12C water under wall to wall cloud, the light makes a big difference, so I can't say I can tolerate xC for x minutes with hands submerged or not submerged, because it varies dependent on the environment as a whole. As a general rule I am much more observant of skin changes when the water temp goes below 5C, and more so if there is also a very cold wind. The only time I don't CT outside is in very strong winds which grounds even the larger birds, or during thunderstorms.

On my second session this morning, just dipping my hands in and out of the water, I reached a point where I could see that from the knuckles to the fingertips were going white .... time to get out, before it gets painful (which could raise cortisol). Get out whilst I'm a woman in the cold not a cold woman.  Had I kept them submerged, I would have been out much faster, and had I done it during the first session, I may not have got in for the second session 30 minutes later. So by not keeping my hands and feet submerged, I gained extra time in the water, keeping my spine submerged, and it was challenging without being stressful/painful. I think the spine is a better systemic environmental temperature sensor than the hands, particularly for women. In an icy wind, women will normally turn their backs to it, protecting their breasts. I've read that ice cold breasts can stop breast milk ... so in evolutionary terms this behaviour sort of makes sense, and because cold on the back stimulates BAT to generate heat, even more important because women generally have less muscle.

### sfast — 2016-12-13T09:59:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hands-in-or-hands-out.18816/post-205206>

I think different context is important: I am a fit and healthy guy who doesn't want to fix broken health but to get after it. I don't mind the pain (up to a point of course). I am more on the antifragile road and not so into fixing fragilities.

However, one could test the hypothesis we would need one group that cold adapts with hands submerged and one with the hands out. Afterwards, they have to be tested with hands out for comparison. I do both. I have two 24h-fasting days (only eating supper) and I do only approximately half of the duration I do on my normal days but with hands in. After I adopted this practice, my cold tolerance seemed to expand a bit faster.

Of course, with hands out I am able to be in the cold for quite a bit longer. But this does not mean that the adaptions are better. Like in endurance training: Some adaptions are better when interval training even though you are reducing the overall training volume.

### drezy — 2017-04-07T01:30:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hands-in-or-hands-out.18816/post-211941>

> [sfast said:](/goto/post?id=205206)
>
> However, one could test the hypothesis we would need one group that cold adapts with hands submerged and one with the hands out. Afterwards, they have to be tested with hands out for comparison.

Good points but I fear that groups of people, all with different exposures nnEMF and degenerative issues, might still not match up with your own personal N=1.

I'm in a similar boat as you as far as not having any obvious issues at this point, doing 24 hour fasting, and being a collector of Taleb books (presuming that you anti fragile reference comes from him).

When you say "with hands out I am able to be in the cold for quite a bit longer" roughly how much time are you talking? I'll try out both alternatives and see what I notice. So far I've done hands in and out and usually just stay in for a 48 min timer.

On a side note I once tried to rush dinner too soon after a hand-in CT session before my muscles loosened back up. I ended up slicing right into 1/10 of my left hand index finger knuckle. It should have gushed like wild, but instead I got a good look at what a dorsal digital artery was. The cut itself healed really quick too FWIW.

# Thread: Sharing a possible nnEMF biohack...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharing-a-possible-nnemf-biohack.18842/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2016-12-12T04:44:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharing-a-possible-nnemf-biohack.18842/post-205171>

So at night when I shutdown the power to the home, I go to the circuit breaker to do it at this time. That outputs around 100+ milliGauss when in close proximity to it. Doing this at 10pm before going to sleep I'm thinking can be disrupting, even a 10 second exposure. In the first month of moving into my new home, I noticed that after getting into bed and shutting down the power, I get a few minutes of tinnitus afterwards. So if you're following my pattern, I've semi-scientifically connected "the shutting down of the power" to "my exposure and the quick tinnitus" thereafter.

To compensate, I think I've successfully hacked it in a way you may not expect. After flipping the switches, I perform 'Immediate Grounding' immediately thereafter (within about 10 seconds). I can do this because in my home, I have an internal stone-wall that's connected to the earth (image below). So I experimented this past week.

After shutting down the power, I go over to the wall and touch it for about 2 minutes. Voila... it appears I have little to no tinnitus when I crawl into bed. Last night I only touched the wall for a few second and then got into bed, and I noticed the tinnitus, but wasn't as intense and went away after just a minute. So I'm thinking there's an actual perceptive difference when I ground after touching the circuit breaker, and when I don't ground after touching the circuit breaker.

For these reasons, maybe this could have broader application... if you know you've been exposed to a higher nnEMF source, go ground immediately thereafter. Or simply, weave in a bit more grounding in the day. I'm actually doing this at work. I'll get about an hour of work in and then take a 5min break outside leaning against a metal bike rack, a stone, taking off my shoes for a few mins (this is more problematic with boots in the snow), or whatever is available to ground upon. I also keep in mind the proximity to power sources and have measured to see if there are fields in those grounding areas.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Queen of Elves — 2016-12-12T09:17:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharing-a-possible-nnemf-biohack.18842/post-205176>

Thank you for sharing your hack! It bothered me so much, that I had to protect my floor against humidity so that no grounding is possible anymore, but I also have such a stone wall, that is connected to the ground. Never thought about that - thank you so much!

### Linz — 2016-12-12T16:48:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharing-a-possible-nnemf-biohack.18842/post-205185>

I use a broomstick to switch off the shower pump so I don't have to stand too close. Will think about some grounding afterwards too. Thanks.

### paskovanaryba — 2016-12-13T04:20:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharing-a-possible-nnemf-biohack.18842/post-205195>

I can attest from my n=1 personal experience that your proposed approach of "discharging" nnEMF by taking short, but regular grounding breaks works. Being sensitive to such exposure I have practiced this approach for about a year now and by doing it I'm able to bring down my sensitivity to a manageable level. I simply go outside and walk barefoot on humid grass for a little while.

### Scompy — 2016-12-13T06:02:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharing-a-possible-nnemf-biohack.18842/post-205202>

Thanks for confirming your N=1... I tried again last night and grounded for a little longer (hands and feet) and had literally no tinnitus. I'll take the advice of using a sturdy stick to turn off the power. No need to put my body that close and be a lab-rat. Nearly all of the world have been nnEMF labrats for quite sometime without their knowledge.

### PaulG — 2016-12-13T08:08:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharing-a-possible-nnemf-biohack.18842/post-205203>

Get an electrician to run a cable and switch into the house from the circuit breaker so that you can turn it off without going to the breaker.

### JCDC — 2016-12-15T00:31:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anybody-put-black-lights-in-car.18855/post-205280>

I spend a pretty decent amount of time driving. I usually like to drive with my windows down but sometimes it is raining, snowing, I'm on a well shaded road or its just impossible to hear my podcasts on the highway. Also, without a sunroof, the extreme asymmetry in the parts of my body exposed to the sun can't be that great.

Has anyone setup a black light or other favorable lighting in their car?

On the topic of automotive hacks, what about hacking a way to ground one's self to the car, good or bad idea?

Any other ideas for ways to my my driving time less detrimental to long term health?

### Benny Hammond — 2017-08-17T17:25:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anybody-put-black-lights-in-car.18855/post-221823>

I have been working on this - grounded my car and myself in the car and turn off the cabin lights. Although that might be dangerous at night not being able to see the gauges. My plan is to get a light ballast to plug into the cigarette lighter and change out a UV bulb and red bulb depending on the time of day. It seems like a good choice - the current coming from the cig lighter is DC.

### Benny Hammond — 2017-08-17T17:28:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anybody-put-black-lights-in-car.18855/post-221824>

Has anyone done this with good results?

# Thread: EMF Protection Blushield Tesla Gold Portable Powerful, State-of-the-art Active EMF Protection - EMF
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-protection-blushield-tesla-gold-portable-powerful-state-of-the-art-active-emf-protection-emf.18885/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2016-12-22T01:08:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-protection-blushield-tesla-gold-portable-powerful-state-of-the-art-active-emf-protection-emf.18885/post-205628>

So I'm listening to this guy Jason Bawden-Smith who Dr. K referenced or at least the solari.com website was referenced - he wrote a book called:
**In The Dark: New Ways to Avoid the Harmful Effects of Living in a Technologically Connected World**
[https://www.amazon.com/Dark-Harmful...swatch\_0?\_encoding=UTF8&qid=1482354525&sr=8-1](https://www.amazon.com/Dark-Harmful-Effects-Technologically-Connected-ebook/dp/B01M3124NM/ref=tmm_kin_swatch_0?_encoding=UTF8&qid=1482354525&sr=8-1)
and has this podcast which thus far has been great:
[https://solari.com/blog/protecting-against-emf-radiation-with-jason-bawden-smith/](https://solari.com/blog/protecting-against-emf-radiation-with-jason-bawden-smith/)

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-12-17T18:14:24+0200

>
> Anyway, he says this Blueshield device just rocks as far as making your cells coherent - and I was wondering if anyone had any thoughts on this?

Matt,

Both of the links you cited have **disappeared into a black hole** on my web browser, so no dice.

I did do a search on _Blushield_ and poked around that company's home page.

As I suspected, they are peddling another 'frequency' device that is supposed to cancel out harmful EMF frequencies in your environment by working at a subtle, not physical level.

There have been other threads here on these unproven devices, and I believe I am correct in saying Dr Kruse does not believe they are effective.

I am not saying they are snake oil-- I haven't tried any similar devices except for the **Q-Link** pendant. They might possibly have a beneficial effect, but unless you can put an RF meter near one and see radiation go down to near zero you have to color me a glowing neon pink skeptic.

I hope any Kruse members that purchase and use these devices will do us a favor and give us their anecdotal reports on whether they noticed a benefit that is not placebo-like.

### Matt Justice — 2018-12-17T21:09:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-protection-blushield-tesla-gold-portable-powerful-state-of-the-art-active-emf-protection-emf.18885/post-258324>

Yeah, my gut feeling is that it's a band-aid at best... although I am open to band-aids if they work well, until I can make more significant lifestyle shifts to my environment.

But I own every EMF meter needed to test RF, Electric Fields, Magnetic Fields, and Body Voltage separately, and most of these devices I've tested show hardly any change in the past... doesn't mean they don't do ANYTHING, but it's definitely hard to quantify.

Schumann resonance generators are great, but you're not overriding the source in my opinion. (and my opinion could change, I have a lot to learn!)

### Sun Disciple — 2018-12-18T03:15:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-protection-blushield-tesla-gold-portable-powerful-state-of-the-art-active-emf-protection-emf.18885/post-258339>

Reviving this thread- I too feel way more thirsty and I'm almost 4 wks into Epi-Paleo Rx and 3 wks into Leptin Reset. I changed from our well water to Costco bottled water a number of days ago and that seemed to make it worse- wondered if it was the minerals? There is sodium added. Today I have Icelandic, but I'm not sure if I can keep up with it financially. I have enough here for the next 2 days so we'll see if I notice a difference. Yeah get into the cold baths and showers and then see what happens. Immediately after you shouldn't be thirsty, but it may wear off. Do you work in an office? Own an iphone? Wi-Fi at home? Drive a new car? Live near the city? Block of flats?

For every Yes, you will likely need more CT and Sun and DDW to off-set that. You don't get much sun, or enough, so do CT or expect to pay for Icelandic.

Living in UK, when I went Epi-Paleo Keto/ Leptin Rx I had to move from tap water/regular water to full time San Pellegrino. Costed me £150 a month for water for over a year. I'm now moving onto Voss and Icelandic...

But in a few weeks I'll be living at the Equator. It's just too difficult to survive at these Northern Latitude/nnEMF hell hole cities these days, and it will get worse with 5G...

# Thread: A random musing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-random-musing.18905/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JoeT — 2016-12-29T17:16:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-random-musing.18905/post-205883>

Jack, I've been going back and trying to read all of your old blogs so that I can get up to speed in the forum; and so I'm trying to be quiet and not post questions until I learn more......I hardly know any Science, but I do know some Finance, and while reading your old CT1 blog, I immediately felt as if I had read this sort of thing before.
Then I realized, this sounds like George Soros, but on Science! (Note: I find his politics intolerable, but doesn’t change the fact that he is probably the greatest pure Trader of all time.) Specifically, Soros has highlighted the fact that the theories that modern Economics and Finance are predicated upon, actually make a number of massive assumptions, based upon Newtonian Physics, in order to present an appearance of mathematical precision, and to try to bring a sense of “certainty” to what are really Social Sciences. The only problem is these theoretical assumptions frequently bear no resemblance to reality!! (See any of the numerous “bubbles” we have experienced in the last 20 years).

Uncannily, he named his fund THE QUANTUM FUND ! He chose that name because of the linkages he observed between the Heisenberg Principle and market observation and participation.

To this today, the economic “establishment" does not really take him seriously (although behavioral economics has made some progress); and he has always said that the only reason anyone pays any attention to him is because his track record is factually undeniable. But in general, even most Financial professionals still do not, or cannot, understand how he perceives markets, nor can they make it through his book, the Alchemy of Finance.

So, as I am learning about your work, I think I’m hearing the same message from you in connection with how Biochemistry has had to subtract water from proteins to conduct studies, and/or how evidence based medicine seeks to draw conclusions predicated upon generalized population data, when in reality we are all radically different (n=1).

Here is a link to a brief overview of this work if any one is interested.

_[http://www.politicsforum.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=30747](http://www.politicsforum.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=30747)_

presently i light my room at night with my computer screen with flux set to 1200K.

In a week i'm going to start a biohack of having no computer in the house. Problem arises: I'm not sure how i'm going to light my room.

Candles or any other fire source are a last resort but if you know of any completely risk free set up then please share.

I thought there'd be a big market for bulbs that don't emit blue light but couldn't find any. If you know any please let me know.

Any other means of lighting up a room would be a big help

### Flawed — 2017-11-02T13:57:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/household-lighting-problem.18906/post-227170>

I ended up buying a screen filter for blue light from lowbluelights.com, then i made a conic structure around my bedroom light using an opaque fabric. So all the light from my standard bulb went through the filter. Worked quite well although i think it got quite hot under there.

This could have potential business promise if anyones motivated - a cubic structure with no upper surface made of blue light filtering material that encases your household lighting.

# Thread: Very low carb for women
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-low-carb-for-women.18910/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Verena1028 — 2016-12-30T17:14:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-low-carb-for-women.18910/post-205912>

Because it s winter time, I do a ketogenic diet.
I try to eat mainly fish, seafood, seaweed,
very little veggies like onion.
There are positiv aspects like I am more mindfull, more clear in my head.
But my sleep and my energie sucks,
now I try to add some carbs like raw honey, dark choclat but I got really bad cravings ..
So what to do? Any experience?

### JanSz — 2016-12-30T18:35:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-low-carb-for-women.18910/post-205916>

> [Verena1028 said:](/goto/post?id=205912)
>
> Because it s winter time, I do a ketogenic diet.
> I try to eat mainly fish, seafood, seaweed,
> very little veggies like onion.
> There are positiv aspects like I am more mindfull, more clear in my head.
> But my sleep and my energie sucks,
> now I try to add some carbs like raw honey, dark choclat but I got really bad cravings ..
> So what to do? Any experience?

Eat is such a way that when you do blood test,
your fasting insulin is no more than 2-3

Eat in such a way that when you do blood test,
Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum
on that test
all fatty acids are
ideally at the mid range of respective laboratory range
practically plus minus 25% of laboratory mid range is acceptable

Eat in such a way that when you do blood test,
your (natural) Total cholesterol is more that 160, ideally 180-220

Read attached chart for your height,
get your weight from mid-range of recommended weights (dark blue area)
unless you are bodybuilder, then 15-20% for woman

Use water fast few times a month if you need loose weight.
Liver holds about 2 days worth of fuel, fast must be longer than 2 days.
After 2 days, if insulin falls down to 2-3 (sometimes it is big if), it will allow body fat to be used,
so that is the point when one starts loosing body fat.
Sometimes one must wait more than the above 2 days, (3-4) waiting for insulin to come down.
..
/////////////////

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-low-carb-for-women.18910/post-205917>

# Thread: Is non-gradual CT damaging?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-non-gradual-ct-damaging.18914/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ryan dawn — 2017-01-02T02:10:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-non-gradual-ct-damaging.18914/post-206019>

I live in HH, SC and have been CTing in the ocean, after which I often feel drained and weakened.

I have not adhered to any gradual protocol and am suddenly plunging into the cold - am I experiencing cortisol flooding and overwhelming stress as a result?

I thought that the benefits would be experientally available through my attempts yet I think I am actually doing harm to myself.

\[age 27\]
\[CT DURATION: 5-20 min\]
\[ocean temp: around 60 degrees\]

### Penny — 2017-01-05T23:08:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-non-gradual-ct-damaging.18914/post-206185>

> [ryan dawn said:](/goto/post?id=206019)
>
> I live in HH, SC and have been CTing in the ocean, after which I often feel drained and weakened.
>
> I have not adhered to any gradual protocol and am suddenly plunging into the cold - am I experiencing cortisol flooding and overwhelming stress as a result?
>
> I thought that the benefits would be experientally available through my attempts yet I think I am actually doing harm to myself.
>
> ...ge 27\]
> \[CT DURATION: 5-20 min\]
> \[ocean temp: around 60 degrees\]

Well, look at the bright side - when Dave Asprey went straight to ice baths, he landed in the hospital with hypothermia and frost bite...

Go slow, always -in fact, I have found that face dunks alone have some amazing benefits for the weak of flesh among us...

Drink 16 oz of water before CT - eat lots of fat/protein - if you can't hack the cold, maybe you have too much omega 6 fatty acids vs omega 3 - in my house, everyone is eating a SAD - when the water heater died, everyone was quite sad taking a cold shower but not me

I eat seafood every day and could care less about cold showers - it has something to do with your cell membranes - and seafood is great for your cell membranes -

### brent-next — 2017-02-12T12:56:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-non-gradual-ct-damaging.18914/post-208451>

> [ryan dawn said:](/goto/post?id=206019)
>
> I live in HH, SC and have been CTing in the ocean, after which I often feel drained and weakened.

I swam in the river every day last winter at 50 deg. north. When I started the water temperatures were about 12 C and when I stopped in January they were about 3 C and and people were chipping a hole in the ice to do it.

My time in the water was typically 15 to 30 seconds. One time I stayed in 10 deg. C water for four minutes and got yelled at by the locals. This is the eastern european tradition of winter swimming.

I did start with face dunking before I tried it and my first cold water swim was in and out as fast as I could.

I noticed a wild day to day variation in how good I felt after this. Some days I felt so good I thought I must be in heaven, other days, cold and slightly tired.

If I were to do this again, I would measure my blood sugar and ketones before each swim to try to make sure I had an ATP surplus. Measure again when you get out to see how much it has changed.

I'm guessing that if I started my swim slightly hypoglycemic without a good dose of ketones to power my swim, that is when I felt tired and cold.

If you come out of the ocean feeling drained and weakened you are probably not having the intended effect. Cut your time way down and see if you can trigger the good feelings.

Dx: Melasma (upper lip and some of my cheek) for the past 1.5 years. Near sighted -6.5 in one eye, -4.5 in the other. Tired sometimes at work, neck pains
\----------------------------------------------------------
Melasma is thought to be the stimulation of melanocytes (cells in the epidermal layer of skin that produce a pigment called melanin) by the female sex hormones estrogen and progesterone to produce more melanin pigments when the skin is exposed to sun.
The incidence of melasma increases in patients with thyroid disease.\[6\]
It is thought that the overproduction of melanocyte-stimulating hormone (MSH) brought on by stress can cause outbreaks of this condition.
\----------------------------------------------------------
Health Goals: Focus on work (programming), Body composition, Fertility, Vision, Melasma gone
\----------------------------------------------------------
**Age: 37------->Fertility---------> hurry up**

After a month get tested (FreeT3 and Cortisol,serum(7;30AM)
draw blood for this tests at 7:30AM before taking your daily NTH dose.
adjust dose after seeing test results.

\========================:

\===============================
You are starting low.
Good numbers in this area are
IGF-1=(220-300)ug/l
IGFBP3=3000ug/l
problem is that likely you have not used the desirable test.
Usually this tests are done using automatic chemolumonoscence technoque
much more reliable is manual RIA (Radioimmunoassay)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-lifeextension-labs-thus-far.18921/post-206244>

DHEA is an active hormone.
7keto-DHEA is different hormone than DHEA.
7keto-DHEA is known to be thermogenic, you may possibly feel warmer using it and be able to shed few pounds (maybe).
You will not raise DHEAs by eating 7keto-DHEA.

I do not know much about 7keto-DHEA, could use help.

You can find DHEA on simplified chart here (right side, blue):

You can find DHEA & DHEAs here.
There is also (I hope) 7keto-DHEA
but I do not know which one it is.

### JanSz — 2017-01-12T01:13:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-lifeextension-labs-thus-far.18921/post-206638>

> [SFBaySwimmer said:](/goto/post?id=206628)
>
> Oh, JanSz, I've been googling around for the right IGF-1 manual Radioimmunoassay test. I can't seem to find which company provides that, do you know of any?

The requirement about Radioimmunoassay type of testing comes from
Thierry Hertoghe The Hormone Book 2nd ed, page 58
He says that IGF-1 must be done using Radioimmunoassay, because chemoluminescence is not accurate.
(Assume) that IGFBP3 is ok any way it is done.

Really you want only IGF-1 and IGFBP-3.
But I had it done (2014) using:
Growth Assessment Test Group X (Growth Hormone, IGF-I, IGF-II, IGFBP-3)
Test Code 500901 ... CPT Code 83003, 84305, 83519, 83519
\===============================================================
Very recently LabCorp changed their website.

LabCorp have Esoterix division (Endocrine Sciences)
\-----------------------------------------------------------------------
I just went on LabCorp site and cannot find Group X
plus
I was not able to get IGFBP-3 done at Esoterix (Radioimmunoassay)

500632 Growth Hormone, RIA (Endocrine Sciences)
503660 Insulin-like Growth Factor 1 (IGF-1), Pediatric With Z Score (Endocrine Sciences)
141770 Insulin-like Growth Factor 2 (IGF-2) (Endocrine Sciences)

\------
Insulin-like Growth Factor-binding Protein 3 (IGFBP-3)
TEST: 140152
CPT: 83520
\--------------------------------------------------------------------------
To run your GH protocol you need just thise two, IGF-I, IGFBP-3
plus you need to know your goals.

Goal is to achieve
IGF-1~(300-350)ng/mL (men)
IGF-1~(220-300)ng/mL (women)
IGFBP3~3mg/L

If you can't get IGFBP3 exactly, then observe (IGF-1)/(IGFBP3) ratio, that would be (for me=men)
300/3=100, 350/3=116
Must be something really similar ti that for women.
At this time I do not remember on what page that was written, leave it for latter.

\--------------------
You really need the two tests,
but,
you must oblige your doctor.
I got above as described. Your doc may want something else.
He writes script for GH, he says what kind backup he wants.
Norditropin is really handy with its pen.

/////////////////

### JanetJewell — 2017-01-05T13:06:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/led-lighting.18926/post-206153>

Hi this Janet

# Thread: HIIT ideas, plans and discussion
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hiit-ideas-plans-and-discussion.18968/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2017-01-19T21:33:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hiit-ideas-plans-and-discussion.18968/post-207115>

Hello ladies and gentlemen,

this shall be a thread where we can collect ideas for different HIIT workouts and also how you can vary them and progress optimally.

I begin with a simple training idea based on the 40 yard sprints which are mentioned in the Leptin FAQ.
I often have not much time to go out for that and also in winter I dont want to do that on frozen, slippy ground.
So I used kettlebell swings for the same program idea that will look as following:

10 Swings x up to 20 times / break in between as long as you need to be able to talk

\-start with 2 hands and aim for maximum power output on every rep, hardstyle

### FNJORD — 2017-01-19T23:50:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hiit-ideas-plans-and-discussion.18968/post-207118>

Tabata squats or burpees = 20 seconds exercise as many as you can do + 10 seconds rest repeat 8 times.
There are youtube videos that have timers in them, which is useful.

### Chris James — 2017-04-28T17:13:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hiit-ideas-plans-and-discussion.18968/post-213046>

I have found that a circuit training type system works great for me. All exercises I do to failure or for 30 seconds, then rest for 10-20 seconds.

I start with pullups, then swings, dips, goblet squats, pushups on the dip bars and then kb deadlifts. I repeat this circuit 3 or 4 times.

# Thread: Common Cold, Cold Sores, and Chapped Lips
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/common-cold-cold-sores-and-chapped-lips.19001/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2017-01-28T20:35:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/common-cold-cold-sores-and-chapped-lips.19001/post-207482>

About a month ago, I had a cold (congestion, aches, sore throat, cough, but no fever). As far as I can remember, this is the only cold I have ever had and I only had the flu twice. About a week after the cold, I got cold sores (which I understand is fairly common). When the cold sores went away, I started having chapped lips. The only time I have had chapped lips before was from severely cold weather.

I asked my doc if this was an indicator of something that I should be concerned about. She said the cold sores were from the cold, and she understood what I was asking, but didn't know why I would have caught a cold for the 1st time.

Any ideas? Could it be as simple as being dehydrated?

ETA: I just turned 68, which is why I am suspect about these things happening for the 1st time.

ETA 2: I used campho-phenique on the cold sores and then used it on chapped lips. Could that be what is causing the chapped lips? I will stop using it and see if that helps.

# Thread: Contact senators about 5g - apparently they are voting soon...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/contact-senators-about-5g-apparently-they-are-voting-soon.19016/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2017-01-31T22:47:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/contact-senators-about-5g-apparently-they-are-voting-soon.19016/post-207684>

Dear Everyone,

US FCC wants to "streamline" the approval process for DAS (Distributed Antenna System) 4G/5G ubiquitous cell towers by exempting them from the 1996 Telecommunications Act!

And the US Senate will imminently be voting on S.19 and S.88, to expand DAS 4G/5G deployment!

If such efforts succeed, millions of small cell towers (on existing light poles/utility poles, or new ones) will be deployed everywhere, throughout residential neighborhoods. The millimeter microwave radiation deployed will produce horrific health effects, worsening EVERYONE's health, and will remove the remaining pollinators. It's THAT serious. The bees in particular will die off rapidly, per studies of bees and microwave radiation.

See link below for list of Senators and their info, by State, for ease in writing and phoning.

[**https://www.senate.gov/senators/contact/**](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=r9kx0sYbx5dVEvxtyHxKR7PObM0lZ1Uu)

Be sure to write and call your own senators' offices, and also contact as many others as possible.

**Email messages to senators:**

1.  Introduce yourself with as much expertise as possible, and if you are a constituent, say so.
2.  Explain that millimeter microwave radiation is a hazard, particularly to the eyes and brain. However, through S.88 and S.19 such radiation would be unleashed in a more potent way than all the prior usage of the electromagnetic spectrum throughout human history, combined. This will devastate health and the environment. Therefore the Senator must oppose these Bills.
3.  Explain that the public health science community has tried to halt previous microwave radiation deployments, based on the bioeffects science of nearly 25,000 studies.
4.  Insist on the scheduling of a public hearing prior to any vote. Tell the Aide these Bills must be blocked. The Senator is obligated to protect his/her constituents and not allow their assault by microwave irradiation.
5.  See below for more important info, before you write or call.

**Phone calls to senators:**

1.  Ask to speak with an Aide for environment or health.
2.  Introduce yourself with as much expertise as possible, and if you are a constituent, say so.
3.  Tell the legislative aides that you cannot have a cell tower in front of your home or workplace irradiating you with pulse-modulated millimeter microwaves, which are hazardous. This is what Senate Bills 19 and 88 would do! These Bills must be blocked!
4.  Insist on the scheduling of a public hearing prior to any vote.

**Use the below to provide more info.** And if possible, also go to your Senators' local offices, speak with the directors there, and ask them to block these Bills.

**IMPORTANT INFORMATION - READ BEFORE YOU CALL OR WRITE, PLEASE....**

Do NOT tell Senate office staffers "how you feel". You want to be effective and not waste your time, right?

RATHER, when informing the legislative aide ("LA") why his/her Senator absolutely must oppose S.19 and S.88:

1.  Provide FACTS about radiofrequency/microwave (RF/MW) radiation's adverse health and environmental effects, with a body of science nearly 25,000 studies strong.
2.  Elaborate substantively with the above, citing more than one study or set of studies (e.g., respectively [NTP](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=G%2FGZ9c7Kx1r3kFsy6d35NrPObM0lZ1Uu) or [BioInitiative](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=ZROA9%2BXMZWEf9iNn638EObPObM0lZ1Uu)): at least three (3) primary scientific sources _\[editor's note: scroll down to the bottom of this email for Josh's list\]._
3.  Mention that radiation from the wireless infrastructures such as are intended to be deployed by way of these Senate Bills 19 and 88 is highly unnatural, xenobiotic (foreign-to-life) radiation that is pulse-modulated. (If possible, say what you understand about harmful bioeffects of modulation.).
4.  Inform the Aide that this radiation from DAS - Distributed Antenna System - 4G/5G - is intended to deploy more bandwidth than has ever been deployed in all of human history, combined. State that the 4G/5G millimeter microwave radiation intended for deployment in all residential areas near bedrooms will not only cause horrific nervous system, heart, reproductive and other harm, including immediate suffering, especially to children, elders and persons with certain disabilities: it will also kill off all remaining pollinators, first and foremost bees, in part because of their size and thus maximal absorption of mm-wave radiation.
5.  Say that scientific consensus groups have been calling halt to wireless radiation infrastructure since 1997, and in blue-ribbon groups prior to that time.
6.  Remind the Aide that Congress amended the Public Health Service Act in 1968, requiring that the public "be protected against electronic product emissions." So there is simply no room at all for S.19 or S.88 to move forward. These Bills must be stopped, as they are in overt violation of this Congressional Amendment and of the most basic duties of Senators to protect and not physically and otherwise harm their constituents and the environment.
7.  Tell the Aide that various advocacy groups for health, environment, privacy and freedom from warrantless surveillance are demanding that Congress not shove these bills through without public knowledge or consent, but rather that public hearings be immediately scheduled. If indeed the new President wishes this Government to belong to the American People as he said numerous times in his inaugural address, then hearings must be called on each of these matters in relation to S.19 and S.88: health, environment, privacy and freedom from warrantless surveillance.

If the Legislative Aide says, "Thank you for expressing your concerns", respond:
"I have expressed no matter of mere personal concern, but rather solely facts and law: matters rising to the level of **assault, battery and other crimes against constituents and their home and external environments.** Kindly assure me you will properly note the record as such."

**PLEASE SEND THIS OUT TO ALL THOSE WHO CAN MAKE CALLS AND/OR WRITE SENATORS. TRY TO COMMUNICATE WITH AT LEAST 10 SENATORS' OFFICES, BETTER MORE.**

\* \* \* \* \*

Thank you for taking a moment to do this. Together we are becoming the inspiring change we all want to see.

Onward,
Josh del Sol

# Thread: Intutition, BO, Frozen Belly Fat, Too-Big Skin Suit [?s]
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intutition-bo-frozen-belly-fat-too-big-skin-suit-s.19018/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Imogen Skye — 2017-02-01T08:11:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intutition-bo-frozen-belly-fat-too-big-skin-suit-s.19018/post-207696>

Hi all

I started my ice bath Jan 8, 2017, and it is always under ~1C (33-34F), 35 minutes. Twice, I have had a very strong intuitive feeling to not do my bath. What reasons may there be to skip it for a day?

What is the reason for sudden armpit odour? I didn't sweat much before and have never had odour, and also have not masked it with products since I have used only citric acid in water, salt water, or coconut oil since 1998. But now suddenly I am having an issue. Is this detox? It comes suddenly and leaves just as quickly a few times a day randomly. I can't pin in on anything in my diet. Very _unpleasant_.

Today I did 1hr spot-icing on my belly pooch (I have the caesarian shelf  ), and there were areas under my skin that froze solid. The skin was nicely pink, malleable, and is still fine 8 hrs later. No pain. It felt like frozen fat. It was so solid that I could pull it away as a solid chunk of something under my skin and twist it and it retained its frozen form until it thawed fully. Is this dangerous somehow? I used frozen chickens I won't be eating but that sit neatly on my lap against my belly, while I read to my children.

Also, before my body shape was fairly smooth and no longer strong-looking (post-accident), but at least skinny-fat, and now I look very lumpy and squishy and feel like I'm wearing a too-big skin suit. My knees are baggy. I feel my skin sort of sloshing around as I move. Is this weightloss? I've never had this happen before and I've lost 25-40lbs postpartum, 5x, and this didn't happen.... Will I smooth out or am I now a milder version of a human sharpei? I won't quit; the benefits so far have been so amazing I don't care much, but just for future wardrobe planning....

Thanks for any replies!

### Inger — 2017-02-01T14:19:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intutition-bo-frozen-belly-fat-too-big-skin-suit-s.19018/post-207706>

Imogen, I think others too have had the skin thing appear after deep CT... I think you have frozen off some fat cells and they are about to disappear?
The iced belly thing is not dangerous I think. I did it once too, but mine really hurt as it thawed and I got blue marks on my belly as someone would have beaten me...lol But it healed in a few days. Just be careful to watch the skin does not turn white, I think that is when it is not a good thing anymore (mine did turn white and hard..)
You can read the CT rx, there it says it all, what to be careful about

Wow you are really tough to be so long time in that cold water! Wow, I could not do it... my max in that cold (it was -0,5 degree C) is 7 minutes.. and that was even too much...lol felt weird after.

BO might be detox as the fat cells are also a deposit for stuff we do not want in our bodies. Now when your fat melts away the stuff is freed into your blood maybe. Maybe that is why you feel you sometimes do not want to CT, because your body needs time to clear it all up, cant detox too much in one day


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Structuring Water dispenser

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2

### JCDC — 2017-02-02T11:22:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structuring-water-dispenser.19025/post-207772>

I have a home RO filter I have been drinking from for the past 3 years but I want to play around with some other options. I am having Mountain Valley Spring water delivered and I will occasionally fill a 5 gallon glass carboy from a local spring I found awn findaspring.com.

My question is what is the best option for a dispenser? I know ceramic over metal and electric has been mentioned in the past but can anyone point me in the direction of something that may add some vortexing/(running over beads, etc), magnets and/or infrared via amethyst (or other non nnemf emitting method)?

Or if not the dispenser itself another efficient method for increasing the health benefits of my water?

### JCDC — 2017-02-12T23:03:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structuring-water-dispenser.19025/post-208482>

I found this [https://www.structuredwaterunit.com/store/water-coolers/jivara-everstream-water-cooler](https://www.structuredwaterunit.com/store/water-coolers/jivara-everstream-water-cooler)

Very interesting if you ask me.

Seems pretty interesting. It offers water structuring and the addition of UV filter as well as, granular activated carbon and KDF 55 and high yield activated aluminum oxide specially formulated for the reduction of fluoride in drinking water.

# Thread: A simple little shack to call home......
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-simple-little-shack-to-call-home.19036/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2017-02-05T06:28:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-simple-little-shack-to-call-home.19036/post-207980>

For right now this is a theoretical exercise ....

We would like to buy a small piece of property .... relatively near the ocean - but in a secluded location.

I think a one big multi purpose room.

We would need to pay attention to the direction our shack faces to take optimal advantage of sunlight and the prevailing winds.

What kind of glass for windows?

What material for outside and inside?

Straw bale exterior?

what to do about electricity? I am thinking a kitchen and laundry in a small building by itself? not ideal - but workable..... maybe connected by a patio.

would we need electricity in our shack at all??? we now keep our breakers off except one for the fridge and a few outlets on the same breaker. We are quite accustomed to no lights at night.

maybe even a separate outdoor bath and toilet? an outdoor shower for sure.

maybe a tree house?

Any thoughts? What would you do if you could?

what materials would you use? even simple furnishings and how they are made/materials used have to be considered.

### fitness@home — 2017-02-05T13:40:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-simple-little-shack-to-call-home.19036/post-207990>

A separate kitchen would help keep the main living space cooler during summer when cooking (like in the old days).

Not keen on a separate outdoor bath and toilet, but that's just my personal preference.

Will you have a fireplace for cooler nights? I've always wanted a small built in "oven" space that was heated by the fireplace. Dual use!

A big covered porch for relaxing on and an open air patio for sunlight!

### Martin — 2017-02-05T15:57:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-simple-little-shack-to-call-home.19036/post-207992>

What fun! I'd do a masonry house with as much natural materials as possible. Block with stucco, maybe the newer lightweight blocks available? No vapor barrier in the concrete floor, no rebar or reinforcing wire, any electric circuits would be twisted pairs of wires in metal conduit with thoughtful runs to avoid exposure to fields, metal roof? or natural play tile?, no straw bale -(mold prone?, dusts?), Quartz windows whenever possible ($!), place utilities room - heat, mains panel, as far away as possible, south... oops, NORTH facing for solar, isolated bedroom with expert grounding capability, magnesium-based drywall where needed?

### caroline — 2017-02-06T00:16:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-simple-little-shack-to-call-home.19036/post-208023>

Martin- my man! glad you showed up! great ideas!

would we have solar panels? but they are connected to the grid - right?

stone floors?

Why masonry? not timber?

stone floors?

### caroline — 2017-02-06T00:17:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-simple-little-shack-to-call-home.19036/post-208024>

How are you doing Martin? we need an update.......

### Martin — 2017-02-06T18:44:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-simple-little-shack-to-call-home.19036/post-208062>

A/C ! My Woman! Solar panels seem good if you can isolate and shield from EMF's as much as possible. I haven't looked into DC from solar panels as an alternative to pulsing, vibrating, alternating current as a safer replacement for the ubiquitous A/C power. I would if I built a new place. Look into the maintenance req'd for solar setups.

Wood is good (I'm chuckling at my superfluous writing abilities!) but the less organic materials for mold to grow on was what I was looking at back when. Consider the humidity of your area and your present susceptibility to myco-crap. NO metal framing in the walls (but you knew that) so wood can be a choice if masonry walls are not desired inside. Is that what you mean by "timber"? Or the main beams in the ceiling? Tell us more.

Stone is good, just as long as there is an unimpeded link to Earth as much as possible.

An update is brewing...

M.

### Penny — 2017-02-06T20:34:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-simple-little-shack-to-call-home.19036/post-208068>

If you go solar be aware that there is a wifi meter that phones home - something about selling power back to the utility - and once you go there, you can't take it out -

### fitness@home — 2017-02-06T21:13:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-simple-little-shack-to-call-home.19036/post-208070>

How about a small CT pond or tank? You could also build it to provide hot water made with a fire. Fournier effect for you outside - no matter what season!

I used to live in a 100 year old home and my _then-husband_ converted the hot-water heat to a wood burning shack outside. It was plumbed to run into the basement and then through the distribution system/radiators. Worked like a charm.

### Huck — 2017-02-06T21:50:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-simple-little-shack-to-call-home.19036/post-208074>

I've owned this book for about 10 years and seriously considered building a small "green" house. There is a newer version, but this is the one I have. [https://www.amazon.com/Building-Green-Complete-How-Alternative/dp/1579905323/ref=sr\_1\_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1486410412&sr=8-2&keywords=straw bale, cob,
](https://www.amazon.com/Building-Green-Complete-How-Alternative/dp/1579905323/ref=sr_1_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1486410412&sr=8-2&keywords=straw+bale%2C+cob%2C)
Definitely consider solar. You can get most appliances as 12v. Challenges would be refrigerator and air conditioning; otherwise, you wouldn't need but 1 or 2 panels.

I can easily run my lights, water pump, and charge all my devices with 1 100 w solar panel mounted on the roof of my rv trailer. Refrigerator runs on propane (or 12v or 110v). I have a problem you wouldn't have. You can build your house using passive solar techniques and use shade in the summer. When I go camping at the beach, there is no shade, so AC is a must.

You wouldn't want to connect to the grid. Solar prices have come down enough where you could afford a small, standalone solar system.

### Penny — 2017-02-06T23:28:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-simple-little-shack-to-call-home.19036/post-208079>

I wonder if you could actually build something with plaster - like a metal mesh like chicken wire when they dangle a 5g antennae outside my bedroom window... I wonder how small the mesh would have to be to reflect that brand of wifi - like they say in this article:
[https://www.mistralsolutions.com/dos-donts-wi-fi-connectivity-maximizing-range-reception/](https://www.mistralsolutions.com/dos-donts-wi-fi-connectivity-maximizing-range-reception/)

The first thing to do is to check the construction of the walls. In theory, Wi-Fi signals are capable of passing through walls and other obstacles relatively easily. However, in reality, some walls are thicker or use reinforced concrete and may block some of the signals. Materials such as drywall, plywood, other kinds of wood and glass can be easily penetrated by wireless signals. However, materials such as brick, plaster, cement, metal, stone, and double-glazed glass may cause problems. The following facts should therefore be kept in mind:
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Metal bodies absorb Wi-Fi signals. Therefore, wireless surveillance solutions do not guarantee connectivity between floors of buildings and between thick reinforced concrete walls

If the walls are made of non-porous materials, your wireless connection may have a shorter range or a slower speed

Elevators block Wi-Fi signals to a great extent. When placing an IP camera; make sure the elevator does not come between the camera and the Wireless Access Point

Tinted glass panes carry metal constituents. So if you have tinted glasses anywhere between WAP and the camera, you can expect a drop in signal strength.

So, turn my house into a mission with 2ft thick walls and I can survive my impending doom...

### Scompy — 2017-02-07T07:33:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-simple-little-shack-to-call-home.19036/post-208099>

Why stop at just 1 antenna?

"In a two-year joint project, the IBM and Ericsson researchers packed key components of an antenna module, such as power amplifiers, into a single IC (integrated circuit). Then they combined four of those ICs, plus 64 solid-state silicon antennas, in a package that measures 2.8 inches (7cm) on each side."

[http://www.pcworld.idg.com.au/article/613860/5g-starts-chips-like-ibm-ericsson-silicon-antenna/](http://www.pcworld.idg.com.au/article/613860/5g-starts-chips-like-ibm-ericsson-silicon-antenna/)

Regarding nnEmf through walls, I would consider using iron bearing rock such as basalt in the walls if available. Charcoal and various graphitic substances are rf absorbers. A plaster coating with such substances mixed into the mix should work well. You can make the charcoal yourself as well.
