### Matt Wilson — 2017-03-08T14:11:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-a-stab-at-these-labz.19186/post-209863>

Hey Kruseaders... I’m an athletic 31, 5’10” and 150 lbs and need some help deciphering my WellnessFX Comprehensive labs…

My lifestyle is pretty damn strict, but I am on the computer 5+ hours per day (grounded) and have 15 employees, so there is some stress…. I eat very keto; eggs, fish, avocados, and sauerkraut at most meals. I strength train 3x/week, meditate 30-60min daily, drink R/O, tan my @$$ at 7am every morning, wear my blueblockers, and haven't taken a warm shower in over a year...

**I’m a concerned about my Thyroid and hormone panel…** T3, Free T3, and IGF1 are a little low, although Testosterone comes in at 1191 ng/dl, Androgen at 520 ug/dL, and SHBG at 60 nmo/L (all high!) Also notable when talking about Thyroid is my Total Iron Binding Capacity is 229 ug/dL (low).

My theory is that low body fat (less than 8%), combined with intermittent fasting, long hours, and lots of travel, has pointed me in the direction of hypothyroidism.... My high levels of testosterone aren't converting into Free Testosterone all that well, and my LargeHDL is low. _I'm guessing not enough carbs and calories has put my body into "you're starving mode" instead of "make babies and relax mode"..._

This was deduced from listening to [Ben Greenfield decipher his own labs](https://bengreenfieldfitness.com/2014/05/most-comprehensive-blood-test/) + [Elle Russ’s Paleo Thyroid Solution](https://bengreenfieldfitness.com/2017/01/the-paleo-thyroid-solution-book-with-elle-russ/), but maybe I’m wrong…

I know this drawing of blood is just a snapshot of this a moment in my life, but any insights on this would be appreciated! Attached are my labs.

### kris90 — 2017-03-09T20:41:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-a-stab-at-these-labz.19186/post-209966>

I think you've identified the problem. Your body is in starvation mode.

I actually just recently discovered this myself after battling hypogonadism for several years in addition to having high TSH levels. I think ketosis is meant for mammals who are carrying a lot of fat. Trying to do the ketosis/cold adaptation when you are already lean seems to never end well atleast in my experience. IMO, this superhuman fat burning/heat generating ability of the cold adapted pathway can't work its magic in absence of excess body fat. Works wonders for diabetics or others with metabolic syndrome, but not lean, ripped guys.

I think aside from light, glucose status is one of the driving factors that determine whether we are in "rest/digest" or "fight/flight". If you don't have much bodyfat, you need a constant supply of glucose, and exogenous is preferential. Why? Because there's too little triglycerides in adipose tissue to provide glucose substrate for the body, so bodily tissue (i.e. muscle, bone) gets broken down for glucose through the action of cortisol.

Since I stopped counting calories, and focused on eating as much quality food as possible with a macronutrient ratio somewhere around 40% Fat/40% Carbs/20% Protein. I'm eating a ton of sugar from fruits, honey, cane sugar, chocolate, ice cream, and my fats are mostly saturated from animal sources, butter and coconut oil. I've limited my PUFA intake to below 10g per day, I've been feeling like a million bux after just 2 short weeks.

I didn't have high cortisol in this set of labs, but with IGF1 low, it makes sense that my body needs more carbs for insulin production.

I'd be thrilled to know what anyone else thinks about my labs. Thanks!

My 2c
I would argue (only a little) about GH--->cancer connection.
My 78yo friend, (near) of end stages of prostate cancer.
Unchecked his testosterone raises to 1000ng/dl.
End stage is when cancer cells are able to make its own testosterone,
he is not there yet, but had metastasis that was gamma knife radiated.
While trying lots of hacks, it was easy for him to try GH (his son uses it)
Immediately he saw sharp raise in PSA. So hi stopped GH.
Me. 77yo. 10 years ago had a surgery in my left thigh.
Liposarcoma.
After surgery had yearly doctors examinations (just by his touch).
Now told to come in 3 years.
Tried GH, see no effect with small doses.
Unfortunately, thanks to **Obama Care** I have lost a doctor who was willing to prescribe it to me.
My current observation:
Yes, GH should not be used in some cancers,
but
it is too valuable tool,
to not make serious evaluation about its use.
**There is a chance,
that similarly to testosterone
in the right situation
its use may prevent development of disease.**
\===================================================
@[Matt Wilson](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19871/)
You have some items that are not usually seen.
Natural very high Total Testosterone
Natural very high DHEAs
Natural very low GH
Natural very high SHBG
Likely you have high Androstenedione since DHEAs-->DHEA-->Androstenedione-->Estrone

You are very lean, little fat.
Aromatase enzyme that is mostly in the fat, converts
Testosterone--->Estradiol (E2=30pg/mL ok)
Androstenedione>Estrone
Presence of estrogens gives raise to SHBG
presence of testosterone lovers SHBG
since
you have little fat, likely your estrogens are low and then I have to wonder why your SHBG is high, it does not look like it is from excessive estrogens. Something else. SHBG is made in the liver (and little bit in testicles)
But check
estrone
Total Estrogens
.....

**Testosterone doesn't convert well to Free T (seen in my labs)
**
**Wrong conclusion.**
Or at least not how it works.
\===================================
Available testosterone is divided into three parts.

1\. Strongly bound testosterone (attached to SHBG)
2\. Weakly bound testosterone (attached to Albumin)
3\. Remaining testosterone, (Free Testosterone)

When testosterone activity is considered it is assumed that it is due to
(Free plus weakly bound) testosterone

[https://www.labcorp.com/test-menu/35486/testosterone-free-and-weakly-bound](https://www.labcorp.com/test-menu/35486/testosterone-free-and-weakly-bound)
Testosterone, Free and Weakly Bound

Free and weakly bound testosterone (FWBT),
also referred to as bioavailable testosterone,
is thought to reflect an individual's biologically active,
circulating testosterone.
FWBT includes free testosterone and testosterone that is bound to albumin.
FWBT does not include sex hormone binding globulin-bound testosterone.
The SHBG-bound fraction is biologically inactive because of the high binding affinity of SHBG for testosterone.
The rapid dissociation of “weakly bound” testosterone from albumin results in the availability of essentially all albumin-bound testosterone for steroid-receptor interaction.1
\-----------------

IIRC, to disassociate testosterone from SHBG it takes 30-40 minutes
(could use better info)
\=================================================

# Thread: Vitamin E and OxLDL
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-e-and-oxldl.19187/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 3

### CoolSwede — 2017-03-08T16:47:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-e-and-oxldl.19187/post-209886>

Attached to my latest labs, there was a lab suggestion of high dose Vit E and betacarotenes in order to reduce my sky high OxLDL.

In this post @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) says that exogenous Vit E should be avoided. [which vit E supplement is based on olive oil](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/which-vit-e-supplement-is-based-on-olive-oil.18252/#post-199313)

Is vitamin E a friend or foe in my case?

(Of course I would like to find out which gear in my mitochondrial engine that is faulty instead of supplementing...)

### CoolSwede — 2018-03-01T11:01:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-e-and-oxldl.19187/post-236026>

Ok, so here I am almost one year later, asking the same question since my latest labs once again came back sky high after a trend of decreasing values. 2095 ug/l in a reference range of 20-170 makes me uneasy, to say the least...

All conventional wisdom says "throw in antioxidants" so it is tempting to biohack Vit E and betacarotenes. But at the same time, it feels like hoping a fuel additive in a Ferrari (or rather Volvo) tank would magically fix a blown piston...

### Martha Ray — 2021-01-12T04:55:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-e-and-oxldl.19187/post-294547>

Agree about focus on mitochondria. Seems like more of a root cause focus to me too.

Re: Supplements
Your 2018 comments bring up my own questions about Vit E and the pro and con information out today just past 2020 about antioxidants (Ronda Patrick PhD).
I found this You Tube video, I like Dr. Jay Davidson but a m not sure about these conclusions.
**Do Not Supplement with Vitamin E - Genetics Genomics - Dr. Jay Davidson**

Here is another online article 2005 I found [https://www.geneticlifehacks.com/vitamin-e-genetics-and-inflammation/](https://www.youtube.com/redirect?stzid=UgxOgb3K75ubHbIk1sF4AaABAg&event=comments&q=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.geneticlifehacks.com%2Fvitamin-e-genetics-and-inflammation%2F&redir_token=QUFFLUhqbXl6bGxxcFZCUnprT0RrOHhNRG5RYjh5emJRQXxBQ3Jtc0trWDZDbGowRU5ST3dJclEwWGpMek9GSTlBNFdBZ2NpdFlmNXFaSGFjSEUyR3EyS0xITm9feEsta1hDSFZVazZDdTZaRGU2S3p4a21zc0pKVkNrT0Q1RTBSLVI1NTFkNzNGcS1mMkZDU1pOX1ZvaWtpYw%3D%3D) I am also sure the form of Vit E matters, quality and perhaps there are other environmental or nutrient co-factors that are still unknown.

I would like to get away from taking synthetic supplements and I prefer 100% food and herbal supplements, but at this time I do take a lot of all types of supplements and have felt good results with a number of them.

Wondering if you have learned more about Vit E?

# Thread: Why do we crave oxidizing and dehydrating agents
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-we-crave-oxidizing-and-dehydrating-agents.19194/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### drezy — 2017-03-10T04:16:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-we-crave-oxidizing-and-dehydrating-agents.19194/post-210004>

Hi all. I'm reading through Epi-Paleo RX and watching as much Jack Kruse youtube as I can find.

Can someone point me to any material to help me better understand why we seem to crave oxidizing and dehydrating agents?

When I think about all addictions to substances, they are either oxidizers, dehydrators, or often both. Epi-Paleo RX(Chapter 5) has a mention of the occasional usage of red wine to boost endotoxin binding.

Aside from maybe some wild corner cases or poor pica sufferers I don't know about nobody in history that seems to have ever been compulsively addicted to hydrating or antioxidants. What is it about the substances we abuse that draws us to them while they demolish our mitochondria?

The only answer I've come up with is that in some weird Malthusian way people are self selecting themselves out of evolution. That's a bit creepy even for me.

### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2017-03-10T09:00:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-we-crave-oxidizing-and-dehydrating-agents.19194/post-210023>

I think people are addicted to their frequency of reality. I'm bumping this thread because I find myself addicted to a lower frequency, achieved by eating junk etc, and is interested in explanations.

### drezy — 2017-03-10T23:15:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-we-crave-oxidizing-and-dehydrating-agents.19194/post-210072>

Wow! The forum really is a goldmine of information and I'm glad to have it.

@[Nicolaj Sølvsten](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19289/) because you are also interested I'll share the breadcrumb trail I've picked up so far:

Doctor Kruse often mentions Nora Volkow. Here is a good starting point for Volkow's work(gets juicy 7-8 min in but it's all worth it):

Searching the forum with "Volkow" will point you toward her more detailed and related work.

Dopamine RX:
[https://www.jackkruse.com/the-dopamine-rx-good-choices-or-bad/](https://www.jackkruse.com/the-dopamine-rx-good-choices-or-bad/)
pay particular attention to:

> Dopamine codes for repetition of good choices for survival in our species, in case you were wondering. That repetition does not have to be all positive or all negative. It can be both of a combo of both and still help us in many obtuse ways. The better balanced the system is, the better choices we make and the more likely it is we will survive a long time without illness.

These are starting points. If you find anything interesting down this rabbit hole please post it here.

# Thread: List biohacking gears/METHOD you follow
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/list-biohacking-gears-method-you-follow.19198/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nija — 2017-03-10T23:50:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/list-biohacking-gears-method-you-follow.19198/post-210077>

I have hard times to find list of biohacking gear/Tools suggested by Dr JK .
As a new user , I keep searching and searching .I am listing here few after searching various thread .

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) @[fitness@home](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5474/) @[Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/)

-   Computer blue blocking glass

-   Red light for night

-   Grounded Laptop

-   Magnetico
-   morning light for winter >[http://www.withings.com/us/en/products/aura](http://www.withings.com/us/en/products/aura)

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-11T11:26:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/list-biohacking-gears-method-you-follow.19198/post-210100>

Hi Nija

My rule of thumb is to spend smarter, not harder.  I'm willing to spend the cash if I understand what something is doing, and what its not doing, and any negative implications of using it at the wrong time, or for too long. I find it a huge temptation to spend too long in the wrong environment, because I think my biohacking gear is making me safe .... and it isn't, at best I'm just not getting as damaged as I was before I made the changes. Its not just about the visual colour of lamps, its also about circadian rhythm, (what does say a 4 hour fake sunset do to it?) nnemf, resonance, unexpected spikes of blue that aren't visually obvious, flicker (i.e. what do my mitochondria "see" that my visual system isn't aware of)...........If I worked in an office all day under fluorescent lights, blue lit screens, nnemf etc, my protection and mitigation choices would be different. I think its about looking at the whole, not through a hole.

I also don't see any point in getting anything where I won't be sure what the results mean, how the environment my mitochondria faced impacted the readings, or what to do about it if it if the results look "bad", it just becomes another source of stress.

To test how much difference it makes before investing in a proper blackout blind, I just made an extra blackout curtain from an old blanket clipped to the existing curtain with clothes pegs. It gave me the information I wanted, and I can choose to buy a proper blackout curtain or blind, or if money was tight it might not look as good, but what I've got does the job. I wouldn't spend 200 dollars on a fancy clock that when I started on my journey I might have bought, but would have finished up out in the shed with my other mistakes .... that same 200 dollars will get me two months gold membership, with access to all the webinars, and Q and A's. The cold vest price could get me another month, and there's other ways to do it cheaper .... Hell just not being a slave to the latest food fads, tests that might not be necessary or trying to supplement my way out of a hole pays for my membership ....And those webinars and Q&A's are what give me the information to make better decisions about the purchases I do make. My membership is my ultimate biohacking tool.

My two recent investments were a Cornet to assess my home for things I might be missing .... that was well worth the money. My other purchase was a cheap spectroscope (under £10) which doesn't give me info on spikes if the light source covers the same range, but does show a spike in a colour that is otherwise not there and gives me a better general idea of the colour ranges that my lights and screens are emitting. The results from both gadgets were surprising .... changed a couple of things I was doing, and a couple more things headed for the shed .....

I have a light and colour therapy system (had it years but it would cost me around £2000 if I needed to replace it), but if I didn't have it or when I need to replace it, I would get a Quantlet, because I understand how it works (and compared to my system is a bargain .... and I do love a photonic bargain.).

### Lahelada — 2017-03-12T02:26:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/list-biohacking-gears-method-you-follow.19198/post-210168>

A most excellent post.Seconding everything Sue said.

### nija — 2017-03-13T05:29:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/list-biohacking-gears-method-you-follow.19198/post-210229>

@[Sue](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/854/) .. I love ur all post .
Can you please provide your cheap/Alternative biohacking method ?For example without cold vest or 20/30 lb ice , how do u di CT ?
Please suggest tour few alternative biohack

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-13T10:23:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/list-biohacking-gears-method-you-follow.19198/post-210232>

> [nija said:](/goto/post?id=210229)
>
> @[Sue](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/854/) .. I love ur all post .
> Can you please provide your cheap/Alternative biohacking method ?For example without cold vest or 20/30 lb ice , how do u di CT ?
> Please suggest tour few alternative biohack

I have 2 baths in the garden (I'm rural and have the space). One is a metal small seated one, cost about £10? at an auction. I have it so that seated I'm facing east. The second is a conventional fibreglass one that I nabbed from my neighbours when they were putting in a new bathroom. In that one I can face east in the morning (plug end), but its more for west in the evening, and its easier to float on the top of the water to harvest photons. I sometimes get in that one first, then as it warms up I get out and go to the second one. When I started if the water was too warm I used plastic bottles to freeze water as ice cubes, but I don't do that now, I take what the environment gives. In the summer the water is coolest at dawn, and I get the early morning light at the same time. If I get out at first light in the summer, I can fit in an hour, and its a lovely meditative experience. In winter when the water is colder, I am in for a lot less time as sunrise is much later, but the stimulus is much stronger, so even if its a few minutes after having to break the ice with hammer, I still love it. The only time I won't do it, is if its a full blown storm or a very cold strong wind. I even like outside CT in the pouring rain as long as there's a reasonable light stimulus. I am lucky enough to be able to CT nude unless I have visitors, then I use a tan through costume. (A good investment). If I had to CT indoors I would probably do it in the evening, using plastic bottles to get it colder before I got in, and perhaps use candlelight, try to meditate, get more out of it than just getting the cold stimulus, but nothing beats being able to watch the clouds, listen to the birds and watch the seasons change ... But either way is working with what I've got, or can do cheaply.

In terms of an ice vest, it would be too limiting for me. A friend who sews made me an apron that fits two freezer packs (belly cooling). I have a migraine cap that I keep in the freezer. (Cost £30, my most expensive CT purchase, I bought another for a hack I was doing at the time, but for my current hack one is enough). I can use it anytime, but my current hack is to put it on when I'm getting ready for bed, and for a few minutes when I first get into bed, to help cool my CSF to help signal sleep. Then I just take it off, and to stay horizontal I slide it just under the bed, and put it back in the freezer in the morning. I also have a shoulder cold pack, that goes from the shoulder down the back, stimulating the brown fat area. I have a couple of cold wraps that I strap round my wrists. But one cap, belly cooling apron packs, the shoulder wrap and wrist straps costs less than a vest would cost me, and if money is an issue they don't all need to be bought at once. I can target something specifically, (blood cooling through the wrist), stimulating brown fat, cooling CSF, unwanted belly fat etc etc... or put them on in any combination, or all together. If I've worked on the computer I can put on the cap, pull the flaps down in front of my closed eyes and rest them, put the wrist straps on ..etc etc. Far more possibilities than a vest ....

### nija — 2017-03-13T23:13:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/list-biohacking-gears-method-you-follow.19198/post-210283>

Copying from another thread just for reference . Please feel free to add

1\. Biohack DHA/Seafood (Oysters from PacNW, Shrimp from Florida, Salmon from Norway, grassfed foods/butter ~ 7x per week, staples in diet): For increase magnetism and mitrochondrial funneling of electrons for optimal energy use in body tissues
2\. Biohack Cysteine (Garlic, Asparagus, Bell Pepper, Egg, Chicken ~ staples in diet): Hardest to find amino acid in nature to help with energy management with Glutathione.
4\. Biohack Gut flora boosting (Probiotic and Saukroot)
5\. Biohack Gaining Electrons (RO Water, Taking flights of stairs down holding metal-railing connected to ground, 30mins minimum Grounding/earthing, etc.) Replenishment of electrons to compensate for electrons suckage from environment
6\. Biohack nnEMF (Backreflecting RF-EMF with silver/copper threaded clothing & caps on brain, mitigating lots of tech, hardwiring connections in home instead of Wifi, etc.) Less overall suckage like the leech in the sky that it is
7\. Biohack Light (Morning light exposure, using certain types of indoor lights at various times, blue-blocking with amber glasses, F.lux, etc.) Controlling light for optimal use in the time of day .2-3 hours of sun . Primarly morning and evening
8.CT on my eyes several times a day . CT face dunk in water and old ie pad
9\. Sungazing --->Sun gazing 20 min a day bare foot .During my sun sessions, I always looked into the direction of the sun
10 . Daily mindfulness practice 20 min
11\. Upgrade my water source( RO or spring water)
12.Change my lighting environment: I want to get UV bulbs and red lights inside of my room, maybe see if I can utilize a type of light source that can wake me up like the sun does as well?

All I changed was adding the sunshine/grounding in the morning,
eating more protein in breakfast,
more seafood and reducing the nnEMF (which I need to do more).
I will focus a lot on grounding and sunlight and CT
blue blockers Extremely important sine I am feeling better sleep and relax eye.

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-14T22:00:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/list-biohacking-gears-method-you-follow.19198/post-210358>

> [nija said:](/goto/post?id=210283)
>
> Copying from another thread just for reference . Please feel free to add
>
> 7\. Biohack Light (Morning light exposure, using certain types of indoor lights at various times, blue-blocking with amber glasses, F.lux, etc.) **Controlling light for optimal use in the time of day** .2-3 hours of sun . Primarly morning and evening
>
> 12.Change my lighting environment: I want to get UV bulbs and red lights inside of my room, **maybe see if I can utilize a type of light source that can wake me up like the sun does as well?**

This picture might help save you wasting money on artificial lighting that (apart from any eye irritating flicker, blue light spikes you can't see, nnemf or potential mercury resonance) is giving either a signal at the wrong time, or leaving your circadian rhythm stuck at a particular time of day.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2017-3-14_17-16-56-png.4514/)
Although the red frequencies might predominate at dawn, there is enough blue to switch off melatonin. There is a crescendo of light up to noon, and then a decrescendo. The sun gives the right signals at the right time, no errors.

CT 7 is also a good blog to read about how light influences hormones etc as the day progresses ...... I found Nonchalant's drawing she did in relation to that blog helpful too, its on page 14 post 270 of her journal "Goodbye City Life."

If I left for and arrived at work in darkness, I'd work harder at finding a good artificial set up to use before I left. If the lighting I worked under all day was crap and I could do nothing about it, its about mitigation. At home my current hack is to keep all artificial lights off in the day now that spring has arrived, and go out for regular breaks in the natural light. I think of it as an animal who goes out to collect food and takes it back into the den for her cubs. She is regularly going back out and sampling the natural changing environment. Maybe nuts, but at least with no flicker and blue spikes its easier on my eyes.

# Thread: Stuck and Scared: TRT and Fertility
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuck-and-scared-trt-and-fertility.19202/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 29
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MatRusLab — 2017-03-11T11:20:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuck-and-scared-trt-and-fertility.19202/post-210099>

Is there anything to help bring back sperm production if on TRT?

I ask that question first just to save time and it is where I am at. I realize I am not asking the right question yet and need some help.

My wife and I would really like to have children. And have been trying for a few years. There was a pregnancy a few years back that miscarried. That was prior to TRT.

I feel kind of running out of time. My wife is going to be 40. I am turning 38. This is a bigger long term problem that needs correcting.

I am wondering if there are some thoughts about how to make the best short term modifications and maybe a longer term plan.

We realized recently that I am producing ZERO sperm and this is probably related to TRT.

I have read about HCG being able to possibly preserve production while on TRT.

My Doc does not feel comfortable using HCG and referred me to endocrinologist.

I saw endo who wanted me to stop TRT completely for a few months to see what would happen.

I did try to to cut back. 1 less pump per day was okay. Did that for a few weeks. 2 pumps less per day and I became very emotional and nonfunctional.

I am limited financially by what can be done to correct this to insurance mostly as far as treatment. I cannot afford to order HCG online.

I know it is not ideal to be on TRT. I have been on it a few years now. I really did not educate myself on it.

A bit stuck and scared of how to proceed.

Also scared to ask here but I know this is the smartest place to be even though I am already not doing what I should be doing by taking testosterone.

Thoughts are appreciated. Please be gentle.

Matt

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-11T13:29:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuck-and-scared-trt-and-fertility.19202/post-210103>

Hi Matt

I would suggest you start a journal in the Optimal Journal section. Give as much info as possible, and what your home and work environment is like in terms of nnemf, blue light hazards, wifi etc. Also if you have made any changes as result of listening to any of the podcasts, or reading blogs or anything on the forum. Have you read [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/) ? If not I hope it gives you a sense of the possibilities ......

Welcome to the forum

LH--->testosterone
FSH--->sperm
HCG--->similar to LH (but not to FSH) (already expensive)
HMG--->similar to FSH (actually expensive)

Sometime ago, prior to TRT, you were making sperm, got your wife pregnant, she miscarried.

Now, you are not making sperm because supplemental testosterone shut out your LH and FSH production.

You must understand that you have two problems at hand not just one.

Using Testosterone (without) simultaneous use of HCG, destroys testicles.
Testicles and scrotum, in some men, shrink to nothing.

......................

for currently born
someone who may get born despite this great difficulties will have much greater chances to have high head winds
this is the place where love should start talking

### JanSz — 2017-03-11T22:35:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuck-and-scared-trt-and-fertility.19202/post-210159>

HCG gotten over internet is just as good, easier to get and may cost less depending on insurance and how much you have to beg your doctor to give you script and how often he feels that you should come and beg.

In normal life, aiming just to maintain physical shape and size of scrotum and testicles,
you may want to think of 800-1000iu/week average dose, divided at least 2x/week
You can just watch the effects and adjust dose.
You do not want excessive HCG dose, for some it raises E2, estradiol.
High E2 & E1 = loss of erections, gyno, emotional distress.

When buying you prefer in vials over ampoules. Larger cheaper.
10000iu is usually largest, but I have heard of 11000iu.
Think of yearly supply to make life easier.
If you end up with ampoule you have to purchase sterile vials.
Get also
Bacteriostatic Water (BW)
Box or boxes of 31Ga 5/16 insulin syringes 3/10cc for injections
and
5-10 or more 3, 5 or 10 mL syringes with needle (larger gauge), for mixing
currently I use
10000iu HCG
450iu each shot
so I get 22 shots
assumed volume of each shot=10iu
required volume of added BW=222.22units=2.23mL
after mixing, I draw all into insulin syringes
then put them into tea glass and into freezer
take one out as need

...
Fertility mode is different, look up that chart.

...

### JanSz — 2017-03-14T22:50:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuck-and-scared-trt-and-fertility.19202/post-210360>

> [MatRusLab said:](/goto/post?id=210356)
>
> Hey Jan,
>
> I've been trying to figure out how much to buy. Thank you again for what you have provided here.
>
> I think I understand this part...
>
> 10000iu HCG
> 450iu each shot
> so I get 22 shots
>
> 1 vial of 10k will make 22 @ 450iu so if I did 450 every 3 days for example I could go 66 days at that dosage. Which I have seen in other places that is an amount that can help.
>
> I don't understand this part
>
> assumed volume of each shot=10iu
>
> In the graphic from earlier in this thread it says 2000iu would that be per dose? Like a 10000iu vial would be for 5 doses?
>
> Sorry this is so new to me.
>
> Matt

We are discussing here two protocols.
Fertility protocol----2000iu per single injection (3 injections per week)
Long term HCG supplementation to maintain size of testicles and scrotum----450iu per single injection. Two of 450iu injections per week
Year =52 weeks
one HCG vial gives 22 shots good for 11 weeks
52/11=4.7
Five ampoules are good for 5\*11=55 weeks (or slightly over one year) for maintenance protocol.
\-------------------------
HCG is a powder.
To be injected it is mixed with Bacteriostatic Water (BW).
Amount of water is not fixed.
One can use more or less BW.
When mixing you must assume some amount of BW.
HCG powder does not increase or decrease volume of mixture.
I assumed 10iu volume of each 450iu shot.
In above sentence I used iu twice. They are not the same.
iu in regard to injection volume is a mark on insulin syringe (marking volume).
There are three types of insulin syringes, 1cc, 0.5cc and 3/10cc (that I suggested for you to use)
On any insulin syringe there are marks.
Most 3/10cc syringes have marks to mark half unit.
1mL=1cc=100iu
so 10iu on insulin syringe =0.1cc
The other iu is in regard to HCG.
You likely are going to buy vials containing 10000iu worth of HCG powder.
From each HCG vial, you are going to have 22shots, each shot will have a volume of 10iu=0.1cc
You will need BW 22x10=220 units (add half unit for wetting walls)

////////////

### JanSz — 2017-03-15T00:35:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuck-and-scared-trt-and-fertility.19202/post-210367>

This post is about testicle and scrotum maintenance only, by using 450iu shots of HCG done twice a week.
Assumptions:
You are buying HCG powder that is contained in vials.
There is 10000iu worth of powder in that vial.
You have:
Bacteriostatic water
31Ga 3/10cc insulin syringes
mixing syringes 3, 5 or 10cc with 1" or 1.5" long needle
calculations
there is enough poder to make 10000/450=22 shots
assume that each shot have a volume of 10iu=0.1cc=0.1mL=10units
therefore, combined, 22 shots have total volume of 2.2mL=220units (add half a unit for extra)
put
225units=2.25mL BW into mixing syringe
insert needle into vial, put all water in
rotate gently vial to mix, do not shake
fill up all 22 insulin syringes, each up to line indicating 0.1cc
put all syringes into freezer
take syringe out of freezer as need
///

### kris90 — 2017-03-15T12:50:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuck-and-scared-trt-and-fertility.19202/post-210393>

I was diagnosed with secondary hypogonadism back in 2013. My wife and I at the time were trying for 5-6 months to conceive with no success. I had to wait for an appointment to see a Urologist, but I decided to self medicate with HCG. I ran a 3-week protocol and BOOM wife got pregnant. Our daughter is 2.5 years old now, and doing extremely well!

Fast forward, and when I saw my Urologist, he prescribed me HCG monotherapy as my HRT rather than testosterone since I'm young and wanted to have more children. We attempted a Clomid restart several times with no success, but I was able to dial in an HCG dose that put my T levels up where they need to be and that was fine and dandy for a while. I am in the middle of attempting another Clomid restart and learned that my history of chronic restrictive diets (low cal/low carb) and spending too much time in the gym with insufficient recovery is likely what led to my dysfunctional HPTA.

I suggest you order some good quality HCG if your doc is not willing to prescribe. As long as you have good functioning testes, it should work wonders. It did for me.

We don't know for sure if he's secondary or primary? Sometimes healthcare professionals go ahead and prescribe TRT not taking into consideration that secondary hypogonadism can be treated with gonadotropin (HCG) replacement and/or possibly clomid to restart the HPTA. At this point, if we don't have a baseline of LH and FSH, the only way to find out testicular function would be to begin HCG and stop the T injections.

### JanSz — 2017-03-16T14:46:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuck-and-scared-trt-and-fertility.19202/post-210450>

You are right about the unknown status of LH & FSH before starting on testosterone.
In my discussion with (MatRusLab) I have found:
1--- hi is using supplemental testosterone
2--- when his testosterone is low he is in terrible state not able to live comfortably or run his business. Having proper testosterone level is very high priority for him
3--- he have very high drive to become father regardless of his own problems and his wife's, when overall there is higher possibility of having health deficient child if conception is successful
4--- indeed his LH & FSH levels from before testosterone are unknown (to me)
5--- his testicles already are somewhat shrunken after testosterone use

On post #8 I provided one possibility of conducting fertility protocol.
That starts with halting exogenous testosterone supplementation.

You when you saying that:
"the only way to find out testicular function would be to begin HCG and stop the T injections"
that is about how this type of protocol starts.

I would like to add that I have heard of protocols that are planned similarly as in chart on post #8 except that testosterone supplementation is not stopped.
I do not know more details about that protocol,
but
I think that in such a protocol one would keep blood testosterone on some rather minimal level,
when without fertility consideration that would be rather high normal.
Say
1000ng/dL high normal
500ng/dL when attempting to conceive.

### MatRusLab — 2017-03-16T21:19:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuck-and-scared-trt-and-fertility.19202/post-210476>

Thank you for the additional conversation here.

I understand now the dosages. I wouldn't be able to afford that but still curious to see if it could help at lower dosage.

I do not know what I was diagnosed with (primary or secondary). I think maybe primary.

My blood level was 128. It was ridiculously low I was told.

Prescribed 4 pumps a day of Androgel and brought it up to 300s.

Then doubled it and now I think 800-900 range.

I know I am not on the numbers here.

I have definitely had shrinkage. Was hard to notice incrementally and then one day was just like yeah these used to fill up my hand really well. Hahaha.

I see a urologist in May.

### kris90 — 2017-03-16T21:24:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuck-and-scared-trt-and-fertility.19202/post-210477>

It's too bad you weren't diagnosed as secondary or primary, because if you come off the meds now, your LH and FSH will be suppressed due to the exogenous T in your system, so you won't get a true measure of these hormones.

I think the best bet would be HCG monotherapy for a month or so, then get bloods and a semen analysis to see if you responded. Typically within 2 weeks on HCG, I feel a difference and notice significant growth and fullness in the family jewels.

### MatRusLab — 2017-03-16T21:31:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuck-and-scared-trt-and-fertility.19202/post-210478>

My primary won't prescribe HCG but I wondered about asking him to order labs for a baseline and then trying myself prior to seeing the urologist.

### JanSz — 2017-03-17T01:32:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuck-and-scared-trt-and-fertility.19202/post-210484>

\[qu ote="MatRusLab, post: 210476, member: 19498"\]Thank you for the additional conversation here.

I understand now the dosages. I wouldn't be able to afford that but still curious to see if it could help at lower dosage.
I see HCG 5 ampoules of 10000iu for US$ 105.00 plus shipping
That should last you for a year when at 450iu twice a week.
If that is to expensive,
remember that HCG is for cosmetic reasons,
you do not have to use it.

I do not know what I was diagnosed with (primary or secondary). I think maybe primary.

My blood level was 128. It was ridiculously low I was told.

Prescribed 4 pumps a day of Androgel and brought it up to 300s.

Then doubled it and now I think 800-900 range.

It is good idea to keep your testosterone at 800-900ng/dL range
Additionally, when you are using Androgel your DHT is increasing, which may make you feel better.
Personally, I had to switch to Androgel 1.62% for that reason.

I know I am not on the numbers here.

When I tried to go down to 6 pumps I was not stable.

I have definitely had shrinkage. Was hard to notice incrementally and then one day was just like yeah these used to fill up my hand really well. Hahaha.
Do not worry too much about shrinkage, when you get spare $100 you can re-build it (cosmetically).

I see a urologist in May.

When I saw Endocrinologist he said that my primary should have tried to figure out why someone in mid 30s had such low testosterone. Wanted me off everything for a while to evaluate pituitary functioning.\[/quote\]
\=====================================================
Figuring out your pituitary is important.
Unfortunately that it is a endocrinologist's job, not GP's.
So the answer endo have given you is only half right.
You may try to go back to him,
unless you can find another endo.

The way it is now, you more or less do not have to worry about LH & FSH.
So, do not let him push you off testosterone/Androgel.
But,
pituitary gives numerous other hormones
and he should help you evaluate all of them or at least most of them.

One question to ask is:
did the all thing started suddenly?
Have you been healthy, energetic, full of life,
and
suddenly that changed?
If that is so, have you been hit in the head, forehead at the tip of your nose where glasses rest?
Car accident can do that.
That could have messed up your pituitary.

If above does not ring the bell,
Have you been healthy, energetic, full of life,
and slowly life got tough.
If that is more likely:
get pituitary MRI.
Look for pituitary tumor.

....
You already know that your testosterone is affected.
The other may be
thyroid
and
Growth Hormone.

If you get right endo he can write you couple scripts and make new man out of you.

...

# Thread: Sun Diciples Labs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-diciples-labs.19204/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sun Disciple — 2017-03-11T18:09:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-diciples-labs.19204/post-210138>

I just got back from the Yucatan but before I left I decided to get some labs with the left over change from my trip budget. Ill be doing something more comprehensive in a few months once I make and apply some adjustments to my environment. So any comments, questions , tips and ticks and critiques are welcome.

test name Result reference range

Urea 3.6 mmol/l 2.7-8.4

creatine 82 umol/l 67-117

EGFR 109 >90

I did the conversions to US units and made calculations and came up with 10/1 Bun creatine ratio.

HS-CRP 0.60 mg/l

Free-testosterone 60 pmol/l 196-636

DHEA-s 87 umol/l <15

Us conversion on DHEA-s here is 320 ug/dl

estradiol-17 beta <40 pmol/l <162

US conversion for estradiol is 10.89 pg/ml

25-hydroxy vitamin D 128 mmol/l 75-250

Free T3 3.4 pmol/l 3.1-6.2

Free T4 12 pmol/l 9-19
Us conversion gave me .9324 ng/dl .8-1.8

TSH 2.86 mIU/l .32-4.00

Ok so I made my analysis based off Jacks optimizing labs linked here [https://www.jackkruse.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/Optimizing-Labs.pdf](https://www.jackkruse.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/Optimizing-Labs.pdf)
[https://www.jackkruse.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/Optimizing-Labs.pdf](https://www.jackkruse.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/Optimizing-Labs.pdf)
Bun creatine ratio of 10/1 is ok not optimal need to get down to a 7/1

HS-crp of .6 is ok but should get it down below .5

My testosterone is in the basement. so basically Im workin with the mojo of a 90 year old man

DHEA needs to come up.

Estradiol is ok assuming jack is measuring in pg/ml his recomendation is <25 and Im at 10pg/ml

As for my thyroid T3 is ok. T4 is low at .9 Jack looks like he wants a 1.5 or greater.
TSH Looks on the high side as jack is looking for .5-1.00 and im clocking in at 2.86

Ok so a little context these have been my hacks over the last three months .
I did 2 months of CT before this blood draw. Now my bathroom is close to the electrical box so im hoping to move my CT outside here in the next few weeks with a metal feed troth or rain barrel and get it grounded.
\- focused heavily on light hacking rising with the sun and making like the sphinx.
\-Getting outside during the day when sun is out.
\-using natural light during the day eliminating blue light after dark.
\-Wearing blue blockers before and after sunrise.
\-Eating epi paleo with a focus on seasonal eating.
\-Eating within the light cycle 2 meals a day eaten within 8 hours giving me a 16 hour fast.
\-Last meal is always atleast 6 hours before bedtime.
\-black out room before bedtime.
\-Getting to sleep before 9 rising at dawn.
\-Using sperti Vit D lamp with near infrared lamp for 10 mins 2-3x per week.
\-Occasionally using solar glow lizard lamp to simulate sunrise when we've had a few days drought of morning sun.
\-Aiming to go out for hikes in the woods 2x per week.
\-meditation/breathing exercises.
\-yoga or bodyweight excersises after ct.
\-limiting screen time to an hour or 2 per day all before sunset with use of Gunnars.
\-Ive tried to reduce my EMF exposure as much as could without purchasing meters.
\-I Turn off breaker to room and airplane mode phone at night.
\-Always keep phone at a distance and use with airtube headset or speaker phone.
\-Eliminated blue tooth devices.
\-disconnected stereo and amplifier in car.
\-Keep distance from computer with usb mouse and keyboard.
\-2 to three litres of spring water a day.
\-Getting outside barefoot as much as possible.
\-keeping room cool and sleeping with thin sheet naked.
\-installing chlorine shower and batrh filters(water is not fluoridated here).

Now im really surprised my testosterone is still trashed after all these hacks Its still at the same level as a year ago. After Ct my mood and energy and aggressiveness is all elevated so I was almost sure It had to be somewhat higher. I guess Im just feeling an increase in neurotransmitters like dapamine and seratonin? My energy levels and motivation are up as well. Now my libido is still almost non existent so that is consistent with my low test result. My sleep is a lot better. I fall asleep immediately after lying down in bed and im only getting up once a night to pee and falling right back to sleep. I used to wake multiple times
and have trouble getting back to sleep. I always wake up just before my alarm just before dawn. No issues with lying around in bed or not wanting to get up. My number of dreams have increased at night as well as my recall of them.

I wish I had a bench mark immediately prior to these hacks but at least I have definitely felt some positive changes. Im a little discourage3d and surprised my testosterone hasn't budged at all. If anyone has any specific guidance there Id appreciate It.
My plan going forward was to purchase EMF meters and test my environment, Get an outdoor CT tank and hopefully purchase a magnetico.

Look forward to hearing your thoughts.

### JanSz — 2017-03-11T18:23:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-diciples-labs.19204/post-210140>

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### kris90 — 2017-03-11T21:08:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-diciples-labs.19204/post-210148>

What are your meals? Are you on strict keto, or eating carbs? Are you eating low calorie? Do you have Cortisol levels? What is your weight/height? Bodyfat % ?

Honestly, it looks to me like your thyroid is suppressed probably due to chronic stress, as is your testosterone. Did you know CT is a stress? Low calorie diets are as well, as is low carb IF you are already quite lean.

Also, the whole no eating within 4-5 hours of bed is bullshit in my opinion, UNLESS you are overweight and need to lose weight. The most crucial time for our brain to acknowledge being in the "fed state" is before sleep. Being fed = stress relief = solid sleep. Being semi-fasted before bed = stress = poor sleep. Again, the exception is if overweight, because the excess fat feeds the body, and fasting/keto becomes beneficial.

### Sun Disciple — 2017-03-13T16:53:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-diciples-labs.19204/post-210252>

> [kris90 said:](/goto/post?id=210148)
>
> What are your meals? Are you on strict keto, or eating carbs? Are you eating low calorie? Do you have Cortisol levels? What is your weight/height? Bodyfat % ?
>
> Honestly, it looks to me like your thyroid is suppressed probably due to chronic stress, as is your testosterone. Did you know CT is a stress? Low calorie diets are as well, as is low carb IF you are already quite lean.
>
> Also, the whole no eating within 4-5 hours of bed is bullshit in my opinion, UNLESS you are overweight and need to lose weight. The most crucial time for our brain to acknowledge being in the "fed state" is before sleep. Being fed = stress relief = solid sleep. Being semi-fasted before bed = stress = poor sleep. Again, the exception is if overweight, because the excess fat feeds the body, and fasting/keto becomes beneficial.

Im 5'11 178 pounds bodyfat is probably like maybe 14 - 15% I havnt done a dexa so im guessing on that.
Im doing Keto because Its winter and nothing is growing where I live. my macros are generally 30 to 40 grams of carbs a day 90 grams of protien 250 grams of fat.
I eat 2 meals a day. Breakfast is at dawn with either sardines or sockeye with 100 grams of duck fat some veggies and onions with turmeric and black pepper.
second meal is late afternoon either grass fed beef, lamb or seafood with lots of butter or ghee sea weed on the side. Im satiated and not hungry before bed as long as i get enough fat.

I havnt done cortisol in over a year but I can go get it done. should I be doing a full 5 point or just a morning spit in the tube? Anything else I can throw in there test wise that I missed on the last one?

I assumed CT was a stress but Its usefull to jumpstart hormone production no? I eased into it slowly following jacks protocol.
How many hours before bed would you suggest my last meal be? I can bring it up a bit since the sun is now setting a little later with the clocks moving forward here.
So what im hearing is based on my labs I should hold off on Jacks recomendations that you pointed out until my test and thyroid come up. specifically up my carbs cut CT maybe add in another meal to eliminate intermitent fasting? How many carbs can I eat without interfering with circadian biology in a low light cycle when nothing is growing here. It might be spring where you are but im looking at -15 degrees C and a couple feet of snow right now.

### kris90 — 2017-03-13T20:44:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-diciples-labs.19204/post-210273>

Cortisol would be helpful, and maybe rT3 as well, as it will be elevated under chronic stress conditions.

CT can be helpful to your hormonal profile similar to lifting weights, but the key is that excessive stress from CT, much like exercise can hurt you. Especially if there are other environmental factors causing chronic stress (i.e. malnutrition, toxins, allergies, etc). If your hormones are trashed currently, CT is not going to help you at all. You need to address the cause of havoc on your hormones (which again is usually chronic stress. Cortisol test could confirm).

I can't put a time on when you should eat, that's an N=1. I just had an idea that if you wait too long between your last meal and bed, your body might be in a hungered state going to sleep which is not good. The best sleep is when your stress is low. Naturally, when we are fed (especially with carbs) we sleep better with low stress. Does a lion sleep when he's hungry? No, he hunts his prey. Then after the feast, he sleeps.

Regarding carbs, don't stress over it. Just try adding them back to your diet slowly and see how you react. I eat most of my carbs at night now, and the results have been great. Libido is improving, as is sleep and body composition. I can eat way more calories now too, and my temperature is ALWAYS 37 C now, so it feels like my thyroid function has improved substantially. I don't care if it's winter, the carbs are helping me, and I personally think it's BS not to eat carbs in winter, unless you plan to hibernate. But if you are still required to function the same way you do in the summer, then IMO you need the carbs year round.

# Thread: Inflammatory skin reaction to sweating ? (iodine, leptin, water related?)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inflammatory-skin-reaction-to-sweating-iodine-leptin-water-related.19214/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Remo — 2017-03-13T13:33:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inflammatory-skin-reaction-to-sweating-iodine-leptin-water-related.19214/post-210237>

Friends I need your help,

I was doing (relatively) incredibely well last summer: I was outdoors most of the time, I started climbing, weight training (inside). When fall transitioned to winter I started to loose my virality again and most of all my ability to sweat. Dry skin had always been an issue but during winter it was always worse ( i reasoned due to indoor heating mostly)

A few months ago I became very ill. I recovered but whenever I went to the gym to exercise I did a few weight reps and BAM... at the exact moment my body wanted to sweat I would feel a 1000 little needles causing excruciating pain and inflammed red skin spots. With cooling they would go away within minutes.
I have this reaction now also when im feeling panicked (especially in artificially crowded blue-lit EMF environments).
IF I prevail to the pain and my body starts sweating the reaction goes away so actually I do **not** think it is an inflammatory reaction of the sweat itself but rather some neurological problem (?)

Some history:
\-I used to get daily panick attacks almost daily since a little kid until I left high school (prickly skin, anxiety, red head, huge flight or fight response).

My other complaints I have now:
1\. Brain Fog, inability to focus.
2\. Fatigue
3\. Bi-polar symptoms
4\. General Anxiety, ADD
5\. Blue light sensitivity

Incidentely it is wise to mention that my gym switched their colored lights (green, blue, red) to bright white (blue lights) some months ago.

### JanSz — 2017-03-13T15:16:49+0200

Exercise outdoor.
Minimize time indoor.
Maximize time outdoor.

You have already told me that you have messed up fatty acids.
That + autism do not go well together.
Fatty acids can be adjusted relatively easy,
you just have to put the on the list of your priorities.

...

Exercising outdoors is on the list... ! I will start with doing body weighted HIIT just outside.

I sleep grounded (directly to the earth) and within my own room I disconnect all devices when not used. Sadly I live with other people who are 100% against turning the Wifi routers off at night even if I would switch it on at 6am. yuk.

# Thread: EMFs, the WiFi Router, and the Smart Meter
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-the-wifi-router-and-the-smart-meter.19220/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-13T19:22:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-the-wifi-router-and-the-smart-meter.19220/post-210268>

Quick question: why is it that the WiFi Router and the Smart Meter are so bad, and I mean the DETAILS?

I keep hearing people say this, but I have used a meter to test the magnetic, electrical, and microwave fields of the WiFi box and the Smart Meter, and while they do give off something, it's not that much by the time you're a couple feet away from it. My electric piano puts off a lot more of a field than either of those, measuring strictly by the number of milligauss it puts off. So what's the big deal? I feel like I'm missing something important here.

### Inger — 2017-03-13T19:53:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-the-wifi-router-and-the-smart-meter.19220/post-210270>

I think it is because they are pulsed?
And when you use your devices on wifi mode and not corded, you will get a LOT more radiation too.. I have measured it... It is so much better to use corded internet, really much much less EMF.

Smart meter too makes some weird pulsed EMF that goes through all the electrical devices in the house. NOT good. You might not see it on your meter but it is there.... The meter is not that very precise, you might have to buy a real expensive one top be able to measure everything. But a meter still helps a lot. To give an idea.

Are you shutting off the breaker to your room? That would be big. I do, and after I started to do it I do not have to pee at all in the night anymore! My meter shows the same... but something have changed..... believe me!

Just try to shut of everything you can.
Even if your meter shows no difference. I am very sure that it has a benefit

### Inger — 2017-03-13T19:55:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-the-wifi-router-and-the-smart-meter.19220/post-210271>

Do you have a smart meter at your house?
If yes, where is it located?

If you cant get rid of it, there are filters to buy and install ( a bit pricey tho..) that can filter some pulsed signals away. I installed such a filter on the house of my parents. You can also shield it with aluminium foil but it will not help the pulsed signal going through the house electricity tho...

### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-13T21:41:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-the-wifi-router-and-the-smart-meter.19220/post-210277>

>
> Do you have a smart meter at your house?
> If yes, where is it located?
>
> If you cant get rid of it, there are filters to buy and install ( a bit pricey tho..) that can filter some pulsed signals away. I installed such a filter on the house of my parents. You can also shield it with aluminium foil but it will not help the pulsed signal going through the house electricity tho...

How are you measuring the radiation? I've used a Trifield meter, and that doesn't show anything. I'd like to know, because my parents (whom I live with) will do this to help me, but first they want to understand exactly why it's bad. And the device we have apparently doesn't measure radiation of the type you're finding.

I'm also curious about this pulsed signal. . . how is that different from a standard electromagnetic field, or even just a standard electrical meter?

### OptimalSkr — 2017-03-13T23:48:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-the-wifi-router-and-the-smart-meter.19220/post-210285>

The are three types of emfs Radio frequency. Magnetic. And electric. All require different meters to measure them properly.

### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-14T00:45:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-the-wifi-router-and-the-smart-meter.19220/post-210287>

### Inger — 2017-03-14T09:23:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-the-wifi-router-and-the-smart-meter.19220/post-210307>

Trifield is great for magnetic and electric

### Michalis — 2017-03-14T13:18:53+0200

> [matthewsb77 said:](/goto/post?id=210268)

> Quick question: why is it that the WiFi Router and the Smart Meter are so bad, and I mean the DETAILS?
>
> I keep hearing people say this, but I have used a meter to test the magnetic, electrical, and microwave fields of the WiFi box and the Smart Meter, and while they do give off something, it's not that much by the time you're a couple feet away from it. My electric piano puts off a lot more of a field than either of those, measuring strictly by the number of milligauss it puts off. So what's the big deal? I feel like I'm missing something important here.

Check this out, start with the basic fact sheets on the website:

[http://www.oscillatorium.com/](http://www.oscillatorium.com/)
_A gallery of images and maps, visual learning tools for the study of unnatural oscillations -- non-ionizing electromagnetic fields -- and their effects on living tissues and biodiverse systems._

### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-14T19:08:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-the-wifi-router-and-the-smart-meter.19220/post-210345>

Thank you guys so much!

### Penny — 2017-03-14T20:19:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-the-wifi-router-and-the-smart-meter.19220/post-210352>

> [matthewsb77 said:](/goto/post?id=210277)
>
> How are you measuring the radiation? I've used a Trifield meter, and that doesn't show anything. I'd like to know, because my parents (whom I live with) will do this to help me, but first they want to understand exactly why it's bad. And the device we have apparently doesn't measure radiation of the type you're finding.
>
> I'm also curious about this pulsed signal. . . how is that different from a standard electromagnetic field, or even just a standard electrical meter?

Here is the tester most people get to measure RF - if you have a cell phone, there are free apps which will work in a pinch - great movie BTW - you can see if smart meter removal is free in MN also -
[https://takebackyourpower.net/shop/electromagnetic-radiation-testers/](https://takebackyourpower.net/shop/electromagnetic-radiation-testers/)

# Thread: Tietze syndrome

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thomas — 2017-03-14T19:02:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tietze-syndrome.19227/post-210343>

Hello guys.

I am asking if maybe Jack has any clues about the tietze syndrome? My mom got diagnosed with it, she has thoracic pain at times. Is it a problem linked with mitochondria? Is there anything else to do apart from symptomatic pain management?

thanks.

### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2017-03-14T19:19:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tietze-syndrome.19227/post-210346>

I think tietze syndrom = inflammation = circardian mismatch. So to help fix it the cause needs to be addressed.. This includes sleeping when the sun is down, or atleast block blue light, Spending time outset during the day. If her sleep is messed up, it should be clear that factors disrupting the sleep are very important to fix. Best regards!

### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2017-03-14T19:20:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tietze-syndrome.19227/post-210347>

I think grounding would be incredibly helpful because it reduces inflammation.

### Mito1 — 2017-03-15T15:58:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tietze-syndrome.19227/post-210403>

.
I know first hand I had the same issue due to a car accident - it popped the 2nd and 3rd rib out of the sternum. Very painful and even more painful to pop it back in place. Even if a D.O. could pop it back in place - poof it popped out again. Luck for me I ran into a very specialized chiropractor - perhaps less than 5 docs are trained in his field. It wasn't the rib per se the issue but the vertebrae in the back that was out. Mine was C-7 - popping that in place was beyond painful but once in place the rib never popped out again.

.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/microcirculation-headache.19230/post-216798>

Welp, an improved microcirculation would definitely be beneficial to our overall health. One of the treatments I've seen online is carboxytherapy (C02 therapy) wherein therapeutic C02 levels will be introduced to our body. There's this video-microscopic study of nailfold capilliaries on patients having classical migraine wherein the findings demonstrated patients with migraine often have subclinical microcirculatory disorders. # Thread: Mito Labs Question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mito-labs-question.19232/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-15T17:34:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mito-labs-question.19232/post-210412>

Making new threads for individual topics to focus my search.

My question for this one: if the labs show your mitochondria are working correctly, does that mean they are, or is that just one little indicator?

Mine was a Genova Diagnostics NutrEval test that included Metabolic Analysis Markers. Under the sections for Carb Metabolism, everything was out of whack (two low, one high). But under the Energy Metabolism section, everything was within range (see below for the names of what was checked) except one, which was trending low but not dangerously, and that one was Cis-Aconitic Acid. Malic acid was just about to leave the green (healthy) section toward high.

Tested: Citric Acid, Cis-Aconitic Acid, Isocritic Acid, a-Ketoglutaric Acid (AKG), Succinic Acid, Malic Acid, b-Oh-B-Methylglutaric Acid (HMG).

### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-15T19:21:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-rid-of-melatonin-supplements.19233/post-210421>

Hey guys! I've recently learned through Dr. Kruse how bad it is to take exogenous melatonin. I've taken it for about 8.5 years. The last two years, due to some severe health problems, I got very little sleep of any kind. Then, finally, after incorporating Jack's work (light environment, Magnetico, etc.), I'm finally starting to sleep consistently. I have had dreams three or four nights in a row now. I haven't had that . . . ever.

**My question now is, how do I get rid of the melatonin without going insomniac again?** Obviously some of you will disagree with this, but getting sleep is more important than being perfect and not taking melatonin. However, I want to detox from using it in a way that will allow my body to start producing its own again. How should I go about that? **Assume I'm already doing everything else correctly with light, magnetism, water, EMFs, etc.** **and that other sleep aids don't help. Assume this is going to be more of a process than an immediate cold-turkey.** Thanks!

(Phrased in another way, how important is stopping melatonin use in the hierarchy of important things to correct in order to be well? Obviously sleep is at or near #1.)

### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-15T19:36:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-rid-of-melatonin-supplements.19233/post-210422>

By the way, I don't find that relaxing herbs or teas, progress relaxation, or any other supplement helps me, except Diphenhydramine citrate (Benadryl, basically, which I'm sure is as bad as or worse than taking melatonin).

### Brent Patrick — 2017-03-16T10:04:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-bulbs-australia.19235/post-210442>

Hello Caroline ,thank you for your prompt reply

would you be able to tell me exactly what you purchased?

### JanSz — 2017-03-16T16:06:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-bulbs-australia.19235/post-210459>

> [Brent Patrick said:](/goto/post?id=210440)
>
> Hi hoping Members from Australia can steer me in the right direction regarding purchasing Black bulbs suitable for OZ ?
>
> I have acquired incandescent bulbs and NIR Bulbs but I am at a loss to find suitable Black Bulbs
>
> any advice would be greatly appreciated!

Black bulbs are about UV.
Those bulbs have insignificant amounts of UV.
Usually we do not even know what kind and how much UV they produce.
UV that we want to get is UVa and UVb.
We want it because
UVa makes vit A and skin tan
UVb makes vit D
One can do blood test and see where they are.
That is a best way to gauge UV exposure.
UV from sunlight is the best.
We need a lots of UV over max skin area.
To receive sufficient amounts of UV skin must be hardened first.
Without hardened skin one talks skin-burn, cancer.
It takes about a month of small incremental increases to harden skin.
That is a lots of work, make sure to do it only once (and do not loose it).
Suntan saloons have usually worry about skin tanning, so they have only UVa.
Besides, that is separate trip, hard to keep up daily.
Better solution is using Sperti lamp at home.
Again, there is a White and Red Sperti.
We want Red Sperti.
This one:
Sperti Fiji SUN Table Top Tanning Lamp
[https://www.amazon.com/Sperti-Fiji-Table-Tanning-Lamp/dp/B001ID8P78/ref=sr\_1\_fkmr0\_2\_a\_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1489672760&sr=8-2-fkmr0&keywords=red sperti](https://www.amazon.com/Sperti-Fiji-Table-Tanning-Lamp/dp/B001ID8P78/ref=sr_1_fkmr0_2_a_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1489672760&sr=8-2-fkmr0&keywords=red+sperti)

I have already hardened skin.
My skin is originally white. Sort of normal white, not darkish not overly white.
Got this lamp last beginning of September
Use it 16min every morning.
Recently got blood test, waiting for results of my Vit D

Vit D goal:
minimum:
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)
up to 1.5 of above is still within normal
do not
go above 2x of that (it would be really really hard to do)

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

...

Note;
Last few years I got a lots of sun.
I still must supplement vit A.
At one point I thought that I may cut the dose, had to bring it back afterward.

### Steele — 2017-03-17T17:19:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-help.19239/post-210530>

Hello all,

If anyone is willing to help me understand my labs I would greatly appreciate it.

Age: 23
Sex: male
Height: 6' 1"
Weight: 157
BMI: 20.7
HR: 50 bpm
BP: 120/80

Name: David
Occupation: Physician Assistant student
Home: Lynchburg, Virginia.
Exercise: 2012: College soccer athlete, tons of HITT training; June 2015: adopted LCHF which destroyed my athletic performance, decreased aerobic capacity, cramping muscles; 2017: currently weight train and hike when able
Diet: June of 2015: ketogenic (Horrible Idea/mismatch) - could barely walk up stairs without being short of breath (decreased HR significantly, increased respiration rate); past 1.5 years: seasonal, paleo, high seafood, LCHF
Environment: 2012-2016: college campus; 2016-2017: full time student, currently inside (no sunlight), Wifi environment, and sit from roughly 8am-5pm M-F. Attempt to get sunshine/exercise every chance I get
Observations: 2015: after going low carb: poor ability to clot blood, scab, and wound heal (lots of scars), decreased aerobic performance, continual 'low carb flu' sxs past 2 years that have been improving with time (considering the idea that LCHF may not be ideal for me)
Biohacks: sleep with grounding wire, wear blue-light blockers nearly every evening, sleep with Wifi off and phone on airplane mode, drink filtered water, CT: I have extremely cold hands and feet even in the summer to the point of chilblains on my toes, I do not feel myself warm-up afterward or acclimate easily
Health: gluten intolerant/Celiac disease since 1 y/o

\***abnormal values bold** and the questionable values underlined

Labs:
**CBC:
WBC: 3.2 L (3.4-10.8)**
RBC 4.86 (4.14-5.80)

**LDL 182 H (0-99)**

**Iron
TIBC: 235 L (250-450)**
UIBC 144 (111-343)

**Vit D, 25-Hydroxy: 28.3 L (30-100)**

Total testosterone: 622 (348-1197)

> Diet: June of 2015: ketogenic (Horrible Idea/mismatch) - could barely walk up stairs without being short of breath (decreased HR significantly, increased respiration rate); past 1.5 years: seasonal, paleo, high seafood, LCHF
> Environment: 2012-2016: college campus; 2016-2017: full time student, currently inside (no sunlight), Wifi environment, and sit from roughly 8am-5pm M-F. Attempt to get sunshine/exercise every chance I get
> Observations: 2015: after going low carb: poor ability to clot blood, scab, and wound heal (lots of scars), decreased aerobic performance, continual 'low carb flu' sxs past 2 years that have been improving with time (considering the idea that LCHF may not be ideal for me)
> Biohacks: sleep with grounding wire, wear blue-light blockers nearly every evening, sleep with Wifi off and phone on airplane mode, drink filtered water, CT: I have extremely cold hands and feet even in the summer to the point of chilblains on my toes, I do not feel myself warm-up afterward or acclimate easily

Thank you very much for your response JanSz.

Do you or anyone else have any recommendations/comments on:

6\. It seems a lower BUN/Cr ratio closer to 10 would be better, any suggestions on how this can be accomplished or the pathophysiology to this? Mine is 19.

Thanks again for any feedback.

9\. What causes a normal total testosterone but a low free testosterone? What is hindering the conversion?

Total Testosterone is located in three parts of body.
strongly bound to SHBG
weakly bound to albumin
remaining part is free, hence FreeT

It is considered that FreeT and Albumin bound testosterone are active

10\. Significance of very low estradiol (<5.0) in men?--------------may loose bones

### JanSz — 2017-03-22T15:43:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-help.19239/post-210879>

2\. Is it possible that the low DHEA, Testosterone, and estradiol are due to pregnenolone steal syndrome or a low T3 level from hypothyroidism preventing adequate synthesis of sex steroid hormones from LDL? I am awaiting my salivary cortisol (ASI) before moving forward here.

Every hormone is not stationary but it flows with time.

Testosterone is made in adrenals (very little) and in testicles.
Low testosterone is called hypogonadism.
It can be due to
testicles not being able to produce---->primary hypogonadism
good testicles but not getting signal (LH) to produce---->secondary hypogonadism

\------------------------
First steroid hormone is pregnenolone that is produced from cholesterol.
We
can live without any steroid hormones (poor life)
except for
cortisol

body would steal (pregnenolone originally earmarked for making DHEA)
just to get cortisol

.....

There are variety of reasons why body is not producing enough pregnenolone

......

### enyaw — 2017-03-18T01:07:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-light-better-decisions-or-i-need-an-rx-which-one.19242/post-210557>

**H mtDNA Haplogroup, I live at 42.9 N latitude.

10/28/16: 5'8' 169 lbs**
HDL: 56 mg/dL
LDL (measured): 203 mg/dL
Trigs: 82 mg/dL
Total chol: 272 mg/dL
FBG: 98 mg/dL
Vitamin D: not tested
BUN/Cr ratio: ~17

**3/13/17: 5'8" 179 lbs ~23% BF** (the 10 pound gain happened in December)
HDL: 53 mg/dL
LDL (measured): 300 mg/dL
Trigs: 209 mg/dL
Total chol:
FBG: 108 mg/dL
Vitamin D: 32.9 ng/dL
BUN/Cr ratio: ~16.5
CRP: 0.050 mg/dL

I'm trying to figure out which protocol I should do. I'm at 23% BF and have about 20 pounds to lose. I live in Michigan, but I've been on the Gulf in Mississippi since January and I'm staying until June. I've been getting 10-30 mins of UVB exposure daily, but I only get to see the sunrise for a few minutes each day during the work week. On the weekends, I sit in the sunrise for about an hour. I do use an IR/UVA bulb combo when I'm in my condo. I also do face dunks and cold-ish showers. (The water only goes down to ~70, but it still feels brisk and gives me goosebumps.)

### enyaw — 2017-03-18T16:34:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-light-better-decisions-or-i-need-an-rx-which-one.19242/post-210598>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=210564)
>
> what are you eating? drinking?

I've been eating epi-paleo. However, in Jan/Feb, I found myself on a weekly carb binge, usually in the form of corn tortilla chips. I've since stopped. For the last few weeks I've been eating 1/2 pound of shrimp with eggs, garlic, cream and romano cheese for breakfast. I also put heavy cream in a cup of coffee. For lunch, if I eat it, it's been a salad that consists of lettuce, EVOO, red wine vinegar and salt. Dinner is usually a meat and some veggies with lots of Kerrygold.

On weekends I don't set an alarm and I wake up within 30 minutes of the sun rising. I sit in the morning sun then eat the breakfast I mentioned. When I do this, I almost always skip lunch because I'm not hungry. During the weekend it's inconsistent whether I skip lunch or not. I wake up at 5:15 and, as of last week, that is 2 hours before sunrise.

I drink whatever spring or RO water is available at the commissary on base. The city water is fluoridated. I also drink coffee with heavy cream. Soon I'll switch that over to CO.

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=210564)
>
> do you C/T?

I've been doing a nightly face dunk for the last couple of weeks. I try to do one after breakfast too, but I rarely have time during the work week. I take baths that are cold relative to my body temp, but not close to the 55 recommendation of the CT protocol. It gets down to about 70. I also take showers that alternate between hot and cold. I like to do a face dunk, then dump the cold water on my head and enjoy about 60 seconds in the shower of hot torso/cold head. I also keep the thermostat set at 65 once the sun goes down. That's as low as it'll go.

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=210564)
>
> Leptin reset?

Interestingly, I found myself in this same city 5 years ago from May - Aug 2012. I was probably the same weight that I am now, but I don't have labs from then to compare. I started the Leptin Rx a week before I got here, then found it hard to continue because of the lack of protein offered at breakfast in the chow hall. I did make up for that lack of protein with 2 Tbs of CO in my morning coffee. I kept my barracks room as cold as it'd go, took a cold-ish shower every night before bed, slept in darkness and drank non-fluoridated water. I didn't pay attention to circadian signalling (woke for work before sunrise, turned on my fluorescent light to get ready, then sat in a building all day), but I did get intermittent sunrises on my eyes and the occassional UVB on my legs, arms and face on weekends. That's when I've felt the best I've ever felt in the last 10 years. The weight came off quickly and I had tons of energy. I could eat anything.

I'm trying to replicate that here, but I'm not noticing any changes, YET.

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=210564)
>
> what are you doing to mitigate nnEMF?

I put black poster board on my bedroom windows so I can sleep in the dark. I turn off wi-fi and my cell phone, but I'm living in a condo building. I'm sure my neighbors don't. I wear Blutech lenses when I'm at work, and UVEX when I'm home after sunset.

I'm actually a little concerned about this one. The RF reading from my Cornet show ambient power density around my condo to be around 0.05 mW/m2, but spikes up to 0.5 mW/m2. I sit on my balcony to get UVB on my lunch breaks, but the power density there spikes up to 2.0 mW/m2. Will that interfere with Vitamin D production?
