Speaking about one month,
remember to get your skin sun hardened.
Just that takes about one month of incremental exposure.

I suggest to stay away from any estimates of what you should get by having certain time under estimated UV light.
It have to be blood test.

.............
We are talking vit D here.
But really Vit A & D are of concern. Test both.

..

>

I've been getting natural sun everyday. I already have a nice tan going. I'm definitely not as white as I was when I came down from Michigan in January. My main concern is the RF exposure. I think it might be high enough that I'm going to ask the manager if I can try other rooms. Ultimately I actually think the barracks on base have a better EMF environment but with different challenges. The main challenge there, besides getting a room, is eating three meals a day with no kitchen.

# Thread: is it just me? or is the structure of this forum really big and clunky now?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-just-me-or-is-the-structure-of-this-forum-really-big-and-clunky-now.19253/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Richelle Jones — 2017-03-20T06:43:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-just-me-or-is-the-structure-of-this-forum-really-big-and-clunky-now.19253/post-210729>

I find it really hard to keep up with new posts from JK. As much as I value everyones input I don't have time to read thru everything but I am trying to keep up with JK posts. Which are hard for me to see because I am viewing things during a different time zone. Most of the relevant posts are posted to FB but not all of them. Is it time for a restructure now that there are so many members?

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-20T09:56:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-just-me-or-is-the-structure-of-this-forum-really-big-and-clunky-now.19253/post-210732>

If I'm short of time I click on Jack's profile page, then postings or recent activity. Short posts I read straight away, anything longer or a succession of posts on the same subject, I cut and paste into a word document, print it out and I have a bonus mini blog to read outside.

### caroline — 2017-03-21T02:55:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-just-me-or-is-the-structure-of-this-forum-really-big-and-clunky-now.19253/post-210810>

It can be hard to keep up ...... but it is doable

### Sheddie — 2017-07-28T02:24:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-just-me-or-is-the-structure-of-this-forum-really-big-and-clunky-now.19253/post-220254>

In the Forums I use the _Search_ box most of all! It helps me quickly jump to & follow-on from multiple, multi-level questions and/or blank spaces in my mind. _Search_ is easy enough so I 'hold that thought' while I'm deep, thrashing around, in the 'jungle adventure.'

The indexed Blog section is when I'm up for some serious, slower reading & thinking, 'illumination & digestion!' The whole site is a bigger 'curriculum' than an average degree program in, to me, a new 'synergistic' field of inquiry! (Inter-disciplinary falls short -- more like inter-paradigmatic? In a way, we're witnessing something like a cosmic galactic star nursery expanding in our collective consciousness?)

I see some titles showing up on FaceBook and then again, on the website, although I'm not sure if FB is posted first, or not. FB is annoying to me as I see posts and then can't find them again later... Generally, I feel 'safer' using the website, and have had a few Lost & Found wins.

Maybe these different approaches explain why I can't identify with the website as "clunky." I enjoy & depend on all the available means for learning including podcasts, YouTube, and some I probably haven't discovered yet. I need the audio materials to sit back & close my UV-blue-ravaged eyes; I need the Forums to get inspiration on HOW to DO some of these original lifestyle changes; I need the print materials to buckle down and really study the content that explains the WHYs of this massive paradigm-busting revolution. The "gargantuan" complexity is amazingly accessible. Sue-UK's steps for the latest in JK's posts is spot on target.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-09-09T20:16:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/least-expensive-diys.19255/post-250584>

Well, if **amplified cross crawling** sounds too creepy, try other energy disciplines that tap into _the_ endless supply of **_life energy_** (Chi, Ki, Prana, etc.-- it goes by many names but has _One Cosmic Source_).

Chi Kung (Tai Chi, etc. etc) has already been mentioned. The practice I rely on is **Reiki**. Most of Dr Kruse's Swans have heard of it, but there might be some that don't understand it.

A crude analogy (I may get hammered on this) is that it is a little like Chi Kung or similar discipline but without a physical body workout. It is most similar in principle to energy practices like 'laying on of the hands' but without a religious message.

At a most modest investment in time and money for Reiki training and requisite _Initiation_ ceremonies you can access this unlimited energy rest of your life and for free (Initiation is not for show or pomp-- it is required to establish open energy channels-- you can't do it yourself, only a trained Reiki master can).

At Reiki Level I you are ready to heal and invigorate your body anytime anyplace just using your hands (who doesn't have plenty of time to do this during boring staff meetings-- and you don't have to meditate or be mindful while Reiki works-- although you will get more results if you do). At Level II and III you are ready to heal others and do **remote healing**\-- a huge benefit and a way to be of service to others.

# Thread: Cornet users beware of Mg readings
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-users-beware-of-mg-readings.19258/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JoeT — 2017-03-21T02:08:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-users-beware-of-mg-readings.19258/post-210805>

So, I have a Cornet meter, but recently sprung for the 300 bucks to get something with better accuracy, particularly at levels below 1Mg....mostly to find suitable places to ground. I went with this one.....

[https://www.lessemf.com/gauss.html#153](https://www.lessemf.com/gauss.html#153)

I didn't notice any highly unusual variations between Cornet and the Alpha Labs meter around my house; however, in my car the Cornet always read about 10 Mg....but the Alpha is reading only 1.5 Mg! Needless to say, I like my car much better now!

### caroline — 2017-03-21T02:47:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-users-beware-of-mg-readings.19258/post-210807>

interesting Joe .... but how do you judge the accuracy of either????

### JoeT — 2017-03-21T02:55:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-users-beware-of-mg-readings.19258/post-210811>

The Alpha is a higher end meter that measures specifically Magnetic fields only. It's more sensitive and accurate. It's not very likely that the inaccuracy is coming from it.

### caroline — 2017-03-21T03:19:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-users-beware-of-mg-readings.19258/post-210813>

so - we really need a selection of meters?

### Sun Disciple — 2017-03-21T22:46:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-users-beware-of-mg-readings.19258/post-210842>

Ya I chose to avoid the cornet and went with a acousticom 2 and alpha UHS 2 as per Michael Nuerts recommendation. The cornet ed88t has decent RF but the gauss meter function is not accurate enough. . Unfortunately if you want accuracy you need independent meters for Magnetic fields, electric fields and RF.

Here is his review of the cornet for anyone on the fence

### Antonis — 2018-12-10T13:29:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-users-beware-of-mg-readings.19258/post-257710>

> [Sun Disciple said:](/goto/post?id=210842)
>

The cornet ed88t has decent RF but the gauss meter function is not accurate enough. . Unfortunately if you want accuracy you need independent meters for Magnetic fields, electric fields and RF.
>
> Here is his review of the cornet for anyone on the fence

Hey guys,

my grandfather (87y) broke his femoral neck last monday (march 13th) and was operated short after for a endoprothesis of the head. All was going good and he kept doing progress but this sunday he had a stroke and was carried to the stroke unit.
Because of the surgery he couldnt get global lyse, but he now seem to make progress again fast.

Is there anything we can do to help him? My familiy probably wont do this, because they are not into this but I could try.

# Thread: Blue Light Induces Mitochondrial DNA Damage and Free Radical Production in Epithelial Cells
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-induces-mitochondrial-dna-damage-and-free-radical-production-in-epithelial-cells.19265/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-03-22T11:25:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-induces-mitochondrial-dna-damage-and-free-radical-production-in-epithelial-cells.19265/post-210864>

"In conclusion, the vulnerability of mtDNA to mitochondria-derived ROS in response to blue light strongly supports a role for visible irradiation in cellular dysfunction. Such dysfunction will be maximal in tissues such as skin and eye, which are regularly exposed to blue light and thus ROS throughout life ([54](http://www.jbc.org/content/280/22/21061.full#ref-54), [55](http://www.jbc.org/content/280/22/21061.full#ref-55)). The accumulation of stochastic oxidative damage in mitochondria supports a number of hypotheses of aging in which the mitochondrion plays a central role ([33](http://www.jbc.org/content/280/22/21061.full#ref-33), [56](http://www.jbc.org/content/280/22/21061.full#ref-56)–[58](http://www.jbc.org/content/280/22/21061.full#ref-58)). Furthermore, the ROS-induced aging is also likely to contribute to a variety of age-related pathologies such as skin cancer and age-related macular degeneration."

[http://www.jbc.org/content/280/22/21061.full#fn-2](http://www.jbc.org/content/280/22/21061.full#fn-2)

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-23T14:02:31+0200

Ah the importance of context....

Linda I have no experience of doing the leptin reset whilst working nights. In your position I would post the details in my journal, including whether you work 3 nights in a row, or whether its 2 and 1 later in the week blah blah, your meal timings on night shift, what your timings are on days you don't work. Then if you put what you are trying now, and anything you notice about how it feels and affects your sleep. In the context of your journal as a whole, you have a better chance of getting some helpful suggestions or feedback or being pointed to the right blog.

# Thread: Weight loss plateau on Leptin RX
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-plateau-on-leptin-rx.19281/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh Tamblyn — 2017-03-27T01:57:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-plateau-on-leptin-rx.19281/post-211177>

I have been following the leptin rx protocol as close as I can for the past three weeks. Being on a somewhat limited budget I have eaten the same two meals everyday for the past 2 weeks
AM Meal:
4oz grass fed beef
2 eggs
2tbs butter
2tbs mct oil
1/2 avocado
3tbsp of bullet proof cologne protein

PM:
4oz wild caught salmon (canned)
1/2 avocado
1egg
2tbs beef tallow or Coconut oil (seasonal)
1 grass fed organic beef hotdog
2tbsp butter

I also eat oysters and bone broth once a week.

I have not been working out and within the three week time frame I have lost 21 pounds.
Sleep has improved ( although I still wake up around 2/3 and 4/5am) I wake rested and ready to start the day. Mental clarity is 100% better but I still have some motivational issues. Could just be I'm in my senior year of college and stress is grinding on me.

For the past 4 days I ha e stopped loosing weight and have stayed the same. I was 291 @5'11 when I started and am now 270.4. I have not been using CT. My bathtub kind of sucks. I've done face dunking but I can handle a lot more. I'm wondering if this quick weight loss and a plateau is common and is it as simple as adding CT or have I run into a possible hormone issue. I have been eating between 2000-2200 calories per day. Eating at 63o am and 6 pm. Any help would be appreciated!

Josh

### caroline — 2017-03-27T11:14:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-plateau-on-leptin-rx.19281/post-211202>

Likely your body is adjusting ....give it some time. You are doing great!

C/T is a game changer. Why does your bathtub suck? Can you take cold showers?

can you get in an ocean? lake? river?

what about sardines? easy on the budget.....

Roast lamb?

bullet proof protein??? that must cost a fortune?

What is your nnEMF situation? How much grounding do you get? early am sunshine?

what water are you drinking?

It isn't about the food....... It is all about the three legged stool

### Mitchell97 — 2017-03-28T13:28:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-plateau-on-leptin-rx.19281/post-211277>

Up your protein... and eat a lot of fish. Light, water and magnetism is the key.

### JanSz — 2017-03-29T17:39:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-plateau-on-leptin-rx.19281/post-211338>

> [Josh Tamblyn said:](/goto/post?id=211177)
>
> I have lost 21 pounds.
> Sleep has improved ( although I still wake up around 2/3 and 4/5am) I wake rested and ready to start the day.
> I was 291 @5'11 when I started and am now 270.4.
> I have been eating between 2000-2200 calories per day.
> **Eating at 63o am and 6 pm.**
> Any help would be appreciated!
>
> Josh

Consider eating your daily food
either all at once or at least shorten the time between meals.

To be able to burn body fat one must arrange for insulin to fall below some rather low level.
The longer you insulin is below that level the more time you spend burning body fat.

...

### caroline — 2017-03-27T06:59:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reality-8.19284/post-211187>

Thoughts from all the ladies on Reality #8????

My instincts are telling me to stop messing around with hormones - for me .....my n=1

After getting lots and lots of ocean/sunshine time - I was feeling fantastic.

I pretty much stopped taking Pg. and still felt really good. I then had to start taking an oestrogen cream for an issue. Months later I realised that I had thrown my hormones out of sync. I should have added back the Pg. for a time.

It does seem that Mother Nature has us covered..........

So - should we leave the balancing act up to Mother Nature? ...... providing we are doing a lot of other things right?

What say you.......

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-27T08:56:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reality-8.19284/post-211189>

Cool  I was thinking of starting a thread on this blog (after the discussion on @[Debntx](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19762/) journal). Entangled ....?
If menopause gains us time, do we start losing time again if we start messing with exogenous hormones? Perhaps if women have survived long enough to reach menopause by thinking outside the box ..... there's more opportunity, more time at menopause to experience that there is no box ....?

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-27T09:28:32+0300

From my journal ....

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=210827)
>
> I seem to be needing less sleep. 5 hours with vivid dreams seems to be enough at the moment, perhaps partly because its spring. I think its the 10 minutes of light therapy before bed, with its subtraction of yellow. When I'm out at first light the sky lightens, but the slow crescendo of yellow appears before the orange and red. When it reaches a certain level, the birds start chirping, even before sunrise. I like to think the birds help call the sunrise in.  I was out at 4 a.m. this morning, lying on the ground looking at the moon and the stars. I've started keeping as warm as I need to, not trying to make it an air CT time, I stay out longer. Just make sure I'm grounded underneath, and then cosy up under the blankets. But I was looking first at the moon, (the light reflected being more in the now ....) and then at the stars. I was thinking of my eyes emitting photons which were interacting with photons from stars whose light had left them thousands of years ago. Had I found the photons or did they find me? (I've thought about that in the past, but this morning I was re-mind-ed). I was thinking about a woman's years connected to her moon time, versus a post menopausal woman now has time to look beyond her moon time to the stars. If she is fully connected during the day, is the naturally lowering melatonin levels of aging her chance to experience more of the quantum All That Is during grounded, colder, darkness wakefulness without the penalty of sunlight speeding up time? Came back in to find Reality 8, how women gain time at menopause..... Gotta just love a bit of synchronicity.

I am way calmer these days, way more vulnerable, way more open to just "being", way more open to what life has to offer.

At some moment in Time ....I stepped off the tread mill and really noticed my world and all the simple and wonderful treasures of my life....

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-03-27T17:22:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reality-8.19284/post-211222>

What a great thread and thanks for starting it Caroline . The past two years have been a journey of healing and self discovery for me. A beautiful and at times, heart wrenching time of self reflection and growth here. Something profound happened when I let go and I let Nature and I haven't looked back since. As a result of letting go and letting Nature, I feel more alive and vibrant than I have in years, perhaps decades. I haven't gone back to look at my journal, but from what I can remember writing, I'm no longer that same woman. Gratitude! Perhaps this is why I become emotional when I see others who are having such great success here when they just relax and allow to let Nature heal.

Every morning, when I awaken, the very first thing that I do, is to give gratitude and I actually choose a word for the day and I write my feelings, in my gratitude journal, based on this one word. Today's was was "afraid." I also enjoy listening to TED Talks and after I journaled, went online to see what TED Talks I could find based on this one word. This is the talk that I found this morning and I feel that it's a great talk to share with you two gals and anyone else who wants to listen. Scilla touched my heart this morning. Enjoy!

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-28T09:59:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reality-8.19284/post-211263>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=211255)
>
> what a great thought to ponder Sue .... I think you are on to something!

My n=1 ponderings

I know men can and do raise children, but in terms of menopause …..Bringing up my children, I think I was happy to accept the benefits of society’s norms , the (fertility?) box, as part of my kid’s environment, IF my kids benefited from it. The problems arise when they don’t, or if a crisis occurs, or if the box worked for one but not another. That forced me to look outside the box more than once. Society’s norms now include less time outside, nnEMF, wifi, etc etc, so my granddaughters are facing an environment that my children weren’t facing at the same age. Although some may choose not to, (or may not have children at all), a woman may also put her own dreams on hold while she rears the next generation, or takes responsibility for ensuring the wellbeing of her or her partner’s parents until they pass. She may know that the possibility of there not being a box exists, and even experience it as moments of grace prior to menopause, if her redox is high enough.

I think there’s a truth in the saying that a woman is only as happy as her saddest child, women willingly give up their energy to their children or loved ones, even if it leaves them on their redox knees. If consciousness emerges from mitochondria, then how we experience menopause may depend on whether we can meet it head on from a position of redox strength, or whether we reach it crawling on all fours. But even if a woman makes it crawling to menopause, historically we are the survivors of famine, war and pestilence. Do the losses of males in battle make survival of the human species dependent on female longevity? Our experience also has survival value to the next generation.

With the right knowledge and the ability to apply it, I think out of the box or no box thinking is the opportunity to get back on her feet. If a woman understands the issues and makes an informed choice about temporarily supplementing with exogenous hormones, its one thing, but in a low redox state there is a higher risk is not understanding it fully from a quantum perspective and sleepwalking into it, often on the "knowledgeable advice" of some random person on the internet. Reality 8 isn't an easy read for a newbie, but what I love about it is that it isn't just about knowledge and science, its about wisdom ... it speaks to that part of my being that recognises it as such, I am re-mind-ed. Dopamine lift from that thought! I want my daughter and granddaughters to know this stuff ..... How better to show them than by walking the walk .. Mirror menopause to them as a gift, not a curse ...

### caroline — 2017-03-28T11:42:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reality-8.19284/post-211269>

Sue - you have given me so much to think about^^^^^

I willingly welcomed the box of raising children..... I loved being a Wife and Mother, and I always loved working too. On the other hand - my Mother was always very frustrated by life and its limitations for a wife and mother.

As far as BHRT ..... _I did need some help to get me on my feet. Dr. k. helped me with that. I did make an informed decision with a wonderful Doc that I have.

The science is mostly over my head - but I did follow JK's protocols as exactly as I could.....and voila - here I am!_

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reality-8.19284/post-211275>

So I'm not sure where this is leading me, but I'll take the first step anyway ....
I was thinking about ferritin and iron's role in menopause .... and then wondered what happened if a woman was low on ferritin and iron when she entered the menopause transition. Is it possible for there to be no symptoms (other than her periods stopping), so she thinks she is sailing through it. But she is losing energy from the "wave part" of sunlight, but can't access the particle aspect of light in the same way, to harvest more magnetic energy from the sun's rays naturally to gain more energy back than she lost in the process of menopause. She loses, but doesn't gain. Does time march on as before, or does it -more likely? - speed up. Or if redox is bad, does keeping ferritin and iron low stop her from frying her mitochondria? Waiting for a better environment?

I can see how the absence of menopausal symptoms could be a good thing (losing wave, gaining particle, good redox) or a bad thing (losing wave, no particle gain, poorer redox). Need to think about this a bit more in relation to my own menopause transition ...

The sun makes a dawn en-trance, it offers at-one-ment (or a-tone-ment), the birds are singing the chorus, and I am en-chant-ed, at Mother Nature's alter(ed state of consciousness).

### caroline — 2017-03-30T03:07:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reality-8.19284/post-211365>

I love that Sue ....Mother Nature's alter.

I am not so keen on atonement ....implies suffering to me...maybe being Catholic?

The sun makes a dawn en-trance ....and we are enchanted and entranced - perfect!

Tony loves birds and he always talks to them - as if they are human ....perhaps they are somewhat. Thy seem to listen intently and intelligently and often come right up to him.

I don't know how this fits in here. We walk almost every morning, more or less the same route. We always stop to do stretches at a particular corner. There is a lovely older, very well cared for, home. I always feel distressed around this house like the people there are so unhappy. We have seen the man a few times, we say good morning - no answer. His wife is the same. One day a little girl\[grandaughter\] was on the from porch and she came off the porch to say "hi". Her mother just screeched at her to get in the house this minute or there would be dire consequences.

what i mean is: in my life's experiences (to simplify) - my life goes one way - in a certain direction - then something major happens (a major life transition/decision/luck/opportunity whathaveyou) - then life goes another direction (totally different circumstances) - all these time periods/changes seem just like another life - so, there's one then another until finally you can say "in a previous life" - that's what time used to seem like to me -time seemed longer - now time seems different - hard to explain - but not re-incarnation type changes  An example- Maybe in one "lifetime" i went to church then another one I didn't -

sounds like ordinary life right? but i suppose it was/is my perception of these moments in time that makes them feel like lifetimes - there was a beginning a middle and an end (apparently) - then another opportunity - to think and be different or access different qualities in my own evolving self - still the same person yet at the same time not - anyway - this paragraph i just wrote seemed like it took a lifetime <g> jk

# Thread: Stomach Function
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stomach-function.19316/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2017-04-05T19:15:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stomach-function.19316/post-211818>

A long time ago someone here mentioned that stomach function was a lot more involved than people usually think. I know that water is absorbed through the stomach lining, and of course about the acid and the churning, and the valves at the top and bottom. But I recall that this person talked about the different regions of the stomach doing different things, and I know Jack has mentioned that certain nutrients, such as DHA and D-ribose, are shuttled away quickly, and don't go the usual route. Or is that in the small intestine?

Can someone suggest a good, mid-level book to read? I'm sure it will probably just be conventional wisdom, but I'd like to learn more. Do different branches of the vagus nerve in the stomach function differently?

Which brings up another old forum question from long ago--why does the vagus nerve stop at the descending colon? Inquiring minds want to know!

### drezy — 2017-04-05T19:23:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stomach-function.19316/post-211819>

I'm not aware of any books on this but here are maybe some related articles:

[https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2014/05/140514133438.htm](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2014/05/140514133438.htm)

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257695/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257695/)

Note the ncbi link states something that Jack has to battle regularly:

> Endogenous synthesis of DHA from ALA in humans is much lower and more limited than previously assumed.

### nonchalant — 2017-04-05T20:26:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stomach-function.19316/post-211823>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=211819)
>
> I'm not aware of any books on this but here are maybe some related articles:
>
> [https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2014/05/140514133438.htm](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2014/05/140514133438.htm)
>
> [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257695/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257695/)
>
> Note the ncbi link states something that Jack has to battle regularly:

Thanks, Drezy!
Jack doesn't disagree that we can synthesize much DHA, does he? That's why we eat so many oysters. In fact I think recently he mentioned that algae and seaweeds don't help as much, since their DHA isn't paramagnetic, in the SN-2 position. Apparently we aren't that good at flipping the switch. Maybe we need better grounding to be good at it, like our ocean buddies. Or insects, on the other hand.

I know my guinea fowl sure fly a lot after I've fed them some mealworms. On cloudy days they are pretty sedate, but if I feed them worms they will be on the roof and in the trees making all kinds of racket. I guess grass and seeds only go so far. They have cleared out all the grasshoppers in this part of the county (a miracle, I tell ya) so they have to get their DHA somewhere. They have been acting more like chickens, this year, scratching under the leaves for bugs.

### drezy — 2017-04-05T21:08:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stomach-function.19316/post-211828>

@[nonchalant](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/191/)

No mam. The ALA -> DHA conversion efficiency (or lack thereof ) itself is mentioned in that article. AKA don't think your flaxseeds are doing it for you. As a fellow Texan, I assume we are both eating the same gulf oysters which are direct DHA.

I assume you have the guinea fowl for the eggs. I always had a minor carnivore's moral dilemma until I tried raising chickens. On a perfect 3 acre setup they one-by-one chose to run to the neighbor's fence, hop it, and get crushed and gnawed on by a dog. Though I have a low tolerance for animal cruelty, I realized that mankind has domesticated these creatures into something incredibly dumb. I still feel a moral weight with being a carnivore, but not so much with chickens anymore.

This is not so much of a question but more of my thoughts on observing the benefits of CT
I have been doing CT for the past month and started with short cold showers increasing length
of time. I have upgraded to immersion in a tub with frozen Ice bricks and at the moment I am able to
stay in the tub for 20 mins and hope to build on this.

I have noticed my energy levels have increased and better clarity of thought with much deeper sleep
but I wasn't expecting old scar tissue to heal as it has. Scars have become smaller and have blended in
with surrounding skin much to my surprise. I actually look forward to CT which I have been doing each evening.

Friends have noticed on the complexion of my skin (glow) of late. Getting several hours of morning Sun
with Grounding and increased DHA from wild Salmon most days ,,feeling great !
Please any one with thoughts on skin health and CT I would welcome your input.

2016 Webinar "Dopamine and how its linked to biological time" Dr Kruse mentioned Cold Coupled to UV Light ,I really took a lot away from this presentation ,I am really interested in exploring this with the Coming Winter here in Australia.

### TANN — 2017-04-09T12:49:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fungal-issues-leptin-and-ct.19325/post-212047>

I'm considering diving into the leptin reset again whilst trying the CT (face)
I have long standing gut issues. Usually when I eat less carbs I start to get backed up with sluggish digestion.
My question is this, can I expect the leptin reset and CT along with plenty of daylight to correct my gut issues. I have tried many times to sort out my candida baceteria overload but feel that I have some very resistant fungals deep in my gut wall.
Would live to know opinions on this if anyone wants to share. Many thanks

### Allin — 2017-04-13T21:13:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fungal-issues-leptin-and-ct.19325/post-212280>

When your Redox is good, you will not have gut issues. Fixing gut issues starts with fixing your eye Clock first, then receptors on your skin, and then gut and lungs are next. Face dawn sun for 10-15 minutes as naked as you can get. Then AM sun as naked as you can get with some UV (I use D-minder application which shows when UV is available for your location). Start with 10-15 minutes each day to build up a sun callous. Drink bottled, well or spring water with NO fluoride, chlorine or bromide in it. Start off eating 3 raw oysters twice a week and increase that to 6 after 3-4 weeks. Start doing face dunks after two weeks of SUN. After two weeks of face dunks, start with cold water in tub for 30 minutes for another two weeks. If you can do the 30 minutes of tub and then the AM sun with UV in it would be best, but if not then do the 30 minutes of tub in the evening. Start adding ice after two weeks to the tub.

So after a month: you should have your dawn sun for 15 minutes, ice baths 30-45 minutes, UV sun for 30-60 minutes, 6 raw oysters twice a week, drinking good water every day. Protect your eyes in the evening, after 8 PM by wearing some type of blue blocking glasses. My rule of thumb, if you have had gut issues for 6 years; then I target & reassess 6 months out. I know you can do it and you will be amazed at how you feel six months from now.

# Thread: Carb question on Leptin RX
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carb-question-on-leptin-rx.19326/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### shaneoco — 2017-04-10T10:37:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carb-question-on-leptin-rx.19326/post-212097>

Hi, I've been doing the Leptin RX for about 2 weeks and am wondering about the amount of carbs I can eat. I am very cautious with this as I burned out with adrenal fatigue before with a very low carb/ keto diet with too much intermittant fasting (skipping breakfast!) and lots of exercise. Right now I'm eating a high-fat, high-protein diet with approx 150-200g of carbs a day. I find that if I lower my carb consumption I get a real stressy feeling (craving) even though I amn't actually hungry . If I eat a maybe 50-70g carbs it's then gone. So I'm guessing that even though I'm eating large amounts of fat (50g or more of butter or coconut oil) and protein per meal my body isn't really using it as fuel and when the carbs run low I'm getting the stressy/ craving feeling telling my body to eat more carbs again. Is this correct or am I missing something?

I'm 5'8, 65kg, lean but muscular build. Follow a fairly strict Epi-paleo diet and exercise minimally most days. Keep a good eye on my light situation, especially after sundown. I definitely don't want to lose
weight and could probably handle another 2-5kgs comfortably.
I'd be very slow to try keto/ v low carb again just because of the last time as I'm still not over the adrenal issue - have very poor fragmented sleep/ salt cravings etc. On a side note - sleep has been majorly improved by just doing a week of CT.

1) So I'm wondering is it a waste of time doing the Leptin RX and CT with so many carbs per day or will it just take longer? Please keep in mind I'm in the Northern hemisphere with 16 hours of daylight now.
2) And, why does Jack recommend having most of your daily carb intake in the morning and least in the evening? This seems counter-intuitive to other adrenal protocols which advocate a higher carb meal in the evening to help with sleep.
3) If all the fat I'm eating clearly isn't getting burned as fuel, what is happening to it?

Would appreciate if anyone could shed any light on this. Thank you.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

### Penny — 2017-04-16T19:02:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/infrared-near-infrared-light-question.19350/post-212408>

That depends on if you want to apply it directly to your skin and need a small portable get-between-your-neck-and-shoulder-blade thing or a giant IR lamp you get the whole body experience with - a brooders lamp (like the rubilux) as I understand should be kept 18" away from you or you can burn - plus, they can be a fire hazard - especially if you fall asleep under one - it is really easy to fall asleep with any of these lamps BTW -

A small CCTV light can just be pressed against a particular place you want to target for pain - I have this one and it works great:
[https://www.amazon.com/Towallmark-4...&qid=1492358404&sr=8-2&keywords=cctv+ir+light](https://www.amazon.com/Towallmark-48-LED-Infrared-Vision-Illuminator/dp/B0067S8IZ8/ref=sr_1_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1492358404&sr=8-2&keywords=cctv+ir+light)

also, notice how it isn't $100.00...  You need a special cord with it - I also use the rubilux 660nm bulbs, wrap them in plastic wrap and press against my skin - I'd love to know what yellow does - it seems to be great for skin issues and the immune system -

So, True story. My wife and I were at a "Fair" type event, it was acutally some sort of home and garden expo or something if I remember right quite a few years back. We happened to be pretty flush with cash at that time, and were just ripe for the stupid impulse buy. Some dude was selling these vibration platform thingys with handles on them. Long story short, we made it home a thousand bucks lighter, but the regretful new owners of a cobweb collector. I think we got on the thing like ten times....sad.

Anyway, I drug the thing out of the gargage and to my surprise, it still works great. Been riding it for a few days, like 10 minutes is all. It acutally is quite stimulating, upon more careful observation. Thinking about grounding the platform and like sticking out in the backyard in the Sun.

Naked Grounded Vibration! I call it NGB! What could go wrong?

So, is anyone else using anything like this?

repeat 12 months

### Rubicon — 2017-04-23T02:23:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phlebotomy-induced-reduction-of-body-iron.19365/post-212756>

I try to donate blood on a 'regular' basis with the goal of lowering my iron levels. I'm averaging one donation every ~9-months since 2014. Over that time my ferritin, iron, and hemoglobin levels have varied considerably, often measured on the high end of the reference range.

A couple of times after donating I've been noticeably physically tired for a few days after. Once, about 3-days after a blood draw I went for a 20-min walk and got completely weak/pale. I was breathing heavily as I slowly made my way back home. It really surprised me and gave my wife quite a scare. I'm not completely sure if that incident was related to blood donation, but I couldn't identify any other reason for it happening. It was a one-time occurrence.

Other times I've donated blood and felt a noticeable uptick in energy, clear headed, etc. I plan to continue the mitohack.

A few years back, I was injecting human growth hormone as part of hormone replacement. My iron level became too high to donate blood unless I obtained a script from a doctor to have a pint drawn. I stopped HGH for a couple months, my iron value dropped and I was able to donate blood. Then I would donate every 6-9 months and never again had an issue with a high iron level. I never had a issue of feeling weak after a blood donation. Realize I was living in front of a couple of computer monitors for 10+ hours a day and working-out inside a gym 4-5 times a week...

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-05-01T15:37:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phlebotomy-induced-reduction-of-body-iron.19365/post-213150>

"So if you read Time #21 and you wondering how ferritin works as a semiconductor you are in luck. Iron is an essential trace element that is used to form molecules in the body, such as hemoglobin. Ferritin is the major storage protein for iron. Free iron is toxic for cells because of its reactivity. So what controls its release? Its charge within the ferritin protein controls its release. Since iron is very reactive to sunlight becuase of its large amount of D shell electrons. Iron has a higher rate of emissivity than other atoms in cells and atoms with this ability readiate more energy than expected. When their emissivity is in the IR range of light, the proteins containing iron seem to be more energized. Emission of light energy reflects the intensity of charge movement in a protein. Ferritin is a solar active protein. Sunlight wavelengths can change when light travels through the atomic material in a cell or in blood. This change in incident light frequency is called a Stokes shift. The reason for this is when sunlight energized blood plasma solar radiation drives the charge movement which generates new electromagnetic waves that can also generate other electromagnetic waves that change the charges in their local environment. So when ferritin rises it is usually because people are not spending enough time in the sun. The body up-regulates ferritin production in this case awaiting its activation. Many people think that ferritin elevation is a sign of inflamamtion but it is actually a sign that these people live a solar spectral deficient life. If you lack sunlight, you have less electrons and more protons in tissues and this lowers the pH and this decreases the exclusion zone (EZ) in water. Ferritin is the protein within the body that stores iron and releases it through channels in a controlled fashion. The unique structure of ferritin forms a spherical shell in which the iron is "stored" as Fe(III) in a crystalline mineral. Ferritin consists of 24 peptide subunits that form two types of channels where these subunits intersect; the 3-fold channel is polar and the 4-fold channel is nonpolar. The residues that line the channels determine the polarity of the channel. Polarity refers to significant differences in charges due to the electronegativity between adjacent atoms in a molecule. The polarity of the channel link to the size of the EZ in blood plasma. The iron in the ferritin core is stored as Fe(III) in a crystalline solid. This crystal acts as a semiconductor inside of ferritin. The smallest repeating unit of the ferritin lattice is called the unit cell. The unit cell for iron is ferrihydrite. In the mineral ferrihydrite, every Fe(III) ion is coordinated to six O(II) ions. Oxygen is very electronegative compared to Fe(III). However, in ferritin, the mineral core has approximately 10% of the Fe(III) ions coordinated to five O(II) ions and 1 phosphate group. Phosphate is very active photosynthetically with sunlight. Most of the phosphate groups that are coordinated to the iron ions lie on the outside of the crystalline structure in ferritin, and are used to bind the mineral to the residues on the inside of the ferritin protein shell. When the Fe(III) in the crystalline mineral is reduced (added electrons from phosphorus or the charge separation of water by sunlight) to Fe(II) state, the iron becomes solvated in blood plasma and ferritin releases the solvated iron to tissues, in this form Fe(H2O)62+, through ferritin's 3-fold polar channel. In order for iron to be released from ferritin, the mineral lattice must be dissolved (i.e., to allow the iron atoms to break away from the lattice structure deep inside ferritin). This removal is accomplished by reducing iron from the Fe(III) (ferric) oxidation state to the Fe(II) (ferrous) oxidation state. In the Fe(II) state, iron breaks away from the lattice as the Fe2+ ion. The POSITIVE CHARGE of the Fe2+ ion attracts the electronegative oxygen atoms (ELECTRONEGATIVE) in water, and so a water "cage" forms around the ion. That cage is a Farady cage which shelters the iron from other electromagnetic waves. Hence, ferritin can control the amount of available iron in the body electrostatically, preventing iron disorders like anemia and iron overload. If someone's environment ruins the electrostatic mechanism expalined above for any reason it will effect iron status and ferritin levels in the blood. This mechanism is linked to presence or absence of charge in blood plasma or in phosphorus as a dopant. The EZ potential energy holds the key to what your iron status ultimately will be. Anemia is becoming epidemic in women and children these days for a deep reason: humans have created a low quantum yield world and this alters the charge of blood plasma which directly impacts the release of iron from ferritin. "

"The three-dimensional structure of ferritin is crucial to understand its quantum function within the body. In fact, the three-dimensional stucture of any molecule is critical in determining a molecule's properties and function optically. Hence, to better understand ferritin's role in the body, we must familiarize our self to different types of molecular representations to study ferritin's three-dimensional stucture. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3105](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3105608/)608

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...d-its-link-to-mitochondria.18340/#post-200468](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/ferritin-and-its-link-to-mitochondria.18340/#post-200468)

It always pays to go back and do your research.

### drezy — 2017-04-30T19:59:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-stage-2-compression-shirt.19374/post-213114>

> [TANN said:](/goto/post?id=213089)
>
> What stage are you at with CT and how's it working out for you drezy?

I average about 3 C/T's per week since mid February(2 months running so far) . My tub temperatures are anywhere from 47 to 55 depending on ambient temperature and how much ice I bought.
My wife (58 degrees or so) and son (50-52) join me once a week now.

The biggest changes I've noticed right upfront is:

-   Wife sleeps well almost all the time (In the past I've seen her sleeping, but she'd still claim she didn't sleep well)

-   With few exception I've always slept well, but my clock got readjusted. Now after sunset my body sends me signals that it's time to sleep. This was odd and mildly annoying at first since I'm the type that would usually be up till 2AM on a computer. I'm not complaining though since BP is down and libido is up
-   I ditched some saddlebags I had. Weight can creep on tall guys right over their kidneys. There a psychological effect when you go from feeling that it's OKish to pull off a shirt to being happy to pull the shirt off and have people ask what it is you are doing to be in shape. Down from ~16.9% bodyfat to ~ 13%
-   My wife's nighttime itchy skin went away. This seemed like a hormonal thing that was assisted by CT.

Coupled with grounding (I ran a separate ground to my tub AND do separate grounding as well) and UV I also noticed:

-   My cracked heels are almost completely healed.
-   My complexion is the best it's ever been.

With my wife's sleep and oxy squared away, my son is where my focus is currently. Since he's growing so quickly it's not easy to tell what CT effects are on him. I don't see any negative effects and he really likes doing the CT every week.

**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-mitochondriacs-thoughts-on-light-vibrations-ancient-pyramids-and-geometry.19389/>

### lohd2015 — 2017-04-27T16:18:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-mitochondriacs-thoughts-on-light-vibrations-ancient-pyramids-and-geometry.19389/post-213009>

I decided to create this thread after reading Dr. K's post on FB, April 25, 2017.
[https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1747482461982767](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1747482461982767)

I am truly excited as I believe this to be MASSIVE.

There has always been theories on the vibrational effects of Ancient pyramids, and the use of special materials such as mica.

**ANCIENT PYRAMIDS WERE HIGH FREQUENCY POWER STATIONS**
[http://revelationnow.net/2015/09/27/ancient-pyramids-were-high-frequency-power-stations/](http://revelationnow.net/2015/09/27/ancient-pyramids-were-high-frequency-power-stations/)

**A New Theory for the Great Pyramid**
[http://www.newdawnmagazine.com/arti...-how-science-is-changing-our-view-of-the-past](http://www.newdawnmagazine.com/articles/a-new-theory-for-the-great-pyramid-how-science-is-changing-our-view-of-the-past)
"the subtle electrical field created by the Great Pyramid would be of little use for means of powering equipment. However, such a field would deflect very low frequencies (VLF) and extremely low frequencies (ELF), frequencies that exist at all times in the atmosphere as a result of thunderstorms around the world. In agricultural experiments, VLF and ELF have been shown to promote plant growth. With the Great Pyramid operating as the engine, all of the pyramids could be connected by a subtle electrical field to create a canopy of sorts thereby deflecting VLF and ELF into the surrounding fields."
**The Place of the Gods**
[https://www.bibliotecapleyades.net/stargate/stargate10.htm](https://www.bibliotecapleyades.net/stargate/stargate10.htm)
This is an article on the Stargate at Abu Ghurab, or “the crow’s nest" as it is called, is a closed to the public archaeological site in the pyramid fields that run along side the Nile south of Cairo. Egyptologists quaintly refer to it as a ‘sun temple’, a ‘burial center’ or ‘funerary complex’ for a new cult of Ra (they usually use these terms when the actual function of a place is unclear).

There is a striking similarity between Abu Ghurab and Teotihuacan, which Dr. K talked about.

"Underneath the Pyramid of the Sun archaeologists discovered mica, a dielectric mineral composed of delicate crystal that is a semiconductor. The word “mica” is thought to be derived from the Latin word micare, meaning to shine, in reference to the brilliant appearance of this mineral (especially when in small scales).
Mica has a high dielectric strength and excellent chemical stability, making it a favored material for manufacturing capacitors for radio frequency applications. It has also been used as an insulator in high voltage electrical equipment. Sheet mica is used as an insulating material and as a resonant diaphragm in certain acoustical devices. Sitchin was perplexed by the presence of this mineral beneath the pyramid.......
Indeed, the Abu Ghurab site was beginning to look a lot like the Teotihuacan site.
Those basins, it turns out, were decanters.
The only thing missing was the mica."

**The ancient use of mica in pyramids**
[https://www.bibliotecapleyades.net/ciencia/pyramid\_thoughts/pyramidthoughts03b.htm](https://www.bibliotecapleyades.net/ciencia/pyramid_thoughts/pyramidthoughts03b.htm)

****Pyramid of Secrets - The Singing Pyramid and the Myth of Creation****
[http://robertbauval.co.uk/articles/articles\_guest/pos.html](http://robertbauval.co.uk/articles/articles_guest/pos.html)

"ANCIENT Egyptian literature testifies to the importance of sound at the time of the creation. In several versions of the myth, the Creator-God's emergence from the earth is accompanied by a great noise and commotion. The gods shout. The Earth resounds."

**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2017-04-28T19:24:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alergic-reaction-to-seafood-from-smell.19393/post-213051>

A couple of years ago I had either an allergic reaction or more likely, histamine reaction, to shrimp and cod. I have eaten both for years and never had a problem prior to this.

I cooked cod today for lunch and felt my left eye starting to swell up. This was while I was cooking, not eating. I immediately washed my hands and flushed my eyes with cold water. That seemed to resolve issue.

The obvious answer is that I touched the fish and then touched my eye, but I was careful to avoid touching my face while cooking. So that got me wondering. could it be that when I put pepper on seafood or fish, that histamine is released and the smell of pepper acts as the carrier?

# Thread: Creator of PEMF device response
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creator-of-pemf-device-response.19394/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cap — 2017-04-28T23:25:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creator-of-pemf-device-response.19394/post-213054>

So I reached out to the creator of the ICES device after being freaked out to learn about their risks after hearing Jack talk about their potential dangers and looking over the NTP report. I am pissed I have purchased the ICES device and sent over the NTP report to the creator asking if he could comment. This was his reply:

"Thanks for the interesting link. Yes, this has been a long-standing controversy, and I remain cautious about it. Here are my various thoughts on the matter, as I have been considering it for a very long time:

It is important to consider the effect of "publication bias", the tendency for scientific journals to prefer
long-standing controversy, and I remain cautious about it. Here are my various thoughts on the matter, as I have been considering it for a very long time:

It is important to consider the effect of "publication bias", the tendency for scientific journals to prefer to publish papers that show positive results rather than those papers that publish negative results. In many fields of science it is 5 to 10 times easier to publish positive results (where something happens) than negative results (where nothing happens). There are many examples of papers where no effect of RF radiation was found, no increase in cancer risk was linked to cell phone use. These papers are probably under-represented in the literature against positive results (RF causes cancer) by a factor of 5 to 10 simply because the positive results are much easier to get published, and in much higher impact journals. In two papers that I know of from 2011, where the researchers used cell phone radiation to show an increase of cancer risk in mice, they showed just the opposite: the cancer risk was slightly \*reduced\*, but also they noticed that the risk of age-related dementia was also reduced when the mice were exposed to cell phone RF energy. You can find the facts that you want to find in this area. I really do not see a clear scientific consensus.

Other things to consider are the fact that RF from cell phones is very different from the RF generated by other devices such as low frequency PEMF, micro-wave ovens, wifi, or even power lines. Scientists have been trying to show for decades that high power lines cause cancer... but the results are equivocal, some positive, some negative. "Electrosmog" is also an issue.

Since most of these common devices have much higher power RF radiation than ICES, you might be concerned about any of the following:
cell phones
micro-wave ovens
AC line power
wifi
digital cameras
computers (GHz clock speeds now)
computer monitors
electric motors on cars, appliances, elevators, etc..
fans, HVAC systems

I do think we need to consider the risks inherent in all electronic devices. But I also do not think these should raise serious alarm and fear since the evidence remains unclear, even after many thousands of scientific studies.

For example, low frequency PEMF is thousands of millions of times lower frequency than common RF signals. Also, the magnetic fields of PEMF devices close in on themselves to form tight loops, unlike RF radiation from an antenna, which is a point source that radiates energy to infinite distance. The fields from many PEMF systems are below the background noise even just a few feet away, unlike radio signals from an antenna.

Finally, I personally have concerns about excess RF exposure, so I have spent about 20 years reducing the excess energy in PEMF waveforms. This has resulted in the modern version of ICES technology which uses only about 1/500th of the energy of the original PEMF devices of 20 years ago. That's a 99.8% reduction in RF energy in ICES, without loss of biological benefit. That is why you can run our device on a 9V battery, whereas most PEMF systems require access to power from the main lines.

You also need to consider bias in the study: who funded it? Was it supported at least in part by a group that has a reason to see the outcome they reported. Such bias in science is a huge (and growing) problem. The first well-documented case of this was when petro companies funded a lot of research to show that leaded gasoline was safe for people and the environment. It clearly is not, but they heavily supported biased science and to this day some people still cite those reports.

My opinion: in this case the science is unclear, but when we see an effect is seems to be small unless a lot of RF energy at high frequency is used in the study. I advise caution, and I continue to advise people to carefully weigh the risk vs benefit of applying PEMF on or near the head. In general I think people should avoid unnecessary exposure to all forms of RF. but necessary forms of exposure can be OK.

I hope that perspective is helpful,

Bob"

Anyone here (smarter than me) want to chime in with something to ask him or maybe link him to any more resources?

thought this would be a good share and I still have much to learn to l get to everyone else's 'level' on here

# Thread: Trying to figure out why I sleep like shit at my house.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-figure-out-why-i-sleep-like-shit-at-my-house.19407/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RyanMSauce — 2017-05-01T23:24:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-figure-out-why-i-sleep-like-shit-at-my-house.19407/post-213180>

I am having trouble sleeping at my house that I moved into a few months ago. I picked this house because it was relatively low in EMF and the magnetic fields from the power lines is lower than most homes I checked out in the area.

The only negative thing I know about this area is that they have the bad type of smart meters that send a signal out every minute probably. I had mine removed and I'm trying to get my neighbor to do the same.

It seems I can't get a deep nights sleep. Sometimes I can sleep a straight 4 or 5 hours then I wake up and its hard to fall back asleep or I will fall back asleep and toss and turn the rest of the night.

I turn my power off at night to my house and my cell phone is off as well.

As I lay in my room it feels like I have a small headache.

I wake up here and feel like there isn't enough dopamine in my brain. I am trying to get a lot of creative work done and I don't have enough willpower.

I live in phoenix Arizona. I work from home so I am able to get outside a lot.

I know something is up because when I go to California and am sleeping in terrible emf environments with wifi and florescent lights at hotels and hostels I still seem to sleep better and my dopamine comes back. I have more motivation there.

I wear blue blockers at night.

I can't live like this my life is wasting. I have too much to accomplish.

I am going to get a mycotoxin test. Maybe there is mold somewhere. I know I have allergies to all the pollen here.

Any ideas of what else I can test for? Maybe its the smart meters? I have to fix this!

### Inger — 2017-05-02T11:33:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-figure-out-why-i-sleep-like-shit-at-my-house.19407/post-213204>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-figure-out-why-i-sleep-like-shit-at-my-house.19407/post-213205>

what about hiring an electrical engineer to come over and test everything? Sometimes water pipes can be an issue - sort of cross contamination as I understand it.

Is moving an option?

### RyanMSauce — 2017-05-03T20:02:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-figure-out-why-i-sleep-like-shit-at-my-house.19407/post-213312>

The neighbors are close. But there is no on behind me. There smart meter is 40-50 feet away maybe?
Sometimes my eyes burn if I touch them. Im allergic to something.
I could move its just a huge pain in the ass. I would need to break my lease. I looked a so many places until I found one in an area I like and that didn't have a 1mg field coming from a power line.

# Thread: Trying leptin reset- wondering how broken I am...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-leptin-reset-wondering-how-broken-i-am.19414/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lyse — 2017-05-02T21:37:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-leptin-reset-wondering-how-broken-i-am.19414/post-213253>

Hi there everyone. Hi Jack! You are brilliant. I am so glad I found you and your book and this forum! I have a couple questions... wanted to give you all some background on me... was hoping for some answers....

Where to begin... lets see. I was an avid HCG user... I did probably 11 rounds over the course of 5 years. Horrid I know... Homeopathic and RX HCG, Patches, Inhaller, I tried it all. I followed Colleen Coble as my mentor for HCG for years. She helped me get back on course when I started to regain the weight. I kept regaining the 30-40 lbs I tried to keep off which put me at my "ideal weight" of 145. I always made excuses like, I didn't do it right, I added to many things back, I had to many cheat meals etc which is why the weight always came back, even after 9 months of holding it etc. I kept going back to HCG because it was always so easy to knock off 15 lbs in 3 weeks... and do 2 rounds back to back... basically I think I broke myself.

I have tried to lose weight now without HCG by severe low carb/ and crazy amount of exercise and I actually gained weight... I had signed myself up for personal trainer 3x a week and 2x a week HIT class and was literally watching weight come on me... It was horrible. I have had 2 pregnancies but only 1 child. The other was in my tubes and had to be removed via a chemo shot last september 2016. That probably caused even more issues... basically the second I eat carbs even in small amounts or even fruit I gain weight... I try to stay away from sugar completely. Unless I do constant steak days I am literally watching weight come on in a mater of days.

When I found this Leptin Reset protocol it sounded just like me. I am extremely irritable. Very low energy, cold all the time, I sleep with so many blankets in the summer, low sex drive, constant headaches, fat, could never wake up in the moring, extremely low Vitamin D Levels, thyroid normal apparently etc. I read about the exercise actually causing harm when Leptin resistant and thought- wow- that's what was happening to me! My husband thought I was crazy blaming the exercise as actually causing more harm than good....anyway....

My first question is- how long do you think it will take me to fix my hormones? 10 weeks?? longer? I feel like I really messed myy body up.... also when should I start cold therapy? what is your opinion about people that used HCG ???

I have been doing the Leptin Reset protocol for 1 week. I am trying to follow it to the T. I hate seafood, but none the less, I have been buying shrimp and scallops and tuna by the boatload. Eating within 30 minutes of rising, 3 meals a day only, nothing after 7pm with at least 4 hours between meals. I have completely removed dairy, grains, fruit and nightshades.. except for 2tbsp of cream in my morning coffee... I could remove it if you thought it would help.... I am trying to lose this last 30 lbs I keep regaining.... none of my clothes fit. I only do walking as exercise right now- I quit everything else. I am taking Vitamin D 5000 IU and K2 supplements, Magnesium, a probiotic and fish oil with my multivitamin.

2nd question- the first 4 days I was eating humas with my meat at dinner thinking it was allowed.... I quickly learned it wasn't. Do I have to wait 2 weeks now to restart the protocol? Did I mess up already? I actually have been feeling pretty good. I can wake up for the first time ever without even trying at 7am! This is like amazing for me. I feel refreshed. I already see my energy level doing well. However I wasnt sure if I messed up. I was going to try to start doing the cold therpy in the next couple days- I was waiting to get almost 2 weeks in....

3rd question- Can we cook with coconut flour or almond flour? I am eating meat and seafood and veggies only- very low carb no fruit and tons of eggs. I have horrid cravings for other things but can abstain, just curious if I can cook with coconut flour and make something other than meat 3x a day. I am really good at making coconut pancakes with eggs, coconut flour and coconut oil with a tiny bit of stevia. I know no other sugars are allowed- I only use stevia anyway. Its totally fine if I can't I can do anything to fix myself- just was curious about this because it would be nice to have more variety.

4th question- Can I add nuts in or should i continue to avoid them? I have just been eating the seafood, bacon, eggs, steak, turkey, sausage and leafy greens/cauliflower but was wanting to have some almonds or cashews etc. Yes or Hell no?

5th question- Why does Paleo have so many contradictions on what you can eat and can't. I have been reading non stop paleo cook books at the library and they all have varrying degrees of things you can and can't have.... is there a EPI Paleo cookbook I can look at- I don't want to mess this Leptin Reset up by using things I shouldnt... like peanut butter or cottage cheese etc.

### JanSz — 2017-05-02T23:39:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-leptin-reset-wondering-how-broken-i-am.19414/post-213256>

\[qu ote="Lyse, post: 213253, member: 20042"\] "ideal weight" of 145.
\---------------------------------------------
Very low energy, cold all the time, I sleep with so many blankets in the summer, low sex drive, constant headaches, fat, could never wake up in the moring, extremely low Vitamin D Levels, thyroid normal apparently etc.

Start with Circadian Rhythm. Follow what sun tells you.

You will know you did good job with Circadian Rhythm when:
1-- your saliva cortisol is real close to a top line (on test report sheet).
2-- your vitamin D (without any supplementation) is about:
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)
3-- your sleep is good

To fix hormone you have to test them first.
To know what to eat ------>test:
fatty acids
micronutrients

Fish oils, krill oils are most abused and hurtful fatty acids, do not use them.

When you have to eat something that have something real 100% very good.
First be careful, someone is trying to use magic trick and turn off your attention from the fact that with that good stuff comes poison (in small amounts).

............
The more hours between meals, the faster you will loose weight.
You cannot loose weight if your fasting insulin is over 2-3.

......
What you should eat highly depends on latitude of your residence.

...

**Nothing is very good.
Nothing is very bad.
We need everything in just right amounts.**

Hi!
Ok sounds good. I will start a journal. I did read his book and have it on my kindle. I don't listen to the podcasts yet and do not have John Ott's book. Who is this? Sorry I am a newbie... I am trying to digest Jack's his blogs and reading other peoples messages on the forum as fast as I can. I just started this protocol and so far only am doing the diet piece. I will quickly remedy that. I wanted to add the cold therapy in the next couple days starting with the face dunks. How do people do the ice bath in a normal tub? Do we just run to the gas station and buy ice everyday? I don't have a deep freezer yet so no where to store ice in my tiny freezer at moment.

I do not have the blue blocker glasses... I ordered them today.
I started going outside first thing in the AM 2 days ago barefoot... I am still wearing a jacket.
I am a stay at home mom from Chicago. I drink bottled water or smart water.
I guess I am a bit overwhelmed with all the things to do... I found the leptin reset and thought this was my answer, and now see it is but one piece in the puzzle to my health. I am excited to do everything just catching my breath as to where to begin....

Thanks for your help. I will start my journal.

### caroline — 2017-05-05T07:02:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-leptin-reset-wondering-how-broken-i-am.19414/post-213428>

Hi Lyse .....yes there is so much here - but just start slowly with the leptin reset and face dunks and listen to Jack's podcasts. It is a much quicker way to get information right now.

concentrate on light, water and magnetism......

Chicago is a huge issue .....your postcode matters.

You absolutely need blue blockers. Fake light is a huge issue.

Have you got all electronics out of your bedroom?

John Ott's book is a small book and a very easy read ......Helath and light.

Don't stress ......just follow the leptin reset and do face dunks.

Jack mentions krill oil at around 53 mins in on his cold Thermogenesis webinar
also,,,

Cold Thermogenesis 11: Paleo FX To Practice

Beginning in week 1, I began an aggressive Mg supplementation program as follows:

-   Daily A.M. – [Mg Threonate @ 200mg orally](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B006P536E6/ref=as_li_qf_sp_asin_il_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=9325&creativeASIN=B006P536E6&linkCode=as2&tag=jackru-20) / Mg Chloride Daily
-   Daily P.M. – 60 minutes foot soaks w 2 oz / [ZMA Supplement](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0013OZGQ0/ref=as_li_qf_sp_asin_il_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=9325&creativeASIN=B0013OZGQ0&linkCode=as2&tag=jackru-20) w 450mg Mg & 400mg Mg Glycinate.
-   b) I also have added [Grass Fed Beef Liver](http://www.grasslandbeef.com/StoreFront.bok?affId=135230) (offal) and other sources of dietary Mg into my diet.

7) After Week 4, I moved into a maintenance CT mode @ 2-3 times per week at 60 minutes each session.

8) Additional supplementation of:

-   [Krill Oil](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B003O1Y4C2/ref=as_li_qf_sp_asin_il_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=9325&creativeASIN=B003O1Y4C2&linkCode=as2&tag=jackru-20) @ 750mg daily
-   [Curcumin](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000X9P5GM/ref=as_li_qf_sp_asin_il_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=9325&creativeASIN=B000X9P5GM&linkCode=as2&tag=jackru-20) @ 750mg
-   [Resveratrol](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B003Q8SAZU/ref=as_li_qf_sp_asin_il_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=9325&creativeASIN=B003Q8SAZU&linkCode=as2&tag=jackru-20) @ 1000mg

Cold Thermogenesis 5: Biologic magnetism ( I think I just foumd the answer to my question in the next paragraph)

-
    This is why omega three fats would be favored over omega six fats in mammals who live in the coldest environments on Earth. The reason is that omega three fats have more unsaturated bonds that allow for more fluidity in the cell membranes for ideal signaling. There are also electrical reasons for this as well. DHA can select for certain phospholipids to work in a more optimal electric manner. Take a guess where the omega three sources come from? The deep ocean. It appears evolution is using form for function ideally, once again. The best natural source is krill and other fish that [support huge ecosystems in our polar seas](http://brent.xner.net/pdf/JungeXnerStaley2011_ExtremeHandbook6.3.pdf) for these mammals. Seafood is always the right choice, but the use of krill oil use makes a lot more sense for warm adapted mammals than fish oil does because of its stability in warm temperatures. DHA is also not optically stable outside of fish. This is a major down side to fish oils.

-
    cheers WalterNL reading it now!

# Thread: How do photons stored in hemoglobin exert a physiologic affect?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-photons-stored-in-hemoglobin-exert-a-physiologic-affect.19421/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty_M — 2017-05-04T23:53:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-photons-stored-in-hemoglobin-exert-a-physiologic-affect.19421/post-213391>

I understand they are absorbed when blood comes to the surface because of UV stimulating NO.

Once stored in hemoglobin, what do they do and how? How to they get to the mitochondria?

Are there any posted blogs on specifically this?

### Lahelada — 2017-05-06T23:40:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-photons-stored-in-hemoglobin-exert-a-physiologic-affect.19421/post-213538>

**Got that in my collection from Dr Ks facebook:**
"
"Tonight's photon factoid: Can we explain in real simple terms the journey of a photons interaction with a protein or cell membrane inside our circulatory system?
\-Photon leaves sun after one million years of confinement

\-Travels to skin and hits the skin and skin releases nitric oxide. The NO causes a phototropism effect as the surface arterioles come to the surface of the skin and the blood gets irradiated by all frequencies of the light. (leafs move toward sun too)
\-Sun light is absorbed differently by each tissue.
Hemoglobin absorbs 270-600 nm with a sharp cut off at 600 nm. Hits electron in cell membrane of red blood cells and this changes DHA and sulfates them
\-Electron energy in proteins are raised called excitement. This creates an exciton. Since RBC's have no mitochondria excitons react with water in blood plasma. (93%)
...
\-electron is now close enough to reactant because of water and water is turned into a sea of EZ that creates forward flow in the closed tube This lowers BP and affects renin angiotensin syst,e far away. Sunlight is also a calcium channel blocker so it lowers calcium flows to lower BP too. And dermal pooling also has a BP lowering effect to cause local reaction in the vessels.
\-raised electron facilitates one of the many reactions constantly happening in cell simultaneously as excitons are apt to do. This happens in leaves too.

Or in the case of hemoglobin
\-Exciton ejected from iron when they come into contact with a mitochondria at some tissue.
\-Exciton finds the best route out of the protein using cool quantum effects that mimic what we see in leaves reaction centers.
\-The formation of EZ and a photoelectric change to the surface of the RBC somehow helps the red blood cell move through body electromagnetically. As a RBC travels through the vessel it sheds sulfate groups which carry a negative charge. This also means within the circulatory system we develop both and electric and magnetic field. The voltage change is caused by the motuons of the RBC itself.......DHA and sulfates in the cell membrane. The hemoglobin inside it a ferry boat of light to be given to cytochrome 1 of mitochondria. It is not involved in the shedding phenomena. Remember RBC's have no mitochondria by design......they have to bring light to cells with mitochondria. The voltage change with blood flow acts like an induction magnet. As the RBC's moves through the circulation the magnetic field induced allows the vessel wall to release nitric oxide and sulfate cholesterol. This occurs in the blood plasma which is filled with 93% water. The EZ augments blood flow this way.....because there is a lot of positive charge in the artery wall and a large negative charge in the RBC's and EZ of plasma. This allows the like like likes action to occur between the EZ and RBC's to improve laminar flow.

Cholesterol plays a huge role here......Cholesterol stabilizes the artery wall by stablizing the cells in this tissue by stabilizing the inner mitochondrial membrane to lower vibrations in the Q cycle. This makes sure the " light deli slicer" (recent webinar) is accurate in making the key red light frequencies from the 340nm excited electrons that enter at cytochrome that will be shuttled to cytochrome 3 for the distal cytochromes. Most people know that sunlight has 42% IR-A light present in it. Most people do not know that red light at 613.5 and 623.5 nm (in one spectrum, at 606 nm), in the red maximum have some interesting effects on human tissue. RBC's like red light up until 600 nm only. That is where hemoglobin's peak ends sharply for absoption. Are there other key frequencies buried in the sun for healing other parts of our tissues? Yes. The far-red maximum has exact peak positions between 667.5 and 683.7 nm in different spectra. Two near infrared maxima have peak positions in the range 750.7-772.3 nm and 812.5-846.0 nm. Might these peaks be critical in our mitochondria too? Might this be why low cholesterol kills us with cancers? I think so and this mechanism above shows you why.

Cholesterol is a polar molecule that needs the sun to sulfate it so that molecular vibrations are lowered. The isomerization step in the skin requires an elevated surface temperature, that UVB light gives the skin to photonically change cholesterol to its sulfated form and then S- cholesterol to its sulfated Vitamin D3 form. This is fundamentally why Ancel Keys 7 countries Study was completely worthless on quantum biology merits. He found that high un sulfated cholesterol linked to heart disease. That link should have been expected because in all seven countries he sampled the zip codes were missing UVB light. Lack of UVB light is what drives heart disease not the presence of LDL cholesterol. When you ascribe causation to food and not to light you make catastrophic errors in science."

### Inger — 2017-05-05T12:01:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bit-confused-questions-found-coconut-butter-ok-on-reset.19429/post-213434>

I would say, listen to your body  I do not do too well with coconut butter... makes me feel weird... (but I am from the high north .. I know people that do great with coconut butter and for them it is just fine, food is just 10% from all things that impact our health anyways) and coconut pancakes are for me kinda junk food..lol The more you fry stuff the less nutrients you get.... For a treat once in a while it might be just fine
I love simple foods and as less cooked/processed as possible, makes me feel the best. But if you feel great with it, why not
Also you might need some transition foods to not have a too hard way mentally to give up old foods, I did that too  After a while you might not want the "comfort foods" anymore.
