# Thread: Graham Hancock's PreHistory Gets a Boost
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graham-hancocks-prehistory-gets-a-boost.19430/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jason Coates — 2017-05-05T13:13:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graham-hancocks-prehistory-gets-a-boost.19430/post-213435>

[http://www.dailymail.co.uk/sciencet...e-Age-wiped-cvilisation-13-000-years-ago.html](http://www.dailymail.co.uk/sciencetech/article-4457530/Mini-Ice-Age-wiped-cvilisation-13-000-years-ago.html)

I stopped reading Hancock 10 years ago but his flood v catastrophe wiping out an advanced civilization just got more legs.
Hancock believes the Gobekli stones not only describe an ancient cosmic collision, but predict another.

He thinks that what hit Earth in 10,950BC was actually a massive piece of debris in the Taurid meteor stream, a belt containing millions of space rocks.

Read more: [http://www.dailymail.co.uk/sciencet...ilisation-13-000-years-ago.html#ixzz4gCKZ7bak](http://www.dailymail.co.uk/sciencetech/article-4457530/Mini-Ice-Age-wiped-cvilisation-13-000-years-ago.html#ixzz4gCKZ7bak)

### JoeBranca — 2017-05-06T03:16:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graham-hancocks-prehistory-gets-a-boost.19430/post-213483>

Magicians of the Gods is a worthwhile read. Definitely sparks my curiosity even though I can't say whether his theories are water tight. Hard for me to tell if a book that appears well researched is just cherry picking data and unexplained phenomena to strengthen a thesis, or he's really onto something everyone's overlooked. He does try to do justice to critics and mainstream (non-cataclysmic) interpretations of various sites and finds, while also poking various holes in the gradualism paradigm.

### JoeBranca — 2017-05-10T06:19:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/graham-hancocks-prehistory-gets-a-boost.19430/post-213797>

what do we really know about history of bipedal hominids in North America? possible 130kya evidence found among mastodon site in coastal California!

# Thread: New house shielding

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KB48kg — 2017-05-07T02:30:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-house-shielding.19438/post-213546>

I purchased an old house and removed all walls, ceiling and floors to the studs.
My ultimate goal is to protect my family was emf. I thought about chicken wire shielding, that will create Faraday cage and may cause problems. Also it is unclear on how to protect windows and doors. I also thought about insulating only top floor with the bedrooms. Then I saw a suggestion of using using protection on the walls and ceiling, leaving it all the floor to avoid Faradey cage issues. Obviously netting will need to be grounded. I also saw something about using Shumann resonance generator for the Faraday cages.
I searched the forum and did not find a clear advise.
I would appreciate a guidence or pointing to the place where I can find one.

### mobeus — 2017-05-31T05:36:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-house-shielding.19438/post-215157>

Magnesium oxide wallboard shields EMF. It's available. Chicken wire won't do jack.

BUT....think "repattern" not "shield". Shielding is actually a misnomer. The nnEMF is not bouncing back to where it came, and it's not being absorbed by the "shield". It's actually being repatterned. Centripetal (fractal) charge is the opposite of "radiating" charge.

Your best defense is a strong 'aura'. Do the work here and build your aura.

There is also technology available that uses high dielectric paramagnetic minerals and holograms to repattern nnEMF. I think this is much more realistic and practical than believing you can "shield" it. Not sure this works, but it's easy to implement and there's no harm in trying it.

[https://www.safespaceprotection.com/products/](https://www.safespaceprotection.com/products/)

### Limpei — 2017-06-06T11:47:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-house-shielding.19438/post-215478>

I have been asking myself the same question regarding my place.

Research in a nutshell here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/lps-optimal-journal.17998/page-3#post-214468](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/lps-optimal-journal.17998/page-3#post-214468)

Higher frequency 5G = Smaller hole size.
.
So far, if it's to reflect nnEMF/shield it from some regions, shielding doesn't seem required.
Think of a reflector..

Do let me know what else you may find. I'm also on this journey.
It's either wire mesh, or aluminium foil baked into the walls --> replastering house --> $$$$$
